### `PO/Core/Variable.lean` ‚ÄĒ PO variables and regimed variables

Abstracts the `S.hXbool.measurable.comp ((measurable_pi_apply _).comp (P.measurable_eval _))`
chain used throughout IV/LATE-style proofs.  A `POVar P őĪ` bundles a node
`v : P.V` with a measurable equivalence `P.X v ‚ČÉŠĶź őĪ`, so factual and
counterfactual realisations land in a fixed measurable type.  `RegimedVar`
further attaches a regime and exposes a uniform `value : P.ő© ‚Üí őĪ`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POVar P őĪ` | `structure { v : P.V, equiv : P.X v ‚ČÉŠĶź őĪ }` | PO system variable identified with a fixed measurable value type `őĪ` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cf a r` | `P.ő© ‚Üí őĪ` | Counterfactual value `a` under regime `r`: `ŌČ ‚Ü¶ a.equiv (P.eval r ŌČ a.v)` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.factual a` | `P.ő© ‚Üí őĪ` | Factual value `a.cf Regime.empty` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.measurable_cf` | `Measurable (a.cf r)` | One-liner measurability of the counterfactual map | **Proved** |
| `POVar.measurable_factual` | `Measurable a.factual` | Specialisation of `measurable_cf` to the empty regime | **Proved** |
| `POVar.event a x` | `Set P.ő©` | Preimage `a.factual ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' {x}` ‚ÄĒ the event `{a = x}` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.measurableSet_event` | `MeasurableSet (a.event x)` | Measurability of the factual event (needs `MeasurableSingletonClass őĪ`) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cfUnder a w y` | `P.ő© ‚Üí őĪ` | Counterfactual `a` under the single-node intervention `{w ‚Üź y}`, i.e. `a.cf (Regime.single w.v (w.equiv.symm y))`; replaces hand-rolled `DofZ z`, `YofD d` patterns | **Defined** |
| `POVar.measurable_cfUnder` | `Measurable (a.cfUnder w y)` | Measurability of the single-intervention counterfactual | **Proved** |
| `RegimedVar P őĪ` | `structure { var : POVar P őĪ, regime : Regime P.V P.X }` | PO variable bundled with an intervention regime | **Defined** |
| `RegimedVar.value` | `P.ő© ‚Üí őĪ` | `rv.var.cf rv.regime` ‚ÄĒ uniform evaluator used by `IndepCF` and `POCFBundle.jointValue` | **Defined** |
| `RegimedVar.measurable_value` | `Measurable rv.value` | Measurability of the uniform evaluator | **Proved** |
| `RegimedVar.ofFactual a` | `RegimedVar P őĪ` | Factual bundling `‚ü®a, Regime.empty‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ the standard shape for "factual Z is independent of the cf bundle" | **Defined** |
| `RegimedVar.ofSingle a w x` | `RegimedVar P őĪ` | Bundling under the single-node intervention `{w ‚Üź x}` ‚ÄĒ shortcut for `dUnderZ`/`yUnderD` style variables | **Defined** |
| `POVar.indicator a x` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Real-valued indicator `1_{a = x}` (defined as `(a.event x).indicator 1`); subsumes per-system `indD`/`indA`/`indM` boilerplate | **Defined** |
| `POVar.indicator_eq_event_indicator` | `a.indicator x = (a.event x).indicator (fun _ => 1)` | Set-indicator form (definitional `rfl`) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.indicator_apply_eq_one` | `a.factual ŌČ = x ‚Üí a.indicator x ŌČ = 1` | Pointwise value on `{a = x}` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.indicator_apply_eq_zero` | `a.factual ŌČ ‚Č† x ‚Üí a.indicator x ŌČ = 0` | Pointwise value off `{a = x}` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.measurable_indicator` | `Measurable (a.indicator x)` | Measurability of the indicator | **Proved** |
| `POVar.stronglyMeasurable_indicator_comap` | `StronglyMeasurable[comap a.factual _] (a.indicator x)` | Strong measurability w.r.t. the `a.factual` ŌÉ-algebra | **Proved** |
| `POVar.integrable_indicator` | `[IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ] ‚Üí Integrable (a.indicator x) P.őľ` | Indicator is bounded by 1, hence integrable | **Proved** |
| `POVar.indicator_eq_one_or_zero` | `a.indicator x ŌČ = 1 ‚ą® a.indicator x ŌČ = 0` | Binary value | **Proved** |
| `POVar.indicator_add_indicator_not` | `a.indicator true ŌČ + a.indicator false ŌČ = 1` | Binary-treatment complementarity (`POVar P Bool`) | **Proved** |

### `PO/Assumptions/ConsistencyLemmas.lean` ‚ÄĒ Pointwise consistency for `POVar`

Generalises the two pointwise consistency specialisations of `LATE.lean`
(`DofZ_eq_factualD_on_zEvent`, `factualY_eq_YofD_factualD`) to arbitrary
`POVar` pairs on distinct nodes.  Each lemma discharges the `Consistency.factual`
clause by reducing it to a singleton-regime agreement check.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POSystem.factualAgrees_empty` | `‚ąÄ ŌČ, P.FactualAgrees Regime.empty ŌČ` | Trivial base case: every `ŌČ` factually agrees with the empty regime (target is `‚ąÖ`) | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.factualAgrees_sqcup` | `{r‚āĀ r‚āā : Regime}`, `h : r‚āĀ.Disjoint r‚āā`, `P.FactualAgrees r‚āĀ ŌČ` ‚Üí `P.FactualAgrees r‚āā ŌČ` ‚Üí `P.FactualAgrees (r‚āĀ.sqcup r‚āā h) ŌČ` | Combinator: `FactualAgrees` for a disjoint union reduces componentwise. Lets theorem files assemble multi-target consistency hypotheses from per-variable pieces without hand-rolling a `Finset.mem_union` case split | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factualAgrees_single` | `(a : POVar P őĪ) (x : őĪ)`, `a.factual ŌČ = x` ‚Üí `P.FactualAgrees (Regime.single a.v (a.equiv.symm x)) ŌČ` | Combinator: turns a factual equality `a.factual ŌČ = x` into `FactualAgrees` for the singleton regime `{a.v ‚Üź a.equiv.symm x}`. Base case for building compound `FactualAgrees` via `factualAgrees_sqcup` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a w : POVar`, `a.v ‚Č† w.v`, `ŌČ ‚ąą w.event y` ‚Üí `a.cfUnder w y ŌČ = a.factual ŌČ` | Consistency of `a` under `{w ‚Üź y}`: on the event `{w = y}`, the `w`-intervened counterfactual of `a` equals the factual | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factual_eq_cfUnder_self_selected` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a w : POVar`, `a.v ‚Č† w.v`, `‚ąÄ ŌČ` ‚Üí `a.factual ŌČ = a.cfUnder w (w.factual ŌČ) ŌČ` | Pointwise: the factual equals the counterfactual under `{w ‚Üź factual w(ŌČ)}` ‚ÄĒ the identity used to rewrite factual outcomes in terms of cf outcomes on the natural treatment choice | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factual_mul_indicator_eq_cfUnder_mul_indicator` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a : POVar P ‚ĄĚ`, `w : POVar P ő≤`, `a.v ‚Č† w.v` ‚Üí `(fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ a.factual ŌČ * 1_{w=y} ŌČ) = (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ a.cfUnder w y ŌČ * 1_{w=y} ŌČ)` | Integrated form of consistency: multiplying by the indicator `1_{w=y}` lets factual and cf values be freely interchanged; workhorse of backdoor-style Step-1 rewrites | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factual_mul_indicator_eq_cfUnder_mul_indicator_fn` | `hC`, `y : POVar P ‚ĄĚ`, `w : POVar P ő≤`, `[MeasurableSingletonClass ő≤]`, `y.v ‚Č† w.v` ‚Üí `(fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ y.factual ŌČ * w.indicator x ŌČ) = (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ y.cfUnder w x ŌČ * w.indicator x ŌČ)` | Pointwise-function variant matching the new `POVar.indicator` shape; one-line reduction to the `Set.indicator` form above. Used by backdoor/Manski/frontdoor/DTR Step-1 rewrites | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cf_eq_factual_of_factualAgrees` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a : POVar P őĪ`, `r : Regime`, `a.v ‚ąČ r.target`, `P.FactualAgrees r ŌČ` ‚Üí `a.cf r ŌČ = a.factual ŌČ` | **Multi-target** consistency: pointwise, `a`'s counterfactual under any regime `r` (not naming `a.v`) coincides with its factual at every `ŌČ` that factually agrees with `r`. Generalises `cf_eq_factual_on_event` from singleton to arbitrary regimes | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factual_mul_indicator_eq_cf_mul_indicator` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a : POVar P ‚ĄĚ`, `r : Regime`, `a.v ‚ąČ r.target`, `E : Set P.ő©`, `(‚ąÄ ŌČ ‚ąą E, P.FactualAgrees r ŌČ)` ‚Üí `(fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ a.factual ŌČ * 1_E ŌČ) = (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ a.cf r ŌČ * 1_E ŌČ)` | **Multi-target** integrated consistency: on any event `E` of full factual agreement with `r`, the factual outcome and the multi-target counterfactual outcome are interchangeable inside `a ¬∑ 1_E`. Generalises `factual_mul_indicator_eq_cfUnder_mul_indicator` to arbitrary regimes; consumed directly by `cdtr_backdoor` Step 1 | **Proved** |
| `POVar.eventCondExp_cfUnder_eq_factual_on_event` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `y : POVar P ‚ĄĚ`, `a : POVar P ő≤`, `y.v ‚Č† a.v` ‚Üí `eventCondExp őľ (a.event a‚āÄ) (y.cfUnder a a‚āÄ) = eventCondExp őľ (a.event a‚āÄ) y.factual` | Consistency-on-event for `eventCondExp`: on the stratum `{a = a‚āÄ}`, the `{a ‚Üź a‚āÄ}`-counterfactual and the factual have the same event-level conditional mean. Shared rewrite in `Manski/{MTR,MTS}` and (via the `Fintype` total law below) the integrated MIV bounds; lives in `EventCondExp.lean` alongside the other `eventCondExp` tooling | **Proved** |

### `PO/Assumptions/IndepCF.lean` ‚ÄĒ Independence of PO counterfactual bundles

Named shapes for `IndepFun` / `CondIndepFun` over a heterogeneous bundle of
regimed PO variables.  Replaces bespoke product-tuple encodings such as the
`cfTuple` pattern in `PO/ID/Exact/LATE.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POCFBundle P` | `structure { n : ‚Ąē, type : Fin n ‚Üí Type*, inst : ‚ąÄ i, MeasurableSpace (type i), vars : ‚ąÄ i, RegimedVar P (type i) }` | Heterogeneous finite-length bundle of regimed PO variables over a fixed PO system | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.instMeasurableSpaceType` | `MeasurableSpace (B.type i)` | Per-component measurable-space instance derived from the `inst` field (for typeclass inference) | **Instance** |
| `POCFBundle.jointValue B` | `P.ő© ‚Üí (‚ąÄ i : Fin B.n, B.type i)` | Joint evaluator returning the dependent tuple of cf values | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.measurable_jointValue` | `Measurable B.jointValue` | Measurability of the joint evaluator (via `measurable_pi_lambda`) | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.nil P` | `POCFBundle P` | Empty bundle (length 0) | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.cons a B` | `POCFBundle P` | Prepend a `RegimedVar` to a bundle (length `B.n + 1`); used to build the `(D(1), D(0), Y(1), Y(0))` bundle in LATE | **Defined** |
| `POSystem.IndepCF` | `RegimedVar P őĪ ‚Üí POCFBundle P ‚Üí Measure P.ő© ‚Üí Prop` | `IndepFun a.value B.jointValue őľ` ‚ÄĒ uniform shape for "regimed variable ‚üā bundle" | **Defined** |
| `POSystem.CondIndepCF` | `RegimedVar P őĪ ‚Üí POCFBundle P ‚Üí RegimedVar P ő≥ ‚Üí Measure P.ő© ‚Üí Prop` | Conditional independence of `a` from `B` given ŌÉ(`c.value`); requires `StandardBorelSpace P.ő©` | **Defined** |
| `IndepCF.toIndepFun` / `IndepCF.ofIndepFun` | `P.IndepCF a B őľ ‚ÜĒ IndepFun a.value B.jointValue őľ` | Trivial bridges between `IndepCF` and the underlying `IndepFun` | **Proved** (`id`) |
| `CondIndepCF.toCondIndepFun` | `P.CondIndepCF a B c őľ ‚Üí CondIndepFun (comap c.value _) _ a.value B.jointValue őľ` | Unfolding bridge to Mathlib's `CondIndepFun` with the comap ŌÉ-algebra | **Proved** (`id`) |
| `POSystem.condIndepCF_congr_cond` | `comap c.value = comap c'.value ‚Üí P.CondIndepCF a B c őľ ‚Üí P.CondIndepCF a B c' őľ` | Transports conditional independence across conditioning variables that generate the same ŌÉ-algebra | **Proved** |
| `IndepCF.project` | `P.IndepCF a B őľ ‚Üí Measurable Ōą ‚Üí IndepFun a.value (Ōą ‚ąė B.jointValue) őľ` | Push `IndepCF` through an arbitrary measurable projection `Ōą : (‚ąÄ i, B.type i) ‚Üí ő≤` ‚ÄĒ replaces open-coded `.comp measurable_id` dances in ATE/LATE | **Proved** |
| `IndepCF.component` | `P.IndepCF a B őľ ‚Üí (i : Fin B.n) ‚Üí IndepFun a.value (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ B.jointValue ŌČ i) őľ` | Specialisation of `project` to single-coordinate extraction (e.g. pick out `Y(d)` from a joint cf bundle) | **Proved** |
| `CondIndepCF.project` | `P.CondIndepCF a B c őľ ‚Üí Measurable Ōą ‚Üí CondIndepFun (comap c.value _) _ a.value (Ōą ‚ąė B.jointValue) őľ` | Analogue of `IndepCF.project` at the conditional-independence level | **Proved** |
| `CondIndepCF.component` | `P.CondIndepCF a B c őľ ‚Üí (i : Fin B.n) ‚Üí CondIndepFun (comap c.value _) _ a.value (fun ŌČ ‚Ü¶ B.jointValue ŌČ i) őľ` | Analogue of `IndepCF.component` at the conditional-independence level | **Proved** |

### `PO/Conditioning/Bundle.lean` ‚ÄĒ Bundle-conditioned conditional expectation

Lifts the single-`POVar` conditioning machinery in `CondExpTooling.lean` to a
`POCFBundle`, i.e. conditioning on the *joint* cf-value map of a tuple of
regimed variables.  The conditioning ŌÉ-algebra `B.sigma` is
`MeasurableSpace.comap B.jointValue inferInstance`.  Used by the multi-stage
DTR / sequential-backdoor identification arguments (`PO/ID/Exact/DTR/`)
where the conditioning ŌÉ-algebra is generated by a *bundle* of covariates rather
than a single one; mirrors all the lemmas of `POVar.condExpGiven` /
`POVar.condExpRatio` at the bundle level.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POCFBundle.sigma B` | `MeasurableSpace P.ő©` | `MeasurableSpace.comap B.jointValue inferInstance` ‚ÄĒ the ŌÉ-algebra generated by the bundle's joint cf-value map | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.sigma_le` | `B.sigma ‚Č§ inferInstance` | `B.sigma` is a sub-ŌÉ-algebra of the ambient ŌÉ-algebra on `P.ő©` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven B g őľ` | `POCFBundle P ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Measure P.ő© ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | `őľ[g \| B.sigma]` ‚ÄĒ Mathlib `condExp` on the bundle's comap ŌÉ-algebra (bundle analogue of `POVar.condExpGiven`) | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.stronglyMeasurable_condExpGiven_comap` | `StronglyMeasurable[B.sigma] (B.condExpGiven g őľ)` | `condExpGiven g` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the sub-ŌÉ-algebra `B.sigma` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.stronglyMeasurable_condExpGiven` | `StronglyMeasurable (B.condExpGiven g őľ)` | `condExpGiven g` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the ambient ŌÉ-algebra | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.integrable_condExpGiven` | `Integrable (B.condExpGiven g őľ) őľ` | `condExpGiven g` is integrable | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_tower_of_le` | `m ‚Č§ B.sigma ‚Üí [SigmaFinite (őľ.trim _)] ‚Üí őľ[B.condExpGiven g őľ \| m] =ŠĶź[őľ] őľ[g \| m]` | Tower property against an arbitrary smaller sub-ŌÉ-algebra `m ‚Č§ B.sigma`; bundle analogue of `POVar.condExpGiven_tower_of_le` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left` | `StronglyMeasurable[B.sigma] f ‚Üí Integrable (f*g) őľ ‚Üí Integrable g őľ ‚Üí B.condExpGiven (f*g) őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] f * B.condExpGiven g őľ` | Pull-out-left: a `B.sigma`-measurable factor pulls outside `őľ[¬∑\|B.sigma]` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_right` | Symmetric right-pull-out | Pull-out-right form of the same identity | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_indicator_mul` | `MeasurableSet[B.sigma] s ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ ‚Üí B.condExpGiven (1_s * g) őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] 1_s * B.condExpGiven g őľ` | Specialisation of pull-out-left to indicators of `B.sigma`-measurable sets | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_congr_ae` | `f =ŠĶź[őľ] g ‚Üí B.condExpGiven f őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] B.condExpGiven g őľ` | a.e. congruence: equal-a.e. integrands give equal-a.e. bundle conditional expectations | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpRatio B g h őľ` | `POCFBundle P ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Measure P.ő© ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Pointwise ratio `B.condExpGiven g őľ / B.condExpGiven h őľ` ‚ÄĒ bundle analogue of `POVar.condExpRatio`, used to package the inner/outer adjusted regressions in DTR | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.measurable_condExpRatio` | `Measurable (B.condExpRatio g h őľ)` | Pointwise-division measurability of `condExpRatio` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.stronglyMeasurable_condExpRatio` | `StronglyMeasurable (B.condExpRatio g h őľ)` | Strong measurability of `condExpRatio` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpRatio_eq_of_mul` | `B.condExpGiven g őľ =ŠĶź B.condExpGiven h őľ * target ‚Üí (‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, B.condExpGiven h őľ ŌČ ‚Č† 0) ‚Üí B.condExpRatio g h őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] target` | Product‚Üíratio characterisation; the exact shape consumed by `cdtr_backdoor` | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.CondIndepCFBundle` | `RegimedVar P őĪ ‚Üí POCFBundle P ‚Üí POCFBundle P ‚Üí Measure P.ő© ‚Üí Prop` | Bundle-conditioned conditional independence: `CondIndepFun C.sigma C.sigma_le a.value B.jointValue őľ` ‚ÄĒ bundle analogue of `POSystem.CondIndepCF`; requires `StandardBorelSpace P.ő©` | **Defined** |
| `CondIndepCFBundle.toCondIndepFun` | `P.CondIndepCFBundle a B C őľ ‚Üí CondIndepFun C.sigma C.sigma_le a.value B.jointValue őľ` | Trivial unfolding bridge to Mathlib's `CondIndepFun` against `C.sigma` | **Proved** (`id`) |
| `CondIndepCFBundle.project` | `P.CondIndepCFBundle a B C őľ ‚Üí Measurable Ōą ‚Üí CondIndepFun C.sigma C.sigma_le a.value (Ōą ‚ąė B.jointValue) őľ` | Push `CondIndepCFBundle` through an arbitrary measurable projection of the bundle joint value ‚ÄĒ analogue of `CondIndepCF.project` for bundle-conditioned CI | **Proved** |

### `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExp.lean` ‚ÄĒ Event-level conditional expectation and drop-of-conditioning

Thin wrapper around `‚ąę_A g ‚ąāőľ / (őľ A).toReal` paired with a **drop-of-conditioning**
lemma: when the event-defining variable is `IndepCF` of a bundle `B`,
conditioning on a `value = x` event has no effect on `‚ąę h ‚ąė B.jointValue`.
Both multiplied and quotient forms are provided.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `eventCondExp őľ A g` | `‚ĄĚ`, defined as `(‚ąę ŌČ in A, g ŌČ ‚ąāőľ) / (őľ A).toReal` | Event-level conditional expectation, packaging the hand-rolled `condExpDZ` / `condExpYZ` idiom as a standalone operator | **Defined** |
| `eventCondExp_mul_measure_toReal` | `[IsFiniteMeasure őľ]`, `A : Set ő©`, `f : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` ‚Üí `eventCondExp őľ A f * (őľ A).toReal = ‚ąę ŌČ in A, f ŌČ ‚ąāőľ` | Quotient-to-set-integral conversion (including the zero-measure case, where both sides collapse to `0`). Domain-agnostic; consumed by the Fintype total law below and by the Manski MTR/MTS set-integral manipulations | **Proved** |
| `integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp` | `[Fintype őĻ]`, `[IsFiniteMeasure őľ]`, `A : őĻ ‚Üí Set ő©` measurable / pairwise disjoint / covering `univ`, `f` integrable ‚Üí `‚ąę f = ‚ąĎ i, (őľ (A i)).toReal * eventCondExp őľ (A i) f` | **Finite-partition total law.** Instantiates at `őĻ := Bool` for `Manski/{MTR,MTS}` binary decompositions and at `őĻ := őĪ` (discrete IV value space) for the integrated MIV bounds. Domain-agnostic ‚ÄĒ an upstream-ready identity | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_eq_sum_condProb_mul_eventCondExp` | `[Fintype őĻ]`, `[IsFiniteMeasure őľ]`, `A` measurable, `C : őĻ ‚Üí Set ő©` measurable / pairwise disjoint / covering `univ`, `f` integrable ‚Üí `eventCondExp őľ A f = ‚ąĎ i, (őľ (A ‚ą© C i)).toReal / (őľ A).toReal * eventCondExp őľ (A ‚ą© C i) f` | **Conditional finite-partition total law (law of iterated expectations).** `E[f‚ą£A] = ‚ąĎ i P(C i‚ą£A)¬∑E[f‚ą£A ‚ą© C i]`. The measure-theoretic core of the finite‚Üípopulation bridge for cell-indexed estimands: a finite covariate-weighted average of within-cell means equals the population conditional expectation `E[f‚ą£G=g]` when the weights are the conditional cell probabilities. Domain-agnostic | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_congr_ae` | `f =ŠĶź[őľ] g ‚Üí eventCondExp őľ A f = eventCondExp őľ A g` | a.e.-equal integrands give equal `eventCondExp`; shared congruence used across DID / Manski consistency rewrites | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_congr_on` | `MeasurableSet A ‚Üí (‚ąÄ ŌČ ‚ąą A, f ŌČ = g ŌČ) ‚Üí eventCondExp őľ A f = eventCondExp őľ A g` | Equal-on-`A` integrands give equal `eventCondExp`; specialisation of the a.e. version to a pointwise identity on a measurable set | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_mono_ae` | `IntegrableOn f A őľ ‚Üí IntegrableOn g A őľ ‚Üí f ‚Č§ŠĶź[őľ] g ‚Üí eventCondExp őľ A f ‚Č§ eventCondExp őľ A g` | Monotonicity of `eventCondExp` under an a.e. inequality of integrable functions; workhorse of the Manski stratum-sandwich bounds | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_add` | `IntegrableOn g‚āĀ A őľ`, `IntegrableOn g‚āā A őľ` ‚Üí `eventCondExp őľ A (g‚āĀ + g‚āā) = eventCondExp őľ A g‚āĀ + eventCondExp őľ A g‚āā` | Additivity | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_sub` | Analogous subtraction identity | Subtraction | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_smul` | `eventCondExp őľ A (c ‚ÄĘ g) = c * eventCondExp őľ A g` | Scalar linearity | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_of_ae_eq_IndepFun` | `IndepFun z B őľ`, measurable `z`, `B`, `h`, `factualF =ŠĶź[őľ.restrict (z ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' {x})] h ‚ąė B`, `0 < (őľ (z ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' {x})).toReal` ‚Üí `eventCondExp őľ (z ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' {x}) factualF = ‚ąę h (B ŌČ) ‚ąāőľ` | Plain-`IndepFun` consistency-on-cell + drop-of-conditioning helper, reusable when the event variable is not packaged as a `POVar`; used by Angrist-Imbens factual Wald reductions | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.integral_restrict_value_eq_mul_of_IndepCF` | `P.IndepCF rv B őľ`, `Measurable h` ‚Üí `‚ąę ŌČ in rv.value ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' {x}, h (B.jointValue ŌČ) ‚ąāőľ = (őľ (rv.value ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' {x})).toReal * ‚ąę ŌČ, h (B.jointValue ŌČ) ‚ąāőľ` | Drop-of-conditioning (multiplied form): the core identity, direct application of `IndepFun.integral_restrict_preimage_eq_mul` | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.integral_event_eq_mul_of_IndepCF` | Same identity with `A = a.event x` for factual `POVar a` | Specialisation to the factual event `{a = x}` | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.eventCondExp_eq_integral_of_IndepCF` | Under `IndepCF` and `őľ A ‚ąČ {0, ‚ä§}`, `eventCondExp őľ (rv.value ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' {x}) (h ‚ąė B.jointValue) = ‚ąę h ‚ąė B.jointValue ‚ąāőľ` | Quotient form of drop-of-conditioning | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.eventCondExp_event_eq_integral_of_IndepCF` | Quotient form specialised to `a.event x` | Specialisation used directly in `late_wald`-style derivations | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF` | `P.IndepCF (ofFactual a) B őľ`, `Measurable h`, `(‚ąÄ ŌČ ‚ąą a.event x, factualF ŌČ = h (B.jointValue ŌČ))`, `őľ (a.event x) ‚ąČ {0, ‚ä§}` ‚Üí `eventCondExp őľ (a.event x) factualF = ‚ąę h (B.jointValue ŌČ) ‚ąāőľ` | Workhorse combo: consistency-on-event rewrite + drop-of-conditioning in one call; collapses the repeated `hCE` blocks of `first_stage_identity` / `reduced_form_identity` | **Proved** |

### `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExpBundle.lean` ‚ÄĒ bundle analogue of `eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF`

Lifts the single-period `EventCondExp` workhorse to the bundle-conditional setting (`P.CondIndepCFBundle a B C`) used by the Dynamic LATE bridges.  Per-stage workhorse: under bundle-conditional independence + consistency-on-event, the bundle conditional expectation of `factualF ¬∑ 1_{a=x}` factors into the conditional expectation of `h ‚ąė B.jointValue` times the conditional expectation of `1_{a=x}`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_mul_of_consistency_CondIndepCFBundle` | `P.CondIndepCFBundle (ofFactual a) B C őľ`, `Measurable h`, `(factualF ¬∑ 1_{a=x}) =ŠĶź (h ‚ąė B.jointValue) ¬∑ 1_{a=x}` ‚Üí `C.condExpGiven (factualF ¬∑ 1_{a=x}) =ŠĶź C.condExpGiven (h ‚ąė B.jointValue) ¬∑ C.condExpGiven (1_{a=x})` | Product form of the bundle workhorse.  Proof reduces to `Causalean.condExp_mul_of_condIndep` with `f := a.factual`, `g := B.jointValue`, `u y := ({x} : Set őĪ).indicator 1`, `v := h`, exploiting `(RegimedVar.ofFactual a).value = a.factual` (rfl). | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpRatio_of_consistency_CondIndepCFBundle` | Same hypotheses + `‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, C.condExpGiven (1_{a=x}) ŌČ ‚Č† 0` ‚Üí `C.condExpRatio (factualF ¬∑ 1_{a=x}) (1_{a=x}) =ŠĶź C.condExpGiven (h ‚ąė B.jointValue)` | Ratio form, derived from the product form via `condExpRatio_eq_of_mul`. | **Proved** |

### `PO/Conditioning/CondExpTooling.lean` ‚ÄĒ ŌÉ-algebra-based conditional expectation

Thin wrapper around `MeasureTheory.condExp` keyed by the factual map of a
`POVar`.  Continuous-`X` companion to the event-based `eventCondExp` in
`EventCondExp.lean`; used by the backdoor ATE argument under the PO
framework, where the conditioning covariate may be continuous and the
integrand has the shape `Y(d)¬∑1{D=d}` or `1{D=d}`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POVar.condExpGiven c g őľ` | `POVar P ő≥ ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Measure P.ő© ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | `őľ[g \| ŌÉ(c.factual)]` ‚ÄĒ Mathlib `condExp` on the comap ŌÉ-algebra generated by `c.factual` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.comap_factual_le` | `MeasurableSpace.comap c.factual inferInstance ‚Č§ mő©` | The conditioning ŌÉ-algebra `ŌÉ(c.factual)` is a sub-ŌÉ-algebra of the ambient one on `P.ő©` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_add` | `Integrable f őľ ‚Üí Integrable g őľ ‚Üí c.condExpGiven (f+g) őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] c.condExpGiven f őľ + c.condExpGiven g őľ` | Additivity of `condExpGiven` (a.e.) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_sub` | Analogous subtraction identity | Subtraction linearity of `condExpGiven` (a.e.) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_smul` | `c.condExpGiven (k ‚ÄĘ g) őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] k ‚ÄĘ c.condExpGiven g őľ` | Scalar homogeneity of `condExpGiven` (a.e.) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.stronglyMeasurable_condExpGiven_comap` | `StronglyMeasurable[ŌÉ(c.factual)] (c.condExpGiven g őľ)` | `condExpGiven g` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the sub-ŌÉ-algebra `ŌÉ(c.factual)` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.stronglyMeasurable_condExpGiven` | `StronglyMeasurable (c.condExpGiven g őľ)` | `condExpGiven g` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the ambient ŌÉ-algebra | **Proved** |
| `POVar.integrable_condExpGiven` | `Integrable (c.condExpGiven g őľ) őľ` | `condExpGiven g` is integrable | **Proved** |
| `POVar.integrable_mul_indicator` | `Integrable f P.őľ ‚Üí Measurable f ‚Üí Integrable (fun ŌČ => f ŌČ * a.indicator x ŌČ) P.őľ` | Multiplication by a factual `{0,1}`-valued `POVar` indicator preserves integrability; removes repeated bounded-indicator proofs in ATE, DTR, and Manski arguments | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left` | `StronglyMeasurable[ŌÉ(c.factual)] f ‚Üí Integrable (f*g) őľ ‚Üí Integrable g őľ ‚Üí c.condExpGiven (f*g) őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] f * c.condExpGiven g őľ` | Pull-out-left: a `ŌÉ(c.factual)`-measurable factor pulls outside `őľ[¬∑\|ŌÉ(c.factual)]` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_right` | Symmetric right-pull-out | Pull-out-right form of the same identity | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_indicator_mul` | `MeasurableSet[ŌÉ(c.factual)] s ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ ‚Üí c.condExpGiven (1_s * g) őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] 1_s * c.condExpGiven g őľ` | Specialisation of pull-out-left to indicators of `ŌÉ(c.factual)`-measurable sets | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_tower_of_le` | `m ‚Č§ ŌÉ(c.factual) ‚Üí [SigmaFinite (őľ.trim ‚Ä¶)] ‚Üí őľ[c.condExpGiven g őľ \| m] =ŠĶź[őľ] őľ[g \| m]` | Tower property against an arbitrary smaller sub-ŌÉ-algebra `m ‚Č§ ŌÉ(c.factual)` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_tower` | Between two POVars with `ŌÉ(c‚āĀ.factual) ‚Č§ ŌÉ(c‚āā.factual)`: `c‚āĀ.condExpGiven (c‚āā.condExpGiven g őľ) őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] c‚āĀ.condExpGiven g őľ` | Two-POVar tower property | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpRatio c g h őľ` | `POVar P ő≥ ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Measure P.ő© ‚Üí (P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Pointwise ratio `c.condExpGiven g őľ / c.condExpGiven h őľ` ‚ÄĒ the adjusted-regression functional used in backdoor ATE | **Defined** |
| `POVar.measurable_condExpRatio` | `Measurable (c.condExpRatio g h őľ)` | Pointwise-division measurability of `condExpRatio` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.stronglyMeasurable_condExpRatio` | `StronglyMeasurable (c.condExpRatio g h őľ)` | Strong measurability of `condExpRatio` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpRatio_eq_of_mul` | `c.condExpGiven g őľ =ŠĶź c.condExpGiven h őľ * target ‚Üí (‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, c.condExpGiven h őľ ŌČ ‚Č† 0) ‚Üí c.condExpRatio g h őľ =ŠĶź[őľ] target` | Product‚Üíratio characterisation; the exact shape consumed by `cate_backdoor` Step 4 | **Proved** |
| `POVar.integral_sub_eq_integral_sub_of_condExpGiven_ae_eq` | `Integrable f őľ`, `Integrable g őľ`, `c.condExpGiven f őľ =ŠĶź f'`, `c.condExpGiven g őľ =ŠĶź g'` ‚Üí `‚ąę (f - g) ‚ąāőľ = ‚ąę (f' - g') ‚ąāőľ` | CATE-to-ATE integrator: lifts an a.e. identity of conditional expectations to an identity of unconditional integrals of differences; closes `ate_backdoor` in one line | **Proved** |

---

### `PO/Analysis/Quantile.lean` ‚ÄĒ Distribution, cdf, and quantile of a real potential outcome

Distributional reading of a real-valued potential outcome: for a real `POVar a`
and regime `r`, the counterfactual `a.cf r : P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` has a law, cdf, and
quantile. Gives meaning to "the `ŌĄ`-quantile of `Y(d)`"; input to the QTE
(`PO/ID/Exact/QTE/QuantileEffect.lean`). Builds on `Stat/Quantile/Quantile.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POVar.cfLaw a r őľ` | `Measure ‚ĄĚ` | Law of the potential outcome `a(r)`: `őľ.map (a.cf r)`. | **Defined** |
| `POVar.instIsProbabilityMeasureCfLaw` | instance | `cfLaw` is a probability measure when `őľ` is. | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cfCDF a r őľ` | `StieltjesFunction ‚ĄĚ` | Distributional potential outcome `F_{a(r)} = cdf (a.cfLaw r őľ)`. | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cfQuantile a r őľ ŌĄ` | `‚ĄĚ` | `ŌĄ`-quantile of `a(r)`: `Stat.quantile (a.cfLaw r őľ) ŌĄ`. | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cfCDF_eq_measureReal` | `a.cfCDF r őľ y = (a.cfLaw r őľ).real (Iic y)` | The cdf at `y` is `P(a(r) ‚Č§ y)`. | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cfUnderLaw a w y őľ` | `Measure ‚ĄĚ` | Law of `a` under single intervention `{w ‚Üź y}` (the `Y(d)` shape). | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cfUnderQuantile a w y őľ ŌĄ` | `‚ĄĚ` | `ŌĄ`-quantile of `a` under `{w ‚Üź y}` ‚ÄĒ the `ŌĄ`-quantile of `Y(d)`. | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cfUnderQuantile_eq` | `= Stat.quantile (a.cfUnderLaw w y őľ) ŌĄ` | Unfolds `cfUnderQuantile` to the law-quantile. | `rfl` |

## 9. `SCM/ID/Identifiable.lean` ‚ÄĒ Identifiability

Namespace: `Causalean.SCM.ID`.

### Identifiability definitions

Identifiability is phrased in terms of **measure-theoretic observational
equivalence** against the SCM observational kernel: `obsEquiv M‚āĀ M‚āā` is
`HEq` of `Causalean.SCM.obsKernel M‚āĀ` and `Causalean.SCM.obsKernel M‚āā` (the `HEq`
is necessary because `obsKernel` has dependent domain/codomain via
`FixedValues M` and `ObservedValues M`).

| Definition | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `CausalQuery` | `SCM N ő© ‚Üí őĪ` | Functional of a gSCM (ATE, LATE, interventional law, etc.) |
| `obsEquiv` | `SCM N ő© ‚Üí SCM N ő© ‚Üí Prop` | `HEq (SCM.obsKernel M‚āĀ) (SCM.obsKernel M‚āā)` |
| `Identifiable` | `SWIGGraph N ‚Üí CausalQuery N ő© őĪ ‚Üí Prop` | Same SWIG graph (`M.toSWIGGraph = G`, pinning observed/latent/fixed + edges) + `obsEquiv` ‚üĻ same query value. Faithful to `def:scm-identifiability` (model class shares a SWIG graph, not merely a DAG) |
| `NonIdentifiable` | `SWIGGraph N ‚Üí CausalQuery N ő© őĪ ‚Üí Prop` | Negation of `Identifiable` |
| `IdentifiableUnder` | `SWIGGraph N ‚Üí Af ‚Üí As ‚Üí CausalQuery N ő© őĪ ‚Üí Prop` | Identifiable given functional (`Af`) and structural (`As`) assumption predicates over `SCM N ő©`, within the model class sharing SWIG graph `G` |

### Theorems

| Theorem | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `nonIdentifiable_iff` | Non-identifiability iff there exist two models with the same SWIG graph and `obsEquiv` but different query values | Equivalence characterization of non-identifiability |
| `identifiable_eq_identifiableUnder_true` | Identifiability without assumptions is the special case `Af = As = fun _ => True` | Links unconditional identifiability to `IdentifiableUnder` with trivial assumptions |
| `identifiableUnder_mono` | Stronger assumptions `(Af‚āā, As‚āā)` that imply `(Af‚āĀ, As‚āĀ)` preserve identifiability | Monotonicity: identifiability under weaker assumptions follows from identifiability under stronger ones |

---

## 9a. `SCM/ID/Overlap.lean` ‚ÄĒ Rule 2 / backdoor overlap predicate

The kernel-level absolute-continuity overlap predicate consumed by the
kernel-native do-calculus Rule 2 and the backdoor / frontdoor identification
theorems.  (The quantitative small-value/weak-overlap rate analysis ‚ÄĒ polynomial
lower-tail inverse moments ‚ÄĒ is developed separately under `Stat/PolynomialTail/`.)

| Definition | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Rule2JointOverlap M' Z hZ_obs hZ_fixed W hZrW s'` | `Prop` | **Canonical Rule 2 overlap.** Absolute continuity `((M'.fixSet Z).obsKernel s').map ŌÄ_{Zr‚ą™W} ‚Č™ (M'.obsKernel ‚ąė fixSetProj s').map ŌÄ_{Zr‚ą™W}` ‚ÄĒ a kernel-level AC predicate with no pointwise singleton positivity requirement. Holds trivially in the discrete case; holds in the continuous case whenever the SCM's structural functions are measurable and latents match. Hypothesis of `obsCondKernel_fixSet_eq_ae_witness` (canonical posterior witness-kernel Rule 2) and through it of `do_rule2_kernel` and backdoor / frontdoor identification. | Complete |

Reference: Basic Concepts.tex, Definition 2.12 (Overlap assumption).

---

## 9b. Structural Assumption Ownership

Causalean no longer carries a standalone IV/DID/RDD/proxy assumption catalogue.
Structural assumptions are owned by the theorem module that consumes them
(`PO/ID/Exact/*`, `PO/ID/Partial/*`, or downstream CausalSmith artifacts), while
`SCM/ID/Identifiable.lean` still provides generic `Prop`-valued assumption slots
for abstract identifiability statements.

---

## 9c. `SCM/ID/GraphicalThms/` ‚ÄĒ Graphical Identification Theorems

Formalizes Basic Concepts.tex ¬ß4.1.  Several live files:

* `InducedSubgraph.lean` ‚ÄĒ graph-only utilities (ancestral closure,
  proper-descendant / non-descendant sets in `G_R`).
* `CComponentFactor.lean` ‚ÄĒ c-factor definition (`qFactor`),
  c-component parent helpers, and the per-node chain-rule factorization
  theorem `M.obsKernel s = M.qFactorProduct s` (Tian full-history
  convention).
* `QFactorIdentity.lean` ‚ÄĒ Tian's Q-factor identity (Prop 2.19),
  intervention-target simplification (`fact4`), and the district-id
  corollary.  The Q-factor identity is currently formalized as the
  real marginal fixing identity obtained from Rule 3 and
  `induce_marginal_compat`; the final conditional-`qFactor` reindexing
  remains future work.

The old kernel-era files (`Fixable.lean`, `FixingStep.lean`,
`CFactor.lean`) are **archived** under
`archive/SCM/ID/GraphicalThms/`.  The Shpitser‚ÄďPearl c-component
recursion (the chosen design path for the Session-5 ID algorithm) does
not consume the fixing-step machinery, so it is no longer in the build.

### `InducedSubgraph.lean`

| Definition | Description |
|---|---|
| `SWIGGraph.isAncestrallyClosed` | Every observed parent of `v ‚ąą G.observed` is itself in `G.observed`; `Decidable` |
| `InducedFrom G R` | `G.induce R` ‚ÄĒ the `G_R` notation from the tex |
| `SWIGGraph.properDescIn G R v‚āÄ` | `D := (G.induce R).dag.descendants v‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ proper descendants of `v‚āÄ` in `G_R`; `DAG.descendants` is already irreflexive |
| `SWIGGraph.nonDescIn G R v‚āÄ` | `T := (R.erase v‚āÄ) \ (G.induce R).dag.descendants v‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ non-descendants of `v‚āÄ` in `G_R`, matching the tex convention that `De(v‚āÄ)` includes `v‚āÄ` |
| `v‚āÄ_not_mem_properDescIn` | `v‚āÄ ‚ąČ G.properDescIn R v‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ irreflexivity |
| `v‚āÄ_not_mem_nonDescIn` | `v‚āÄ ‚ąČ G.nonDescIn R v‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ by the `erase` in the definition |
| `properDescIn_subset_erase` | `G.properDescIn R v‚āÄ ‚äÜ R.erase v‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ **sorry** (needs `descendants_induce_subset`) |
| `nonDescIn_subset_erase` | `G.nonDescIn R v‚āÄ ‚äÜ R.erase v‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ proven via `Finset.sdiff_subset` |
| `nonDescIn_subset` | `G.nonDescIn R v‚āÄ ‚äÜ R` |
| `properDescIn_disjoint_nonDescIn` | `Disjoint (properDescIn R v‚āÄ) (nonDescIn R v‚āÄ)` |
| `properDescIn_union_nonDescIn_eq_erase` | `D ‚ą™ T = R.erase v‚āÄ` (for `v‚āÄ ‚ąą R`) ‚ÄĒ **sorry** (needs the subset helper) |

### `CComponentFactor.lean` ‚ÄĒ c-component factorization (Tian, 2002)

Formalizes `thm:scm-c-factor` (Basic Concepts.tex:662‚Äď672) against
`obsKernel`.  The Pa‚Āļ semantics is the standard Tian (2002) convention:
*topological predecessors*, i.e. observed nodes preceding `v` in a fixed
topological order ‚ÄĒ not the direct-parent set.

| Definition / Statement | Description |
|---|---|
| `SWIGGraph.observedPredecessors v` | `Pa‚Āļ_G(v) := {w ‚ąą G.observed | topoOrder w < topoOrder v}` |
| `SWIGGraph.qFactorParents C` | `(‚čÉ_{v ‚ąą C} Pa‚Āļ_G(v)) \ C` ‚ÄĒ conditioning set of `Q[C]` |
| `observedPredecessors_subset_observed` / `qFactorParents_subset_observed` | sanity lemmas (proven) |
| `SCM.qFactor M C hC s` | Tian's c-component factor `Q[C]` as a slice of `obsCondKernel M C (qFactorParents C)` at `s` (via `Kernel.comap`); requires `StandardBorelSpace`, `Nonempty`, `IsFiniteMeasure`, `CountableOrCountablyGenerated` instances |
| `SCM.c_component_factorization` | `M.obsKernel s = M.qFactorProduct s`; per-node chain-rule factorization over observed nodes, now sorry-free via `obsKernel_eq_qFactorProduct`; c-component grouping is deferred to a later theorem |

### `QFactorIdentity.lean`

Hypothesis-quantified frames for Tian's identity, fact4, and the
district-id corollary.  The statements are non-vacuous: Tian's identity is the
conditional q-factor equality (a.e. under the induced parent marginal), the
marginal fixing identity is retained as a proved helper, and fact4 is an
`SCM.Equiv` statement.

| Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `QFactorMarginalFixingConclusion M R T Wn` | Under ancestral closure, `T ‚äÜ (M.induce R).observed`, `Wn.image .random = R \ T`, and the Rule-3 non-descendant condition on `T`, the `T`-marginal of `(M.fixSet Wn).obsKernel` equals the `T`-marginal of `(M.induce R).obsKernel` at the projected fixed slice | **Proved** marginal fixing identity |
| `q_factor_marginal_fixing` | Proves the marginal fixing form by composing `condDistrib_intervention_ancestral_eq` with `induce_marginal_compat` and projection composition | **Proved** |
| `QFactorIdentityConclusion M R T Wn` | Conditional Tian identity: the induced `qFactor (M.induce R) T` equals a.e. the `T | qFactorParents T` conditional extracted from `(M.fixSet Wn).obsKernel`, with the non-descendant condition strengthened to `T ‚ą™ qFactorParents T` for the needed joint identity | Real conditional statement |
| `q_factor_identity` | Proves the conditional Q-factor identity by first deriving the joint law identity on `T ‚ą™ qFactorParents T`, reindexing it to the `(qFactorParents T, T)` pair law, then applying `Kernel.condKernel` uniqueness/disintegration under the common parent marginal | **Proved** |
| `InterventionTargetSimpConclusion M Dn Yn T` | `SCM.Equiv ((M.fixSet Dn).fixSet Yn) (M.fixSet (Dn ‚ą™ Yn))` under observed/fixed/disjointness well-formedness hypotheses | Real `SCM.Equiv` statement |
| `intervention_target_simp` | Proves `fact4` by Finset induction over `Yn`, using the singleton-insert composition helper | Proven modulo `swigInterventionSet_insert_equiv` |
| `SCM.swigInterventionSet_insert_equiv` | Structural equivalence between intervening on `X` then fresh `{y}` and intervening on `insert y X` in one shot | **Proved** |
| `DistrictIdConclusion M T Wn` | Specialization of `QFactorIdentityConclusion` at `R := M.observed` | `:= QFactorIdentityConclusion M M.observed T Wn` |
| `district_id` | Specializes `q_factor_identity` at `R := M.observed` | Direct specialization of the conditional Q-factor identity; **proved** |

### `Density/ReferenceMeasure.lean` ‚ÄĒ dominated-model density foundation

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. Foundation for the **density-assisted** c-component
factorization: Tian's assembly `P(v) = ‚ąŹ_C Q[C]` is a commutative regrouping of
scalar density factors with no kernel-composition analogue, so the
`doKernelY` identification (obligation 2) is routed through the joint density.
Unifies discrete (counting reference) and continuous (Lebesgue reference) models.

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ReferenceMeasures ő©` | structure: `őľ : ‚ąÄ v, Measure (swigő© ő© v)` + per-node `SigmaFinite` | A ŌÉ-finite reference measure per SWIG-node value space (counting for discrete, Lebesgue for continuous) |
| `ReferenceFaithful ref` | `‚ąÄ v x, ref.őľ v {x} ‚Č† 0` | Full-support condition for finite references; counting reference measures satisfy it |
| `jointRef ref I` | `Measure (ValuesOn I (swigő© ő©))` | Finite product `Measure.pi` of the per-node references over a node set `I` |
| `DominatedObs M ref` | `‚ąÄ s, M.obsKernel s ‚Č™ jointRef ref M.observed` | The gSCM's observational law is absolutely continuous w.r.t. the joint reference (admits a density) |
| `obsDensity M ref s` | `ValuesOn M.observed (swigő© ő©) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě` | Joint observational density `(obsKernel s).rnDeriv (jointRef ‚Ä¶)` |
| `withDensity_obsDensity_eq` | `DominatedObs M ref ‚Üí (jointRef ‚Ä¶).withDensity (obsDensity ‚Ä¶) = obsKernel s` | Density determines the law (Proved) |
| `obsKernel_eq_of_obsDensity_ae_eq` | a.e.-equal `obsDensity` slices ‚üĻ equal `obsKernel` slices (single model) | Density injectivity used downstream for the soundness transport (Proved) |

### `Density/FiniteReference.lean` ‚ÄĒ finite discrete reference infrastructure

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. Instances and helpers for the finite/discrete density
path: when every base-node value space is finite and has measurable singletons,
the SWIG value spaces and finite product references inherit the corresponding
finite/measurable structure needed by the finite-reference RN chain rule.

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `instFintypeSwigő©` / `instMeasurableSingletonClassSwigő©` | `[‚ąÄ n, Fintype (ő© n)]` / `[‚ąÄ n, MeasurableSingletonClass (ő© n)]` ‚üĻ the same instances for `swigő© ő© sn` | Transfers finite and singleton-measurable structure from base nodes to random/fixed SWIG nodes |
| `isFiniteMeasure_of_finite_measurableSingleton` | a ŌÉ-finite measure on a finite measurable-singleton type is finite | Generic finite-space measure helper, proved by covering the space with finitely many measurable singletons |
| `instIsFiniteMeasure_refMu` / `instIsFiniteMeasure_jointRef` | finite node spaces ‚üĻ `IsFiniteMeasure (ref.őľ v)` and `IsFiniteMeasure (jointRef ref I)` | Supplies the finite fibre and finite product-reference instances used by discrete density proofs |
| `jointRef_singleton_ne_zero` / `absolutelyContinuous_jointRef_of_faithful` | `ReferenceFaithful ref` ‚üĻ every product singleton has nonzero `jointRef` mass, hence every measure on the finite coordinate product is `‚Č™ jointRef ref I` | Full-support infrastructure used to prove do-law ancestral marginal domination |
| `aemeasurable_fiber_rnDeriv_of_finite` | any raw fibre RN selector `(őļ p.1).rnDeriv ŌĀ p.2` is a.e.-measurable on a finite measurable-singleton product | Discharges the explicit product a.e.-measurability hypothesis of the finite-reference composition-product RN lemma |

### `Density/PiUnion.lean` ‚ÄĒ `Measure.pi` over a disjoint union

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. The reference-splitting foundation for the density chain
rule: `Measure.pi` over a disjoint union of coordinates factors as a product.

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `unionSumEquiv hDisj` | `({a // a ‚ąą A} ‚äē {b // b ‚ąą B}) ‚ČÉ {i // i ‚ąą A ‚ą™ B}` | Index equivalence for disjoint `A`, `B` |
| `measurePreserving_valuesUnionEquiv` | `MeasurePreserving (valuesUnionEquiv hDisj) (Measure.pi őľ_{A‚ą™B}) ((Measure.pi őľ_A).prod (Measure.pi őľ_B))` | **`Measure.pi` over a disjoint union = the product measure.** Fully proved, axiom-clean; routes the project's union equiv through Mathlib `sumPiEquivProdPi` + `piCongrLeft` |

### `Density/ChainRuleDensity.lean` ‚ÄĒ observational chain-rule density

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. Density analogue of the proven kernel chain rule:
the observational density is reduced to the scalar product of the
Radon--Nikodym derivatives of the one-node conditional kernels
`obsStepCondKernel`, evaluated along the observed topological order.

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `obsStepCondDensity M ref s i` | `ValuesOn M.observed (swigő© ő©) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě` | One observed-coordinate conditional density: the RN derivative of `obsStepCondKernel i.isLt` against the node reference measure, evaluated at the prefix and coordinate read from a full observed assignment |
| `qFactorDensityProduct M ref s` | `ValuesOn M.observed (swigő© ő©) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě` | Product over `Fin M.observed.card` of the per-node conditional density factors in observed topological order |
| `qFactorProduct_rnDeriv_eq_qFactorDensityProduct` | with finite measurable-singleton node spaces, `DominatedObs M ref ‚Üí (M.qFactorProduct s).rnDeriv (jointRef ref M.observed) =ŠĶź ‚Ä¶ qFactorDensityProduct ‚Ä¶` | Analytic RN-derivative chain rule. **The `DominatedObs` hypothesis is essential** ‚ÄĒ false without it (singular part ‚üĻ vanishing rnDeriv vs nonzero conditional-density product). Fully proved in the finite-reference setting |
| `obsChainKernel_rnDeriv_eq_prefixDensityProduct` | with finite measurable-singleton node spaces, general-`k` prefix induction: `(obsChainKernel k s).rnDeriv (jointRef prefix k) =ŠĶź prefixDensityProduct k` | **Proved.** Base on `ValuesOn ‚ąÖ`; successor peels `extendObsPrefix` via `MeasurableEmbedding.rnDeriv_map`, splits via `jointRef_extendObsPrefix`, derives joint product domination from base and fibre domination, and uses finite-space measurability for the raw fibre derivative |
| `obsChainKernel_absolutelyContinuous_jointRef_prefix` | `M.obsChainKernel k hk s ‚Č™ jointRef ref (M.prefixNodes k)` | **Proved (no Mathlib gap).** Chain = prefix marginal of the dominated `obsKernel`; the reference marginal is a scalar multiple of the prefix reference via the disjoint-union split (`measurePreserving_valuesUnionEquiv`) + `Measure.map_fst_prod` |
| `rnDeriv_compProd_prod_sigmaFinite` | `(őľ ‚äó‚āė őļ).rnDeriv (őĹ.prod ŌĀ) =ŠĶź fun p => f p.1 * (őļ p.1).rnDeriv ŌĀ p.2`, for finite `őľ`/`ŌĀ`/`őļ`, ŌÉ-finite `őĹ`, `őľ ‚Č™ őĹ`, **joint domination** `őľ ‚äó‚āė őļ ‚Č™ őĹ.prod ŌĀ`, product a.e.-measurability of the raw fibre derivative, and `őľ.rnDeriv őĹ =ŠĶź f` | Finite-reference composition-product RN identity in `Causalean/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/RnDerivCompProdSigmaFinite.lean`. Finiteness of `ŌĀ` makes `Kernel.const _ ŌĀ` a finite kernel, so Mathlib extracts őľ-a.e. fibre domination from joint domination. The raw `Measure.rnDeriv` fibre representative still carries an explicit product-measurability hypothesis; Mathlib's measurable representative is `Kernel.rnDeriv őļ (Kernel.const _ ŌĀ)` |
| `map_pi_valuesEquivOfEq`, `jointRef_prefix_card_map`, `singletonValues_map_ref_eq_jointRef`, `jointRef_extendObsPrefix`, `rnDeriv_compProd_same_left`, `absolutelyContinuous_of_map_measurableEmbedding`, `qFactorProduct_rnDeriv_eq_obsChainKernel_card_pullback_of_jointRef`, `obsChainKernel_card_rnDeriv_pullback_eq_qFactorDensityProduct_of_prefix` | reference reindex / singleton split / per-step `extendObsPrefix` reference split / embedding-pullback of `‚Č™` / `rnDeriv_map` peels / a.e. transport | The transport + reference-split sub-lemmas of the chain rule ‚ÄĒ **all proved, axiom-clean** |
| `obsDensity_eq_qFactorDensityProduct` | with finite measurable-singleton node spaces, `M.obsDensity ref s =ŠĶź[jointRef ref M.observed] M.qFactorDensityProduct ref s` | Dominated observational density factors as the product of one-node conditional densities; D1 is now sorry-free in the finite-reference setting |

### `Density/CComponentDensity.lean` ‚ÄĒ c-component regrouping of the density

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. The commutative regrouping with **no kernel analogue**:
the scalar density product is collected by c-component. (Composed kernels can't
be permuted to gather a non-contiguous c-component's factors; scalar densities
can.)

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cComponentDensityFactor M ref s C` | `ValuesOn M.observed (swigő© ő©) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě` | Product of the one-node conditional density factors over the observed indices whose node lies in c-component `C` (density analogue of Tian's `Q[C]`) |
| `qFactorDensityProduct_eq_prod_cComponentFactor` | `M.qFactorDensityProduct ref s x = ‚ąŹ C ‚ąą cComponentSet, cComponentDensityFactor ‚Ä¶ C x` | The chain-rule density product regroups as a product over c-components. **Fully proved, axiom-clean** (pure `Finset.prod_fiberwise` regrouping; the kernel-impossible step) |

### Archived (under `archive/SCM/ID/GraphicalThms/`)

* `Fixable.lean` ‚ÄĒ `SWIGGraph.isFixable`, `isFixableSeq` (kernel-era; no
  longer used by the chosen Shpitser‚ÄďPearl recursion).
* `FixingStep.lean` ‚ÄĒ Lemma 1 skeleton.
* `CFactor.lean` ‚ÄĒ kernel-era c-factor (replaced by `CComponentFactor.lean`).

## 10. `SCM/Examples/IV.lean` ‚ÄĒ IV DAG Example

Demonstrates the full API on the standard instrumental variable DAG:

```
    U
   ‚Üô ‚Üė
  Z ‚Üí D ‚Üí Y
```

**What it tests:**

| Module | Tests |
|---|---|
| `Graph/DAG.lean` | `parents`, `children`, `isRoot`, `isAncestor`, `isAncestor_irrefl/trans` |
| `Graph/DSep.lean` | `dSep` with various conditioning sets; collider bias; instrument independence |
| `Graph/SWIG.lean` | `SWIGGraph` construction; initial SWIG; SWIG edge tests |
| `Graph/CComponents.lean` | `directlyConfounded` on `SWIGGraph`; `cComponentOf` |
| `SCM/Model/EdgeType.lean` | `EdgeTypeAssignment`; monotonic vs nonparametric; `refines` |
| `SCM/Model/SCM.lean` | Trivial `Causalean.SCM` instance `ivSCM` with `ő©IV := fun _ => Unit`; `structFun` constant; `latentDist := Measure.dirac ()`; `iota_valueSpace` discharged via `Finset.notMem_empty`. Verifies `ivSCM.isStandard` |

---

## 10b. `SCM/Examples/BackDoor.lean` ‚ÄĒ Backdoor Adjustment Example

Demonstrates the backdoor criterion DAG:

```
    Z
   ‚Üô ‚Üė
  D ‚Üí Y
```

Observed: D (treatment), Y (outcome), Z (observed confounder). Latent roots U‚āĀ, U‚āā, U‚āÉ added to satisfy `unobs_are_roots`.

**What it tests:**

| Module | Tests |
|---|---|
| `Graph/DAG.lean` | `parents`, `isRoot`, `isAncestor` (irreflexivity via `isAncestor_topoOrder_lt`) |
| `Graph/DSep.lean` | `dSep` for the observational graph; no d-separation by {Z} (direct edge D ‚Üí Y remains open) |
| `Graph/SWIG.lean` | `SWIGGraph` construction with three latent roots; `swig_random_root_of_root` for each |
| `Graph/SWIGSplitMono.lean` | Backdoor condition (ii): `splitMonoDAG {bdD}` d-separates Y from D given Z, verified by `native_decide` on the computable `splitMonoDAG` |
| `Graph/CComponents.lean` | Each observed node is its own singleton C-component (no confounding) |
| `SCM/Model/EdgeType.lean` | `allNonparametric` edge type assignment |

The file now additionally demonstrates the full `bdSWIG.backdoorCriterion` on this concrete 3-node DAG.  Condition (i) (no `z ‚ąą Z` is a descendant of any `random D ‚ąą X`) is discharged by `native_decide` + `simp`.  Condition (ii) is routed through `splitMonoDAG` (since `splitMono` itself is `noncomputable`) and also closed by `native_decide`.  No full SCM is built ‚ÄĒ only the graphical criterion is exercised.

---

## 10c. `SCM/Examples/ContinuousBackdoor.lean` ‚ÄĒ Linear-Gaussian backdoor (continuous treatment)

Validation of the posterior witness-kernel backdoor pipeline: a 3-node SCM with continuous value spaces (`‚ĄĚ`) on every observed node, exercising `backdoor_completeness_ae` end-to-end on a treatment variable that is *not* discrete.

DAG: `Z ‚Üí X`, `Z ‚Üí Y`, `X ‚Üí Y` (so `Z` is a backdoor-admissible confounder for the effect of `X` on `Y`).  All three observed nodes have value space `‚ĄĚ` (via `CBő© : Fin 3 ‚Üí Type := fun _ => ‚ĄĚ`).  The latent set is empty, so the SCM is fully deterministic ‚ÄĒ the kernel-native pipeline does not need probabilistic latents to demonstrate the continuous-treatment regime; what matters is the continuity of the value spaces.

The example uses a *computable* SWIGGraph (`cbSWIGGraph`) so the backdoor criterion can be discharged via `native_decide` even though the parent `SCM` is `noncomputable` (carries `Measure`s).  `continuousBackdoorSCM.toSWIGGraph := cbSWIGGraph` definitionally.

| Declaration | Description |
|---|---|
| `CBNode`, `CBő©` | Node set `Fin 3` (`Zidx = 0`, `Xidx = 1`, `Yidx = 2`) with continuous value spaces `‚ĄĚ` |
| `cbEdgeBool`, `cbEdge`, `cbDAG` | Edge relation (`Bool`-lifted for `decide`-friendly `Decidable`) and underlying DAG |
| `cbSWIGGraph` | Computable SWIGGraph (empty latents, `observed = {Z, X, Y}` as `.random` nodes), used for graphical criterion proofs |
| `continuousBackdoorSCM` | The full SCM: extends `cbSWIGGraph`, constant-`0` structural functions, empty `latentDist`.  Marked `noncomputable` only because of the `Measure` API in the SCM structure |
| `cb_Xrand_obs`, `cb_Xfixed` | Witnesses that `random X` is observed and `fixed X` is not in `fixed`; supplied to `backdoor_completeness_ae`'s subset-hypotheses |
| `cb_backdoor_criterion` | Discharges `cbSWIGGraph.backdoorCriterion {X} _ _ {Y} {Z}`.  Condition (i) (`Z` not descendant of `X`) by `native_decide`; condition (ii) (d-separation in splitMono) by `native_decide` on the computable `splitMonoDAG` |

**What this validates.**  Continuous value spaces (`‚ĄĚ`) flow through the kernel-native pipeline (`obsKernel`, `obsCondKernel`, `Rule2JointOverlap`, `backdoor_completeness_ae`), and `SWIGGraph.backdoorCriterion` discharges by `native_decide` on the continuous SCM.  This example is sorry-free.

---

## 9d. `SCM/ID/Adjustment.lean` ‚ÄĒ Adjustment Functionals

Graph-level adjustment functionals that turn an SCM's observational
kernel into a post-intervention `Y`-marginal kernel under the backdoor
or frontdoor criterion.  The split into a *single-SCM completeness*
lemma plus a *cross-SCM congruence* lemma factors the cross-SCM HEq
plumbing out of the do-calculus assembly in `SCM/ID/Backdoor.lean` /
`PO/ID/Exact/Frontdoor.lean`.

| Definition / Statement | Description |
|---|---|
| `SCM.backdoorAdjustment M X hX_obs hX_fixed Y Z hY hZ` | Kernel `Kernel (M.fixSet X).FixedValues (ValuesOn Y _)` implementing `‚ąę_z P(Y \| X, Z=z) dP(z)` as a kernel-native `((zMarginalPost ‚äó‚āĖ M.obsCondKernel Y (X.image .random ‚ą™ Z)).comap ...).map Prod.snd` chain.  The conditional's `(X.image .random ‚ą™ Z)`-argument is assembled via `M.fillZrW X hX_obs hX_fixed Z s_post z`, which fills the `X.image .random` slice from `s_post` (reindexed from `X.image .fixed` through `zFixedAsRandom`) and pairs it with the outer `Z`-value `z` ‚ÄĒ routing through `fillZrW` (rather than an inline `valuesUnionMk ‚ąė zFixedAsRandom ‚ąė valuesProjection`) removes a reindexing-bridge gap in the downstream backdoor completeness proof.  Signature carries `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn Y (swigő© ő©))]`, `[Nonempty (ValuesOn Y (swigő© ő©))]`, `[‚ąÄ s : M.FixedValues, IsFiniteMeasure (M.obsKernel s)]`, and a `CountableOrCountablyGenerated` instance for the conditional kernel |
| `SCM.frontdoorAdjustment M X hX_obs hX_fixed Y Z hY hZ` | **Proved** (sorry-free).  Kernel `Kernel (M.fixSet X).FixedValues (ValuesOn Y _)` implementing the frontdoor integral as a chain of `‚äó‚āĖ` legs: outer X-marginal `(M.obsKernel.map projXr).comap fixSetProj`, middle `M.obsCondKernel Z (X.image .random)` (Rule 2 leg, `P(Z|X)`), inner `M.obsCondKernel Y (X.image .random ‚ą™ Z)` (Rule 2 leg on inner `do(Z)`, with `X` as adjustment set).  The three kernels compose to `((xMarginal ‚äó‚āĖ zCondX) ‚äó‚āĖ yCondXZ).map Prod.snd`.  Signature requires `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn Y _)]`, `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn Z _)]`, `[Nonempty (ValuesOn Y _)]`, `[Nonempty (ValuesOn Z _)]`, `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn (X.image .random) _)]`, `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn (X.image .random ‚ą™ Z) _)]`, `[‚ąÄ s, IsFiniteMeasure (M.obsKernel s)]`, and two `CountableOrCountablyGenerated` instances for the conditional kernels |
| `SCM.backdoorAdjustment_invariant` | **Proved** (sorry closed).  HEq cross-SCM invariance: two SCMs sharing `toSWIGGraph` and `obsKernel` produce the same `backdoorAdjustment` kernel.  Proof destructs both SCMs via `obtain`, `cases h_swig` unifies the SWIGGraph fields definitionally, then `apply heq_of_eq` reduces HEq to Eq. `eq_of_heq _h_obs` strips the obsKernel HEq; `unfold SCM.backdoorAdjustment` + nested `congr 1` peels off `.map Prod.snd` and `‚äó‚āĖ`, leaving the `zMarginalPost` branch (closed by `rw [h_ok]`) and the `condPost` branch (closed by unfolding `obsCondKernel`/`obsCondPairKernel` and `rw [h_ok]`). Does not require `heq_obsCondKernel` ‚ÄĒ `cases h_swig` already aligns the kernel arguments |
| `SCM.frontdoorAdjustment_invariant` | **Proved** (sorry-free).  Same shape as `backdoorAdjustment_invariant`.  After destructure + `cases h_swig`, `unfold SCM.frontdoorAdjustment` exposes the `‚äó‚āĖ` chain; unfolding `obsCondKernel`/`obsCondPairKernel` exposes the `condKernel`-of-`obsKernel.map`-joint pattern; `simp_rw [h_ok]` rewrites every `obsKernel` mention to the M‚āā-side, then `rfl` closes the remaining proof-irrelevance noise on `fixSetProj` (only SWIGGraph fields are used in `fixSetProj`; all unified after `cases h_swig`) |

---

## 9e. `SCM/ID/BackdoorCriterion.lean` ‚ÄĒ Backdoor criterion + Rule-3 leg

The graphical backdoor criterion (Basic Concepts.tex:636‚Äď645, Pearl 2009
Theorem 3.3.2) and the single regime-independent do-calculus leg the backdoor
identification proof consumes.  The identification *theorems* live in
`SCM/ID/Backdoor.lean` (¬ß9e').

**Design note on condition (ii).**  `Graph/Mutilation.lean` is not yet
implemented, so the lower-bar mutilation `G_{XŐ≤}` is encoded via
`SWIGGraph.splitMono X hX_obs hX_fix`: splitting `X` reroutes outgoing
edges of `random D` (for `D ‚ąą X`) to root nodes `fixed D`, which is
exactly the effect of lower-bar mutilation for d-separation purposes.

| Declaration | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `SWIGGraph.backdoorCriterion G X hX_obs hX_fix Y Z` | Two-condition criterion: (i) no `z ‚ąą Z` is a descendant of any `random D` with `D ‚ąą X` in `G`; (ii) `Z ‚ą™ X.image .fixed` d-separates `Y` from `X.image .random` in `G.splitMono X hX_obs hX_fix` (encodes `G_{XŐ≤}` d-sep; fixed_X conditioning is vacuous in splitMono but carried to match `do_rule2`'s shape) | Defined; type-checks without sorry |
| `SCM.backdoor_rule3_Z_marginal` | **Rule-3 leg**: under criterion (i), `((M.fixSet X).obsKernel s_post).map proj_Z = (M.obsKernel (fixSetProj s_post)).map proj_Z`. Applies `do_rule3` with `Y_param := ‚ąÖ`, `W_param := Z` and bridges `hNoDesc` via `fixSet_isAncestor_fixed_forward`. Consumed by `backdoor_completeness_ae`. | **Proved** |

---

## 9e'. `SCM/ID/Backdoor.lean` ‚ÄĒ Backdoor Identification (continuous, a.e.)

The sound **continuous-treatment** backdoor results, stated a.e. in the
treatment value (so they never read `obsCondKernel`/`backdoorAdjustment` on a
`{X = x}` null slice).  Built on the posterior witness-kernel Rule 2
(`condDistrib_fixSet_cross_SCM_bridge`).  Headline theorems
`backdoor_completeness_ae` and `backdoor_identifiable_ae`.

| Declaration | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `SCM.treatmentMarginal M X hXr s0` | The observational treatment marginal `őĹX = (M.obsKernel s0).map ŌÄ_{X.random}` ‚ÄĒ the supported measure the a.e. statements are anchored at | Defined |
| `SCM.BackdoorPositivityAE M X Z ‚Ä¶ s0` | Product positivity / overlap `Prop`: `őĹX ‚äó őľZ ‚Č™ őľ_{X.rand ‚ą™ Z}` (the standard continuous-treatment positivity; vacuous-free, covers both atomic and non-atomic regimes) | Defined |
| `SCM.doKernelY M X ‚Ä¶ Y hY s0` | Post-intervention `Y`-marginal as a kernel in the treatment value `t`: `t ‚Ü¶ ((M.fixSet X).obsKernel (extend s0 t)).map ŌÄ_Y`. Type `Kernel (ValuesOn (X.image .random)) (ValuesOn Y)` ‚ÄĒ independent of `M`'s fixed values | Defined |
| `SCM.adjustmentKernelY M X ‚Ä¶ Y Z hY hZ s0` | Backdoor-adjustment `Y`-marginal as a kernel in the treatment value: `(M.backdoorAdjustment ‚Ä¶).comap (fixSetExtend s0)` | Defined |
| `SCM.backdoor_completeness_ae_compProd M X ‚Ä¶ s0 hOverlap hPositivity` | `őĹX ‚äó‚āė doKernelY = őĹX ‚äó‚āė adjustmentKernelY` at base `s0`, under backdoor criterion + joint overlap + `BackdoorPositivityAE`. The compProd (joint) primary form ‚ÄĒ well-posed for continuous treatment (never reads `obsCondKernel` on a null slice) | **Proved** (sorry-free) |
| `SCM.backdoor_completeness_ae M X ‚Ä¶ s0 hOverlap hPositivity` | **Continuous backdoor completeness (a.e.).** For `őĹX`-a.e. treatment value `t`, `doKernelY ‚Ä¶ t = adjustmentKernelY ‚Ä¶ t`. Derived from the compProd form via `Kernel.ae_eq_of_compProd_eq`. **Headline.** | **Proved** (sorry-free) |
| `SCM.backdoor_identifiable_ae M‚āĀ M‚āā h_swig X Y Z ‚Ä¶ h_obs h_s0` | **Continuous backdoor identifiability (a.e., cross-SCM).** Two SCMs sharing the SWIG graph (`h_swig`) and observational kernel (`h_obs : HEq ‚Ä¶`) with aligned base points (`h_s0 : HEq s0‚āĀ s0‚āā`) produce `doKernelY M‚āĀ s0‚āĀ =ŠĶź[treatmentMarginal M‚āĀ s0‚āĀ] doKernelY M‚āā s0‚āā`. A plain `=ŠĶź` (not HEq) since `doKernelY` is `M`-fixed-value-independent. Proof: `backdoor_completeness_ae` on each side + cross-SCM `adjustmentKernelY` invariance (`backdoorAdjustment_invariant` after destructure + `cases h_swig`/`h_s0`). **Headline.** | **Proved** (sorry-free) |

---

## 9f. `PO/ID/Exact/LATE.lean` ‚ÄĒ IV/LATE Identification Theorem

Formalizes the Wald identification of LATE in the PO framework (Basic Concepts.tex: def:po-iv-system, def:po-iv-assumptions, def:po-late, prop:po-late, rem:po-late).

**Implementation note (2026-04-23 refactor).** The file now consumes the PO
abstraction layer introduced in `PO/`: counterfactual maps are built
as `POVar` (`zVar`, `dVar`, `yVar`), single-node interventions as
`RegimedVar.ofSingle` / `RegimedVar` (`dUnderZ`, `yUnderD`), joint bundles as
`POCFBundle` (`cfBundle`), and the IV independence premise as `P.IndepCF`.
A subsequent pass (2026-04-23) further collapsed the two `hCE` blocks in
`first_stage_identity` / `reduced_form_identity` via
`POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF`, deleting the product-form
`cfTuple` / `measurable_cfTuple` / `indepFun_factualZ_cfTuple` /
`integral_zEvent_of_cfTuple` scaffolding in favour of direct
`POCFBundle.jointValue` usage.
Net reduction: 429 ‚Üí 388 lines.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POIVSystem P` | `structure { Z, D, Y, hZbool, hDbool, hYreal, distinct }` | IV subsystem over a PO system | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.zVar`, `dVar`, `yVar` | `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P ‚ĄĚ` | `POVar` wrappers around `Z`, `D`, `Y` using the `hZbool`/`hDbool`/`hYreal` equivalences; abstract away the hand-rolled measurability chains | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.instrumentRegime S z` | `Regime P.V P.X` | Regime `r_z := ({Z}, z)` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.treatmentRegime S d` | `Regime P.V P.X` | Regime `r_d := ({D}, d)` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.DofZ S z`, `YofD S d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` / `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Counterfactual treatment `D(z)` and outcome `Y(d)` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.factualZ`, `factualD`, `factualY` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` / `Bool` / `‚ĄĚ` | Factual instrument / treatment / outcome | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.complierEvent` | `Set P.ő©` | `{ŌČ | D(1)(ŌČ)=1 ‚ąß D(0)(ŌČ)=0}` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.zEvent S z` | `Set P.ő©` | `{ŌČ | Z(ŌČ) = z}`, defined as `S.zVar.event z` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.measurable_{DofZ,factualZ,factualD,factualY,YofD}` | `Measurable ‚Ä¶` | Measurability of the counterfactual/factual variables (now one-line delegations to `POVar.measurable_cf` / `POVar.measurable_factual`) | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.measurableSet_complierEvent` / `measurableSet_zEvent` | `MeasurableSet ‚Ä¶` | Measurability of the two event sets | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.dUnderZ S z` | `RegimedVar P Bool` | `‚ü®S.dVar, Regime.single S.Z (S.hZbool.symm z)‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ regimed form of `D(z)` used to populate `cfBundle` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.yUnderD S d` | `RegimedVar P ‚ĄĚ` | `‚ü®S.yVar, Regime.single S.D (S.hDbool.symm d)‚ü©` ‚ÄĒ regimed form of `Y(d)` used to populate `cfBundle` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.cfBundle` | `POCFBundle P` | The bundle `(D(1), D(0), Y(1), Y(0))` built via iterated `POCFBundle.cons`; target of the IV independence hypothesis | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.YofDofZ S z` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | `Y(D(z)) := 1_{D(z)=1} Y(1) + 1_{D(z)=0} Y(0)` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.condExpDZ S z`, `condExpYZ S z` | `‚ĄĚ` | Event-level `E[D | Z=z]`, `E[Y | Z=z]` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, instrumentIndep, monotonicity, relevance }` where `instrumentIndep : P.IndepCF (RegimedVar.ofFactual S.zVar) S.cfBundle P.őľ` | def:po-iv-assumptions; the instrument-independence field is now phrased against the bundle-based `IndepCF` shape | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.LATE` | `‚ĄĚ` | `(‚ąę_C (Y(1)-Y(0)) ‚ąāőľ) / (őľ C).toReal` ‚ÄĒ def:po-late | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.DofZ_eq_factualD_on_zEvent` | `ŌČ ‚ąą zEvent z ‚Üí DofZ z ŌČ = factualD ŌČ` | Pointwise D-consistency on `{Z = z}` | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.factualY_eq_YofD_factualD` | `factualY ŌČ = YofD (factualD ŌČ) ŌČ` | Pointwise Y-consistency everywhere | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.first_stage_identity` | `condExpDZ 1 - condExpDZ 0 = (őľ C).toReal` | Step 1 of rem:po-late | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.reduced_form_identity` | `condExpYZ 1 - condExpYZ 0 = ‚ąę (YofDofZ 1 - YofDofZ 0) ‚ąāőľ` | Step 2 of rem:po-late | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.pointwise_monotonicity` | `‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, Y(D(1))-Y(D(0)) = (Y(1)-Y(0))¬∑1_C` | Step 3 of rem:po-late | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.event_conditioning_identity` | `‚ąę (Y(1)-Y(0))¬∑1_C ‚ąāőľ = (őľ C).toReal ¬∑ LATE` | Step 4 of rem:po-late | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.late_wald` | `(condExpYZ 1 - condExpYZ 0) / (condExpDZ 1 - condExpDZ 0) = LATE` | Wald identification of LATE, prop:po-late | **Proved** |

Drop-of-conditioning and consistency-on-event rewrites are delegated to
`POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF` from `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExp.lean`;
no private helper remains in this file.

---

## 9f'. `PO/ID/Exact/HeckmanRoy/{Setup,Wald}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Heckman‚ÄďVytlacil IV / Generalized Roy Selection Model (PO framework)

Formalises the Heckman‚ÄďVytlacil IV / generalized Roy selection model from `Basic Concepts.tex` (def:po-iv-heckman-roy-system, def:po-iv-heckman-roy-assumptions, def:po-iv-heckman-roy-late, prop:po-iv-heckman-roy-wald, rem:po-iv-heckman-roy-lean). Generalises `LATE.lean` to:

* an arbitrary measurable instrument value space `őĪ` (with `MeasurableSingletonClass`), instead of `Bool` ‚ÄĒ same parametrization pattern as `Manski/Setup.lean`;
* a latent uniform rank `U : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` (`U ~ Unif[0,1]`) plus a propensity map `p : őĪ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, replacing the binary `D` potentials and monotonicity by threshold crossing `D(z) = 1_{U ‚Č§ p(z)}`;
* a **pairwise** Wald identity: for any `z‚āÄ, z‚āĀ : őĪ` with `p z‚āÄ < p z‚āĀ` and positive measure on `{Z = z‚āÄ}, {Z = z‚āĀ}`,
  `(E[Y|Z=z‚āĀ] ‚ąí E[Y|Z=z‚āÄ]) / (E[D|Z=z‚āĀ] ‚ąí E[D|Z=z‚āÄ]) = LATE(z‚āÄ, z‚āĀ)`.

**Out of scope (follow-ups):** the marginal treatment effect `MTE` (def:po-iv-heckman-roy-mte) and the continuous-Z / regression-representative formulation (rem:po-iv-heckman-roy-local-iv); both need the `IsRegressionFunction` infrastructure of `PO/Analysis/Regression.lean` to extend to non-singleton instruments.

### `HeckmanRoy/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ data layer

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|

### `HeckmanRoy/Wald.lean` ‚ÄĒ proof layer

Mirrors the five-step decomposition of rem:po-iv-heckman-roy-lean.  All `theorem`/`lemma` proofs below are filled (Codex-assisted).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `DofZ_eq_factualD_on_zEvent` | `ŌČ ‚ąą zEvent z ‚Üí DofZ z ŌČ = factualD ŌČ` | Step 1: pointwise D-consistency on `{Z = z}`; one-line via `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` | **Proved** |
| `factualY_eq_YofD_factualD` | `factualY ŌČ = YofD (factualD ŌČ) ŌČ` | Step 1: pointwise Y-consistency; one-line via `POVar.factual_eq_cfUnder_self_selected` | **Proved** |
| `interval_indicator_sub` | `1_{U ‚Č§ p z‚āĀ} ŌČ - 1_{U ‚Č§ p z‚āÄ} ŌČ = (intervalComplierEvent z‚āÄ z‚āĀ).indicator 1 ŌČ` (for `p z‚āÄ ‚Č§ p z‚āĀ`) | Step 5 (algebra): pointwise interval-subtraction identity | **Proved** |
| `complier_measure` | `(őľ (intervalComplierEvent z‚āÄ z‚āĀ)).toReal = p z‚āĀ - p z‚āÄ` (for `p z‚āÄ < p z‚āĀ`) | Step 4: uniform-threshold lemma applied at `p z‚āÄ, p z‚āĀ` plus measure subtraction | **Proved** |
| `first_stage_identity` | `condExpDZ z‚āĀ - condExpDZ z‚āÄ = p z‚āĀ - p z‚āÄ` | Step 1+3 (analogue of LATE `first_stage_identity`); uses `POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF` | **Proved** |
| `reduced_form_identity` | `condExpYZ z‚āĀ - condExpYZ z‚āÄ = ‚ąę (YofDofZ z‚āĀ - YofDofZ z‚āÄ) ‚ąāőľ` | Step 1+3 (analogue of LATE `reduced_form_identity`); same drop-of-conditioning lemma | **Proved** |
| `pointwise_threshold_identity` | `‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, YofDofZ z‚āĀ ŌČ - YofDofZ z‚āÄ ŌČ = (Y(1)-Y(0))(ŌČ) ¬∑ 1_{intervalComplierEvent z‚āÄ z‚āĀ}(ŌČ)` | Step 2: replaces LATE `pointwise_monotonicity`; case-split on `U ŌČ ‚Č§ p z‚Īľ` using `thresholdCrossing` | **Proved** |
| `event_conditioning_identity` | `‚ąę (Y(1)-Y(0)) ¬∑ 1_C ‚ąāőľ = (őľ C).toReal ¬∑ LATE z‚āÄ z‚āĀ` | Direct copy of LATE `event_conditioning_identity` with `intervalComplierEvent` | **Proved** |
| `wald_pairwise` | `(condExpYZ z‚āĀ - condExpYZ z‚āÄ) / (condExpDZ z‚āĀ - condExpDZ z‚āÄ) = LATE z‚āÄ z‚āĀ` | Main theorem (prop:po-iv-heckman-roy-wald); stitches the helpers via `field_simp` | **Proved** |

---

## 9f''. `PO/ID/Exact/DynamicLATE/{Setup,Consistency,Bridges,WhenToTreat}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Two-Period Dynamic LATE (PO framework)

Formalises the two-period dynamic IV/LATE identification of Sojitra (2025) in the bare potential-outcome framework: `subsec:po-dynamic-late`, `def:po-dynamic-late-system`, `def:po-dynamic-late-assumptions`, `def:po-dynamic-late`, `def:po-dynamic-late-observable-functionals`, `prop:po-dynamic-late-when-to-treat`, `rem:po-dynamic-late-bridge`, `rem:po-dynamic-late-lean`. Generalises `LATE.lean` to two periods with sequential encouragements `Z‚āĀ,Z‚āā`, noncompliant treatments `D‚āĀ,D‚āā`, baseline state `S‚āÄ : POVar P ő≥‚āÄ`, intermediate state `S‚āĀ : POVar P ő≥‚āĀ`, and real outcome `Y`.

**Phase A scope.** Definitions, observable functionals, the assumption bundle, the four bridge identities of `rem:po-dynamic-late-bridge`, and the algebraic ratio identifications `whenToTreat_wald`, `mixtureLATE_wald`, plus the heterogeneous (`S‚āÄ`-conditional) versions. The bridge proofs and the structural composition-consistency rewrites are filled.

**Out of scope (Phase B):** `prop:po-dynamic-late-always-treat` and `cor:po-dynamic-late-staggered` (always-treat LATE under cross-period mean-independence / staggered-compliance). Will live in a future `AlwaysTreat.lean`.

### `DynamicLATE/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ data layer

Pairwise distinctness packaged as `Function.Injective` on a `Fin 7 ‚Üí P.V` accessor. | **Defined** |

### `DynamicLATE/Consistency.lean` ‚ÄĒ pointwise consistency-on-event rewrites

Structural pointwise identities used by the bridge proofs.  Each lemma is a direct consequence of PO consistency (def:po-consistency) ‚ÄĒ single-event `cf_eq_factual_on_event` for stage-2 rewrites and `Consistency.composition` for stage-1 (mirroring the existing `YofDofZ_eq_YofD_on_DofZEq` proof in `WhenToTreat.lean`).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `YofZ2_eq_factualY_on_z2Event` | `(hŌČ : factualZ2 ŌČ = z‚āā) ‚Üí YofZ2 z‚āā ŌČ = factualY ŌČ` | On `{Z‚āā = z‚āā}`, `Y(D‚āĀ,D‚āā(Z‚āĀ,z‚āā)) = Y` ‚ÄĒ single-event consistency at `Z‚āā` for the outcome `Y`. | **Proved** |
| `D2ofZ2_eq_factualD2_on_z2Event` | `(hŌČ : factualZ2 ŌČ = z‚āā) ‚Üí D2ofZ2 z‚āā ŌČ = factualD2 ŌČ` | On `{Z‚āā = z‚āā}`, `D‚āā(Z‚āĀ,z‚āā) = D‚āā` ‚ÄĒ single-event consistency at `Z‚āā` for `D‚āā`. | **Proved** |
| `eval_encouragement_eq_eval_encZ2_on_z1Event` (private) | `(hŌČ : factualZ1 ŌČ = z 0) ‚Üí v ‚Č† Z‚āĀ ‚Üí v ‚Č† Z‚āā ‚Üí P.eval (encouragementRegime z) ŌČ v = P.eval (encZ2Regime (z 1)) ŌČ v` | Shared helper: composition consistency at any `v ‚ąČ {Z‚āĀ, Z‚āā}`, combining `Consistency.composition` + `Regime.ext` (encouragementRegime ‚ÜĒ sqcup of two singletons) + `As.exclusion_Z1` (for the `IntermediateAgrees` premise at Z‚āĀ). | **Proved** |
| `YofDofZ_eq_YofZ2_on_z1Event` | `(hŌČ : factualZ1 ŌČ = z 0) ‚Üí YofDofZ z ŌČ = YofZ2 (z 1) ŌČ` | On `{Z‚āĀ = z 0}`, `Y` under `encouragementRegime z` agrees with `Y` under `encZ2Regime (z 1)` ‚ÄĒ instance of the helper at `v := S.Y`. | **Proved** |
| `D2ofZ_eq_D2ofZ2_on_z1Event` | `(hŌČ : factualZ1 ŌČ = z 0) ‚Üí D2ofZ z ŌČ = D2ofZ2 (z 1) ŌČ` | Instance of the helper at `v := S.D2`. | **Proved** |
| `D1ofZ_eq_factualD1_on_z1Event` | `(hŌČ : factualZ1 ŌČ = z 0) ‚Üí D1ofZ z ŌČ = factualD1 ŌČ` | On `{Z‚āĀ = z 0}`, `D‚āĀ(z) = D‚āĀ` ‚ÄĒ combines `As.exclusion_D1` (reduces to `Regime.single Z‚āĀ (z 0)`) with `cf_eq_factual_on_event` at `Z‚āĀ`. | **Proved** |

### `DynamicLATE/Bridges.lean` ‚ÄĒ counterfactual / observable bridges

The four bridge identities of `rem:po-dynamic-late-bridge`, plus the one-sided-noncompliance corollary and two stage-2 inner-ratio sub-bridges used by the conditional bridge proofs.  Conditional bridges (`cOutcome_bridge`, `cCompliance_bridge`) are the workhorses; unconditional bridges (`outcome_bridge`, `compliance_bridge`) are short integration corollaries.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `probDofZ_eq_zero_of_not_preceq` | `(h : ¬¨ Preceq d z) ‚Üí P.őľ (DofZEq z d) = 0` | Under one-sided noncompliance, `D(z) ‚ąą {d \| d ‚Čľ z}` a.s.; the complement has measure zero. | **Proved** |
| `innerCondY_mul_z1_indicator` | `innerCondY z ¬∑ 1_{Z‚āĀ=z 0} =ŠĶź 1_{Z‚āĀ=z 0} ¬∑ historyBundle2.condExpGiven (YofZ2 (z 1))` | Stage-2 outcome ratio bridge multiplied by `1_{Z‚āĀ=z‚āĀ}`. Single application of `condExpRatio_of_consistency_CondIndepCFBundle` with `a := z2Var`, `B := cfBundle2 (z 1)`, `C := historyBundle2`, plus `historyBundle2`-pullout of the `1_{Z‚āĀ=z 0}` factor. | **Proved** |
| `innerCondD_mul_z1_indicator` | `innerCondD z d ¬∑ 1_{Z‚āĀ=z 0} =ŠĶź 1_{Z‚āĀ=z 0} ¬∑ 1_{D‚āĀ=d 0} ¬∑ historyBundle2.condExpGiven 1_{D‚āā(Z‚āĀ,z 1)=d 1}` | Stage-2 compliance ratio bridge multiplied by `1_{Z‚āĀ=z‚āĀ}`.  Same shape as above for the compliance event, factoring out the bundle2-measurable `1_{D‚āĀ=d 0}`. | **Proved** |
| `cOutcome_bridge` | `(hd : Preceq d z) ‚Üí historyBundle1.condExpGiven (YofDofZ z) =ŠĶź cObsMean z` | `S‚āÄ`-conditional outcome bridge.  Two-stage: stage-2 via `innerCondY_mul_z1_indicator`, stage-1 via `condExpGiven_mul_of_consistency_CondIndepCFBundle` + tower + consistency rewrite at `Z‚āĀ`. | **Proved** |
| `cCompliance_bridge` | `(hd : Preceq d z) ‚Üí historyBundle1.condExpGiven (1_{DofZEq z d}) =ŠĶź cObsProb z d` | `S‚āÄ`-conditional compliance bridge.  Same shape as `cOutcome_bridge`; the consistency rewrite uses `D2ofZ_eq_D2ofZ2_on_z1Event` and `D1ofZ_eq_factualD1_on_z1Event` (the latter via `As.exclusion_D1`). | **Proved** |
| `outcome_bridge` | `(hd : Preceq d z) ‚Üí ‚ąę YofDofZ z = obsMean z` | Unconditional outcome bridge, derived by integrating `cOutcome_bridge` and using `‚ąę B.condExpGiven f = ‚ąę f`. | **Proved** |
| `compliance_bridge` | `(hd : Preceq d z) ‚Üí (P.őľ (DofZEq z d)).toReal = obsProb z d` | Unconditional compliance bridge, derived analogously from `cCompliance_bridge` via `integral_indicator_const`. | **Proved** |

### `DynamicLATE/WhenToTreat.lean` ‚ÄĒ ratio identifications (algebraic from the bridges)

The when-to-treat / mixture Wald identifications of `prop:po-dynamic-late-when-to-treat`. Helper lemmas factor the structural composition-consistency content; the main `whenToTreat_wald` is then assembled algebraically from the bridges plus helpers.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `YofDofZ_eq_YofD_on_DofZEq` | `‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, DofZ z ŌČ = d ‚Üí YofDofZ z ŌČ = YofD d ŌČ` | Composition consistency on the encouragement event (uses `As.consistency.composition` + `As.exclusion`) | **Proved** |
| `DofZ_zero_eq_zero` | `‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, DofZ ![false,false] ŌČ = ![false,false]` | Under one-sided noncompliance, `D(0) = 0` a.s. | **Proved** |
| `YofDofZ_zero_ae_eq_YofD_zero` | `YofDofZ ![false,false] =ŠĶź YofD ![false,false]` | A.s. `Y(D(0)) = Y(0)` | **Proved** |
| `DofZ_in_two_values` | `(hd : d ‚ąą {(1,0),(0,1)}) ‚Üí ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, DofZ d ŌČ = d ‚ą® DofZ d ŌČ = 0` | Under one-sided + single-period encouragement, `D(d) ‚ąą {d, 0}` a.s. | **Proved** |
| `YofDofZ_decomposition` | `YofDofZ d =ŠĶź YofD d ¬∑ 1_{D(d)=d} + YofD 0 ¬∑ 1_{D(d)=0}` | Two-piece pointwise decomposition combining `YofDofZ_eq_YofD_on_DofZEq` + `DofZ_in_two_values` | **Proved** |
| `int_outcome_difference_identity` | `‚ąę YofDofZ d - ‚ąę YofDofZ 0 = ‚ąę_{D(d)=d} (Y(d) - Y(0)) dőľ` | Numerator rewrite from the decomposition + `YofDofZ_zero_ae_eq_YofD_zero` | **Proved** |
| `whenToTreat_wald` | `(hd : d ‚ąą {(1,0),(0,1)}) ‚Üí ŌĄ_d = (obsMean d - obsMean 0) / obsProb d d` | **Main when-to-treat identification (prop:po-dynamic-late-when-to-treat).** Combines the outcome / compliance bridges with `int_outcome_difference_identity`. | **Proved** |
| `mixtureLATE_wald` | `ő≤_z = (obsMean z - obsMean 0) / (1 - obsProb z 0)` | **Main mixture identification, totalized at `z = 0` by Lean division.** | **Proved** |
| `cWhenToTreat_wald` | `(hd : d ‚ąą {(1,0),(0,1)}) ‚Üí cŌĄ_d =ŠĶź (cObsMean d - cObsMean 0) / cObsProb d d` | `S‚āÄ`-conditional when-to-treat ratio | **Proved** |
| `cMixtureLATE_wald` | `cő≤_z =ŠĶź (cObsMean z - cObsMean 0) / (1 - cObsProb z 0)` | `S‚āÄ`-conditional mixture ratio, totalized at `z = 0` by Lean division | **Proved** |

**Status as of 2026-04-27 redesign.** The bridges are refactored around a per-stage workhorse (in `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExpBundle.lean`) and a layer of structural pointwise consistency rewrites (in `DynamicLATE/Consistency.lean`).  The Dynamic LATE files in this section typecheck with their local bridge and ratio proofs filled; remaining proof debt, if any, is inherited only from broader project-wide Mathlib/measure-theory stubs already documented elsewhere.

**Setup.lean** also gained:
* `exclusion` field on `Assumptions` (primitive-process clause `Y(D, S)`).
* `exclusion_D1` field on `Assumptions` (primitive-process clause `D‚āĀ(Z‚āĀ, S‚āÄ)`: `D‚āĀ` does not depend on `Z‚āā`); needed to identify `D‚āĀ(z)` with the factual `D‚āĀ` on the event `{Z‚āĀ = z 0}` in the conditional compliance bridge.
* Helpers `encouragementRegime_disjoint_treatmentRegime` and `encTreatRegime`.
