## 9g. `PO/ID/Exact/ATE.lean` ‚ÄĒ Backdoor ATE Identification Theorem (PO framework)

Formalizes the standard backdoor-adjustment identification of the average treatment effect at the potential-outcome level. Mirrors the structure of `LATE.lean` but uses ŌÉ-algebra conditional expectations (instead of event-based `eventCondExp`) because the adjustment variable `X` is continuous. The covariate type `ő≥` is an arbitrary measurable space, so the file simultaneously handles discrete/continuous/vector-valued `X` (e.g. `ő≥ := ‚ĄĚ‚ĀŅ`).

Core identity (CATE level):

    őľ[Y(d) | ŌÉ(X)]  =ŠĶź[őľ]  őľ[Y ¬∑ 1_{D=d} | ŌÉ(X)] / P[D=d | ŌÉ(X)].

Integrated corollary: `ATE = adjustedATE`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POBackdoorSystem P ő≥` | `structure { D, Y, Xvar, hDbool, hYreal, distinct }` | Backdoor subsystem: binary treatment `D`, real outcome `Y`, covariate `POVar P ő≥` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.{dVar, yVar, xVar}` | `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P ‚ĄĚ` / `POVar P ő≥` | `POVar` wrappers around `D`, `Y`, `X` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.YofD S d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(d) := yVar.cfUnder dVar d` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.{factualD, factualY, factualX}` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` / `‚ĄĚ` / `ő≥` | Factual treatment / outcome / covariate | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.measurable_{YofD, factualD, factualY, factualX}` | `Measurable ‚Ä¶` | Measurability of the counterfactual/factual variables (one-line delegations) | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.sigmaX` | `MeasurableSpace P.ő©` | ŌÉ-algebra generated by `factualX` (`MeasurableSpace.comap factualX _`) | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.sigmaX_le` | `sigmaX ‚Č§ P.measő©` | ŌÉ(X) is a sub-ŌÉ-algebra of the ambient one | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.yUnderD S d` | `RegimedVar P ‚ĄĚ` | Regimed form of `Y(d)` used to populate `cfBundle` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.cfBundle` | `POCFBundle P` | Bundle `(Y(1), Y(0))` built via `POCFBundle.cons`; target of the unconfoundedness hypothesis | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.propScore S d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Propensity score `P[D=d | ŌÉ(X)] := őľ[dVar.indicator d | sigmaX]` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.CATE S d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Conditional ATE `őľ[Y(d) | ŌÉ(X)]` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.ATE` | `‚ĄĚ` | `‚ąę (Y(1) - Y(0)) ‚ąāőľ` ‚ÄĒ average treatment effect | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.adjustedCE S d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Observable adjusted-outcome functional `őľ[Y ¬∑ 1_{D=d} | ŌÉ(X)] / propScore d` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.adjustedATE` | `‚ĄĚ` | `‚ąę (adjustedCE 1 - adjustedCE 0) ‚ąāőľ` ‚ÄĒ identified side of ATE | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, unconfoundedness, overlap, integrable_{Y1,Y0,factualY} }` | Backdoor assumptions (PO level); `unconfoundedness : P.CondIndepCF (ofFactual dVar) cfBundle (ofFactual xVar) P.őľ`; **pointwise** overlap `‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, 0 < propScore 1 ŌČ ‚ąß propScore 1 ŌČ < 1` (relaxed from the uniform-`c` strong-overlap form to bare positivity, mirroring DTR) | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.cate_backdoor` | `S.CATE d =ŠĶź[P.őľ] S.adjustedCE d` | CATE-level backdoor adjustment identity; reduces to (a) projecting `hA.unconfoundedness` onto the `d`-th bundle coord via `CondIndepCF.project` and (b) applying `Mathlib.CondIndep.condExp_mul_of_condIndep`; `hne` (nonzero propScore at both `d=true/false`) closed via pointwise overlap ‚ÄĒ `d=true` directly, `d=false` via `propScore false = 1 - propScore true` from the indicator sum identity. Uses `POVar.indicator` / `POVar.factual_mul_indicator_eq_cfUnder_mul_indicator_fn` throughout | **Proved** (conditional on `condExp_mul_of_condIndep` and `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep` Mathlib stubs) |
| `POBackdoorSystem.ate_backdoor` | `S.ATE = S.adjustedATE` | Integrated ATE identification; one-line call to `POVar.integral_sub_eq_integral_sub_of_condExpGiven_ae_eq` fed by `cate_backdoor` at `d = true, false` | **Proved** (conditional on `cate_backdoor`) |
| `POBackdoorSystem.propScore_ne_of_overlap` | `(hov : ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, 0 < propScore true ŌČ ‚ąß propScore true ŌČ < 1) (d) ‚Üí ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, propScore d ŌČ ‚Č† 0` | Per-arm non-vanishing of the propensity from the (two-sided) **overlap predicate alone** (no full `Assumptions`); reusable by the estimation-layer constructors | **Proved** |

### Regression-adjustment: the adjustment functional is an observable regression

Third beat of the identification story (after `cate_backdoor`, `ate_backdoor`): `adjustedCE d` ‚ÄĒ the ŌÉ(X)-stratified adjustment functional ‚ÄĒ equals the **observable outcome regression** `E[Y | D=d, X]`, a functional of the observed law of `(X,D,Y)` only (overlap, NO unconfoundedness). Certifies that the back-door nuisance `őľ` is a genuine regression problem (the one `ML/Causal/RegressionBridge.lean` solves). Heavy step factored into the reusable Mathlib kernel `condExp_setIndicator_condExp_of_le` (¬ß10k').

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POBackdoorSystem.factualDX` / `measurable_factualDX` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool √ó ő≥` | Factual `(D, X)` pair `ŌČ ‚Ü¶ (factualD ŌČ, factualX ŌČ)` and its measurability | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.sigmaDX` / `sigmaDX_le` / `sigmaX_le_sigmaDX` | `MeasurableSpace P.ő©` | ŌÉ(D,X) `:= comap factualDX`; sub-ŌÉ-algebra of ambient; and `ŌÉ(X) ‚Č§ ŌÉ(D,X)` (tower) | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.outcomeReg` / `stronglyMeasurable_outcomeReg` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Outcome regression `E[Y | D,X] := őľ[factualY | ŌÉ(D,X)]` (observable) + its ŌÉ(D,X)-strong-measurability | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.exists_regFn` / `regFn` / `measurable_regFn` / `outcomeReg_eq_regFn` | `Bool √ó ő≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Doob‚ÄďDynkin lift (`Measurable.exists_eq_measurable_comp`) of `outcomeReg` to the value-space regression function `regFn (d,x) = E[Y|D=d,X=x]`, with `outcomeReg = regFn ‚ąė factualDX` | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.exists_propScoreLift` / `eLift` / `measurable_eLift` / `propScore_true_eq_eLift` | `ő≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Doob‚ÄďDynkin lift of `propScore true` to the value-space propensity `eLift x` (`E[D|X=x]`), with `propScore true = eLift ‚ąė factualX` | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.regression_adjustment` | `(d) (hY : Integrable factualY) (h_ne : ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, propScore d ŌČ ‚Č† 0) ‚Üí adjustedCE d =ŠĶź (fun ŌČ => regFn (d, factualX ŌČ))` | **Regression-adjustment identification (observable).** `adjustedCE d =ŠĶź E[Y|D=d,X]`; overlap-only, no unconfoundedness. Proof: `condExpRatio_eq_of_mul` ‚Üí Mathlib kernel `condExp_setIndicator_condExp_of_le` (ŌÉ(X)‚ÜíŌÉ(D,X) tower) ‚Üí arm collapse on `{D=d}` ‚Üí ŌÉ(X) pull-out | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.regression_adjustment_of_assumptions` | `(hA : Assumptions) (d) ‚Üí adjustedCE d =ŠĶź (fun ŌČ => regFn (d, factualX ŌČ))` | Packaged wrapper: discharges `Integrable factualY` (via `Assumptions.integrable_factualY`) and overlap (via `propScore_ne_of_overlap`) from `hA` | **Proved** |

## 9g'. `PO/ID/Exact/ATT.lean` ‚ÄĒ Backdoor ATT Identification Theorem (PO framework)

Mirrors `PO/ID/Exact/ATE.lean` but identifies the **average treatment effect on the treated** (ATT). Reuses `POBackdoorSystem` (and its `Assumptions`/`adjustedCE`/`propScore`/`CATE`) verbatim. Adds the population-treated-fraction `propTreated`, the PO-level `ATT = E[A¬∑(Y(1)‚ąíY(0))] / ŌÄ_T`, the observable outcome-regression form `adjustedATT = E[A¬∑(őľ‚āĀ(X)‚ąíőľ‚āÄ(X))] / ŌÄ_T`, an extended assumption bundle `ATTAssumptions` (adds `0 < propTreated`), and the AIPW corollary at the ŌÉ(X) representative level. The two main theorems delegate the heavy lifting to private helpers:

* **Outcome-regression numerator equality** (per arm `d`): `‚ąę A ¬∑ Y(d) dőľ = ‚ąę A ¬∑ adjustedCE d dőľ` ‚ÄĒ by tower over ŌÉ(X) and the unconfoundedness factorization `őľ[A¬∑Y(d)|ŌÉ(X)] = őľ[A|ŌÉ(X)] ¬∑ őľ[Y(d)|ŌÉ(X)] = e(X)¬∑CATE d`, then `cate_backdoor` to swap `CATE d ‚Ü¶ adjustedCE d`. The ŌÉ(X)-measurable side does the same tower step on the OR side.
* **AIPW IPW-correction vanishing**: `‚ąę (1‚ąíA) ¬∑ (e/(1‚ąíe)) ¬∑ (Y ‚ąí adjustedCE false) dőľ = 0` ‚ÄĒ pull `e/(1‚ąíe)` (ŌÉ(X)-measurable) out of `őľ[¬∑|ŌÉ(X)]`, rewrite `(1‚ąíA)¬∑Y = (1‚ąíA)¬∑Y(false)` via consistency, factor by unconfoundedness, then collapse `(1‚ąíA)¬∑Y(false) ‚ąí (1‚ąíA)¬∑adjustedCE false` because `őľ[Y(false)|ŌÉ(X)] =ŠĶź adjustedCE false`.
* **Treated-arm consistency rewrite**: `‚ąę A¬∑(Y ‚ąí adjustedCE false) dőľ = ‚ąę A¬∑(adjustedCE true ‚ąí adjustedCE false) dőľ` ‚ÄĒ apply the OR numerator equality at `d = true` to the `A¬∑Y` piece (using `Y = A¬∑Y(1) + (1‚ąíA)¬∑Y(0)` consistency-collapsed to `A¬∑Y = A¬∑Y(1)` on the treated set).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POBackdoorSystem.propTreated S` | `‚ĄĚ` | Marginal treatment probability `ŌÄ_T = E[1_{D=1}] = ‚ąę dVar.indicator true dőľ` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.ATT S` | `‚ĄĚ` | PO-level ATT `(‚ąę A ¬∑ (Y(true) ‚ąí Y(false)) dőľ) / ŌÄ_T` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.adjustedATT S` | `‚ĄĚ` | Observable outcome-regression form `(‚ąę A ¬∑ (adjustedCE true ‚ąí adjustedCE false) dőľ) / ŌÄ_T` | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.ATTAssumptions S` | `structure extends Assumptions S { propTreated_pos }` | Extended bundle: `Assumptions` + `0 < propTreated`. The `< 1` half of overlap (already in `Assumptions.overlap`) suffices for the AIPW corollary | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.propScore_false_ae` | `S.propScore false =ŠĶź[őľ] (1 - S.propScore true)` | Indicator-pair sum identity transported through `őľ[¬∑|ŌÉ(X)]` (private) | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.{stronglyMeasurable_adjustedCE_comap, measurable_adjustedCE, integrable_adjustedCE}` | various | ŌÉ(X)-strong-measurability, ambient measurability, and a.e. integrability of `adjustedCE d` (private; integrability via `cate_backdoor` transport from `CATE d = condExpGiven Y(d)`) | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.att_numerator_arm` | `(hA : Assumptions S) (d) ‚Üí ‚ąę A ¬∑ Y(d) dőľ = ‚ąę A ¬∑ adjustedCE d dőľ` | Per-arm OR numerator equality (private); fed by tower + `condExp_mul_of_condIndep` + `cate_backdoor` | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.propScore_ne_zero` | `(hA : Assumptions) (d) ‚Üí ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, propScore d ŌČ ‚Č† 0` | Both-arm non-vanishing of propensity from `Assumptions.overlap` (mirrors the ATE helper) (private) | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.condExp_indicator_residual_adjusted_zero` | `őľ[(1‚ąíA) ¬∑ (Y ‚ąí adjustedCE false) | ŌÉ(X)] =ŠĶź 0` | Residual zero-conditional-mean for the `A=0` arm using `adjustedCE false` (private) | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.weighted_false_residual_integral_zero` | `‚ąę (1‚ąíA) ¬∑ (e/(1‚ąíe)) ¬∑ (Y ‚ąí adjustedCE false) dőľ = 0` (under `hIPW` integrability + `Assumptions`) | IPW-correction vanishing (private) | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.treated_factual_residual_integral_eq_adjusted` | `‚ąę A¬∑(Y ‚ąí adjustedCE false) dőľ = ‚ąę A¬∑(adjustedCE true ‚ąí adjustedCE false) dőľ` | Treated-arm consistency rewrite (private) | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.ATT_eq_adjustedATT` | `(hA : ATTAssumptions S) ‚Üí S.ATT = S.adjustedATT` | **Main ATT identification.** Reduces to numerator equality via `congr 1` (denominator `propTreated > 0`) and applies `att_numerator_arm` at `d = true, false` | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.adjustedATT_eq_aipwForm` | `(hA : ATTAssumptions S) (hIPW : Integrable IPW-correction) ‚Üí S.adjustedATT = AIPW-form numerator / propTreated` | **AIPW corollary.** AIPW form uses the ŌÉ(X)-measurable representatives `adjustedCE false` (for `őľ‚āÄ(X)`) and `propScore true` (for `e(X)`). The extra `hIPW` integrability hypothesis is honest about the fact that `e/(1‚ąíe)` is unbounded under bare-positivity overlap; this is the standard registered hypothesis for the Hahn (1998) ATT IF | **Proved** |

## 9g''. `PO/ID/Exact/QTE/QuantileEffect.lean` ‚ÄĒ Backdoor Quantile Treatment Effect (Firpo 2007)

Marginal quantile treatment effect `QTE(ŌĄ) = F_{Y(1)}‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(ŌĄ) ‚ąí F_{Y(0)}‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(ŌĄ)`
under Firpo (2007) ignorability: unconfoundedness + common support (`0 < e_d < 1`,
in `Assumptions`; strict overlap is *not* an identification assumption). Reuses
`POBackdoorSystem` (binary `D`, real `Y`, covariate `X`) and its `Assumptions`
from ¬ß9g; covariates enter only in identification, not the estimand (standard
marginal QTE). The quantile layer makes QTE a *functional of the two
potential-outcome laws*: once those laws are identified by observable measures,
QTE is the difference of their quantiles.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
The distributional engine (`ipwDensity`, `ipwLaw`, `cfUnderLaw_eq_ipwLaw`) lives
in `DistributionalBackdoor.lean` (¬ß9g''b) and is imported here; this file is the quantile
layer on top.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POBackdoorSystem.qtdQuantile S d őľ ŌĄ` | `‚ĄĚ` | `ŌĄ`-quantile of `Y(d)` (`yVar.cfUnderQuantile dVar d`). | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.qte S őľ ŌĄ` | `‚ĄĚ` | QTE estimand `F_{Y(1)}‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(ŌĄ) ‚ąí F_{Y(0)}‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(ŌĄ)`. | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.qtdQuantile_eq_quantile_cfUnderLaw` | `= Stat.quantile (cfUnderLaw ‚Ä¶) ŌĄ` | Quantile of `Y(d)` = quantile of its law. | `rfl` |
| `POBackdoorSystem.qte_eq_of_law_eq` | `cfUnderLaw ‚Ä¶ true = őĹ‚āĀ ‚Üí cfUnderLaw ‚Ä¶ false = őĹ‚āÄ ‚Üí qte = quantile őĹ‚āĀ ŌĄ ‚ąí quantile őĹ‚āÄ ŌĄ` | **QTE as a functional of the identified laws** ‚ÄĒ the payoff of the quantile layer; reduces QTE identification to distributional identification. | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.qte_backdoor` | `qte P.őľ ŌĄ = quantile (ipwLaw 1) ŌĄ ‚ąí quantile (ipwLaw 0) ŌĄ` | Backdoor QTE identification; chains `qte_eq_of_law_eq` with the proven `cfUnderLaw_eq_ipwLaw`. | **Proved** |

## 9g''b. `PO/ID/Exact/QTE/DistributionalBackdoor.lean` ‚ÄĒ Distributional backdoor (Firpo 2007)

Lifts the mean-level backdoor (`ate_backdoor`/`cate_backdoor`, ¬ß9g) to the whole
law of `Y(d)`: under Firpo ignorability (unconfoundedness + bare common support
`0 < e_d`), `Law(Y(d))` equals the observable inverse-probability-weighting law.
This is the engine behind ¬ß9g'' QTE identification.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POBackdoorSystem.ipwDensity S d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | IPW density `1_{D=d} / e_d(X)`. | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.ipwLaw S d őľ` | `Measure ‚ĄĚ` | Observable IPW law: `factualY` pushed through `(1_{D=d}/e_d)¬∑őľ`. | **Defined** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.propScore_pos` | `Assumptions ‚Üí ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, 0 < propScore d ŌČ` | Both arms have a.e.-positive propensity score (bare overlap; false arm via `e_true + e_false = 1`). | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.integral_mul_indicator_eq_integral_mul_propScore` | `‚ąę h¬∑1_{D=d} = ‚ąę h¬∑e_d` (ŌÉX-meas `h`) | Conditional-expectation pull-out of the indicator against a ŌÉ(X)-measurable factor. | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.ipwDensity_integrable` | `Assumptions ‚Üí Integrable (ipwDensity d)` | IPW weight integrable under bare overlap, via a truncation / monotone-convergence argument (`‚ąę 1_{D=d}/e_d = 1`, no uniform lower bound on `e_d`). | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.integral_comp_YofD_eq` | `‚ąę g(Y(d)) = ‚ąę g(Y)¬∑1_{D=d}/e_d` (bounded meas `g`) | **Core distributional identity** ‚ÄĒ the `g`-transform of `cate_backdoor`: consistency on `{D=d}`, `Y(d) ‚üā D‚ą£ŌÉX` via `condExp_mul_of_condIndep`, then pull `1/e_d` out and cancel. | **Proved** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.cfUnderLaw_eq_ipwLaw` | `cfUnderLaw ‚Ä¶ d P.őľ = ipwLaw d P.őľ` | **Distributional backdoor** (Firpo): law of `Y(d)` = observable IPW law. Assembled via `Measure.ext` from `integral_comp_YofD_eq` at `g = 1_s`, bridging `Measure.map`/`withDensity` to the set lintegral. | **Proved** |

## 9g'''. `PO/ID/Exact/PartialLinear/{Setup,Identification}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Partially Linear Model (continuous treatment, PO framework)

The partially linear regression (PLR) model `Y = g(X) + őł¬∑D + noise` as a causal
model under the **standard backdoor PO framework with a real-valued (continuous)
treatment** `D ‚ąą ‚ĄĚ`. `Setup.lean` builds the continuous-treatment substrate and the
homogeneous structural model; `Identification.lean` identifies the structural slope
`őł` as the Robinson partialling-out estimand (the causal bridge). All declarations
**proved** (sorry-free).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `POPartialLinearSystem` | structure | Continuous-treatment backdoor subsystem: real treatment node `D`, real outcome `Y`, covariate `X`, value-space equivs, node distinctness. Mirrors `POBackdoorSystem` with `D : ‚ĄĚ`. |
| `POPartialLinearSystem.YofD d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The potential outcome `Y(d)` under dose `d : ‚ĄĚ` (`yVar.cfUnder dVar d`). |
| `POPartialLinearSystem.sigmaX` / `sigmaXD` | `MeasurableSpace P.ő©` | ŌÉ-algebra generated by the covariate, resp. by the covariate and treatment jointly; `sigmaX_le_sigmaXD` records the inclusion. |
| `POPartialLinearModel` | structure (extends system) | Adds the homogeneous linear dose-response `Y(d) = b(X)+őł¬∑d+U`, the backdoor mean-independence `E[U‚ą£ŌÉ(X,D)]=0`, and consistency. |
| `POPartialLinearModel.factualY_eq` | `Y =ŠĶź b(X)+őł¬∑D+U` | Observed-data form of the structural model, from consistency + the dose-response restriction. |
| `POPartialLinearModel.causal_homogeneity` | `Y(d)‚ąíY(d') =ŠĶź őł¬∑(d‚ąíd')` | The causal reading: `őł` is the constant per-unit treatment effect. |
| `POPartialLinearModel.mReg` / `lReg` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Treatment / outcome regressions on the covariate, `E[D‚ą£ŌÉX]` and `E[Y‚ą£ŌÉX]`. |
| `POPartialLinearModel.condExp_U_sigmaX` | `E[U‚ą£ŌÉX] =ŠĶź 0` | The structural error has zero mean given the covariate (backdoor + tower). |
| `POPartialLinearModel.condExp_resid_sigmaX` | `E[D‚ąím‚āÄ(X)‚ą£ŌÉX] =ŠĶź 0` | The treatment residual is conditionally mean-zero. |
| `POPartialLinearModel.integral_U_resid` | `E[U¬∑(D‚ąím‚āÄ(X))] = 0` | Orthogonality of the structural error to the treatment residual (needs `Integrable U`). |
| `POPartialLinearModel.lReg_eq` | `‚Ąď‚āÄ(X) =ŠĶź b(X)+őł¬∑m‚āÄ(X)` | Decomposition of the outcome regression. |
| `POPartialLinearModel.robinson_estimand_eq_theta` | `E[(Y‚ąí‚Ąď‚āÄ)(D‚ąím‚āÄ)] / E[(D‚ąím‚āÄ)¬≤] = őł` | **The causal bridge**: the Robinson partialling-out estimand equals the structural slope, given residual treatment variation `E[(D‚ąím‚āÄ)¬≤]‚Č†0`. |

## 9h. `PO/ID/Exact/DTR/` ‚ÄĒ Sequential Backdoor Identification (Dynamic Treatment Regime, general `n`)

Formalizes `prop:po-dynamic-backdoor` from `Basic Concepts.tex` (subsection
`subsec:po-dynamic-regime`) for arbitrary horizon `n ‚Č• 1`, in the bare
potential-outcome framework.  Reuses the bundle-conditioned conditional
expectation machinery from `PO/Conditioning/Bundle.lean`.

The identification target is the **iterated-conditional-expectation form**

    (historyBundle 0).condExpGiven (Y_of dbar)  =ŠĶź[őľ]  innerReg dbar (n - 1)

where `innerReg dbar j` is a recursively-defined CE-ratio chain over `n`
stages (innermost level `j = 0` conditions on the full history
`historyBundle (n - 1) = (S 0, D 0, ‚Ä¶, S (n - 1))`; outermost level
`j = n - 1` conditions on `historyBundle 0 = (S 0,)`).  Integrated form:
`E[Y(dbar)] = ‚ąę innerReg dbar (n - 1) ‚ąāőľ`.

**Treatment-space and state-space conventions.**  One homogeneous
treatment space `őī : Type` (with `[MeasurableSpace őī] [MeasurableSingletonClass őī]`)
shared by all stages, and per-stage state types `ő≥ : Fin n ‚Üí Type`.  A
treatment sequence is `dbar : Fin n ‚Üí őī`.  The `n = 2, őī = Bool`
specialization recovers the legacy two-stage statement (with
`dbar 0 = d‚āĀ` and `dbar 1 = d‚āā`).

**Per-sequence sequential exchangeability.**  Sequential exchangeability is
quantified per `dbar` (mirroring `def:po-dynamic-assumptions` literally),
avoiding the `Fin (|őī|^n)`-wide counterfactual bundle that the n=2
prototype used.  The conditioning ŌÉ-algebra at stage `k` is the full
ŌÉ-algebra of `historyBundle k` (the `{DŐĄ_{k-1} = dŐĄ_{k-1}}` qualifier from
the tex is dropped per the agreed simplification).

The code is split into five files: Setup / Helpers / Induction /
StrongCancellation / Main.  The dedicated `StrongCancellation.lean` file
houses the unified inductive predicate that merges the cancellation
identity with integrability of `innerReg`, needed under the (relaxed)
*pointwise* overlap hypothesis.

### `DTR/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ Data layer (structure, regimes, bundles, assumptions, observable functionals)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PODTRSystem P n őī ő≥` | `structure { S, D, Y, hDmeas, hYreal, distinctSS, distinctDD, distinctSD, distinctSY, distinctDY }` | General-`n` DTR subsystem: state covariates `S : ‚ąÄ k : Fin n, POVar P (ő≥ k)`, treatments `D : Fin n ‚Üí P.V` with `hDmeas k : P.X (D k) ‚ČÉŠĶź őī`, real outcome `Y` with `hYreal : P.X Y ‚ČÉŠĶź ‚ĄĚ`, and five distinctness fields covering the `2n + 1` declared variables (state-state injectivity, treatment-treatment injectivity, S-D, S-Y, D-Y disjointness) | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.dVar S k` | `POVar P őī` | `POVar` wrapper around `S.D k`: `‚ü®S.D k, S.hDmeas k‚ü©` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.yVar S` | `POVar P ‚ĄĚ` | `POVar` wrapper around `S.Y`: `‚ü®S.Y, S.hYreal‚ü©` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.regimeTarget S k` | `Finset P.V` | The set `{D 0, ‚Ä¶, D (k-1)}` (clamped if `k > n`); the `target` of `regimeUpTo dbar k` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.regimeTarget_mem_iff` | ‚ÄĒ | `v ‚ąą regimeTarget k ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ i : Fin n, i.val < k ‚ąß v = S.D i` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.regimeUpToAux S dbar k h` | `{ r : Regime P.V P.X // r.target = S.regimeTarget k }` | Subtype-bundled regime carrying its target proof; recursion on `k` builds via `Regime.sqcup` of one fresh `Regime.single (S.D ‚ü®k, _‚ü©) _` and the smaller regime, with disjointness discharged from `S.distinctDD` and the carried target equation | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.regimeUpTo S dbar k h` | `Regime P.V P.X` | The regime `{D 0 ‚Üź dbar 0, ‚Ä¶, D (k-1) ‚Üź dbar (k-1)}`; thin `.1` projection of `regimeUpToAux` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.regimeUpTo_target_eq` | `(S.regimeUpTo dbar k h).target = S.regimeTarget k` | `.2` projection from the subtype bundle | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.regime S dbar` | `Regime P.V P.X` | The full-stage regime `regimeUpTo dbar n (le_refl n)` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.Y_of S dbar` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(dbar) := S.yVar.cf (S.regime dbar)` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.S_of S dbar k` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ő≥ k` | Counterfactual stage-`k` state under the first `k.val` interventions: `(S.S k).cf (S.regimeUpTo dbar k.val _)` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.{factualD k, factualY, factualS k}` | `P.ő© ‚Üí őī` / `‚ĄĚ` / `ő≥ k` | Factual treatments / outcome / states (each variable's `.factual`) | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.measurable_*` | ‚ÄĒ | One-liner measurability of the counterfactual / factual maps above | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.indD S dbar k` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Recursive joint indicator `1_{D 0 = dbar 0 ‚ąß ‚Ä¶ ‚ąß D (k-1) = dbar (k-1)}`: `indD 0 = 1`, `indD (k+1) = indD k ¬∑ (S.dVar ‚ü®k, _‚ü©).indicator (dbar ‚ü®k, _‚ü©)` (when `k < n`) | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.measurable_indD` | `Measurable (S.indD dbar k)` | Measurability of the joint indicator (induction on `k`) | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.historyBundle S k h` | `POCFBundle P` (requires `k < n`) | History conditioning bundle at stage `k`: cons-list `(S k, D (k-1), S (k-1), D (k-2), ‚Ä¶, S 0)`.  At `k = 0` it is `(S 0,)`; at general `k` it conses `S k` and `D (k-1)` on top of `historyBundle (k - 1)`.  Matches the legacy two-stage convention (`historyBundle 0 = old historyBundle‚āĀ`, `historyBundle 1 = old historyBundle‚āā`). | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.cfYBundle S dbar` | `POCFBundle P` | Singleton CF bundle `[Y(dbar)]` used as the subject of per-sequence sequential exchangeability | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, exch, overlap, integrable_Y, integrable_factualY }` | Sequential backdoor assumptions (PO level, general `n`): consistency, **per-sequence** sequential exchangeability `‚ąÄ dbar, ‚ąÄ k : Fin n, P.CondIndepCFBundle (RegimedVar.ofFactual (S.dVar k)) (S.cfYBundle dbar) (S.historyBundle k.val k.isLt) P.őľ`, **pointwise** overlap `‚ąÄ dbar, ‚ąÄ k, ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ, 0 < (S.historyBundle k.val k.isLt).condExpGiven ((S.dVar k).indicator (dbar k)) P.őľ ŌČ` (relaxed from `‚ąÉ c > 0, c < condExpGiven ...` to pointwise positivity), integrability of every `Y(dbar)` and `factualY` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.innerReg S dbar j` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Recursive iterated-CE-ratio chain.  `j = 0` is the innermost: `(historyBundle (n-1)).condExpGiven (factualY ¬∑ indD n) / condExpGiven (indD n)`.  `j + 1` adds one outer CE-ratio at `historyBundle (n - j - 2)` with extra factor `(S.dVar ‚ü®n - j - 2, _‚ü©).indicator (dbar ‚ü®n - j - 2, _‚ü©)`.  For `n = 0`, returns `0`. | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.dtrEffect S dbar` | `‚ĄĚ` | Mean potential outcome `E[Y(dbar)] := ‚ąę Y_of dbar ‚ąāP.őľ` | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.adjustedDtr S dbar` | `‚ĄĚ` | Integrated adjusted DTR functional `‚ąę innerReg dbar (n - 1) ‚ąāP.őľ` (observable side); `0` when `n = 0` | **Defined** |

### `DTR/Helpers.lean` ‚ÄĒ Auxiliary measurability, integrability, ŌÉ-algebra-comparison lemmas

These helpers are not exposed publicly beyond the proofs in
`DTR/Induction.lean` and `DTR/Main.lean`, but cannot be `private` since
they are split across files.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PODTRSystem.hb_step_proj S k h` | `(‚ąÄ i, (historyBundle (k+1) h).type i) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ j, (historyBundle k _).type j` | One-step projection from the stage-`(k+1)` joint value to the stage-`k` joint value (forgets the freshly-consed `(S (k+1), D k)` coordinates) | **Defined** |
| `PODTRSystem.measurable_hb_step_proj` | `Measurable (S.hb_step_proj k h)` | Measurability of the projection | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.hb_jointValue_step_eq` | `(historyBundle k _).jointValue = hb_step_proj k h ‚ąė (historyBundle (k+1) h).jointValue` | Factorisation of the smaller history's joint value through the projection | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.historyBundle_sigma_mono_step` | `(historyBundle k _).sigma ‚Č§ (historyBundle (k+1) h).sigma` | One-step ŌÉ-algebra inclusion; needed for the `ŌÉ_k ‚Üí ŌÉ_{k+1}` tower in `cdtr_step` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.historyBundle_sigma_mono` | `(historyBundle j (lt_of_le_of_lt hjk hk)).sigma ‚Č§ (historyBundle k hk).sigma` for `j ‚Č§ k` | Iterated ŌÉ-algebra monotonicity | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.indD_eq_zero_or_one` | `‚ąÄ k ŌČ, S.indD dbar k ŌČ = 0 ‚ą® S.indD dbar k ŌČ = 1` | Pointwise: the joint indicator is 0/1-valued; recursion on `k` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.indD_integrable` | `Integrable (S.indD dbar k) P.őľ` | `1_{D 0 = dbar 0 ‚ąß ‚Ä¶}` integrable on a finite measure (bounded by 1) | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.indD_eq_indicator_event` | `S.indD dbar k = ({ŌČ \| ‚ąÄ i : Fin n, i.val < k ‚Üí S.factualD i ŌČ = dbar i}).indicator (fun _ ‚Ü¶ 1)` | Bridge from the recursive `if`-form to the set-indicator form on the agreement event; required to apply `factual_mul_indicator_eq_cf_mul_indicator` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.indD_factor_split` | `S.indD dbar (k+1) = fun ŌČ => S.indD dbar k ŌČ * (S.dVar ‚ü®k, hk‚ü©).indicator (dbar ‚ü®k, hk‚ü©) ŌČ` | Pointwise factorisation `indD (k+1) = indD k ¬∑ (single-stage indicator)` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.measurable_factualS_sigma_history` | `Measurable[(S.historyBundle m hm).sigma] (S.factualS k')` for `k'.val ‚Č§ m` | `S k'` lives in the stage-`m` history bundle when `k' ‚Č§ m`; coordinate-projection witness | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.measurable_factualD_sigma_history` | `Measurable[(S.historyBundle m hm).sigma] (S.factualD k')` for `k'.val < m` | `D k'` lives in the stage-`m` history bundle when `k' < m` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.stronglyMeasurable_indicator_dVar_sigma_history` | `StronglyMeasurable[(S.historyBundle m hm).sigma] ((S.dVar k').indicator x)` for `k'.val < m` | Real-valued indicator pull-out hypothesis used in `cdtr_step` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.stronglyMeasurable_indD_sigma_history` | `StronglyMeasurable[(S.historyBundle m hm).sigma] (S.indD dbar m')` for `m' ‚Č§ m` | The joint indicator at depth `m'` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the stage-`m` history when `m' ‚Č§ m` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.factualAgrees_regimeUpTo` | `(‚ąÄ i : Fin n, i.val < k ‚Üí S.factualD i ŌČ = dbar i) ‚Üí P.FactualAgrees (S.regimeUpTo dbar k h) ŌČ` | Inductive consistency: ŌČ agrees with the partial regime when the first `k` factual treatments match `dbar`; recursion mirrors `regimeUpTo` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.factualAgrees_regime` | `‚ąÄ ŌČ ‚ąą {ŌČ \| ‚ąÄ i : Fin n, S.factualD i ŌČ = dbar i}, P.FactualAgrees (S.regime dbar) ŌČ` | Multi-target consistency hypothesis; corollary of `factualAgrees_regimeUpTo` at `k = n` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.indD_mul_Y_integrable` | `Integrable (fun ŌČ => S.indD dbar k ŌČ * S.Y_of dbar ŌČ) P.őľ` (given `Integrable (S.Y_of dbar)`) | Bound by `\|Y_of dbar\|`; needed by step_3-style pull-outs | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.measurable_innerReg` | `Measurable (S.innerReg dbar j)` | Measurability of `innerReg` at every depth (recursion on `j`) | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.yVar_notMem_regime` | `S.yVar.v ‚ąČ (S.regime dbar).target` | `Y` is not a target of the full regime (which only names the `D` variables); discharged via `regimeUpTo_target_eq` and `distinctDY` | **Proved** |

(Integrability of `innerReg dbar j` is now in `DTR/StrongCancellation.lean`,
derived from the strengthened cancellation identity rather than from a
direct uniform-`c` bound.)

### `DTR/Induction.lean` ‚ÄĒ Base case and inductive step

The backward-induction identity (‚ėÖ at depth `j`) is

    innerReg dbar j ¬∑ indD dbar (n-1-j)
      =ŠĶź[őľ]
    indD dbar (n-1-j) ¬∑ (historyBundle (n-1-j)).condExpGiven (Y_of dbar)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PODTRSystem.cdtr_base` | `[StandardBorelSpace P.ő©] [IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ]`, `S.Assumptions`, `(dbar : Fin n ‚Üí őī)`, `0 < n` ‚Üí (‚ėÖ at `j = 0`) | **Innermost-stage cancellation** (`j = 0`).  Generalizes `step_1` of the legacy n=2 proof.  Chain: consistency rewrite `factualY ¬∑ indD n =ŠĶź Y_of ¬∑ indD n` (via `indD_eq_indicator_event` + `factualAgrees_regime` + `POVar.factual_mul_indicator_eq_cf_mul_indicator` + `yVar_notMem_regime`); factorisation `indD n = indD (n-1) ¬∑ (dVar ‚ü®n-1‚ü©).indicator` via `indD_factor_split`; pull-out of `indD (n-1)` from the numerator condExp (ŌÉ-strongly-measurable via `stronglyMeasurable_indD_sigma_history`); stage-`(n-1)` exchangeability via `CondIndepCFBundle.project` + `condExp_mul_of_condIndep`; pointwise cancellation case-splitting on `indD_eq_zero_or_one`, with overlap_{n-1} discharging the nonzero branch | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.cdtr_step` | `[StandardBorelSpace P.ő©] [IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ]`, `S.Assumptions`, `(dbar : Fin n ‚Üí őī)`, `(j : ‚Ąē)`, `j + 1 < n`, `n - j - 2 < n`, `Integrable (S.innerReg dbar j) P.őľ`, IH (‚ėÖ at `j`) ‚Üí (‚ėÖ at `j + 1`) | **Inductive step** (`j ‚Üí j + 1`).  Generalizes `step_2`‚Äď`step_4` of the legacy n=2 proof.  Let `k := n - j - 2`.  Via `condExpRatio_eq_of_mul`: (A) numerator-denominator product identity using IH + `indD_factor_split` + reverse pull-out of `(dVar ‚ü®k‚ü©).indicator` inside `ŌÉ(historyBundle (k+1))` via `stronglyMeasurable_indicator_dVar_sigma_history` + tower down to `ŌÉ(historyBundle k)` via `historyBundle_sigma_mono_step` and `condExpGiven_tower_of_le` + stage-`k` exchangeability via `condExp_mul_of_condIndep`.  (B) Denominator nonvanishing via `overlap_k`.  Multiplied back by `indD k` on both sides.  The extra `Integrable (innerReg j)` hypothesis (added 2026-04-26 along with the pointwise-overlap relaxation) supplies the LHS pull-out integrability previously taken from `innerReg_integrable`. | **Proved** |

### `DTR/StrongCancellation.lean` ‚ÄĒ Strengthened cancellation identity (a.e. equality)

Under the **pointwise** overlap hypothesis, the proof of integrability of
`innerReg dbar j` is interlocked with the cancellation identity.  This
file proves a single unified inductive predicate

    P j ‚ÜĒ (innerReg dbar j =ŠĶź[őľ] indD dbar (n-1-j) ¬∑ őľ[Y_of dbar | ŌÉ_{n-1-j}])
          ‚ąß Integrable (innerReg dbar j) őľ

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PODTRSystem.cdtr_strong` | `[StandardBorelSpace P.ő©] [IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ]`, `S.Assumptions`, `(dbar : Fin n ‚Üí őī)`, `0 < n`, `‚ąÄ j < n` ‚Üí `(strong identity at j) ‚ąß Integrable (innerReg j)` | **Strengthened cancellation identity (joint with integrability).**  By induction on `j`: base case `j = 0` reuses `cdtr_base` for the weak form and case-splits on `indD m ‚ąą {0,1}` to upgrade to the strong form (vanishing on `{indD m = 0}` from numerator/denominator both factoring through `indD m`).  Step case `j ‚Üí j+1` calls `cdtr_step` with the integrability part of the IH, then upgrades via `indD_factor_split` and the strong IH to show `őľ[innerReg j ¬∑ ind_k | ŌÉ_k]` factors through `indD k`, hence `innerReg(j+1) =ŠĶź 0` on `{indD k = 0}`. | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.innerReg_integrable` | `[StandardBorelSpace P.ő©] [IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ]`, `S.Assumptions`, `j < n` ‚Üí `Integrable (S.innerReg dbar j) P.őľ` | Integrability of every iterated CE-ratio.  Re-derived from `cdtr_strong`'s second conjunct (replacing the previously sorry'd direct-bound proof in `Helpers.lean`). | **Proved** |

### `DTR/Main.lean` ‚ÄĒ Final theorems

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PODTRSystem.cdtr_iter` | `[StandardBorelSpace P.ő©] [IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ]`, `S.Assumptions`, `0 < n`, `‚ąÄ j < n` ‚Üí (‚ėÖ at `j`) | Backward-induction iterate combining `cdtr_base` (`j = 0`) and `cdtr_step` (`j ‚Üí j + 1`); proved by `Nat.rec` on `j`, with index-bridging via `omega`.  Threads the integrability hypothesis required by `cdtr_step` from `cdtr_strong`. | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.cdtr_backdoor` | `[StandardBorelSpace P.ő©] [IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ]`, `S.Assumptions`, `(dbar : Fin n ‚Üí őī)`, `0 < n` ‚Üí `(S.historyBundle 0 _).condExpGiven (S.Y_of dbar) P.őľ =ŠĶź[P.őľ] S.innerReg dbar (n - 1)` | **General-`n` CDTR backdoor identification.**  Specialise `cdtr_iter` at `j = n - 1`; the partial indicator `S.indD dbar 0 = fun _ => 1` collapses both `¬∑ indD 0` factors via `mul_one`/`one_mul` | **Proved** |
| `PODTRSystem.dtr_backdoor` | `[StandardBorelSpace P.ő©] [IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ]`, `S.Assumptions`, `(dbar : Fin n ‚Üí őī)`, `0 < n` ‚Üí `S.dtrEffect dbar = S.adjustedDtr dbar` | **General-`n` integrated DTR identification.**  `E[Y(dbar)] = ‚ąę innerReg dbar (n - 1)`.  Two-line corollary of `cdtr_backdoor` via `MeasureTheory.integral_condExp` and `integral_congr_ae` | **Proved** |

**Status / known gaps.**
- Both `cdtr_backdoor` and `dtr_backdoor` are unconditional **for arbitrary `n ‚Č• 1`**, modulo the standing project-wide `Mathlib.CondIndep` stubs (`condExp_mul_of_condIndep`) shared with ATE.lean and DID.lean.  No `sorry` in any of the four DTR files.
- The **nested-integral form** that matches `prop:po-dynamic-backdoor` literally in the `.tex` (`‚ąę ‚čĮ ‚ąę őľ[Y \| SŐĄ_n, DŐĄ_n=dŐĄ_n] dP(S_n \| SŐĄ_{n-1}, DŐĄ_{n-1}=dŐĄ_{n-1}) ‚čĮ dP(S_1)`) is *deferred*: it requires the not-yet-available `condDistrib`-as-kernel infrastructure (cf. `Causalean/Mathlib/CondDistrib.lean`, the *condDistrib ‚Üí Kernel.condKernel migration*).
- The remark in the tex about **history-dependent regimes** `d_k : (SŐĄ_k, DŐĄ_{k-1}) ‚Üí őī` is not formalized here; the same induction pattern would carry over once the corresponding regime-builder is available.

---

## 9i. `PO/ID/Exact/DID.lean` ‚ÄĒ Two-Period DID Identification of the ATT (PO framework)

Formalizes `def:po-did-system`, `def:po-did-assumptions`, and `prop:po-did-att`
from `Basic Concepts.tex` (lines 1693‚Äď1809): the two-period difference-in-differences
identification of the ATT in the bare potential-outcome framework.  Mirrors
`LATE.lean` / `ATE.lean` in style but is strictly simpler ‚ÄĒ no ŌÉ-algebra
conditional expectations, no independence assumption, no Wald ratio, no
monotonicity.  The whole file lives on the event-level `eventCondExp` layer
plus `POVar` consistency.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PODIDSystem P` | `structure { D, Y‚āÄ, Y‚āĀ, hDbool, hY0real, hY1real, hDY0, hDY1 }` | Two-period DID subsystem: binary treatment `D`, real pre/post outcomes `Y‚āÄ, Y‚āĀ`; `D` distinct from each outcome | **Defined** |
| `PODIDSystem.dVar`, `y0Var`, `y1Var` | `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P ‚ĄĚ` / `POVar P ‚ĄĚ` | `POVar` wrappers around `D`, `Y‚āÄ`, `Y‚āĀ` using the `hDbool` / `hY0real` / `hY1real` equivalences | **Defined** |
| `PODIDSystem.Y0ofD S d`, `Y1ofD S d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Counterfactual outcomes `Y‚āÄ(d) := y0Var.cfUnder dVar d`, `Y‚āĀ(d) := y1Var.cfUnder dVar d` | **Defined** |
| `PODIDSystem.factualD`, `factualY‚āÄ`, `factualY‚āĀ` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` / `‚ĄĚ` / `‚ĄĚ` | Factual treatment and outcomes | **Defined** |
| `PODIDSystem.dEvent S d` | `Set P.ő©` | Factual event `{D = d}`, defined as `S.dVar.event d` | **Defined** |
| `PODIDSystem.measurable_{Y0ofD, Y1ofD, factualD, factualY‚āÄ, factualY‚āĀ}` | `Measurable ‚Ä¶` | One-line delegations to `POVar.measurable_cfUnder` / `POVar.measurable_factual` | **Proved** |
| `PODIDSystem.measurableSet_dEvent` | `MeasurableSet (S.dEvent d)` | Measurability of the treatment event | **Proved** |
| `PODIDSystem.ATT` | `‚ĄĚ` | `eventCondExp P.őľ (dEvent true) (Y1ofD true - Y1ofD false)` ‚ÄĒ def:po-did-system | **Defined** |
| `PODIDSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, noAnticipation, parallelTrends, posTrue_{ne_zero,ne_top}, posFalse_{ne_zero,ne_top}, intY0ofD_false, intY1ofD_false, intY1ofD_true }` | def:po-did-assumptions; positivity split into four fields, parallel-trends stated directly on `eventCondExp` of `Y‚āĀ(0) ‚ąí Y‚āÄ(0)`, integrability required only for the three counterfactuals that actually appear | **Defined** |
| `PODIDSystem.factualDiff_eq_cfDiff_on_dEvent` (private) | `P.Consistency ‚Üí ‚ąÄ ŌČ ‚ąą dEvent d, factualY‚āĀ ŌČ - factualY‚āÄ ŌČ = Y1ofD d ŌČ - Y0ofD d ŌČ` | Pointwise consistency on `{D = d}`: combines `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` on `y1Var` and `y0Var` | **Proved** |
| `PODIDSystem.att_did` | `S.ATT = eventCondExp (dEvent true) (factualY‚āĀ - factualY‚āÄ) - eventCondExp (dEvent false) (factualY‚āĀ - factualY‚āÄ)` | DID identification of the ATT, prop:po-did-att; proof is a five-step `calc` on the event-level conditional expectation (no-anticipation split + additivity + two consistency rewrites + parallel-trends bridge) | **Proved** |

## 9i*. `PO/ID/Exact/CSDID.lean` ‚ÄĒ Callaway--Sant'Anna group-time DID identification

Formalizes `def:po-cs-did-system`, `def:po-cs-did-assumptions`, and
`prop:po-cs-did-att` from `Basic Concepts.tex` (lines 2439‚Äď2565): the
no-covariate Callaway--Sant'Anna staggered-adoption DID identification of the
group-time ATT(g, t) under irreversibility, no anticipation, and never-treated
parallel trends. Extends two-period `DID.lean` from one cohort + one
post-period to a finite horizon `T` with cohorts `G_g = {D_{g‚ąí1}=0, D_g=1}` and
never-treated event `C = ‚čā_s {D_s = 0}`. Periods are 0-indexed in Lean
(`Fin T` with `2 ‚Č§ T`), so the .tex bound `2 ‚Č§ g ‚Č§ t ‚Č§ T` becomes
`1 ‚Č§ g.val ‚Č§ t.val < T`. Proof structure mirrors `PODIDSystem.att_did` line by
line; the only genuinely new ingredients are (i) recursive construction of the
treatment-path regimes `regOf g`, `regNT` via iterated `Regime.sqcup`, and
(ii) reduction of `cohortEvent` plus irreversibility to `FactualAgrees (regOf g)`
almost surely.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POCSDIDSystem P` | `structure { T, hT : 2 ‚Č§ T, D Y : Fin T ‚Üí P.V, hDbool, hYreal, hDinj, hYinj, hDY }` | Staggered-adoption DID subsystem: real-valued outcomes `Y s` and binary treatments `D s` for `s : Fin T`; `D`/`Y` injective and disjoint as node families | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.dVar`, `yVar` | `Fin S.T ‚Üí POVar P Bool` / `Fin S.T ‚Üí POVar P ‚ĄĚ` | Per-period `POVar` wrappers around `D s`, `Y s` | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.factualD`, `factualY`, `dEvent` | `Fin S.T ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` / `Fin S.T ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` / `Fin S.T ‚Üí Bool ‚Üí Set P.ő©` | Factual treatment / outcome at period `s`; factual event `{D s = b}` | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.measurable_factualD`, `measurable_factualY`, `measurableSet_dEvent` | `Measurable ‚Ä¶` / `MeasurableSet ‚Ä¶` | One-line delegations to the `POVar` API | **Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.dTargetUpTo` | `POCSDIDSystem P ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí Finset P.V` | Recursive standalone target: image of `S.D` on indices `< k`; lets us prove disjointness for the next `Regime.sqcup` step | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.dTargetUpTo_mem_iff` | `v ‚ąą S.dTargetUpTo k ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ i : Fin S.T, i.val < k ‚ąß v = S.D i` | Membership characterisation of `dTargetUpTo`, used to drive injectivity arguments | **Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.regUpToAux` | `(b : Fin S.T ‚Üí Bool) ‚Üí (k ‚Č§ S.T) ‚Üí { r : Regime // r.target = S.dTargetUpTo k }` | Subtype-valued recursive auxiliary that builds a regime over the first `k` periods together with its target equality (mirrors `PODTRSystem.regimeUpToAux`) | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.regimeBy`, `regimeBy_target_eq` | `(Fin S.T ‚Üí Bool) ‚Üí Regime P.V P.X` / `(S.regimeBy b).target = S.dTargetUpTo S.T` | Full-horizon regime parameterised by a Bool assignment, with target equality | **Defined / Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.regOf`, `regNT` | `Fin S.T ‚Üí Regime P.V P.X` / `Regime P.V P.X` | Cohort regime (`D s = 1` for `g.val ‚Č§ s.val`, else `0`) and never-treated regime (`D s = 0` always) | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.Y_notin_dTargetUpTo`, `Y_notin_regimeBy`, `yVar_v_notin_regOf_target`, `yVar_v_notin_regNT_target` | `S.Y t ‚ąČ ‚Ä¶` / `(S.yVar t).v ‚ąČ ‚Ä¶` | The outcome node `Y t` lies outside any `regimeBy`/`regOf`/`regNT` target ‚ÄĒ required precondition for `cf_eq_factual_of_factualAgrees` | **Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.YofCohort`, `YofNT` | `Fin S.T ‚Üí Fin S.T ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` / `Fin S.T ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Counterfactual outcomes `Y_t(g)` and `Y_t(‚ąě)` as `cf` under the corresponding regimes | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.measurable_YofCohort`, `measurable_YofNT` | `Measurable ‚Ä¶` | Measurability of the counterfactuals | **Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.predFin` | `(g : Fin S.T) ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ g.val ‚Üí Fin S.T` | Predecessor index `g ‚ąí 1` as `Fin S.T`; hypothesis is a precondition marker (body uses truncating ‚Ąē subtraction) | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.cohortEvent`, `measurableSet_cohortEvent` | `(g : Fin S.T) ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ g.val ‚Üí Set P.ő©` / `MeasurableSet ‚Ä¶` | Cohort event `G_g = {D_{g‚ąí1}=0} ‚ą© {D_g=1}`; measurability | **Defined / Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.neverTreatedEvent`, `measurableSet_neverTreatedEvent` | `Set P.ő©` / `MeasurableSet ‚Ä¶` | Never-treated event `C = ‚čā_s {D_s = 0}`; measurability | **Defined / Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.ATT` | `(g t : Fin S.T) ‚Üí 1 ‚Č§ g.val ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | `eventCondExp P.őľ (cohortEvent g hg) (Y_t(g) ‚ąí Y_t(‚ąě))` ‚ÄĒ def:po-cs-did-system | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, irreversibilityBase, irreversibilityStep, noAnticipation, parallelTrends, posCohort, posNT, intYofCohort, intYofNT }` | def:po-cs-did-assumptions; irreversibility split into base case (`D_0 = 0` a.s.) and absorbing step (`D_s = 1 ‚üĻ D_{s+1} = 1` a.s.); parallel-trends stated as a long difference on `Y_t(‚ąě) ‚ąí Y_{g‚ąí1}(‚ąě)`; positivity and integrability required for all admissible cohort-period pairs | **Defined** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.factualY_eq_YofCohort_on_cohortEvent` (private) | `S.Assumptions ‚Üí ‚ąÄ g t hg, ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ ‚ąāP.őľ, ŌČ ‚ąą cohortEvent g hg ‚Üí YofCohort t g ŌČ = factualY t ŌČ` | A.e. cohort consistency: on `cohortEvent g`, irreversibility forces the factual D-path to agree with `regOf g`, and consistency then gives `Y_t(g) = factual Y_t`. Filled by Codex (gpt-5.5 high) using the `regUpToAux` induction template plus forward/backward irreversibility chains | **Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.factualY_eq_YofNT_on_NT` (private) | `S.Assumptions ‚Üí ‚ąÄ t, ‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ ‚ąāP.őľ, ŌČ ‚ąą neverTreatedEvent ‚Üí YofNT t ŌČ = factualY t ŌČ` | Pointwise NT consistency: on `neverTreatedEvent`, the D-path matches `regNT` directly (no irreversibility needed); apply consistency. Filled by Codex | **Proved** |
| `POCSDIDSystem.att_csdid` | `S.Assumptions ‚Üí ‚ąÄ g t hg hgt, S.ATT g t hg = eventCondExp (cohortEvent g hg) (factualY t ‚ąí factualY (predFin g hg)) ‚ąí eventCondExp neverTreatedEvent (factualY t ‚ąí factualY (predFin g hg))` | Callaway--Sant'Anna group-time DID identification of `ATT(g, t)`, prop:po-cs-did-att. Five-step proof mirroring `PODIDSystem.att_did` (no-anticipation split, additivity, cohort consistency, parallel-trends bridge, NT consistency). Filled by Codex | **Proved** |

## 9i0. `PO/Analysis/Regression.lean` ‚ÄĒ Regression-Function Representatives

Shared predicate `IsRegressionFunction` and derived lemmas, extracted from the RDD
files so SharpRDD and FuzzyRDD can both use the same interface without duplication.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsRegressionFunction őľ X g f` | `Measure ő© ‚Üí (ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | `f : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` is a regression-function representative of `g` over `X`: measurability of `f`, integrability of `g` and of `f ‚ąė X`, and the integral identity `‚ąę_{X‚ĀĽ¬ĻA} g = ‚ąę_{X‚ĀĽ¬ĻA} f ‚ąė X` for all measurable `A` | **Defined** |
| `IsRegressionFunction.integrable_pushforward` | `IsRegressionFunction őľ X g f ‚Üí AEMeasurable X őľ ‚Üí Integrable f (őľ.map X)` | `f` is integrable under the pushforward of `X` whenever `X` is AE-measurable | **Proved** |
| `IsRegressionFunction.sub` | `IsRegressionFunction őľ X g‚āĀ f‚āĀ ‚Üí IsRegressionFunction őľ X g‚āā f‚āā ‚Üí IsRegressionFunction őľ X (g‚āĀ - g‚āā) (f‚āĀ - f‚āā)` | Linearity: difference of representatives is the representative of the difference | **Proved** |
| `IsRegressionFunction.aeEq_of_aeEq_response` | `g‚āĀ =ŠĶź[őľ] g‚āā ‚Üí IsRegressionFunction őľ X g‚āĀ f‚āĀ ‚Üí IsRegressionFunction őľ X g‚āā f‚āā ‚Üí f‚āĀ =ŠĶź[őľ.map X] f‚āā` | If two response variables are ae-equal, their regression representatives agree ae on the pushforward | **Proved** |

## 9i0a. `Panel/Analysis/Residualization.lean` ‚ÄĒ Population Residualization Core (FWL framework)

Framework primitives for the **population estimand-characterization layer**: a linear `L¬≤` nuisance class, a residualization witness `(V_H, ŠĻľ)` decomposing a square-integrable variable against the class, the residualized population coefficient `ő≤_H(Y, D) = E[DŐÉ¬∑ŠĽł] / E[DŐÉ¬≤]`, and the **population Frisch‚ÄďWaugh‚ÄďLovell theorem**. Pure measure-theoretic; no `POSystem`/`CausalModel`/`SWIGGraph` dependence. Downstream paper-specific files (SŇāoczyŇĄski saturated OLS, TWFE decompositions, event-study, 2SLS, ‚Ä¶) instantiate `ResidualizationWitness` for their nuisance class (controls, unit + time fixed effects, cohort effects, ‚Ä¶) and reduce to the FWL ratio via this theorem. Specifies `def:po-estimand-residualization-witness`, `def:po-estimand-residualized-coefficient`, and `prop:po-estimand-fwl` from `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/residualization_core.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `LinearL2Class őľ` | `structure { mem : (ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop, memLp, zero_mem, add_mem, smul_mem }` | A linear class of `MemLp 2 őľ` real-valued functions on `ő©`, predicate-style: `mem` carves out the underlying set, with closure under scalar multiplication and addition, and every member required to be square-integrable | **Defined** |
| `ResidualizationWitness őľ H V` | `structure { VH, Vtilde : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ, VH_mem, Vtilde_memLp, decomp : V =ŠĶź[őľ] VH + Vtilde, orthogonal }` | Population residualization witness for `V` against `H`: bundles the in-class projection `V_H ‚ąą H`, the L¬≤-residual `ŠĻľ`, the a.e. decomposition `V = V_H + ŠĻľ`, and population orthogonality `‚ąÄ h ‚ąą H, ‚ąę ŠĻľ¬∑h dőľ = 0` | **Defined** |
| `residualizedCoefficient őľ H wY wD` | `noncomputable def : ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized population coefficient `ő≤_H(Y, D) := (‚ąę DŐÉ¬∑ŠĽł dőľ) / (‚ąę DŐÉ¬∑DŐÉ dőľ)`, the population Frisch‚ÄďWaugh‚ÄďLovell representation of the coefficient on `D` in a linear projection that also includes the nuisance terms `H`. Positivity of the denominator is supplied at theorem-use time | **Defined** |
| `residualizedCoefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` | `(Y_memLp) (D_memLp) (wY) (wD) (hDtilde_pos : 0 < ‚ąę DŐÉ¬≤) (ő≤) (Hő≤) (Hő≤_mem) (h_normal_D) (h_normal_H) ‚Üí ő≤ = residualizedCoefficient őľ H wY wD` | **Population Frisch‚ÄďWaugh‚ÄďLovell theorem** (`prop:po-estimand-fwl`): if `ő≤` and `Hő≤ ‚ąą H` jointly satisfy the population normal equations for projecting `Y` on `D` and `H` (`‚ąę (Y ‚ąí ő≤D ‚ąí Hő≤)¬∑D = 0` and `‚ąÄ h ‚ąą H, ‚ąę (Y ‚ąí ő≤D ‚ąí Hő≤)¬∑h = 0`), and `E[DŐÉ¬≤] > 0`, then `ő≤` equals the residualized coefficient. Proof: build `h* = wY.VH ‚ąí ő≤¬∑wD.VH ‚ąí Hő≤ ‚ąą H`, derive `‚ąę e¬∑DŐÉ = 0` from the two normal equations and `wD.decomp`, expand `e = h* + (ŠĽł ‚ąí ő≤¬∑DŐÉ)` a.e., use `wD.orthogonal h*` to kill `h*`, divide by `hDtilde_pos.ne'` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-CellBridge. `Panel/CellBridge.lean` ‚ÄĒ Generic Finite Indicator-Span Bridges

Reusable probability-space infrastructure for finite cell partitions in the estimand-characterization layer. The module hoists the one-axis saturated indicator class used by SŇāoczyŇĄski and the two-axis cohort+period class used by Goodman-Bacon, plus shared cell-mass, cell-mean, finite-partition integral, and indicator-span orthogonality lemmas.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CellBridge.cellIndicator` | `(X : ő© ‚Üí őĻ) ‚Üí őĻ ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Real-valued singleton-cell indicator `ūĚüô{X = i}` | **Defined** |
| `CellBridge.indicatorSpan` | `Measure ő© ‚Üí (X : ő© ‚Üí őĻ) ‚Üí Measurable X ‚Üí LinearL2Class őľ` | One-axis finite linear `L¬≤` span of singleton indicators `ūĚüô{X = i}` | **Defined** |
| `CellBridge.twoAxisIndicatorSpan` | `Measure ő© ‚Üí (X : ő© ‚Üí őĻ) ‚Üí (Z : ő© ‚Üí őļ) ‚Üí Measurable X ‚Üí Measurable Z ‚Üí LinearL2Class őľ` | Two-axis finite linear `L¬≤` span of `X`-cell and `Z`-cell indicators, used for cohort+period nuisance classes | **Defined** |
| `CellBridge.cellMass` / `jointCellMass` / `cellMean` | `(őľ, X, i)` / `(őľ, X, Z, i, k)` / `(őľ, F, X, i) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Generic finite cell mass, joint cell mass, and totalized cell mean helpers | **Defined** |
| `CellBridge.indicator_cell_memLp` | `IsProbabilityMeasure őľ ‚Üí Measurable X ‚Üí MemLp (cell indicator) 2 őľ` | Cell indicators are square-integrable under a probability measure | **Proved** |
| `CellBridge.integral_cell_indicator_one_eq_cellMass` | `‚ąę ūĚüô{X=i} dőľ = cellMass őľ X i` | Integral of a singleton-cell indicator equals its real cell mass | **Proved** |
| `CellBridge.cell_integral_div_mul_cellMass` / `cellMean_mul_cellMass` | `((‚ąę F¬∑ūĚüô{X=i}) / cellMass) * cellMass = ‚ąę F¬∑ūĚüô{X=i}` | Dividing by cell mass and multiplying back recovers the raw indicator-weighted integral, including zero-mass cells by totalized division | **Proved** |
| `CellBridge.integral_mul_indicatorSpan_eq_zero_of_cell` | `‚ąÄ i, ‚ąę V¬∑ūĚüô{X=i}=0 ‚Üí ‚ąę V¬∑(ő£ c_iūĚüô{X=i})=0` | Cell-wise orthogonality implies orthogonality to the whole one-axis indicator span | **Proved** |
| `CellBridge.integral_mul_twoAxisIndicatorSpan_eq_zero_of_axes` | `‚ąÄ i, ‚ąę V¬∑ūĚüô{X=i}=0 ‚Üí ‚ąÄ k, ‚ąę V¬∑ūĚüô{Z=k}=0 ‚Üí ‚ąę V¬∑h=0` | Per-axis orthogonality implies orthogonality to a two-axis indicator-span representative | **Proved** |
| `CellBridge.integral_eq_sum_cell` | `Integrable F őľ ‚Üí ‚ąę F dőľ = ő£_i ‚ąę F¬∑ūĚüô{X=i} dőľ` | Integrates an integrable function by summing over finite cells | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-AdoptionPath. `Panel/AdoptionPath.lean` ‚ÄĒ Shared Staggered-Adoption Path Helpers

Paper-agnostic helpers for adoption paths encoded as `WithTop (Fin T)`, with `‚ä§` representing never-treated. Sun-Abraham's path helpers are compatibility wrappers around these declarations; Goodman-Bacon's `AdoptionDate` namespace uses the same raw `WithTop` encoding for proof stability.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `AdoptionPath.finite` | `Fin T ‚Üí WithTop (Fin T)` | Embeds a finite adoption period into the shared adoption-path type | **Defined** |
| `AdoptionPath.le` / `lt` | `WithTop (Fin T) ‚Üí Fin T ‚Üí Prop` | Treated-by-period and untreated-before-adoption predicates for finite periods | **Defined** |
| `AdoptionPath.isFinite` / `isInfinite` | `WithTop (Fin T) ‚Üí Prop` | Eventually-treated and never-treated path predicates | **Defined** |
| `AdoptionPath.isNeverTreated` / `isEventuallyTreated` | `WithTop (Fin T) ‚Üí Prop` | Sun-Abraham-compatible names for infinite and finite adoption paths | **Defined** |
| `AdoptionPath.absorbingTreatment` | `WithTop (Fin T) ‚Üí Fin T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Absorbing treatment path `1{A ‚Č§ t}`, totalized so `‚ä§` is untreated in every finite period | **Defined** |
| `AdoptionPath.not_le_of_lt` / `lt_of_isInfinite` | order helpers | `t < A` rules out `A ‚Č§ t`, and never-treated paths are untreated in every finite period | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-VariableIntensityIV. `PO/ID/Exact/VariableIntensityIV/{OrderedTreatment,VariableIntensity}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Variable-Intensity IV ACR (Angrist-Imbens)

Specific-paper scaffold for Angrist-Imbens (1995) variable treatment intensity and average causal response, formulated **inside the potential-outcome framework** (`POSystem`): the instrument, treatment intensity, and outcome are system variables; potential intensities `D(z)` and treatment-indexed potential outcomes `Y(d)` are genuine `POVar.cfUnder` counterfactuals; consistency (`D=D(Z)`, `Y=Y(D)`) is the canonical `POSystem.Consistency` and instrument independence is `POSystem.IndepCF` over a counterfactual bundle (not ad-hoc structure fields). `OrderedTreatment.lean` holds the finite ordered-intensity algebra (adjacent margins, crossing indicators, telescoping identities) directly ‚ÄĒ its sole consumer is `VariableIntensity.lean` in this folder, so it lives here rather than under `Panel` ‚ÄĒ and re-exports the shared generic `NormalizedWeights` normalized-weight helpers. `VariableIntensity.lean` packages a directed instrument-cell contrast, crossing events, indicator-weighted ACR, conditional-margin ACR, Wald/2SLS interface definitions, and the headline ACR characterization statements. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/angrist_imbens_variable_intensity.md`.

### `VariableIntensityIV/OrderedTreatment.lean`

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `OrderedTreatment.lowerLevel` / `upperLevel` | `Fin J ‚Üí Fin (J+1)` | Lower and upper treatment levels for the margin `j ‚Üí j+1` | **Defined** |
| `OrderedTreatment.intensityValue` | `Fin (J+1) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Real-valued numeric representation of an ordered treatment level | **Defined** |
| `OrderedTreatment.marginIncrement` | `(Fin (J+1) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Fin J ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Finite-difference increment `f(j+1)-f(j)` on a treatment margin | **Defined** |
| `OrderedTreatment.Crossing` | `Fin (J+1) ‚Üí Fin (J+1) ‚Üí Fin J ‚Üí Prop` | Predicate that a movement from level `a` to level `b` crosses margin `j ‚Üí j+1` | **Defined** |
| `OrderedTreatment.crossingIndicator` | `Fin (J+1) ‚Üí Fin (J+1) ‚Üí Fin J ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Real-valued indicator of the crossed-margin predicate | **Defined** |
| `OrderedTreatment.normalizedWeight` | `(őĻ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí őĻ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Generic finite normalized weight `a_i / ő£_k a_k` | **Defined** |
| `OrderedTreatment.ordered_telescope_indicator` | `a ‚Č§ b ‚Üí f b - f a = ő£_j marginIncrement f j * crossingIndicator a b j` | Ordered finite telescoping identity over crossed margins, proved by reindexing to the half-open Nat interval `[a.val,b.val)` and applying `Finset.sum_Ico_sub` | **Proved** |
| `OrderedTreatment.ordered_telescope_identity` | `a ‚Č§ b ‚Üí intensityValue b - intensityValue a = ő£_j crossingIndicator a b j` | Identity-function version of the ordered telescoping lemma, equating intensity movement with crossed-margin count | **Proved** |
| `OrderedTreatment.normalizedWeight_nonneg` | `(‚ąÄ i, 0 ‚Č§ a i) ‚Üí 0 < ő£_i a_i ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ normalizedWeight a i` | Nonnegativity of normalized finite weights from nonnegative raw weights and positive total mass | **Proved** |
| `OrderedTreatment.sum_normalizedWeight_eq_one` | `0 < ő£_i a_i ‚Üí ő£_i normalizedWeight a i = 1` | Normalized finite weights sum to one when the normalizing total is positive | **Proved** |

| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.ValidContrastAssumptions` | `ūĚíĶ ‚Üí ūĚíĶ ‚Üí Prop` | Assumption bundle for IV validity: `POSystem.Consistency`, `POSystem.IndepCF` instrument independence, directed monotonicity, positive first stage, and outcome integrability (exclusion is structural, not a field) | **Defined** |

| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.firstStage_eq_sum_crossingProb` | `firstStageContrast z0 z1 = ő£_j crossingProb z0 z1 j` | First-stage denominator equals total crossed-margin probability, using monotonicity, ordered-treatment telescoping, and indicator integrals over crossing events | **Proved** |

| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.sum_crossingWeight_eq_one` | `ValidContrastAssumptions z0 z1 ‚Üí ő£_j crossingWeight z0 z1 j = 1` | ACR crossing weights sum to one under positive first stage | **Proved** |
| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.reducedForm_eq_sum_crossingEffects` | `reducedFormContrast z0 z1 = ő£_j indicatorWeightedEffect z0 z1 j` | Reduced-form contrast decomposes into indicator-weighted crossed-margin causal responses via pointwise ordered telescoping and finite integral exchange | **Proved** |

| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.wald_eq_averageCausalResponse` | `wald z0 z1 = averageCausalResponse z0 z1` | Headline Angrist-Imbens ACR characterization for a valid directed contrast with positive instrument cells; reduces factual Wald cells to potential first-stage/reduced-form contrasts via `POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF` (consistency + `IndepCF` drop-of-conditioning over the counterfactual bundle), then applies the ordered-treatment algebra | **Proved** |
| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.SpecialCases.binaryMargin` | `J = 1 ‚Üí Fin J` | Canonical single treatment margin obtained by transporting `0 : Fin 1` under the binary-intensity equality | **Defined** |
| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.SpecialCases.wald_eq_late_of_binaryIntensity` | `J = 1 ‚Üí wald z0 z1 = eventCondExp őľ (crossingEvent z0 z1 (binaryMargin h)) (marginResponse (binaryMargin h))` | Binary-treatment single-margin specialization, corresponding to the LATE-style complier event on the unique crossing margin | **Proved** |
| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.SpecialCases.crossingEvent_eq_complianceEvent` | `J = 1 ‚Üí crossingEvent z0 z1 (binaryMargin h) = {ŌČ \| DofZ z1 ŌČ = Fin.last J ‚ąß DofZ z0 ŌČ = 0}` | Under `J=1` the unique crossing event equals the LATE complier event `{D(z1)=top ‚ąß D(z0)=0}` (Fin.last 1 = 1), making `wald_eq_late_of_binaryIntensity` explicitly the Imbens-Angrist (1994) binary LATE | **Proved** |

| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.SpecialCases.wald_eq_marginResponseAverage` | `(‚ąÄ j, m j = conditionalMarginResponse z0 z1 j) ‚Üí wald z0 z1 = ő£_j crossingWeight z0 z1 j * m j` | Margin-specific response average restatement of the headline ACR characterization | **Proved** |

| `VariableIntensityIVSystem.TwoSLSContrastDecomposition` | `structure { contrastWeight, pairFirstStage, pairReducedForm, ... }` | Deferred interface for expanding multivalued-instrument 2SLS into signed ordered cell contrasts | **Defined** |

## 9i0a-MultipleInstrumentIV. `PO/ID/Exact/MultipleInstrumentIV/{FiniteIndex,ResponseTypes,Population,Main,POBridge}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Multiple-Instrument IV (Mogstad-Torgovitsky-Walters)

Specific-paper finite-support algebra for the MTW multiple-IV characterization.  This layer works with an ordered saturated first-stage index, a finite matrix first-stage projection, support masses, tail coefficients, binary response types, response-type effects, a saturated finite-support population bridge, and an observed measure-backed population 2SLS bridge.  It proves the ordered-tail coefficient nonnegativity, the finite tail-sum interchange identity, the response-type ratio representation, the population-bridge equality from reduced-form/first-stage moments to the finite response-type ratio, the observed `E[h(Z)Y] / E[h(Z)D]` bridge to that ratio, and the positive-weight normalization result under MTW sign alignment.

`POBridge.lean` grounds this algebra in an explicit potential-outcome system (`POMultipleIVSystem`): the response type `G = (D(z‚Āį),‚Ä¶,D(z^{K-1}))` is a genuine counterfactual, and under `Assumptions` (consistency + per-cell instrument independence `Z ‚üā (D(zŠĶŹ),Y(1),Y(0))`) the two `ObservedBridge` conditional-mean fields are *derived*, not assumed ‚ÄĒ discharging Gap G5 of the faithfulness audit.  Its flagship `POMultipleIVSystem.observedBeta2SLS_eq_responseTypeWeightedSum` restates the observed 2SLS ratio as the response-type weighted sum `ő£_g ŌČ_g őĒ_g` with `őĒ_g = E[Y(1)-Y(0) | G=g]`, i.e. entirely in potential-outcome language, via the same drop-of-conditioning + total-law pattern as `PO/ID/Exact/LATE.lean`.  Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/mogstad_torgovitsky_walters_multiple_iv.md`.

| `FiniteIndex.upperTail` / `tailCoeff` | `FiniteIndex K ‚Üí Adj K ‚Üí Finset (Fin K)` / `FiniteIndex K ‚Üí Adj K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Ordered upper tail `T_j` and MTW tail coefficient `B_j = ő£_{k‚ąąT_j} ŌĀ_k (dhat_k ‚ąí meanIndex)` | **Defined** |

## 9i0a-UniformTwoWayPanel. `Panel/UniformTwoWayPanel.lean` ‚ÄĒ Uniform Balanced Two-Way Panel Algebra

Paper-agnostic finite balanced-panel helpers for TWFE and two-way Mundlak arguments under the uniform unit-period measure. Lives directly under `Panel/` (general panel substrate, not estimand-characterization-specific) and exposes bridge lemmas showing it is the equal-unit-weight specialization of `WeightedTwoWayPanel`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.BalancedPanel Unit Time` | `structure { unit_card_ge_two, time_card_ge_two }` | Balanced finite panel side conditions from `def:po-estimand-wooldridge-two-way-population`: at least two units and at least two periods | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.uniformWeights` | `0 < Fintype.card Unit ‚Üí WeightedTwoWayPanel.UnitWeights Unit` | Equal unit-weight probability vector `p_i = 1 / |Unit|`, used to instantiate the weighted two-way panel substrate | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.unitMean` / `timeMean` / `grandMean` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ...` | Uniform finite-panel unit means, time means, and grand mean for a scalar array | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Uniform double-demeaned residual `V_it - unitMean V i - timeMean V t + grandMean V` | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.inner` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Unnormalized uniform finite unit-period inner product used for orthogonality statements | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_eq_weighted` / `inner_eq_card_smul_weighted` | bridge identities for `uniformWeights` | Uniform double demeaning equals weighted double demeaning with equal unit weights, and the unnormalized uniform inner product is `|Unit|` times the weighted inner product | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.finiteResidualizedCoefficient` | `(Dtilde Ytilde : Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Finite scalar residualized coefficient `inner Dtilde Ytilde / inner Dtilde Dtilde` for supplied residualized regressor and outcome arrays | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.finite_residualized_coefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` | `normal equations + residual bridges ‚Üí ő≤ = finiteResidualizedCoefficient Dtilde Ytilde` | Paper-agnostic finite scalar FWL handoff: from raw-regressor and nuisance normal equations, an explicitly residualized regressor orthogonal to the nuisance class, an outcome residual bridge, and positive residualized sum of squares, derives the finite residualized-ratio coefficient. Used by Wooldridge `twfe_twm_equivalence` instead of re-deriving scalar FWL algebra locally | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.IsUnitTimeAdditive` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | Alias for `Causalean.Panel.Weighted.IsUnitTimeAdditive`, the unit/time fixed-effect span `h_it = a_i + b_t` | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.unitTimeProjection` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Removed uniform unit/time component `unitMean + timeMean - grandMean` | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_reconstruct` | `ddot V i t + unitMean V i + timeMean V t - grandMean V = V i t` | Pointwise reconstruction identity for uniform double demeaning | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.unitTimeProjection_additive` | `IsUnitTimeAdditive (unitTimeProjection V)` | The removed component belongs to the unit/time additive nuisance class | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.sub_ddot_eq_unitTimeProjection` | `V i t - ddot V i t = unitTimeProjection V i t` | Pointwise form showing the part removed by uniform residualization is the unit/time projection | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_orthogonal_unit` | `BalancedPanel Unit Time ‚Üí ‚ąĎ i, ‚ąĎ t, ddot V i t * a i = 0` | Uniform double-demeaned arrays are orthogonal to arbitrary unit-only functions | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_orthogonal_time` | `BalancedPanel Unit Time ‚Üí ‚ąĎ i, ‚ąĎ t, ddot V i t * b t = 0` | Uniform double-demeaned arrays are orthogonal to arbitrary time-only functions | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_orthogonal_unit_time` | `BalancedPanel Unit Time ‚Üí IsUnitTimeAdditive h ‚Üí inner (ddot V) h = 0` | Uniform double-demeaned arrays are orthogonal to the whole unit/time additive nuisance span | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-FlexibleDIDMundlak. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/FlexibleDIDMundlak/{TWFE,VectorTWFE,VectorMundlak,DID,PopulationBridge,PopulationOrigin}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Wooldridge TWFE/Mundlak and Flexible DID

Specific-paper formalization scaffold for `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/wooldridge_twfe_mundlak_did.md`. `TWFE.lean` proves the scalar finite-panel slice of Wooldridge's TWFE and two-way Mundlak equivalence by instantiating the shared `UniformTwoWayPanel.finite_residualized_coefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` finite FWL handoff. `DID.lean` is a finite-cell composition theorem: it now DERIVES the untreated-prediction recovery from no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends + additivity (no longer an assumed field), eliminates the former opaque `Prop`+`_holds` placeholders, and derives the POLS/ETWFE cell links from finite-cell residual normal equations justified by a saturated block-diagonalization lemma. **G5 de-scaffold (2026-05-30):** `m0`'s exact untreated fit is no longer a posited field ‚ÄĒ `SaturatedUntreatedRegression` now carries the genuine weighted least-squares projection origin (`untreatedWeight`, `untreatedWeight_pos`, `untreatedNormalEq`: `m0` solves the untreated-only normal equations against the additive class `H0`), and the exact fit `m0 = YgMean` on untreated cells is the DERIVED theorem `untreatedFit` (the projection is exact because no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends place the untreated observed mean inside `H0`). **G9 de-scaffold (2026-05-30):** the finite imputation estimand is now bridged to the population conditional expectation ‚ÄĒ `PopulationBridge.lean` proves `thetaImp g t = eventCondExp őľ cohortEvent őĒ` (őł^imp = E[őĒ‚ą£G=g]) via the law of iterated expectations over the finite covariate partition, under explicit identification hypotheses (covariate weights are conditional cell probabilities; cell residuals are within-cell conditional means). **G1 de-scaffold (2026-05-30):** the scalar-only restriction is removed ‚ÄĒ `VectorTWFE.lean` introduces the genuine K-vector TWFE problem with a `Matrix K K ‚ĄĚ`-valued residualized Gram matrix `Q_ddotX`, the full-rank condition `IsUnit (gram X).det`, and the matrix normal equation; existence and uniqueness of the closed-form coefficient `(gram X)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ő£ ddot X ddot Y)` are proved, and `ScalarTWFEProblem` is shown to be the `K = Fin 1` specialization (`ScalarTWFEProblem.toVector` + `toVector_betaTWFE`). **`VectorMundlak.lean` (2026-05-30)** completes Theorem A in vector form: the matrix Frisch-Waugh-Lovell handoff `matrix_fwl_eq_of_normalEqs` (generalizing the scalar finite FWL), the K-vector two-way Mundlak nuisance span `IsVectorTwoWayMundlakNuisance`, the coding-free `VectorTWMFit`, and the equivalence `vec_twfe_twm_equivalence : fit.beta = P.betaTWFE` plus the optional-controls-invariance corollary. The remaining deferred Wooldridge gap is the population conditional-expectation origin of `m0` itself (G5 measure layer).

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/TWFE.lean`

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time` | `structure { panel, Y, X, ddotX_ss_pos }` | Scalar balanced-panel TWFE problem: outcome, scalar regressor, panel side conditions, and positive residualized sum of squares standing in for full rank of `Q_ddotX` | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.twfeDenominator` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized-design denominator `‚ąĎ ddot X_it^2` | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.twfeNumerator` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized numerator `‚ąĎ ddot X_it * ddot Y_it` | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.betaTWFE` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Scalar population TWFE coefficient, the finite FWL ratio | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.twfeNormalEq` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí Prop` | Scalar double-demeaned normal equation for a proposed TWFE coefficient | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_normalEq` | `P.twfeNormalEq P.betaTWFE` | The closed-form scalar TWFE coefficient satisfies the double-demeaned normal equation | **Proved** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_unique` | `P.twfeNormalEq ő≤ ‚Üí ő≤ = P.betaTWFE` | Positive residualized sum of squares gives uniqueness of the scalar TWFE normal-equation solution | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.IsTwoWayMundlakNuisance` | `(X) ‚Üí (Z ‚Üí Unit ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (M ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | Scalar two-way Mundlak nuisance span: constants, unit means of `X`, time means of `X`, optional unit-only controls, and optional time-only controls | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.mundlak_nuisance_unit_time` | `IsTwoWayMundlakNuisance X Zvar Mvar h ‚Üí IsUnitTimeAdditive h` | Every scalar two-way Mundlak nuisance term is unit/time additive | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWMFit` | `structure { beta, nuisance, nuisance_mem, normal_X, normal_H }` | Coding-free scalar two-way Mundlak fit, represented by a coefficient, nuisance function, and normal equations against `X` and the nuisance span | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.twfe_twm_residual_common` | `IsUnitTimeAdditive (X - ddot X) ‚ąß ‚ąÄ h ‚ąą H_twm, inner (ddot X) h = 0` | Residualizing `X` against the two-way Mundlak nuisance span leaves the double-demeaned residual | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.twfe_twm_equivalence` | `ScalarTWMFit P Zvar Mvar ‚Üí fit.beta = P.betaTWFE` | Scalar finite-panel TWFE--two-way Mundlak equivalence (`thm:po-estimand-wooldridge-twfe-twm-equivalence`, scalar slice) | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.twfe_twm_optional_controls_invariant` | `fit‚āĀ.beta = fit‚āā.beta` | Adding or removing optional unit-only/time-only controls does not change the scalar coefficient because both fits equal `betaTWFE` | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/VectorTWFE.lean` ‚ÄĒ K-vector TWFE matrix normal equation (audit G1)

Closes G1: the K-vector generalization of the TWFE problem. The vector double-demeaned array is component-wise scalar double demeaning (`ddotVec X i t k = ddot (X ¬∑¬∑ k) i t`), so the uniform-panel orthogonality infrastructure is reused.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ddotVec` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Component-wise double-demeaned vector regressor | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.gram` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Matrix K K ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized Gram matrix `Q_ddotX = ő£_it ddot(X_it) ddot(X_it)ŠĶÄ` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.numer` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized numerator vector `ő£_it ddot(X_it) ddot(Y_it)` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time K` | `structure { panel, Y, X, gram_unit }` | K-vector balanced-panel TWFE problem; the full-rank condition is `IsUnit (gram X).det`, matching nonsingular `Q_ddotX` in the source | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.betaTWFE` | `VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time K ‚Üí K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Closed-form vector TWFE coefficient `(gram X)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (ő£ ddot X ddot Y)` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.vecTwfeNormalEq` | `VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time K ‚Üí (K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | Coordinate-wise vector double-demeaned normal equation | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vecNormalEq_iff_mulVec` | `(coordinate-wise normal eq) ‚ÜĒ (gram X).mulVec ő≤ = numer X Y` | The coordinate normal equation equals the matrix normal equation `Q_ddotX ő≤ = ő£ ddot X ddot Y` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_normalEq` | `P.vecTwfeNormalEq P.betaTWFE` | Existence: the closed-form coefficient solves the matrix normal equation under Gram nonsingularity | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_unique` | `P.vecTwfeNormalEq ő≤ ‚Üí ő≤ = P.betaTWFE` | Full-rank uniqueness of the matrix normal-equation solution | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWFEProblem.toVector` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time ‚Üí VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time (Fin 1)` | The scalar problem embeds as the singleton-`K` vector problem; `IsUnit (gram).det` reduces to `ddotX_ss_pos` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWFEProblem.toVector_betaTWFE` | `P.toVector.betaTWFE 0 = P.betaTWFE` | The singleton-coordinate vector coefficient recovers the scalar coefficient, so the scalar theorem is the `K = Fin 1` case | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/VectorMundlak.lean` ‚ÄĒ K-vector two-way Mundlak equivalence (Theorem A, vector form)

Extends `twfe_twm_equivalence` to K-vector regressors via a matrix FWL handoff.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.gramOf` / `numerOf` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Matrix K K ‚ĄĚ` / `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí (Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Generic residualized Gram matrix / numerator of a supplied residualized regressor | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.gram_eq_gramOf` / `numer_eq_numerOf` | `gram X = gramOf (ddotVec X)` / `numer X Y = numerOf (ddotVec X) (ddot Y)` | The TWFE Gram/numerator are the residualized instances of the generic versions | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.sum_dotRegressor` | `‚ąĎ_it Dt¬∑k (‚ąĎ_j D¬∑j ő≤_j) = ‚ąĎ_j (‚ąĎ_it Dt¬∑k D¬∑j) ő≤_j` | A residualized regressor against a `ő≤`-combination factors through the cross-Gram | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.matrix_fwl_eq_of_normalEqs` | `(decompositions + per-coordinate orthogonality + IsUnit gram + normal eqs) ‚Üí ő≤ = (gramOf Dtilde)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (numerOf Dtilde Ytilde)` | Matrix Frisch-Waugh-Lovell handoff: vector coefficient satisfying pooled normal equations against `D` and nuisance class `H` equals the residualized matrix coefficient | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.IsVectorTwoWayMundlakNuisance` | `(X) ‚Üí (Z ‚Üí Unit ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (M ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | K-vector two-way Mundlak nuisance span: constants, unit/time means of every coordinate of `X`, optional unit-only and time-only controls | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vector_mundlak_nuisance_unit_time` | `IsVectorTwoWayMundlakNuisance X Zvar Mvar h ‚Üí IsUnitTimeAdditive h` | Every vector Mundlak nuisance term is unit/time additive | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.sum_ite_one_mul` | `‚ąĎ_k' (if k'=k then 1 else 0) * f k' = f k` | Coordinate-selection indicator collapse | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWMFit` | `structure { beta, nuisance, nuisance_mem, normal_X, normal_H }` | Coding-free K-vector two-way Mundlak fit, normal equations against each regressor coordinate and the nuisance span | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vec_twfe_twm_equivalence` | `VectorTWMFit P Zvar Mvar ‚Üí fit.beta = P.betaTWFE` | K-vector finite-panel TWFE--two-way Mundlak equivalence (`thm:po-estimand-wooldridge-twfe-twm-equivalence`, full vector form) | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vec_twfe_twm_optional_controls_invariant` | `fit‚āĀ.beta = fit‚āā.beta` | Adding or removing optional unit-only/time-only controls does not change the vector coefficient | **Proved** |

| `StaggeredATTCells.tauAgg` | `StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar ‚Üí (Cohort ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Weighted aggregate ATT over the treated-cell support | **Defined** |
