The former opaque `Prop`+`_holds` placeholders (untreated_design_connected, full_rank_after_normalization, covariates_baseline) and the former posited `untreatedFit` field were removed. | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.SaturatedUntreatedRegression.untreatedFit` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí untreatedCell g t ‚Üí m0 g t c = YgMean g t c` | **DERIVED exact untreated fit (G5).** The weighted projection reproduces the cohort-`g` mean on every untreated cell. Proof: no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends make the untreated observed mean additive, so the residual `m0 ‚ąí Y0Mean` is additive; plugging it into `untreatedNormalEq` forces its positively-weighted squared sum over the untreated design to zero, hence the residual vanishes cellwise | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.IsCellAdditive` | `(Cohort ‚Üí Time ‚Üí Covar ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | Additive cell function `d g t c = ő≥ g c + őī t c` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.UntreatedDesignIdentifies P` | `Prop` | Connected/full-rank condition: an additive cell function vanishing on all untreated cells vanishes on all treated cells | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.SaturatedUntreatedRegression.recovers_target_Y0` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí treatedCell g t ‚Üí m0 g t c = Y0Mean g t c` | Saturated regression recovers untreated means on treated cells, DERIVED from no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends + additivity + untreated fit + design identifiability | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellIndicator` | `(g : Cohort) ‚Üí (t : Time) ‚Üí Cohort ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Saturated treated-cell indicator `1{(g',t')=(g,t)}` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellIndicator_normalEq_eq_cellResidual` | `(full saturated indicator normal equation) ‚ÜĒ cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t` | Saturated block-diagonalization: the full normal equation summed against a cell's indicator collapses (cross-cell terms vanish by orthogonality) to that cell's residual equation, justifying the POLS/ETWFE per-cell normal-equation fields | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta` | `(P) ‚Üí SaturatedUntreatedRegression P ‚Üí Cohort ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Imputation residual mean using `observedMean - m0` within a treated cell | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t` | `Prop` | Finite-cell residual normal equation `‚ąĎ_c w_gc (observedMean_gtc - m0_gtc - theta) = 0` for a candidate cell coefficient | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellResidualNormalEq_eq_imputationTheta` | `cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t ‚Üí theta = imputationTheta P S g t` | A saturated treated-cell normal equation identifies the coefficient with the imputation residual mean, using `covarWeight_sum_one` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.FlexibleDIDEstimands P S` | `structure { thetaImp, thetaPOLS, thetaETWFE, thetaImp_eq_imputation, pols_cell_normalEq, etwfe_cell_normalEq }` | Coding-free flexible imputation/POLS/ETWFE cell estimands; POLS and ETWFE coefficients now carry finite-cell residual normal equations instead of direct equality assumptions | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDEstimands.thetaPOLS_eq_imputationTheta` / `thetaETWFE_eq_imputationTheta` | `treatedCell g t ‚Üí thetaPOLS/ETWFE g t = imputationTheta P S g t` | Derived POLS and ETWFE cell equalities from their residual normal equations; the ETWFE normal equation is the residualized equation supplied by the FWL/TWFE-Mundlak bridge | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDEstimands.pols_cell_eq_imputation` / `etwfe_cell_eq_pols` | `treatedCell g t ‚Üí thetaPOLS = thetaImp` / `thetaETWFE = thetaPOLS` | Compatibility aliases; both equalities are now consequences of the normal-equation lemmas rather than structure fields | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.psiImp` / `psiPOLS` / `psiETWFE` | `(P) ‚Üí FlexibleDIDEstimands P S ‚Üí (Cohort ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Aggregate estimands formed by weighting the imputation, POLS, and ETWFE cell coefficients over treated cells | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta_eq_tauCell` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí treatedCell g t ‚Üí E.thetaImp g t = P.tauCell g t` | Imputation recovers a treated ATT cell from consistency and the DERIVED `recovers_target_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_cell_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí treatedCell g t ‚Üí thetaImp = thetaPOLS = thetaETWFE = tauCell` | Cell-level theorem: the untreated-prediction witness plus POLS/ETWFE residual normal equations imply all three cell estimands equal the ATT cell | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_aggregate_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí AggregateWeights P ‚Üí psiImp = tauAgg ‚ąß psiPOLS = tauAgg ‚ąß psiETWFE = tauAgg` | Aggregate theorem obtained by summing the normal-equation-derived cell equalities over treated cells | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_cell_characterization_of_estimand_equalities` / `flexible_did_aggregate_characterization_of_estimand_equalities` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí ‚Ä¶` (same signatures as cell/aggregate characterization respectively) | Compatibility aliases for the earlier scaffold names, now taking the explicit causal hypotheses | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_scaffold_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí cell characterization ‚ąß aggregate characterization` | Finite-cell Wooldridge DID characterization from saturated untreated prediction plus POLS/ETWFE finite-cell residual normal equations | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/PopulationBridge.lean` ‚ÄĒ finite estimand/projection ‚Üí population conditional expectation (audit G9 + G5 population half)

Bridges the finite-cell imputation estimand and the saturated projection `m0` to population conditional expectations, reusing the conditional finite-partition total law `Causalean.PO.eventCondExp_eq_sum_condProb_mul_eventCondExp`. Hypothesis-driven: identification hypotheses connect the finite scaffold's primitive means to a probability model ‚ÄĒ they are exactly the paper's *definitions* (őł^imp = E[őĒ‚ą£G=g]; m0 = E[Y(‚ąě)‚ą£G,t,C]), not strengthenings.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta_eq_eventCondExp` | `(őľ, P, S, g, t, cohortEvent, covarCell, őĒ)`, partition + integrability + `hweight : covarWeight g c = őľ(cohortEvent ‚ą© covarCell c)/őľ(cohortEvent)` + `hcell : observedMean ‚ąí m0 = eventCondExp őľ (cohortEvent ‚ą© covarCell c) őĒ` ‚Üí `imputationTheta P S g t = eventCondExp őľ cohortEvent őĒ` | **G9 finite‚Üípopulation bridge.** The finite imputation residual mean equals the population conditional expectation `E[őĒ‚ą£G=g]` on the cohort event, by the law of iterated expectations over the covariate partition | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.thetaImp_eq_eventCondExp` | `treatedCell g t ‚Üí` (same hypotheses) `‚Üí E.thetaImp g t = eventCondExp őľ cohortEvent őĒ` | **G9 (őł^imp = E[őĒ‚ą£G=g]).** The `FlexibleDIDEstimands` imputation cell estimand equals the population conditional expectation on each treated cell; composes `thetaImp_eq_imputation` with `imputationTheta_eq_eventCondExp` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí treatedCell g t ‚Üí` `hY0 : Y0Mean g t c = eventCondExp őľ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` `‚Üí S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp őľ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 population origin (treated cells).** The saturated projection's fitted value equals `E[Y_t(‚ąě)‚ą£G=g,t,C=c]`; causal content (additive extrapolation) carried by `recovers_target_Y0`, `hY0` reads `Y0Mean` as that conditional mean | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_untreated` | `NoAnticipation P ‚Üí ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P ‚Üí untreatedCell g t ‚Üí` `hY0 : Y0Mean g t c = eventCondExp őľ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` `‚Üí S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp őľ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 population origin (untreated cells).** Same conclusion via `untreatedFit` + no-anticipation | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/PopulationOrigin.lean` ‚ÄĒ `ofPopulation` constructor making the m0 conditional-expectation origin definitional (audit G5 measure layer)

Closes the *measure-layer* half of G5: instead of *assuming* `Y0Mean g t c = E[Y(‚ąě)‚ą£cell]` (the `hY0` hypothesis above), it builds a `StaggeredATTCells` whose cell means are *defined* as that `eventCondExp`, so `hY0` holds by `rfl`. Mirrors `DCDHPanel.ofPopulation`. The cell-weight data (`cohortShare`, `covarWeight`) is supplied as a witness (an FWL/partition construction orthogonal to the conditional-expectation origin); the pointwise consistency inputs are the paper's consistency axiom restricted to each cell.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.StaggeredATTCells.ofPopulation` | `(őľ : Measure ő©) [IsProbabilityMeasure őľ] (cellEvent) (Y0pop Ygpop Yobspop : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (treatedCell untreatedCell) (cohortShare covarWeight) (hmeas) (weight side-conditions) (hcons_tr hcons_ut) ‚Üí StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar` | **G5 (panel from a population model).** Defines `Y0Mean/YgMean/observedMean g t c := eventCondExp őľ (cellEvent g t c) {Y0pop/Ygpop/Yobspop}`; derives `consistency_treated`/`consistency_untreated` from pointwise PO consistency via `eventCondExp_congr_on`; weights passed as witnesses | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated_ofPopulation` | for `P = ofPopulation őľ ‚Ä¶`, `hNA ‚Üí hCPT ‚Üí treatedCell g t ‚Üí S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp őľ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 payoff (treated, definitional).** Applies `m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated` with `hY0 := rfl` ‚ÄĒ the conditional-expectation origin of `m0` is now definitional, not assumed | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_untreated_ofPopulation` | for `P = ofPopulation őľ ‚Ä¶`, `hNA ‚Üí hCPT ‚Üí untreatedCell g t ‚Üí S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp őľ (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 payoff (untreated, definitional).** Untreated-cell analogue, `hY0 := rfl` | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudy. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/Imputation.lean` ‚ÄĒ Robust Imputation Event Studies (BJS)

Specific-paper finite-cell formalization of the Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess robust imputation event-study characterization. The module works directly with finite treated and untreated cell index types, arbitrary signed target weights, treated/untreated regressor rows, primitive observed-law means, a nuisance vector, and treated-cell effects. It proves the population imputation identification theorem (taking `PredictionIdentified` as a propositional existence hypothesis and extracting the witness internally) and the intentionally-weak `linear_unbiased_of_imputation_representation` algebra helper. The **source-strength** linear-unbiased representation theorem and the combined iff characterization now live in the companion `PanelBridge.lean` (¬ß9i0a-ImputationEventStudyBridge), where the finite left-null-space adjustment is rebased onto the panel `WeightedSupport`/`Subspace` substrate. A staggered-adoption two-way-FE grounding (closing the abstract-index scope gap) lives in `PanelGrounding.lean` (¬ß9i0a-ImputationEventStudyGrounding). The Gauss-Markov/BLUE efficiency half of `prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation` is in `Efficiency.lean` (¬ß9i0a-ImputationEventStudyEfficiency), built on the finite Gauss-Markov layer ¬ß13gm. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/borusyak_jaravel_spiess_imputation.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ImputationEventStudy.dot` | `(Regressor ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Regressor ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Finite regressor dot product used for untreated-outcome linear predictions | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.BJSPanel` | `structure { qT, qU, a, EY_T, EY_U, EY0_T, EY0_U, beta0, tau }` | Finite BJS treated/untreated cell design: regressor rows, arbitrary target weights, observed means, untreated potential-outcome means, nuisance vector, and treated-cell effects | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.theta` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Target weighted sum `‚ąĎ c, a c * tau c`; weights may be signed and need not sum to one | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.targetForTau` | `(Treated ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Target functional evaluated at an arbitrary unrestricted treated-effect vector, used in the linear-unbiasedness statement | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.UntreatedOutcomeModel` | `Prop` | Deterministic mean version of the untreated-outcome linear model plus untreated-cell no-anticipation / observation equation | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.TreatmentEffectFixed` | `Prop` | Treated observed means equal untreated means plus the fixed/design-conditional treated-cell effect | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.ImputationWeights` | `structure { weight, row_identity }` | Witness-form prediction-span condition: for every target-relevant treated cell, its regressor row is a weighted combination of untreated rows | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.PredictionIdentified` | `Prop` | Propositional existence wrapper (`Nonempty ImputationWeights`) for valid target-relevant imputation weights; natural hypothesis for identification ‚ÄĒ callers supply existence, not a pre-built witness | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.psiImp` | `(Treated ‚Üí Untreated ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Population observed-law imputation functional `‚ąĎ a_c (EY_T c - ‚ąĎ h_cu EY_U u)` for arbitrary imputation weights | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_imputation_identification` | `UntreatedOutcomeModel ‚Üí PredictionIdentified ‚Üí TreatmentEffectFixed ‚Üí ‚ąÉ h : ImputationWeights, psiImp h.weight = theta` | BJS population identification theorem: under the untreated model and prediction-span condition, some imputation weight matrix recovers the weighted treated-cell target; witness extracted internally via `Classical.choice` | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator` | `structure { vT, vU }` | Linear estimator coefficients on treated and untreated observed cell outcomes | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.value` | `(Treated ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Untreated ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Evaluates a treated-plus-untreated linear estimator on arbitrary outcome arrays | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.observedValue` | `‚ĄĚ` | Evaluates a linear estimator on the panel's observed-law treated and untreated means | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.modelValue` | `(Regressor ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Treated ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Model-implied value of the linear estimator for a nuisance vector and unrestricted treated-effect vector | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.unbiasedForAllTau` | `Prop` | Linear unbiasedness for every nuisance vector and every unrestricted treated-effect vector | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.vT_eq_a` | `unbiasedForAllTau ‚Üí ‚ąÄ c, vT c = a c` | Hoisted fact: unrestricted treatment-effect heterogeneity forces treated-cell weights to equal target weights (indicator-`tau` test); reused by the efficiency layer | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.nuisance_coord` | `unbiasedForAllTau ‚Üí ‚ąÄ r, (‚ąĎ c, a c * qT c r) + ‚ąĎ u, vU u * qU u r = 0` | Hoisted fact: nuisance unbiasedness gives the BJS left-null-space coordinate constraint `aŠĶÄQ_T + vUŠĶÄQ_U = 0`; reused by the efficiency layer | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.HasImputationRepresentation` | `structure { weights, untreated_weight_representation }` | Explicit witness that a linear estimator's untreated coefficients are represented by valid BJS imputation weights; used as an algebra helper for the source-strength theorem | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.linear_unbiased_of_imputation_representation` | `unbiasedForAllTau ‚Üí HasImputationRepresentation ‚Üí ‚ąÉ H, vT = a ‚ąß vU = -aŠĶÄH ‚ąß (‚ąÄ YT YU, value YT YU = ‚ąĎ c, a c * (YT c - ‚ąĎ u, H.weight c u * YU u)) ‚ąß observedValue = psiImp H.weight` | Intentionally weak algebra helper: given a pre-built imputation representation witness, derives the estimator identity; source-strength derivation lives in `linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified` | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyBridge. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/PanelBridge.lean` ‚ÄĒ Imputation Event Study ‚ÜĒ panel substrate bridge

Connects the bespoke finite design-matrix algebra of the BJS imputation scaffold to the shared panel inner-product / subspace substrate (`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/`). The OLS imputation estimator is the *uniform-weight* special case of the panel WLS substrate, so the untreated cells carry the uniform `WeightedSupport`; the keystone lemma proves the BJS "left-null-space of `Q_U`" condition **is** `ip`-orthogonality to the regressor column span. The source-strength linear-unbiased theorem and the combined iff characterization (moved here from `Imputation.lean`, statements verbatim) consume that substrate-based left-null-space adjustment; `DecidableEq Untreated` is obtained internally via `Classical.decEq`, so their public signatures are unchanged.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ImputationEventStudy.untreatedSupport` | `(Untreated : Type*) ‚Üí [Fintype, DecidableEq, Nonempty] ‚Üí WeightedSupport Untreated` | The untreated cells as the uniform `WeightedSupport` `ŌČ_u ‚Č° 1/\|U\|` ‚ÄĒ the OLS = uniform-weight WLS instantiation through which BJS consumes the panel `ip`/`Subspace` API | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.regressorColumn` | `Regressor ‚Üí (Untreated ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | The `r`-th regressor column `u ‚Ü¶ q_{ur}` as an array over untreated cells | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.columnSpan` | `Submodule ‚ĄĚ (Untreated ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Span of all regressor columns; orthogonality to it is the BJS left-null-space condition | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.ip_untreatedSupport_regressorColumn` | `ip v (regressorColumn r) = (\|U\|)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * ‚ąĎ u, v u * qU u r` | The panel inner product of an untreated vector with a regressor column equals the BJS left-null-space sum, rescaled by the uniform weight | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.ip_regressorColumn_eq_zero_iff` | `ip v (regressorColumn r) = 0 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąĎ u, v u * qU u r = 0` | `ip`-orthogonality to a single column ‚üļ that column's left-null coordinate vanishes | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.columnSpan_ip_orthogonal_iff` | `(‚ąÄ h ‚ąą columnSpan, ip v h = 0) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ r, ‚ąĎ u, v u * qU u r = 0` | **Keystone bridge**: a vector is `ip`-orthogonal to the regressor column span iff it satisfies the BJS left-null-space condition (the panel-substrate restatement of "`v` lies in the left null space of `Q_U`") | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.exists_imputationWeights_of_gap_orthogonal` | `(hc0 : a c0 ‚Č† 0) ‚Üí H0 ‚Üí (gap ip-orthogonal to columnSpan) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ H, ‚ąÄ u, ‚ąĎ c, a c * H.weight c u = -vU u` | Audit M3 left-null-space row adjustment, phrased through the panel substrate: one nonzero target row absorbs the orthogonal gap, yielding imputation weights representing the untreated coefficients | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified` | `unbiasedForAllTau ‚Üí PredictionIdentified ‚Üí (‚ąÉ c, a c ‚Č† 0) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ H, vT = a ‚ąß vU = -aŠĶÄH ‚ąß (‚ąÄ YT YU, value YT YU = ‚ąĎ c, a c * (YT c - ‚ąĎ u, H.weight c u * YU u)) ‚ąß observedValue = psiImp H.weight` | Source-strength BJS linear-unbiased representation theorem; now consumes `exists_imputationWeights_of_gap_orthogonal` (empty-`Untreated` case handled separately) instead of an inline finite-algebra construction | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_linear_unbiased_iff_imputation_form` | `PredictionIdentified ‚Üí (‚ąÉ c, a c ‚Č† 0) ‚Üí (unbiasedForAllTau ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ H, vT = a ‚ąß vU = -aŠĶÄH ‚ąß (‚ąÄ YT YU, value = ‚ąĎ a_c (YT c - ‚ąĎ h_cu YU u)) ‚ąß observedValue = psiImp H.weight)` | Combined iff characterization (`prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation`, identification half): unbiased for every tau iff BJS imputation form; (‚üĻ) is `linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified`, (‚üł) is pure row-span algebra | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyGrounding. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/PanelGrounding.lean` ‚ÄĒ Imputation Event Study staggered-adoption grounding

Closes the abstract-index scope gap (audit G5) *constructively*: exhibits `BJSPanel` as an instance built from genuine panel primitives ‚ÄĒ units `I`, periods `Fin T`, and a staggered-adoption path `g : I ‚Üí WithTop (Fin T)` via `Causalean.Panel.AdoptionPath`. Treated cells are those whose treatment has switched on (`g i ‚Č§ t`); untreated cells are those still untreated (`t < g i`), faithfully including every never-treated cell and every pre-adoption cell of a treated unit. The regressor design is the canonical two-way fixed-effect block `I ‚äē Fin T`, so the untreated-outcome model `E[Y(0)] = őĪ_i + őĽ_t` holds by construction and the BJS hypotheses discharge to a concrete panel.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ImputationEventStudy.TreatedCell` | `(g : I ‚Üí WithTop (Fin T)) ‚Üí Type` | Subtype of cells `(i,t)` whose treatment has switched on (`AdoptionPath.le (g i) t`) | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.UntreatedCell` | `(g : I ‚Üí WithTop (Fin T)) ‚Üí Type` | Subtype of still-untreated cells (`AdoptionPath.lt (g i) t`); includes never-treated and pre-adoption cells | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.treated_not_untreated` | `le (g c.1) c.2 ‚Üí lt (g c.1) c.2 ‚Üí False` | The cell partition is exclusive: no cell is both adopted-by-`t` and untreated-at-`t` | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.treated_or_untreated` | `‚ąÄ c, le (g c.1) c.2 ‚ą® lt (g c.1) c.2` | The cell partition is exhaustive: every cell is treated or untreated | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.feRow` | `(I √ó Fin T) ‚Üí (I ‚äē Fin T) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Two-way fixed-effect design row for a cell: unit-`i` indicator stacked with time-`t` indicator | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.dot_feRow` | `dot (feRow c) (Sum.elim őĪ lam) = őĪ c.1 + lam c.2` | The FE row evaluates the additive fixed-effect model `őĪ_i + őĽ_t` | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE` | `(g) ‚Üí (őĪ : I ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (lam : Fin T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (a tau : TreatedCell g ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí BJSPanel (TreatedCell g) (UntreatedCell g) (I ‚äē Fin T)` | Grounding constructor: builds a `BJSPanel` from an adoption path and two-way fixed effects | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE_untreatedModel` | `(ofStaggeredTWFE ‚Ä¶).UntreatedOutcomeModel` | The grounded panel satisfies the BJS untreated-outcome model (both cell means equal `őĪ_i + őĽ_t`; untreated cells have no anticipation) | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE_treatmentFixed` | `(ofStaggeredTWFE ‚Ä¶).TreatmentEffectFixed` | The grounded panel satisfies the BJS fixed-effect equation `E[Y_T] = E[Y_T(0)] + ŌĄ` | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyEfficiency. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/Efficiency.lean` ‚ÄĒ Imputation Event Study Efficiency / BLUE half

The efficiency half of `prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation`: under spherical errors the OLS imputation estimator is the minimum-variance linear unbiased estimator (Gauss-Markov / BLUE). The BJS imputation estimator is recognized as the OLS estimator for the *event-study design* `designFull` (a treated-cell indicator block stacked with the covariate block), so the result is a direct instantiation of the finite Gauss-Markov theorem `GaussMarkov.variance_blue_spherical` (¬ß13gm) at that design. The unbiasedness-to-design-constraint bridge uses the hoisted `LinearEstimator.vT_eq_a` and `LinearEstimator.nuisance_coord`. An additive fixed-effect-block bridge (`designFull_col_mem_indicatorSpan`) identifies the ŌĄ-columns of `designFull` with the panel `IndicatorSpan` substrate (`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/IndicatorSpan.lean`), connecting the event-study FE design to the panel indicator-span layer; the BLUE proof itself stays on the finite Gauss-Markov substrate (¬ß13gm), the correct home for the variance argument. No `sorry`/`admit`/`axiom`. Companion NL doc section C3.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BJSPanel.designFull` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor ‚Üí Matrix (Treated ‚äē Untreated) (Treated ‚äē Regressor) ‚ĄĚ` | Event-study design: rows are observed cells, columns are treated-cell fixed effects (ŌĄ block, cell-indicator on treated rows / zero on untreated rows) stacked with covariates (ő≤ block, `qT`/`qU`) | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.cFull` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor ‚Üí (Treated ‚äē Regressor ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Target functional in design coordinates: target weights `a` on the ŌĄ block, zero on the covariate block | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.weightOf` | `LinearEstimator P ‚Üí (Treated ‚äē Untreated ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | A linear estimator's full weight vector over all observed cells (`Sum.elim vT vU`) | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.weightOf_vecMul_designFull` | `unbiasedForAllTau ‚Üí weightOf L ŠĶ•* designFull P = cFull P` | Unbiasedness bridge: an unbiased estimator's weight satisfies the design constraint (captures both `vT = a` and the nuisance left-null constraint) | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_ols_imputation_min_variance_spherical` | `(‚ąÄ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 őľ) ‚Üí SphericalFamily Y őľ ŌÉ ‚Üí IsUnit ((designFull P)ŠĶÄ * designFull P).det ‚Üí unbiasedForAllTau L ‚Üí Var[‚ąĎ olsWeight ‚Ä¶ * Y] ‚Č§ Var[‚ąĎ weightOf L * Y]` | **BJS efficiency theorem (BLUE):** for a full-rank event-study design and spherical cell outcomes, the OLS imputation estimator has variance no larger than any linear unbiased estimator of the target | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.treatedClassifier` | `(Treated ‚äē Untreated) ‚Üí Option Treated` | Classifier on observed cells: each treated cell to its own label, every untreated cell to `none`; its `Some`-indicators are the ŌĄ-columns of `designFull` | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.designFull_col_eq_cellIndicator` | `(fun i => designFull P i (Sum.inl d)) = cellIndicator treatedClassifier (some d)` | Each treated-cell fixed-effect column of `designFull` is the panel cell indicator of the treated-cell classifier | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.designFull_col_mem_indicatorSpan` | `(fun i => designFull P i (Sum.inl d)) ‚ąą indicatorSpan treatedClassifier` | The treated-cell FE block of `designFull` lies in the panel `IndicatorSpan`: the BJS event-study FE design is the panel indicator-span substrate (additive; BLUE proof unchanged) | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-HeterogeneousTWFE. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/HeterogeneousTWFE/{FinitePanel,FWLBridge,PopulationBridge}.lean` ‚ÄĒ de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020) TWFE Signed Weights

Specific-paper finite-cell formalization of the de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille TWFE heterogeneous-effects warning. `FinitePanel.lean` works over finite group-time cells with positive weights `ŌÄ_gt`, binary treatment `D_gt`, observed outcomes `Y_gt`, untreated outcomes `Y0_gt`, treatment effects `ŌĄ_gt`, and a residualized-treatment witness `DŐÉ_gt` against the group-plus-time fixed-effect span. It proves the finite FWL decomposition `ő≤^twfe = B0 + ‚ąĎ_{D=1} ŌČ_gt ŌĄ_gt`, the zero-untreated-residual-contrast corollary, the fact that treated-cell weights sum to one while inheriting the sign of `DŐÉ`, and the constructive sign-reversal theorem. `FWLBridge.lean` closes audit gaps **G2/G6**: for the uniform balanced panel it builds a genuine `DCDHPanel` whose `DŐÉ` is the *derived* Frisch-Waugh-Lovell residual (double-demeaned treatment `ddot D` from `UniformTwoWayPanel`), with the membership and orthogonality fields PROVED rather than posited ‚ÄĒ only nonzero double-demeaned treatment variation (`S_D > 0`), binary treatment, and consistency remain as primitives. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/de_chaisemartin_dhaultfoeuille_twfe.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.IsGTFE` | `(G ‚Üí T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | Compatibility alias for `AdditiveSpan.IsUnitTimeAdditive`, specialized to group-time fixed effects `a_g + b_t` | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.DCDHPanel G T` | `structure { pi, D, Y, Y0, tau, Dtilde : G ‚Üí T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ, pi_pos, pi_sum_one, D_binary, consistency, D_minus_resid_mem, Dtilde_orthogonal, SD_pos }` | Finite DCDH group-time panel plus residualized-treatment witness (`def:po-estimand-dcdh-panel`, `def:po-estimand-dcdh-residualized-treatment`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.SD P` | `DCDHPanel G T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized-treatment denominator `S_D = ‚ąĎ ŌÄ_gt DŐÉ_gt¬≤` | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.betaTWFE P` | `DCDHPanel G T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Finite FWL/TWFE coefficient `(‚ąĎ ŌÄ_gt DŐÉ_gt Y_gt) / S_D` (`def:po-estimand-dcdh-twfe-coefficient`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.untreatedBias P` | `DCDHPanel G T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Bias term `B0 = (‚ąĎ ŌÄ_gt DŐÉ_gt Y0_gt) / S_D` in the DCDH decomposition | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.omega P g t` | `DCDHPanel G T ‚Üí G ‚Üí T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Normalized residualized-treatment cell weight `ŌČ_gt = ŌÄ_gt DŐÉ_gt / S_D`, interpreted on treated cells | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.treatedCells P` | `DCDHPanel G T ‚Üí Finset (G √ó T)` | Finite set of cells with `D_gt = 1`, used to state treated-cell sums without partial weights | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.DWeightedTau P` / `DCDHPanel.treatedWeightedTau P` | `DCDHPanel G T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | All-cell `D`-weighted treatment-effect component and equivalent treated-cell weighted sum | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast P` | `DCDHPanel G T ‚Üí Prop` | Exact DCDH bias-free condition `‚ąĎ ŌÄ_gt DŐÉ_gt Y0_gt = 0` (`ass:po-estimand-dcdh-untreated-parallel`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.inner_Dtilde_D_eq_SD` | `‚ąĎ ŌÄ_gt DŐÉ_gt D_gt = P.SD` | Denominator identity derived by applying residualized-treatment orthogonality to `D ‚ąí DŐÉ` | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast_of_Y0_mem_gtfe` | `IsGTFE P.Y0 ‚Üí P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast` | Additive group-plus-time untreated outcomes are a sufficient, not necessary, condition for zero untreated residual contrast | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_untreatedBias_add_DWeightedTau` | `P.betaTWFE = P.untreatedBias + P.DWeightedTau` | All-cell version of the DCDH TWFE decomposition after substituting consistency | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.DWeightedTau_eq_treatedWeightedTau` | `P.DWeightedTau = P.treatedWeightedTau` | Binary-treatment filtering lemma: the all-cell effect component equals the treated-cell weighted sum | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_untreatedBias_add_treated_weighted_tau` | `P.betaTWFE = P.untreatedBias + P.treatedWeightedTau` | Headline decomposition `ő≤^twfe = B0 + ‚ąĎ_{D=1} ŌČ_gt ŌĄ_gt` (`thm:po-estimand-dcdh-twfe-decomposition`) | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_treated_weighted_tau_of_zeroUntreatedContrast` | `P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast ‚Üí P.betaTWFE = P.treatedWeightedTau` | Bias-free DCDH causal weighted-sum corollary under the exact zero untreated residual contrast | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_sum_eq_one` | `‚ąĎ_{gt ‚ąą treatedCells} omega P gt.1 gt.2 = 1` | Treated-cell DCDH weights sum to one (`prop:po-estimand-dcdh-weights-sum-one`) | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_neg_iff_Dtilde_neg` | `P.omega g t < 0 ‚ÜĒ P.Dtilde g t < 0` | Negative-sign direction of `sign(ŌČ_gt)=sign(DŐÉ_gt)`; needs only `ŌÄ_gt>0`, `S_D>0` | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_pos_iff_Dtilde_pos` | `0 < P.omega g t ‚ÜĒ 0 < P.Dtilde g t` | Positive-sign direction, completing the sign triplet | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_zero_iff_Dtilde_zero` | `P.omega g t = 0 ‚ÜĒ P.Dtilde g t = 0` | Zero-sign direction, completing the sign triplet | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.neg_component_of_exists_Dtilde_neg` | `(‚ąÉ g t, P.D g t = 1 ‚ąß P.Dtilde g t < 0) ‚Üí ‚ąĎ_{treated,ŌČ<0} ŌČ < 0` | Bridge: the paper's natural entry condition "some treated cell has DŐÉ_gt<0" implies the negative-weight-sum precondition of the sign-reversal theorem | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.two_cell_signed_weights_positive_effects_negative_sum` | `0 < c ‚Üí 0 < őĶ ‚Üí ((1+c)őĶ)/c < M ‚Üí 0 < őĶ ‚ąß 0 < M ‚ąß (1+c)őĶ + (-c)M < 0` | Explicit two-cell sign-reversal arithmetic from `prop:po-estimand-dcdh-sign-reversal` | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.exists_positive_effects_negative_weighted_sum_of_negative_component` | `(‚ąĎ w = 1) ‚Üí (‚ąĎ_{w<0} w < 0) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ ŌĄ, (‚ąÄ i, 0 < ŌĄ i) ‚ąß ‚ąĎ i, w i ŌĄ i < 0` | Reusable finite signed-average construction: a strictly negative total weight component can dominate with positive effects | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.exists_panel_with_positive_treated_effects_twfe_negative_of_negative_component` | `P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast ‚Üí (‚ąĎ_{treated,ŌČ<0} ŌČ < 0) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ P', same ŌÄ,D,Y0,DŐÉ, (‚ąÄ g t, 0 < P'.tau g t), P'.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast ‚ąß P'.betaTWFE < 0` | Panel-level DCDH sign-reversal theorem: keeping the treatment/residualization geometry fixed, one can choose globally positive treated effects (for all g t) so TWFE is negative | **Proved** |

### `HeterogeneousTWFE/FWLBridge.lean` ‚ÄĒ `DŐÉ` as a derived FWL residual (audit G2/G6)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.uniformWeight G T` | `‚ĄĚ` | Uniform unit-period cell weight `1/(|G|¬∑|T|)` for the balanced-panel DCDH instance | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.uniformWeight_pos` | `UniformTwoWayPanel.BalancedPanel G T ‚Üí 0 < uniformWeight G T` | Positivity of the uniform weight from the `‚Č•2` cardinality side conditions | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel` | `BalancedPanel G T ‚Üí (D Y Y0 tau) ‚Üí (D binary) ‚Üí (consistency) ‚Üí (0 < ‚ąĎ uniformWeight¬∑(ddot D)¬≤) ‚Üí DCDHPanel G T` | **G2/G6:** builds a genuine `DCDHPanel` whose `Dtilde := ddot D` (double-demeaned FWL residual); `D_minus_resid_mem` and `Dtilde_orthogonal` are DERIVED from `UniformTwoWayPanel` (`sub_ddot_eq_unitTimeProjection`, `unitTimeProjection_additive`, `ddot_orthogonal_unit_time`), not posited | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel_Dtilde` | `(ofTwoWayPanel ‚Ä¶).Dtilde = ddot D` | The residualized treatment of the FWL-derived panel is exactly the double-demeaned treatment | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel_betaTWFE` | `(ofTwoWayPanel ‚Ä¶).betaTWFE = finiteResidualizedCoefficient (ddot D) Y` | The DCDH TWFE coefficient of the FWL-derived panel equals the uniform-panel FWL residualized coefficient of `Y` on `ddot D` (the uniform weight cancels) | **Proved** |

### `HeterogeneousTWFE/PopulationBridge.lean` ‚ÄĒ cell consistency from a potential-outcome contrast (audit G10)

Supplies the population origin of the `DCDHPanel.consistency` field and of `tau` as `Y(1) ‚ąí Y(0)`, at the level of cell-conditional means, reusing `Causalean.PO.eventCondExp_congr_on`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.cellMean_consistency` | `(őľ, A measurable, Y Y0 Y1, d ‚ąą {0,1})`, `hcons : ‚ąÄ ŌČ ‚ąą A, Y ŌČ = Y0 ŌČ + d¬∑(Y1 ŌČ ‚ąí Y0 ŌČ)` ‚Üí `eventCondExp őľ A Y = eventCondExp őľ A Y0 + d¬∑(eventCondExp őľ A Y1 ‚ąí eventCondExp őľ A Y0)` | **G10 finite‚Üípopulation bridge.** On a cell where treatment is constant `D ‚Č° d` and PO consistency holds pointwise, the cell-conditional means satisfy the DCDH consistency identity `»≤ = »≤(0) + d¬∑ŌĄŐĄ` with `ŌĄŐĄ = E[Y(1)‚ą£A] ‚ąí E[Y(0)‚ą£A]` a genuine potential-outcome contrast. Proof: case split on `d`, `eventCondExp_congr_on`, `ring` | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.DCDHPanel.ofPopulation` | `[IsProbabilityMeasure őľ]`, cell partition `cellEvent`, outcomes `Yfac Y0pop Y1pop`, binary cell treatment `d`, `Dtilde` witness; partition/positivity + pointwise consistency `hcons` + `Dtilde` orthogonality/`SD_pos` hypotheses ‚Üí `DCDHPanel G T` | **G10 `DCDHPanel` from a population model.** Builds a genuine panel with `Y/Y0 = E[¬∑‚ą£cell]`, `tau = E[Y(1)‚ą£cell] ‚ąí E[Y(0)‚ą£cell]` (a real PO contrast), `pi = őľ(cell)`. `consistency` is DERIVED via `cellMean_consistency`; `pi_sum_one` from `measure_biUnion_finset` over the partition. `Dtilde` (general-weight FWL residual) is a witness, as in the base panel ‚ÄĒ the uniform case is `ofTwoWayPanel` | **Defined** |
