## 9i''. `PO/ID/Exact/RDD/FuzzyRDD.lean` ‚ÄĒ Fuzzy RDD Identification (PO framework)

Formalizes the fuzzy regression discontinuity example from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(`def:po-fuzzy-rdd-system`, `def:po-fuzzy-rdd-assumptions`, and
`prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`) in the bare potential-outcome framework.  The Lean theorem
uses the regression-representative Wald form described in the implementation
plan: `őľ_D(z, ¬∑)` represents `E[D(z) | X=¬∑]`, `őľ_Y(z, ¬∑)` represents
`E[Y(D(z)) | X=¬∑]`, and the observable regressions `őĹ_D`, `őĹ_Y` are identified
with the corresponding right/left latent regressions at the cutoff.  Local
exclusion is encoded by the `Y(d)` interface; monotonicity is kept in the
assumption bundle to match the classical fuzzy RDD assumption list, though the
representative-level Wald identity itself only uses the bridge from consistency,
cutoff eligibility, continuity, support, and one-sided limit existence.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem P` | `structure { Xvar, Zvar, Dvar, Yvar, c, hYD, hZD, hDY }` | Fuzzy RDD subsystem: real running variable `X`, binary cutoff-eligibility instrument `Z`, binary treatment `D`, real outcome `Y`, cutoff `c`, and distinctness witnesses for consistency rewrites | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{factualX, factualZ, factualD, factualY}` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` / `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` / `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` / `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Empty-regime factual running variable, eligibility instrument, treatment, and outcome | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.DofZ` | `Bool ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` | Instrument-specific potential treatment `D(z) := Dvar.cfUnder Zvar z` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YofD` | `Bool ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Treatment-specific potential outcome `Y(d) := Yvar.cfUnder Dvar d` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YofDofZ` | `Bool ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Composed potential outcome `Y(D(z))`, implemented as an if-then-else over the Boolean `D(z)` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{measurable_factualX, measurable_factualZ, measurable_factualD, measurable_factualY, measurable_DofZ, measurable_YofD, measurable_YofDofZ}` | `Measurable ‚Ä¶` | Basic measurability lemmas for factual variables and counterfactual/composed maps | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.zEvent` | `Bool ‚Üí Set P.ő©` | Factual eligibility event `{Z = z}` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, cutoffEligibility, monotonicity, muD, muY, nuD, nuY, *_isReg, *_continuousAt, support_right, support_left, one-sided limit witnesses, firstStageJump }` | Fuzzy RDD assumptions: consistency, a.s. cutoff rule `Z = 1{X ‚Č• c}`, monotonicity `D(1) ‚Č• D(0)`, latent and observable regression representatives with integral characterisations, continuity of latent regressions at `c`, two-sided local support for the law of `X`, existence of observable one-sided limits, and a nonzero latent first-stage jump at the cutoff | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_FRD` | `S.Assumptions ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Regression-representative cutoff Wald estimand `(őľ_Y(1,c)-őľ_Y(0,c))/(őľ_D(1,c)-őľ_D(0,c))`, the formal counterpart used for `prop:po-fuzzy-rdd` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{nuD_right_limit, nuD_left_limit, nuY_right_limit, nuY_left_limit}` | `S.Assumptions ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Chosen witnesses of the four one-sided observable limits via `Classical.choose` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{tendsto_nuD_right_limit, tendsto_nuD_left_limit, tendsto_nuY_right_limit, tendsto_nuY_left_limit}` | `Tendsto ‚Ä¶` | Spec lemmas: the chosen witnesses are genuine one-sided limits of `őĹ_D` and `őĹ_Y` | **Proved** |
| private D-side bridge lemmas | ‚ÄĒ | Cutoff eligibility + consistency: on right/left measurable slices of `X`, factual treatment agrees a.e. with `D(1)`/`D(0)`, yielding integral and pushforward identities between `őĹ_D` and `őľ_D` | **Proved** |
| private Y-side bridge lemmas | ‚ÄĒ | Consistency rewrites `Y` to `Y(factualD)` and then, on `{Z=z}`, to `Y(D(z))`; combined with the regression representative identities this gives the analogous bridges between `őĹ_Y` and `őľ_Y` | **Proved** |
| private a.e. agreement and extraction lemmas | ‚ÄĒ | Apply `Integrable.ae_eq_of_forall_setIntegral_eq`, local support, and `Measure.exists_mem_of_measure_ne_zero_of_ae` to obtain agreement points arbitrarily close to `c` on both sides for treatment and outcome regressions | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{nuD_right_limit_eq, nuD_left_limit_eq, nuY_right_limit_eq, nuY_left_limit_eq}` | `Tendsto őĹ_* (ūĚďĚ[>/<] c) (ūĚďĚ L) ‚Üí L = őľ_*(z,c)` | Any one-sided observable regression limit equals the corresponding latent regression representative at the cutoff, by uniqueness of limits along the agreement subfilter | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.frd_identification` | `tau_FRD hA = (nuY_right_limit hA - nuY_left_limit hA) / (nuD_right_limit hA - nuD_left_limit hA)` | **Fuzzy RDD identification at the cutoff** (regression-representative form of `prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`): the cutoff Wald estimand equals the Wald ratio of one-sided observable regression limits | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YdiffComplier` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | `(Y(1) - Y(0)) ¬∑ 1_{D(1)=1 ‚ąß D(0)=0}`: the Y-difference weighted by the complier indicator; equals `Y(D(1)) - Y(D(0))` a.e. under monotonicity | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.Assumptions.{mu_Ydiff_complier, mu_Ydiff_complier_isReg, mu_Ydiff_complier_continuousAt}` | `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, `IsRegressionFunction`, `ContinuousAt` | Regression representative for `E[(Y(1)-Y(0))¬∑1_C \| X=¬∑]`, its `IsRegressionFunction` witness, and continuity at `c`; together they support the LATE bridge | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_LATE` | `S.Assumptions ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Cutoff-local LATE `E[Y(1)-Y(0) \| X=c, C_c]` in regression-representative form: `mu_Ydiff_complier c / (muD true c - muD false c)` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_late_identification` | `tau_LATE hA = tau_FRD hA` | **Fuzzy RDD identifies cutoff-local LATE** (`prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`): under all fuzzy RDD assumptions including monotonicity, the Wald ratio `ŌĄ_FRD` equals the cutoff-local LATE `ŌĄ_LATE = E[Y(1)-Y(0)\|X=c, C_c]`; proved via `IsRegressionFunction.sub` + monotonicity case analysis + `aeEq_of_aeEq_response` + continuity at `c` | **Proved** |

---

## 9j. `PO/ID/Partial/Manski.lean` ‚ÄĒ Manski ATE Bounds under a Discrete IV (PO framework)

Formalizes the **Manski bound family** for the ATE under a discrete IV,
from `Basic Concepts.tex` ¬ß`subsec:po-iv-manski`, ¬ß`subsec:po-iv-manski-shape`,
and `rem:manski-bound-family` (lines ~1561‚Äď2010).  Split into one file per
shape restriction.  Instrument value space `őĪ` is arbitrary with
`MeasurableSingletonClass`; everything lives on the event-level
`eventCondExp` layer plus `POVar` consistency (no `CondIndepFun` stubs).

Layout:

- `Manski/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ data layer (`POManskiIVSystem`) + bound functionals.
- `Manski/Assumptions.lean` ‚ÄĒ `BaseAssumptions` + shape restrictions.
- `Manski/Helpers.lean` ‚ÄĒ shared utilities + conditional stratum bounds.
- `Manski/NonAsp.lean` ‚ÄĒ prop:po-iv-manski (baseline / mean-independence).
- `Manski/MTR.lean` ‚ÄĒ prop:po-iv-mtr (monotone treatment response).
- `Manski/MTS.lean` ‚ÄĒ prop:po-iv-mts (monotone treatment selection).
- `Manski/MIV.lean` ‚ÄĒ prop:po-iv-miv (monotone IV; integrated forms under `[Fintype őĪ]`).
- `Manski/Combined.lean` ‚ÄĒ cor:po-iv-mtr-mts, cor:po-iv-mtr-miv.
- `Manski.lean` ‚ÄĒ thin re-export.

### Setup (`Manski/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POManskiIVSystem P őĪ` | `structure { Z, D, Y, hZ, hDbool, hYreal, hZD, hZY, hDY }` | Discrete-IV subsystem over a PO system: instrument `Z : őĪ`, binary `D`, real `Y`, pairwise distinctness | **Defined** |
| `zVar` / `dVar` / `yVar` | `POVar P őĪ` / `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P ‚ĄĚ` | `POVar` wrappers around `Z`, `D`, `Y` | **Defined** |
| `YofD S d` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(d) := yVar.cfUnder dVar d` | **Defined** |
| `factualZ` / `factualD` / `factualY` | `P.ő© ‚Üí őĪ / Bool / ‚ĄĚ` | Factual instrument / treatment / outcome | **Defined** |
| `zEvent S z` / `dEvent S d` | `Set P.ő©` | Factual events `{Z = z}` / `{D = d}` | **Defined** |
| measurability lemmas | `measurable_{YofD, factualZ, factualD, factualY}`, `measurableSet_{zEvent, dEvent}` | One-line delegations to `POVar` | **Proved** |
| `ATE` | `‚ĄĚ := ‚ąę (YofD true - YofD false) ‚ąāP.őľ` | Target parameter, average treatment effect | **Defined** |
| `support S` | `Set őĪ := {z \| P.őľ (zEvent z) ‚Č† 0}` | Support of the instrument | **Defined** |
| `boundArm d c z` | `‚ĄĚ` | Unified arm bound functional `eventCondExp őľ (zEvent z) (factualY ¬∑ dVar.indicator d + c ¬∑ dVar.indicator (!d))`; specialises to the four named bounds below | **Defined** |
| `lowerBound1 lo z` | `‚ĄĚ := boundArm true lo z` | Observable `L_{1,z}`; now a one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `upperBound1 hi z` | `‚ĄĚ := boundArm true hi z` | Observable `U_{1,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `lowerBound0 lo z` | `‚ĄĚ := boundArm false lo z` | Observable `L_{0,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `upperBound0 hi z` | `‚ĄĚ := boundArm false hi z` | Observable `U_{0,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |

### Assumptions (`Manski/Assumptions.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BaseAssumptions S` | `structure { consistency, lo, hi, hle, bounded_one, bounded_zero, integrable_{Y1,Y0} }` | Shared hypotheses: consistency, outcome bounds `lo ‚Č§ Y(d) ‚Č§ hi` a.s., integrability of `Y(1)` and `Y(0)`. Factual-`Y` integrability is *derived*, not bundled (see `integrable_factualY`) | **Defined** |
| `BaseAssumptions.bounded hA d` | `‚ąÄŠĶź ŌČ ‚ąāP.őľ, hA.lo ‚Č§ S.YofD d ŌČ ‚ąß S.YofD d ŌČ ‚Č§ hA.hi` | Binary-folded form of `bounded_one` / `bounded_zero`: `Y(d)` is a.s. in `[lo, hi]` uniformly in `d : Bool`. Derived by `cases d` | **Proved** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrable_YofD hA d` | `Integrable (S.YofD d) P.őľ` | Binary-folded form of `integrable_Y1` / `integrable_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrable_factualY hA` | `Integrable S.factualY P.őľ` | Factual outcome integrability, *derived* from arm integrability via consistency (`factualY = ő£_d Y(d)¬∑1{D=d}` a.e.); no longer an assumed field | **Proved** |
| `MeanIndep S` | `Prop { meanIndep_one, meanIndep_zero }` | Mean independence `E[Y(d) \| Z=z] = E[Y(d)]` on `eventCondExp`, for `d ‚ąą {0,1}` | **Defined** |
| `MTR S` | `Prop { monotone }` | Monotone treatment response: `Y(0) ‚Č§ Y(1)` a.s. | **Defined** |
| `MTS S` | `Prop { mts_one }` | Monotone treatment selection: `eventCondExp őľ (dEvent false) (YofD d) ‚Č§ eventCondExp őľ (dEvent true) (YofD d)` for `d ‚ąą {0,1}` | **Defined** |
| `MIV S` | `structure { inst, monotone }` | Monotone instrumental variable: bundled `LinearOrder ‚ÜĎS.support` and monotonicity of `u ‚Ü¶ eventCondExp őľ (zEvent u) (YofD d)` on the support | **Defined** |

### Helpers (`Manski/Helpers.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| integrability lemmas | `factualY_mul_indD_integrable`, `YofD_mul_indD_integrable`, `const_mul_indD_integrable` | Integrability side conditions under `[IsFiniteMeasure P.őľ]` + `BaseAssumptions`; now consume `POVar.indicator` + `integrable_indicator` / `indicator_eq_one_or_zero` | **Proved** |
| pointwise fill-in lemmas | `dVar_inds_at_ŌČ` (private), `YofD_indD_plus_lo_le_YofD`, `YofD_le_YofD_indD_plus_hi` | a.e. "floor/ceiling fill-in" bounds; `dVar_inds_at_ŌČ` packages the binary case split for `(indicator d, indicator (!d))` | **Proved** |
| `boundArm_lo_le_cond_YofD` | `[IsFiniteMeasure], BaseAssumptions, d : Bool, z ‚ąą support ‚Üí boundArm d hA.lo z ‚Č§ eventCondExp őľ (zEvent z) (YofD d)` | Parametric conditional stratum lower bound; the mean-indep-free core used by `NonAsp` and `MIV` | **Proved** |
| `cond_YofD_le_boundArm_hi` | analogous upper, parametric in `d` | Parametric conditional stratum upper bound | **Proved** |
| `lowerBound1_le_cond_Y1` | ‚ÄĒ | Legacy corollary: `boundArm_lo_le_cond_YofD hA true` | **Proved** |
| `cond_Y1_le_upperBound1` | ‚ÄĒ | Legacy corollary: `cond_YofD_le_boundArm_hi hA true` | **Proved** |
| `lowerBound0_le_cond_Y0` | ‚ÄĒ | Legacy corollary: `boundArm_lo_le_cond_YofD hA false` | **Proved** |
| `cond_Y0_le_upperBound0` | ‚ÄĒ | Legacy corollary: `cond_YofD_le_boundArm_hi hA false` | **Proved** |

### NonAsp (`Manski/NonAsp.lean`) ‚ÄĒ prop:po-iv-manski

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lowerBound1_le_integral_Y1` | `[IsFiniteMeasure], BaseAssumptions, MeanIndep, z ‚ąą support ‚Üí lowerBound1 hA.lo z ‚Č§ ‚ąę YofD true ‚ąāőľ` | Stratum-level lower bound for `E[Y(1)]` via `lowerBound1_le_cond_Y1` + mean-indep collapse | **Proved** |
| `integral_Y1_le_upperBound1` | analogous upper | Stratum-level upper bound for `E[Y(1)]` | **Proved** |
| `lowerBound0_le_integral_Y0` | ‚ÄĒ | Stratum-level lower bound for `E[Y(0)]` | **Proved** |
| `integral_Y0_le_upperBound0` | ‚ÄĒ | Stratum-level upper bound for `E[Y(0)]` | **Proved** |
| `manski_bounds_ATE` | per-stratum-pair sandwich `L_{1,z‚āĀ} - U_{0,z‚āÄ} ‚Č§ ATE ‚Č§ U_{1,z‚āĀ} - L_{0,z‚āÄ}` | Manski bounds, per-pair form; strictly stronger than sup/inf form | **Proved** |
| `ciSup_lowerBound1_le_integral_Y1` | `support.Nonempty ‚Üí ‚®Ü z, L_{1,z} ‚Č§ ‚ąę Y(1)` | sup aggregation over support | **Proved** |
| `integral_Y1_le_ciInf_upperBound1` | inf form | inf aggregation of `U_{1,¬∑}` | **Proved** |
| `ciSup_lowerBound0_le_integral_Y0` / `integral_Y0_le_ciInf_upperBound0` | ‚ÄĒ | sup/inf aggregations for arm 0 | **Proved** |
| `manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup` | `support.Nonempty ‚Üí (‚®Ü L_{1}) - (‚®Ö U_{0}) ‚Č§ ATE ‚Č§ (‚®Ö U_{1}) - (‚®Ü L_{0})` | Manski bounds in tex form (sup/inf over support) | **Proved** |

### MTR (`Manski/MTR.lean`) ‚ÄĒ prop:po-iv-mtr

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mtr_E_Y_le_E_Y1` | Step 1: `‚ąę Y ‚Č§ ‚ąę Y(1)` via a.s. inequality `Y ‚Č§ Y(1)` from consistency + MTR | **Proved** |
| `mtr_E_Y0_le_E_Y` | Step 4: `‚ąę Y(0) ‚Č§ ‚ąę Y` symmetrically | **Proved** |
| `mtr_E_Y1_le_upper` | Step 2: `‚ąę Y(1) ‚Č§ p¬∑E[Y\|D=1] + (1-p)¬∑hi` via total-law decomposition + consistency + ceiling bound | **Proved** |
| `mtr_lower_le_E_Y0` | Step 3: `(1-p)¬∑E[Y\|D=0] + p¬∑lo ‚Č§ ‚ąę Y(0)` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_nonneg_ATE` | `0 ‚Č§ ATE` from MTR a.s. inequality | **Proved** |
| `mtr_bounds_ATE` | Two-sided ATE sandwich: `0 ‚Č§ ATE ‚Č§ (p¬∑E[Y\|D=1]+(1-p)¬∑hi) - ((1-p)¬∑E[Y\|D=0]+p¬∑lo)` | **Proved** |

### MTS (`Manski/MTS.lean`) ‚ÄĒ prop:po-iv-mts

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mts_lower_le_E_Y1` | `p¬∑E[Y\|D=1] + (1-p)¬∑lo ‚Č§ ‚ąę Y(1)` (MTS lower on arm 1) | **Proved** |
| `mts_E_Y1_le_condY1` | `‚ąę Y(1) ‚Č§ E[Y\|D=1]` (MTS upper on arm 1) | **Proved** |
| `mts_condY0_le_E_Y0` | `E[Y\|D=0] ‚Č§ ‚ąę Y(0)` (MTS lower on arm 0) | **Proved** |
| `mts_E_Y0_le_upper` | `‚ąę Y(0) ‚Č§ p¬∑hi + (1-p)¬∑E[Y\|D=0]` (MTS upper on arm 0) | **Proved** |
| `mts_bounds_ATE` | Two-sided MTS ATE sandwich, obtained by subtracting the four arm bounds | **Proved** |

### MIV (`Manski/MIV.lean`) ‚ÄĒ prop:po-iv-miv

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mLower1`, `mUpper1`, `mLower0`, `mUpper0` | Envelope functions `sSup { L_{d,u} \| u ‚Č§ z }` and `sInf { U_{d,u} \| z ‚Č§ u }` on the MIV linear order | **Defined** |
| `miv_mLower1_le_cond_Y1` | `mLower1 z ‚Č§ eventCondExp őľ (zEvent z) (YofD true)` on support; chains `Helpers` conditional bound with MIV monotonicity | **Proved** |
| `miv_cond_Y1_le_mUpper1` | analogous upper | **Proved** |
| `miv_mLower0_le_cond_Y0` | lower for arm 0 | **Proved** |
| `miv_cond_Y0_le_mUpper0` | upper for arm 0 | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_mLower1_le_integral_Y1` | `[Fintype őĪ]`: `‚ąę mLower1(Z) ‚Č§ ‚ąę Y(1)`; decomposes each side via `integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp` and compares stratum-wise, exploiting that off-support strata have measure `0` | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_Y1_le_integral_mUpper1` | `[Fintype őĪ]`: `‚ąę Y(1) ‚Č§ ‚ąę mUpper1(Z)`; symmetric to the lower bound | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_mLower0_le_integral_Y0` | `[Fintype őĪ]`: arm 0 integrated lower | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_Y0_le_integral_mUpper0` | `[Fintype őĪ]`: arm 0 integrated upper | **Proved** |
| `miv_bounds_ATE` | `[Fintype őĪ]`: `‚ąę (mLower1(Z) - mUpper0(Z)) ‚Č§ ATE ‚Č§ ‚ąę (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))`; follows by linearity from the four integrated bounds | **Proved** |

### Combined (`Manski/Combined.lean`)

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mtr_mts_bounds_ATE` | cor:po-iv-mtr-mts: `0 ‚Č§ ATE ‚Č§ E[Y\|D=1] - E[Y\|D=0]` under MTR + MTS; combines `mtr_nonneg_ATE` with `mts_E_Y1_le_condY1 ‚ą© mts_condY0_le_E_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_miv_bounds_ATE` | `[Fintype őĪ]`: cor:po-iv-mtr-miv: `0 ‚Č§ ATE` (MTR) and `ATE ‚Č§ ‚ąę (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))` (MIV); forwards from `miv_bounds_ATE` | **Proved** |

### IntervalForm (`Manski/IntervalForm.lean`) ‚ÄĒ `Set.Icc` restatements

Pure restatements of the ATE sandwiches as `S.ATE ‚ąą Set.Icc lo hi`, obtained by
feeding each `L ‚Č§ ATE ‚ąß ATE ‚Č§ U` pair through the
`Causalean.PartialID.mem_Icc_of_sandwich` bridge (`Basic.lean`). No new
mathematical content; same hypotheses/instances as each source theorem.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `manski_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the Manski no-assumption per-stratum-pair ATE sandwich (`manski_bounds_ATE`), via the `mem_Icc_of_sandwich` bridge | **Proved** |
| `manski_ATE_mem_Icc_ciSup` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the sup/inf-aggregated Manski no-assumption ATE sandwich (`manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup`) | **Proved** |
| `mtr_mts_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the MTR + MTS ATE sandwich (`mtr_mts_bounds_ATE`): `ATE ‚ąą [0, E[Y\|D=1] - E[Y\|D=0]]` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_miv_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | `[Fintype őĪ]`: closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the MTR + MIV ATE sandwich (`mtr_miv_bounds_ATE`): `ATE ‚ąą [0, ‚ąę (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))]` | **Proved** |

**Scope note.** Two baseline aggregation forms are exposed in `NonAsp`: the
per-stratum-pair `manski_bounds_ATE` is strictly stronger (pick any
`z‚āĀ, z‚āÄ ‚ąą support`), while `manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup` matches the tex's
sup/inf form but requires `support.Nonempty` (Lean `‚®Ü`/`‚®Ö` fall back to `0`
on empty index sets). The MIV integrated bounds require a `[Fintype őĪ]`
hypothesis on the instrument value space (standard Manski setting with a
discrete IV) and are discharged via the `integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp`
total law in `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExp.lean`; no outstanding `sorry` in the folder.

---

## 9j2. `PO/ID/Partial/` ‚ÄĒ Abstract Partial-Identification Bounds Engine

Reusable, PO-framework-free partial-identification primitives. Two files, both in
namespace `Causalean.PartialID`:

- `Basic.lean` ‚ÄĒ the sharp identified interval + a sharp-interval API, including the
  two scalar-sandwich bridge helpers (`mem_Icc_of_sandwich`, `mem_Icc_csInf_csSup`)
  that turn concrete `L ‚Č§ őł ‚Č§ U` bounds into the engine's `Set.Icc` vocabulary.
- `Frechet.lean` ‚ÄĒ Fr√©chet‚ÄďHoeffding copula bounds + the easy Makarov lower bound.

### `Basic.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible` | `Set ‚ĄĚ` | Sharp identified interval: range of `obj` over all `{x // feasible x}`; the set of all attainable objective values | **Defined** |
| `mem_identifiedInterval hx` | `obj x ‚ąą IdentifiedInterval obj feasible` | If `x` is feasible then `obj x` is in the identified interval | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_subset_Icc hL hU` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible ‚äÜ Set.Icc L U` | Outer bound: uniform two-sided bounds `L ‚Č§ obj ‚Č§ U` on the feasible set imprison the identified set in `[L, U]` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_eq_Icc hL hU hLmem hUmem hconn` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible = Set.Icc L U` | Sharp bound (order-connected form): outer bound + both endpoints attained + identified set order-connected ‚áí identified set is exactly `[L, U]` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_param_Icc hfeas hcont hL hU hbound` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible = Set.Icc L U` | Mixing-pattern constructor: feasible set = image of `[0,1]` under `ő≥`, `obj‚ąėő≥` continuous, endpoint values `L,U` bracketing `obj` along the path ‚áí sharp interval `[L,U]` (continuous image of a connected set is order-connected) | **Proved** |
| `mem_Icc_of_sandwich hlo hhi` | `őł ‚ąą Set.Icc L U` | Scalar-sandwich bridge: the literal reading of a two-sided bound `L ‚Č§ őł ‚Č§ U` as `őł ‚ąą [L, U]`; names the inequality-pair ‚Üí `Set.Icc` step every concrete bound ends in. Consumers: `Manski`/`Proxy` `IntervalForm` | **Proved** |
| `mem_Icc_csInf_csSup hb ha hy` | `y ‚ąą Set.Icc (sInf s) (sSup s)` | Worst/best case over a nuisance: every member of a bounded set `s ‚äÜ ‚ĄĚ` lies between its infimum and supremum (the `csInf_le`/`le_csSup` step of a sharp bound). Consumers: `Lee/TrimBound`, `BalkePearl/IntervalForm` | **Proved** |

### `Frechet.lean`

Self-contained measure theory over a probability space `P` with two real random
variables `X, Y`; CDF/joint-CDF values written as `.toReal` of event measures.
Only the *easy* (fixed-joint-law) directions are asserted ‚ÄĒ no sharpness claim.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `frechet_upper u v` | `(P{X‚Č§u ‚ąß Y‚Č§v}).toReal ‚Č§ min (P{X‚Č§u}).toReal (P{Y‚Č§v}).toReal` | Fr√©chet‚ÄďHoeffding upper bound on the joint CDF (joint event ‚äÜ each marginal event); needs no measurability ‚ÄĒ pure measure monotonicity | **Proved** |
| `frechet_lower hY u v` | `max ((P{X‚Č§u}).toReal + (P{Y‚Č§v}).toReal - 1) 0 ‚Č§ (P{X‚Č§u ‚ąß Y‚Č§v}).toReal` | Fr√©chet‚ÄďHoeffding lower bound on the joint CDF (inclusion‚Äďexclusion `P A + P B - 1 ‚Č§ P(A‚ą©B)`); only `Measurable Y` needed | **Proved** |
| `makarov_lower_param hY s a` | `max ((P{X‚Č§a}).toReal - (P{Y<a-s}).toReal) 0 ‚Č§ (P{X-Y‚Č§s}).toReal` | Makarov lower bound on the CDF of the difference `X-Y`, per reference point `a` (from `{X‚Č§a}‚ą©{a-s‚Č§Y} ‚äÜ {X-Y‚Č§s}` + Fr√©chet lower); only `Measurable Y` needed | **Proved** |
| `makarov_lower_iSup hY s` | `‚®Ü a, max ((P{X‚Č§a}).toReal - (P{Y<a-s}).toReal) 0 ‚Č§ (P{X-Y‚Č§s}).toReal` | Sup-convolution envelope form: the supremum over reference points still lower-bounds the CDF of `X-Y` | **Proved** |

### `RandomSet/Interval.lean`

Tier-0 random-set substrate for partial identification (the measure-theoretic
upgrade of `IdentifiedInterval`). An interval random set is a pair of measurable
endpoints `L, U : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with `L ‚Č§ U`, viewed as `ŌČ ‚Ü¶ [L ŌČ, U ŌČ]`. Provides the
two facts a sharpness proof needs ‚ÄĒ measurable selection and the selection
(Aumann) expectation ‚ÄĒ both proved *elementarily* (no Kuratowski‚ÄďRyll-Nardzewski,
no atomlessness/Lyapunov). Endpoints are reported via `sInf`/`sSup` to match the
`SandwichInterval` convention and keep the support-function generalisation a
drop-in. Namespace `Causalean.PartialID.RandomSet`; all sorry-free + axiom-clean.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `randomInterval L U` | `ő© ‚Üí Set ‚ĄĚ` | The interval-valued random closed set `ŌČ ‚Ü¶ [L ŌČ, U ŌČ]` | **Defined** |
| `IsSelection L U f` | `Prop` | `f` is a measurable everywhere-selection: `Measurable f ‚ąß ‚ąÄ ŌČ, f ŌČ ‚ąą [L ŌČ, U ŌČ]` | **Defined** |
| `isSelection_left hL hLU` | `IsSelection L U L` | The lower endpoint is always a selection ‚áí the random set has a measurable selection | **Proved** |
| `isSelection_iff_exists_param hL hU hLU f` | `IsSelection L U f ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ t, Measurable t ‚ąß (‚ąÄ ŌČ, t ŌČ ‚ąą [0,1]) ‚ąß ‚ąÄ ŌČ, f ŌČ = L ŌČ + t ŌČ¬∑(U ŌČ‚ąíL ŌČ)` | Measurable selection of an interval random set: every selection is `L + t¬∑(U‚ąíL)` for a measurable `t : ő© ‚Üí [0,1]`, and conversely (divide-by-width construction) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation L U őľ` | `Set ‚ĄĚ` | The selection (Aumann) expectation: integrals of integrable measurable selections, `{‚ąęf \| f a selection}` | **Defined** |
| `integral_le_integral_of_le hLint hUint hLU` | `(‚ąęL) ‚Č§ ‚ąęU` | Endpoint integrals are ordered (monotonicity of the Bochner integral) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation_eq_Icc hL hU hLint hUint hLU` | `selectionExpectation L U őľ = Set.Icc (‚ąęL) (‚ąęU)` | **Tier-0 core:** the selection expectation is exactly `[‚ąęL, ‚ąęU]` (‚äÜ by integral monotonicity; ‚äá realised by a *constant* `t ‚ąą [0,1]`, so no atomlessness needed) | **Proved** |
| `sInf_selectionExpectation ‚Ä¶` | `sInf (selectionExpectation L U őľ) = ‚ąęL` | Sharp lower endpoint as the infimum over selections | **Proved** |
| `sSup_selectionExpectation ‚Ä¶` | `sSup (selectionExpectation L U őľ) = ‚ąęU` | Sharp upper endpoint as the supremum over selections | **Proved** |

*Out of scope (flagged for later):* conditional selection expectation
`E[X‚ą£ūĚíĘ] = [E[L‚ą£ūĚíĘ], E[U‚ą£ūĚíĘ]]` (Tier-0.5, for regression envelopes such as
Manski‚ÄďTamer interval regression), and the support-function / Artstein /
CLR-inference layers.

### `LP/ConicDuality.lean`

Abstract conic linear-programming duality over a pair of **real Hilbert spaces**,
the value-form backbone for sharp partial-ID bounds posed as
`min ‚ü™c,x‚üę s.t. A x = b, x ‚ąą K`. Built on Mathlib's `ProperCone` / `innerDual`.
Hilbert generality is already infinite-dimensional (e.g. `L¬≤(őľ)` for proxy/bridge
functions). The *measure*-cone case (weak-‚ąó duality of measures against `C(X)`)
is **out of scope**: Mathlib gives `FiniteMeasure` only a `Module ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0` (no signed
`‚ĄĚ`-module), no ordered-cone instance, and a non-order weak-‚ąó topology ‚ÄĒ so the
nonnegative-measure `ProperCone` needs new infrastructure (a module note in the
file records this). Namespace `Causalean.PartialID`; weak duality + Farkas are
sorry-free, strong duality is a CQ-gated statement awaiting a modelling decision.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ConicProgram E F` | structure | Primal data `(K, A, b, c)`: cone `K : ProperCone ‚ĄĚ E`, operator `A : E ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] F`, rhs `b : F`, objective `c : E` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.PrimalFeasible x` | `Prop` | `A x = b ‚ąß x ‚ąą K` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.DualFeasible y` | `Prop` | reduced cost in the dual cone: `c ‚ąí AŠĶÄ y ‚ąą innerDual K` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.primalValue` | `‚ĄĚ` | `sInf { ‚ü™c,x‚üę : x primal feasible }` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.dualValue` | `‚ĄĚ` | `sSup { ‚ü™b,y‚üę : y dual feasible }` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.weak_duality hx hy` | `‚ü™b,y‚üę ‚Č§ ‚ü™c,x‚üę` | Pointwise weak duality: any dual-feasible value lower-bounds any primal-feasible value (via `mem_innerDual` + adjoint) | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.dualValue_le_primalValue hP hD` | `dualValue ‚Č§ primalValue` | Value form of weak duality (both feasible; no boundedness needed) | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.farkas` | `b ‚ąą ProperCone.map A K ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ y, AŠĶÄ y ‚ąą innerDual K ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ ‚ü™b,y‚üę` | Feasibility alternative / strong-duality engine; restatement of `ProperCone.relative_hyperplane_separation`. `map` bakes in the closure of `A(K)` ‚ÄĒ exactly the CQ gap | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.augmentedImage` | `Set (F √ó ‚ĄĚ)` | The augmented image cone `{(A x, ‚ü™c,x‚üę) : x ‚ąą K}`; its closedness is the primal-attainment CQ | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.strong_duality_primal_attained hP hbdd hCQ` | `‚ąÉ x, PrimalFeasible x ‚ąß ‚ü™c,x‚üę = primalValue` | Strong duality I (closedness CQ): the primal optimum is attained ‚ÄĒ an extremal feasible point exists. Via the closed value-slice + `IsClosed.csInf_mem` | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.strong_duality_zero_gap hP hbdd hCQ` | `primalValue = dualValue` | Strong duality II (closedness CQ ‚ÄĒ same as I): no duality gap. Closedness suffices (no Slater needed). Proof: for each `őĶ>0`, separate `(b, primalValue‚ąíőĶ)` from the augmented cone `map √É K` (`√É x = (A x, ‚ü™c,x‚üę)`) via `ProperCone.hyperplane_separation_point` on the normed product `F √ó ‚ĄĚ`; Riesz-decompose the functional as `‚ü™y‚āÄ,v‚üę + s¬∑t`, rule out `s ‚Č§ 0`, and read off the dual-feasible `‚ąí(1/s)‚ÄĘy‚āÄ`. | **Proved** |

## 9j2inf. `PO/ID/Partial/Inference/` ‚ÄĒ Confidence intervals for interval-identified parameters

First statistical-inference layer over the partial-identification *geometry*. Where the rest of `PartialID/` produces population sandwiches `L ‚Č§ őł‚āÄ ‚Č§ U`, this folder turns them into finite-sample confidence intervals, formalizing the two coverage notions of the literature (Horowitz‚ÄďManski 2000 set coverage vs. Imbens‚ÄďManski 2004 parameter coverage). Built on `Stat/Concentration/ConfidenceInterval` (Hoeffding/Bernstein miss bounds) + a union bound.

### `Inference/Basic.lean` ‚ÄĒ coverage vocabulary + abstract honest-CI lemma (pure measure theory)

| Declaration | Type / statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `RandomCoversPoint A B őł` | `Set ő©` | Event that the random interval `[A ŌČ, B ŌČ]` contains the fixed value `őł` (Imbens‚ÄďManski parameter-coverage notion) | **Defined** |
| `RandomCoversIcc A B L U` | `Set ő©` | Event that `[A ŌČ, B ŌČ]` contains the whole population interval `[L, U]` (Horowitz‚ÄďManski set-coverage notion) | **Defined** |
| `randomCoversIcc_subset_randomCoversPoint` | `őł ‚ąą [L,U] ‚Üí RandomCoversIcc A B L U ‚äÜ RandomCoversPoint A B őł` | Set coverage ‚üĻ point coverage; the easy half of Imbens‚ÄďManski (2004, Lemma 1): parameter-coverage CIs are weakly smaller than set-coverage CIs | **Proved** |
| `randomCoversIcc_compl_subset` | `(RandomCoversIcc (lo‚ąíwL) (hi+wU) L U)Š∂ú ‚äÜ {wL ‚Č§ |lo‚ąíL|} ‚ą™ {wU ‚Č§ |hi‚ąíU|}` | Non-coverage is contained in the two one-sided endpoint-failure events; geometric heart of the union bound | **Proved** |
| `honest_ci_set_cover` | `1 ‚ąí őīL ‚ąí őīU ‚Č§ őľ.real (RandomCoversIcc (lo‚ąíwL) (hi+wU) L U)` | **Abstract honest CI.** Given one-sided miss bounds `őľ{wL‚Č§|lo‚ąíL|}‚Č§őīL`, `őľ{wU‚Č§|hi‚ąíU|}‚Č§őīU`, the widened interval covers `[L,U]` w.p. `‚Č•1‚ąíőīL‚ąíőīU` (single union bound; stated in miss probabilities so any concentration inequality plugs in) | **Proved** |
| `honest_ci_point_cover` | `őł ‚ąą [L,U] ‚Üí 1 ‚ąí őīL ‚ąí őīU ‚Č§ őľ.real (RandomCoversPoint (lo‚ąíwL) (hi+wU) őł)` | Parameter-coverage corollary: covering the set suffices to cover any point of it (conservative Horowitz‚ÄďManski parameter CI) | **Proved** |

### `Inference/IntervalCI.lean` ‚ÄĒ finite-sample plug-in instantiations (Hoeffding / Bernstein)

Plug-in Manski sandwich: bounding statistics `f_L, f_U` with population means `L = ‚ąę f_L`, `U = ‚ąę f_U`; estimate each endpoint by its sample mean and widen by a concentration half-width.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hoeffding_honest_ci_set_cover` | `1‚ąíőīL‚ąíőīU ‚Č§ őľ.real (RandomCoversIcc [XŐĄ‚āô(f_L)‚ąíwL, XŐĄ‚āô(f_U)+wU] L U)` | Honest set CI with Hoeffding half-widths for bounded statistics `f_L‚ąą[aL,bL]`, `f_U‚ąą[aU,bU]`; valid at every `n` | **Proved** |
| `hoeffding_honest_ci_point_cover` | `őł‚āÄ‚ąą[‚ąęf_L,‚ąęf_U] ‚Üí 1‚ąíőīL‚ąíőīU ‚Č§ őľ.real (RandomCoversPoint ‚Ä¶ őł‚āÄ)` | Parameter-coverage version (Hoeffding) | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_honest_ci_point_cover` | as above with `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` | Variance-adaptive version; far tighter when the bounding statistics have small variance (leading term `2ŌÉ‚ąö(log(2/őī)/n)`) | **Proved** |

### `Inference/ImbensManski.lean` ‚ÄĒ asymptotic one-sided sharp interval (Imbens‚ÄďManski 2004)

When the identified set has positive width `őĒ = U‚ąíL > 0`, covering the single true `őł‚āÄ` (not the whole set) needs protection on only **one** side: the `O(n^{-1/2})` sampling error is asymptotically negligible vs the fixed `őĒ`, so non-coverage is one-sided and a *one-sided* critical value `c = z_{1‚ąíőĪ}` already gives `1‚ąíőĪ` coverage (vs the two-sided `z_{1‚ąíőĪ/2}` the set-coverage interval needs). Normalized endpoint statistics `S‚āó,‚āô = ‚ąön(őłŐā‚āó‚ąíL)/ŌÉ‚āó`, `SŠĶ§,‚āô = ‚ąön(őłŐāŠĶ§‚ąíU)/ŌÉŠĶ§ ‚áí N(0,1)`; offsets `a‚āô=‚ąön(őł‚āÄ‚ąíL)/ŌÉ‚āó‚Č•0`, `b‚āô=‚ąön(U‚ąíőł‚āÄ)/ŌÉŠĶ§‚Č•0`. Sources: Imbens‚ÄďManski 2004 (Lemma 1), Stoye 2009, Molinari 2020 ¬ßPointwise-vs-Uniform.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_frontier_Iic c` | `gaussianMeasure 0 1 (frontier (Iic c)) = 0` | Standard normal gives zero mass to the boundary `{c}` of a half-line; continuity-point fact for one-sided portmanteau | **Proved** |
| `farEnd_vanishes_of_tendsto_atBot` | `SŠĶ§ ‚áí N(0,1) ‚Üí t ‚ü∂ atBot ‚Üí (őľ{SŠĶ§,‚āô < t‚āô}).toReal ‚ü∂ 0` | The `őĒ>0` mechanism: as the far threshold drifts to `‚ąí‚ąě` (`b‚āô‚Üí‚ąě`), the far-endpoint failure probability vanishes. Bounds the moving tail by a fixed Gaussian tail (`gaussian_tail_small`) + portmanteau | **Proved** |
| `imbensManski_pointwise_coverage` | `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí ‚ąÄőĶ>0, ‚ąÄŠ∂† n, ő¶(c) ‚ąí őĶ ‚Č§ (őľ{S‚āó,‚āô‚Č§c+a‚āô ‚ąß ‚ąí(c+b‚āô)‚Č§SŠĶ§,‚āô}).toReal` | **Core asymptotic guarantee.** With `S‚āó‚áíN(0,1)`, `a‚āô‚Č•0`, and the far end vanishing, IM coverage of `őł‚āÄ` eventually exceeds `ő¶(c)‚ąíőĶ` where `ő¶(c)=N(0,1)((-‚ąě,c])`. One-sided `c=z_{1‚ąíőĪ}` ‚üĻ `‚Č•1‚ąíőĪ`. Pointwise (not uniform; Stoye's superefficiency caveat not addressed) | **Proved** |

Supporting lemma `Causalean.Stat.gaussian_tail_small` (Gaussian half-line tails `<őĶ` for large cutoff) was factored out of the quantile-Bahadur rate proof into `Causalean/Stat/CLT/GaussianTail.lean` for reuse here.

## 9j2sf. `PO/ID/Partial/SupportFunction/` ‚ÄĒ Support-Function Identified Sets (convex/Hilbert spine)

The multivariate generalization of the scalar `Basic.lean` engine: when the
feasible set `C` is **convex** in a real inner-product space and the target is the
**linear functional** `x ‚Ü¶ ‚ü™d, x‚üę`, the sharp identified interval is governed by
the *support function* `supportFn C d = sup { ‚ü™d,x‚üę : x ‚ąą C }`. Three files, all in
namespace `Causalean.PartialID`. Boundedness/nonemptiness side conditions are carried
explicitly (the real `sSup` convention), matching `SandwichInterval`/`RandomSet`.
This is the abstraction the proximal-L2-bridge, sensitivity-model, and reweighting
partial-ID problems bottom out in.

- `Basic.lean` ‚ÄĒ `supportFn` definition + the two monotone spine lemmas.
- `Calculus.lean` ‚ÄĒ the support-function algebra (direction + set operations).
- `Interval.lean` ‚ÄĒ sharp identified interval `[-h(-d), h(d)]`, width, point-identification, scalar-engine bridge.
- `AffineBall.lean` ‚ÄĒ closed-form endpoints over a Hilbert affine fiber `{A h = b}` intersected with a ball `‚ÄĖh‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ B` (the proximal-L2-bridge identified set).

### `Basic.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supportFn C d` | `‚ĄĚ := sSup ((fun x => ‚ü™d,x‚üę) '' C)` | Support function: best-case value of the linear target `‚ü™d,¬∑‚üę` over the feasible set `C` | **Defined** |
| `supportFn_eq_iSup_image C d` | `supportFn C d = sSup (‚Ä¶)` | Unfolding lemma exposing the `sSup`-of-image form | **Proved** |
| `le_supportFn hx hbdd` | `‚ü™d,x‚üę ‚Č§ supportFn C d` | Lower sandwich: a feasible point's value is `‚Č§` the support value (needs the image bounded above) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_le hne hb` | `supportFn C d ‚Č§ b` | Upper sandwich: a uniform upper bound `b` on the functional over nonempty `C` bounds the support value | **Proved** |

### `Calculus.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supportFn_singleton d x‚āÄ` | `supportFn {x‚āÄ} d = ‚ü™d,x‚āÄ‚üę` | Support function of a point is the functional value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_nonneg_of_zero_mem h0 hbdd` | `0 ‚Č§ supportFn C d` | `0 ‚ąą C` forces a nonnegative support value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_smul_dir ht hne hbdd` | `supportFn C (t‚ÄĘd) = t * supportFn C d` | Positive homogeneity in the direction (`t ‚Č• 0`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_add_dir_le hne hb‚āĀ hb‚āā` | `supportFn C (d‚āĀ+d‚āā) ‚Č§ supportFn C d‚āĀ + supportFn C d‚āā` | Subadditivity / sublinearity in the direction | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_mono hCD hne hbdd` | `supportFn C d ‚Č§ supportFn D d` | Monotone in the set (`C ‚äÜ D`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_union hC hD hbC hbD` | `supportFn (C‚ą™D) d = max (supportFn C d) (supportFn D d)` | Union rule | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_inter_le hne hbC hbD` | `supportFn (C‚ą©D) d ‚Č§ min (supportFn C d) (supportFn D d)` | Intersection rule (one-sided) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_translate x‚āÄ hne hbdd` | `supportFn ((x‚āÄ+¬∑) '' C) d = ‚ü™d,x‚āÄ‚üę + supportFn C d` | Translation shifts the support value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_smul_set ha hne hbdd` | `supportFn (a‚ÄĘC) d = a * supportFn C d` | Positive set-scaling (`a ‚Č• 0`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_minkowski hC hD hbC hbD` | `supportFn (C+D) d = supportFn C d + supportFn D d` | Additivity over Minkowski sums | **Proved** |

### `Interval.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `neg_supportFn_neg_le hx hbdd'` | `-supportFn C (-d) ‚Č§ ‚ü™d,x‚üę` | Lower endpoint `-h(-d)` bounds the functional from below (worst case) | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_subset_Icc hbdd hbdd'` | `(‚ü™d,¬∑‚üę) '' C ‚äÜ Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | Outer bound: identified set sits inside the support interval | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_ordConnected hC` | `((‚ü™d,¬∑‚üę) '' C).OrdConnected` | Convex `C` ‚áí identified set has no gaps (linear image of convex is convex in `‚ĄĚ`) | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_eq_Icc hC hbdd hbdd' hUmem hLmem` | `(‚ü™d,¬∑‚üę) '' C = Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | **Sharp** interval (attainment form): convex + both endpoints attained ‚áí identified set is exactly `[-h(-d), h(d)]` | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_eq_Icc_of_isCompact hcomp hC hne` | `(‚ü™d,¬∑‚üę) '' C = Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | **Sharp** interval (compact convex form): the workhorse ‚ÄĒ continuity gives bounded + attained automatically | **Proved** |
| `width C d` | `‚ĄĚ := supportFn C d + supportFn C (-d)` | Width of the identified set (upper minus lower endpoint) | **Defined** |
| `width_nonneg hne hbdd hbdd'` | `0 ‚Č§ width C d` | The identified width is nonnegative | **Proved** |
| `width_eq_zero_iff hne hbdd hbdd'` | `width C d = 0 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą C, ‚ü™d,x‚üę = supportFn C d` | **Point identification**: zero width iff the target is constant on `C` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_eq_image obj C` | `IdentifiedInterval obj (¬∑ ‚ąą C) = obj '' C` | Bridge: the scalar `Basic.lean` engine's identified set is the set-image of the objective (so concrete theorems consume either vocabulary) | **Proved** |

### `AffineBall.lean`

Closed-form endpoints over the **bridge fiber** `affineBall A b B = {h | A h = b ‚ąß ‚ÄĖh‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ B}` in a real Hilbert space `H` (operator `A : H ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] F`). Decomposing `h = h‚āÄ + v` (min-norm solution `h‚āÄ ‚ä• ker A`, free direction `v ‚ąą ker A`), the Pythagorean split + Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz give the sharp value/width in terms of `P c = orthogonalProjection (ker A) c`. This is the `pid_proximal_l2_bridge` identified set.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `opKer A` | `Submodule ‚ĄĚ H := LinearMap.ker ‚ÜĎA` | Free-direction subspace `ker A` of the operator (closed; has an orthogonal projection ‚ÄĒ instance provided) | **Defined** |
| `affineBall A b B` | `Set H := {h | A h = b ‚ąß ‚ÄĖh‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ B}` | The affine-ball feasible fiber: equation solutions inside the norm ball | **Defined** |
| `mem_affineBall` | `h ‚ąą affineBall A b B ‚ÜĒ A h = b ‚ąß ‚ÄĖh‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ B` | Membership unfolding (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_affineBall_eq A hsol hperp hfit` | `supportFn (affineBall A b B) c = ‚ü™c,h‚āÄ‚üę + ‚ąö(B¬≤‚ąí‚ÄĖh‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤)¬∑‚ÄĖP c‚ÄĖ` | Closed-form support value (best case), given min-norm solution `h‚āÄ ‚ä• ker A` fitting the ball | **Proved** |
| `width_affineBall_eq A hsol hperp hfit` | `width (affineBall A b B) c = 2¬∑‚ąö(B¬≤‚ąí‚ÄĖh‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤)¬∑‚ÄĖP c‚ÄĖ` | Closed-form identified width | **Proved** |
| `affineBall_point_identified_iff A hsol hperp hfit` | `width (affineBall A b B) c = 0 ‚ÜĒ P c = 0` | **Point identification** (strict ball slack `‚ÄĖh‚āÄ‚ÄĖ<B`): collapse iff `c ‚ä• ker A`, i.e. `c` in the closed row space of `A` | **Proved** |

## 9j2rs. `PO/ID/Partial/RandomSet/` ‚ÄĒ Random sets, Hausdorff distance & the support-function bridge (Beresteanu‚ÄďMolinari 2008)

The estimation/inference layer for set-identified parameters. Beresteanu & Molinari (2008) show the whole inference theory rests on one identity (H√∂rmander's embedding, their eq. (A.1)): for compact convex `A,B`, `H(A,B) = sup_{‚ÄĖp‚ÄĖ=1} |s(p,A) ‚ąí s(p,B)|`. This folder builds that bridge, its `d = 1` (interval) specialization (the scalar interval-data CLT, Theorems 3.1/3.2, in `IntervalCLT.lean`), and the general-`d` finite-grid support-process CLT on genuine random sets (`SetValued.lean` + `SupportProcess.lean`, Theorem A.2). Plan: `doc/beresteanu_molinari_plan.md`. All declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

### `Interval.lean` ‚ÄĒ interval random sets & Aumann (selection) expectation (Tier-0, pre-existing)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `randomInterval L U` | `ő© ‚Üí Set ‚ĄĚ := fun ŌČ => Icc (L ŌČ) (U ŌČ)` | The interval-valued random closed set `ŌČ ‚Ü¶ [L ŌČ, U ŌČ]` | **Defined** |
| `IsSelection L U f` | `Prop` | `f` is a measurable everywhere-selection of `[L,U]` | **Defined** |
| `isSelection_iff_exists_param` | selection ‚ÜĒ `L + t¬∑(U‚ąíL)`, `t : ő©‚Üí[0,1]` measurable | Elementary measurable selection (no Kuratowski‚ÄďRyll-Nardzewski) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation L U őľ` | `Set ‚ĄĚ` | Selection (Aumann) expectation: integrals of integrable selections | **Defined** |
| `selectionExpectation_eq_Icc` | `= Icc (‚ąęL) (‚ąęU)` | **Aumann expectation `= [‚ąęL, ‚ąęU]`** (`‚äá` uses a constant `t`, no atomlessness) | **Proved** |
| `sInf_selectionExpectation` / `sSup_selectionExpectation` | `= ‚ąęL` / `= ‚ąęU` | Sharp endpoints as inf/sup over selections | **Proved** |

### `Hausdorff.lean` ‚ÄĒ directed/symmetric Hausdorff distance + the geometric keystone

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |

| `hausdorffDist A B` | `‚ĄĚ := max (dŠīī A B) (dŠīī B A)` | Symmetric Hausdorff distance | **Defined** |
| `infDist_Icc hcd x` | `infDist x [c,d] = max 0 (max (c‚ąíx) (x‚ąíd))` | Distance from a real point to an interval (zero inside; signed gap outside) | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_Icc hab hcd` | `dŠīī([a,b],[c,d]) = max 0 (max (c‚ąía) (b‚ąíd))` | Directed Hausdorff between intervals (worst over-reach, attained at endpoints) | **Proved** |

The Tier-1 capstone: `‚ąön ¬∑ H(»≤‚āô, E[Y]) ‚áí max(|z_L|, |z_U|)`, a continuous-mapping image of the multivariate CLT (`Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit`) through the Hausdorff keystone.

Takes the centered-`Ōą` multivariate-CLT hypotheses + statistic measurability | **Proved** |

### `SetValued.lean` ‚ÄĒ compact-convex set-valued RVs & the Minkowski-mean support bridge (Tier-2 substrate, general `d`)

The general-`d` substrate for the support-process CLT. A set-valued random variable takes **body** values (nonempty compact convex sets, the class `ūĚí¶‚āĖ(E)`); on a compact set the linear functional `‚ü™d,¬∑‚üę` attains its sup so `supportFn (F ŌČ) d` is well-defined. The one load-bearing fact is the **Minkowski-mean support bridge**, which turns the support value of an empirical Minkowski average into an ordinary sample mean. Pure convex geometry ‚ÄĒ no probability, no atomlessness.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsBody C` | `structure` (`nonempty`, `isCompact`, `convex`) | A body: nonempty compact convex subset of `E` ‚ÄĒ the value type of an SVRV | **Defined** |
| `bddAbove_inner_image hC d` | `BddAbove ((‚ü™d,¬∑‚üę) '' C)` | On a compact set the linear functional is bounded above (Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz on the bounded set) | **Proved** |
| `IsBody.bddAbove h d` | `BddAbove ((‚ü™d,¬∑‚üę) '' C)` | A body's support function is well-defined | **Proved** |
| `isBody_finsetSum s F h` | `IsBody (‚ąĎ i ‚ąą s, F i)` | A finite Minkowski sum of bodies is a body | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_finsetSum s F d h` | `supportFn (‚ąĎŠĶĘ FŠĶĘ) d = ‚ąĎŠĶĘ supportFn (FŠĶĘ) d` | Support function commutes with finite Minkowski sums | **Proved** |
| `minkowskiMean s F` | `Set E := (\|s\|)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ÄĘ (‚ąĎ i ‚ąą s, F i)` | The empirical Minkowski average `(1/n) ‚ÄĘ ‚ąĎŠĶĘ FŠĶĘ` | **Defined** |
| `supportFn_minkowskiMean s F d h` | `supportFn (minkowskiMean s F) d = (\|s\|)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ ‚ąĎŠĶĘ supportFn (FŠĶĘ) d` | **The keystone**: support of a Minkowski average = average of supports (makes `s(p,FŐĄ‚āô)` an iid sample mean) | **Proved** |

### `SupportProcess.lean` ‚ÄĒ Tier-2 finite-direction support-process CLT (general `d`)

The general-`d` analogue of `IntervalCLT`, the honest finite-dimensional projection of Beresteanu‚ÄďMolinari Theorem A.2 (the full `C(SŠĶą‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)` Banach CLT is the deferred Tier-3 gap). Over `k` fixed directions, the centered support process `Ōą(z)_j = s(p‚Īľ,F(z)) ‚ąí E s(p‚Īľ,F)` ‚áí a `k`-variate Gaussian; the Hausdorff-over-grid `‚Ąď^‚ąě` statistic converges accordingly. The `SetValued` section instantiates the abstract shell on genuine random sets.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `maxAbsK w` | `‚ĄĚ := Finset.univ.sup' _ (fun j => \|w j\|)` | The `‚Ąď^‚ąě`/Hausdorff-over-grid functional `sup‚Īľ \|w‚Īľ\|` on `EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin k)` (`[NeZero k]`); generalizes `maxAbs` (`Fin 2`) | **Defined** |
| `continuous_maxAbsK` / `measurable_maxAbsK` | `Continuous`/`Measurable maxAbsK` | The grid functional is continuous (hence measurable) | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess_sup_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (maxAbsK ‚ąė normalizedSum) ((gaussianLimit Ōą).map maxAbsK) őľ _` | **BM Theorem A.2, finite-grid form (abstract shell)**: `maxAbsK` of the vector normalised process ‚áí `(gaussianLimit Ōą).map maxAbsK` = law of `sup‚Īľ \|z(p‚Īľ)\|`. General-`d` analogue of `normalizedSum_maxAbs_clt` | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess F EF p` | `X ‚Üí EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin k)` | The centered **support process** `Ōą(x)_j = s(p‚Īľ, F x) ‚ąí s(p‚Īľ, EF)` over a grid of `k` directions; `‚ąęŌą=0` is the carried general-`d` Artstein condition | **Defined** |
| `supportProcess_ofLp` | `(supportProcess F EF p x).ofLp j = s(p‚Īľ,F x) ‚ąí s(p‚Īľ,EF)` | Coordinate access (`rfl`) | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess_normalizedSum_apply` | `normalizedSum (supportProcess ..) n ŌČ j = ‚ąön ¬∑ (s(p‚Īľ, FŐĄ‚āô) ‚ąí s(p‚Īľ, EF))` | **The Minkowski-mean bridge in coordinates**: the `j`-th coordinate of the normalised process is the rescaled support deviation of the empirical Minkowski average `FŐĄ‚āô = minkowskiMean (range n) (F‚ąėZ)`; via `supportFn_minkowskiMean` | **Proved** |
| `setValued_supportProcess_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (maxAbsK ‚ąė normalizedSum (supportProcess ..)) ((gaussianLimit Ōą).map maxAbsK) őľ _` | **BM Theorem A.2 on genuine random sets**: the `‚Ąď^‚ąě`-over-grid statistic of normalised support deviations ‚áí `max‚Īľ\|z(p‚Īľ)\|`; coordinates given by the bridge above (literal form `‚ąön¬∑max‚Īľ\|s(p‚Īľ,FŐĄ‚āô)‚ąís(p‚Īľ,EF)\|`) | **Proved** |

### `GridTest.lean` ‚ÄĒ finite-grid specification test (Beresteanu‚ÄďMolinari Theorem 2.2)

The two-sided test of `H‚āÄ : E[F] = EF` built on `setValued_supportProcess_clt`, rejecting when the rescaled Hausdorff-over-grid statistic exceeds a critical value.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gridTestStat S F EF p` | `‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The test statistic `T‚āô = maxAbsK(normalizedSum(supportProcess ..))` = `max‚Īľ ‚ąön¬∑\|s(p‚Īľ,FŐĄ‚āô)‚ąís(p‚Īľ,EF)\|` | **Defined** |
| `gridTestReject S F EF p n c` | `Set ő© := {ŌČ \| c < T‚āô ŌČ}` | Rejection region at critical value `c` | **Defined** |
| `gridTestStat_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (gridTestStat ..) ((gaussianLimit Ōą).map maxAbsK) őľ _` | Under `H‚āÄ`, `T‚āô ‚áí max‚Īľ\|z(p‚Īľ)\|`; thin restatement of `setValued_supportProcess_clt` | **Proved** |
| `gridTest_asymptotic_level` | `őľ(gridTestReject .. c) ‚Üí L(Ioi c)` (`L{c}=0`) | **BM Theorem 2.2 asymptotic level**: at a continuity point `c` of the limit law `L`, the rejection probability ‚Üí the limit-law tail `L(c,‚ąě)`; at the `(1‚ąíőĪ)`-quantile this is level `őĪ`. Via portmanteau (`tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier_of_tendsto'`) on `Ioi c` (frontier `{c}` null) | **Proved** |

Additional results landed in `IntervalCLT.lean` (moment discharge): `measurable_intervalIFVec`, `intervalIFVec_var_integrable`, `intervalIFVec_integrable`, `intervalIFVec_mean_zero`, `intervalIFVec_sum_aemeasurable`, `intervalIFVec_hHmeas` discharge the six analytic side-conditions of `interval_data_clt` from clean hypotheses, yielding the self-contained headline **`interval_data_clt_of_memLp`** (`‚ąön¬∑H(»≤‚āô,E[Y]) ‚áí max(|z_L|,|z_U|)` requiring only `Measurable yL/yU`, `MemLp yL/yU 2 P`, `yL ‚Č§ yU`). All proved, axiom-clean.

| `subset_dilate_iff_directedHausdorff_le` | `[őľL,őľU] ‚äÜ [yl,yu]‚äē[‚ąír,r] ‚ÜĒ dŠīī ‚Č§ r` | **BM Prop 2.7 core**: one-sided coverage ‚ÜĒ directed Hausdorff within radius | **Proved** |

Via the directed CLT + portmanteau `tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier_of_tendsto'` (frontier `{c}` null) on `coverage_event_eq` | **Proved** |

**Frontier (Tier 3, deferred):** the full `C(SŠĶą‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)` Banach CLT (Jain‚ÄďMarcus) + BLP (Thm 4.3) ‚ÄĒ same Mathlib gap class as the bracketing-entropy chaining frontier (¬ß12ep.d).

## 9j2cs. `PO/ID/Partial/CriterionSet/` ‚ÄĒ Criterion-function set inference (Chernozhukov‚ÄďHong‚ÄďTamer 2007)

The second pillar of partial-ID inference, complementary to the support-function/random-set program (¬ß9j2rs): a partially identified parameter is the **argmin set** of a population criterion `Q`, estimated by a **level set** of the sample criterion `QŐā‚āô`. Consistency is phrased in the *same* metric Hausdorff distance (`RandomSet.directedHausdorff`/`hausdorffDist`) that drives Beresteanu‚ÄďMolinari, tying the two pillars to one geometry. All declarations **proved, sorry-free**.

### `Basic.lean` ‚ÄĒ criterion, identified set, level-set estimator

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `identifiedSet Q` | `Set őė := {őł \| Q őł = 0}` | The identified set = zero set (argmin of a nonnegative criterion normalised to inf 0) | **Defined** |
| `levelSet Qn c` | `Set őė := {őł \| Qn őł ‚Č§ c}` | The level-set estimator (sample analogue, relaxed by cutoff `c`) | **Defined** |
| `mem_identifiedSet` / `mem_levelSet` | membership `Iff.rfl` (`@[simp]`) | Unfolding lemmas | **Proved** |
| `levelSet_mono h` | `c‚āĀ ‚Č§ c‚āā ‚Üí levelSet Qn c‚āĀ ‚äÜ levelSet Qn c‚āā` | Level set monotone in the cutoff | **Proved** |
| `identifiedSet_eq_levelSet_zero_of_nonneg hQ` | `identifiedSet Q = levelSet Q 0` | For `Q ‚Č• 0` the identified set is the level-`0` set | **Proved** |
| `identifiedSet_subset_levelSet_self hc` | `identifiedSet Q ‚äÜ levelSet Q c` (`c‚Č•0`) | Identified set sits inside any nonneg-cutoff level set of the same criterion | **Proved** |

### `Consistency.lean` ‚ÄĒ Hausdorff consistency of the level-set estimator

The mathematical core is a deterministic rate bound under a linear identifiability minorant; consistency is then a squeeze. Reuses the metric Hausdorff machinery of `RandomSet/Hausdorff.lean`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `LinearMinorant Q őī` | `Prop := ‚ąÄ őł, őī ¬∑ d(őł, őė_I) ‚Č§ Q őł` | The CHT polynomial-minorant identifiability condition (degree `ő≥=1`): `Q` grows ‚Č• linearly away from `őė_I` | **Defined** |
| `directedHausdorff_nonneg` / `hausdorffDist_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ ¬∑` | Metric Hausdorff distances are nonnegative | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_le_of_forall hM h` | `(‚ąÄ a‚ąąA, infDist a B ‚Č§ M) ‚Üí dŠīī A B ‚Č§ M` | Directed Hausdorff bound from a uniform point-to-set bound | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_eq_zero_of_subset h` | `A ‚äÜ B ‚Üí dŠīī A B = 0` | Directed distance vanishes on a subset | **Proved** |
| `hausdorffDist_levelSet_le` | `H(levelSet Qn c, identifiedSet Q) ‚Č§ (c+őĶ)/őī` | **Deterministic CHT rate bound** under `LinearMinorant Q őī` (`őī>0`), sup-norm error `\|Qn‚ąíQ\|‚Č§őĶ`, cutoff `c‚Č•őĶ`: estimator within `(c+őĶ)/őī` of `őė_I` (minorant pins `őėŐā‚āô`; `őė_I ‚äÜ őėŐā‚āô` outright) | **Proved** |
| `tendsto_hausdorffDist_levelSet` | `Tendsto (fun n => H(levelSet (Qn n) (c n), őė_I)) atTop (ūĚďĚ 0)` | **Hausdorff consistency**: with `őĶ‚āô‚Üí0` (ULLN input) and `őĶ‚āô ‚Č§ c‚āô ‚Üí 0`, the level-set estimator is consistent for the identified set (squeeze on the rate bound) | **Proved** |

## 9j2sg. `PO/ID/Partial/SupportFunction/Sensitivity.lean` ‚ÄĒ Reweighting / Sensitivity Models (worst-case mean under a budget)

Sensitivity analysis replaces an unidentified reweighting `w` (the weights that would recover the estimand under unconfoundedness) by an **ambiguity set** `W` and reports the sharp range of the reweighted mean `‚ü™c, w‚üę` over `W`. This is the **applications layer of `SupportFunction/`**: the support-function picture instantiated for sensitivity budgets (general robust-bound vocabulary + the Ōá¬≤/L¬≤ ball), all in one file.

### Reweighting layer ‚ÄĒ sensitivity vocabulary + structural facts

The general ambiguity-set layer: the robust bounds are the support-interval endpoints, monotone in the budget and collapsing under no ambiguity.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `robustUpper W c` | `‚ĄĚ := supportFn W c` | Upper robust bound (worst case from above) of the reweighted mean over ambiguity set `W` | **Defined** |
| `robustLower W c` | `‚ĄĚ := -supportFn W (-c)` | Lower robust bound (worst case from below) | **Defined** |
| `robustLower_le_robustUpper` | `robustLower W c ‚Č§ robustUpper W c` | The robust interval is well-ordered (width `‚Č• 0`) | **Proved** |
| `le_robustUpper` / `robustLower_le` | `‚ü™c,w‚üę ‚Č§ robustUpper W c` / `robustLower W c ‚Č§ ‚ü™c,w‚üę` | A feasible weight's reweighted mean lies in the robust interval | **Proved** |
| `robustUpper_mono` | `W ‚äÜ W' ‚Üí robustUpper W c ‚Č§ robustUpper W' c` | **Budget monotonicity** (upper): a larger ambiguity set raises the upper bound | **Proved** |
| `robustLower_antitone` | `W ‚äÜ W' ‚Üí robustLower W' c ‚Č§ robustLower W c` | **Budget monotonicity** (lower): a larger ambiguity set lowers the lower bound (interval widens) | **Proved** |
| `robustUpper_singleton` / `robustLower_singleton` | `robust* {w‚āÄ} c = ‚ü™c,w‚āÄ‚üę` | **No ambiguity ‚áí point identification** at `‚ü™c,w‚āÄ‚üę` (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `robustInterval_eq_image` | `(‚ü™c,¬∑‚üę)''W = Icc (robustLower W c) (robustUpper W c)` | For compact convex `W` the robust bounds are sharp (the interval is the identified set) | **Proved** |

### Ōá¬≤/L¬≤ divergence-ball model ‚ÄĒ closed form via the affine-ball engine

The Ōá¬≤/L¬≤ ambiguity set `l2Ball e ŌĀ = {w | ‚ü™e,w‚üę = 1 ‚ąß ‚ÄĖw ‚ąí e‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ŌĀ}` (unit base direction `e`, signed-reweighting relaxation of the Ōá¬≤-ball). Substituting `v = w ‚ąí e` reduces it to the affine-ball fiber of `A = innerSL ‚ĄĚ e`, giving the classic **mean ¬Ī `ŌĀ¬∑SD`** band. Reading `e` as the constant and `c = Y` in `L¬≤(P)`: `‚ü™e,c‚üę = ūĚĒľ[Y]`, `‚ÄĖc‚ÄĖ¬≤‚ąí‚ü™e,c‚üę¬≤ = Var(Y)`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `l2Ball e ŌĀ` | `Set H := {w | ‚ü™e,w‚üę = 1 ‚ąß ‚ÄĖw‚ąíe‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ŌĀ}` | The Ōá¬≤/L¬≤ ambiguity set: weights normalized against `e`, within an L¬≤-ball of radius `ŌĀ` | **Defined** |
| `mem_l2Ball` | `w ‚ąą l2Ball e ŌĀ ‚ÜĒ ‚ü™e,w‚üę = 1 ‚ąß ‚ÄĖw‚ąíe‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ŌĀ` | Membership unfolding (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `opKer_innerSL_eq` | `opKer (innerSL ‚ĄĚ e) = (‚ĄĚ ‚ąô e)Šóģ` | The free-direction kernel is the orthogonal complement of the base direction | **Proved** |
| `norm_orthogonalProjection_opKer_innerSL` | `‚ÄĖP_{ker} c‚ÄĖ = ‚ąö(‚ÄĖc‚ÄĖ¬≤‚ąí‚ü™e,c‚üę¬≤)` | Free-direction projection norm equals the standard deviation (under `‚ÄĖe‚ÄĖ=1`) | **Proved** |
| `l2Ball_eq_translate` | `l2Ball e ŌĀ = (e + ¬∑)''(affineBall (innerSL ‚ĄĚ e) 0 ŌĀ)` | Reduction to the affine-ball engine (substitution `v = w‚ąíe`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_l2Ball_eq` | `supportFn (l2Ball e ŌĀ) c = ‚ü™e,c‚üę + ŌĀ¬∑‚ąö(‚ÄĖc‚ÄĖ¬≤‚ąí‚ü™e,c‚üę¬≤)` | **Closed-form worst case** (`mean + ŌĀ¬∑SD`), `‚ÄĖe‚ÄĖ=1`, `ŌĀ‚Č•0` | **Proved** |
| `width_l2Ball_eq` | `width (l2Ball e ŌĀ) c = 2ŌĀ¬∑‚ąö(‚ÄĖc‚ÄĖ¬≤‚ąí‚ü™e,c‚üę¬≤)` | Closed-form identified width (`2ŌĀ¬∑SD`) | **Proved** |
| `l2Ball_point_identified_iff` | `width (l2Ball e ŌĀ) c = 0 ‚ÜĒ ‚ÄĖc‚ÄĖ¬≤ = ‚ü™e,c‚üę¬≤` | **Point identification** (`ŌĀ>0`): collapse iff `c` collinear with `e` (outcome constant; equality in Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz) | **Proved** |

## 9j2msm. `PO/ID/Partial/Sensitivity/MSM/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ Marginal Sensitivity Model for `E[Y(1)]` (Tan / Zhao‚ÄďSmall‚ÄďBhattacharya)

The **marginal sensitivity model** in the measure-theoretic backdoor PO framework (`POBackdoorSystem`): partial identification of `E[Y(1)]` when no-unmeasured-confounding is relaxed by a bounded odds ratio `őõ`. The complete propensity `e‚āÄ(X,Y(1)) = P[D=1 | ŌÉ(X,Y(1))]` (conditioning on the covariate *and* the treated potential outcome) is allowed to differ from the observed propensity `e(X)`, with the discrepancy controlled by `őõ`. Builds on `PO/ID/Exact/ATE.lean` (`propScore`, the condExp machinery); the IPW bridge is the genuine measure-theoretic content.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `OR p‚āĀ p‚āā` | `‚ĄĚ := (p‚āĀ/(1‚ąíp‚āĀ))/(p‚āā/(1‚ąíp‚āā))` | The odds ratio of two probabilities | **Defined** |
| `completeProp` | `ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ := őľ[1_{D=1} \| ŌÉ(X,Y(1))]` | The complete propensity `e‚āÄ(X,Y(1))` ‚ÄĒ conditions on covariate + treated potential outcome | **Defined** |
| `candMean ŠļĹ` | `‚ĄĚ := ‚ąę 1_{D=1}¬∑Y/ŠļĹ` | Candidate IPW mean `E[A¬∑Y/ŠļĹ]` for a candidate complete propensity `ŠļĹ` | **Defined** |
| `Y1mean` | `‚ĄĚ := ‚ąę Y(1)` | The estimand `őľ = E[Y(1)]` | **Defined** |
| `MSMSet őõ` | `Set (ő©‚Üí‚ĄĚ)` | The MSM ambiguity set: candidates a.e. in `(0,1)` with `1/őõ ‚Č§ OR(ŠļĹ, e(X)) ‚Č§ őõ` a.e. | **Defined** |
| `msmUpper őõ` / `msmLower őõ` | `‚ĄĚ := sSup/sInf (candMean '' MSMSet őõ)` | The MSM interval endpoints | **Defined** |
| `candMean_completeProp_eq_Y1mean` | `candMean e‚āÄ = E[Y(1)]` | **IPW / tower bridge** ‚ÄĒ the candidate mean at the *true* complete propensity recovers the estimand (condExp pull-out + cancellation; needs consistency, `e‚āÄ>0` a.e., IPW-integrability of `A¬∑Y/e‚āÄ`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `Y1mean_mem_Icc` | `E[Y(1)] ‚ąą Icc (msmLower őõ) (msmUpper őõ)` | **The bound** ‚ÄĒ the estimand lies in the MSM interval (truth is a feasible candidate) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `MSMSet_mono` | `1‚Č§őõ ‚Üí őõ‚Č§őõ' ‚Üí MSMSet őõ ‚äÜ MSMSet őõ'` | The ambiguity set grows with the sensitivity parameter | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpper_mono` / `msmLower_anti` | interval widens in `őõ` | **Budget monotonicity** of the MSM interval | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `MSMSet_one_eq` | `ŠļĹ ‚ąą MSMSet 1 ‚Üí ŠļĹ =ŠĶź e(X)` | **őõ=1 collapse**: no slack forces `OR=1 ‚áí ŠļĹ = e(X)` a.e. (point identification = the NUC/IPW value) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Closed form (`Bounds.lean`), with `wMin = 1+(1‚ąíe)/(őõe)`, `wMax = 1+őõ(1‚ąíe)/e` the box endpoints:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `wMin őõ` / `wMax őõ` | `ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The smallest/largest admissible inverse-propensity weight (`OR = 1/őõ` / `OR = őõ` endpoints) | **Defined** |
| `msmUpperForm őõ` / `msmLowerForm őõ` | `‚ĄĚ := ‚ąę A¬∑Y¬∑(wMax/wMin if Y‚Č•0 else wMin/wMax)` | The closed-form upper/lower integrals | **Defined** |
| `msmUpper_eq` / `msmLower_eq` | `msmUpper őõ = msmUpperForm őõ` / `msmLower őõ = msmLowerForm őõ` | **Closed form of the MSM bounds** ‚ÄĒ the box optimization is pointwise-separable (worst-case weight `wMax` where `Y‚Č•0`, `wMin` where `Y<0`), attained by a feasible boundary `ŠļĹ*`. Needs `1‚Č§őõ`, overlap, envelope-integrability, and a.e.-measurability of candidates | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Sharp (calibrated) set (`Sharp.lean`) ‚ÄĒ the Dorn‚ÄďGuo refinement: data-compatibility adds the calibration constraint `E[Z/ŠļĹ | ŌÉ(X)] = 1`:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Calibrated ŠļĹ` / `MSMSetCalib őõ` | `Prop` / `Set (ő©‚Üí‚ĄĚ)` | calibration `E[Z/ŠļĹ\|ŌÉ(X)] =ŠĶź 1`; the box members that are calibrated (data-compatible) | **Defined** |
| `msmUpperCalib őõ` / `msmLowerCalib őõ` | `‚ĄĚ := sSup/sInf (candMean '' MSMSetCalib őõ)` | the **sharp** interval endpoints | **Defined** |
| `completeProp_calibrated` | `Calibrated e‚āÄ` | **the truth is calibrated** ‚ÄĒ `E[Z/e‚āÄ\|ŌÉ(X)]=1` by the tower property (`e‚āÄ` conditions on `Y(1)`); same condExp pull-out as the IPW bridge | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `Y1mean_mem_Icc_calib` | `E[Y(1)] ‚ąą Icc (msmLowerCalib őõ) (msmUpperCalib őõ)` | **the sharp interval is valid** (contains the truth) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib_le_msmUpper` / `msmLower_le_msmLowerCalib` | `msmUpperCalib őõ ‚Č§ msmUpper őõ`, `msmLower őõ ‚Č§ msmLowerCalib őõ` | **the sharp interval is tighter than the ZSB interval** (`MSMSetCalib ‚äÜ MSMSet`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Quantile balancing (`QuantileBalance.lean`) ‚ÄĒ the Dorn‚ÄďGuo sharp closed form, via the Neyman‚ÄďPearson exchange argument:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `cutoffProp őõ c` | `ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ := 1/(wMax if Y > c(X) else wMin)` | the quantile-cutoff complete propensity at `ŌÉ(X)`-measurable cutoff `c` | **Defined** |
| `cutoff_optimal` | `ŠļĹ ‚ąą MSMSetCalib őõ ‚Üí candMean ŠļĹ ‚Č§ candMean (cutoffProp őõ c)` | **optimality of the cutoff weight** ‚ÄĒ Neyman‚ÄďPearson/Dantzig‚ÄďWald exchange: `candMean ŠļĹ ‚ąí candMean(1/w_c) = ‚ąęA¬∑(Y‚ąíc)¬∑(w_ŠļĹ‚ąíw_c) ‚Č§ 0` (the `c`-term cancels by calibration, the rest is `‚Č§0` pointwise) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib_eq_cutoff` | `msmUpperCalib őõ = candMean (cutoffProp őõ c)` | **the sharp upper bound has the quantile-balancing closed form** (given a calibrating cutoff `c`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Cutoff existence ‚ÄĒ calibration brackets (`CutoffExists.lean`): `wMin_mul_propScore_le_one` (`wMin¬∑e = e+(1‚ąíe)/őõ ‚Č§ 1`), `one_le_wMax_mul_propScore` (`1 ‚Č§ e+őõ(1‚ąíe) = wMax¬∑e`), and the condExp pull-outs `condExp_treat_wMin_eq` / `condExp_treat_wMax_eq` (`E[Z¬∑wMin\|ŌÉ(X)] = wMin¬∑e`, resp. `wMax`). Together: the conditional calibration value at the extreme cutoffs is `E[Z¬∑wMin\|ŌÉ(X)] = wMin¬∑e ‚Č§ 1 ‚Č§ wMax¬∑e = E[Z¬∑wMax\|ŌÉ(X)]`, so `1` is bracketed and a calibrating cutoff exists. **Proved, axiom-clean.**

Cutoff calibration ‚Üí survival equation (`CutoffSelection.lean`): `cutoff_calibValue_eq` decomposes `E[Z/cutoffProp őõ c\|ŌÉ(X)] = wMin¬∑e + (wMax‚ąíwMin)¬∑G(c)` (`G(c) = E[Z¬∑1{Y>c}\|ŌÉ(X)]` the conditional treated-survival); `cutoffProp_calibrated_of_survival` ‚áí the cutoff is calibrated iff `G(c) = survTarget := (1‚ąíwMin¬∑e)/(wMax‚ąíwMin)`; `cutoffProp_mem_MSMSet` (the cutoff is always in the box); and `cutoffProp_mem_MSMSetCalib_of_survival` ‚ÄĒ **the Phase-4 `hcut_mem` holds as soon as `c` solves `G(c) = survTarget`**. **Proved, axiom-clean.**

Cutoff construction ‚ÄĒ the sharp bound closed unconditionally (`CutoffConstruct.lean`): constructs the calibrating cutoff and discharges `hcut_mem`. `treatedXYLaw` (treated push-forward of `(X,Y)`), `treatedCondCDF` (its Mathlib-`condCDF`), `calibLevel őõ = 1 ‚ąí survTarget/e`:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `treatedSurv_const_eq` | `E[Z¬∑1{Y>t}\|ŌÉ(X)] =ŠĶź e(X)¬∑(1 ‚ąí F(t\|X))` | **the core bridge** ‚ÄĒ the weighted conditional survival equals the treated conditional CDF scaled by the propensity (condExp-uniqueness on ŌÉ(X)-sets: both sides `= őľ(s‚ą©{D=1}‚ą©{Y‚Č§t})`; `setLIntegral_condCDF` + `e=E[Z\|ŌÉX]` pull-out + ŌÉ(X)-factorization) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `treatedSurv_eq` | `E[Z¬∑1{Y>c(X)}\|ŌÉ(X)] =ŠĶź e(X)¬∑(1 ‚ąí F(c(X)\|X))` | the variable-cutoff bridge (frozen ŌÉ(X)-measurable `c`; variable-cutoff `condCDF` set-integral via the `condKernel` disintegration) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `exists_calibrating_cutoff` | `‚ąÉ c, Measurable[ŌÉX] c ‚ąß treatedSurv c =ŠĶź survTarget őõ` | **the calibrating cutoff exists** ‚ÄĒ the conditional quantile `Q_{calibLevel}(X)` (via `measurable_condQuantile_and_attains`); needs overlap, `1<őõ`, atomless treated outcome law, interior level | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib_eq_cutoff_unconditional` | `‚ąÉ c, ‚Ä¶ ‚ąß msmUpperCalib őõ = candMean (cutoffProp őõ c)` | **the Dorn‚ÄďGuo sharp upper bound, UNCONDITIONALLY** ‚ÄĒ the quantile-balancing closed form with `hcut_mem` discharged (only overlap / `1<őõ` / atomless / interior-level / envelope-integrability regularity) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Lower bound (`LowerBound.lean`) ‚ÄĒ the `sInf` mirror: the lower cutoff `lowerCutoffProp őõ c = 1/(wMin if Y>c else wMax)` (opposite weights) *minimizes* the candidate mean; `survTargetLower = (wMax¬∑e‚ąí1)/(wMax‚ąíwMin) = e ‚ąí survTarget`:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lowerCutoffProp` / `survTargetLower` / `calibLevelLower` | defs | the minimizing cutoff weight, its calibration target, and quantile level | **Defined** |
| `cutoff_optimal_lower` | `candMean (lowerCutoffProp őõ c) ‚Č§ candMean ŠļĹ` for calibrated `ŠļĹ` | the lower cutoff is the Neyman‚ÄďPearson minimizer | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmLowerCalib_eq_cutoff` / `lowerCutoffProp_mem_MSMSetCalib_of_survival` | closed form given a calibrating cutoff; calibration ‚üļ `treatedSurv c = survTargetLower` | mirror of the upper reductions | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `exists_calibrating_cutoff_lower` | `‚ąÉ c, ‚Ä¶ ‚ąß treatedSurv c =ŠĶź survTargetLower őõ` | the lower calibrating cutoff exists (`Q_{calibLevelLower}(X)`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmLowerCalib_eq_cutoff_unconditional` | `‚ąÉ c, ‚Ä¶ ‚ąß msmLowerCalib őõ = candMean (lowerCutoffProp őõ c)` | **the Dorn‚ÄďGuo sharp LOWER bound, unconditionally** | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Control-arm cutoff stack (`ControlCutoff.lean`, `ControlQuantileBalance.lean`, `ControlCutoffConstruct.lean`, `ControlLowerBound.lean`) ‚ÄĒ the exact `D=0` mirror of the treated cutoff development (`wMin0`/`wMax0`/`cutoffProp0`/`lowerCutoffProp0`/`controlSurv`/`survTarget0`/`controlXYLaw`, the control condExp‚ÜĒcondCDF bridge `controlSurv_const_eq`/`controlSurv_eq`, optimality, calibration‚Üísurvival reduction, construction). Capstones: `msmUpperCalib0_eq_cutoff_unconditional` and `msmLowerCalib0_eq_cutoff_unconditional` ‚ÄĒ the control sharp bounds in unconditional closed form. **Proved, axiom-clean.**

ATE closed form (`ATEClosedForm.lean`) ‚ÄĒ combines the four arm closed forms into the sharp ATE interval:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ate_endpoints_eq_cutoff` | `ateUpperCalib = candMean(cutoffProp cTU) ‚ąí candMean0(lowerCutoffProp0 cCL)`, `ateLowerCalib = candMean(lowerCutoffProp cTL) ‚ąí candMean0(cutoffProp0 cCU)` | **the sharp ATE endpoints in closed form** (treated upper ‚ąí control lower, treated lower ‚ąí control upper), each a conditional-quantile candidate mean | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `ate_mem_Icc_cutoff` | `ate ‚ąą Icc (‚Ä¶closed lower‚Ä¶) (‚Ä¶closed upper‚Ä¶)` | **the true ATE lies in the sharp closed-form interval** (endpoints + `ate_mem_Icc_calib` validity) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

Gaussian special case (`Gaussian.lean`) ‚ÄĒ the Dorn‚ÄďGuo corollary under a conditional-Gaussian treated outcome law:

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `calibLevel_eq` | `calibLevel őõ =ŠĶź őõ/(őõ+1)` | **the cutoff quantile level is exactly `őõ/(őõ+1)`**, independent of the propensity (since `survTarget = e/(őõ+1)`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `GaussianTreatedModel m ŌÉ` | structure | the treated conditional law of `Y` given `X` is `N(m(X), ŌÉ(X)¬≤)` (`condCDF treatedXYLaw a = ő¶((¬∑‚ąím a)/ŌÉ a)`) | **Defined** |
| `gaussianCutoff` / `gaussianCutoff_calibrates` | `c(X)=m(X)+ŌÉ(X)¬∑ő¶‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(őõ/(őõ+1))`; it solves `treatedSurv c =ŠĶź survTarget őõ` | **the explicit Gaussian quantile cutoff** calibrates ‚ÄĒ no measurable-selection needed | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib_gaussian` | `msmUpperCalib őõ = candMean (cutoffProp őõ (m(X)+ŌÉ(X)¬∑ő¶‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(őõ/(őõ+1))))` | **the sharp upper bound at the explicit Gaussian cutoff** | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

| `GaussianOutcomeModel` / `msmUpperCalib_gaussian_halfWidth` (`GaussianHalfWidth.lean`) | `msmUpperCalib őõ = ‚ąę m(X) + (őõ¬≤‚ąí1)/őõ¬∑ŌÜ(ő¶‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(őõ/(őõ+1)))¬∑‚ąę (1‚ąíe(X))¬∑ŌÉ(X)` | **the Dorn‚ÄďGuo Gaussian closed-form half-width** ‚ÄĒ point-identified mean plus the half-width (`gaussianMSMHalfWidthFactor`); via the conditional Gaussian mean/truncated-mean moments + the per-stratum collapse `wMin¬∑e+(wMax‚ąíwMin)e/(őõ+1)=1` | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

(The reusable standard-normal substrate `ő¶`, `probit = ő¶‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`, the truncated first moment `‚ąę_c^‚ąě tŌÜ = ŌÜ(c)`, and the general Gaussian truncated moment `‚ąę_c^‚ąě y dN(m,v) = m(1‚ąíő¶)+‚ąöv¬∑ŌÜ` are in `Mathlib/Probability/StdNormalCDF.lean`, `StdNormalMoments.lean`, `GaussianMoments.lean`, ¬ß10a'''.)

Scope: Phases 1‚Äď7 complete for BOTH arms (`E[Y(1)]`, `E[Y(0)]`) and the **ATE** ‚ÄĒ the sharp interval has unconditional quantile-balancing closed forms at every endpoint ‚ÄĒ plus the **Gaussian special case in full closed form** (explicit quantile cutoff + the half-width number). The MSM sharp-bound program is complete. The atomless (continuous-outcome) hypothesis is the Dorn‚ÄďGuo caveat, faithfully carried; the covariate space `ő≥` stays a general measurable space. See `doc/msm_sensitivity_plan.md`.

Control arm (`ControlSetup.lean`, `ControlSharp.lean`) ‚ÄĒ the `D=0` reflection of `Setup.lean`/`Sharp.lean`, bounding `E[Y(0)]` by IPW-weighting `1_{D=0}` against the complete control propensity `P[D=0 | ŌÉ(X,Y(0))]` (vs. observed `P[D=0 | ŌÉ(X)] = propScore false`). Every declaration mirrors the treated arm with a `0` suffix: `sigmaXY0`, `completeProp0`, `candMean0`, `Y0mean`, `MSMSet0`, `msmUpper0`/`msmLower0`, `Calibrated0`, `MSMSetCalib0`, `msmUpperCalib0`/`msmLowerCalib0`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `candMean0_completeProp0_eq_Y0mean` | `candMean0 e‚āÄ = E[Y(0)]` | **IPW/tower bridge (control)** ‚ÄĒ the `D=0` mirror of the treated bridge | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `Y0mean_mem_Icc` / `Y0mean_mem_Icc_calib` | `E[Y(0)] ‚ąą Icc (msmLower0 őõ) (msmUpper0 őõ)` / `‚Ä¶ Icc (msmLowerCalib0 őõ) (msmUpperCalib0 őõ)` | **the control ZSB / sharp interval is valid** | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `completeProp0_calibrated` | `Calibrated0 e‚āÄ` | the true complete control propensity is calibrated (`E[(1‚ąíZ)/e‚āÄ\|ŌÉ(X)]=1`) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `MSMSet0_mono` / `msmUpper0_mono` / `msmLower0_anti` / `MSMSet0_one_eq` | budget monotonicity + `őõ=1` collapse (control) | mirrors the treated structural lemmas | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `msmUpperCalib0_le_msmUpper0` / `msmLower0_le_msmLowerCalib0` | sharp control interval tighter than ZSB | mirrors the treated tightness lemmas | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

ATE interval (`ATE.lean`) ‚ÄĒ combines the two arms into a partial-ID interval for `ŌĄ = E[Y(1)] ‚ąí E[Y(0)]` by *opposing* the arm bounds (`ŌĄ‚Āļ = Ōą_T‚Āļ ‚ąí Ōą_C‚ĀĽ`, `ŌĄ‚ĀĽ = Ōą_T‚ĀĽ ‚ąí Ōą_C‚Āļ`):

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ate` | `‚ĄĚ := Y1mean ‚ąí Y0mean` | the average treatment effect `ŌĄ` | **Defined** |
| `ateUpperCalib őõ` / `ateLowerCalib őõ` | `‚ĄĚ := msmUpperCalib őõ ‚ąí msmLowerCalib0 őõ` / `msmLowerCalib őõ ‚ąí msmUpperCalib0 őõ` | the **sharp** ATE interval endpoints (treated upper paired with control lower, and vice versa) | **Defined** |
| `ateUpper őõ` / `ateLower őõ` | `‚ĄĚ := msmUpper őõ ‚ąí msmLower0 őõ` / `msmLower őõ ‚ąí msmUpper0 őõ` | the ZSB ATE interval endpoints | **Defined** |
| `sub_mem_Icc_of_mem_Icc` | `a‚ąą[a‚āó,aŠĶ§] ‚Üí b‚ąą[b‚āó,bŠĶ§] ‚Üí a‚ąíb‚ąą[a‚āó‚ąíbŠĶ§, aŠĶ§‚ąíb‚āó]` | interval-subtraction arithmetic core | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `ate_mem_Icc_calib` / `ate_mem_Icc` | `ŌĄ ‚ąą Icc (ateLowerCalib őõ) (ateUpperCalib őõ)` / `‚Ä¶ (ateLower őõ) (ateUpper őõ)` | **the sharp / ZSB ATE interval is valid** (contains the true `ŌĄ`), from the two arm-wise validity statements | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `ateCalib_subset` | `Icc (ateLowerCalib őõ) (ateUpperCalib őõ) ‚äÜ Icc (ateLower őõ) (ateUpper őõ)` | the sharp ATE interval nests inside the ZSB ATE interval | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

## 9j3. `PO/ID/Partial/BalkePearl/` ‚ÄĒ Balke-Pearl Sharp ATE Bounds (Binary IV, PO framework)

Formalizes sharp LP bounds on ATE from Balke & Pearl (1994) for a binary IV system.
All three variables Z, D, Y are binary (`Bool`). Split into four files:

- `BalkePearl/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ `POBalkePearlSystem` data layer, `ATE`, `cellProb`, `boolToReal`.
- `BalkePearl/Assumptions.lean` ‚ÄĒ `BaseAssumptions`, `cfBundle`.
- `BalkePearl/LatentTable.lean` ‚ÄĒ `latentProb` (16-cell table), partition lemmas, `ATE_eq_sum_latent`, `cellProb_eq_sum_latent`.
- `BalkePearl/Main.lean` ‚ÄĒ `BPFeasible`, `BPObjective`, `BPIdentifiedInterval`, necessity theorem.
- `BalkePearl/Sharp.lean` ‚ÄĒ canonical model construction, sharpness theorem.
- `BalkePearl/IntervalForm.lean` ‚ÄĒ necessity in closed-interval (`Set.Icc`) form via the SandwichInterval engine bridge.
- `BalkePearl/ClosedForm.lean` ‚ÄĒ the endpoints as explicit formulas in the observed cell probabilities: `bpLower`/`bpUpper` as a max/min of eight affine expressions.
- `BalkePearl/Attainment/{Basic,Lower,Upper}.lean` ‚ÄĒ cell-probability validity and the sixteen witness tables attaining the individual closed-form expressions.
- `BalkePearl/ClosedFormAttainment.lean` ‚ÄĒ assembles the witnesses into `sInf = bpLower` and `sSup = bpUpper` (sharpness of the closed form).

### Setup (`BalkePearl/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POBalkePearlSystem P` | `structure { Z, D, Y, hZbool, hDbool, hYbool, hZD, hZY, hDY }` | Binary-IV subsystem: three distinct variables with Bool codomains | **Defined** |
| `boolToReal` | `Bool ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | `false ‚Ü¶ 0`, `true ‚Ü¶ 1` | **Defined** |
| `ATE` | `‚ĄĚ := ‚ąę (YofD_real true - YofD_real false) ‚ąāP.őľ` | Target parameter, average treatment effect | **Defined** |
| `cellProb y d z` | `‚ĄĚ` | Observed joint probability `P(Y=y, D=d \| Z=z)` (as a real) | **Defined** |

### IntervalForm (`BalkePearl/IntervalForm.lean`)

Restates necessity in the engine's closed-interval vocabulary. `BPObjective` is an
average of `¬Ī1`/`0` contrasts against a probability vector, hence always in `[-1,1]`;
this boundedness feeds the bridge `Causalean.PartialID.mem_Icc_csInf_csSup`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BPObjective_le_one hnn hsum` | `BPObjective ŌÄ ‚Č§ 1` | The LP objective is at most 1 on any feasible (nonneg, sum-one) table | **Proved** |
| `neg_one_le_BPObjective hnn hsum` | `-1 ‚Č§ BPObjective ŌÄ` | The LP objective is at least -1 on any feasible table | **Proved** |
| `bddBelow_BPIdentifiedInterval hA` | `BddBelow (S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA)` | The identified interval is bounded below (by -1) | **Proved** |
| `bddAbove_BPIdentifiedInterval hA` | `BddAbove (S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA)` | The identified interval is bounded above (by 1) | **Proved** |
| `ATE_mem_Icc_csInf_csSup hA` | `S.ATE ‚ąą Set.Icc (sInf (S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA)) (sSup (S.BPIdentifiedInterval hA))` | NECESSITY, closed-interval form: the true ATE lies between the LP minimum and maximum, via the SandwichInterval bridge | **Proved** |

### Sharp (`BalkePearl/Sharp.lean`)

Canonical-model construction (~750 lines) discharging the sharpness direction.
The model has `V' = Fin 3` (Z, D, Y), `X' v = Bool`, sample space
`ő©' = Bool √ó (Bool √ó Bool √ó Bool √ó Bool)`, and product measure
`őľ' = őľ_Z ‚äó ŌÄ_meas` where `őľ_Z` is `S`'s Z-marginal and `ŌÄ_meas` weights Bool‚Āī
by ŌÄ. The eval cascades Z ‚Üí D ‚Üí Y, respecting interventions.

| `POBalkePearlSharp.canonicalBP S ŌÄ hŌÄ_nn hŌÄ_sum` | `POBalkePearlSystem _` | Canonical BP system | **Defined** |
