**Outstanding sorrys:** None. Both necessity (`ATE_mem_BPIdentifiedInterval`) and sharpness (`balkePearl_sharp`, `balkePearl_sharp_of_mem`) are fully proved.

### ClosedForm (`BalkePearl/ClosedForm.lean`)

`Main.lean` and `IntervalForm.lean` characterize the endpoints only *implicitly*, as the
infimum and supremum of the LP objective over feasible latent tables. Neither is a formula
an analyst can evaluate on a data set. `ClosedForm.lean` supplies the explicit solution of
the linear program, following Balke & Pearl (1997).

Both endpoints are piecewise-linear in the eight observed cell probabilities
`cellProb y d z = P(Y = y, D = d | Z = z)`. Concretely `bpLower` is the maximum of eight
affine expressions `bpLowerTerm i` and `bpUpper` the minimum of eight `bpUpperTerm i`. The
eight are the vertices of the dual feasible region: four of the "short" form
`p(0,0,z) + p(1,1,z') - 1` over the four instrument pairs `(z, z')`, and four "long" vertices
in which one cell probability enters with weight two. Because each expression is a dual
vertex, it is a nonnegative combination of the feasibility constraints ‚ÄĒ which is exactly
why every `bpLowerTerm i ‚Č§ BPObjective ŌÄ` discharges by `linarith` from the eight marginal
equations, normalization, and the sixteen nonnegativity facts.

The analyst-facing statement is `ATE_mem_Icc_bpLower_bpUpper : S.ATE ‚ąą Set.Icc S.bpLower S.bpUpper`.

Attainment of the endpoints is proved separately, in `ClosedFormAttainment.lean`.

### ClosedFormAttainment (`BalkePearl/ClosedFormAttainment.lean`)

Validity alone leaves open whether the closed form could be loose. This file closes that gap
on the lower side: `csInf_BPIdentifiedInterval_eq_bpLower` shows `bpLower` is *exactly* the
LP minimum, not merely a lower bound.

The proof exhibits, for each of the eight regions (the region where `bpLowerTerm i` is the
largest), a feasible latent table whose objective equals that expression. The eight witnesses
are the primal optima matching the eight dual vertices, located by complementary slackness.
That derivation is offline scaffolding: no duality theory appears in the Lean, each witness is
an explicit table checked directly against nonnegativity, normalization, and the eight
marginal equations.

Two details worth recording. First, four of the eight witnesses carry a free direction along
which the objective is constant; the free variable is pinned at the largest of its lower
bounds (feasible whenever anything is), which is the `max 0 _` in `bpAux*`. Setting those free
variables to zero instead yields *negative* entries and does not work. Second, feasibility of
a witness is not derivable from the observed cells being nonnegative and summing to one per
instrument value ‚ÄĒ one obligation is Pearl's instrumental inequality (`p(0,0,0) + p(1,0,1) ‚Č§ 1`).
The witness theorems therefore take a feasible table `ŌÄ‚āÄ` as a hypothesis, discharged at the
call site by `latentProb_feasible`.

Note this is a different claim from `balkePearl_sharp`, which shows every feasible objective
value is realized by some PO model but says nothing about the closed-form endpoints. Both are
needed for the closed form to be a sharp bound on the ATE.

Both endpoints are covered: `csInf_BPIdentifiedInterval_eq_bpLower` and
`csSup_BPIdentifiedInterval_eq_bpUpper`, packaged as `bpLower_bpUpper_eq_csInf_csSup`. Together
with `ClosedForm.lean`'s validity result this says the computable interval `[bpLower, bpUpper]`
has both endpoints attained, so it cannot be narrowed without further assumptions.

**Outstanding sorrys:** None.

---

## 9j4. `PO/ID/Partial/Proxy/` ‚ÄĒ Proximal Partial-Identification Bounds (Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen Tchetgen 2024, Sections 2-3)

Formalises the partial-identification bounds of arXiv 2304.04374 ‚ÄĒ partial bounds on
ATE / `E[Y(a) | A=¬¨a]` using proxy variables of an unobserved confounder, **without
the completeness condition** required for exact identification. Reuses
`POProximalSystem` from `PO/ID/Exact/Proximal/Setup.lean` (six POVars X, A, Z, W, Y, U).
Nine files (Helpers and ZBased are split into subdirectories):

- `Proxy/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ additional ŌÉ-algebras, target parameters, envelope predicates.
- `Proxy/Assumptions.lean` ‚ÄĒ three weakened bundles (`WBased`, `ZBased`, `TwoProxy`).
- `Proxy/Helpers.lean` ‚ÄĒ facade; re-exports `Helpers/{Common,BridgeW,BridgeWZ,CondExpQ}`.
- `Proxy/Helpers/Common.lean` ‚ÄĒ `meanYofA_eq_strata` + `YofA_essbound_*` clamp lemmas.
- `Proxy/Helpers/BridgeW.lean` ‚ÄĒ W-only bridge identity `condIntYofA_eq_h_arm`.
- `Proxy/Helpers/BridgeWZ.lean` ‚ÄĒ two-proxy bridge identity `condIntYofA_eq_hq_armSwap_twoProxy` (paper Theorem 3, identity (‚ėÖ), same-arm form, prob-ratio q convention).
- `Proxy/Helpers/CondExpQ.lean` ‚ÄĒ q-collapse on `{A=a}` ŌÉ_AX (prob-ratio q): `condExp_q_eq_stratumOddsRatio_arm_AX`.
- `Proxy/WBased.lean` ‚ÄĒ Theorem 1 + Corollary 1 (outcome-confounding-proxy).
- `Proxy/ZBased.lean` ‚ÄĒ Theorem 2 + Corollary 2 (imports `ZBased/ArmChain`).
- `Proxy/ZBased/ArmChain.lean` ‚ÄĒ paired arm-chain lemmas (`condIntYofA_le_envelope_arm` / `envelope_le_condIntYofA_arm`), ~600 lines. Both arm chains are now sorry-free in their bodies: the `hStep67` factorisation step is extracted as a private helper `condIntYq_factor_arm` and shared between the upper and lower variants.
- `Proxy/TwoProxy.lean` ‚ÄĒ Theorem 3 (two conditionally independent invalid proxies).
- `Proxy/IntervalForm.lean` ‚ÄĒ `Set.Icc` interval restatements of all five sandwich bounds (via the `SandwichInterval` bridge `mem_Icc_of_sandwich`).

### Setup (`Proxy/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `S.AX`, `S.ŌÉ_AX`, `S.ŌÉ_X` | tuple maps + ŌÉ-algebras | Conditioning targets `(A,X)` and `X` for partial-ID proofs | **Defined** |
| `S.ŌÉ_X_le_ŌÉ_AX` | `S.ŌÉ_X ‚Č§ S.ŌÉ_AX` | ŌÉ_X is coarser than ŌÉ_AX since `X = snd ‚ąė (A,X)`. Used in `WBased.lean` to lift ŌÉ_X-measurable `stratumOddsRatio ¬∑ Uenv(a,X)` to ŌÉ_AX for the pull-out step | **Proved** |
| `S.ŌÉ_AX_le_ŌÉ_AUX` | `S.ŌÉ_AX ‚Č§ S.ŌÉ_AUX` | ŌÉ_AX is coarser than ŌÉ_AUX since `(A,X)` factors through `(A,U,X)` via projection. Used by the ŌÉ_AX-tower step in `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX` | **Proved** |
| `S.ŌÉ_X_le_ŌÉ_AUX` | `S.ŌÉ_X ‚Č§ S.ŌÉ_AUX` | Composition `ŌÉ_X ‚Č§ ŌÉ_AX ‚Č§ ŌÉ_AUX`. Used by `ArmChain.hStep9` to lift `Uenv(a,X)` ŌÉ_X-meas to ŌÉ_AUX for L1 pull-out | **Proved** |
| `S.ŌÉ_X_le_ŌÉ_UX` | `S.ŌÉ_X ‚Č§ S.ŌÉ_UX` | `X = snd ‚ąė (U, X)`. Used by `ArmChain.hStep10` to lift `Uenv(a,X)` ŌÉ_X-meas to ŌÉ_UX for L2 arm-swap | **Proved** |
| `S.ŌÉ_AZX_le_ŌÉ_AZUX` | `S.ŌÉ_AZX ‚Č§ S.ŌÉ_AZUX` | `(A,Z,X) = proj ‚ąė (A,Z,U,X)`. Reserved for ŌÉ_AZX-pull-out steps | **Proved** |
| `S.ŌÉ_AUX_le_ŌÉ_AZUX` | `S.ŌÉ_AUX ‚Č§ S.ŌÉ_AZUX` | `(A,U,X) = proj ‚ąė (A,Z,U,X)`. Reserved for ŌÉ_AUX-tower steps | **Proved** |
| `S.meanYofA őľ a` | `‚ĄĚ := ‚ąę Y(a) dőľ` | Marginal potential outcome mean | **Defined** |
| `S.condMeanYofA őľ a` | `‚ĄĚ := (őľ {A‚Č†a}).toReal‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * ‚ąę_{A‚Č†a} Y(a) dőľ` | Conditional PO mean `E[Y(a) | A=¬¨a]` | **Defined** |
| `S.ATE őľ` | `‚ĄĚ := meanYofA őľ true - meanYofA őľ false` | Average treatment effect | **Defined** |

| `S.IsUpperEnvZ őľ a Uenv` | `Prop` | Pointwise upper bound `(őľ[Y \| ŌÉ_AZX]) ‚Č§ Uenv(a,X)` on `{A=a}` (Thm 2) | **Defined** |
| `S.IsLowerEnvZ őľ a Lenv` | `Prop` | Mirror lower bound | **Defined** |
| `S.IsUpperEnvWZ őľ a Uenv` | `Prop` | Same-arm joint-vs-product W-Z envelope (Thm 3): for nonneg measurable `ŌÜ_W(W,X)`, `ŌÜ_Z(Z,X)`, őľ-a.e. on `{A=a}`, `őľ[ŌÜ_W¬∑ŌÜ_Z\|ŌÉ_AX] ‚Č§ Uenv(a,X)¬∑őľ[ŌÜ_W\|ŌÉ_AX]¬∑őľ[ŌÜ_Z\|ŌÉ_AX]`. Operational form of `ŌĀ(w,z,x)=p(w,z\|a,x)/(p(w\|a,x)¬∑p(z\|a,x)) ‚Č§ Uenv(a,x)`; canonical sharp choice `Uenv(a,x)=sup_{w,z} ŌĀ(w,z,x)` | **Defined** |
| `S.IsLowerEnvWZ őľ a Lenv` | `Prop` | Mirror with inequality reversed; canonical sharp `Lenv(a,x)=inf_{w,z} ŌĀ(w,z,x)` | **Defined** |

### Assumptions (`Proxy/Assumptions.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `WBasedAssumptions S őľ` | `structure { consistency, latent_exch, proxy_WA, h, h_nonneg, bridge, ‚Ä¶, Y_bdd_below/above }` | Theorem 1 bundle: drops `proxy_YZ`, `completeness`, Z-fields. `h_nonneg : ‚ąÄ x, 0 ‚Č§ h x` is bundled (paper Assumption 2: a non-negative bridge is *posited* to exist; not derived from `Y ‚äÜ [0, ‚ąě)`, which does not force a bridge to be non-negative) | **Defined** |
| `ZBasedAssumptions S őľ` | `structure { consistency, latent_exch, proxy_YZ, q, bridge_q, likelihoodRatio_swapA, integrable_likelihoodRatio_swapA, integrable_condExpYofA_mul_L, integrable_condExpY_mul_q, integrable_Y_mul_q, ‚Ä¶, overlap_strong }` | Theorem 2 bundle: drops `proxy_WAZ`, `completeness`. **Probability-ratio q convention**: `likelihoodRatio_swapA_spec` operationally encodes `E[ūĚüô{A=a}\|ŌÉ_UX]¬∑L =ŠĶź E[ūĚüô{A=¬¨a}\|ŌÉ_UX]`, i.e. `L = p(¬¨a\|U,X)/p(a\|U,X)` on the support of `E[ūĚüô{A=a}\|ŌÉ_UX]`. This avoids RN derivatives; differs from the paper's density-ratio q `p(U\|¬¨a,X)/p(U\|a,X)` by a factor of stratumOddsRatio (both yield the same observable bound). `overlap_strong` encodes paper Assumption 3 positivity `p(A=a\|U,X) > 0`. Four explicit integrability fields cover finiteness gaps not implied by strict overlap (paper's "all integrals finite" convention; Remark 5 forbids a Y-clamp): `integrable_likelihoodRatio_swapA` (L ‚ąą L¬Ļ), `integrable_condExpYofA_mul_L` (`őľ[Y(a)\|ŌÉ_UX]¬∑L` ‚ąą L¬Ļ), `integrable_condExpY_mul_q` (`őľ[Y\|ŌÉ_AZX]¬∑q(Z,a,X)` ‚ąą L¬Ļ, used inside the ŌÉ_AZX-conditional envelope step), and `integrable_Y_mul_q` (`Y¬∑q(Z,a,X)` ‚ąą L¬Ļ, used inside the proxy_YZ factorisation step that materialises `őľ[Y\|ŌÉ_AZX]` from `őľ[Y\|ŌÉ_AUX]¬∑őľ[q\|ŌÉ_AUX]`) | **Defined** |
| `TwoProxyAssumptions S őľ` | `structure { consistency, latent_exch, proxy_WZ_indep, h, h_nonneg, q, bridge_h, bridge_q, likelihoodRatio_swapA, integrable_hq_arm, ‚Ä¶ }` | Theorem 3 bundle: requires both bridges, `h_nonneg`, and `W ‚üā Z \| (A,X,U)` (paper Assumption 6). No `W ‚üā A \| (U,X)` requirement (does not bundle `proxy_WA`); also drops `proxy_YZ`. Same prob-ratio q convention as `ZBasedAssumptions`. Bundles `integrable_hq_arm : ‚ąÄ a, Integrable (h(a,W,X)¬∑q(Z,a,X)) őľ` (separate L¬Ļ does not imply product L¬Ļ) | **Defined** |

### Helpers (`Proxy/Helpers.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `meanYofA_eq_strata` | `meanYofA = ‚ąę_{A‚Č†a} Y(a) + ‚ąę_{A=a} Y` | Marginalisation identity (consistency on the on-arm); used to derive Cor 1, 2 from Thm 1, 2 | **Proved** |
| `YofA_essbound_above` / `_below` | `Y(a)` inherits `Y`'s a.e. essential bound | Lifts essential bounds on `Y` to the counterfactual `Y(a)` via `latent_exch` + consistency-on-arm; consumed by the trivial-clamp steps in W-side and TwoProxy theorems | **Proved** |
| `condIntYofA_eq_h_arm` | `‚ąę_{A=¬¨a} Y(a) dőľ = ‚ąę_{A=¬¨a} h(a,W,X) dőľ` | Bridge-substitution identity for the W-only bundle | **Proved** |
| `condIntYofA_eq_hq_armSwap_twoProxy` | `‚ąę_{A‚Č†a} Y(a) dőľ = ‚ąę_{A=a} h(a,W,X) ¬∑ q(Z,a,X) dőľ` | Two-proxy bridge-substitution identity in **same-arm form** (paper Theorem 3, identity (‚ėÖ), prob-ratio q convention). No `stratumOddsRatio` weight: the off-arm-to-on-arm transport is absorbed into `q` itself via `likelihoodRatio_swapA_spec`. The RHS is a clean `{A=a}` integral that the ŌÉ_AX-conditional envelope `IsUpperEnvWZ`/`IsLowerEnvWZ` applies to directly ‚ÄĒ no ŌÉ_AX-fiber transport needed. Proof structured as 4 named `have`s mirroring paper steps (a)‚Äď(d): `hStepA` (latent_exch + tower at ŌÉ_AUX ‚Üí ‚ąę_{A‚Č†a} őľ[Y(a)\|ŌÉ_UX]), `hStepC` (likelihoodRatio_swapA_spec arm-swap via L2 ‚Üí ‚ąę_{A=a} őľ[Y(a)\|ŌÉ_UX]¬∑L), `hStepB` (consistency + bridge_h substitution on `{A=a}` ‚Üí swap őľ[Y(a)\|ŌÉ_UX] for őľ[h(a,W,X)\|ŌÉ_AUX]), `hStepD1` (bridge_q ‚Üí swap L for őľ[q(Z,a,X)\|ŌÉ_AUX]), `hStepD2` (proxy_WZ_indep + condExp_mul_of_condIndep on (W,X) ‚üā (Z,X) ‚ÄĒ built by two prodMk-left lifts via condIndepFun_prodMk_of_measurable_left + symm), `hStepD3` (tower {A=a} ‚ąą ŌÉ_AUX). Black-box dependencies: `condExp_mul_of_condIndep`, `condIndepFun_prodMk_of_measurable_left`; `setIntegral_eq_setIntegral_mul_of_likelihoodRatio_swap` (LikelihoodRatioSwap.lean) | **Proved** |
| `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX_core` | `őľ[Y \| ŌÉ_AX] =ŠĶź[restrict {A=a}] őľ[h(a,W,X) \| ŌÉ_AX]` | Raw-input collapse identity: takes a bridge function `h` together with `bridge`, `integrable_Y`, `integrable_h`, `integrable_h_arm` directly (no assumption-bundle dependency). Shared core used by both the WBased and TwoProxy wrappers. Proof: bridge equation + indicator-zero argument at ŌÉ_AUX, then ŌÉ_AX-tower via `ŌÉ_AX_le_ŌÉ_AUX` and `condExp_condExp_of_le` | **Proved** |
| `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX` | wrapper for `WBasedAssumptions` | Bundled-input collapse identity for the W-only bundle; specialises `_core` with `HA.h, HA.bridge, HA.integrable_Y, HA.integrable_h, HA.integrable_h_arm` | **Proved** |
| `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX_twoProxy` | wrapper for `TwoProxyAssumptions` | Same as above, but for the two-proxy bundle (`HA.bridge_h` plays the role of `bridge`); `TwoProxyAssumptions` does not contain `proxy_WA` so it cannot be projected to `WBasedAssumptions` | **Proved** |
| `setIntegral_mul_condExp_of_stronglyMeasurableLeft` | `‚ąę_s f¬∑g dőľ = ‚ąę_s f¬∑őľ[g\|m] dőľ` for m-stronglyMeasurable f, integrable g and f¬∑g, m-measurable s | Generic Mathlib-style helper combining `condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left` with `setIntegral_condExp`. Used in `WBased.lean` to swap `h(a,W,X)` for `őľ[Y\|ŌÉ_AX]` inside `{A=a}` integrals (Fix C) | **Proved** |
| `stratumOddsRatio_spec` (private) | `őľ[ūĚüô_{A=a}\|ŌÉ_X] ¬∑ stratumOddsRatio =ŠĶź őľ[ūĚüô_{A=¬¨a}\|ŌÉ_X]` | ŌÉ_X projection of `likelihoodRatio_swapA_spec`: algebraic safe-division identity granted ŌÉ_X-positivity of `őľ[ūĚüô_{A=a}\|ŌÉ_X]`. Closed via `field_simp` after invoking `Causalean.ae_pos_condExp_indicator_of_le` (the ŌÉ_X-projection of `overlap_strong`, isolated in `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean`) | **Proved** (modulo the labelled `ae_pos_condExp_indicator_of_le` stub in `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean`) |
| `condExp_q_eq_stratumOddsRatio_arm_AX` | `őľ[q(Z,a,X) \| ŌÉ_AX] =ŠĶź[restrict {A=a}] stratumOddsRatio` | Prob-ratio q-collapse on `{A=a}` ŌÉ_AX. Under the codebase's prob-ratio q convention, the ŌÉ_AX-conditional expectation of `q(Z,a,X)` collapses on `{A=a}` to the ŌÉ_X-measurable stratum-odds ratio. Used in `condMeanYofA_WZ_bounds` to remove the latent bridge `q` from the public statement (parallel to `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX` for `h`). Proof plan (see docstring): `ae_eq_condExp_of_forall_setIntegral_eq` over `(őľ.restrict {A=a}).trim` + ŌÉ_AX ‚Üí ŌÉ_X test-set reduction + the L1+L2+L2 chain (`setIntegral_mul_condExp_of_stronglyMeasurableLeft` + `setIntegral_eq_setIntegral_mul_of_likelihoodRatio_swap` twice) + `bridge_q` + `stratumOddsRatio_spec`. All substantive measure-theoretic gaps now isolated upstream ‚ÄĒ remaining body is mechanical bookkeeping (descent/integrability/measurability) | **Sorry** (mechanical bookkeeping only; no substantive gap remains in this body ‚ÄĒ substantive gap is now in `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean` `ae_pos_condExp_indicator_of_le`) |

### WBased (`Proxy/WBased.lean`) ‚ÄĒ Theorem 1 + Corollary 1

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `condMeanYofA_W_bounds` | sandwich on `condMeanYofA őľ a` | Theorem 1, observable form: `max(essinf Y, env-low) ‚Č§ E[Y(a) \| A=¬¨a] ‚Č§ min(essup Y, env-up)` where the envelope integrals contain the **observable** factor `őľ[Y \| ŌÉ_AX]` (not the latent bridge `h`). Removed `h` from the public statement via the collapse identity `condExp_Y_eq_condExp_h_arm_AX` (Fix C). Takes 2 caller-supplied integrability hypotheses `hU_int_h`/`hL_int_h` for `(stratumOddsRatio ¬∑ Uenv/Lenv(a,X)) ¬∑ h(a,W,X)` (paper's implicit "all integrals finite" convention; not bundled because `Uenv`/`Lenv` are theorem-level parameters) | **Proved** |
| `meanYofA_W_bounds` | sandwich on `meanYofA őľ a` | Corollary 1, observable form with **trivial-clamp** (paper arXiv lines 177-195): `max(essinfY ¬∑ őľ{A‚Č†a}, envelope-lower) + ‚ąę_{A=a} Y dőľ ‚Č§ E[Y(a)] ‚Č§ min(essupY ¬∑ őľ{A‚Č†a}, envelope-upper) + ‚ąę_{A=a} Y dőľ`. The trivial clamp `essinfY ¬∑ őľ{A‚Č†a}` corresponds to the worst-case potential outcome on the off-arm stratum. Derived via `meanYofA_eq_strata` from the conditional bound, with the off-arm trivial clamp from `YofA_essbound_above`/`_below`. Takes 4 caller-supplied integrability hypotheses for `(stratumOddsRatio ¬∑ Uenv/Lenv(a,X)) ¬∑ h(a,W,X)` and the `őľ[Y\|ŌÉ_AX]` analogues (paper's implicit "all integrals finite" convention; not bundled because `Uenv`/`Lenv` are theorem-level parameters) | **Proved** |

Chain restructured to use ŌÉ_AUX (not ŌÉ_UX) at step (4), avoiding the missing `Y ‚üā A \| ŌÉ_UX` lift. No clamp needed (Remark 5). ## 9j5. `PO/ID/Partial/Lee/` ‚ÄĒ Lee Bounds for Sample-Selection ATT (PO framework)

Formalises `prop:po-lee-bounds` from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(¬ß`subsec:po-lee-bounds`): a partial-identification sandwich bound for the
**always-selected ATT** `ŌĄ_AS = E[Y(1) - Y(0) | alwaysSelected]` under
sample selection. Hypotheses are random assignment (encoded as the
pair-level independence `A ‚ęę (Y(a), Sel(a))` for each `a`), monotone sample
selection `Sel(0) ‚Č§ Sel(1)`, and a finite-support restriction on
`L(Y | A=true, Sel=true)`. The bound takes the form
`underline_m‚āĀ - m‚āÄ ‚Č§ ŌĄ_AS ‚Č§ overline_m‚āĀ - m‚āÄ`, where
`underline_m‚āĀ`, `overline_m‚āĀ` are the lower and upper Lee trimmed means and
`m‚āÄ = E[Y | A=false, Sel=true]` is the observable selected-control mean.

The quantile form (rem:po-lee-quantile) is deferred to a follow-on file.

Layout (one file per topic, all proofs complete):

- `Lee/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ `POLeeSystem` data + factual / counterfactual
  accessors + observable cells `selectedTreated`, `selectedControl`.
- `Lee/Assumptions.lean` ‚ÄĒ `BaseAssumptions` (consistency, pair-level
  random assignment, positivity, integrability) + `MonotoneSelection`.
- `Lee/Trim.lean` ‚ÄĒ selected-cell probabilities `p0`, `p1`, ratio `rho`,
  observable density `f1`, `LeeTrimWeight`, mean `Mw`, trimmed means
  `lowerTrimMean` / `upperTrimMean`, observable selected-control mean `m0`.
- `Lee/PrincipalStrata.lean` ‚ÄĒ latent events `alwaysSelected`,
  `helpedSelected`, `harmedSelected`; the pure set equality
  `selOfATrueSet = alwaysSelected ‚ą™ helpedSelected`; under monotone
  selection, the a.s. identities collapsing latent events to observables.
- `Lee/ControlMean.lean` ‚ÄĒ Step A: `m0_eq_eventCondExp_Y0_alwaysSelected`
  collapses observable `m‚āÄ = E[Y|A=false,Sel=true]` to latent
  `E[Y(0)|alwaysSelected]`.
- `Lee/MixtureIdentity.lean` ‚ÄĒ selected-treated decomposition (the doc's
  "mixture law"): `selectedTreated_integral_split` (Step B),
  `selectedTreated_measure_split` (Step B'), and the indicator-integrand
  variant `selectedTreated_integral_split_indicator`.
- `Lee/LatentSupport.lean` ‚ÄĒ `YofA_true_in_finset_ae_alwaysSelected`:
  finite outcome support transfers from observable cell to latent strata.
- `Lee/TrimWeight.lean` ‚ÄĒ `f1AS` density + the
  `alwaysSelectedTrimWeight` `LeeTrimWeight` witness with all four field
  proofs (`nonneg`, `le_one`, `zero_off`, `sum_eq`).
- `Lee/TrimMean.lean` ‚ÄĒ `Mw_alwaysSelectedTrimWeight_eq_condExp_Y1_AS`:
  the witness's trimmed mean equals `E[Y(1)|alwaysSelected]`.
- `Lee/TrimBound.lean` ‚ÄĒ `trimmed_bounds_condExp_Y1_AS`: corresponding
  sandwich `lowerTrimMean ūĚíī ‚Č§ E[Y(1)|alwaysSelected] ‚Č§ upperTrimMean ūĚíī`.
  The `csInf`/`csSup` step is now routed through the engine bridge
  `Causalean.PartialID.mem_Icc_csInf_csSup` (see ¬ß9j2 `SandwichInterval.lean`);
  the trimmed-mean endpoints are exactly the inf/sup of `Mw` over the
  unidentified trim-weight nuisance.
- `Lee/Main.lean` ‚ÄĒ `lee_bounds_ATT_AS` (prop:po-lee-bounds): final
  assembly via `eventCondExp_sub` on `alwaysSelected`.
- `Lee.lean` ‚ÄĒ umbrella re-export.

### Setup (`Lee/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POLeeSystem P` | `structure { A, Sel, Y, hAbool, hSelbool, hYreal, hASel, hAY, hSelY }` | Lee sample-selection subsystem over a PO system: binary `A`, binary `Sel` (selection), real `Y`, pairwise distinctness | **Defined** |
| `aVar` / `selVar` / `yVar` | `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P ‚ĄĚ` | `POVar` wrappers for the three nodes | **Defined** |
| `SelOfA S a` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` | Counterfactual selection `Sel(a) := selVar.cfUnder aVar a` | **Defined** |
| `YofA S a` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(a) := yVar.cfUnder aVar a` | **Defined** |
| `factualA / factualSel / factualY` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool / Bool / ‚ĄĚ` | Factual treatment / selection / outcome | **Defined** |
| `aEvent S a` / `selEvent S s` | `Set P.ő©` | Factual events `{A = a}` / `{Sel = s}` | **Defined** |
| `selectedTreated` | `Set P.ő© := aEvent true ‚ą© selEvent true` | Observable selected-treated cell | **Defined** |
| `selectedControl` | `Set P.ő© := aEvent false ‚ą© selEvent true` | Observable selected-control cell | **Defined** |
| measurability lemmas | `measurable_{SelOfA, YofA, factualA, factualSel, factualY}`, `measurableSet_{aEvent, selEvent, selectedTreated, selectedControl}` | One-line delegations to `POVar` | **Proved** |

### Assumptions (`Lee/Assumptions.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BaseAssumptions S` | `structure { consistency, randAssign, posSelectedTreated, posSelectedControl, posATrue, posAFalse, posSelTrFinite, posSelCtFinite, integrableY1, integrableY0 }` | Common bundle for prop:po-lee-bounds. Factual-`Y` integrability is *derived*, not bundled (see `integrableY`) | **Defined** |
| `BaseAssumptions.randAssign` | `‚ąÄ a : Bool, IndepFun S.factualA (fun ŌČ => (S.YofA a ŌČ, S.SelOfA a ŌČ)) P.őľ` | Pair-level random assignment; weaker than the doc's 4-tuple joint indep, but strictly stronger than mean independence ‚ÄĒ carries the conditional distribution of `(Y(a), Sel(a))` given `A` | **Defined** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrable_YofA hA a` | `Integrable (S.YofA a) P.őľ` | Binary-folded form of `integrableY1` / `integrableY0` | **Proved** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrableY hA` | `Integrable S.factualY P.őľ` | Factual outcome integrability, *derived* from arm integrability via consistency (`factualY = ő£_a Y(a)¬∑1{A=a}` a.e.); no longer an assumed field | **Proved** |
| `MonotoneSelection S` | `Prop { monotone }` | Monotone sample selection: `Sel(0) ‚Č§ Sel(1)` a.s. | **Defined** |

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `pSelGivenA S a` | `‚ĄĚ` | `eventCondExp őľ (aEvent a) (selVar.indicator true)` ‚ÄĒ conditional selection probability | **Defined** |
| `p0` / `p1` | `‚ĄĚ` | Selected-cell probabilities `P(Sel=true | A=false)` / `P(Sel=true | A=true)` | **Defined** |

| `lowerTrimMean ūĚíī` / `upperTrimMean ūĚíī` | `‚ĄĚ` | `sInf` / `sSup` of `Set.range (fun wt => Mw wt)` | **Defined** |

|---|---|---|---|
| `f1AS y` | `‚ĄĚ := eventCondExp őľ alwaysSelected (fun ŌČ => if YofA true ŌČ = y then 1 else 0)` | Conditional density of `Y(1)` on `alwaysSelected` at `y` | **Defined** |
| `alwaysSelectedTrimWeight hA hMono ūĚíī hSupp` | `LeeTrimWeight ūĚíī` with `w(y) := if y ‚ąą ūĚíī ‚ąß S.f1 y ‚Č† 0 then S.rho * S.f1AS y / S.f1 y else 0` | Lee-trim-weight witness for the always-selected sub-distribution; the four field proofs (`nonneg`, `le_one`, `zero_off`, `sum_eq`) collapse via `selectedTreated_integral_split_indicator` and `YofA_true_in_finset_ae_alwaysSelected` | **Proved** |

### TrimMean (`Lee/TrimMean.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Mw_alwaysSelectedTrimWeight_eq_condExp_Y1_AS` | `Mw (alwaysSelectedTrimWeight hA hMono ūĚíī hSupp) = eventCondExp őľ alwaysSelected (YofA true)` | The trimmed mean of the always-selected witness equals `E[Y(1)|alwaysSelected]`; the `ŌĀ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` in `Mw` cancels the `ŌĀ` numerator built into `w` | **Proved** |

### TrimBound (`Lee/TrimBound.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `trimmed_bounds_condExp_Y1_AS` | `lowerTrimMean ūĚíī ‚Č§ eventCondExp őľ alwaysSelected (YofA true) ‚Č§ upperTrimMean ūĚíī` | Sandwich corollary of `Mw_alwaysSelectedTrimWeight_eq_condExp_Y1_AS`: the witness lies in `Set.range Mw`, so its value sits between the inf and sup | **Proved** |

### Main (`Lee/Main.lean`) ‚ÄĒ prop:po-lee-bounds

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lee_bounds_ATT_AS` | `[IsFiniteMeasure őľ], BaseAssumptions, MonotoneSelection, ūĚíī : Finset ‚ĄĚ, hSupp ‚áí lowerTrimMean ūĚíī - m0 ‚Č§ eventCondExp őľ alwaysSelected (YofA true - YofA false) ‚Č§ upperTrimMean ūĚíī - m0` | **Main theorem** prop:po-lee-bounds; subtracts Step A (`m0 = E[Y(0)|alwaysSelected]`) from Step C (sandwich for `E[Y(1)|alwaysSelected]`) via `eventCondExp_sub` | **Proved** |

### Status

- Lee module is sorry-free; full `lake build Causalean.PO.ID.Partial.Lee` succeeds with zero errors and zero `sorry` warnings on Lee files.

## 9k. `PO/ID/Exact/Frontdoor.lean` ‚ÄĒ Frontdoor ATE Identification Theorem (PO framework)

Formalizes `prop:po-frontdoor-ate` from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(¬ß`subsec:po-frontdoor-ate`, lines ~1518‚Äď1691): **classical frontdoor
identification of the ATE** under binary treatment `A`, discrete mediator `M`
(finite value space `ő≤`), and real outcome `Y`. The proof pattern relies on the
*composition* clause of `POSystem.Consistency` to collapse the two-variable
counterfactual `Y(a, M(a))` to `Y(a)` on `{M(a) = m}`, plus full mediation and
two exchangeability assumptions.  Event-level style throughout
(`eventCondExp`), matching `Manski.lean` / `DID.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POFrontdoorSystem P ő≤` | `structure { A, M, Y, hAbool, hMequiv, hYreal, hAM, hAY, hMY }` | Frontdoor PO subsystem: binary treatment `A`, discrete mediator `M : ő≤` (finite), real outcome `Y`, pairwise distinctness | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{aVar, mVar, yVar}` | `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P ő≤` / `POVar P ‚ĄĚ` | `POVar` wrappers | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{YofA, MofA}` | `Bool ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` / `Bool ‚Üí P.ő© ‚Üí ő≤` | Single-target counterfactuals `Y(a)`, `M(a)` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.regimeAM S a m` | `Regime P.V P.X` | Two-target intervention `{A ‚Üź a, M ‚Üź m}` as a `Regime.sqcup` of two singletons (disjointness from `hAM`) | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.YofAM S a m` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ := yVar.cf (regimeAM a m)` | Two-variable counterfactual `Y(a, m)` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{factualA, factualM, factualY}` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool` / `ő≤` / `‚ĄĚ` | Factual treatment / mediator / outcome | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.measurable_{YofA, MofA, YofAM, factualA, factualM, factualY}` | `Measurable ‚Ä¶` | One-line measurability delegations to `POVar.measurable_{cf, cfUnder, factual}` | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{aEvent, mEvent}` | `Set P.ő©` | Factual events `{A = a}`, `{M = m}` (indicators are now `POVar.indicator` on the corresponding `POVar`) | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.measurableSet_{aEvent, mEvent}` | ‚ÄĒ | Measurability of the factual events | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.mUnderA S a` | `RegimedVar P ő≤` | `M(a)` as a regimed variable, for bundling | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.mBundle S` | `POCFBundle P` | Two-entry bundle `[M(true), M(false)]`, used to state `A ‚ä• (M(1), M(0))` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{pA, pMgivenA, EYgivenAM}` | `‚ĄĚ` | Observable `P(A=a)`, `P(M=m \| A=a)`, `E[Y \| A=a, M=m]` (event-level) | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.frontdoorTerm S a` | `‚ĄĚ := ‚ąĎ m, (EYgivenAM 1 m ¬∑ pA 1 + EYgivenAM 0 m ¬∑ pA 0) ¬∑ pMgivenA m a` | Frontdoor-adjusted functional `ŌÜ(a)` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.{ATE, frontdoorATE}` | `‚ĄĚ` | Target parameter `E[Y(1) - Y(0)]` and its observable counterpart `ŌÜ(1) - ŌÜ(0)` | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, fullMediation, exch_AM, exch_MY, posA, posAM, indep_Y_M, integrable_{factualY, YofA, YofAM} }` | Full bundle of PO-level frontdoor assumptions from `def:po-frontdoor-ate-assumptions`.  The extra field `indep_Y_M : ‚ąÄ a m, IndepFun (MofA a) (YofAM true m) őľ` is a cross-world independence assumption needed for the drop-of-conditioning step; it is not derivable from the other fields in the bare PO framework (would follow from d-separation in the graphical frontdoor setup) | **Defined** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.MofA_eq_factualM_on_aEvent` | `P.Consistency ‚Üí ŌČ ‚ąą aEvent a ‚Üí MofA a ŌČ = factualM ŌČ` | On `{A = a}`, the single-target counterfactual `M(a)` coincides with the factual mediator; one-line shim on `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.YofAM_eq_factualY_on_aMEvent` | `P.Consistency ‚Üí ŌČ ‚ąą aEvent a ‚ą© mEvent m ‚Üí YofAM a m ŌČ = factualY ŌČ` | On `{A = a} ‚ą© {M = m}`, the two-variable counterfactual `Y(a,m)` coincides with the factual outcome; built on `POVar.cf_eq_factual_of_factualAgrees` via a `sqcup`-case-split factual-agreement proof | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.YofAM_eq_YofA_on_MofA_event` | `P.Consistency ‚Üí MofA a ŌČ = m ‚Üí YofAM a m ŌČ = YofA a ŌČ` | **Composition lemma**: on `{M(a) = m}`, `Y(a,m) = Y(a)` pointwise; reduces the two-target counterfactual to the single-target one via `Consistency.composition` applied to the disjoint singletons `{A ‚Üź a}` and `{M ‚Üź m}` | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.integral_eq_sum_integral_MofA` | `Integrable f őľ ‚Üí ‚ąę f ‚ąāőľ = ‚ąĎ m : ő≤, ‚ąę ŌČ in {MofA a = ¬∑}, f ŌČ ‚ąāőľ` | Finite-partition helper: decompose an integral along the level sets of the counterfactual mediator `MofA a` (valid since `ő≤` is `Fintype`) | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.integral_eq_sum_eventCondExp_aEvent` | `Integrable g őľ ‚Üí ‚ąę g ‚ąāőľ = ‚ąĎ a' : Bool, eventCondExp őľ (aEvent a') g ¬∑ őľ(aEvent a').toReal` | Bool-partition tower: express `‚ąę g` as the sum over treatment arms of arm-conditional means weighted by arm probability | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.EofY_eq_frontdoorTerm` | `S.Assumptions ‚Üí ‚ąÄ a, ‚ąę YofA a ‚ąāP.őľ = frontdoorTerm a` | **Frontdoor identification (individual regime):** `E[Y(a)] = ŌÜ(a)` for each `a ‚ąą {0,1}`.  Closes in four blocks: (A) mediator-marginal identity `őľ(MofA a = m).toReal = pMgivenA m a` via `exch_AM.component` + consistency; (B) on-slice composition + full-mediation rewrite `‚ąę_{MofA a = m} YofA a = ‚ąę_{MofA a = m} YofAM true m`; (C) drop-of-conditioning via `indep_Y_M`; (D) inner tower `‚ąę YofAM true m = ‚ąĎ_{a'} EYgivenAM a' m ¬∑ pA a'` via `integral_eq_sum_eventCondExp_aEvent` + `exch_MY` + `fullMediation` + `YofAM_eq_factualY_on_aMEvent` | **Proved** |
| `POFrontdoorSystem.ate_frontdoor` | `S.Assumptions ‚Üí S.ATE = S.frontdoorATE` | **Frontdoor identification of the ATE** `prop:po-frontdoor-ate`: immediate corollary of `EofY_eq_frontdoorTerm` at `a = 1, 0` by linearity of the integral | **Proved** |

---

## 9l. `PO/ID/Exact/Proximal/` ‚ÄĒ Proximal ATE Identification (PO framework, skeleton)

Proximal causal inference ATE identification under binary treatment `A`,
outcome-side proxy `W`, treatment-side proxy `Z`, latent confounder `U`, and covariate `X`.
Implements def:po-proximal-ate from Basic Concepts.tex ¬ß2555‚Äď2622.
Split into four files: **Setup** (data layer), **Assumptions** (assumption bundle), **Helpers** (CE manipulations), **Main** (identification theorems).

### `Proximal/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ Data layer

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POProximalSystem P ő≥_X ő≥_Z ő≥_W ő≥_U` | `structure { Xvar, Avar, Zvar, Wvar, Yvar, Uvar }` | Six `POVar`s: covariate `X`, binary treatment `A`, treatment-side proxy `Z`, outcome-side proxy `W`, real outcome `Y`, latent confounder `U` | **Defined** |
| `S.{X, A, Z, W, Y, U}` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ő≥_*` | Factual maps for each variable | **Defined** |
| `S.YofA a` | `P.ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Potential outcome `Y(a) := Yvar.cfUnder Avar a` | **Defined** |
| `S.{AZX, AUX, UX, AZUX}` | `P.ő© ‚Üí Bool √ó ‚Ä¶` | Tuple maps used as conditioning targets | **Defined** |
| `S.{ŌÉ_AZX, ŌÉ_AUX, ŌÉ_UX, ŌÉ_AZUX}` | `MeasurableSpace P.ő©` | ŌÉ-algebras generated by the respective tuples | **Defined** |
| `S.{ŌÉ_AZX_le, ŌÉ_AUX_le, ŌÉ_UX_le, ŌÉ_AZUX_le}` | `S.ŌÉ_* ‚Č§ inferInstance` | Sub-ŌÉ-algebra proofs for each | **Proved** |

### `Proximal/Assumptions.lean` ‚ÄĒ Assumption bundle

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POProximalSystem.Assumptions S őľ` | `structure { consistency, latent_exch, proxy_YZ, proxy_WAZ, h, measurable_h, integrable_hAWX, bridge, positivity_arm, completeness, integrable_{Y,YofA0,YofA1,h0WX,h1WX} }` | Full proximal assumption bundle (def:po-proximal-assumptions): consistency, latent exchangeability Y(a) ‚üā A \| (U,X), outcome proxy Y ‚üā Z \| (A,U,X), treatment proxy W ‚üā (A,Z) \| (U,X), outcome bridge function `h` with E[Y ‚ąí h(A,W,X) \| A,Z,X] = 0, `positivity_arm` (Miao 2018 Asm. 7: each treatment arm has positive conditional probability given (U,X)), stratum-wise Z-completeness for (U,X) functionals on each arm `{A=a}` (Miao 2018 Asm. 8), and integrabilities | **Defined** |

### `Proximal/Helpers.lean` ‚ÄĒ Conditional-expectation manipulation lemmas

Six helper lemmas for the proximal ATE proof.  The five "drop conditioning" lemmas remain `sorry`'d pending downstream proof cleanup around the now-proved generic drop-conditioning lemma; the sixth (`eq_zero_globally_of_eq_zero_on_arm`) is proved and uses `positivity_arm` to lift a stratum-wise zero ŌÉ_UX-measurable function to global őľ-zero.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `condExp_drop_Z HA` | `őľ[S.Y \| S.ŌÉ_AZUX] =ŠĶź[őľ] őľ[S.Y \| S.ŌÉ_AUX]` | Drop Z from conditioning on Y: Y ‚üā Z \| (A,U,X) makes Z irrelevant in ŌÉ(A,Z,U,X) | **sorry** (needs `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep`) |
| `condExp_h_drop_Z HA a` | `őľ[h(a,W,X) \| ŌÉ_AZUX] =ŠĶź[őľ] őľ[h(a,W,X) \| ŌÉ_AUX]` | Drop Z from conditioning on h(a,W,X): via weak union from W ‚üā (A,Z) \| (U,X) | **sorry** (needs `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep` + weak union) |
| `condExp_h_drop_A HA a` | `őľ[h(a,W,X) \| ŌÉ_AUX] =ŠĶź[őľ] őľ[h(a,W,X) \| ŌÉ_UX]` | Drop A from conditioning on h(a,W,X): W ‚üā A \| (U,X) (projection from W ‚üā (A,Z) \| (U,X)) | **sorry** (needs `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep` + CI projection) |
| `latent_exch_to_condExp HA a` | `őľ[S.YofA a \| S.ŌÉ_AUX] =ŠĶź[őľ] őľ[S.YofA a \| S.ŌÉ_UX]` | Drop A from conditioning on Y(a): Y(a) ‚üā A \| (U,X) makes A irrelevant | **sorry** (needs `condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep`) |
| `consistency_event HA a hAY` | `őľ[S.Y \| S.ŌÉ_AUX] =ŠĶź[őľ.restrict {A=a}] őľ[S.YofA a \| S.ŌÉ_AUX]` | On {A=a}, Y = Y(a); requires `hAY : S.Avar.v ‚Č† S.Yvar.v`.  Step 1 (Y =ŠĶź Y(a) on restrict) proved via `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` + `ae_restrict_of_forall_mem`.  Step 2 (lift to condExp on restrict) `sorry`'d | **sorry** (step 2 needs condExp-over-restrict lift lemma) |
| `eq_zero_globally_of_eq_zero_on_arm HA a hf_meas hf_zero_on_arm` | `f =ŠĶź[őľ] 0` (when `f` is ŌÉ_UX-measurable and zero on arm `{A=a}`) | Lift a stratum-wise zero ŌÉ_UX-measurable function to global őľ-zero via `positivity_arm` (Miao 2018 Asm. 7). | **Proved** |

### `Proximal/Main.lean` ‚ÄĒ Identification theorems

Ties the helper lemmas together into the two main identification results.
`ate_proximal` is fully proved (one-line, reduces to `Eofyofa_eq_Eh`).
`Eofyofa_eq_Eh` is `sorry`'d pending resolution of the helper blockers and
the `g_a` measurable-selection construction.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Eofyofa_eq_Eh HA a hAY` | `‚ąę ŌČ, S.YofA a ŌČ ‚ąāőľ = ‚ąę ŌČ, HA.h (a, S.W ŌČ, S.X ŌČ) ‚ąāőľ` | For each treatment level `a`, counterfactual mean equals bridge mean; requires constructing `g_a : ő≥_U √ó ő≥_X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` via ŌÉ-algebra factorisation and applying completeness | **sorry** (blocked on helpers + needs measurable-selection for `g_a`) |
| `ate_proximal HA hAY` | `‚ąę YofA true ‚ąāőľ ‚ąí ‚ąę YofA false ‚ąāőľ = ‚ąę h(true,W,X) ‚ąāőľ ‚ąí ‚ąę h(false,W,X) ‚ąāőľ` | ATE equals difference of bridge integrals; immediate from `Eofyofa_eq_Eh` for both treatment levels | **Proved** |

## 10. `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/PolynomialImageDimension/` ‚ÄĒ affine polynomial-image dimension

This algebraic-geometry substrate turns concrete polynomial parameterizations into exact dimensions of their complex Zariski image closures. It provides the closure and irreducibility language, identifies an image closure's coordinate ring with the coordinate subalgebra, and then derives dimension from either a transcendence-degree certificate or a nonzero Jacobian minor plus a matching upper certificate. It is neutral infrastructure for algebraic statistical models and generic-identifiability arguments.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.ZariskiClosure -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure` | `Set (őĻ ‚Üí ‚Ąā) ‚Üí Set (őĻ ‚Üí ‚Ąā)` | Algebraic closure in a complex affine space. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure_extensive` | `A ‚äÜ Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure A` | Every set is contained in its affine Zariski closure. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure_mono` | `A ‚äÜ B ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure A ‚äÜ Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure B` | Affine Zariski closure is monotone. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure_idem` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure (Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure A) = Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure A` | Affine Zariski closure is idempotent. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure_eq_zeroLocus` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure Z = Z ‚Üí Z = MvPolynomial.zeroLocus ‚Ąā (MvPolynomial.vanishingIdeal ‚Ąā Z)` | A closed affine set is the zero locus of its vanishing ideal. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure_inter` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure A = A ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure B = B ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure (A ‚ą© B) = A ‚ą© B` | The intersection of two affine-closed sets is affine-closed. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure_zero_of_polynomial` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure {x \| (MvPolynomial.eval x) P = 0} = {x \| (MvPolynomial.eval x) P = 0}` | The zero set of one multivariate polynomial is affine-closed. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure_union` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure A = A ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure B = B ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure (A ‚ą™ B) = A ‚ą™ B` | The union of two affine-closed sets is affine-closed. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure_nonvanishing_eq_univ` | `P ‚Č† 0 ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure {x \| (MvPolynomial.eval x) P ‚Č† 0} = Set.univ` | The nonvanishing locus of a nonzero polynomial is Zariski dense. |
<!-- /GEN -->

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.Irreducibility -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.IsIrreducibleAffineClosed` | `Set (őĻ ‚Üí ‚Ąā) ‚Üí Prop` | An irreducible affine-closed set is a nonempty polynomially closed set that cannot be expressed as the union of two smaller polynomially closed sets. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.vanishingIdeal_ne_top_of_nonempty` | `Z.Nonempty ‚Üí MvPolynomial.vanishingIdeal ‚Ąā Z ‚Č† ‚ä§` | A nonempty affine set has a proper vanishing ideal. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.nonempty_zeroLocus_of_prime` | `(MvPolynomial.zeroLocus ‚Ąā P).Nonempty` | A prime ideal in a finite complex polynomial ring has a nonempty zero locus. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.irreducibleAffineClosed_iff_isPrime` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure Z = Z ‚Üí Z.Nonempty ‚Üí (Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.IsIrreducibleAffineClosed Z ‚ÜĒ (MvPolynomial.vanishingIdeal ‚Ąā Z).IsPrime)` | A nonempty polynomially closed complex set is irreducible exactly when the polynomial equations that vanish on it form a prime ideal. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.irreducible_zeroLocus_of_prime` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.IsIrreducibleAffineClosed (MvPolynomial.zeroLocus ‚Ąā P)` | The zero locus of a prime ideal is irreducible affine-closed. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.vanishingIdeal_strict_anti` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure A = A ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.affineZariskiClosure B = B ‚Üí A ‚äā B ‚Üí MvPolynomial.vanishingIdeal ‚Ąā B < MvPolynomial.vanishingIdeal ‚Ąā A` | Strict inclusion of affine-closed sets strictly reverses their vanishing ideals. |
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.zeroLocus_strict_anti` | `P < Q ‚Üí MvPolynomial.zeroLocus ‚Ąā Q ‚äā MvPolynomial.zeroLocus ‚Ąā P` | Strict inclusion of prime ideals strictly reverses their complex zero loci. |
<!-- /GEN -->

|---|---|---|
| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.irreducible_coordinate_constant_of_finite_range` | `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.IsIrreducibleAffineClosed Z ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (c : őļ) (S : Finset ‚Ąā), (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą Z, x c ‚ąą S) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ z ‚ąą S, ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą Z, x c = z` | A regular coordinate taking only finitely many values on a nonempty irreducible affine algebraic set is constant. |

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.CodimensionOne -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|

| `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension.HasAffineCodimensionIn` | `‚Ąē ‚Üí Set (őĻ ‚Üí ‚Ąā) ‚Üí Set (őĻ ‚Üí ‚Ąā) ‚Üí Prop` | A locus has affine codimension `d` in an ambient set when every irreducible component admits an endpoint-fixed chain of length `d`, and one component admits no such chain of length `d + 1`. |

This is the endpoint-fixed form of Krull's principal ideal theorem needed by `HasAffineCodimensionIn 1`. |

The proof appends `Y` and `X` to a maximal-length chain inside `C`, contradicting the upper bound for `X`. |

## 10a. `Mathlib/CondDistrib.lean` ‚ÄĒ Missing `condDistrib` lemmas (stubs to upstream)

Collects general-purpose conditional-distribution lemmas that are not
specific to this project and should plausibly be upstreamed to Mathlib.
Statements here are independent of the SCM / SWIG infrastructure.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.condDistrib_comp_right_measurableEquiv` | `‚ąÄŠĶź x ‚ąā(őľ.map X), condDistrib Y (e ‚ąė X) őľ (e x) = condDistrib Y X őľ x` (for `e : őĪ ‚ČÉŠĶź ő≤`) | Push-forward invariance of `condDistrib` under a measurable equivalence of the conditioning variable; proof via uniqueness of disintegration (`condDistrib_ae_eq_iff_measure_eq_compProd`) applied to the transported kernel `(condDistrib Y X őľ).comap e.symm e.symm.measurable`. `Rule2Kernel`'s `hA_extract` step was closed inline without this stub. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.measure_eq_bind_marginal_condDistrib` | `őľ = (őľ.map Prod.snd).bind (fun c => (condDistrib Prod.fst Prod.snd őľ c).map (fun b => (b, c)))` for finite `őľ : Measure (ő≤ √ó ő≥)`, `ő≤` standard Borel | Measure-theoretic disintegration / chain rule: a finite measure on `ő≤ √ó ő≥` equals the `bind` of its `ő≥`-marginal against the regular conditional distribution of `ő≤ | ő≥`; consumed by the backdoor completeness chain-rule step | **Proved** |

## 10a''. `Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/GraphMapProd.lean` ‚ÄĒ graph push-forward of a product measure

General measure-theoretic core of "exogenous noise + a structural mechanism gives a composition
product": pushing a product measure forward along a graph map preserving the first coordinate is a
`compProd`. Independent of the SCM/SWIG layer; used by Invariant Causal Prediction (`MechanismFactor`).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.GraphMapProd.mechanismKernel` | `(őĹ‚āó : Measure ő≥) (ő¶ : ő≤ √ó ő≥ ‚Üí őī) : Kernel ő≤ őī`, with `mechanismKernel őĹ‚āó ő¶ o = őĹ‚āó.map (ő¶ (o, ¬∑))` | The structural-mechanism kernel: at each value `o` of the first coordinate, push the second-factor law `őĹ‚āó` through the slice `ő¶ (o, ¬∑)`. Markov when `őĹ‚āó` is a probability measure and `ő¶` measurable. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.GraphMapProd.map_graph_prod_eq_compProd` | `Measure.map (fun p => (p.1, ő¶ p)) (őĪ.prod őĹ‚āó) = őĪ.compProd (mechanismKernel őĹ‚āó ő¶)` (`őĪ` sfinite, `őĹ‚āó` probability, `ő¶` measurable) | Pushing the product `őĪ ‚äó őĹ‚āó` along the graph map `(o,l) ‚Ü¶ (o, ő¶(o,l))` equals the composition product against `mechanismKernel`. | **Proved** |

## 10a'''. `Mathlib/Probability/StdNormalCDF.lean` & `StdNormalMoments.lean` ‚ÄĒ standard-normal CDF, probit, truncated moment

Self-contained one-dimensional standard-normal analysis (Mathlib gap; upstream-contribution candidate), used by the Gaussian special case of the Dorn‚ÄďGuo MSM bound. `ő¶` reuses Mathlib's `cdf (gaussianReal 0 1)` (inheriting monotonicity + `atBot/atTop` limits); the rest is proven here. All `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdNormalPDF` / `stdNormalCDF` | `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | the standard-normal density `ŌÜ = gaussianPDFReal 0 1` and CDF `ő¶ = cdf (gaussianReal 0 1)` | **Defined** |
| `stdNormalCDF_continuous` | `Continuous stdNormalCDF` | `ő¶` continuous ‚ÄĒ the standard normal is atomless (StieltjesFunction jump = 0) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `stdNormalCDF_strictMono` | `StrictMono stdNormalCDF` | `ő¶` strictly increasing ‚ÄĒ positive mass on every interval | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `stdNormalCDF_pos` / `stdNormalCDF_lt_one` | `0 < ő¶ x` / `ő¶ x < 1` | full support: `ő¶` stays in the open interval `(0,1)` | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `probit` | `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ := sInf {x \| p ‚Č§ ő¶ x}` | the standard-normal quantile `ő¶‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` (generalized inverse) | **Defined** |
| `stdNormalCDF_probit` / `probit_stdNormalCDF` | `ő¶(ő¶‚ĀĽ¬Ļ p)=p` on `(0,1)` / `ő¶‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(ő¶ x)=x` | exact two-sided inversion (IVT + strict monotonicity) | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `integral_Ioi_stdNormalPDF` | `‚ąę_{c}^‚ąě ŌÜ = 1 ‚ąí ő¶(c)` | the survival integral | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `integral_Ioi_id_mul_stdNormalPDF` | `‚ąę_{c}^‚ąě t¬∑ŌÜ(t) dt = ŌÜ(c)` | the **truncated first moment** (antiderivative `‚ąíŌÜ` + FTC) ‚ÄĒ the identity behind the closed-form Gaussian MSM half-width | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `gaussianMSMHalfWidthFactor` | `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ := (őõ¬≤‚ąí1)/őõ ¬∑ ŌÜ(ő¶‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(őõ/(őõ+1)))` | the Dorn‚ÄďGuo Gaussian half-width scalar | **Defined** |

`Mathlib/Probability/MeasurableCondQuantile.lean` (**proved, axiom-clean**) builds the remaining Dorn‚ÄďGuo Phase-7 substrate from Mathlib's `condCDF`: `condQuantile ŌĀ ŌĄ a = sInf {x | ŌĄ a ‚Č§ condCDF ŌĀ a x}` (the conditional generalized inverse) and `measurable_condQuantile_and_attains` ‚ÄĒ it is measurable in `a` (sublevel-set identity `{q ‚Č§ t} = {a | ŌĄ a ‚Č§ condCDF ŌĀ a t}` + `measurable_condCDF`) and, under atomless conditional laws (`Continuous (condCDF ŌĀ a)`), attains the level `condCDF ŌĀ a (condQuantile ŌĀ ŌĄ a) = ŌĄ a` (right-continuity ‚áí inf attained; IVT ‚áí exact). The conditional analogue of `Causalean.Stat.quantile`; an upstream-Mathlib-contribution candidate. It is consumed by `MSM/CutoffConstruct.lean` (¬ß9j2msm) to construct the calibrating cutoff and close the sharp bound.

## 10a''''. `Mathlib/Analysis/SecondOrderDescent.lean` ‚ÄĒ one-dimensional smooth descent

Mathlib-shaped scalar optimization helper: a second-derivative upper bound on a real interval
gives a quadratic Taylor upper model, and a strictly negative initial slope gives an explicit
descent gap at the clipped model-optimal step. The module is objective-agnostic; callers supply
the C¬≤ regularity and pointwise curvature bound.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.SecondOrderDescent -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep` | `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The quadratic-model step chooses the endpoint when curvature is zero and otherwise clips the unconstrained optimizer at the available interval length. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep_of_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep 0 c T = T` | With zero curvature bound, the clipped quadratic-model step is the endpoint. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep_of_pos` | `0 < M ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (c T : ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T = min T (c / M)` | With a positive curvature bound, the clipped quadratic-model step is the smaller of the available interval length and the unconstrained quadratic-model optimizer. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep_mem_Icc` | `0 ‚Č§ M ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ c ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ T ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T` | The clipped quadratic-model step is feasible whenever the interval length, slope margin, and curvature bound are nonnegative. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.second_order_upper_bound` | `0 ‚Č§ T ‚Üí ContDiffOn ‚ĄĚ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) ‚Üí DifferentiableAt ‚ĄĚ f 0 ‚Üí (‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ‚Č§ M) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {t : ‚ĄĚ}, t ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T ‚Üí f t ‚Č§ f 0 + deriv f 0 * t + M / 2 * t ^ 2` | A twice differentiable real function whose second derivative is bounded above on an interval is no larger than its tangent at the left endpoint plus the quadratic curvature allowance. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.first_order_upper_bound` | `0 ‚Č§ T ‚Üí ContDiffOn ‚ĄĚ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) ‚Üí DifferentiableAt ‚ĄĚ f 0 ‚Üí (‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ‚Č§ 0) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {t : ‚ĄĚ}, t ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T ‚Üí f t ‚Č§ f 0 + deriv f 0 * t` | A real function with nonpositive second derivative on an interval lies below its tangent line at the left endpoint. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.second_order_descent_gap` | `0 ‚Č§ T ‚Üí 0 < c ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ M ‚Üí ContDiffOn ‚ĄĚ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) ‚Üí DifferentiableAt ‚ĄĚ f 0 ‚Üí (‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ‚Č§ M) ‚Üí deriv f 0 ‚Č§ -c ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T ‚ąß f 0 - f (Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T) ‚Č• c * Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T - M / 2 * Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T ^ 2` | A negative initial slope plus an upper curvature bound gives a guaranteed decrease at the clipped quadratic-model step. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.second_order_descent_gap_min` | `0 ‚Č§ T ‚Üí 0 < c ‚Üí 0 < M ‚Üí ContDiffOn ‚ĄĚ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) ‚Üí DifferentiableAt ‚ĄĚ f 0 ‚Üí (‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ‚Č§ M) ‚Üí deriv f 0 ‚Č§ -c ‚Üí f 0 - f (min T (c / M)) ‚Č• c / 2 * min T (c / M)` | With positive curvature, the clipped quadratic-model step gives at least half of the linear descent term. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.first_order_descent_gap` | `0 ‚Č§ T ‚Üí 0 < c ‚Üí ContDiffOn ‚ĄĚ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) ‚Üí DifferentiableAt ‚ĄĚ f 0 ‚Üí (‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ‚Č§ 0) ‚Üí deriv f 0 ‚Č§ -c ‚Üí f 0 - f T ‚Č• c * T` | With zero curvature, the endpoint step gives the full linear descent guaranteed by the negative initial slope. |
| `Mathlib.Analysis.second_order_descent_gap_half` | `0 ‚Č§ T ‚Üí 0 < c ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ M ‚Üí ContDiffOn ‚ĄĚ 2 f (Set.Icc 0 T) ‚Üí DifferentiableAt ‚ĄĚ f 0 ‚Üí (‚ąÄ t ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T, deriv (deriv f) t ‚Č§ M) ‚Üí deriv f 0 ‚Č§ -c ‚Üí Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 T ‚ąß f 0 - f (Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T) ‚Č• c / 2 * Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.descentStep M c T` | In both zero and positive curvature regimes, the clipped quadratic-model step gives a uniform half-linear decrease. |
<!-- /GEN -->

## 10a'. `Mathlib/CondDistribWitness.lean` ‚ÄĒ Witness-kernel form of a conditional distribution

The measure-theoretic core of a backdoor / Rule-2 argument, stated purely in terms of
Mathlib's `condDistrib` (no SCM machinery).  When the outcome is a measurable function of the
conditioning variable and a residual coordinate, its conditional law is the pushforward of the
residual's conditional law by that function ‚ÄĒ the "witness kernel".

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ProbabilityTheory.witnessKernel` | `Kernel (ūĚí≥ √ó ūĚíĶ) ūĚíī`, `p ‚Ü¶ (condDistrib C Z őľ p.2).map (h p.1 p.2)` | Packages the posterior witness kernel `p ‚Ü¶ (condDistrib C Z őľ p.2).map (h p.1 p.2)` as a `Kernel`; Markov when `őľ` is finite. | **Proved** |
| `ProbabilityTheory.condDistrib_map_of_condDistrib_fst_eq` | If `Y = h X Z C` pointwise and `C ‚ä• X ‚ą£ Z` (as `condDistrib C (X,Z) őľ =ŠĶź condDistrib C Z őľ`), then `condDistrib Y (X,Z) őľ =ŠĶź (fun p => (condDistrib C Z őľ p.2).map (h p.1 p.2))` | The witness-kernel form of a conditional distribution under conditional independence; the SCM-agnostic heart of the backdoor / Rule-2 identification. Proof by uniqueness of disintegration on rectangles. | **Proved** |
| `ProbabilityTheory.condDistrib_map_of_funext` | If `Y = H Z C` pointwise then `condDistrib Y Z őľ =ŠĶź[őľ.map Z] (fun z => (condDistrib C Z őľ z).map (H z))` | The **no-treatment** (`X`-free) specialization of the witness lemma: when the conditioning variable `Z` already carries all the information used to split `Y`, no conditional-independence hypothesis is needed. Consumed by the do-side bridge `doSide_M2_pullback_eq_M1_witness`. | **Proved** |

## 10b. `Mathlib/CondIndep.lean` ‚ÄĒ Graph-independent CI axioms (re-export)

Re-exports the four sub-modules below.  Existing `import Causalean.Mathlib.CondIndep`
statements continue to work unchanged.

### 10b-i. `Mathlib/CondIndep/SemiGraphoid.lean` ‚ÄĒ Semi-graphoid axioms on `ValuesOn` projections

Weak union and contraction for `CondIndepFun` specialized to `ValuesOn I ő©` projections.
Requires `Causalean.SCM.Model.SCM`.

### 10b-ii. `Mathlib/CondIndep/CondExp.lean` ‚ÄĒ CondExp + CondIndepFun lemmas

Drop-of-conditioning, product factorization, weak union via `prodMk`, and extension
by an `m`-measurable function.  No SCM import required.

### 10b-iii. `Mathlib/CondIndep/AELift.lean` ‚ÄĒ A.e.-equality/inequality lifts under overlap

Single-arm a.e.-lift and essential-bound inheritance for potential-outcome variables.

### 10b-iv. `Mathlib/CondIndep/Integrability.lean` ‚ÄĒ Integrability via condExp indicators

ŌÉ-projection of indicator positivity and integrability bound from a condExp product identity.

**SemiGraphoid** (`CondIndep/SemiGraphoid.lean`):

|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.condIndep_valuesProjection_weak_union` | `CondIndepFun (ŌÉ_Z) (valProjX) (valProjYW) őľ ‚Üí CondIndepFun (ŌÉ_ZW) (valProjX) (valProjY) őľ` | Weak-union axiom on `ValuesOn` projections; proved by decomposing the union projection into `(Y,W)` and applying the generic `prodMk` weak-union helper | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndep_valuesProjection_contraction` | `CondIndepFun (ŌÉ_ZW) X Y őľ ‚Üí CondIndepFun ŌÉ_Z X W őľ ‚Üí CondIndepFun ŌÉ_Z X (valProjYW) őľ` | Contraction axiom on `ValuesOn` projections; proved by `condIndepFun_contraction_of_prodMk` plus projection-union assembly | **Proved** |

| `Causalean.condExp_sup_comap_eq_of_condIndep` | `CondIndepFun m hm g f őľ ‚Üí Measurable h ‚Üí Integrable (h ‚ąė g) őľ ‚Üí őľ[h ‚ąė g \| m ‚äĒ ŌÉ(f)] =ŠĶź[őľ] őľ[h ‚ąė g \| m]` | Drop-of-conditioning: if `g ‚üā f \| m`, enlarging conditioning by `ŌÉ(f)` leaves `h ‚ąė g`'s condExp unchanged; reduces to the private `ŌÉ(g)`-measurable helper | **Proved** |

Proof via `indepFun_iff_measure_inter_preimage_eq_mul` + `Measure.map_apply_of_aemeasurable` | **Proved** |

## 10d. `Mathlib/IndepIntegral.lean` ‚ÄĒ Drop-of-conditioning integral identity

Upstream candidate.  Contains no Causalean-specific definitions; namespaced
directly under `ProbabilityTheory` so it can be contributed back to Mathlib
verbatim.  Extracted from the IV/LATE first-stage and reduced-form proofs.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |

| `ProbabilityTheory.IndepFun.integral_restrict_preimage_eq_mul` | `IndepFun f g őľ`, `Measurable f`, `Measurable g`, `MeasurableSet E`, `Measurable h : ő≤ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` ‚Üí `‚ąę ŌČ in f ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' E, h (g ŌČ) ‚ąāőľ = (őľ (f ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ' E)).toReal * ‚ąę ŌČ, h (g ŌČ) ‚ąāőľ` | Drop-of-conditioning: under independence of `f` and `g`, conditioning on an event in the `f`-fibre factors out as a scalar multiple.  Proof rewrites the set integral as an indicator-weighted integral, applies `IndepFun.integral_fun_comp_mul_comp`, and evaluates via `integral_indicator_one` | **Proved** (upstream candidate ‚ÄĒ no Causalean types) |

parameter map `ŌČ ‚Ü¶ ‚ÄĖg ŌČ‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P)}` by rewriting the L¬≤ norm as a parametric
lintegral and applying Tonelli measurability.

|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.measurable_eLpNorm_two_toReal_of_uncurry` | `Measurable (Function.uncurry g)` ‚Üí `Measurable (fun ŌČ => (eLpNorm (g ŌČ) 2 P).toReal)` | Top-ŌÉ-algebra version. Uses `Measurable.lintegral_prod_right'` on `‚ÄĖg ŌČ x‚ÄĖ‚āĎ^2`, rewrites `eLpNorm`, and post-processes with `.toReal`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.measurable_eLpNorm_two_toReal_of_uncurry_of_factor` | `Measurable[mő©.prod ŌÉ_X] (Function.uncurry g)` ‚Üí `Measurable[mő©] (fun ŌČ => (eLpNorm (g ŌČ) 2 P).toReal)` | Sub-ŌÉ-algebra product version. The theorem requires joint measurability with respect to the sub-ŌÉ-algebra product; coordinate/cylinder measurability alone is not enough to invoke Mathlib's parametric lintegral measurability lemma. | **Proved** |

## 10d'. `Mathlib/IIDCenteredSum.lean` ‚ÄĒ Conditional 2nd-moment bound for centered i.i.d. sums

Upstream candidate.  Isolates the central probabilistic inequality used by the
fold-B empirical-process bound in `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.lean`: for a
finite family of i.i.d. samples independent of a sub-ŌÉ-algebra `m_A`, the
expected square of the centered scaled sum is bounded by the expected
`L¬≤(P)`-norm squared of the integrand.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lintegral_ofReal_inv_sqrt_smul_sum_sq_le` (private) | `0 < s.card`, unscaled square `‚ąę‚ĀĽ(ő£ Y_i)¬≤ ‚Č§ s.card ¬∑ ‚ąę‚ĀĽ B` ‚Üí scaled `‚ąę‚ĀĽ ((1/‚ąös.card)¬∑ő£ Y_i)¬≤ ‚Č§ ‚ąę‚ĀĽ B` | Deterministic algebraic step: the `(1/‚ąös.card)¬≤` prefactor cancels the `s.card` factor on the RHS via `lintegral_const_mul'` and `Real.sq_sqrt`. **Proved by Codex.** |
| `eLpNorm_two_sq_toReal_eq_integral_sq` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` ‚Üí `ENNReal.ofReal ((eLpNorm f 2 P).toReal ^ 2) = ENNReal.ofReal (‚ąę f^2 dP)` | Converts the real `L¬≤` norm square back to the square integral, using `MemLp.eLpNorm_eq_integral_rpow_norm` and nonnegativity of `‚ąę f^2`. **Proved.** |
| `centered_sq_lintegral_le_eLpNorm_two_sq` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` ‚Üí `‚ąę‚ĀĽ ofReal ((f - ‚ąęf)^2) dP ‚Č§ ofReal (‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ¬≤‚āā)` | Diagonal variance bound: rewrites the centered square lintegral as `variance f P`, applies `variance_le_expectation_sq`, then uses `eLpNorm_two_sq_toReal_eq_integral_sq`. **Proved.** |
| `pi_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` ‚Üí under `Measure.pi (fun _ : s => P)`, `‚ąę‚ĀĽ ofReal ((ő£_i (f(v_i)‚ąí‚ąęf))¬≤) ‚Č§ |s|¬∑ofReal(‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ¬≤‚āā)` | Fixed-integrand finite-product estimate. Uses `variance_sum_pi` to eliminate cross terms, proves the centered coordinate mean is zero by `integral_map` along `measurePreserving_eval`, and bounds each variance by `variance_le_expectation_sq`. **Proved.** |
| `indep_trim_prod_map_eq` (private) | `Indep m_A (comap Z) őľ`, `m_A ‚Č§ mő©`, measurable `Z` ‚Üí `map (fun ŌČ => (ŌČ, ZŌČ)) őľ = (őľ.trim hm_A_le).prod (map Z őľ)` | Independent-joining bridge: converts sub-ŌÉ-algebra independence to `IndepFun id Z`, applies `indepFun_iff_map_prod_eq_prod_map_map`, and identifies the first marginal as the trimmed measure. **Proved.** |
| `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le_of_joined_law` (private) | Joined product law `(ŌČ, W_s ŌČ) ‚ąľ (őľ.trim hm_A_le) √ó P^s` plus the public measurability/L¬≤ hypotheses ‚Üí unscaled bound | Product-space form: rewrites the original lintegral through the joined law, applies Tonelli, uses `pi_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` pointwise in `ŌČ`, pulls out `|s|`, and rewrites the trimmed lintegral back to `őľ`. **Proved.** |
| `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le` (private) | Same hypotheses as the public lemma but with conclusion `‚ąę‚ĀĽ (ő£ Y_i)¬≤ ‚Č§ s.card ¬∑ ‚ąę‚ĀĽ ‚ÄĖg ŌČ‚ÄĖ¬≤‚āā` (no `1/‚ąös.card` factor) | Reduces the original hypotheses to the joined product law using `indep_trim_prod_map_eq` and `hW_iid_pi`, then calls `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le_of_joined_law`. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` | `m_A ‚Č§ mő©`, `Indep m_A (comap (W on s)) őľ`, `őľ.map (W on s) = Measure.pi (fun _ => P)`, `Measurable[m_A.prod ŌÉ_X] (uncurry g)`, `‚ąÄ ŌČ, MemLp (g ŌČ) 2 P` ‚Üí `‚ąę‚ĀĽ ŌČ, ENNReal.ofReal (((1/‚ąös.card) * ő£_{i‚ąąs} (g ŌČ (W i ŌČ) - ‚ąęg ŌČ dP))¬≤) ‚ąāőľ ‚Č§ ‚ąę‚ĀĽ ŌČ, ENNReal.ofReal (‚ÄĖg ŌČ‚ÄĖ¬≤_{L¬≤(P)}) ‚ąāőľ` | Public theorem. **Proved.** |

## 10e. `Mathlib/ConvergenceInDistribution.lean` ‚ÄĒ Scalar Slutsky / varying maps

Upstream candidates for convergence in distribution.  Contains no Causalean-specific
definitions; the project-level `Tendsto_dist` wrappers in `Stat/Limit/Convergence.lean`
consume these statements.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MeasureTheory.tendstoInMeasure_const_of_tendsto_real` | `aŠĶĘ ‚Üí a‚āÄ` ‚Üí constant random variables `ŌČ ‚Ü¶ aŠĶĘ` converge in measure to `ŌČ ‚Ü¶ a‚āÄ` | Deterministic topological convergence as convergence in measure for constant real-valued random variables | **Proved** |
| `MeasureTheory.TendstoInDistribution.const_mul_of_tendsto_const` | `XŠĶĘ ‚áí Z` and `aŠĶĘ ‚Üí a‚āÄ` ‚Üí `aŠĶĘ¬∑XŠĶĘ ‚áí a‚āÄ¬∑Z` | Same-probability-space deterministic-scalar Slutsky theorem; proved by Mathlib's product Slutsky theorem `continuous_comp_prodMk_of_tendstoInMeasure_const` with multiplication as the continuous map | **Proved** |
| `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto` | `őĹŠĶĘ ‚Üí őĹ` weakly and `aŠĶĘ ‚Üí a‚āÄ` ‚Üí `(x ‚Ü¶ aŠĶĘx)_#őĹŠĶĘ ‚Üí (x ‚Ü¶ a‚āÄx)_#őĹ` | Measure-level deterministic-scalar Slutsky theorem for weak convergence on ‚ĄĚ. Proved by rewriting `(x ‚Ü¶ aŠĶĘx)_#őĹŠĶĘ` as the push-forward of `őī_{aŠĶĘ} √ó őĹŠĶĘ` by multiplication, then applying continuity of Dirac measures, product probability measures, and the ordinary continuous-mapping theorem | **Proved** |

## 10f. `Mathlib/FWL.lean` ‚ÄĒ Frisch‚ÄďWaugh‚ÄďLovell on a finite-dim inner product space

Upstream candidate.  Self-contained algebraic content of the
Frisch‚ÄďWaugh‚ÄďLovell theorem on a real inner-product space `V` with a
nuisance subspace `H` admitting an orthogonal projection.  Causal- and
panel-agnostic; depends only on Mathlib (`Submodule.orthogonalProjection`,
`Matrix.mulVec`, `Matrix.nonsing_inv`).  **Live consumer:**
`Panel/FWLInstanceL2.lean` (¬ß9i0a-FWLInstanceL2)
instantiates this development on `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` to give the conditional-mean
estimand papers a shared, measure-theory-native FWL engine.  (The finite
weighted-support tower `Weighted/FWL.lean` does **not** instantiate this file:
its inner product is only positive-semidefinite, so it carries its own FWL
built on `Mathlib/SemiInnerProjection`; the two are connected at the data level
via `Weighted/OfProbabilityMeasure.lean`.)  NL artifact:
`doc/basic_concepts/mathlib/fwl.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualize H v` | `Submodule ‚ĄĚ V ‚Üí V ‚Üí V` | Orthogonal residual operator `M_H v = v - P_H v` as a self-map of `V`, defined via `Submodule.orthogonalProjectionFn` (which lands in `V`, not `‚Ü•H`) so the result is a `V`-vector. Requires `[H.HasOrthogonalProjection]`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.inner_residualize_of_mem` | `(v : V) {w : V} (hw : w ‚ąą H) ‚Üí ‚ü™residualize H v, w‚üę = 0` | Residual orthogonality: `M_H v ‚ä• H`. Direct unfolding of `Submodule.orthogonalProjectionFn_inner_eq_zero`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualize_inner_swap_right` | `(v w : V) ‚Üí ‚ü™residualize H v, w‚üę = ‚ü™residualize H v, residualize H w‚üę` | Symmetric residualization in the right slot. Splits `w = M_H w + P_H w` and uses orthogonality of the residual to `H`. The key identity used by FWL: residualizing only one side suffices. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.gramResid H X` | `Submodule ‚ĄĚ V ‚Üí (Fin K ‚Üí V) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized Gram matrix `Q_{XX} j k := ‚ü™M_H (X j), M_H (X k)‚üę`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residInnerVec H X Y` | `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí V ‚Üí (Fin K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Residualized inner-product vector `r_j := ‚ü™M_H (X j), Y‚üę`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef H X Y` | `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí V ‚Üí (Fin K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | The FWL coefficient `őłŐā := Q_{XX}‚ĀĽ¬Ļ *ŠĶ• residInnerVec H X Y`. Defined unconditionally; only the normal-equations identity below needs invertibility of `Q_{XX}`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.gramResid_mulVec_fwlCoef` | `(Y : V) (hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) ‚Üí (gramResid H X) *ŠĶ• fwlCoef H X Y = residInnerVec H X Y` | Residualized normal equations for the FWL coefficient. Pure matrix algebra: `A *ŠĶ• (A‚ĀĽ¬Ļ *ŠĶ• b) = b` via `Matrix.mulVec_mulVec`, `Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv`, `Matrix.one_mulVec`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residInnerVec_eq_residualize_right` | `(Y : V) ‚Üí residInnerVec H X Y = residInnerVec H X (residualize H Y)` | The FWL right-hand side is invariant under residualizing `Y`. Pointwise consequence of `residualize_inner_swap_right`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fittedValue X ő≤` | `(Fin K ‚Üí V) ‚Üí (Fin K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí V` | Finite fitted value `‚ąĎ j, ő≤ j ‚ÄĘ X j`; helper lemmas record linearity under coefficient subtraction and commutation with residualization. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualizedObjective H X Y ő≤` | `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized least-squares objective `‚ü™M_H Y ‚ąí ‚ąĎ j ő≤_j ‚ÄĘ M_H X_j, M_H Y ‚ąí ‚ąĎ j ő≤_j ‚ÄĘ M_H X_j‚üę`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.originalObjective X Y ő≤ h` | `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí V ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Original least-squares objective with an explicit nuisance term `h`: `‚ü™Y ‚ąí Xő≤ ‚ąí h, Y ‚ąí Xő≤ ‚ąí h‚üę`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualizedObjective_eq_fwlCoef_add_error` | `(hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) ‚Üí residualizedObjective H X Y ő≤ = residualizedObjective H X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) + ‚ü™XŐÉ(fwlCoef‚ąíő≤), XŐÉ(fwlCoef‚ąíő≤)‚üę` | Residualized completing-square identity. The nonnegative error term yields residualized minimization and, with Gram invertibility, coefficient uniqueness. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_residualized_minimizes` | `(hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) ‚Üí residualizedObjective H X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) ‚Č§ residualizedObjective H X Y ő≤` | Completing-square theorem: the matrix-defined FWL coefficient minimizes the residualized least-squares objective. Uses the residualized normal equations to kill the cross term. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.originalObjective_eq_residualizedObjective_add_projectionError` | `(hh : h ‚ąą H) ‚Üí originalObjective X Y ő≤ h = residualizedObjective H X Y ő≤ + ‚ü™P_H (Y ‚ąí Xő≤) ‚ąí h, P_H (Y ‚ąí Xő≤) ‚ąí h‚üę` | Pythagorean decomposition of the original objective into residualized loss plus squared nuisance-projection error. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_original_minimizes` | `(hQ) (hh : h ‚ąą H) ‚Üí originalObjective X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) (P_H (Y ‚ąí X fwlCoef)) ‚Č§ originalObjective X Y ő≤ h` | Standard finite-dimensional FWL optimizer theorem: the residualized coefficient paired with the projection of its raw residual minimizes the original least-squares problem over coefficients and nuisance terms in `H`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_eq_of_original_minimizer` | `original-objective minimizer hypothesis ‚Üí ő≤ = fwlCoef H X Y` | ő≤-block uniqueness theorem: any minimizer of the original least-squares problem has coefficient vector equal to the residualized FWL coefficient. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.original_minimizer_eq_fwlCoef_projection` | `original-objective minimizer hypothesis ‚Üí ő≤ = fwlCoef H X Y ‚ąß h = P_H (Y ‚ąí X fwlCoef)` | Full standard FWL uniqueness characterization: every original-problem minimizer is exactly the residualized coefficient paired with the nuisance projection of its raw residual. | **Proved** |

## 10h. `Mathlib/TwoStateMarkov.lean` ‚ÄĒ two-state Markov transition matrix, stationary projection, geometric ergodicity

Project-generic shim (depends only on Mathlib; no causal/panel/Causalean
content) for the two-state Markov chain on `Fin 2` with rows
`(1 - a, a)` and `(b, 1 - b)`. Carries the two transition/stationary
matrix definitions and the substrate for long-T compact-uniform
convergence required by
`CausalSmith/Panel/Q1_SpectralThreshold/LongT.lean`: spectral
decomposition of the transition matrix plus uniform geometric
convergence to the stationary projection on compact subsets of the open
transition-parameter square `(0,1)¬≤`. **Both definitions and all 4
theorems are in place; all theorems proved.**

This file consolidates the two definitions (previously local to
`CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Panel/Q1_SpectralThreshold/Basic.lean`) and the
four ergodicity theorems (previously in
`CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Mathlib/MarkovErgodicity.lean`, now deleted)
into a single neutral Causalean module. `Q1_SpectralThreshold/Basic.lean`
re-exports the two definitions under the short names so existing
downstream code is unchanged.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix` | `(a b : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ‚ĄĚ` | The 2√ó2 row-stochastic transition matrix with rows `(1 - a, a)` and `(b, 1 - b)`. `noncomputable`. | **Defined.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.stationaryProjection` | `(a b : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ‚ĄĚ` | The rank-one projector whose two rows both equal the stationary distribution `(b/(a+b), a/(a+b))`. `noncomputable`. | **Defined.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix_pow_eq_spectral` | `(a b : ‚ĄĚ) (ha_pos : 0 < a) (ha_lt_one : a < 1) (hb_pos : 0 < b) (hb_lt_one : b < 1) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ k, M^k = ő† + (1 - a - b)^k ‚ÄĘ (1 - ő†)` | Spectral decomposition: powers of the transition matrix split into the stationary projection plus a geometrically-decaying correction along `1 - ő†`. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.one_minus_a_b_abs_lt_one` | `(0 < a) (0 < b) (a < 1) (b < 1) ‚Üí |1 - a - b| < 1` | Pointwise spectral gap on the open transition-parameter square. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.one_minus_a_b_uniform_gap_on_compact` | `(K : Set (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ)) (IsCompact K) (K ‚äÜ (0,1)¬≤) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ ŌĀ < 1, ‚ąÄ p ‚ąą K, |1 - p.1 - p.2| ‚Č§ ŌĀ` | Uniform spectral gap on compact subsets via `IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn` + averaging the maximum with `1`. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix_pow_tendsto_stationary_uniform` | `(K compact ‚äÜ (0,1)¬≤) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ őĶ > 0, ‚ąÉ N, ‚ąÄ k ‚Č• N, ‚ąÄ p ‚ąą K, ‚ąÄ i j, |((M p.1 p.2)^k - ő† p.1 p.2) i j| ‚Č§ őĶ` | Uniform geometric convergence of `M^k` to the stationary projection on compact subsets, combining the spectral decomposition (entries of `1 - ő†` bounded by `1`) with the uniform gap (geometric decay rate `ŌĀ < 1`). **Proved.** |

## 10g. `Mathlib/SemiInnerProjection.lean` ‚ÄĒ semidefinite finite-dimensional projections

Upstream candidate for finite-dimensional projection under a symmetric
positive-semidefinite bilinear form.  This is the generic support-quotient
architecture consumed by `Panel/Subspace.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.exists_orthogonalProjection_of_posSemidef` | finite-dimensional `V`, bilinear form `B`, symmetry, `‚ąÄ x, 0 ‚Č§ B x x`, submodule `H` ‚Üí `‚ąÉ P : V ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] V, P X ‚ąą H ‚ąß B (X - P X) h = 0` | Generic semidefinite projection existence. Proof uses the restricted Gram map `H ‚Üí‚āó Dual ‚ĄĚ H`, shows each right-hand side lies in its range via the PSD radical fact and dual-annihilator/range duality, then chooses a linear right inverse of the range restriction. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10i. `Mathlib/StandardGaussian.lean` ‚ÄĒ standard Gaussian on a finite-dim inner-product space

Constructs the standard Gaussian measure on an arbitrary finite-dimensional
real inner-product space `E` (mean zero, covariance = inner product) and proves
its basic moments. Construction: take `b := stdOrthonormalBasis ‚ĄĚ E`, build the
product of `n = finrank ‚ĄĚ E` standard real Gaussians on `Fin n ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, transport
to `EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin n)` via `toLp`, then pull back to `E` along the
isometry `b.repr.symm`. Upstream candidate.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian` | `[FiniteDimensional ‚ĄĚ E] [BorelSpace E] ‚Üí Measure E` | The standard Gaussian measure on `E`: `(prod of gaussianReal 0 1).map toLp` pulled back along `b.repr.symm`. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.isProbabilityMeasure_stdGaussian` | `IsProbabilityMeasure (stdGaussian E)` | Probability measure (pushforward of a `Measure.pi` of probability measures). | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.isGaussian_stdGaussian` | `IsGaussian (stdGaussian E)` | Gaussian law; via `iIndepFun.hasGaussianLaw` for the product, then `isGaussian_map_equiv`. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian_mean` | `‚ąę x, x ‚ąā(stdGaussian E) = 0` | Mean is zero; each coordinate `gaussianReal 0 1` has mean 0, pushed through the continuous-linear maps. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.covarianceBilin_stdGaussian` | `covarianceBilin (stdGaussian E) u v = ‚ü™u, v‚üę_‚ĄĚ` | Covariance bilinear form equals the inner product. Via `covarianceBilin_map` (adjoint of isometry = inverse), `covarianceBilin_apply_pi`, coordinate covariances (Kronecker delta from independence + unit variance), and `OrthonormalBasis.repr` isometry. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian_map_normSq_eq_pi` | `(stdGaussian E).map ‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ÄĖ¬≤ = (Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (finrank ‚ĄĚ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)).map (‚ąĎ (w i)¬≤)` | Exposes the product-of-1-D-Gaussians structure of the squared-norm law (rotation-invariance of `‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ÄĖ¬≤` strips the orthonormal-basis transport), used for Ōá¬≤ atomlessness. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10j. `Mathlib/OperatorSqrt.lean` ‚ÄĒ positive square root of a positive operator

Builds the **operator square root** of a positive symmetric operator on a
finite-dimensional real inner-product space ‚ÄĒ a result Mathlib lacks (it has the
spectral theorem and `LinearMap.IsPositive`, but no operator sqrt). Construction:
in the orthonormal eigenbasis `b` with real eigenvalues `őľ` (both from
`LinearMap.IsSymmetric.eigenvectorBasis`/`eigenvalues`), `posSqrt` acts as
`‚ąö(őľ i)` on each eigenvector (via `Basis.constr`). Upstream candidate. All in
namespace `LinearMap.IsPositive` (with `hT : T.IsPositive`).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `eigBasis` / `eigVals` | `OrthonormalBasis (Fin (finrank ‚ĄĚ E)) ‚ĄĚ E` / `Fin _ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Orthonormal eigenbasis and real eigenvalues of the positive operator `T`. | **Proved.** |
| `eigVals_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ hT.eigVals i` | Eigenvalues of a positive operator are nonnegative (`őľ i = ‚ü™T (b i), b i‚üę ‚Č• 0`). | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt` | `E ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] E` | The square root: `posSqrt (b i) = ‚ąö(őľ i) ‚ÄĘ b i`, extended by `Basis.constr`. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_mul_self` | `hT.posSqrt ‚ąė‚āó hT.posSqrt = T` | Square law, via `Real.mul_self_sqrt` and `Basis.ext`. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_inner` | `‚ü™posSqrt x, y‚üę = ‚ąĎ i, ‚ąö(őľ i) ‚ü™b i,x‚üę ‚ü™b i,y‚üę` | Symmetric weighted-sum form of the quadratic data. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_isSymmetric` / `posSqrt_isPositive` | `IsSymmetric` / `IsPositive` | `posSqrt` is itself symmetric and positive. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM` | `E ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] E` | `posSqrt` packaged as a continuous linear map. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM_isSelfAdjoint` / `posSqrtCLM_adjoint` | `IsSelfAdjoint posSqrtCLM` / `adjoint posSqrtCLM = posSqrtCLM` | Self-adjointness of the continuous square root. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM_comp_self` | `posSqrtCLM ‚ąėL posSqrtCLM = T.toContinuousLinearMap` | Continuous square law. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10j. `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/` ‚ÄĒ finite Vandermonde systems

Reusable nonsingularity, synthesis-dimension, and block-injectivity certificates
for ordinary and confluent Vandermonde systems.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.ConfluentVandermonde -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
