## 10j'. `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Cholesky.lean` ‚ÄĒ real Cholesky factorization (existence + uniqueness)

The **Cholesky factorization** of a real symmetric positive-definite matrix `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ‚ĄĚ`: there is a unique upper-triangular matrix `U` with strictly positive diagonal such that `M = UŠĶÄ U` (here "upper-triangular" means `j < i ‚Üí U i j = 0`, all strictly-below-diagonal entries vanish). Existence is derived from Mathlib's LDL decomposition (`LDL.lower_conj_diag`) by absorbing the square roots of the diagonal factor: `U = ‚ąöD ¬∑ LŠĶÄ` where `L = LDL.lower` and `D = LDL.diag`. Uniqueness reduces to the fact that an orthogonal upper-triangular matrix with positive diagonal is the identity. Mathlib has LDL but no Cholesky; upstream candidate. Built for the causal-disentanglement formalization. Namespace `Causalean.Mathlib.LinearAlgebra`. Sorry-free (axioms: `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsUpperTri` / `isUpperTri_iff_blockTriangular` | `‚ąÄ i j, j < i ‚Üí U i j = 0` / `‚ÜĒ U.BlockTriangular id` | Strict upper-triangularity predicate and its identification with Mathlib's `BlockTriangular id`. | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lowerInv_diag_one` | `LDL.lowerInv hM i i = 1` | The Gram-Schmidt lower-inverse matrix is unitriangular (diagonal entries are `1`). | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_diagEntries_pos` | `0 < LDL.diagEntries hM i` | The LDL diagonal entries of a positive-definite matrix are strictly positive (each is `xŠĶÄMx > 0` for the nonzero `x = lowerInv` row). | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lowerInv_blockTriangular` / `ldl_lower_blockTriangular` | `(‚Ä¶).BlockTriangular toDual` | `LDL.lowerInv` and `LDL.lower` are lower-triangular. | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lower_diag_one` | `LDL.lower hM i i = 1` | `LDL.lower` is unitriangular (inverse of a unitriangular lower-triangular matrix). | **Proved.** |
| `cholesky_exists` | `‚ąÉ U, (‚ąÄ i j, j<i ‚Üí U i j = 0) ‚ąß (‚ąÄ i, 0 < U i i) ‚ąß M = UŠĶÄ U` | **Existence.** Every real positive-definite matrix factors as `UŠĶÄ U` with `U` upper-triangular, positive-diagonal. | **Proved.** |
| `orthogonal_upperTri_pos_diag_eq_one` | `WŠĶÄ W = 1 ‚Üí upper-tri ‚Üí pos-diag ‚Üí W = 1` | **Key uniqueness lemma.** An orthogonal upper-triangular matrix with positive diagonal is the identity (its transpose is both upper and lower triangular, hence diagonal, hence `¬Ī1`, hence `1`). | **Proved.** |
| `cholesky_unique` | `M = UŠĶÄ U = VŠĶÄ V ‚Üí (both upper-tri pos-diag) ‚Üí U = V` | **Uniqueness.** The factorization is unique: `W = V U‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` is orthogonal upper-triangular with positive diagonal, so `W = 1`. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10k. `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/PartitionIntegral.lean` ‚ÄĒ finite-partition integral algebra

Decomposing an integral over the fibers of a finite-valued measurable map `H : ő© ‚Üí őĻ` (a finite partition of the sample space). Causal-agnostic, Mathlib-shaped measure theory (a Mathlib-PR candidate); relocated here from `Stat/` so any layer (estimation, panel cell algebra, partial-ID) can use it without pulling in `Stat/` or `PO/`. Complements `Causalean.PO.integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp` (the `eventCondExp`/measure-weighted form for an explicit set family): here the form is measurable-map / set-integral, plus the cell-constant pull-out the `PO` lemma does not provide. Index type carries `[Fintype őĻ] [MeasurableSpace őĻ] [MeasurableSingletonClass őĻ]`; no measurability of the cell-weight `c : őĻ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` is needed (it is constant on each fiber). Namespace `Causalean.Mathlib.MeasureTheory`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `integral_eq_sum_setIntegral_fiber` | `‚ąę f = ‚ąĎ h, ‚ąę_{H‚ĀĽ¬Ļ{h}} f` | **Fiber decomposition.** For integrable `f` and finite-valued measurable `H`, the integral splits as the sum of set-integrals over the fibers. Wraps `integral_iUnion_fintype` over the fiber cover of `univ`. **Proved.** |
| `integral_cellConst_mul` | `‚ąę c(H ŌČ)¬∑f ŌČ = ‚ąĎ h, c h ¬∑ ‚ąę_{H‚ĀĽ¬Ļ{h}} f` | **Cell-constant pull-out.** An integrand weighted by a cell-dependent value `c (H ŌČ)` decomposes with `c h` pulled out per cell. Workhorse for panel cell-by-cell regression algebra and cell-weighted-average estimands. No measurability of `c` required. **Proved.** |
| `integral_cellConst` | `‚ąę c(H ŌČ) = ‚ąĎ h, c h ¬∑ őľ(H‚ĀĽ¬Ļ{h})` | **Cell-weight aggregation.** For a finite measure, the integral of a cell-constant function is the cell-weighted sum of cell masses. Special case of `integral_cellConst_mul` with `f ‚Č° 1`. **Proved.** |

## 10k'. `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/CondExpIndicatorTower.lean` ‚ÄĒ conditioning ‚ÜĒ inner-regression weighting

Causal-agnostic conditional-expectation identity (a Mathlib-PR candidate; namespace `MeasureTheory`): the measure-theoretic kernel behind "regression adjustment = inverse-propensity weighting". Reused by `regression_adjustment` (¬ß9g) and earmarked for the ATT/DTR sequential analogs.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `condExp_setIndicator_condExp_of_le` | `(hm : m ‚Č§ m') (hm' : m' ‚Č§ m0) [SigmaFinite (őľ.trim hm')] (hs : MeasurableSet[m'] s) (hf : Integrable f őľ) ‚Üí őľ[s.indicator f \| m] =ŠĶź őľ[s.indicator (őľ[f\|m']) \| m]` | **Masked outcome ‚ÜĒ inner regression.** Conditioning the masked `1_s¬∑f` on coarse `m` equals conditioning its inner regression `őľ[f\|m']` (with `s` `m'`-measurable). Proof: inner `condExp_indicator` + tower `condExp_condExp_of_le`. With `m=ŌÉ(X)`, `m'=ŌÉ(D,X)`, `s={D=d}`, dividing by `őľ[1_s\|m]` turns the LHS into the IPW/adjustment functional, the RHS into the regression. **Proved.** |

## 12. `Stat/` ‚ÄĒ Causal-agnostic statistical primitives

Foundation layer for the estimation and inference modules: i.i.d. samples, convergence
modes, asymptotic linearity, Neyman orthogonality, and one-shot sample
splits. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation.tex` Sections 1‚Äď5. Files
here are deliberately project-agnostic and are candidates for upstream
contribution to Mathlib.

**Folder layout.** The top level keeps only the foundational `Sample.lean` and
the `SampleSplit.lean` umbrella; everything else is grouped by topic into
subfolders: `Limit/`
(convergence modes, continuous mapping, WLLN), `CLT/` (asymptotic linearity,
multivariate CLT, Gaussian limit, Ōá¬≤), `Inference/` (őĒ-method, variance
estimation, studentization, Wald), `Quantile/` (cdf/quantile inference stack),
`MEstimation/` (Z/M-estimator CLT), `Orthogonality/` (Neyman orthogonality,
conditional-op helpers), `SampleSplit/` (cross-fitting + fold-restricted CLT),
`Minimax/` (Le Cam / Fano / Assouad lower bounds, ¬ß12w),
and `Concentration/` (vendored toolbox, ¬ß12'). Module paths below reflect this.

### 12np. `Stat/Nonparametric/Approximation/HolderInterpolation.lean` ‚ÄĒ Multivariate H√∂lder interpolation

This reusable minimax lower-bound primitive turns a pointwise separation between
H√∂lder-smooth functions into a local L¬Ļ separation. It supplies the generic
moment-cancelling product kernel and the resulting multidimensional interpolation
inequality used by two-point and Assouad arguments.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.Approximation.HolderInterpolation.Defs -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.Nonparametric.supBall` | `(Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí Set (Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | The closed coordinatewise neighbourhood of radius `r` around a point: the cube containing exactly the covariate values whose every coordinate is within `r` of the corresponding coordinate of the centre. |
| `Stat.Nonparametric.HolderBallStd` | `((Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí Set (Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | The standard multivariate H√∂lder ball of a given smoothness order and radius on a region: derivatives through the conventional highest order are continuous and bounded, and the highest derivative changes at the H√∂lder rate set by that order. |
| `Stat.Nonparametric.prodKernel` | `(‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (d : ‚Ąē) ‚Üí (Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The multivariate product kernel obtained by multiplying the same one-dimensional kernel across all covariate coordinates. |
<!-- /GEN -->

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.Approximation.HolderInterpolation.Kernel -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.Nonparametric.exists_moment_cancelling_kernel_1d` | `‚ąÉ k, Continuous k ‚ąß (‚ąÄ (u : ‚ĄĚ), 1 < \|u\| ‚Üí k u = 0) ‚ąß ‚ąę (u : ‚ĄĚ) in Set.Icc (-1) 1, k u = 1 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ (j : ‚Ąē), 1 ‚Č§ j ‚Üí j ‚Č§ m ‚Üí ‚ąę (u : ‚ĄĚ) in Set.Icc (-1) 1, u ^ j * k u = 0` | A compactly supported one-dimensional kernel can be chosen to have unit total mass while cancelling every polynomial moment through any prescribed finite order. |
| `Stat.Nonparametric.prodKernel_abs_le` | `(‚ąÄ (u : ‚ĄĚ), \|k u\| ‚Č§ B) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (u : Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ), \|Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.prodKernel k d u\| ‚Č§ B ^ d` | A product kernel is uniformly bounded by the coordinatewise bound raised to the dimension, whenever its one-dimensional factor has that bound. |
| `Stat.Nonparametric.prodKernel_integral` | `‚ąę (u : Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.prodKernel k d u = (‚ąę (t : ‚ĄĚ), k t) ^ d` | The total mass of a product kernel factorizes into the product of the one-dimensional masses, so a unit-mass factor yields a unit-mass multivariate kernel. |
| `Stat.Nonparametric.prodKernel_moment` | `‚ąę (u : Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ), (‚ąŹ i, u i ^ őĹ i) * Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.prodKernel k d u = ‚ąŹ i, ‚ąę (t : ‚ĄĚ), t ^ őĹ i * k t` | Each polynomial moment of a product kernel factorizes into the corresponding one-dimensional moments, allowing any cancelled coordinate moment to cancel the entire multivariate moment. |

| `Stat.Nonparametric.l1_lower_of_bias_bound` | `0 < ő≥ ‚Üí 0 < Ksup ‚Üí 0 < őĒ ‚Üí 0 < cstar ‚Üí h = cstar * őĒ ^ (1 / ő≥) ‚Üí 3 * őĒ / 4 ‚Č§ Ksup * h‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ^ d * Ival ‚Üí 3 / (4 * Ksup) * cstar ^ d * őĒ ^ (1 + ‚ÜĎd / ő≥) ‚Č§ Ival` | The bandwidth optimization converts a lower bound on a kernel-smoothed signal into the H√∂lder interpolation rate: local absolute mass grows at least as the pointwise signal to the power one plus dimension divided by smoothness. |
| `Stat.Nonparametric.holder_point_l1_interpolation` | `0 < ő≥ ‚Üí 0 < M ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.supBall x0 r ‚äÜ S ‚Üí ‚ąÉ cH, 0 < cH ‚ąß ‚ąÄ (g : (Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.HolderBallStd g ő≥ M S ‚Üí cH * \|g x0\| ^ (1 + ‚ÜĎd / ő≥) ‚Č§ ‚ąę (x : Fin d ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) in Causalean.Stat.Nonparametric.supBall x0 r, \|g x\|` | H√∂lder pointwise ‚üĻ local `L¬Ļ` mass interpolation. For `ő≥, M, r > 0` and a cube neighbourhood `supBall x0 r ‚äÜ S`, there is a constant `c_H > 0` (depending only on `ő≥, d, M, r`, uniform over the H√∂lder ball) such that for every `g` in the standard `‚Ćąő≥‚ĆČ-1`-convention H√∂lder ball `HolderBallStd g ő≥ M S`, `c_H ¬∑ \|g x0\|^{1 + d/ő≥} ‚Č§ ‚ąę_{supBall x0 r} \|g\|`. |

### 12a0. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/` ‚ÄĒ Product-loss monotone couplings

Coupling-based statistical primitives for extremal dependence with fixed real marginals.
This stack builds explicit comonotone and countermonotone quantile couplings, proves
the sharp Fr√©chet-Hoeffding cdf envelopes, derives Hoeffding's covariance identity,
and concludes that the product expectation is maximized and minimized by those two
monotone couplings under finite second moments.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling -->
_(no documented declarations in Causalean.Stat.Coupling)_
<!-- /GEN -->

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling -->
_(no documented declarations in Causalean.Stat.Coupling.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling)_
<!-- /GEN -->

| `Stat.IsCoupling` | `MeasureTheory.Measure (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí Prop` | `IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ` states that `ŌÄ` is a coupling of `őľ` and `őĹ`: it is a probability measure on `‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ` whose first marginal is `őľ` and whose second marginal is `őĹ`. This is membership in the Fr√©chet class `ő†(őľ, őĹ)`. |
| `Stat.comonotoneCoupling` | `MeasureTheory.Measure ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ)` | The comonotone (quantile) coupling of `őľ` and `őĹ`: the pushforward of the uniform measure on `(0,1)` under `u ‚Ü¶ (quantile őľ u, quantile őĹ u)`. Both coordinates are driven by the *same* uniform draw, giving the maximal positive dependence within `ő†(őľ, őĹ)`. |
| `Stat.countermonotoneCoupling` | `MeasureTheory.Measure ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ)` | The countermonotone coupling of `őľ` and `őĹ`: the pushforward of the uniform measure on `(0,1)` under `u ‚Ü¶ (quantile őľ u, quantile őĹ (1 - u))`. The reflection `u ‚Ü¶ 1 - u` on the second coordinate produces the maximal negative dependence within `ő†(őľ, őĹ)`. |
| `Stat.map_one_sub_unifOI` | `MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun u => 1 - u) Causalean.Stat.unifOI = Causalean.Stat.unifOI` | The reflection `u ‚Ü¶ 1 - u` preserves the uniform measure on `(0,1)`: `unifOI.map (fun u => 1 - u) = unifOI`. |
| `Stat.isCoupling_comonotoneCoupling` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling (Causalean.Stat.comonotoneCoupling őľ őĹ) őľ őĹ` | The comonotone coupling is a coupling of `(őľ, őĹ)`. Its marginals are computed by `Measure.map_map` composing with `Prod.fst`/`Prod.snd`, which reduces each to `unifOI.map (quantile ¬∑)`, equal to the corresponding measure by the probability integral transform `quantile_map_uniform`. |
| `Stat.isCoupling_countermonotoneCoupling` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling (Causalean.Stat.countermonotoneCoupling őľ őĹ) őľ őĹ` | The countermonotone coupling is a coupling of `(őľ, őĹ)`. The first marginal is `őľ` by the PIT; the second marginal is `unifOI.map (quantile őĹ ‚ąė (1 - ¬∑))`, which equals `őĹ` because `u ‚Ü¶ 1 - u` preserves `unifOI` (`map_one_sub_unifOI`) followed by the PIT for `őĹ`. |

| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.jointCdf` | `MeasureTheory.Measure (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The joint cdf of a measure `ŌÄ` on `‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ`: the mass of the lower-left quadrant `Iic x √óňĘ Iic y`, as a real number. |
| `Stat.frechet_hoeffding_upper` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (x y : ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.jointCdf ŌÄ x y ‚Č§ min (‚ÜĎ(ProbabilityTheory.cdf őľ) x) (‚ÜĎ(ProbabilityTheory.cdf őĹ) y)` | Fr√©chet‚ÄďHoeffding upper bound. For any coupling `ŌÄ` of `(őľ, őĹ)`, the joint cdf is bounded above by the smaller marginal cdf: `H_ŌÄ x y ‚Č§ min (cdf őľ x) (cdf őĹ y)`. Proof: `Iic x √óňĘ Iic y ‚äÜ Iic x √óňĘ univ` and `‚äÜ univ √óňĘ Iic y`, whose masses are `őľ (Iic x)` and `őĹ (Iic y)` via the marginals. |
| `Stat.frechet_hoeffding_lower` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (x y : ‚ĄĚ), max (‚ÜĎ(ProbabilityTheory.cdf őľ) x + ‚ÜĎ(ProbabilityTheory.cdf őĹ) y - 1) 0 ‚Č§ Causalean.Stat.jointCdf ŌÄ x y` | Fr√©chet‚ÄďHoeffding lower bound. For any coupling `ŌÄ` of `(őľ, őĹ)`, `max (cdf őľ x + cdf őĹ y - 1) 0 ‚Č§ H_ŌÄ x y`. Proof: inclusion‚Äďexclusion on the complement of `Iic x √óňĘ Iic y`, using `ŌÄ` a probability measure and the two marginals. |

|---|---|---|
| `Stat.jointCdf_comonotoneCoupling` | `Causalean.Stat.jointCdf (Causalean.Stat.comonotoneCoupling őľ őĹ) x y = min (‚ÜĎ(ProbabilityTheory.cdf őľ) x) (‚ÜĎ(ProbabilityTheory.cdf őĹ) y)` | The comonotone coupling attains the Fr√©chet‚ÄďHoeffding upper bound: `H = min (cdf őľ x) (cdf őĹ y)`. For `U ~ Unif(0,1)`, `{u \| quantile őľ u ‚Č§ x ‚ąß quantile őĹ u ‚Č§ y} = {u \| u ‚Č§ cdf őľ x} ‚ą© {u \| u ‚Č§ cdf őĹ y} = {u \| u ‚Č§ min (cdf őľ x) (cdf őĹ y)}`, of uniform mass `min (cdf őľ x) (cdf őĹ y)`. |
| `Stat.jointCdf_countermonotoneCoupling` | `Causalean.Stat.jointCdf (Causalean.Stat.countermonotoneCoupling őľ őĹ) x y = max (‚ÜĎ(ProbabilityTheory.cdf őľ) x + ‚ÜĎ(ProbabilityTheory.cdf őĹ) y - 1) 0` | The countermonotone coupling attains the Fr√©chet‚ÄďHoeffding lower bound: `H = max (cdf őľ x + cdf őĹ y - 1) 0`. For `U ~ Unif(0,1)`, `{u \| quantile őľ u ‚Č§ x ‚ąß quantile őĹ (1-u) ‚Č§ y} = {u \| u ‚Č§ cdf őľ x} ‚ą© {u \| 1 - cdf őĹ y ‚Č§ u}`, of uniform mass `max (cdf őľ x - (1 - cdf őĹ y)) 0 = max (cdf őľ x + cdf őĹ y - 1) 0`. |
| `Stat.jointCdf_le_comonotone` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí ‚ąÄ [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure őľ] [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure őĹ] (x y : ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.jointCdf ŌÄ x y ‚Č§ Causalean.Stat.jointCdf (Causalean.Stat.comonotoneCoupling őľ őĹ) x y` | Corollary: for any coupling `ŌÄ` of `(őľ, őĹ)`, its joint cdf is dominated pointwise by the comonotone joint cdf. This is the pointwise inequality that the covariance identity turns into optimality of `E[XY]`. |
| `Stat.countermonotone_le_jointCdf` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí ‚ąÄ [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure őľ] [MeasureTheory.IsProbabilityMeasure őĹ] (x y : ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.jointCdf (Causalean.Stat.countermonotoneCoupling őľ őĹ) x y ‚Č§ Causalean.Stat.jointCdf ŌÄ x y` | Corollary: the countermonotone joint cdf is dominated pointwise by any other coupling's joint cdf. |

| `Stat.signedTail` | `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | `signedTail a s = ūĚüô{s < a} - ūĚüô{s < 0}`, the *signed tail indicator* of `a`. As a function of `s` it is `+1` on `[0, a)` when `a ‚Č• 0`, `-1` on `[a, 0)` when `a < 0`, and `0` elsewhere; its Lebesgue integral is exactly `a`. |
| `Stat.tailInd_apply` | `Causalean.Stat.tailInd a s = if s < a then 1 else 0` | The tail indicator equals one below the threshold and zero at or above it. |
| `Stat.signedTail_eq_indicator_sub` | `Causalean.Stat.signedTail a = ((Set.Ico 0 a).indicator fun x => 1) - (Set.Ico a 0).indicator fun x => 1` | `signedTail a` is, as a function of `s`, the difference of the indicators of the two intervals `Ico 0 a` and `Ico a 0` (at most one of which is nonempty). | `Stat.measurable_signedTail` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.signedTail a)` | `signedTail a` is measurable in the tail variable `s`. |
| `Stat.measurable_signedTail_uncurry` | `Measurable fun z => Causalean.Stat.signedTail z.1 z.2` | `signedTail` is jointly measurable in `(a, s)`, since `{z \| z.2 < z.1}` is an open (hence measurable) subset of `‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ`. |
| `Stat.abs_signedTail_le_one` | `\|Causalean.Stat.signedTail a s\| ‚Č§ 1` | The signed tail indicator is bounded by `1` in absolute value. |
| `Stat.integrable_signedTail` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.signedTail a) MeasureTheory.volume` | `signedTail a` is Lebesgue integrable: it is a bounded function supported on a bounded interval. |
| `Stat.integral_signedTail` | `‚ąę (s : ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.signedTail a s = a` | Signed tail representation. `‚ąę s, (ūĚüô{s < a} - ūĚüô{s < 0}) ds = a`. |
| `Stat.integral_abs_signedTail` | `‚ąę (s : ‚ĄĚ), \|Causalean.Stat.signedTail a s\| = \|a\|` | The `L¬Ļ` norm of the signed tail indicator is `\|a\|`: the integrand is `¬Ī1` on an interval of length `\|a\|`. |
| `Stat.integrable_signedTail_prod` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun q => Causalean.Stat.signedTail x q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail y q.2) (MeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume)` | The tensor product `(s, t) ‚Ü¶ signedTail x s * signedTail y t` is integrable on `‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ` for Lebesgue√óLebesgue. |
| `Stat.integral_signedTail_prod` | `‚ąę (q : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.signedTail x q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail y q.2 ‚ąāMeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume = x * y` | Product tail representation. `x * y` is the two-dimensional integral of the product of the two signed tail indicators: `‚ąę‚ąę (ūĚüô{s<x} - ūĚüô{s<0})(ūĚüô{t<y} - ūĚüô{t<0}) ds dt = x * y`. |
| `Stat.integral_norm_signedTail_prod` | `‚ąę (q : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ), ‚ÄĖCausalean.Stat.signedTail x q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail y q.2‚ÄĖ ‚ąāMeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume = \|x\| * \|y\|` | The `L¬Ļ` norm of the product tail representation is `\|x\| * \|y\|`. This is the domination bound that makes the Fubini swap against an `L¬≤` coupling valid: its `ŌÄ`-integral is `E\|XY\| < ‚ąě` by Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz. |

| `Stat.integral_prod_eq_integral_fiber` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 őľ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 őĹ ‚Üí ‚ąę (p : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ), p.1 * p.2 ‚ąāŌÄ = ‚ąę (q : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ), ‚ąę (p : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.signedTail p.1 q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail p.2 q.2 ‚ąāŌÄ ‚ąāMeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume` | First swap. `E_ŌÄ[XY]` is the Lebesgue double integral of the fibre integrals: `‚ąę p, p.1 * p.2 ‚ąāŌÄ = ‚ąę q, (‚ąę p, ő¶ q p ‚ąāŌÄ) dq`. Immediate from `integral_signedTail_prod` (pointwise in `p`) followed by `MeasureTheory.integral_integral_swap` applied to `integrable_bigPhi`. |
| `Stat.mean_fst_tail` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 őľ ‚Üí ‚ąę (x : ‚ĄĚ), x ‚ąāőľ = ‚ąę (s : ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.survFst ŌÄ s - Causalean.Stat.tailInd 0 s` | Tail formula for the mean. `E[X] = ‚ąę s, (SX s - ūĚüô{s<0}) ds`, by the same swap on `volume ‚äó ŌÄ` using `integral_signedTail` pointwise. |
| `Stat.mean_snd_tail` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 őĹ ‚Üí ‚ąę (y : ‚ĄĚ), y ‚ąāőĹ = ‚ąę (t : ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.survSnd ŌÄ t - Causalean.Stat.tailInd 0 t` | Tail formula for the mean (second coordinate). |
| `Stat.integrable_survFst_sub` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 őľ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun s => Causalean.Stat.survFst ŌÄ s - Causalean.Stat.tailInd 0 s) MeasureTheory.volume` | The centred marginal survival function `s ‚Ü¶ SX s - ūĚüô{s<0}` is Lebesgue integrable (it is `E[signedTail X ¬∑]`, integrable by `Integrable.integral_prod_left` on `integrable_tail_fst_prod`). |
| `Stat.integrable_survSnd_sub` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 őĹ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun t => Causalean.Stat.survSnd ŌÄ t - Causalean.Stat.tailInd 0 t) MeasureTheory.volume` | The centred marginal survival function `t ‚Ü¶ SY t - ūĚüô{t<0}` is Lebesgue integrable. |
| `Stat.integrable_fiber` | `Causalean.Stat.IsCoupling ŌÄ őľ őĹ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => x) 2 őľ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun y => y) 2 őĹ ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun q => ‚ąę (p : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ), Causalean.Stat.signedTail p.1 q.1 * Causalean.Stat.signedTail p.2 q.2 ‚ąāŌÄ) (MeasureTheory.volume.prod MeasureTheory.volume)` | The fibre `q ‚Ü¶ ‚ąę p, ő¶ q p ‚ąāŌÄ` is integrable on `‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ`, by `Integrable.integral_prod_left` applied to `integrable_bigPhi`. |

|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample ő© X őľ P` | structure | I.i.d. sequence `Z : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí X` on a single ambient space, with mutual independence (`iIndepFun`), identical distribution (`IdentDistrib`), and law-match (`őľ.map (Z 0) = P`). Matches `def:est-iid-sample`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean` | `def` | `(1/n) ő£_{i<n} f (Z i ŌČ)` for a real-valued statistic `f`. |

Canonical product-space construction and finite-product transport for i.i.d.
samples.

### 12b. `Stat/Limit/Convergence.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_inProb`, `Tendsto_L2`, `Tendsto_dist` | `def`s | Aliases for convergence in probability / `L¬≤` / distribution (L√©vy‚ÄďProkhorov-style at the pushforward-measure level). |
| `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto` | `Xn ‚áí Q` and `a‚āô ‚Üí a‚āÄ` ‚áí `a‚āô¬∑Xn ‚áí (x ‚Ü¶ a‚āÄx)_#Q` | Project wrapper around `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto`; handles the pushforward-map bookkeeping for `Tendsto_dist`. **Proved** (depends on Mathlib stub). |
| `IsBigOp Xn rn őľ`, `IsLittleOp Xn rn őľ` | `def`s | `O_p(rn)` and `o_p(rn)` per `def:est-stoch-order`. |
| `IsLittleOp.mul_isBigOp` | `o_p(rn)¬∑O_p(sn) = o_p(rn¬∑sn)` | Requires `0 < rn n`, `0 < sn n` for all `n`. **Proved.** |
| `IsBigOp.add` | `O_p(rn) + O_p(rn) = O_p(rn)` | **Proved.** |
| `IsBigOp.mul_isLittleOp_one_isLittleOp` | Slutsky-style: `O_p(1)¬∑o_p(1) = o_p(1)` | **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.tightness` | `Xn ‚áí Q` ‚áí `Xn = O_p(1)` | Tightness of a single distributional limit on ‚ĄĚ: pick a tail set `{|x| ‚Č• N}` with small `Q`-mass via continuity from above on the antitone family `{|x| ‚Č• n}`, then portmanteau on the closed tail to bound `limsup őľ.map(Xn n) {|x| > N} ‚Č§ Q {|x| ‚Č• N}`. **Proved by Codex.** őĒ-method helper. |
| `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` | `Xn = O_p(1)` ‚ąß `a‚āô ‚Üí 0` ‚áí `a‚āô¬∑Xn = o_p(1)` | Degenerate Slutsky: bounds the bad event `{őĶ < |a‚āô Xn|}` by `{M < |Xn|}` once `|a‚āô| < őĶ/M`, using only the deterministic shrinkage `|a‚āô| ‚Üí 0`. **Proved by Codex.** őĒ-method helper. |

### 12b'. `Stat/Limit/ConvergenceVec.lean`

Vector analogue of `Convergence.lean`'s `Tendsto_dist` plus the Slutsky lemmas needed by the multivariate őĒ-method (`Stat/Inference/DeltaMethod.lean`) and the vector asymptotic-normality corollary (`Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearityVec.lean`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist_vec` | `def` | Vector convergence in distribution: `őľ.map (Xn n)` converges weakly to `Q` in `ProbabilityMeasure E` for `E` a pseudo-metric space with `OpensMeasurableSpace`. Direct port of scalar `Tendsto_dist`. |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one` | vector Slutsky absorption | `Xn ‚áí Q` and `‚ÄĖYn ‚ąí Xn‚ÄĖ = o_p(1)` ‚áí `Yn ‚áí Q`, on `[NormedAddCommGroup E] [BorelSpace E]`. **Proved by Codex** as a metric-space port of the scalar proof using the bounded-Lipschitz portmanteau characterization (`tendsto_iff_forall_lipschitz_integral_tendsto`); integrand `F : E ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` is split over `{‚ÄĖF (Yn) ‚ąí F (Xn)‚ÄĖ < L¬∑őĶ/2}` and its complement, with the complement controlled via Lipschitz pullback through `‚ÄĖYn ‚ąí Xn‚ÄĖ`. |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.congr_ae` | eventual a.e. congruence | Vector analogue of `Tendsto_dist.congr_ae`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` | continuous-map pushforward | `g : E ‚Üí F` continuous ‚áí `Tendsto_dist_vec Xn Q őľ` ‚áí `Tendsto_dist_vec (g ‚ąė Xn) (Q.map g) őľ`. **Proved by Codex** via `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_of_tendsto_of_continuous`. Used by the multivariate őĒ-method to push forward by the Fr√©chet derivative `Dg`. |

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gaussian_tail_small` | `0<v ‚Üí 0<őĶ ‚Üí ‚ąÉ R>0, N(0,v)((-‚ąě,-R]) ‚Č§ őĶ ‚ąß N(0,v)([R,‚ąě)) ‚Č§ őĶ` | Gaussian half-line tail control: both one-sided tails of `N(0,v)` go below any `őĶ` for a large cutoff (continuity-from-above on half-line families shrinking to `‚ąÖ`). Factored out of the quantile-Bahadur rate proof; reused by `PartialID/Inference/ImbensManski.lean` | **Proved** |

### 12c. `Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearity.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gaussianMeasure m ŌÉ¬≤` | `def` | Real-typed wrapper around `ProbabilityTheory.gaussianReal m ŌÉ¬≤.toNNReal`; negative variances clip to `0` (Dirac at `m`). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasureGaussianMeasure` | instance | `IsProbabilityMeasure (gaussianMeasure m v)` for every real `m, v`, inherited from Mathlib's `gaussianReal`. **Proved.** |
| `IsAsymLinear őłn őł‚āÄ Ōą S I` | structure | Asymptotic linearity per `def:est-asym-linear`: `mean_zero`, `finite_var` (encoded as `Integrable (Ōą¬≤)`), `remainder = o_p(1)` over an explicit index family `I` (classically `I n = {0, ..., n-1}`). |
| `IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S Ōą I n` | `def` | The normalised partial sum `(1/‚ąö|I n|) ő£_{i‚ąąI(n)} Ōą(Z_i)` along an i.i.d. sample and an index family `I`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator őłn őł‚āÄ I n` | `def` | The rescaled estimator `‚ąö|I n| (őłn n ‚ąí őł‚āÄ)` for index family `I`. |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalized_sum` | `(1/‚ąön) ő£ Ōą(ZŠĶĘ) ‚áí N(0, ‚ąęŌą¬≤ dP)` | CLT contact point: needs `Ōą` measurable, mean-zero, square-integrable. **Proved** via `ProbabilityTheory.central_limit` from the upstream `RemyDegenne/CLT` package, with variance normalisation and a zero-variance degenerate branch. |
| `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` | Slutsky absorption | If `Xn ‚áí Q` in distribution and `Yn ‚ąí Xn = o_p(1)`, then `Yn ‚áí Q`. **Proved** at the `Tendsto_dist` (measure-level) wrapper via the bounded-Lipschitz characterisation of weak convergence. |
| `Tendsto_dist.congr_ae` | eventual a.e. congruence | If `Xn ‚áí Q` and `Xn n =ŠĶź Yn n` eventually, then `Yn ‚áí Q`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto_gaussian` | deterministic scalar Slutsky for Gaussian targets | If `Xn ‚áí N(0, v)` and deterministic `a n ‚Üí a‚āÄ`, then `a n ¬∑ Xn ‚áí N(0, a‚āÄ¬≤v)`. **Proved** (depends on `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto`) by `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto` plus `gaussianReal_map_const_mul`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` | `‚ąö|I n|(őłn ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ‚ąęŌą¬≤ dP)` | Headline corollary `prop:est-al-implies-an`: combines `clt_normalized_sum` with `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` to absorb the asymptotically-linear remainder (classical corollary uses `I n = Finset.range n`). **Proved.** |

### 12c'. `Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearityVec.lean`

Vector-őł companion to `AsymptoticLinearity.lean`. Adds the vector-valued asymptotic-linearity predicate `IsAsymLinearVec` parametric in a Euclidean space `E`, plus a bridge to the scalar form when `E = ‚ĄĚ`, and the headline multivariate asymptotic-normality corollary.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsAsymLinearVec őłn őł‚āÄ Ōą S I` | structure | Vector-valued asymptotic linearity at `őł‚āÄ : E` with vector IF `Ōą : X ‚Üí E`. Fields: `mean_zero` (Bochner integral), `finite_var` (`‚ÄĖŌą‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚ąą L¬Ļ`), `remainder` (norm `o_p(1)`). |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.normalizedSum`, `rescaledEstimator` | `def`s | Vector versions of the scalar accessors. |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.toScalar` | bridge | When `E = ‚ĄĚ`, `IsAsymLinearVec` reduces to scalar `IsAsymLinear`. **Proved**: field-by-field rewrite using `‚ÄĖx‚ÄĖ = |x|` and `r ‚ÄĘ x = r * x` on `‚ĄĚ`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.toVec` | bridge | Scalar-to-vector lift. **Proved** (mirror of `toScalar`). |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec` | `theorem` | Multivariate analogue of `tendsto_normal`: given a vector CLT contact `_hCLT` (the partial-sum pushforward converges to `Q : ProbabilityMeasure E`), the rescaled estimator pushforward also converges to `Q`. **Proved by Codex** by absorbing `_h.remainder` into `_hCLT` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one`. The multivariate CLT itself is left to the caller. |

### 12d. `Stat/Orthogonality/Orthogonality.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NeymanOrthogonal m ő∑‚āÄ őł‚āÄ P` | `def` | Moment condition `‚ąę m ő∑‚āÄ x őł‚āÄ dP = 0` plus vanishing G√Ęteaux derivative along every direction `ő∑ ‚ąą H`. Restricted realization sets are imposed at instantiation (cf. `H_őĶ` in `Estimation/ATE/InfluenceFunction.lean`). |

### 12e. `Stat/SampleSplit.lean` (umbrella)

Re-export umbrella over per-strategy split modules:
- `Stat/SampleSplit/OneShot.lean` ‚ÄĒ `OneShotSplit` (the original API, unchanged).
- `Stat/SampleSplit/KFold.lean` ‚ÄĒ `KFoldSplit` (K-fold cross-fitting).

Existing consumers that import `Causalean.Stat.SampleSplit` continue to work.

#### 12e.OneShot

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OneShotSplit S` | structure | Fold-size schedule `n‚āĀ : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē` with `bound`, `grow`, `cogrow` per `def:est-one-shot-split`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldA n` | `def` | `{0, ‚Ä¶, n‚āĀ n ‚ąí 1}`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB n` | `def` | `{n‚āĀ n, ‚Ä¶, n ‚ąí 1}` (filtered range). |
| `OneShotSplit.foldA_disjoint_foldB` | `Disjoint (foldA n) (foldB n)` | Pure combinatorics. **Proved.** |
| `OneShotSplit.folds_indep` | `IndepFun (Z\|foldA) (Z\|foldB) őľ` | Direct corollary of `iIndepFun.indepFun_finset` plus disjointness. **Proved.** |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_card` | `(foldB n).card = n - n‚āĀ n` | Cardinality of the suffix-shaped fold-B (filter on `range n`). **Proved.** Used to derive eventual positivity of `(foldB n).card` from `cogrow`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_card_tendsto` | `Tendsto (fun n => (foldB n).card) atTop atTop` | Direct corollary of `cogrow` + `foldB_card`. **Proved.** |

#### 12e.2. `Stat/SampleSplit/KFold.lean`

K-fold sample split (cross-fitting). Sibling to `OneShot.lean`. Used by `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `KFoldSplit S K` | structure | K disjoint folds with `partition`, `cover`, `grow`, `ratio` (`fold(n,k).card/n ‚Üí 1/K`). |
| `KFoldSplit.trainComplement n k` | `def` | `{0,‚Ä¶,n-1} \ fold(n,k)`. |
| `KFoldSplit.fold_disjoint_trainComplement` | `lemma` | Disjointness via `Finset.mem_sdiff`. **Proved.** |
| `KFoldSplit.folds_indep` | `theorem` | `(Z i)_{i ‚ąą fold(n,k)}` independent of `(Z i)_{i ‚ąą trainComplement(n,k)}` under `őľ`. **Proved** (one-line `S.indep.indepFun_finset` mirroring `OneShotSplit.folds_indep`). |

### 12f. `Stat/Orthogonality/ConditionalOp.lean`

Envelope-based stochastic-order helpers + Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz packaging. Consumed by `PlugIn.lean` (single-factor bias) and `Remainder.lean` (product-rate AIPW bias). Causal-agnostic; candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution once stable.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsBigOp.of_sqEnvelope` | `(Xn n)¬≤ ‚Č§ cn n a.s.` ‚ąß `cn = O_p(rn¬≤)` ‚áí `Xn = O_p(rn)` | Square-envelope ‚áí `O_p`, using a.e. event inclusion and limsup monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `IsLittleOp.of_sqEnvelope` | `(Xn n)¬≤ ‚Č§ cn n a.s.` ‚ąß `cn = o_p(rn¬≤)` ‚áí `Xn = o_p(rn)` | Square-envelope ‚áí `o_p`, using a.e. event inclusion and convergence-to-zero monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `abs_integral_le_eLpNorm_two` | `IsProbabilityMeasure őĹ` ‚áí `\|‚ąę f dőĹ\| ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(őĹ)}` | Constant-case Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz via the L¬Ļ/L¬≤ comparison on probability spaces. **Proved.** |
| `integral_abs_mul_le_eLpNorm_mul_eLpNorm` | `‚ąę \|f¬∑g\| dőĹ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ‚āā ¬∑ ‚ÄĖg‚ÄĖ‚āā` | Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz for L¬≤¬∑L¬≤ via Mathlib H√∂lder. **Proved.** |
| `integral_op_of_eLpNorm_op` | `eLpNorm (fn) 2 őĹ = o_p(rn)` ‚áí `‚ąę fn dőĹ = o_p(rn)` | Lifts L¬≤-rate to integrated bias by pointwise domination. **Proved.** |
| `integral_abs_mul_op_of_eLpNorm_op` | `eLpNorm (fn) 2 őĹ = o_p(sn)` ‚ąß fixed `g ‚ąą L¬≤` ‚áí `‚ąę \|fn¬∑g\| dőĹ = o_p(sn ¬∑ ‚ÄĖg‚ÄĖ‚āā)` | Lifts L¬≤-rate to integrated absolute product, splitting the zero/nonzero fixed-norm cases. **Proved.** |

### 12g. `Stat/SampleSplit/PartialFoldCLT.lean`

Fold-restricted CLT for one-shot sample splits. Provides the CLT contact and asymptotic-normality corollary at rate `‚ąö|B(n)|` for split estimators (PlugIn / DML), plus a ‚ąön-rate conversion under a fixed split ratio `|B(n)|/n ‚Üí c ‚ąą (0,1)` with the standard variance inflation `ŌÉ¬≤/c`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedFoldB` | `(1/‚ąö\|B(n)\|) ő£_{i‚ąąB(n)} Ōą(Z_i) ‚áí N(0, ‚ąęŌą¬≤dP)` | Fold-B CLT. **Proved** by reindexing fold-B through a finite-set characteristic-function bridge and composing the existing `IIDSample.clt_normalized_sum` CLT wrapper along `\|B(n)\| ‚Üí ‚ąě`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB` | AL on fold B ‚áí `‚ąö\|B(n)\|¬∑(őłn ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ŌÉ¬≤)` | Mirror of `tendsto_normal` for `I = split.foldB`. **Proved by Codex** (4-line composition of `clt_normalizedFoldB` + `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one`). |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB_sqrt_n` | `\|B(n)\|/n ‚Üí c ‚ąą (0,1)` ‚ąß AL on fold B ‚áí `‚ąön¬∑(őłn ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ŌÉ¬≤/c)` | ‚ąön-rate conversion under fixed split ratio. **Proved** (depends on the Mathlib varying-map stub); handles fold-B measurability, deterministic `‚ąön/‚ąö\|B(n)\| ‚Üí 1/‚ąöc`, eventual a.e. equality, and variance algebra. |

### 12h. `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.lean`

Empirical-process bounds on a centered evaluation-fold sum of a training-fold-measurable random function. Consumed by `DMLChernozhukov.lean` (one-shot) and `DMLCrossFit.lean` (K-fold). The file is structured in three layers:

1. **Abstract layer (private)** ‚ÄĒ parameterised over an arbitrary evaluation-fold schedule `eval : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Finset ‚Ąē`, training ŌÉ-algebra `m_train : ‚Ąē ‚Üí MeasurableSpace ő©`, plus independence and iid-product-law hypotheses.
2. **OneShot wrappers** ‚ÄĒ preserved public signatures `foldB_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` and `sqrtFoldB_integral_isLittleOp_one`, now thin instantiations of the abstract layer.
3. **K-fold corollaries** ‚ÄĒ new public lemmas under `KFoldSplit.*` namespace.

#### Abstract layer (private)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `centeredEvalSum_sq_lintegral_le_core` / `centeredEvalSum_sq_lintegral_le` | `‚ąę‚ĀĽ ŌČ, ((1/‚ąö\|eval n\|) ő£_{eval n} (g(Z_i) ‚ąí ‚ąęg dP))¬≤ ‚ąāőľ ‚Č§ ‚ąę‚ĀĽ ŌČ, ‚ÄĖg(n,ŌČ)‚ÄĖ¬≤_{L¬≤(P)} ‚ąāőľ` | Second-moment bound on the centered eval-sum, parametric in `(eval, m_train, h_train_le, h_indep, h_iid)`. **Proved** via the generic `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` helper in `Causalean/Mathlib/IIDCenteredSum.lean`. |
| `evalSum_truncated_isLittleOp_one` | bounded `g` with `‚ąę‚ĀĽ ‚ÄĖg‚ÄĖ¬≤‚āā ‚Üí 0` ‚áí centered eval-sum is `o_p(1)` | Markov / Chebyshev in `ENNReal` plus bounded convergence. **Proved by Codex (A0)** via `MeasureTheory.meas_ge_le_lintegral_div`. Eventual `0 < (eval n).card` from a separate `h_eval_card_grow` hypothesis. |
| `evalSum_isLittleOp_one` | `‚ÄĖg(n,ŌČ)‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P)} = o_p(1)` (random-norm rate) ‚áí centered eval-sum is `o_p(1)` | Truncation at L¬≤-norm ‚Č§ 1, then `evalSum_truncated_isLittleOp_one` + `IsLittleOp.of_eq_on_asymptotic`. **Proved.** |
| `evalSum_bias_isLittleOp_one` | `(eval n).card / n ‚Üí c ‚ąą (0,1)` ‚ąß `‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` ‚áí `‚ąö\|eval n\|¬∑‚ąęf dP = o_p(1)` | Bias term, fully parametric. **Proved.** |

#### OneShot wrappers (public; signatures preserved for backward compat)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `foldB_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` | fold-A-measurable `f` with `‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P)} = o_p(1)` ‚áí `(1/‚ąö\|B(n)\|) ő£_{i ‚ąą B(n)} (f(n,ŌČ,Z_i ŌČ) ‚ąí ‚ąęf dP) = o_p(1)` | Thin wrapper around the abstract layer, instantiating `eval n := split.foldB n`, `m_train n := comap (foldA n) Z`, `h_indep := split.folds_indep n` (definitional `IndepFun`-to-`Indep` bridge), `h_iid := S.indep.precomp + IdentDistrib.map_eq + S.law`, `h_eval_card_grow := split.foldB_card_tendsto`. **Proved.** |
| `sqrtFoldB_integral_isLittleOp_one` | `\|B(n)\|/n ‚Üí c ‚ąą (0,1)` ‚ąß `‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` ‚áí `‚ąö\|B(n)\|¬∑‚ąęf dP = o_p(1)` | OneShot bias wrapper. **Proved.** |

#### KFold corollaries (public; new)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `KFoldSplit.fold_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` | per `k : Fin K`, training-complement-measurable `f` with `‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P)} = o_p(1)` ‚áí `(1/‚ąö\|fold n k\|) ő£_{fold n k} (f(Z_i) ‚ąí ‚ąęf dP) = o_p(1)` | K-fold per-evaluation-fold centered sum. Instantiates the abstract layer with `eval n := split.fold n k`, `m_train n := comap (trainComplement n k) Z`, `h_indep` via `split.folds_indep n k` (with `.symm` since the K-fold version is stated `IndepFun fold trainComplement`), `h_eval_card_grow := split.grow k`. **Proved.** |
| `KFoldSplit.sqrtFold_integral_isLittleOp_one` | `1 < K` ‚ąß `‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` ‚áí per `k`, `‚ąö\|fold n k\|¬∑‚ąęf dP = o_p(1)` | K-fold per-fold bias. Uses `c := (K:‚ĄĚ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` ‚ąą (0, 1) (whence the `1 < K` requirement) and `split.ratio k`. **Proved.** |

### 12i. `Stat/MEstimation/InfluenceFunction.lean`

Light data structure packaging the three properties an influence function must satisfy: measurability, mean-zero, and finite second moment. Mirrors `def:sp-pathwise-diff` from `doc/basic_concepts/Semi-parametric Inference/semi_parametric_inference.tex` at the *assumption* level ‚ÄĒ pathwise differentiability is deferred.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `InfluenceFunction P Ōą` | `Prop`-valued structure | An influence function for parameter `őł : E` under law `P : Measure X` is a measurable, mean-zero, square-integrable function `Ōą : X ‚Üí E`. Fields: `measurable`, `mean_zero`, `finite_var`. |

### 12j. `Stat/Inference/DeltaMethod.lean`

Smooth-function CLT. Two forms; both proved.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `deltaMethod_scalar` | `theorem` | If `‚ąön (T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ŌÉ¬≤)` and `g : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` differentiable at `t‚āÄ` with derivative `g'`, then `‚ąön (g T_n ‚ąí g t‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, g'¬≤ ŌÉ¬≤)`. **Proved by Codex.** Pipeline: tightness of `‚ąön (T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ)` via `Tendsto_dist.tightness`; consistency `T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ ‚Üí_p 0` via `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` against `(‚ąön)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚Üí 0`; remainder `Rn := ‚ąön ¬∑ (g(T_n) ‚ąí g t‚āÄ ‚ąí g' (T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ))` is `o_p(1)` from `HasDerivAt.isLittleO_sub` on the localizing event `{|T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ| < ŌĀ}`; finally `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto_gaussian` for `g' ¬∑ ‚ąön (T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ)` and `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` to absorb `Rn`. |
| `deltaMethod` | `theorem` | Multivariate form: `‚ąön ‚ÄĘ (T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ) ‚áí Q` (vector limit) and `g : E ‚Üí F` Fr√©chet-differentiable at `t‚āÄ` with derivative `Dg : E ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] F` ‚áí `‚ąön ‚ÄĘ (g T_n ‚ąí g t‚āÄ) ‚áí Q.map Dg`. **Proved by Codex.** Vector port of the scalar pipeline: `‚ÄĖ‚ąön ‚ÄĘ (T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ` is `O_p(1)` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` along `‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ÄĖ : E ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` plus `Tendsto_dist.tightness`; consistency `T_n ‚ąí t‚āÄ ‚Üí_p 0` in norm; remainder controlled by `HasFDerivAt.isLittleO_sub`; linear pushforward `Dg` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` again; vector Slutsky absorption via `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one`. |

### 12j'. `Stat/Inference/HadamardDeriv.lean` + `Stat/Inference/FunctionalDelta.lean`

**Directional (Hadamard) functional delta method.** The Fr√©chet delta method (12j) cannot reach lattice functionals: an interval-identified parameter's sharp endpoints are `sup'`/`inf'` of separately estimable quantities (`PartialID/.../IntersectionBound.lean` `iInter_Icc_eq_Icc : ‚čā‚Īľ [L‚Īľ,U‚Īľ] = [sup' L, inf' U]`; Manski MIV envelopes; combined MTR+MTS bounds), and `max`/`min` are only *Hadamard directionally* differentiable ‚ÄĒ with a **nonlinear** derivative at a tie, which is exactly the binding/kink regime where bound inference is delicate (Hirano‚ÄďPorter 2012; Fang‚ÄďSantos 2019). References: van der Vaart (1998) ¬ß20.2; Shapiro (1991).

`HadamardDeriv.lean` (analysis core, no probability):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasHadamardDirDerivAt ŌÜ ŌÜ' őł` | def | Sequential Hadamard directional differentiability over normed spaces: for every `h`, every `h‚āô ‚Üí h`, every `t‚āô ‚Üí 0‚Āļ`, `(t‚āô)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ÄĘ (ŌÜ(őł + t‚āô‚ÄĘh‚āô) ‚ąí ŌÜőł) ‚Üí ŌÜ' h`. `ŌÜ'` is positively homogeneous, *not* assumed linear. |
| `HasFDerivAt.hasHadamardDirDerivAt` | theorem | **Fr√©chet ‚áí Hadamard**: a Fr√©chet-differentiable `ŌÜ` (derivative `L`) is Hadamard directionally differentiable with derivative `L`. *(One `sorry`: the `(t‚āô)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ÄĘ r‚āô ‚Üí 0` remainder step ‚ÄĒ standard `IsLittleO` divide-by-rate, off the critical path; the `max`/`min` results below do not use it.)* |
| `maxDirDeriv a b` / `minDirDeriv a b` | def | Directional derivative of `max`/`min : ‚ĄĚ√ó‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` at `(a,b)`: a coordinate projection off the diagonal, and `max`/`min` itself (sublinear) at the tie `a=b`. |
| `continuous_maxDirDeriv` / `continuous_minDirDeriv` | theorem | The directional derivatives are continuous. |
| `hasHadamardDirDerivAt_max` / `hasHadamardDirDerivAt_min` | theorem | `max`/`min` are Hadamard directionally differentiable at every `(a,b)` with the derivatives above. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Tie case: `max(b+t h‚āĀ, b+t h‚āā) = b + t¬∑max(h‚āĀ,h‚āā)`; off-diagonal: the dominating coordinate is eventually selected. |

`FunctionalDelta.lean` (the delta theorem; consumes the existing `Tendsto_dist_vec` weak-convergence infra):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `isLittleOp_one_of_measure_ne_tendsto_zero` | theorem | Reusable Slutsky device: if `őľ{Y‚āô ‚Č† X‚āô} ‚Üí 0` then `Y‚āô ‚ąí X‚āô = o_p(1)`. The ingredient for the *off-diagonal* case (lattice functional selects one coordinate except when the estimated ordering is wrong, an event of vanishing probability). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasure_map_max` / `_min` | instance | The lattice pushforward `Q.map (z ‚Ü¶ max/min z.1 z.2)` of a probability measure `Q` is a probability measure. Global instance, so the `Tendsto_dist` conclusions below need *not* assume it. |
| `sqrt_mul_max_sub` / `sqrt_mul_min_sub` | lemma | **The exact tie identity** `‚ąön¬∑(max/min √Ę‚āô bŐā‚āô ‚ąí a) = max/min(‚ąön(√Ę‚āô‚ąía), ‚ąön(bŐā‚āô‚ąía))` ‚ÄĒ the rescaled image equals the lattice functional applied to the joint rescaled deviation, with no remainder. |
| `maxStat_aemeasurable` / `minStat_aemeasurable` | lemma | The image statistic `‚ąön¬∑(max/min √Ę‚āô bŐā‚āô ‚ąí a)` is `AEMeasurable` for every `n`, *derived* from the joint deviation's measurability via the tie identity ‚ÄĒ so the delta theorems need *not* assume it. |
| `deltaMethod_max_tie` | theorem | **Directional delta method for `max` at a tie.** If `‚ąön ‚ÄĘ ((√Ę‚āô,bŐā‚āô) ‚ąí (a,a)) ‚áí Q` on `‚ĄĚ√ó‚ĄĚ`, then `‚ąön¬∑(max √Ę‚āô bŐā‚āô ‚ąí a) ‚áí Q.map (z ‚Ü¶ max z.1 z.2)` (generally *non-Gaussian* ‚ÄĒ the law of `max(Z‚āĀ,Z‚āā)`). The binding/kink case the Fr√©chet method cannot reach. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Proof: the exact identity `‚ąön(max √Ę‚āô bŐā‚āô ‚ąí a) = max(‚ąön(√Ę‚āô‚ąía), ‚ąön(bŐā‚āô‚ąía)) = max‚ąėS‚āô` (`sqrt_mul_max_sub`), then continuous mapping (`Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous`) + `congr_ae`; no linearization remainder. Hypotheses: only the joint-deviation measurability `hSn_meas` and the joint CLT `hCLT` ‚ÄĒ the pushforward-probability instance and the image-statistic measurability are now discharged automatically (via the instance / `maxStat_aemeasurable`). |
| `deltaMethod_min_tie` | theorem | Companion for `min`. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Same discharged-assumption shape. |

*Off-diagonal (`a ‚Č† b`) and the abstract `sup'`/`inf'`-over-finite-families forms are the natural follow-ups; the tie case is the novel/hard one and the reusable `isLittleOp_one_of_measure_ne_tendsto_zero` is the device for the off-diagonal reduction.*

### 12k. `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimator.lean`

Parametric-inference regularity bundle (structure layer). Houses only `ZEstimatorRegularity`; the headline theorem `zEstimator_clt` lives downstream in `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimatorCLT.lean` to keep the import DAG acyclic (the proof needs `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`, which itself imports this file for the structure).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ZEstimatorRegularity Ōą őł‚āÄ P` | structure | Identification (`‚ąę Ōą(¬∑;őł‚āÄ) dP = 0`), Jacobian `J‚āÄ : E ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] E` + invertibility witness, Fr√©chet-derivative spec, finite var, measurability, plus three smoothness fields: `jacobian_continuity` (`ContinuousAt` of `őł ‚Ü¶ ‚ąę Ōą(¬∑;őł) dP` at `őł‚āÄ`; redundant with `J‚āÄ_spec` but exposed for downstream convenience), `psi_int_neighborhood` (`Ōą(¬∑;őł)` is `P`-integrable on a neighborhood of `őł‚āÄ`), and `score_envelope` (integrable `L¬≤` derivative envelope: ‚ąÉ `őī>0`, ‚ąÉ nonneg measurable `F : X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with `F¬≤‚ąąL¬Ļ(P)` such that `‚ÄĖŌąőłz‚ąíŌąőł‚āÄz‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖőł‚ąíőł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬∑F z` for all `z : X` and all `‚ÄĖőł‚ąíőł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ<őī` (pointwise ‚ÄĒ produced by MVT on smooth scores; needed to transfer the bound to random sample-dependent `őłn`) ‚ÄĒ this is the LaTeX-spec form of `def:par-smoothness` and is what discharges `score_diff_L2_isLittleOp_sqrt` via Markov + iid LLN on `F¬≤`). Reference: `def:par-smoothness` (van der Vaart 1998 ¬ß5.6). |

### 12l. `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`

Empirical-process / linearization layer feeding `zEstimator_clt`. References: `def:par-smoothness`, `thm:par-z-clt` in `doc/basic_concepts/Semi-parametric Inference/parametric_inference.tex`; van der Vaart (1998) ¬ß5.6 Thm 5.41; Newey-McFadden (1994) ¬ß7.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `StochEquicontAt Ōą őł‚āÄ P őľ S őłn` | `def` | Asymptotic-equicontinuity predicate at `őł‚āÄ` along `őłn`: `‚ąÄ őĶ>0, ‚ąÉ őī>0` such that the empirical-process gap `(‚ąön)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ő£ (Ōą(őłn,Z_i)‚ąíŌą(őł‚āÄ,Z_i)) ‚ąí ‚ąön ‚ąę (Ōą(őłn,¬∑)‚ąíŌą(őł‚āÄ,¬∑)) dP` exceeding `őĶ` on `{‚ÄĖőłn‚ąíőł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ<őī}` has vanishing probability as `n‚Üí‚ąě`. Standard Donsker-class hypothesis (van der Vaart 1998 Lemma 19.24 / ¬ß19.4); exposed as a hypothesis pending Mathlib's empirical-process layer. |
| `score_diff_L2_isLittleOp_sqrt` | `theorem` | Under `ZEstimatorRegularity` (in particular `score_envelope`) + consistency `őłn ‚Üí_p őł‚āÄ`, the sample mean square `(1/n) ő£_{i<n} ‚ÄĖŌą(őłn,Z_i) ‚ąí Ōą(őł‚āÄ,Z_i)‚ÄĖ¬≤` is `o_p(1)`. Proof: dominated by `‚ÄĖőłn‚ąíőł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤ ¬∑ (1/n) ő£ F(Z_i)¬≤` on the `őī`-good event (pointwise envelope bound), where the second factor satisfies a Markov bound `őľ{Mn>M} ‚Č§ K/M` with `K := ‚ąęF¬≤ dP` (using `IIDSample.identDist + S.law` to push `‚ąęŌČ F(S.Z i ŌČ)¬≤ dőľ = ‚ąęz F z¬≤ dP`); the `őī`-bad event has probability ‚Üí 0 by `hConsistent (ŌĄ/2)`. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalScoreDiff_isLittleOp_sqrt` | `theorem` | Centered empirical-process control: under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, and `hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt Ōą őł‚āÄ P őľ S őłn`, `‚ÄĖ(1/‚ąön) ő£ (Ōą(őłn,Z_i) ‚ąí Ōą(őł‚āÄ,Z_i)) ‚ąí ‚ąön ¬∑ ‚ąę (Ōą(őłn,¬∑) ‚ąí Ōą(őł‚āÄ,¬∑)) dP‚ÄĖ = o_p(1)`. (Subtracting `‚ąön ¬∑ ‚ąę` is the iid bias correction.) Proof: combine `hStochEquicont(őĶ)` with `hConsistent(őī/2)` to remove the conditioning on `{‚ÄĖőłn‚ąíőł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ<őī}` via measure-union bound. **Proved.** |
| `populationScoreDiff_eq_jacobian_plus_remainder` | `theorem` | Population Fr√©chet expansion: `‚ąę (Ōą(őł,¬∑) ‚ąí Ōą(őł‚āÄ,¬∑)) dP ‚ąí J‚āÄ (őł ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) =o[ūĚďĚ őł‚āÄ] ‚ÄĖőł ‚ąí őł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ`. Rephrasing of `J‚āÄ_spec` as `Asymptotics.IsLittleO`. **Proved.** |
| `localStochasticExpansion` | `theorem` | Headline expansion: under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, `hStochEquicont`, and the rate `hRate : ‚ÄĖőłn ‚ąí őł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ = O_p(1/‚ąön)`, the norm `‚ÄĖ(1/‚ąön) ő£ (Ōą(őłn,Z_i) ‚ąí Ōą(őł‚āÄ,Z_i)) ‚ąí ‚ąön ¬∑ J‚āÄ (őłn ‚ąí őł‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ` is `o_p(1)`. Combines (b)+(c)+`hRate` via triangle. Plugged directly into `zEstimator_clt`. **Proved.** |

### 12l'. `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimatorCLT.lean`

Headline theorem layer for the parametric-inference workhorse. Z- / M-estimator CLT for vector-valued estimating equations. OLS, MLE, IV-2SLS instantiate it. Imports `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `zEstimator_clt` | `theorem` | Under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, asymptotic equicontinuity (`hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt Ōą őł‚āÄ P őľ S őłn`), the rate hypothesis `hRate : ‚ÄĖőłn ‚ąí őł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ = O_p(1/‚ąön)`, and empirical-moment vanishing, `őłn` is asymptotically linear at `őł‚āÄ` with IF `‚ąíJ‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ Ōą(¬∑; őł‚āÄ)` (Chernozhukov form). Conclusion is `IsAsymLinearVec`. Reference: van der Vaart 1998 Thm 5.41. **Proved.** All three `IsAsymLinearVec` fields discharged: `mean_zero` (push `‚ąíJ‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` through Bochner integral via `identification`), `finite_var` (operator-norm bound on `J‚āÄ_inv` + `finite_var`), and `remainder` (combine `localStochasticExpansion` + `hMoment`, then bound the rescaled remainder by `J‚āÄ_inv` and the finite-dim left-inverse derived from `J‚āÄ_inverse`). The Donsker-step content is encapsulated in the `hStochEquicont` hypothesis, to be discharged per-instance until Mathlib's empirical-process layer lands. |

### 12l''. `Stat/MEstimation/ExtremumConsistency.lean` ‚ÄĒ discharging the CLT's consistency and equicontinuity hypotheses

Additive bridge: re-exposes the headline CLTs (`zEstimator_clt`, `gmm_asymptotically_linear`) with their two *opaque* hypotheses derived from clean primitives. **Consistency** `hConsistent` is derived from the primitive extremum conditions via the empirical-process layer's `mEstimator_consistent_of_glivenkoCantelli` (¬ß12ep.c) ‚ÄĒ classical reduction (Newey‚ÄďMcFadden 1994 Thm 2.1; van der Vaart 1998 Thm 5.7): consistency follows from a Glivenko‚ÄďCantelli criterion plus a well-separated population maximum. **Equicontinuity** `hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt` is derived from the *class-level* Donsker property `AsymptoticEquicont` (¬ß12ep.d) via `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` ‚ÄĒ the estimator-indexed modulus is reconstructed from the function-class property plus consistency. The original `hConsistent`/`hStochEquicont`-taking theorems are unchanged and remain available for callers who establish those by other means. Imports `Stat/GMM/AsymptoticNormality.lean` + `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/MEstimatorConsistency.lean` + `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Equicontinuity/Modulus.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `consistent_lt_norm_of_le_dist` | `(‚ąÄ őĶ>0, őľ{őĶ ‚Č§ dist (őłn n) őł‚āÄ} ‚Üí 0) ‚áí (‚ąÄ őĶ>0, őľ{őĶ < ‚ÄĖőłn n ‚ąí őł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ} ‚Üí 0)` | **Format bridge** between the consistency conclusion of the empirical-process layer (`‚Č§ dist`) and the form the CLT layer consumes (`< ‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ÄĖ`); on a normed group `dist = ‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ąí¬∑‚ÄĖ` and `{őĶ<‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ÄĖ} ‚äÜ {őĶ‚Č§dist}`, so the measures squeeze to 0. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Z-estimator CLT from extremum primitives.** Replaces `hConsistent` by `(m, hGC : GlivenkoCantelli S m, hArgmax : őłn maximizes P‚āô m over őł‚āÄ, hSep : well-separated population max at őł‚āÄ)`; consistency is derived and fed to `zEstimator_clt`. The Donsker modulus `hStochEquicont`, rate `hRate`, and score FOC `hMoment` are unchanged. Typically `Ōą = ‚ąām/‚ąāőł`, so one estimator satisfies both `hMoment` and `hArgmax`; `m`/`Ōą` kept independent to avoid differentiability bookkeeping. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_extremum` | same `IsAsymLinearVec őłn őł‚āÄ prob.influence ‚Ä¶` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue.** Discharges `hConsistent` from a Glivenko‚ÄďCantelli GMM criterion `m` (classically `m őł = ‚ąíŠł°‚āô(őł)ŠĶÄ W Šł°‚āô(őł)`) with well-separated population max of which `őłn` is a sample maximiser. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Z-estimator CLT with the equicontinuity hypothesis discharged.** Replaces `hStochEquicont` by the class-level `hAEC : AsymptoticEquicont Ōą őł‚āÄ P őľ S` (¬ß12ep.d); `StochEquicontAt` is reconstructed via `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` from `hAEC` + `hConsistent`. Keeps `hConsistent`, `hRate`, `hMoment`. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Both opaque hypotheses discharged.** Assumes neither `hConsistent` nor `hStochEquicont`: takes the extremum package `(m, hGC, hArgmax, hSep)` and the Donsker property `hAEC`. The single derived consistency conclusion is threaded into *both* the linearisation and the `StochEquicontAt` reduction. Fully primitive Z-estimator CLT. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec őłn őł‚āÄ prob.influence ‚Ä¶` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue of `zEstimator_clt_of_donsker`.** `hStochEquicont` replaced by the class-level `hAEC : AsymptoticEquicont prob.score prob.őł‚āÄ P őľ S`. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_extremum_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec őłn őł‚āÄ prob.influence ‚Ä¶` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue of `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum_donsker`.** Neither `hConsistent` nor `hStochEquicont` assumed: consistency from the GC GMM criterion, equicontinuity from `hAEC`. Fully primitive GMM asymptotic-linearity theorem. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |

### 12l'''. Finite Poisson M-estimation substrate

Finite-dimensional Poisson projection tools: a total maximizer selector,
existence and uniqueness under positive weights and an injective linear design,
deterministic argmax stability and finite-table consistency, continuity under
mean perturbations, a one-cell implicit derivative, and the sign of a selected
scalar coefficient from a conditional nuisance fit.

This is the elementary tail estimate used in the finite Poisson maximizer existence argument. |
