### 12m. `Stat/Limit/ContinuousMapping.lean`

Estimator-agnostic convergence-in-probability primitives. Extracted from the (formerly `private`) helpers in `Estimation/NPIV/DR/AsymptoticNormal.lean` so any estimator can reuse them. Consumed by `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_inProb.comp_continuousAt` | `Xn ‚Üí_p c` ‚ąß `ContinuousAt g c` ‚áí `g‚ąėXn ‚Üí_p g c` | Continuous-mapping theorem in probability at a **constant** limit `c`. őĶ-őī pullback of `ContinuousAt` through `tendstoInMeasure_iff_dist`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.pi_comp_continuousAt` | finite `őĻ`, coordinatewise `Y‚āôŠĶĘ ‚Üí_p cŠĶĘ`, `ContinuousAt g c` ‚áí `g(Y‚āô) ‚Üí_p g(c)` | Finite-Pi continuous-mapping theorem in probability. Converts coordinate tails to a finite union bound, then applies continuity in the product metric. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.matrix_comp_continuousAt` | entrywise `M‚āôŠĶĘ‚Īľ ‚Üí_p M‚āÄŠĶĘ‚Īľ`, `ContinuousAt g M‚āÄ` ‚áí `g(M‚āô) ‚Üí_p g(M‚āÄ)` | Matrix-valued corollary of the finite-Pi theorem, reindexing entries by `K√óK`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.inv` | `Yn ‚Üí_p Y‚āÄ` ‚ąß `Y‚āÄ ‚Č† 0` ‚áí `1/Yn ‚Üí_p 1/Y‚āÄ` | Reciprocal continuity (special case of `comp_continuousAt` via `continuousAt_inv‚āÄ`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sub_const` | `Yn ‚Üí_p Y‚āÄ` ‚áí `Yn ‚ąí Y‚āÄ ‚Üí_p 0` | Center a convergence-in-probability statement at its constant limit. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sub` | `Xn ‚Üí_p a` ‚ąß `Yn ‚Üí_p b` ‚áí `Xn ‚ąí Yn ‚Üí_p a ‚ąí b` | Difference of two convergent-in-probability sequences (constant limits); őĶ/2 union bound (Mathlib lacks `TendstoInMeasure.sub`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.isLittleOp_one` | `Yn ‚Üí_p 0` ‚áí `Yn = o_p(1)` | Convergence in probability to zero is `o_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.isBigOp_one` | `Xn ‚Üí_p c` ‚áí `Xn = O_p(1)` | In-probability tightness at a constant limit. **Proved.** |

### 12n. `Stat/Limit/WLLN.lean`

Generic weak law of large numbers for `IIDSample`, plus the consistent-variance corollary. Generalizes the Panel-specific `iidPanel_sampleMean_ae` (boundedness weakened to integrability; a.s. limit converted to in-probability). Supplies the `ŌÉŐā‚āô ‚Üí_p ŌÉ¬≤` consistency input that the studentized/Wald layer takes as a hypothesis.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.measurable_sampleMean` | `Measurable g` ‚áí `Measurable (S.sampleMean g N)` | Measurability of the sample mean (finite sum of `g ‚ąė Z i`, scaled). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` | `Integrable (g‚ąėZ‚āÄ)` ‚áí `S.sampleMean g ‚Üí_p ‚ąę g dP` | WLLN: i.i.d. sample mean converges in probability to the population mean. Wraps `strong_law_ae_real` (a.s.) then `tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_ae` on the finite measure `őľ`; law transfer via `integral_map` + `S.law`. Requires `[IsProbabilityMeasure P]`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleSecondMoment_tendsto_inProb` | `Integrable (Ōą¬≤‚ąėZ‚āÄ)` ‚áí `S.sampleMean Ōą¬≤ ‚Üí_p ‚ąę Ōą¬≤ dP` | Consistent variance for a known influence function `Ōą`: `(1/n)ő£Ōą(ZŠĶĘ)¬≤ ‚Üí_p ŌÉ¬≤`. Direct corollary with `g := Ōą¬≤`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'. `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBWLLN.lean`

Fold-B weak law of large numbers: the analogue of `sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` averaged over the evaluation fold `B(n)` of a `OneShotSplit` (a growing index subset) instead of the full sample. Unlike the full-sample WLLN (an SLLN/a.e. argument) this is a finite-variance Chebyshev argument over the fold, so it restricts cleanly to `B(n)`. Supplies the missing substrate for fold-B sample-mean consistency (e.g. the Jacobian-consistency input of the partially linear feasible-DML estimator).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` | `MemLp g 2 P` ‚áí `(1/\|B(n)\|)ő£_{i‚ąąB(n)} g(ZŠĶĘ) ‚Üí_p ‚ąę g dP` | **Fold-B WLLN.** For a fixed finite-variance `g`, the fold-B sample mean converges in probability to the population mean. Proof: per-index variance/mean transport via `IdentDistrib.variance_eq`/`map`, `IndepFun.variance_sum` over the finset `B(n)` gives `Var = Var(g)/\|B(n)\|`, Chebyshev (`meas_ge_le_variance_div_sq`) and `\|B(n)\|‚Üí‚ąě` (`foldB_card_tendsto`) close it (empty folds handled via the eventual filter). **Proved.** |

### 12n''. `Stat/Inference/VarianceEstimation.lean`

Consistent variance/covariance estimation for the i.i.d. sample type ‚ÄĒ the `ŌÉŐā ‚Üí‚āö ŌÉ‚āÄ` input the studentized/Wald layer takes as a hypothesis. Builds directly on the WLLN.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_mul_tendsto_inProb` | `(1/N)ő£ g‚āĀ(ZŠĶĘ)g‚āā(ZŠĶĘ) ‚Üí‚āö ‚ąę g‚āĀg‚āā dP` | Empirical mean of a product (WLLN with `g := g‚āĀ¬∑g‚āā`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.abs_apply_mul_le_norm_sq` | `\|ŌÜ(Ōą x)¬∑ŌÜ'(Ōą x)\| ‚Č§ (‚ÄĖŌÜ‚ÄĖ‚ÄĖŌÜ'‚ÄĖ)¬∑‚ÄĖŌą x‚ÄĖ¬≤` | Operator-norm domination helper. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleCov_entry_tendsto_inProb` | `(1/N)ő£ ŌÜ(Ōą(ZŠĶĘ))ŌÜ'(Ōą(ZŠĶĘ)) ‚Üí‚āö ‚ąę ŌÜ(Ōą)ŌÜ'(Ōą) dP` | **Covariance consistency** for `Ōą : X ‚Üí E` via continuous coordinate functionals `ŌÜ ŌÜ' : E ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] ‚ĄĚ` (specializes to entrywise ő£Őā‚āĖ‚āó via `EuclideanSpace.proj`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sqrt` | `Vn ‚Üí‚āö v‚āÄ ‚áí ‚ąöVn ‚Üí‚āö ‚ąöv‚āÄ` | Continuous-mapping for `Real.sqrt`. **Proved.** |
| `sqrt_var_tendsto_inProb` | `ŌÉŐā¬≤ ‚Üí‚āö ŌÉ‚āÄ¬≤ ‚ąß ŌÉ‚āÄ>0 ‚áí ‚ąö(ŌÉŐā¬≤) ‚Üí‚āö ŌÉ‚āÄ` | **Studentization-ready** standard-error consistency; feeds `div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''. `Stat/CLT/MultivariateCLT.lean`

Multivariate CLT via the **Cram√©r‚ÄďWold device**, the vector analogue of `clt_normalized_sum`. Discharges the multivariate CLT contact `_hCLT` taken as a hypothesis by `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec` and `deltaMethod` (vector őĒ-method). Weak convergence on a finite-dim real inner-product space `E` is reduced to pointwise charFun convergence (L√©vy continuity, `ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_iff_tendsto_charFun` from the `Clt` package); each direction's charFun is the scalar charFun of the projected sum `‚ü™t, vecNS‚üę`, handled by the scalar CLT.

**Limit-law status:** the Cram√©r‚ÄďWold reduction and per-direction charFun limit `‚Üí exp(‚ąí¬Ĺ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę¬≤ dP)` are fully proven, sorry-free. The theorems here keep the target `Q` abstract, parameterized by `charFun Q t = exp(‚ąí¬Ĺ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę¬≤ dP)`. That abstract hypothesis is **discharged concretely** in `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean` (see 12n'''c): `gaussianLimit Ōą` is the covariance-`ő£` Gaussian built explicitly, and `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec` is the multivariate CLT against it with no abstract `Q`/charFun hypothesis remaining.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `inner_normalizedSum` | `‚ü™t, vecNS n ŌČ‚üę = scalarNS_{‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę} n ŌČ` | Projection identity. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.normalizedSum_vec_charFun_tendsto` | `charFun(őľ.map vecNS)(t) ‚Üí exp(‚ąí¬Ĺ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę¬≤ dP)` | **Per-direction charFun limit** from the scalar CLT via `integral_map`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.of_charFun_tendsto` | pointwise charFun convergence ‚áí `Tendsto_dist_vec` | **Cram√©r‚ÄďWold wrapper**. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec_of_charFun` | Gaussian-charFun `Q` ‚áí `vecNS ‚áí Q` | **Multivariate CLT contact**; discharges `_hCLT`. **Proved.** |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec_clt` | vector asym-linearity + Gaussian-charFun `Q` ‚áí rescaled estimator ‚áí Q | **End-to-end vector asymptotic normality** (no CLT hypothesis). **Proved.** |

### 12n'''b. `Stat/CLT/GaussianCharFunBridge.lean`

Verified glue converting a *concrete* centered Gaussian into the abstract `hQ` characteristic-function target taken as a hypothesis by `MultivariateCLT`/`WaldVec`/`RatioDeltaMethod`. The multivariate-Gaussian charFun formula is `ProbabilityTheory.IsGaussian.charFun_eq'` (`Mathlib/Probability/Distributions/Gaussian/CharFun.lean`), proven at the current pin. The concrete `Q` is now constructed in `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean` (12n'''c), completing de-abstraction.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `charFun_isGaussian_centered` | `IsGaussian Q` ‚ąß `‚ąę x ‚ąāQ = 0` ‚áí `charFun Q t = exp(‚ąí¬Ĺ¬∑covarianceBilin Q t t)` | centered-Gaussian charFun; specialises Mathlib `IsGaussian.charFun_eq'`. **Proved** (axioms: propext/choice/Quot). |
| `charFun_isGaussian_of_cov_eq` | + `‚ąÄ t, covarianceBilin Q t t = ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę¬≤dP` ‚áí `charFun Q t = exp(‚ąí¬Ĺ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę¬≤dP)` | matches the exact `hQ` shape of the multivariate CLT contact, ready to discharge it once `Q` is built. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''c. `Stat/CLT/SecondMomentOperator.lean` + `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean`

**De-abstraction of the multivariate CLT.** Together with `Mathlib/OperatorSqrt.lean` (operator ‚ąö, 10j) and `Mathlib/StandardGaussian.lean` (10i), these build the concrete covariance-`ő£` Gaussian limit and discharge the abstract `hQ` of `MultivariateCLT`. `SecondMomentOperator` is the operator `ő£ t = ‚ąę ‚ü™t,Ōą x‚üę ‚ÄĘ Ōą x ‚ąāP`; `GaussianLimit` sets `gaussianLimit Ōą := stdGaussian.map ‚ąöő£` and proves its charFun is the CLT target.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `secondMomentLM` | `E ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] E` | Second-moment operator `ő£ t = ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę‚ÄĘŌą ‚ąāP` (Bochner integral); linearity via `integral_add`/`integral_smul`. **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentLM_inner` | `‚ü™ő£ t, s‚üę = ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę‚ü™s,Ōą‚üę ‚ąāP` | Quadratic-form identity, via `integral_inner`. **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentLM_isPositive` | `(secondMomentLM Ōą).IsPositive` | `ő£` is positive (symmetric + `‚ü™ő£t,t‚üę = ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę¬≤ ‚Č• 0`). **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit` | `Measure E` | The CLT limit: `(stdGaussian E).map ‚ąöő£`, centered Gaussian with covariance `ő£`. `IsGaussian`/`IsProbabilityMeasure` instances. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_mean` | `‚ąę x, x ‚ąā(gaussianLimit Ōą) = 0` | Centered (mean pushes through the continuous-linear ‚ąöő£; `stdGaussian_mean`). **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_covarianceBilin` | `covarianceBilin (gaussianLimit Ōą) t t = ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę ‚ąāP` | Covariance recovers the asymptotic-variance integral, via `covarianceBilin_map`, `posSqrtCLM_adjoint` (self-adjoint), `posSqrtCLM_comp_self` (‚ąöő£‚ąė‚ąöő£=ő£), `covarianceBilin_stdGaussian`. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_charFun` | `charFun (gaussianLimit Ōą) t = exp(‚ąí¬Ĺ‚ąę‚ü™t,Ōą‚üę¬≤ dP)` | Exactly the abstract `hQ`; via `charFun_isGaussian_of_cov_eq`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec` | centered sq-int `Ōą` ‚áí `vecNS ‚áí gaussianLimit Ōą` | **Multivariate CLT against the concrete Gaussian limit** ‚ÄĒ no abstract `Q`/charFun hypothesis. **Proved** (axiom-clean). |

### 12n''''. `Stat/Inference/WaldVec.lean`

Multivariate Wald / confidence-ellipsoid asymptotic coverage ‚ÄĒ the vector analogue of the scalar `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage`. The Wald statistic `W‚āô = (‚ąön(őłŐā‚āô‚ąíőł‚āÄ))ŠĶÄ ő£Őā‚āô‚ĀĽ¬Ļ (‚ąön(őłŐā‚āô‚ąíőł‚āÄ))` is already ‚ĄĚ-valued, so an ellipsoid `{őł : Wald ‚Č§ c}` has coverage `Ōá(Iic c)` via one-sided portmanteau. The construction of `W‚āô` and the proof `W‚āô ‚áí Ōá` (continuous mapping of the joint limit through `(t,S) ‚Ü¶ tŠĶÄS‚ĀĽ¬Ļt`) is the caller's input, exactly as the scalar `wald_coverage` takes `S‚āô ‚áí N(0,1)`. The limit `Ōá` is kept abstract here, but identifying the image of the concrete Gaussian `gaussianLimit` (12n'''c) under the quadratic form `S ‚Ü¶ SŠĶÄő£‚ĀĽ¬ĻS` as `Ōá¬≤_d` is now **closed** in `Stat/Inference/ChiSquaredWald.lean` (12n'''d).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_Iic` | `W‚āô ‚áí Ōá` ‚ąß `Ōá(‚ąāIic c)=0` ‚ąß bridge ‚áí `coverProb ‚Üí Ōá(Iic c)` | **Ellipsoid coverage** via one-sided portmanteau on `Set.Iic c`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_Iic_of_noAtoms` | `W‚āô ‚áí Ōá` (`[NoAtoms Ōá]`) ‚ąß bridge ‚áí `coverProb ‚Üí Ōá(Iic c)` | Same, boundary-null discharged automatically for any atomless limit (e.g. non-degenerate Ōá¬≤). **Proved.** |

### 12n'''d. `Stat/CLT/ChiSquared.lean` + `Stat/Inference/ChiSquaredWald.lean`

**Ōá¬≤ identification of the Wald limit.** `ChiSquared` *defines* the Ōá¬≤_d law and `ChiSquaredWald` whitens `gaussianLimit` through the Wald quadratic form to recognise its image as Ōá¬≤_d, closing the open step flagged in `WaldVec`. Ōá¬≤_d is defined as the law of `‚ÄĖW‚ÄĖ¬≤` for `W ~ stdGaussian` (textbook squared-norm definition; the `Gamma(d/2,1/2)` density form is not developed ‚ÄĒ Mathlib has no Ōá¬≤). The whitening: `gaussianLimit = stdGaussian.map ‚ąöő£`, and with `ő£` non-degenerate (injective ‚áí invertible in finite dim), `‚ü™‚ąöő£ w, ő£‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(‚ąöő£ w)‚üę = ‚ÄĖw‚ÄĖ¬≤` by self-adjointness of `‚ąöő£`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `chiSqDist` | `‚Ąē ‚Üí Measure ‚ĄĚ` | The Ōá¬≤_d law: `(stdGaussian (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin d))).map ‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ÄĖ¬≤`. `IsProbabilityMeasure` instance. **Proved.** |
| `stdGaussian_map_linearIsometryEquiv` | `(stdGaussian E).map őĻ = stdGaussian F` for `őĻ : E ‚ČÉ‚āóŠĶĘ F` | `stdGaussian` is isometry-invariant; matched characteristic functions (`charFun_isGaussian_centered` + `Measure.ext_of_charFun`, covariance preserved by `őĻ`). **Proved.** |
| `stdGaussian_map_normSq` | `(stdGaussian E).map ‚ÄĖ¬∑‚ÄĖ¬≤ = chiSqDist (finrank ‚ĄĚ E)` | The squared-norm law depends only on dimension. **Proved.** |
| `noAtoms_pi_normSq` / `noAtoms_chiSqDist` | `1 ‚Č§ d ‚áí NoAtoms (chiSqDist d)` | Ōá¬≤_d is atomless for `d ‚Č• 1` (squared first coordinate atomless, Fubini over the independent remainder; reduced to a product of 1-D Gaussians via `stdGaussian_map_normSq_eq_pi`). **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentInv` | `E ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] E` | `ő£‚ĀĽ¬Ļ = (‚ąöő£)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ‚ąė(‚ąöő£)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` from injectivity of `ő£`; `secondMomentInv_secondMomentLM` / `secondMomentLM_secondMomentInv` certify it is the two-sided inverse. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_waldForm_map` | `(gaussianLimit Ōą).map (S ‚Ü¶ ‚ü™S, ő£‚ĀĽ¬Ļ S‚üę) = chiSqDist (finrank ‚ĄĚ E)` | **The Ōá¬≤ identification** (whitening through `‚ąöő£`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_chiSq` | `W‚āô ‚áí Ōá¬≤_d` (`1‚Č§d`) ‚ąß bridge ‚áí `coverProb ‚Üí Ōá¬≤_d(Iic c)` | WaldVec ellipsoid coverage specialised to the identified atomless limit `chiSqDist d`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''e. `Stat/CLT/ChiSquaredProjection.lean` ‚ÄĒ squared-norm of a projected Gaussian is Ōá¬≤

The law of `‚ÄĖP z‚ÄĖ¬≤` for an **orthogonal projection** `P` (self-adjoint idempotent) and `z ~ stdGaussian` is `Ōá¬≤_{rank P}`. Reusable beyond GMM (F-tests, score/LM tests, rank-deficient Wald). Proof: corestrict `P` to its range `S`, transport by the orthonormal-basis isometry `S ‚ČÉ‚āóŠĶĘ EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin r)`; the pushforward of `stdGaussian E` is `stdGaussian S` (centered, covariance = inner product, via `Measure.ext_of_charFun` ‚ÄĒ the crux is that the corestriction's adjoint is the inclusion because `P` fixes its range), then `stdGaussian_map_normSq`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `stdGaussian_map_normSq_orthogonalProjection` | `IsSelfAdjoint P` ‚ąß `P ‚ąėL P = P` ‚áí `(stdGaussian E).map (‚ÄĖP¬∑‚ÄĖ¬≤) = chiSqDist (finrank ‚ĄĚ (range P))` | **Projected-Gaussian Ōá¬≤.** The squared norm of an orthogonally-projected standard Gaussian is `Ōá¬≤` with rank-of-projection degrees of freedom. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12o. `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`

Generic studentized-CLT and Wald-coverage engine, abstracted off the concrete TRAE-DR estimator (the NPIV/DR theorems now instantiate these). Portmanteau / Gaussian-boundary helpers are made public here.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist.tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier` | `Xn ‚áí Q` ‚ąß `Q(‚ąāE)=0` ‚áí `(őľ.map Xn)(E) ‚Üí Q(E)` | Continuity-set portmanteau for the project's `Tendsto_dist` wrapper. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_singleton` | `N(0,1)({x}) = 0` | Standard normal has no atoms. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_frontier_Icc` | `0 < z` ‚áí `N(0,1)(‚ąā[‚ąíz,z]) = 0` | Zero boundary mass for a symmetric interval. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian` | `Xn ‚áí N(0,ŌÉ‚āÄ¬≤)` ‚ąß `ŌÉŐā ‚Üí_p ŌÉ‚āÄ` ‚ąß `ŌÉ‚āÄ>0` ‚áí `Xn/ŌÉŐā ‚áí N(0,1)` | **Studentized CLT** (random-denominator Slutsky). Scales `Xn` by `1/ŌÉ‚āÄ` (`const_mul_tendsto_gaussian`); the remainder `Xn¬∑(1/ŌÉŐā ‚ąí 1/ŌÉ‚āÄ)` is `O_p(1)¬∑o_p(1)` via `tightness` + `comp_continuousAt`; absorbs with `add_isLittleOp_one`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage` | `Sn ‚áí N(0,1)` ‚ąß `coverProb ‚ąí őľ{Sn‚ąą[‚ąíz,z]} ‚Üí 0` ‚áí `coverProb ‚Üí N(0,1)([‚ąíz,z])` | **Wald coverage** (generic): portmanteau on the closed interval plus the caller-supplied event-equivalence bridge. Specialize at `z = z_{1‚ąíőĪ/2}` for `1 ‚ąí őĪ` coverage. **Proved.** |

### 12o'. `Stat/Bootstrap/` ‚ÄĒ Nonparametric bootstrap standard error

The econometric workhorse use of the bootstrap (`vce(bootstrap)`): estimate the standard error of an asymptotically-linear estimator by the bootstrap, then form a studentized / Wald interval. The key fact is that the nonparametric (multinomial / Efron) bootstrap variance of `‚ąön(XŐĄ* ‚ąí XŐĄ)` is **exactly** the empirical variance `(1/n)ő£(Ōą(ZŠĶĘ) ‚ąí ŌąŐĄ‚āô)¬≤` ‚ÄĒ not an asymptotic approximation ‚ÄĒ so a *consistent* bootstrap SE plugs straight into the generic studentized CLT / Wald coverage of `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`. The nonparametric percentile / percentile-t refinements (which need conditional weak convergence) remain out of scope, but the **Gaussian multiplier (wild) bootstrap** below now supplies an *exact* distributional law on the conditioned sample. Three files: `Variance.lean` (the exact variance identity + consistency), `CI.lean` (studentized CLT + Wald coverage), and `GaussianMultiplier.lean` (exact multiplier-bootstrap law).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar` | `noncomputable def`, `‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Bootstrap variance: the conditional variance `E*[Ōą¬≤] ‚ąí (E*Ōą)¬≤ = sampleMean(Ōą¬≤) ‚ąí (sampleMean Ōą)¬≤` of a single multinomial-bootstrap draw `Ōą(Z*‚āĀ)` over the empirical distribution. |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_eq_centered` | `bootstrapVar = (1/n)ő£(Ōą(ZŠĶĘ) ‚ąí ŌąŐĄ‚āô)¬≤` | **Exact variance identity** (`E*[(Ōą‚ąíE*Ōą)¬≤]`), the centered empirical second moment; holds for every `n` (`field_simp`/`ring` after the centered-sum expansion). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ bootstrapVar S Ōą n ŌČ` | Nonnegativity ‚ÄĒ it is a sum of squares (`bootstrapVar_eq_centered`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_tendsto_inProb` | meas/int/sq-int/mean-zero `Ōą` ‚áí `bootstrapVar ‚Üí‚āö ‚ąę Ōą¬≤ dP` | **Consistency of the bootstrap variance**: `sampleMean(Ōą¬≤) ‚Üí‚āö ‚ąęŌą¬≤` and `(sampleMean Ōą)¬≤ ‚Üí‚āö (‚ąęŌą)¬≤=0`, subtracted via `Tendsto_inProb.sub`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapSE` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ąö(bootstrapVar)` | Bootstrap standard error of `‚ąön őłŐā` ‚ÄĒ the bootstrap estimate of the asymptotic SD `‚ąö(‚ąęŌą¬≤)`. |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapSE_tendsto_inProb` | same hyps ‚áí `bootstrapSE ‚Üí‚āö ‚ąö(‚ąę Ōą¬≤ dP)` | **SE consistency**; `Tendsto_inProb.sqrt` of the variance consistency. Supplies the `ŌÉŐā ‚Üí‚āö ŌÉ‚āÄ` input of `div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapStudentized` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ąön(őłŐā‚ąíőł‚āÄ)/ŌÉŐā‚āô` | The bootstrap studentized statistic (full-sample index family). |
| `bootstrap_studentized_tendsto` | `IsAsymLinear őłn őł‚āÄ Ōą S range` ‚ąß `0<‚ąęŌą¬≤` ‚áí `‚ąön(őłŐā‚ąíőł‚āÄ)/ŌÉŐā‚āô ‚áí N(0,1)` | **Bootstrap studentized CLT**: `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` (numerator `‚áí N(0,‚ąęŌą¬≤)`) + bootstrap-SE consistency through `Tendsto_dist.div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `bootstrap_wald_coverage` | + `z>0` ‚ąß coverage-event bridge ‚áí `coverProb ‚Üí N(0,1)([‚ąíz,z])` | **Bootstrap Wald asymptotic coverage**; instantiates `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage` on the bootstrap studentized statistic. `z = z_{1‚ąíőĪ/2}` gives the `1‚ąíőĪ` interval `őłŐā‚āô ¬Ī z_{1‚ąíőĪ/2}¬∑ŌÉŐā‚āô/‚ąön`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12o''. `Stat/Bootstrap/GaussianMultiplier.lean` ‚ÄĒ exact Gaussian multiplier (wild) bootstrap law

The multiplier/wild bootstrap (van der Vaart ¬ß23; Mammen) attaches i.i.d. mean-zero multipliers `őĺŠĶĘ` to the recentred data `aŠĶĘ = xŠĶĘ ‚ąí xŐĄ‚āô`. With **standard Gaussian** multipliers the perturbed statistic is, on a *fixed* data vector, **exactly** a centred Gaussian ‚ÄĒ no CLT and no conditional-distribution machinery, because a linear combination of independent Gaussians is Gaussian with variances adding. Both declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `map_weighted_sum_gaussian` | `iIndepFun őĺ`, each `őľ.map (őĺ i) = gaussianReal 0 1` ‚áí `őľ.map (ŌČ ‚Ü¶ ‚ąĎ i, a i ¬∑ őĺ i ŌČ) = gaussianReal 0 ‚ü®‚ąĎ i, (a i)¬≤, _‚ü©` | **Core fact**: a weighted sum of i.i.d. standard Gaussians has law `N(0, ‚ąĎ aŠĶĘ¬≤)`. `Finset` induction peeling one summand: each `aŠĶĘ őĺŠĶĘ ~ N(0, aŠĶĘ¬≤)` (`gaussianReal_map_const_mul`), the partial sum is independent of the new term (`iIndepFun.indepFun_finset_sum_of_notMem`), and their laws convolve (`gaussianReal_add_gaussianReal_of_indepFun`). |
| `multiplierBootstrap_law` | as above with `aŠĶĘ = n^{-1/2}(xŠĶĘ ‚ąí xŐĄ)` ‚áí law of `T‚āô* = n^{-1/2} ‚ąĎ i, (xŠĶĘ ‚ąí xŐĄ) őĺŠĶĘ` is `gaussianReal 0 ‚ü®s‚āô¬≤, _‚ü©` | **Exact law of the studentized multiplier-bootstrap mean**: the bootstrap statistic is exactly `N(0, s‚āô¬≤)` with `s‚āô¬≤ = (1/n) ‚ąĎ (xŠĶĘ ‚ąí xŐĄ)¬≤` the fixed sample variance. Since `s‚āô¬≤ ‚Üí‚āö ŌÉ¬≤`, this is the distributional bootstrap counterpart of the CLT limit `N(0, ŌÉ¬≤)`. |

---

#### 12p. `Stat/Quantile/Quantile.lean` ‚ÄĒ Quantile function (generalized inverse of a cdf)

Causal-agnostic statistical primitive: the lower quantile function of a real
probability measure, `quantile őľ ŌĄ := sInf {x | ŌĄ ‚Č§ cdf őľ x}`, the
left-continuous generalized inverse of Mathlib's `ProbabilityTheory.cdf`.
Mathlib has the cdf but no generalized inverse; this file supplies it plus the
order (Galois) characterisation. Project-agnostic; upstream-candidate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `quantileSet őľ ŌĄ` | `def`, `Set ‚ĄĚ` | The super-level set `{x | ŌĄ ‚Č§ cdf őľ x}` defining the quantile. |
| `quantile őľ ŌĄ` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | `sInf (quantileSet őľ ŌĄ)` ‚ÄĒ the `ŌĄ`-quantile (left-continuous generalized inverse of the cdf). |
| `quantileSet_up_closed` | `lemma` | `x ‚ąą quantileSet őľ ŌĄ ‚Üí x ‚Č§ x' ‚Üí x' ‚ąą quantileSet őľ ŌĄ` (cdf monotone). **Proved.** |
| `bddBelow_quantileSet` | `lemma` | For `0 < ŌĄ`, the super-level set is bounded below (cdf ‚Üí 0 at ‚ąí‚ąě). **Proved.** |
| `nonempty_quantileSet` | `lemma` | For `ŌĄ < 1`, the super-level set is nonempty (cdf ‚Üí 1 at +‚ąě). **Proved.** |
| `le_cdf_quantile` | `lemma` | For `ŌĄ < 1`, `ŌĄ ‚Č§ cdf őľ (quantile őľ ŌĄ)`. Uses right-continuity of the cdf. **Proved.** |
| `le_cdf_of_quantile_le` | `lemma` | For `ŌĄ < 1`, `quantile őľ ŌĄ ‚Č§ x ‚Üí ŌĄ ‚Č§ cdf őľ x`. **Proved.** |
| `quantile_le_of_le_cdf` | `lemma` | For `0 < ŌĄ`, `ŌĄ ‚Č§ cdf őľ x ‚Üí quantile őľ ŌĄ ‚Č§ x` (`csInf_le`). **Proved.** |
| `quantile_le_iff` | `theorem` | **Galois connection** on `(0,1)`: `quantile őľ ŌĄ ‚Č§ x ‚ÜĒ ŌĄ ‚Č§ cdf őľ x`. **Proved.** |
| `quantile_mono` | `lemma` | Quantile monotone in `ŌĄ` on `(0,1)`. **Proved.** |

#### 12q. `Stat/Quantile/EmpiricalCDF.lean` ‚ÄĒ Empirical cumulative distribution function

Sample analogue of `cdf P`, `FŐā‚āô(y) = (1/n) ő£ 1{Z_i ‚Č§ y}` (the `sampleMean` of the indicator `cdfStat y`), with its fixed-`y` influence function `cdfIF P y z = 1{z ‚Č§ y} ‚ąí F(y)`. Because `‚ąön (FŐā‚āô(y) ‚ąí F(y))` *is* the normalized influence-function sum, the empirical cdf is asymptotically linear with an identically-zero remainder, giving the fixed-`y` CLT directly. Causal-agnostic base layer for `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantile.lean`; upstream-candidate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cdfStat y` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The lower-ray indicator `1{¬∑ ‚Č§ y}` (= `Set.indicator (Iic y) 1`). |
| `measurable_cdfStat`, `cdfStat_nonneg`, `cdfStat_le_one`, `cdfStat_sq` | `lemma`s | Measurability; `0 ‚Č§ 1{¬∑‚Č§y} ‚Č§ 1`; idempotence `(1{¬∑‚Č§y})¬≤ = 1{¬∑‚Č§y}`. **Proved.** |
| `integrable_cdfStat` | `lemma` | `cdfStat y` is integrable (bounded on a finite measure). **Proved.** |
| `integral_cdfStat` | `lemma` | `‚ąę 1{¬∑‚Č§y} dP = cdf P y` (= `P.real (Iic y)`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalCDF S y` | `noncomputable def`, `‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The empirical cdf `FŐā‚āô(y)`, the `sampleMean` of `cdfStat y`. |
| `cdfIF P y` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Fixed-`y` influence function `1{z ‚Č§ y} ‚ąí F(y)`. |
| `measurable_cdfIF`, `cdfIF_mean_zero`, `abs_cdfIF_le_one`, `cdfIF_sq_integrable` | `lemma`s | Measurability; `‚ąę cdfIF dP = 0`; `|cdfIF| ‚Č§ 1`; square-integrability. **Proved.** |
| `cdfIF_variance` | `lemma` | `‚ąę cdfIF¬≤ dP = F(y)(1 ‚ąí F(y))`, the Bernoulli variance of `{Z ‚Č§ y}`. **Proved.** |
| `isLittleOp_zero` | `lemma` | The zero sequence is `o_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `rescaledEmpiricalCDF_eq_normalizedSum` | `lemma` | **Key identity** `‚ąön (FŐā‚āô(y) ‚ąí F(y)) = (1/‚ąön) ő£ cdfIF(Z_i)`. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalCDF_isAsymLinear` | `lemma` | `FŐā‚āô(y)` is `IsAsymLinear` at `F(y)` with IF `cdfIF P y` (remainder ‚Č° 0). **Proved.** |
| `empiricalCDF_tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Empirical-cdf CLT** `‚ąön (FŐā‚āô(y) ‚ąí F(y)) ‚áí N(0, F(y)(1‚ąíF(y)))`. **Proved** (instance of `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal`). |
| `empiricalCDF_tendsto_inProb` | `theorem` | **Consistency** (WLLN) `FŐā‚āô(y) ‚Üí‚āö F(y)`. **Proved.** |

#### 12r. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantile.lean` ‚ÄĒ Sample-quantile asymptotics (Bahadur representation)

`‚ąön`-asymptotic normality of a quantile-estimator sequence `qŐā‚āô` for the `ŌĄ`-quantile of `P`, via the Bahadur representation with influence function `Ōą_ŌĄ(z) = (ŌĄ ‚ąí 1{z ‚Č§ q‚āÄ})/f‚āÄ` and asymptotic variance `ŌĄ(1‚ąíŌĄ)/f‚āÄ¬≤`. The hard empirical-process (Donsker / asymptotic-equicontinuity) content ‚ÄĒ the `o_p(n^{-1/2})` Bahadur remainder ‚ÄĒ is **exposed as a hypothesis** in the `QuantileRegularity` bundle, mirroring the `StochEquicontAt` pattern of `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`; everything downstream is proved. Reusable by any quantile estimator (plain sample quantile, IPW-weighted QTE quantile, ‚Ä¶) satisfying the expansion. References: Bahadur (1966); van der Vaart (1998) ¬ß21.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `quantileIF ŌĄ q‚āÄ f‚āÄ` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Sample-quantile influence function `(ŌĄ ‚ąí 1{z ‚Č§ q‚āÄ})/f‚āÄ`. |
| `measurable_quantileIF` | `lemma` | Measurability of `quantileIF`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_mean_zero` | `lemma` | `‚ąę Ōą_ŌĄ dP = 0`, given `cdf P q‚āÄ = ŌĄ`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_sq_integrable` | `lemma` | `Ōą_ŌĄ` is square-integrable (bounded by `(|ŌĄ|+1)/|f‚āÄ|`). **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_variance` | `lemma` | `‚ąę Ōą_ŌĄ¬≤ dP = ŌĄ(1‚ąíŌĄ)/f‚āÄ¬≤`, the classical sample-quantile asymptotic variance. **Proved.** |
| `QuantileRegularity S qn ŌĄ q‚āÄ f‚āÄ` | `structure` (`Prop`) | Regularity bundle: `ŌĄ ‚ąą (0,1)`, density `f‚āÄ > 0`, `cdf P q‚āÄ = ŌĄ`, `HasDerivAt (cdf P) f‚āÄ q‚āÄ`, and `bahadur` ‚ÄĒ the exposed Donsker remainder (= `IsAsymLinear.remainder` for `(qŐā‚āô, q‚āÄ, Ōą_ŌĄ)`). |
| `QuantileRegularity.isAsymLinear` | `lemma` | The bundle is an `IsAsymLinear` witness for `qŐā‚āô` at `q‚āÄ` with IF `Ōą_ŌĄ`. **Proved.** |
| `QuantileRegularity.tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Sample-quantile CLT** `‚ąön (qŐā‚āô ‚ąí q‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ŌĄ(1‚ąíŌĄ)/f‚āÄ¬≤)`. **Proved** (specializes `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` with the closed-form variance). |

#### 12s. `Stat/Quantile/EmpiricalQuantile.lean` ‚ÄĒ Sample quantile (empirical-cdf inverse)

The `ŌĄ`-sample quantile `qŐā‚āô(ŌĄ) = quantile őĹ‚āô ŌĄ`, the generalized inverse of the empirical cdf, where `őĹ‚āô = (1/n) ő£_{i<n} őī_{ZŠĶĘ}` is the empirical measure. Supplies the deterministic/structural facts (switching relation, monotonicity, atom bound) feeding the Bahadur derivation. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure S n ŌČ` | `noncomputable def` | Empirical measure `(1/n) ő£_{i<n} őī_{ZŠĶĘ}`. |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_isProbabilityMeasure` | `lemma` | For `0 < n`, `őĹ‚āô` is a probability measure. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_real_Iic` | `lemma` | `őĹ‚āô.real (Iic y) = FŐā‚āô(y)`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_cdf` | `lemma` | **cdf bridge** `cdf őĹ‚āô = FŐā‚āô`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile S ŌĄ` | `noncomputable def` | Sample quantile `qŐā‚āô(ŌĄ) = quantile őĹ‚āô ŌĄ`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_le_iff` | `lemma` | **Switching relation** `qŐā‚āô(ŌĄ) ‚Č§ x ‚ÜĒ ŌĄ ‚Č§ FŐā‚āô(x)` (`0<ŌĄ<1`, `0<n`). **Proved** (from `quantile_le_iff` + cdf bridge). |
| `IIDSample.empiricalCDF_monotone` | `lemma` | `y ‚Ü¶ FŐā‚āô(y)` is monotone. **Proved**. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_atom_bound` | `lemma` | **Atom bound** `\|FŐā‚āô(qŐā‚āô) ‚ąí ŌĄ\| ‚Č§ 1/n` a.s., under atomless `P` (`Continuous (cdf P)`); needs `[IsProbabilityMeasure őľ]`. **Proved** (no-atoms from continuous cdf via `measure_singleton`/`leftLim`; a.s. tie-free from `IndepFun`+`NoAtoms`; discrete count `card(Cle q)=card(Clt)+card(Ceq)`, ties `‚Č§1`, strict part `‚Č§ nŌĄ` via threshold below `q`). |

#### 12t. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileBahadur.lean` ‚ÄĒ Bahadur representation (DERIVED)

Derives the Bahadur remainder of the **sample** quantile from elementary tools (Chebyshev + monotone-grid oscillation) ‚ÄĒ no Donsker/empirical-process hypothesis. Turns `SampleQuantile.QuantileRegularity.bahadur` from a hypothesis into a theorem for `qŐā‚āô`. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** This file is now a thin re-export umbrella; the derivation is split by layer under `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileBahadur/`: `Oscillation.lean` (L2 Chebyshev increment + L3 monotone-grid oscillation, incl. `SampleQuantileReg`/`empProcess`), `Rate.lean` (L4 root-`n` rate), `Linearity.lean` (L5 inversion + L6 assembly). Existing imports of `Causalean.Stat.Quantile.SampleQuantileBahadur` are unaffected. The declarations below are listed by their owning layer.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SampleQuantileReg P ŌĄ q‚āÄ f‚āÄ` | `structure` (`Prop`) | Regularity: `ŌĄ‚ąą(0,1)`, `f‚āÄ>0`, `cdf P q‚āÄ=ŌĄ`, `HasDerivAt (cdf P) f‚āÄ q‚āÄ`, **and** `cont` (atomless `P`). |
| `IIDSample.empProcess S n ŌČ y` | `noncomputable def` | Centered scaled empirical process `G‚āô(y) = ‚ąön (FŐā‚āô(y) ‚ąí F(y))`. |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_increment_tendsto_zero` | `lemma` (L2) | `G‚āô(q‚āÄ+u/‚ąön) ‚ąí G‚āô(q‚āÄ) ‚Üí‚āö 0` (Chebyshev second-moment via `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_cell_sandwich`, `empProcess_node_max_tendsto_zero`, `exists_grid_bracket` | `lemma`s | L3 helpers: per-cell monotone sandwich; finite-grid node-max `‚Üí‚āö 0`; uniform-mesh bracketing. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_oscillation` | `lemma` (L3, **crux**) | At random `U‚āô=O_p(1)`: `G‚āô(q‚āÄ+U‚āô/‚ąön) ‚ąí G‚āô(q‚āÄ) ‚Üí‚āö 0` (monotone-grid sandwich). **Proved.** |
| `cdf_increment_sqrt_tendsto` (in `.Oscillation`), `Tendsto_dist.limsup_measure_closed_le`, `gaussian_tail_small`, `IIDSample.empProcess_q0_tendsto_normal`/`_bigO` | `lemma`s | L4 helpers: Taylor limit `‚ąön(F(q‚āÄ+a/‚ąön)‚ąíF(q‚āÄ))‚Üíf‚āÄa` (single canonical lemma defined in `Oscillation.lean`, reused by `Rate.lean` ‚ÄĒ the former duplicate `_pre`/`Rate` copies are consolidated); closed-set portmanteau; Gaussian-tail smallness; `G‚āô(q‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0,ŌĄ(1‚ąíŌĄ))` + its `O_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_rate` | `lemma` (L4) | `‚ąön(qŐā‚āô‚ąíq‚āÄ) = O_p(1)` (two-sided tail via switching + portmanteau). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_atom_term_tendsto_zero`, `sampleQuantile_taylor_remainder_tendsto_zero`, `Tendsto_inProb.add_zero_zero`/`of_isLittleOp_one`/`const_mul_zero` | `lemma`s | L5/L6 helpers: `‚ąön(FŐā‚āô(qŐā‚āô)‚ąíŌĄ)‚Üí‚āö0`; `‚ąön¬∑R(qŐā‚āô)‚Üí‚āö0`; `Tendsto_inProb` `+`/bridge/scalar-mul algebra. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_inversion` | `lemma` (L5) | `G‚āô(qŐā‚āô) = ‚ąíf‚āÄ¬∑‚ąön(qŐā‚āô‚ąíq‚āÄ) + o_p(1)` (switching + atom bound + Taylor). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.normalizedSum_quantileIF_eq` | `lemma` | `(1/‚ąön) ő£ Ōą_ŌĄ(ZŠĶĘ) = ‚ąíG‚āô(q‚āÄ)/f‚āÄ`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_isAsymLinear` | `theorem` (L6) | **Derived** `IsAsymLinear` for `qŐā‚āô` (Bahadur remainder proved `o_p(1)`: `f‚āÄ¬∑RŐÉ‚āô = L5 ‚ąí L3`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_quantileRegularity` | `theorem` | `qŐā‚āô` satisfies `QuantileRegularity` with `bahadur` derived ‚áí scalar CLT `‚ąön(qŐā‚āô‚ąíq‚āÄ)‚áíN(0,ŌĄ(1‚ąíŌĄ)/f‚āÄ¬≤)` for free. **Proved.** |

#### 12u. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileJoint.lean` ‚ÄĒ Joint normality of a quantile vector

Joint asymptotic normality of `(qŐā‚āô(ŌĄ‚āĀ),‚Ä¶,qŐā‚āô(ŌĄ_k))` via the per-coordinate derived Bahadur + the project multivariate CLT (Cram√©r‚ÄďWold). Target `Q` kept abstract via Gaussian charFun, as in the rest of the multivariate-CLT stack. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `eucl v` | `noncomputable abbrev` | Pack `Fin k ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` into `EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin k)` (the `PiLp 2` equiv). |
| `eucl_apply`, `norm_sq_eucl`, `norm_eucl_le_sum_abs` | `lemma`s | `(eucl v) j = v j`; `‚ÄĖeucl v‚ÄĖ¬≤ = ‚ąĎ v‚Īľ¬≤`; `‚ÄĖeucl v‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ąĎ \|v‚Īľ\|`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIFVec ŌĄ q f` | `noncomputable def` | Joint IF `Ōą(z)_j = (ŌĄ‚Īľ‚ąí1{z‚Č§q‚Īľ})/f‚Īľ`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec S ŌĄ` | `noncomputable def` | Sample-quantile vector `(qŐā‚āô(ŌĄ‚Īľ))_j`. |
| `measurable_quantileIFVec` | `lemma` | Measurability of `Ōą`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_cross` | `lemma` | **Covariance entry** `‚ąę Ōą_{ŌĄ‚Īľ}Ōą_{ŌĄ‚āó} dP = (min(ŌĄ‚Īľ,ŌĄ‚āó)‚ąíŌĄ‚ĪľŌĄ‚āó)/(f‚Īľf‚āó)` (`1{‚Č§q‚Īľ}1{‚Č§q‚āó}=1{‚Č§min}`, `F(min)=min(ŌĄ)` by monotonicity). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec_isAsymLinearVec` | `theorem` | Vector asymptotic linearity (coordinatewise from scalar Bahadur; `eucl` commutes with `‚ÄĘ`/`‚ąí`/`‚ąĎ`; norm `‚Č§ ‚ąĎ\|R‚āô‚Īľ\|`, each `o_p(1)`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec_tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Headline** `‚ąön(qŐā‚āô(ŌĄ‚Īľ)‚ąíq‚Īľ)_j ‚áí N(0,ő£)`, `ő£_{jl}=(min(ŌĄ‚Īľ,ŌĄ‚āó)‚ąíŌĄ‚ĪľŌĄ‚āó)/(f‚Īľf‚āó)` (`Q` abstract Gaussian-charFun via `tendsto_normal_vec_clt`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12v. `Stat/GMM/` ‚ÄĒ Generalized Method of Moments (Hansen 1982)

Abstract GMM stack over a moment model `g : E ‚Üí X ‚Üí F` (parameter `őł : E`,
moment vector valued in `F`, over-identified `dim E ‚Č§ dim F`).  Three layers:
the pure operator-algebra **optimal-weighting theorem** (`VarianceAlgebra`), the
statistical **problem bundle + efficiency** (`Setup`), and **asymptotic
normality** as a corollary of the Z-estimator CLT (`AsymptoticNormality`).  All
declarations are namespace `Causalean.Stat`.  Spec: Hansen (1982); Newey &
McFadden (1994), ¬ß3.  All **sorry-free and axiom-clean**.

#### 12v.1. `Stat/GMM/VarianceAlgebra.lean` ‚ÄĒ sandwich/efficient variance algebra

Pure finite-dimensional operator algebra (no measure theory) on continuous
linear maps between real inner-product spaces.  Inverses are carried as *data
with two-sided witnesses* (mirroring `ZEstimatorRegularity.J‚āÄ_inv`), so no
operator inverse is constructed.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmBread G W` | `(adjoint G) ‚ąėL W ‚ąėL G : E ‚ÜíL E` | The **bread** `GŠĶÄWG` of Jacobian `G : E ‚ÜíL F` and weighting `W : F ‚ÜíL F`; symmetric when `W` is, invertible at full-rank `G`, `W ‚ČĽ 0`. |
| `gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv` | `breadInv ‚ąėL GŠĶÄ ‚ąėL W ‚ąėL Cov ‚ąėL W ‚ąėL G ‚ąėL breadInv` | The **sandwich asymptotic variance** `(GŠĶÄWG)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ GŠĶÄW ő£ WG (GŠĶÄWG)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`, given a two-sided inverse `breadInv` of the bread. (`Cov` = covariance `ő£`, since `ő£` is a reserved Lean token.) |
| `adjoint_inv_self` | `adjoint M = M` ‚ąß `M ‚ąėL N = id` ‚áí `adjoint N = N` | Self-adjointness of a right inverse of a self-adjoint operator (left-inverse uniqueness). Single shared copy in `VarianceAlgebra.lean` (imported by `OverID.lean`; the former duplicate in `OverID.lean` is removed). |
| `adjoint_gmmBread` | `adjoint W = W` ‚áí `adjoint (gmmBread G W) = gmmBread G W` | (private) The bread is self-adjoint when `W` is. |
| `gmm_efficiency` | `(gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv ‚ąí effInv).IsPositive` | **GMM optimal-weighting theorem (Hansen 1982, Thm 3.2).** The sandwich variance dominates the efficient variance `effInv = (GŠĶÄCov‚ĀĽ¬ĻG)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` in the L√∂wner order, with equality at `W = Cov‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`. Proof: `V(W) ‚ąí V‚ėÖ = (A‚ąíB)ő£(A‚ąíB)ŠĶÄ ‚™į 0` with `A = (GŠĶÄWG)‚ĀĽ¬ĻGŠĶÄW`, `B = (GŠĶÄő£‚ĀĽ¬ĻG)‚ĀĽ¬ĻGŠĶÄő£‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`; the cross terms collapse to `V‚ėÖ` because `ő£`/`ő£‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` cancel against the bread, and positivity is `IsPositive.conj_adjoint` on `ő£ ‚™į 0`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.2. `Stat/GMM/Setup.lean` ‚ÄĒ GMM problem, influence function, efficiency

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmScore G W g` | `fun őł x => adjoint G (W (g őł x))` | The **combined GMM score** `Ōą(őł,x) = GŠĶÄW g(őł,x)`; its empirical zero is the (linearized) GMM estimator (FOC of `Šł°ŠĶÄWŠł°`). |
| `gmmIF G W breadInv g őł‚āÄ` | `fun x => ‚ąí(breadInv (adjoint G (W (g őł‚āÄ x))))` | The **GMM influence function** `‚ąí(GŠĶÄWG)‚ĀĽ¬ĻGŠĶÄW g(őł‚āÄ,¬∑)`. |
| `GMMProblem P` | structure | Moment model bundle: `g`, truth `őł‚āÄ`, weighting `W` (`hWsa : adjoint W = W`), Jacobian `G`, identification `‚ąę g(őł‚āÄ) dP = 0`, measurability/finite-variance of `g(őł‚āÄ)`, Fr√©chet-derivative spec `jac_spec`, covariance `Cov` with second-moment characterization `hCov`, and two-sided inverse witnesses for `Cov`, `GŠĶÄWG`, `GŠĶÄCov‚ĀĽ¬ĻG`. |
| `GMMProblem.score` / `influence` | `noncomputable def`s | The problem's combined score / influence function. |
| `GMMProblem.asympVar` | `gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv` | The **sandwich asymptotic variance** of the problem. |
| `GMMProblem.effVar` | `effInv` | The **efficient variance** `(GŠĶÄCov‚ĀĽ¬ĻG)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`. |
| `GMMProblem.cov_isPositive` | `prob.Cov.IsPositive` | The covariance is a positive operator ‚ÄĒ it is a second moment (`‚ü™Cov t,t‚üę = ‚ąę ‚ü™t,g‚üę¬≤ ‚Č• 0`, symmetric). **Proved.** |
| `GMMProblem.efficiency` | `(prob.asympVar ‚ąí prob.effVar).IsPositive` | **Hansen optimal-weighting theorem, statistical form**: the sandwich variance dominates the efficient variance in the L√∂wner order; the efficient GMM weighting is `W = Cov‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`. Instantiates `gmm_efficiency`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.3. `Stat/GMM/AsymptoticNormality.lean` ‚ÄĒ GMM CLT via the combined-score Z-estimator

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | `IsAsymLinearVec őłn őł‚āÄ prob.influence S (range n)` | **GMM asymptotic linearity.** If `őłn` solves the combined empirical moment `(1/n) ő£ GŠĶÄW g(Z_i;őłn) = 0`, is consistent at rate `O_p(1/‚ąön)`, and the combined score `prob.score` satisfies `ZEstimatorRegularity` (with `reg.J‚āÄ_inv = breadInv`), then `őłn` is asymptotically linear with IF `‚ąí(GŠĶÄWG)‚ĀĽ¬ĻGŠĶÄW g(őł‚āÄ,¬∑)`. Hence `‚ąön(őłn‚ąíőł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, asympVar)` via `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec`. Reduces GMM to `zEstimator_clt` on `gmmScore`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.4. `Stat/GMM/OverID.lean` ‚ÄĒ over-identification (J / Hansen‚ÄďSargan) test

The whitened J-test: with the moment covariance normalised to the identity (the change of variables the `ő£Őā‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`-weighting performs), the residual maker `M = I ‚ąí G(GŠĶÄG)‚ĀĽ¬ĻGŠĶÄ` is an orthogonal projection of rank `m ‚ąí k`, so the J-statistic limit `‚ÄĖMw‚ÄĖ¬≤` is `Ōá¬≤_{m‚ąík}` via `stdGaussian_map_normSq_orthogonalProjection`. The general-covariance case is the change of variables `z ‚Ü¶ ő£^{-1/2} z`. Inverses carried as data+witnesses (`heL`, `heR`).

**Deferred (known gap).** This file establishes only the *limit law* `‚ÄĖMw‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚áí Ōá¬≤_{m‚ąík}` for a standard Gaussian `w` on the moment space. Wiring the **empirical** J-statistic `n¬∑Šł°‚āô(őłŐā)ŠĶÄ ő£Őā‚ĀĽ¬Ļ Šł°‚āô(őłŐā)` to this limit ‚ÄĒ i.e. proving the empirical moments converge to `Mw` under the efficient weighting and applying continuous mapping ‚ÄĒ is left as future work (needs the moment-CLT contact point + the whitening change-of-variables at the estimator). The Ōá¬≤ target law itself is fully proved.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmHatMatrix G effInv` | `G ‚ąėL effInv ‚ąėL adjoint G` | The **hat matrix** `H = G(GŠĶÄG)‚ĀĽ¬ĻGŠĶÄ`, orthogonal projection onto `range G`. |
| `gmmResidualMaker G effInv` | `id ‚ąí gmmHatMatrix G effInv` | The **residual maker** `M = I ‚ąí H`, orthogonal projection onto `(range G)^‚ä•`. |
| `gmmResidualMaker_isSelfAdjoint` / `_idempotent` | `IsSelfAdjoint M` / `M ‚ąėL M = M` | `M` is an orthogonal projection (uses `effInv` self-adjoint + the inverse witnesses). **Proved.** |
| `gmmResidualMaker_finrank_range` | `finrank (range M) = finrank F ‚ąí finrank E` | **Hansen‚ÄďSargan dof** `m ‚ąí k`. Proof: `range M = ker H`, `range H = range G`, `G` injective (from `heL`); rank‚Äďnullity (`LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker`) + `LinearMap.finrank_range_of_inj`. **Proved.** |
| `jStatistic_chiSq` | `(stdGaussian F).map (‚ÄĖM¬∑‚ÄĖ¬≤) = chiSqDist (finrank F ‚ąí finrank E)` | **Headline: the whitened GMM J-statistic limit law is `Ōá¬≤_{m‚ąík}`.** Applies the projected-Gaussian Ōá¬≤ lemma to `M`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12w. `Stat/Minimax/` ‚ÄĒ Minimax lower bounds (Le Cam two-point)

The converse direction of the inference stack: machinery to certify that *no*
estimator can do better than a given rate (optimality / sharpness), as opposed
to the achievability results in ¬ß12c‚Äď¬ß12v. The whole program reduces to one
chain: *risk lower bound ‚Üź reduction to testing ‚Üź testing error ‚Č• 1 ‚ąí TV ‚Üź TV ‚Č§ f(KL)*.
The first three links are elementary (this folder); the KL bridge (Pinsker)
lives in `Pinsker.lean`. Project-agnostic; candidates for upstream Mathlib.

Three reduction tiers share the same testing core: **Le Cam** (`LeCam.lean`, ¬ß12w.2)
packs two hypotheses; **Fano** (`Fano.lean`, ¬ß12w.7) packs many pairwise-separated
ones (rates from `log`-cardinality); **Assouad** (`Assouad.lean`, ¬ß12w.8) packs a
`d`-cube of coordinate-wise tests (rates that grow with dimension). Fano and Assouad
are both proven *unconditionally* from the elementary testing bound ‚ÄĒ no entropy or
product-measure machinery ‚ÄĒ so the divergence input enters only through `tvDist`.

#### 12w.1. `Stat/Minimax/TotalVariation.lean` ‚ÄĒ statistical TV distance

Mathlib only carries `SignedMeasure.totalVariation` (Jordan norm); this file develops the testing-oriented statistical TV between two measures.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tvDist őľ őĹ` | `‚®Ü A measurable, \|őľ.real A ‚ąí őĹ.real A\|` | **Total variation distance**: sup over measurable sets of the probability gap. |
| `abs_measureReal_sub_le_one` | `\|őľ.real A ‚ąí őĹ.real A\| ‚Č§ 1` | Each defining term is bounded by 1 (probability measures). **Proved.** |
| `bddAbove_tvDist_range` | `BddAbove (range ‚Ä¶)` | The defining family is bounded above by 1. **Proved.** |
| `abs_measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` | `\|őľ.real A ‚ąí őĹ.real A\| ‚Č§ tvDist őľ őĹ` | Each measurable set's gap is `‚Č§ tvDist` (via `le_ciSup`). **Proved.** |
| `measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` | `őĹ.real A ‚ąí őľ.real A ‚Č§ tvDist őľ őĹ` | Signed-gap form. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_nonneg`, `tvDist_le_one` | `0 ‚Č§ tvDist őľ őĹ ‚Č§ 1` | Range. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_symm` | `tvDist őľ őĹ = tvDist őĹ őľ` | Symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `one_sub_tvDist_le_test` | `1 ‚ąí tvDist őľ őĹ ‚Č§ őľ.real A + őĹ.real AŠ∂ú` | **The testing bound**: the total error of any test (rejection region `A`) is at least `1 ‚ąí tvDist`. The single inequality driving the two-point method. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.2. `Stat/Minimax/LeCam.lean` ‚ÄĒ Le Cam two-point method

The reduction from estimation to testing. Parameters live in a `[PseudoMetricSpace őė] [OpensMeasurableSpace őė]`; estimators are arbitrary measurable `est : ő© ‚Üí őė`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `measurableSet_error` | `MeasurableSet {ŌČ \| s ‚Č§ dist (est ŌČ) őł}` | The error region of a measurable estimator is measurable (closed-set preimage). **Proved.** |
| `one_sub_tvDist_le_error_sum` | `1 ‚ąí tvDist P‚āÄ P‚āĀ ‚Č§ P‚āÄ.real {s ‚Č§ dist(est,őł‚āÄ)} + P‚āĀ.real {s ‚Č§ dist(est,őł‚āĀ)}` | **Summed form.** Under `2s`-separation `2s ‚Č§ dist őł‚āÄ őł‚āĀ`, the two error probabilities of any estimator sum to at least `1 ‚ąí tvDist`. Proof = testing bound + triangle inequality + monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `half_one_sub_tvDist_le_max_error` | `(1 ‚ąí tvDist P‚āÄ P‚āĀ)/2 ‚Č§ max (P‚āÄ.real {‚Ä¶}) (P‚āĀ.real {‚Ä¶})` | **Headline (max form).** Every estimator has worst-case error probability at least `¬Ĺ(1 ‚ąí tvDist P‚āÄ P‚āĀ)`: the canonical minimax lower bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.3. `Stat/Minimax/Pinsker.lean` ‚ÄĒ KL bridge

Bridges `tvDist` to Mathlib's `InformationTheory.klDiv`, making the two-point bound usable for concrete families (KL is computable for Gaussians etc.). **Pinsker's inequality is now proven unconditionally** ‚ÄĒ both the scalar core (`klFun_lower_bound`) and the measure-theoretic half (Scheff√© `‚Č§` + weighted Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz). `PinskerBound` remains as a reusable `Prop` packaging but is no longer a substrate gate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `klFun_mul_lower_aux` | `0 ‚Č§ (x+2)¬∑klFun x ‚ąí (3/2)(x‚ąí1)¬≤` for `x ‚Č• 0` | Cleared-denominator Pinsker scalar bound. Proved by full two-level calculus on `ŌÜ` (`HasDerivAt` for ŌÜ, ŌÜ‚Ä≤; `ŌÜ‚Ä≥ = 2(log x + 1/x ‚ąí 1) ‚Č• 0` via `log_le_sub_one`; monotonicity ‚áí `ŌÜ ‚Č• ŌÜ(1)=0`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `klFun_lower_bound` | `(3/2)(x‚ąí1)¬≤/(x+2) ‚Č§ klFun x` for `x ‚Č• 0` | Division-form Pinsker scalar engine. **Proved.** |
| `PinskerBound őľ őĹ` | `Prop`: `tvDist őľ őĹ ‚Č§ ‚ąö((klDiv őľ őĹ).toReal/2)` | Reusable `Prop` packaging of the Pinsker bound for a pair. **Proven** by `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top`. |
| `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top` | `őľ ‚Č™ őĹ` + `klDiv őľ őĹ ‚Č† ‚ä§` ‚áí `PinskerBound őľ őĹ` | **Pinsker's inequality, proven unconditionally**: Scheff√© `‚Č§` (`tvDist_le_half_integral_abs_rnDeriv`) + weighted Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz (`‚ąö(p+2)` weight) + `klFun_lower_bound`. Both hyps are genuinely needed (`.toReal` collapses `klDiv = ‚ä§` to `0`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pinskerBound_pi_iid` | one-sample `őľ ‚Č™ őĹ` + `Integrable (llr őľ őĹ) őľ` ‚áí `PinskerBound (őľ^{‚äón}) (őĹ^{‚äón})` | i.i.d.-product Pinsker: product AC + `klDiv ‚Č† ‚ä§` discharged from marginals via `ProductKLLeCam.pi_iid_*`, then the unconditional inequality. **Proved.** |
| `klForm_two_point_lower_bound_of_pinsker` | from `PinskerBound P‚āÄ P‚āĀ` + 2s-separation: worst-case error `‚Č• (1 ‚ąí ‚ąö(KL/2))/2` | KL-form minimax lower bound from a supplied `PinskerBound` term; composes with `half_one_sub_tvDist_le_max_error`. **Proved from hypothesis.** |
| `klForm_two_point_lower_bound` | `P‚āÄ ‚Č™ P‚āĀ` + `klDiv P‚āÄ P‚āĀ ‚Č† ‚ä§` + 2s-separation ‚áí worst-case error `‚Č• (1 ‚ąí ‚ąö(KL/2))/2` | **Unconditional** KL-form minimax lower bound: derives the Pinsker bridge via `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top` (no `PinskerBound` hypothesis). **Proved.** |

#### 12w.4. `Stat/Minimax/Scheffe.lean` ‚ÄĒ variational TV identity (‚Č§ direction)

The Scheff√© `‚Č§` bound ‚ÄĒ the analytic workhorse for both the Ōá¬≤ route (`TV ‚Č§ ¬Ĺ‚ąöŌá¬≤`) and discharging `PinskerBound`. Proved via the symmetric splitting `‚ąę_A f = ‚ąí‚ąę_{AŠ∂ú} f` (sidesteps posPart/negPart machinery). The reverse `‚Č•` (full equality) was deliberately skipped ‚ÄĒ the `‚Č§` suffices downstream.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `abs_setIntegral_le_half_integral_abs_of_integral_eq_zero` | for integrable `f`, `‚ąę f ‚ąāőĹ = 0`, measurable `A`: `\|‚ąę x in A, f ‚ąāőĹ\| ‚Č§ ¬Ĺ‚ąę\|f\| ‚ąāőĹ` | Generic, probability-free; clean Mathlib-contribution candidate. **Proved.** |
| `measureReal_sub_eq_setIntegral_rnDeriv_sub_one` | under `őľ ‚Č™ őĹ`: `őľ.real A ‚ąí őĹ.real A = ‚ąę x in A, ((őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x).toReal ‚ąí 1) ‚ąāőĹ` | Signed-gap as a density integral. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_le_half_integral_abs_rnDeriv` | `tvDist őľ őĹ ‚Č§ ¬Ĺ‚ąę\|(őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x).toReal ‚ąí 1\| ‚ąāőĹ` (prob measures, `őľ ‚Č™ őĹ`) | **Scheff√© `‚Č§`.** **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.5. `Stat/Minimax/ChiSquared.lean` ‚ÄĒ Ōá¬≤ divergence link

`chiSqDiv` and the Ōá¬≤ control of total variation ‚ÄĒ the divergence that makes two-point indistinguishability computable (and tensorizes over iid samples). Named `chiSqDiv` to avoid colliding with the Ōá¬≤ *distribution* `chiSqDist` (¬ß12n'''d).

| `chiSqDiv őľ őĹ` | `‚ąę ((őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x).toReal ‚ąí 1)¬≤ ‚ąāőĹ` | Ōá¬≤-divergence of őľ relative to őĹ. |
| `chiSqDiv_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ chiSqDiv őľ őĹ` | Integrand is a square. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv` | `tvDist őľ őĹ ‚Č§ ¬Ĺ¬∑‚ąö(chiSqDiv őľ őĹ)` (prob, `őľ‚Č™őĹ`, sq-deviation integrable) | **Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz on Scheff√©** (H√∂lder `2,2` with `g‚Č°1`, `‚ąę1¬≤‚ąāőĹ=1`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_eq` | `chiSqDiv őľ őĹ = (‚ąę (őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x).toReal¬≤ ‚ąāőĹ) ‚ąí 1` (`őľ‚Č™őĹ`) | Expanded form via `‚ąę dőľ/dőĹ ‚ąāőĹ = 1`. **Proved.** |

| `rnDeriv_prod_eq` | `(őľ‚āĀ.prod őľ‚āā).rnDeriv (őĹ‚āĀ.prod őĹ‚āā) =ŠĶź fun z ‚Ü¶ rnDeriv őľ‚āĀ őĹ‚āĀ z.1 ¬∑ rnDeriv őľ‚āā őĹ‚āā z.2` | Product-measure RN-derivative factorization (assembled from `withDensity_rnDeriv_eq` + `prod_withDensity‚āÄ` + `rnDeriv_withDensity‚āÄ`; Mathlib has no packaged version). Reusable. **Proved.** |
| `chiSqDiv_prod` | `1 + chiSqDiv (őľ‚āĀ.prod őľ‚āā) (őĹ‚āĀ.prod őĹ‚āā) = (1+chiSqDiv őľ‚āĀ őĹ‚āĀ)(1+chiSqDiv őľ‚āā őĹ‚āā)` | **Ōá¬≤ tensorization** (binary). Via `rnDeriv_prod_eq` + `chiSqDiv_eq` + Fubini (`integral_prod_mul`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_map_measurableEquiv` | `chiSqDiv (őľ.map e) (őĹ.map e) = chiSqDiv őľ őĹ` for `e : ő© ‚ČÉŠĶź ő©'` (ŌÉ-finite) | **Ōá¬≤ is invariant under a measurable equivalence** (relabelling the sample space). Via `integral_map_equiv` + `MeasurableEmbedding.rnDeriv_map`. Reusable. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_self` | `chiSqDiv ŌĀ ŌĀ = 0` (ŌÉ-finite) | Self-divergence vanishes (`rnDeriv_self =ŠĶź 1`); the `n=0` base case. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pi_iid_absolutelyContinuous` | `őľ ‚Č™ őĹ ‚áí Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ‚Ü¶ őľ) ‚Č™ Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ‚Ü¶ őĹ)` | i.i.d. product a.c. (Mathlib has no `pi`-level a.c. lemma); induction via `piFinSuccAbove` + `AbsolutelyContinuous.prod`/`.map`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` | `1 + chiSqDiv (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ‚Ü¶ őľ)) (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ‚Ü¶ őĹ)) = (1 + chiSqDiv őľ őĹ)‚ĀŅ` (finite `ő©`, prob, `őľ‚Č™őĹ`) | **n-fold i.i.d. Ōá¬≤ tensorization** ‚ÄĒ the iid scaling consumed by `n`-sample two-point/Le Cam bounds. Induction on `Fin n` via `measurePreserving_piFinSuccAbove` + `chiSqDiv_map_measurableEquiv` + binary `chiSqDiv_prod`; integrability discharged for free by `Integrable.of_finite` (restricted to `[Fintype ő©] [MeasurableSingletonClass ő©]`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pi_iid_integrable_sq_dev` | `Integrable ((d(őľ^‚äón)/d(őĹ^‚äón) ‚ąí 1)¬≤) (őĹ^‚äón)` from the one-sample integrability `hint` (general `[MeasurableSpace S]`, probs, `őľ‚Č™őĹ`) | **Integrability propagation** to the i.i.d. product: the squared density deviation stays integrable under `n`-fold products (L¬≤(őĹ)-tensorization of the density). Supplies the side-condition that `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` got for free on a finite space. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid_general` | `1 + chiSqDiv (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ‚Ü¶ őľ)) (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ‚Ü¶ őĹ)) = (1 + chiSqDiv őľ őĹ)‚ĀŅ` (general `[MeasurableSpace S]`, probs, `őľ‚Č™őĹ`, sq-deviation integrable) | **n-fold i.i.d. Ōá¬≤ tensorization on a GENERAL (possibly continuous) measurable space** ‚ÄĒ the continuum analogue of `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` (whose `[Fintype]` only discharged integrability for free). Same `chiSqDiv_prod` induction, with integrability supplied by `hint` + `pi_iid_integrable_sq_dev`. Needed by minimax two-point lower bounds whose sample space is a continuum (e.g. a covariate space). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_prod_ancillary` | `chiSqDiv (őľ.prod ŌĀ) (őĹ.prod ŌĀ) = chiSqDiv őľ őĹ` (probs, `őľ‚Č™őĹ`, `hint` for `őľ,őĹ`) | **Ancillary factor invariance**: a common product factor `ŌĀ` cancels (`1 + Ōá¬≤(ŌĀ‚ÄĖŌĀ) = 1`). The formal "an ancillary observation carries no information"; the bridge from finite-cell to continuous-`X` (¬ß13sa.6). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `rnDeriv_mul_measure_singleton` | `őĹ {x} ¬∑ (dőľ/dőĹ)(x) = őľ {x}` (`őľ‚Č™őĹ`, measurable singletons) | **Discrete Radon‚ÄďNikodym bridge** (a genuine Mathlib gap): pointwise density inversion on atoms, via `setLIntegral_rnDeriv` + `restrict_singleton`/`lintegral_dirac'`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `finite_one_add_chiSqDiv` | `1 + chiSqDiv őľ őĹ = ‚ąĎ x, (őľ.real {x})¬≤ / (őĹ.real {x})` (finite `ő©`, probs, `őľ‚Č™őĹ`) | **Finite Ōá¬≤ formula**: turns Ōá¬≤ into a finite sum of squared point-mass ratios (`chiSqDiv_eq` + `integral_fintype` + the bridge). The enabling step for the Ingster bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.6. `Stat/Minimax/MinimaxRisk.lean` ‚ÄĒ real-functional / n-sample packaging

Causal-agnostic specialization of Le Cam to a real-valued functional estimated from `n` i.i.d. samples ‚ÄĒ the shape consumed by concrete minimax lower bounds (the causal structure-agnostic ATE theorem lives in `Estimation/`, not here).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |

| `real_two_point_lower_bound` | `2s ‚Č§ \|őł‚āÄ‚ąíőł‚āĀ\|` ‚áí `(1‚ąítvDist P‚āÄ P‚āĀ)/2 ‚Č§ max (P‚āÄ.real{s‚Č§\|est‚ąíőł‚āÄ\|}) (P‚āĀ.real{s‚Č§\|est‚ąíőł‚āĀ\|})` | Le Cam at `őė=‚ĄĚ` (`dist a b = \|a‚ąíb\|`). **Proved.** |

| `iid_two_point_lower_bound` | for laws `P‚āÄ,P‚āĀ` on `S`, functional `ŌĄ`, `n` samples (`Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n ‚Ü¶ P‚Īľ)`), `2s ‚Č§ \|ŌĄ P‚āÄ ‚ąí ŌĄ P‚āĀ\|` ‚áí worst-case miss prob `‚Č• ¬Ĺ(1 ‚ąí tvDist)` between the `n`-fold laws | **The n-sample minimax form.** **Proved.** |
| `two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le` | `chiSqDiv P‚āÄ P‚āĀ ‚Č§ c` (+ `‚Č™`, integrability) ‚áí worst-case miss prob `‚Č• (1 ‚ąí ¬Ĺ‚ąöc)/2` | **Ōá¬≤-form bound** (via `tvDist ‚Č§ ¬Ĺ‚ąöŌá¬≤`); the directly-certifiable form since Ōá¬≤ is computable and tensorizes. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.7. `Stat/Minimax/Fano.lean` ‚ÄĒ multiple-hypothesis (M-ary) lower bound

Generalizes Le Cam from *two* hypotheses to *many* pairwise-`2s`-separated ones ‚ÄĒ the testing/TV form of Fano's lemma (Tsybakov ¬ß2.6, Le Cam‚ÄďBirg√©). An estimator induces disjoint acceptance regions `A i = {ŌČ | dist(est ŌČ, őł i) < s}`; comparing each `PŠĶĘ(A i)` to a *reference* hypothesis `i‚āÄ` costs one `tvDist`, bounding the total correct-recovery mass. Proven **unconditionally** from `measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` ‚ÄĒ no entropy/mutual-information machinery; divergence enters only through `tvDist` (bound it by KL/Ōá¬≤ via ¬ß12w.3/¬ß12w.5). Uses a reference index `i‚āÄ` rather than the centroid mixture, so it does **not** depend on `Mixture.lean` (the symmetric mixture-centroid form is a strict sharpening that can be layered on).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `acceptanceRegion est őł s` | `{ŌČ \| dist (est ŌČ) őł < s}` | Points the estimator places strictly within `s` of `őł`. |
| `measurableSet_acceptanceRegion` | `MeasurableSet (acceptanceRegion est őł s)` | Complement of the (closed) error region. **Proved.** |
| `acceptanceRegion_disjoint` / `_pairwiseDisjoint` | `2s ‚Č§ dist őł‚āÄ őł‚āĀ ‚áí Disjoint (A őł‚āÄ) (A őł‚āĀ)`; pairwise version over a family | A point within `s` of both targets would force them within `2s`. **Proved.** |
| `sum_refReal_acceptance_le` | `‚ąĎŠĶĘ (P i‚āÄ).real (A i) ‚Č§ 1` | Reference mass over disjoint regions is `‚Č§ 1` (finite additivity + `‚Č§ univ`). **Proved.** |
| `sum_correct_le` | `‚ąĎŠĶĘ (P i).real (A i) ‚Č§ 1 + ‚ąĎŠĶĘ tvDist (P i‚āÄ) (P i)` | **Heart of Fano**: each `PŠĶĘ(A i)` vs reference costs one `tvDist`. **Proved.** |
| `fano_average_error` | `1 ‚ąí (1 + ‚ąĎŠĶĘ tvDist (P i‚āÄ) (P i))/N ‚Č§ (‚ąĎŠĶĘ (P i).real {s ‚Č§ dist(est,őł i)})/N` (`N = card őĻ`) | **Average-error minimax lower bound** over the family. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `fano_exists_error` | under `tvDist (P i‚āÄ) (P i) ‚Č§ ő≤`: `‚ąÉ i, 1 ‚ąí 1/N ‚ąí ő≤ ‚Č§ (P i).real {s ‚Č§ dist(est,őł i)}` | **Usable form**: large `N` + small `ő≤` ‚áí some hypothesis forces error. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.8. `Stat/Minimax/Assouad.lean` ‚ÄĒ hypercube lower bound (Assouad's lemma)

Packs a `d`-cube `{P ŌĄ : ŌĄ ‚ąą (Fin d ‚Üí Bool)}` in which each coordinate is an independent two-point test ‚ÄĒ converting a `d`-fold product into a `d`-fold *sum* of testing bounds (rates that grow with dimension). A cube estimator `est : ő© ‚Üí (Fin d ‚Üí Bool)` decodes each bit; its expected **Hamming risk** counts mis-decoded coordinates. Proof = coordinate-flip averaging: pairing each vertex `ŌĄ` with its `j`-flip via an involution (`flipPerm`), the two coordinate-`j` masses are `(P ŌĄ).real S + (P (flip j ŌĄ)).real SŠ∂ú`, lower-bounded by `1 ‚ąí tvDist` via `one_sub_tvDist_le_test`. **Unconditional**, no product-measure machinery.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `flipBit j ŌĄ` / `flipPerm j` | `Function.update ŌĄ j (!ŌĄ j)`; as an `Equiv.Perm` | Flip the `j`-th cube coordinate (an involution; the perm reindexes cube-sums). **Proved involutive.** |
| `hammingRisk P est ŌĄ` | `‚ąĎ‚Īľ (P ŌĄ).real {ŌČ \| est ŌČ j ‚Č† ŌĄ j}` | Expected number of coordinates the estimator decodes incorrectly at vertex `ŌĄ`. |
| `measurableSet_decode_ne` | `MeasurableSet {ŌČ \| est ŌČ j ‚Č† b}` from `MeasurableSet {ŌČ \| est ŌČ j = b}` | Decoding-error sets are complements of level sets. **Proved.** |
| `decode_ne_flip_compl` | `{ŌČ \| est ŌČ j ‚Č† (flip j ŌĄ) j} = {ŌČ \| est ŌČ j ‚Č† ŌĄ j}Š∂ú` | The `j`-th error set at the flipped vertex is the complement at `ŌĄ` (`a ‚Č† !b ‚ÜĒ a = b`). **Proved.** |
| `hammingRisk_pair_ge` | `1 ‚ąí tvDist (P ŌĄ) (P (flip j ŌĄ)) ‚Č§ (P ŌĄ).real S‚ĪľŌĄ + (P (flip j ŌĄ)).real S‚Īľ(flip)` | **Per-vertex pair bound** = two-point testing bound on the `j`-th decoded bit. **Proved.** |
| `sum_decode_ge` | `‚ąĎ_ŌĄ (1 ‚ąí tvDist (P ŌĄ)(P (flip j ŌĄ))) ‚Č§ 2¬∑‚ąĎ_ŌĄ (P ŌĄ).real {est j ‚Č† ŌĄ j}` | Per-coordinate cube-sum bound via the flip involution (`Equiv.sum_comp`). **Proved.** |
| `assouad_average` | under `tvDist (P ŌĄ)(P (flip j ŌĄ)) ‚Č§ ő≤`: `(d/2)(1 ‚ąí ő≤) ‚Č§ (‚ąĎ_ŌĄ hammingRisk P est ŌĄ)/card` | **Assouad (average form).** Large `d` + small `ő≤` ‚áí many mis-decoded coordinates. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `assouad_exists` | same hypotheses: `‚ąÉ ŌĄ, (d/2)(1 ‚ąí ő≤) ‚Č§ hammingRisk P est ŌĄ` | **Existence form**: some vertex forces large Hamming risk. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.9. `Stat/Minimax/Mixture.lean` ‚ÄĒ finite mixtures of measures

Generic finite mixture `‚ąĎŠĶĘ wŠĶĘ ‚ÄĘ PŠĶĘ` and the **domination lemma** behind the fuzzy-hypothesis / second-moment method: a mixture over in-class hypotheses with a common functional value never assigns more mass than the worst single hypothesis, so an averaged risk is `‚Č§` the supremum. Consumed by the structure-agnostic ATE lower bound (¬ß13sa) for the `dominated` (realizability) obligation of `TwoPointWitness`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mixture w P` | `‚ąĎ i, w i ‚ÄĘ P i` (`w : Fin K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě`) | Finite mixture of measures with `‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě` weights. |
| `mixture_apply` | `mixture w P A = ‚ąĎ i, w i ¬∑ P i A` | Mass on a set is the weighted sum of the parts' masses. **Proved.** |
| `mixture_isProbabilityMeasure` | `‚ąĎ i, w i = 1 ‚áí IsProbabilityMeasure (mixture w P)` (parts prob) | A convex combination of probability measures is a probability measure. **Proved.** |
| `mixtureReal_le` | `‚ąĎ i, w i = 1, (‚ąÄ i, (P i).real A ‚Č§ B) ‚áí (mixture w P).real A ‚Č§ B` (parts prob) | **Domination**: a weighted average of per-part masses is `‚Č§` a common bound. The averaging step in the fuzzy-hypothesis reduction. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `exists_real_ge_mixture` | `‚ąĎ i, w i = 1 ‚áí ‚ąÉ i, (mixture w P).real A ‚Č§ (P i).real A` (parts prob, `[Nonempty őĻ]`) | **Witness extraction**: a finite average is `‚Č§` its maximal component, so some part carries at least the mixture's mass. Pulls a single hard DGP out of a mixture hypothesis. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

## 12ep. `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/` ‚ÄĒ Uniform LLN (Glivenko‚ÄďCantelli) & extremum-estimator consistency

Empirical-process layer written for the econometrics workflow (van der Vaart
1998, Ch. 19; Newey & McFadden 1994, ¬ß2). A *function class* is an indexed family
`f : őĻ ‚Üí X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`; the empirical process measures how far the sample means `P‚āô f_i`
sit from the population means `P f_i = ‚ąę f_i dP`, **uniformly over the class**.
This is the uniform law of large numbers that the M-estimation CLT stack
(`Stat/MEstimation/`) previously had to assume. All declarations **proved,
sorry-free**.

### 12ep.a `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Basic.lean` ‚ÄĒ vocabulary

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empiricalProcess f i n` | `def`, `ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The centred-scaled process `‚ąön ¬∑ (P‚āô f_i ‚ąí P f_i)` at class member `i` ‚ÄĒ the object whose weak limit (a Gaussian process) is the subject of Donsker theory. |
| `IIDSample.supDeviation f n` | `def`, `ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Finite-class sup deviation `‚®Ü i, \|P‚āô f_i ‚ąí P f_i\|` (meaningful as a statistic when `őĻ` is finite). |
| `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | `def`, `Prop` | **(Weak) uniform LLN**: for every `őĶ > 0`, `őľ {ŌČ | ‚ąÉ i, őĶ ‚Č§ \|P‚āô f_i ‚ąí P f_i\|} ‚Üí 0`. Stated existentially (not via `‚®Ü`) so it is meaningful for infinite/uncountable classes without lattice-junk artefacts. |
| `L1Bracketing f P őĶ` | `structure` | A finite `L¬Ļ(P)` `őĶ`-bracketing: `m` brackets `[lo j, hi j]` (`j : Fin m`) with integrable endpoints, `P`-mean width `‚ąę (hi j ‚ąí lo j) dP ‚Č§ őĶ`, and an `assign : őĻ ‚Üí Fin m` sandwiching each member `lo (assign i) ‚Č§ f i ‚Č§ hi (assign i)`. |
| `HasL1Bracketing f P` | `def`, `Prop` | Finite `L¬Ļ(P)` bracketing numbers: an `őĶ`-bracketing exists for every `őĶ > 0`. The classical sufficient condition for Glivenko‚ÄďCantelli. |

### 12ep.b `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/GlivenkoCantelli.lean` ‚ÄĒ uniform LLN theorems

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `glivenkoCantelli_of_fintype` | `[Finite őĻ]`, each `f i` measurable & `P`-integrable ‚áí `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | A **finite** class of integrable functions is Glivenko‚ÄďCantelli. Union bound over coordinates, each vanishing by the pointwise WLLN (`IIDSample.sampleMean_tendsto_inProb`). **Proved.** |
| `glivenkoCantelli_of_hasL1Bracketing` | each `f i` measurable, `HasL1Bracketing f P` ‚áí `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | A class with **finite `L¬Ļ(P)` bracketing numbers** is Glivenko‚ÄďCantelli (van der Vaart 1998, Thm 19.4; Newey‚ÄďMcFadden Lemma 2.4). Each member is sandwiched in a bracket of mean-width `‚Č§ őĶ/2`, so its deviation is controlled by the `2m` bracket-endpoint deviations plus `őĶ/2`; the endpoints vanish by the finite case. **Proved.** |

Private helpers: `tendsto_measure_zero_of_le` (measure squeeze), `integrable_pullback` (transfer `P`-integrability to `őľ`), `dev_tendsto_zero` (single-statistic WLLN rephrased from the `edist`/`‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě` `TendstoInMeasure` to the real `\|¬∑\|` event), `sampleMean_mono`.

### 12ep.c `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/MEstimatorConsistency.lean` ‚ÄĒ extremum-estimator consistency

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mEstimator_consistent_of_glivenkoCantelli` | `GlivenkoCantelli S m` + sample-argmax domination over `őł‚āÄ` + well-separated population max ‚áí `‚ąÄ őĶ>0, őľ {ŌČ | őĶ ‚Č§ dist (őłŐā‚āô ŌČ) őł‚āÄ} ‚Üí 0` | **Consistency of extremum / M-estimators** (Newey‚ÄďMcFadden 1994, Thm 2.1; van der Vaart 1998, Thm 5.7). `őłŐā‚āô` maximizing the sample criterion `őł ‚Ü¶ P‚āô m(őł,¬∑)` is consistent for the population maximizer `őł‚āÄ`. **Consumes** the `GlivenkoCantelli` engine: the uniform-convergence hypothesis is exactly that predicate. Proof is the classical `M(őł‚āÄ) ‚ąí M(őłŐā‚āô) ‚Č§ 2¬∑sup_őł\|M‚āô‚ąíM\|` sandwich versus the separation gap `ő∑`. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `mEstimator_consistent_of_bracketing` | finite `L¬Ļ(P)` bracketing of `m` + argmax domination + well-separated max ‚áí consistency | **Bracketing headline corollary**: composes `glivenkoCantelli_of_hasL1Bracketing` with the consistency theorem, so a verifiable bracketing condition (instead of an assumed uniform LLN) yields consistency directly. **Proved, sorry-free.** |

### 12ep.d `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Equicontinuity/` ‚ÄĒ stochastic equicontinuity & the `StochEquicontAt` reduction

The empirical-process / Donsker layer at the **parametric** scale, discharging the
`StochEquicontAt` hypothesis consumed by `zEstimator_clt` and
`gmm_asymptotically_linear`. **Key structural finding:** `StochEquicontAt`
(fixed `őī`, inner limit `0`, gap evaluated at the *random* estimator `őłn`) is only
true when `őłn ‚Üí_p őł‚āÄ` ‚ÄĒ it secretly bundles consistency. The honest decomposition
is **class-level asymptotic equicontinuity (`AsymptoticEquicont`, independent of
`őłn`) + consistency ‚üĻ `StochEquicontAt`**. The uniform-in-`n` control of the
empirical process comes from the variance identity `Var(‚ąön(P‚āô‚ąíP)f) = Var_P f` via
Chebyshev ‚ÄĒ no CLT and no chaining for the base cases. All declarations below are
**proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**. `E` is a finite-dimensional real inner-product
space (the `Z`-estimator parameter/value space).

`Process.lean` ‚ÄĒ vocabulary:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empProcVec S f n` | `def`, `ő© ‚Üí E` | **Vector-valued centered empirical process** `G‚āô(f)(ŌČ) = (‚ąön)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ÄĘ ő£_{i<n} f(ZŠĶĘ ŌČ) ‚ąí ‚ąön ‚ÄĘ ‚ąę f dP`; the `E`-valued analogue of `empiricalProcess`. |
| `IIDSample.empProcVec_eq_stochEquicont_gap` | `rfl` bridge | `empProcVec` of the score difference `Ōą(őł,¬∑)‚ąíŌą(őł‚āÄ,¬∑)` equals the centered gap `R_n` of `StochEquicontAt`. |

`SecondMoment.lean` ‚ÄĒ the uniform-in-`n` Chebyshev workhorse:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.measurable_empProcVec` | `Measurable (fun ŌČ => empProcVec f n ŌČ)` | Measurability of the process in `ŌČ`. |
| `empProcVec_sq_lintegral_le` | `f ‚ąą L¬≤(P)` ‚áí `‚ąę‚ĀĽ ‚ÄĖG‚āô(f)‚ÄĖ¬≤ dőľ ‚Č§ ENNReal.ofReal (‚ąę ‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ¬≤ dP)` | **Second-moment / variance identity at the empirical scale**, uniformly in `n`. Proved coordinate-wise: Parseval (`OrthonormalBasis.sum_sq_inner_right`) reduces `‚ÄĖG‚āô(f)‚ÄĖ¬≤` to a sum of scalar centered i.i.d. sums, each bounded by `Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`. No CLT, no chaining. |
| `empProcVec_chebyshev` | `0<őĶ` ‚áí `őľ {‚ÄĖG‚āô(f)‚ÄĖ > őĶ} ‚Č§ ENNReal.ofReal (‚ąę ‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ¬≤ dP) / ENNReal.ofReal (őĶ¬≤)` | **Chebyshev bound** (second moment + Markov, `meas_ge_le_lintegral_div`). The uniform-in-`n` modulus that drives equicontinuity: as `‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P)}` shrinks, the tail shrinks at a rate independent of sample size. |

`Modulus.lean` ‚ÄĒ the class-level predicate and the reduction:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AsymptoticEquicont Ōą őł‚āÄ P őľ S` | `def`, `Prop` | **Class-level asymptotic equicontinuity** (the Donsker content, independent of any estimator): `‚ąÄ őĶ>0, ‚ąÄ ő∑>0, ‚ąÉ őī>0, ‚ąÄŠ∂† n, őľ {ŌČ | ‚ąÉ őł, ‚ÄĖőł‚ąíőł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ<őī ‚ąß őĶ < ‚ÄĖG‚āô(Ōą(őł,¬∑)‚ąíŌą(őł‚āÄ,¬∑))(ŌČ)‚ÄĖ} ‚Č§ ő∑`. Existential over `őł` (not a non-measurable `sup`) so outer-measure bookkeeping is automatic. |
| `empProcVec_atEstimator_tendsto_zero` | `AsymptoticEquicont` + `őłn ‚Üí_p őł‚āÄ` ‚áí `G‚āô(Ōą(őłn,¬∑)‚ąíŌą(őł‚āÄ,¬∑)) ‚Üí_p 0` (unconditional) | The empirical process at the estimator vanishes ‚ÄĒ textbook union bound (split on `{‚ÄĖőłn‚ąíőł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ‚Č•őī}` via consistency; on the complement the gap is witnessed by `őł=őłn`). |
| `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` | `AsymptoticEquicont` + `őłn ‚Üí_p őł‚āÄ` ‚áí `StochEquicontAt Ōą őł‚āÄ P őľ S őłn` | **The reduction.** Discharges the estimator-indexed `StochEquicontAt` hypothesis of `zEstimator_clt`/`gmm_asymptotically_linear` from the clean class-level Donsker property plus consistency. |
| `empProcVec_isLittleOp_of_L2` | deterministic `őłn` with `‚ąę ‚ÄĖŌą(őłn,¬∑)‚ąíŌą(őł‚āÄ,¬∑)‚ÄĖ¬≤ dP ‚Üí 0` ‚áí gap is `o_p(1)` | **Deterministic-curve equicontinuity** (non-vacuousness witness): direct from `empProcVec_chebyshev`, no chaining, no consistency. The base case certifying the `AsymptoticEquicont` machinery is satisfiable. |

`MaximalInequality.lean` ‚ÄĒ sub-exponential maximal inequality (chaining primitive):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.measure_abs_ge_le` | `őľ.real {őĶ ‚Č§ \|X\|} ‚Č§ 2 exp(‚ąíőĶ¬≤/(2(v+bőĶ)))` | Two-sided sub-exponential tail, from the one-sided `measure_ge_le` applied to `X` and `‚ąíX`. |
| `measure_exists_abs_ge_le` | finite family ‚áí `őľ.real {‚ąÉ i‚ąąt, őĶ ‚Č§ \|YŠĶĘ\|} ‚Č§ t.card ¬∑ 2 exp(‚ąíőĶ¬≤/(2(v+bőĶ)))` | **Maximal inequality** for the max of finitely many mean-zero sub-exponential variables (union bound). The reusable primitive a bracketing/chaining maximal inequality is built from. |
| `measure_sup'_ge_le` | same bound for `őľ.real {őĶ ‚Č§ t.sup' (fun i => \|YŠĶĘ\|)}` | `sup'` form of the maximal inequality. |

**Remaining frontier.** Discharging `AsymptoticEquicont` for an *infinite* class
over a continuum of `őł` (genuine bracketing-entropy / Ossiander chaining, van der
Vaart 1998 Lemma 19.34) is the real Mathlib gap ‚ÄĒ the `‚ąön` bracketing bias is
intrinsic and cannot be tamed without chaining (finite classes are trivially
equicontinuous). `MaximalInequality.lean` provides the single-scale primitive for
that future chaining argument.

### 12ep.e `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/CrossFitRate.lean` ‚ÄĒ conditional / cross-fit `O_p` rate

Paper-agnostic workhorse behind every cross-fit / DML "remainder-is-negligible"
step: the centered empirical mean of a (possibly cross-fit / conditional) score is
`O_p(n^{-1/2} ¬∑ (second-moment)^{1/2})`, stated over an arbitrary score and
ŌÉ-field. Reuses the existing `O_p` predicate `Causalean.Stat.IsBigOp` (no second
predicate) and the conditional second-moment engine
`Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`. The Markov "L¬≤-envelope ‚áí `O_p`"
step (previously inlined privately in `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`)
is extracted here as the reusable primitive. All declarations **proved, sorry-free,
axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsBigOp.mono_rate` | `0 ‚Č§ r‚āô ‚Č§ s‚āô`, `O_p(r‚āô)` ‚áí `O_p(s‚āô)` | **Weaken to a larger rate** ‚ÄĒ a larger envelope is a weaker statement. |
| `IsBigOp.scale_rate` | `c>0`, `O_p(c¬∑r‚āô)` ‚áí `O_p(r‚āô)` | **Absorb a positive constant rate factor** into the witness `M`. |
| `IsBigOp.const_rate_collapse` | `0 ‚Č§ N`, `O_p(fun _ => N)` ‚áí `O_p(fun _ => 1)` | **Collapse a fixed constant rate to the unit rate** (normalizes fold-sum `O_p(1)` conclusions). |
| `IsBigOp.const_mul` | `O_p(r‚āô)` ‚áí `c¬∑X‚āô = O_p(r‚āô)` | **Constant multiple** of the random sequence keeps the rate. |
| `IsBigOp.add'` | `O_p(r‚āô) + O_p(s‚āô) = O_p(r‚āô+s‚āô)` (nonneg rates) | **Additivity at the sum rate** (`IsBigOp.add` is the `r‚āô=s‚āô` special case). |
| `IsBigOp.of_abs_le` | pointwise `\|X‚āô\| ‚Č§ \|Y‚āô\|`, `Y‚āô = O_p(r‚āô)` ‚áí `X‚āô = O_p(r‚āô)` | **Domination rule** for stochastic big-O. |
| `IsBigOp.zero` | `0 = O_p(r‚āô)` | Constant-zero sequence is stochastically bounded at any rate. |
| `IsBigOp.finset_sum` | finite sum of terms each `O_p(r‚āô)` ‚áí sum is `O_p(r‚āô)` | Finite-sum bookkeeping for stochastic big-O; constants absorb the finite index set. |
| `IsBigOp.mul` | `0‚Č§r‚āô`, `0‚Č§s‚āô`, `X‚āô=O_p(r‚āô)`, `Y‚āô=O_p(s‚āô)` ‚áí `X‚āôY‚āô=O_p(r‚āôs‚āô)` | **Product rule** for stochastic big-O at two nonnegative rates. |
| `IsBigOp.of_sq_lintegral_le` | `X‚āô` a.e.-meas., `0 ‚Č§ V‚āô`, `‚ąę‚ĀĽ (X‚āô)¬≤ dőľ ‚Č§ V‚āô` ‚áí `X‚āô = O_p(‚ąöV‚āô)` | **The Markov primitive.** A deterministic second-moment envelope yields the matching `O_p` rate (Chebyshev: `őľ{M‚ąöV‚āô<\|X‚āô\|} ‚Č§ 1/M¬≤`). The reusable core every cross-fit rate proof consumes. |
| `isBigOp_centered_crossFit_sum` | eval set `s n` ‚ä• sub-ŌÉ-field `m_A n`, conditionally iid; score `g n` `(m_A n ‚äó ŌÉ_X)`-measurable & `L¬≤(P)`; deterministic `V‚āô ‚Č• ‚ąę_ő© ‚ÄĖg n ŌČ‚ÄĖ¬≤_{L¬≤(P)} dőľ` ‚áí centered fold sum `= O_p(‚ąöV‚āô)` | **Lemma B ‚ÄĒ the conditional / cross-fit lift** (the deliverable). `‚ąę g n ŌČ dP = ūĚĒľ[g\|m_A n](ŌČ)` is the conditional mean (eval fold ‚ä• `m_A n`), so the sum is `‚ąö\|s n\|¬∑(‚Ąô‚āôg ‚ąí ūĚĒľ[g\|m_A n])`. Second moment via the engine, rate via `of_sq_lintegral_le`. Estimand-agnostic; weak-overlap clipped-AIPW is the first consumer. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_sq_lintegral_le` | iid sample, `f ‚ąą L¬≤(P)`, `0<n` ‚áí `‚ąę‚ĀĽ (‚Ąô‚āôf ‚ąí ‚ąęf dP)¬≤ dőľ ‚Č§ E_P[f¬≤]/n` | **Lemma A second-moment bound.** `(1/‚ąön)ő£(f(ZŠĶĘ)‚ąím) = ‚ąön(‚Ąô‚āôf‚ąím)`, so the engine bound divides by `n`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_meas_ge_le` | `0<n`, `t>0` ‚áí `őľ{t ‚Č§ \|‚Ąô‚āôf ‚ąí ‚ąęf dP\|} ‚Č§ E_P[f¬≤]/(n¬∑t¬≤)` | **Chebyshev tail** for the centered sample mean (second-moment bound + Markov). |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_isBigOp` | iid sample, `f ‚ąą L¬≤(P)` ‚áí `(‚Ąô‚āôf ‚ąí ‚ąęf dP) = O_p(‚ąö(E_P[f¬≤]/n))` | **Lemma A ‚ÄĒ the unconditional `O_p` rate.** Feeding the centered statistic `f‚ąí‚ąęf` (whose `E_P[(¬∑)¬≤]=Var_P f`) gives the sharp `O_p(‚ąö(Var_P f/n))` form. The `(‚Ąô‚āô‚ąíP)ŌÜ^bd` consumer. |

## 12x. `Stat/UStatistic/` ‚ÄĒ U-statistics: Hoeffding decomposition, exact variance, and the CLT

The reusable statistical primitive behind variance estimators, rank/sign statistics,
and the higher-order influence-function estimators (¬ßHOIF). The development has two
layers with a one-way bridge:

- **Order-`m` (the general theory).** `OrderM/` builds the fixed-order U-statistic on
  ordered injective `m`-tuples, its H√°jek expansion, the exact order-`m` second moment,
  and ‚ÄĒ the **headline** ‚ÄĒ the fixed-order central limit theorem
  `uStatisticOrder_clt_of_regular` (`OrderM/CLT.lean`): a regular order-`m` U-statistic
  is `‚ąön`-asymptotically normal with the H√°jek variance `‚ąę Ōą¬≤ dP`. Its one genuinely
  probabilistic input, negligibility of the higher-order remainder, is discharged from
  a first-order-degeneracy `L¬≤ ‚Č§ C/n` keystone (`OrderM/FirstDegenKernel.lean` ‚Üí
  `OrderM/RemainderSecondMoment.lean` ‚Üí `OrderM/RemainderNegligible.lean`).
- **Order-2 (a thin `m = 2` face).** `Basic.lean`/`Variance.lean` keep the natural
  pairwise-kernel API (`h : X ‚Üí X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, off-diagonal `uStatistic`, `DegenKernel`, `ő∂`)
  that the HOIF estimators consume. The order-2 *variance* now routes through the
  order-`m` `ExactVariance` internally (via `pairKernel`); the order-2 CLT is recovered
  as the `m = 2` specialization `uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric_via_orderM`
  (`OrderM/OrderTwo.lean`).

_Per-declaration tables below are generated from docstrings ‚ÄĒ do not hand-edit; run `npm run doc:gen`. Edit the Lean docstring to change a description._

### 12x.1. `Stat/UStatistic/Basic.lean` ‚ÄĒ order-2 Hoeffding decomposition

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.Basic -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uStatistic` | `(X ‚Üí X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The order-2 U-statistic with kernel `h` over the first `n` sample points: `(n(n‚ąí1))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ő£_{(i,j) ‚ąą offDiag (range n)} h(ZŠĶĘ, Z‚Īľ)`. The off-diagonal sum ranges over ordered pairs `i ‚Č† j`; for a symmetric kernel this is the usual `(n choose 2)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ő£_{i<j}`. |
| `Stat.uMean` | `(X ‚Üí X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Population mean of the kernel: `őł = ‚ą¨ h dP dP`. |
| `Stat.uProj` | `(X ‚Üí X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure X ‚Üí X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | First Hoeffding projection (centered): `h‚āĀ(x) = ‚ąę h(x, ¬∑) dP ‚ąí őł`. |
| `Stat.uDegen` | `(X ‚Üí X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure X ‚Üí X ‚Üí X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Degenerate second-order kernel: `g(x, y) = h(x, y) ‚ąí őł ‚ąí h‚āĀ(x) ‚ąí h‚āĀ(y)`. |
| `Stat.hoeffding_decomp` | `h x y = Causalean.Stat.uMean h P + Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x + Causalean.Stat.uProj h P y + Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P x y` | Hoeffding decomposition (pointwise identity). `h(x, y) = őł + h‚āĀ(x) + h‚āĀ(y) + g(x, y)`. Purely algebraic. |
| `Stat.uProj_integral_eq_zero` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ‚ąę (y : X), h x y ‚ąāP) P ‚Üí ‚ąę (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x ‚ąāP = 0` | The first projection integrates to zero: `‚ąę h‚āĀ dP = 0`. |
| `Stat.uProj_integrable` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ‚ąę (y : X), h x y ‚ąāP) P ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.uProj h P) P` | The first projection is integrable whenever `x ‚Ü¶ ‚ąę h(x, ¬∑) dP` is. |
| `Stat.uDegen_integral_right_eq_zero` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ‚ąę (y : X), h x y ‚ąāP) P ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P ‚Üí ‚ąę (y : X), Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P x y ‚ąāP = 0` | Degeneracy of the second-order kernel. For every fixed `x`, `‚ąę g(x, ¬∑) dP = 0`. This is the defining property that makes the U-statistic's quadratic remainder negligible at the `‚ąön` scale. |

The bijection composes tuples with the codomain transposition swapping the two fibre values. |
| `Stat.sum_injectiveTuples_apply_eq_range` | `m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (j : Fin m) (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ), ‚ąĎ t ‚ąą Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n, f ‚ÜĎ(t j) = Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n / ‚ÜĎn * ‚ąĎ i ‚ąą Finset.range n, f i` | For each coordinate of an ordered injective tuple, every sample index appears equally often. |

| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_remainder_eq` | `m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí (fun ŌČ => ‚ąö‚ÜĎ(Finset.range n).card * (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n ŌČ - Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) - (‚ąö‚ÜĎ(Finset.range n).card)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * ‚ąĎ i ‚ąą Finset.range n, Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P (S.Z i ŌČ)) = fun ŌČ => ‚ąö‚ÜĎn * Causalean.Stat.uRemainderOrder S h n ŌČ` | The H√°jek remainder in `IsAsymLinear` form is the rescaled higher-order residual U-statistic. |

If the summed first projection is centered and square-integrable, and the higher-order residual is negligible at the `‚ąön` scale, then the fixed-order U-statistic is asymptotically linear with influence function equal to the sum of coordinatewise first projections. |

| Decl | Signature | Description |
