| `Stat.IIDSample.map_tuple_eq` | `Function.Injective t ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun ŌČ j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(t j)) ŌČ) őľ = MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P` | The joint law of an injectively indexed order-`m` sample tuple is `P^m`. |
| `Stat.OrderDegenKernel.integrable` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable g (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)` | A square-integrable order-`m` degenerate kernel is integrable under the product law. |
| `Stat.OrderDegenKernel.integral_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder g P = 0` | A fully degenerate order-`m` kernel has zero product-law mean. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integrable_orderKernelTerm` | `Measurable h ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable h (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {t : Fin m ‚Üí Fin n}, Function.Injective t ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ŌČ => h fun j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(t j)) ŌČ) őľ` | An order-`m` kernel term along an injective tuple is integrable whenever the kernel is integrable under the product law. |

### 12x.7. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/ExactVariance.lean` ‚ÄĒ exact order-`m` second moment

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.ExactVariance -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zeta_of_image_eq` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {n : ‚Ąē} {t q : Fin m ‚Üí Fin n}, Function.Injective t ‚Üí Function.Injective q ‚Üí Finset.image t Finset.univ = Finset.image q Finset.univ ‚Üí ‚ąę (ŌČ : ő©), (g fun j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(t j)) ŌČ) * g fun j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(q j)) ŌČ ‚ąāőľ = Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g` | Equal-image cross term. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples have the same image, then `q` is a reordering of `t`, so by symmetry `g(Z_q) = g(Z_t)` and the cross expectation is `ő∂_m = zetaOrder P g`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zero_of_image_ne` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {n : ‚Ąē} {t q : Fin m ‚Üí Fin n}, Function.Injective t ‚Üí Function.Injective q ‚Üí Finset.image t Finset.univ ‚Č† Finset.image q Finset.univ ‚Üí ‚ąę (ŌČ : ő©), (g fun j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(t j)) ŌČ) * g fun j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(q j)) ŌČ ‚ąāőľ = 0` | Distinct-image cross term. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples have different images, complete degeneracy kills the cross expectation: some index of `t` is absent from `q`, and integrating that coordinate out gives `0`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.card_injectiveTuples_image_eq` | `Function.Injective t ‚Üí {q ‚ąą Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n \| Finset.image q Finset.univ = Finset.image t Finset.univ}.card = m.factorial` | For an ordered injective `m`-tuple `t`, the ordered injective tuples with the same image are exactly its `m!` reorderings. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_injectiveTuples_sum_sq_degen` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), ‚ąę (ŌČ : ő©), (‚ąĎ t ‚ąą Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n, g fun j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(t j)) ŌČ) ^ 2 ‚ąāőľ = ‚ÜĎm.factorial * Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g` | Exact second moment of the injective-tuple sum. `E[(ő£_{t} g(Z_t))¬≤] = m! ¬∑ n^{(m)} ¬∑ ő∂_m`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_sq_degen` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {n : ‚Ąē}, m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ‚ąę (ŌČ : ő©), (‚ąö‚ÜĎn * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ŌČ) ^ 2 ‚ąāőľ = ‚ÜĎn * ‚ÜĎm.factorial * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g / Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n` | Exact variance of the rescaled degenerate fixed-order U-statistic. `E[(‚ąön ¬∑ U‚āô)¬≤] = n ¬∑ m! ¬∑ ő∂_m / n^{(m)}`. For `m = 2` this is `2ő∂ / (n‚ąí1)`. |

### 12x.9. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/RemainderSecondMoment.lean` ‚ÄĒ keystone `L¬≤ ‚Č§ C/n` bound

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.RemainderSecondMoment -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zero_of_shared_le_one` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {n : ‚Ąē} {t q : Fin m ‚Üí Fin n}, Function.Injective t ‚Üí Function.Injective q ‚Üí (Finset.image t Finset.univ ‚ą© Finset.image q Finset.univ).card ‚Č§ 1 ‚Üí ‚ąę (ŌČ : ő©), (g fun j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(t j)) ŌČ) * g fun j => S.Z (‚ÜĎ(q j)) ŌČ ‚ąāőľ = 0` | Cross-term vanishing. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples share at most one sample index, the expected product of the corresponding kernel terms is zero. Zero shared indices ‚áí independence + mean zero; one shared index ‚áí condition on it and use first-order degeneracy of each factor. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_sq_le` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g ‚Üí ‚ąÉ C, 0 ‚Č§ C ‚ąß ‚ąÄ {n : ‚Ąē}, m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ‚ąę (ŌČ : ő©), (‚ąö‚ÜĎn * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ŌČ) ^ 2 ‚ąāőľ ‚Č§ C / ‚ÜĎn` | `L¬≤` bound on the rescaled higher-order remainder. For a first-order degenerate order-`m` kernel there is a constant `C` (depending only on `m` and `ő∂_m = E[g¬≤]`) with `E[(‚ąön ¬∑ U‚āô)¬≤] ‚Č§ C / n` for all `n ‚Č• m`. This is the keystone estimate; it packages cross-term vanishing, the Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz bound `\|E[g_t g_q]\| ‚Č§ ő∂_m`, the `O(n^{2m-2})` count of surviving tuple pairs, and the `n ¬∑ (n^{(m)})‚ĀĽ¬≤` normalization. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.10. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/RemainderNegligible.lean` ‚ÄĒ negligibility discharge

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.RemainderNegligible -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {n : ‚Ąē}, m ‚Č§ n ‚Üí ‚ąę (ŌČ : ő©), ‚ąö‚ÜĎn * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n ŌČ ‚ąāőľ = 0` | The rescaled higher-order remainder has mean zero. |

Under first-order degeneracy, `‚ąön ¬∑ U‚āô ‚Üí 0` in probability, i.e. it is `o_p(1)`. Proof: `L¬≤` boundedness (`memLp_rescaled_sqKernel`) with mean zero and variance `‚Č§ C/n ‚Üí 0` (`integral_rescaled_order_sq_le`), via Chebyshev ‚ÄĒ mirror the order-2 `degenerateNegligible_of_degenKernel`. |
| `Stat.orderDegenerateNegligible_of_residual` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ‚ąę (tail : { k // k ‚Č† j } ‚Üí X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ‚ąāMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (j : Fin m), ‚ąę (x : X), ‚ąę (tail : { k // k ‚Č† j } ‚Üí X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ‚ąāMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P ‚ąāP = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (j : Fin m) (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun tail => h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail)) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)) ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible S h` | The higher-order remainder of a fixed-order U-statistic is negligible. For an order-`m` kernel `h` whose residual `uDegenOrder h P` is measurable and square-integrable and whose slice/Fubini integrability side conditions hold, the rescaled remainder `‚ąön ¬∑ G‚āô` is `o_p(1)`. This discharges the `OrderDegenerateNegligible` hypothesis consumed by the order-`m` CLT `uStatisticOrder_clt` (`Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.CLT`). |

### 12x.11. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/CLT.lean` ‚ÄĒ headline: fixed-order U-statistic CLT

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.CLT -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_clt` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) ‚Üí ‚ąę (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ‚ąāP = 0 ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2) P ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible S h ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (hőłn_meas : ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) őľ), (‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) őľ) ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) fun r => Finset.range r) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (‚ąę (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2 ‚ąāP)) őľ hőłn_meas` | Fixed-order U-statistic CLT. If the first-order influence function is measurable, centered, square-integrable, and the higher-order residual is negligible at the `‚ąön` scale, then the U-statistic is asymptotically normal with variance `‚ąę Ōą¬≤ dP`, where `Ōą` is the sum of coordinatewise first projections. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_clt_of_regular` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ‚ąę (tail : { k // k ‚Č† j } ‚Üí X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ‚ąāMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (j : Fin m), ‚ąę (x : X), ‚ąę (tail : { k // k ‚Č† j } ‚Üí X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) ‚ąāMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P ‚ąāP = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (j : Fin m) (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun tail => h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail)) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)) ‚Üí Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) ‚Üí ‚ąę (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ‚ąāP = 0 ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2) P ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (hőłn_meas : ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) őľ), (‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) őľ) ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) fun r => Finset.range r) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (‚ąę (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2 ‚ąāP)) őľ hőłn_meas` | Fixed-order U-statistic CLT (end-to-end). For an order-`m` kernel `h` whose residual is regular (measurable, square-integrable, with the slice/Fubini side conditions) and whose summed first projection is measurable, centered, and square-integrable, the fixed-order U-statistic is `‚ąön`-asymptotically normal with variance `‚ąę Ōą¬≤ dP`, `Ōą = ő£‚Īľ uProjOrderAt j h P`. This composes `uStatisticOrder_clt` with `orderDegenerateNegligible_of_residual`, so no negligibility hypothesis is required from the caller ‚ÄĒ the order-`m` analogue of `uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.12. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/OrderTwo.lean` ‚ÄĒ `m = 2` bridge to the order-2 CLT

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.OrderTwo -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uMeanOrder_pairKernel` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = Causalean.Stat.uMean h P` | The order-`2` population mean of `pairKernel h` is the order-2 mean of `h`. Requires product-integrability of `h` so that the joint integral over `Fin 2 ‚Üí X` agrees (via Fubini) with the iterated integral defining `uMean`. |
| `Stat.uProjOrderAt_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(‚ąÄ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (j : Fin 2), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = Causalean.Stat.uProj h P` | Each coordinate first projection of `pairKernel h` equals the order-2 first projection of `h`, for a symmetric kernel. (For `j = 0` no symmetry is needed; for `j = 1` it is used to swap the integration coordinate.) |
| `Stat.uInfluenceOrder_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(‚ąÄ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x` | The order-`2` influence function of `pairKernel h` is `2 ¬∑ h‚āĀ`, matching the order-2 CLT influence function `Ōą = 2 ¬∑ uProj h P`. |
| `Stat.uDegenOrder_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(‚ąÄ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P (z 0) (z 1)` | The order-`2` degenerate residual of `pairKernel h` is the order-2 degenerate kernel `uDegen h P`, evaluated at the two coordinates. |
| `Stat.uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric_via_orderM` | `(‚ąÄ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) ‚Üí (Measurable fun p => h p.1 p.2) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P p.1 p.2 ^ 2) (P.prod P) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ‚ąę (y : X), h x y ‚ąāP) P ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) ‚Üí MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x ^ 2) P ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (hőłn_meas : ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMean h P) (fun m => Finset.range m) n) őľ), (‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x) (fun m => Finset.range m) n) őľ) ‚Üí Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMean h P) fun m => Finset.range m) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (‚ąę (x : X), (fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x) x ^ 2 ‚ąāP)) őľ hőłn_meas` | Order-2 U-statistic CLT, derived from the fixed-order-`m` CLT. For a symmetric, square-integrable kernel `h` with finite first-projection variance `ő∂‚āĀ = ‚ąę h‚āĀ¬≤ dP`, the order-2 U-statistic is `‚ąön`-asymptotically normal, `‚ąön (U‚āô ‚ąí őł) ‚áí N(0, ‚ąę (2 h‚āĀ)¬≤ dP) = N(0, 4 ő∂‚āĀ)`. |

## 12wo. `Stat/PolynomialTail/` ‚ÄĒ Polynomial lower-tail inverse-moment asymptotics

Causal-agnostic real-analysis / measure-theory infrastructure for the small-value
(lower-tail) asymptotics of a bounded positive random variable.  This is the moment
calculus underlying *any* inverse-weighted estimand whose weight is floored at `1/őĽ` ‚ÄĒ
not tied to one paper.  It specializes to (and quantitatively refines) the qualitative
`Overlap`/positivity assumption in `Identification/`: taking `U = min(e(X), 1‚ąíe(X))`
recovers the variance/rate ingredients of IPW, AIPW, and H√°jek estimators under thin
overlap, trimming/truncation bias, and weak-overlap minimax rates.  Fix a probability
measure `P` and a measurable `U : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with `0 < U ‚Č§ 1` a.s.  The two-sided
polynomial lower-tail hypothesis `PolyTail P U őļ t‚āÄ cm cp` says
`cm¬∑tŠĶŹ ‚Č§ P{U ‚Č§ t} ‚Č§ cp¬∑tŠĶŹ` on `(0,t‚āÄ]` (`0 < őļ`, `0 < t‚āÄ < 1`, `0 < cm < cp`).  The
library bounds the two truncated inverse moments (`őĽ`-truncation, `0 < őĽ ‚Č§ t‚āÄ`)

    I P U őĽ = ‚ąę U/(max U őĽ)¬≤   (= 1/U on {U‚Č•őĽ}, U/őĽ¬≤ on {U<őĽ}),
    J P U őĽ = ‚ąę (max U őĽ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ    (= 1/U on {U‚Č•őĽ}, 1/őĽ  on {U<őĽ}),

and the leftover trimming weight `wőĽ = 1 ‚ąí U/(max U őĽ) ‚ąą [0,1]` (supported on `{U<őĽ}`).
The key trichotomy is in the tail exponent `őļ`: `őĽ^{őļ-1}` (őļ<1), `log(1/őĽ)` (őļ=1),
bounded (őļ>1).  The lower bounds genuinely use **both** tail constants (the gap
`cm < cp` breaks any one-sided argument).  Method: Mathlib's layer-cake theorem
`MeasureTheory.Integrable.integral_eq_integral_Ioc_meas_le` reduces `J` to a
threshold integral of `P{U ‚Č§ t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ}`, evaluated against `‚ąę t^{-őļ}` (`integral_rpow` /
`integral_inv`).  All declarations **sorry-free and axiom-clean**.

### 12wo.1. `Stat/PolynomialTail/Basic.lean` ‚ÄĒ setup, integrands, basic facts

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PolyTail P U őļ t‚āÄ cm cp` | `structure : Prop` | The polynomial lower-tail hypothesis: `cm¬∑tŠĶŹ ‚Č§ P{U‚Č§t} ‚Č§ cp¬∑tŠĶŹ` on `(0,t‚āÄ]`, with `0<őļ`, `0<t‚āÄ<1`, `0<cm<cp`. Fields `kappa_pos/t0_pos/t0_lt_one/cm_pos/cm_lt_cp/tail_lower/tail_upper`. |
| `PolyTail.cp_pos` | `0 < cp` | Upper constant positive. |
| `TailSetup P U` | `structure : Prop` | Structural hypotheses on `U`: `Measurable U`, `0 < U` a.s., `U ‚Č§ 1` a.s. |
| `truncDen U őĽ ŌČ` | `def := max (U ŌČ) őĽ` | Truncated denominator. |
| `invMomentI P U őĽ` | `def := ‚ąę U/(max U őĽ)¬≤` | Truncated inverse second moment. |
| `invMomentJ P U őĽ` | `def := ‚ąę (max U őĽ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` | Truncated inverse first moment. |
| `trimWeight U őĽ ŌČ` | `def := 1 ‚ąí U/(max U őĽ)` | Leftover weight `‚ąą[0,1]`, supported on `{U<őĽ}`. |
| `measurable_*` | measurability | The three integrands and the denominator are measurable. |
| `measureReal_lt_le` | `P{U<őĽ} ‚Č§ cp¬∑őĽŠĶŹ` | Mass of the open lower level set (monotonicity + upper tail). |
| `invMomentI_integrand_mem` / `invMomentJ_integrand_mem` / `trimWeight_mem` | pointwise `‚ąą[0,1/őĽ¬≤]` / `[0,1/őĽ]` / `[0,1]` | Pointwise integrand sandwiches. |
| `integrable_invMomentI_integrand` / `‚Ä¶J‚Ä¶` / `integrable_trimWeight` | `Integrable ‚Ä¶ P` | Integrability (bounded a.e. on a probability space). |

### 12wo.2. `Stat/PolynomialTail/LayerCakeReduction.lean` ‚ÄĒ layer-cake reduction

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tailIntegral P U őĽ` | `def := ‚ąę t in (1,őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ], P{U ‚Č§ t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ}` | The threshold ("tail") integral. |
| `monotone_cdf` | `Monotone (s ‚Ü¶ P{U‚Č§s})` | The lower CDF is monotone. |
| `measurable_tailIntegrand`, `tailIntegrand_nonneg`, `tailIntegrand_le_one`, `integrableOn_tailIntegrand` | regularity | Measurability/`‚ąą[0,1]`/integrability of `t ‚Ü¶ P{U‚Č§t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ}`. |
| `invMomentJ_eq_one_add_tailIntegral` | `J P U őĽ = 1 + tailIntegral P U őĽ` (`0<őĽ<1`) | **Master layer-cake reduction.** Levels `t‚Č§1` contribute exactly `1` (`(max U őĽ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚Č• 1` a.s.); levels `(1,őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]` reproduce `P{U‚Č§t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ}`. |
| `tailIntegral_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ tailIntegral P U őĽ` | Nonnegativity. |

### 12wo.3. `Stat/PolynomialTail/PowerIntegral.lean` ‚ÄĒ power-law interval integrals

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `zero_notMem_uIcc` | `0 ‚ąČ [[a,b]]` (`0<a‚Č§b`) | Side condition for `integral_rpow`/`integral_inv`. |
| `integral_rpow_neg_Ioc` | `‚ąę t in (a,b], t^{-őļ} = (b^{1-őļ}‚ąía^{1-őļ})/(1‚ąíőļ)` (`őļ‚Č†1`, `0<a‚Č§b`) | Power integral via `integral_rpow`. |
| `integral_inv_neg_Ioc` | `‚ąę t in (a,b], t^{-1} = log b ‚ąí log a` (`0<a‚Č§b`) | `őļ=1` case via `integral_inv`. |

### 12wo.4. `Stat/PolynomialTail/TailIntegralBounds.lean` ‚ÄĒ tail-integral sandwich

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `inv_rpow_eq_rpow_neg` | `(t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)^őļ = t^{-őļ}` (`t>0`) | rpow identity. |
| `tailIntegrand_upper_window` / `tailIntegrand_lower_window` | `cm¬∑t^{-őļ} ‚Č§ P{U‚Č§t‚ĀĽ¬Ļ} ‚Č§ cp¬∑t^{-őļ}` on `(t‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ,őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]` | Polynomial-window integrand sandwich. |
| `integrableOn_rpow_neg_window` | `IntegrableOn (t^{-őļ}) (t‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ,őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]` | Integrability of the power integrand. |
| `tailIntegral_le` | `tailIntegral ‚Č§ (t‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ‚ąí1) + cp¬∑‚ąę_{(t‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ,őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]} t^{-őļ}` | **Upper** sandwich (bounded shell `(1,t‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]` + polynomial window). |
| `tailIntegral_ge` | `cm¬∑‚ąę_{(t‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ,őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]} t^{-őļ} ‚Č§ tailIntegral` | **Lower** sandwich. |

### 12wo.5. `Stat/PolynomialTail/MomentJBounds.lean` ‚ÄĒ three-regime bounds for `J`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `powInt_ne_one` | `‚ąę_{(t‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ,őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ]} t^{-őļ} = (őĽ^{őļ-1}‚ąít‚āÄ^{őļ-1})/(1‚ąíőļ)` (`őļ‚Č†1`) | Regime power-integral value. |
| `powInt_eq_one` | `‚Ä¶ = log(1/őĽ) ‚ąí log(1/t‚āÄ)` (`őļ=1`) | Regime power-integral value. |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_lt` | `‚ąÉ 0<a‚Č§A, ‚ąÄőĽ‚ąą(0,t‚āÄ], a¬∑őĽ^{őļ-1} ‚Č§ J ‚Č§ A¬∑őĽ^{őļ-1}` (`őļ<1`) | **őļ<1.** Constants depend only on `(őļ,cm,cp,t‚āÄ)`: `A=t‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ+cp/(1‚ąíőļ)`, `a=min(cm/(1‚ąíőļ), t‚āÄ^{1‚ąíőļ})`. |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_eq` | `‚ąÉ 0<a‚Č§A, a¬∑log(1/őĽ) ‚Č§ J ‚Č§ A¬∑log(1/őĽ)+A` (`őļ=1`) | **őļ=1.** |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_gt` | `‚ąÉ 0<a‚Č§A, a ‚Č§ J ‚Č§ A` (`őļ>1`) | **őļ>1.** ‚ÄĒ inverse first moment is *bounded* (`a=1` since `J‚Č•1`). |

### 12wo.6. `Stat/PolynomialTail/MomentIBounds.lean` ‚ÄĒ bounds for `I` + IsTheta

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `invMomentI_le_invMomentJ` | `I P U őĽ ‚Č§ J P U őĽ` | Pointwise `U/(max U őĽ)¬≤ ‚Č§ (max U őĽ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`. Gives the **upper** half of the `I` bounds from the `J` bounds. |
| `invMomentJ_sub_invMomentI_le` | `J P U őĽ ‚ąí I P U őĽ ‚Č§ cp¬∑őĽ^{őļ-1}` | Gap controlled by tail mass: pointwise `= wőĽ/(max U őĽ) ‚Č§ őĽ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑wőĽ`, and `‚ąęwőĽ ‚Č§ P{U<őĽ} ‚Č§ cp¬∑őĽŠĶŹ`. The bridge for the `őļ=1` `I`-lower bound. |
| `invMomentI_antitone` | `I P U őĽ‚āā ‚Č§ I P U őĽ‚āĀ` (`őĽ‚āĀ‚Č§őĽ‚āā`) | `I` decreases as `őĽ` grows (`max U őĽ` grows). Bridge for the `őļ>1` constant `I`-floor. |
| `invMomentI_ge_shell` | `(cm/2)¬∑(cm/(2cp))^{1/őļ}¬∑őĽ^{őļ-1} ‚Č§ I P U őĽ` | **Shell lower bound.** The shell `(ŌĀőĽ,őĽ]`, `ŌĀ=(cm/(2cp))^{1/őļ}`, has mass `‚Č•(cm/2)őĽŠĶŹ` (uses **both** tails) on which `U/őĽ¬≤ ‚Č• ŌĀ/őĽ`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_lt` | `‚ąÉ 0<a‚Č§A, ‚ąÄőĽ‚ąą(0,t‚āÄ], a¬∑őĽ^{őļ-1} ‚Č§ I ‚Č§ A¬∑őĽ^{őļ-1}` (`őļ<1`) | Lower = shell, upper = `I ‚Č§ J`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_eq` | `‚ąÉ 0<a‚Č§A, a¬∑log(1/őĽ) ‚Č§ I ‚Č§ A¬∑log(1/őĽ)+A` (`őļ=1`) | Lower = `I ‚Č• J ‚ąí cp` (relation) uniformized with the shell floor; upper = `I ‚Č§ J`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_gt` | `‚ąÉ 0<a‚Č§A, a ‚Č§ I ‚Č§ A` (`őļ>1`) | Bounded inverse second moment: lower `I(őĽ)‚Č•I(t‚āÄ)‚Č•` shell-at-`t‚āÄ` (antitone); upper = `I ‚Č§ J`. |
| `isTheta_of_two_sided` | two-sided bound ‚üĻ `f =őė[ūĚďĚ[>]0] g` | Generic `IsTheta` packager. |
| `invMomentJ_isTheta_lt` / `invMomentI_isTheta_lt` | `J ‚Čć őĽ^{őļ-1}`, `I ‚Čć őĽ^{őļ-1}` (`őļ<1`) | **IsTheta** on `ūĚďĚ[>] 0`. |
| `invMomentJ_isTheta_gt` / `invMomentI_isTheta_gt` | `J ‚Čć 1`, `I ‚Čć 1` (`őļ>1`) | **IsTheta** (bounded regime). |

### 12wo.7. `Stat/PolynomialTail/TrimBias.lean` ‚ÄĒ general bias bound

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `trimWeight_bias_bound` | `\|‚ąę wőĽ¬∑g ‚ąāP\| ‚Č§ Cő≤¬∑cp¬∑őĽ^{őļ+ő≤}` | For `g` with H√∂lder envelope `\|g\|‚Č§Cő≤¬∑őĽ^ő≤` on `{U<őĽ}`: tail-mass (`‚Č§cp¬∑őĽŠĶŹ`) √ó envelope. Elementary (no layer cake). |

## 12'. `Stat/Concentration/` ‚ÄĒ Vendored concentration toolbox (Phase-4a)

Selectively vendored from
[auto-res/lean-rademacher](https://github.com/auto-res/lean-rademacher)
(commit `72d28921dc960f47691640fb973303a1be9d13ca`, MIT License). Audit
trail and porting policy live in
[Causalean/Stat/Concentration/UPSTREAM.md](../Causalean/Stat/Concentration/UPSTREAM.md).
Phase 4a covers definitions and the headline statements only; lower-level
proof chains are scaffolded with `sorry` and filled by Codex in a
separate pass.

#### 12'.a `Stat/Concentration/Rademacher.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Signs n` | `def`, `Type` | `{‚ąí1, +1}`-valued sign vectors of length `n`. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity n f S` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | `(1 / 2‚ĀŅ) ő£_ŌÉ supŠĶĘ |(1/n) ő£‚āĖ ŌÉ k ¬∑ f i (S k)|`. |
| `rademacherComplexity n f őľ X` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Population version: `E[empiricalRademacherComplexity n f (X ‚ąė ŌČ)]` under `ŌČ ‚ąľ őľ‚ĀŅ`. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_without_abs` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Variant without the outer absolute value (used in some symmetrization steps). |
| `uniformDeviation n f őľ X S` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | `supŠĶĘ |(1/n) ő£‚āĖ f i (S k) ‚ąí E[f i ‚ąė X]|`. |
| `uniformDeviation_measurable` | `theorem` | Measurability of `uniformDeviation` for countable function classes with each `f i` measurable. **Proved.** |

#### 12'.a' `Stat/Concentration/ERMOracle.lean` ‚ÄĒ generic ERM oracle inequality

The method-agnostic learning-theory rate: empirical risk minimization over a countable
hypothesis class with bounded loss has excess risk controlled by the Rademacher complexity of
the loss class plus a McDiarmid tail. The engine that turns a complexity bound for a specific
method's class into a concrete excess-risk rate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `erm_excess_le_two_uniformDeviation` | `theorem` | **Deterministic ERM basic inequality:** an empirical minimizer beating a comparator on the sample has excess population risk `‚Č§ 2¬∑uniformDeviation`. **Proved.** |
| `erm_oracle_inequality` | `theorem` | **Oracle inequality:** `őľ‚ĀŅ{ 4¬∑ūĚĒĮ‚āô + 2őĶ < R(ń•) ‚ąí R(h‚čÜ) } ‚Č§ exp(‚ąíőĶ¬≤ t n)`, chaining the basic inequality with FoML's symmetrization + McDiarmid tail (`ūĚĒĮ‚āô` = Rademacher complexity of the loss class). **Proved.** |
| `erm_oracle_inequality_separable` | `theorem` | Separable-class version (`SeparableSpace`/`FirstCountableTopology őĻ`, loss continuous in the parameter) ‚ÄĒ the form that applies to the uncountable-but-separable L¬≤/L¬Ļ-ball linear classes (kernel, lasso). **Proved.** |

pseudometric function space.

| `dudley_entropy_integral_bound` | Same bound with the public sample-size parameter name `n` | Headline wrapper ported from FoML `Main.lean`. |

Elementary real-analysis evaluation used to simplify Dudley entropy-integral

| `log_div_nonneg_of_mem_Icc` | `0 < őĶ`, `x ‚ąą [őĶ, őī]` ‚Üí `0 ‚Č§ log (őī / x)` | Positivity of the logarithmic ratio kernel on the integration interval. |
| `integral_log_div_eq` | `0 < őĶ`, `őĶ ‚Č§ őī` ‚Üí `‚ąę x in őĶ..őī, log (őī / x) = őī - őĶ - őĶ * log (őī / őĶ)` | Exact closed-form evaluation of the logarithmic ratio integral. |
| `integral_log_div_le` | `0 < őĶ`, `őĶ ‚Č§ őī` ‚Üí `‚ąę x in őĶ..őī, log (őī / x) ‚Č§ őī - őĶ` | Interval-length upper bound obtained from `log (őī / őĶ) ‚Č• 0`. |
| `sqrtLog_integral_le` | `0 < őĶ`, `őĶ ‚Č§ őī` ‚Üí `‚ąę x in őĶ..őī, sqrt (log (őī / x)) ‚Č§ őī` | Dudley entropy-integral evaluation using the AM-GM pointwise bound and the logarithmic-ratio integral estimate. |

VC-dimension control of empirical covering numbers for real-valued classes
whose sample evaluations factor through a Boolean class.

| `growthFamily` | `(őĻ ‚Üí ūĚí≥ ‚Üí Bool) ‚Üí (Fin n ‚Üí ūĚí≥) ‚Üí Finset (Finset (Fin n))` | The finite family of Boolean restriction patterns realized on the sample. |
| `card_growthFamily_le_sum_choose` | `ūĚíú.vcDim ‚Č§ d ‚Üí ūĚíú.card ‚Č§ ‚ąĎ k ‚ąą Finset.Iic d, n.choose k` | Sauer-Shelah cardinality bound for a finite family on an `n`-point sample. |
| `vc_coveringNumber_le_growth` | Binary factorization of `F` through `ŌÄ` on the sample and `őĶ > 0` ‚Üí `coveringNumber h' őĶ ‚Č§ (growthFamily ŌÄ S).card` | Bounds the empirical covering number by choosing one representative per realized Boolean pattern. |
| `vc_coveringNumber_le_sum_choose` | Same hypotheses plus `(growthFamily ŌÄ S).vcDim ‚Č§ d` ‚Üí `coveringNumber h' őĶ ‚Č§ ‚ąĎ k ‚ąą Finset.Iic d, n.choose k` | Combines the finite self-cover with Sauer-Shelah. |
| `sum_choose_le_exp_mul_div_pow` | `0 < d`, `d ‚Č§ n` ‚Üí `‚ąĎ_{k‚Č§d} (n choose k) ‚Č§ (e n / d)^d` | Standard binomial-sum estimate; currently the only intentionally scaffolded arithmetic lemma in this file. |
| `log_coveringNumber_le` | Nonempty class, binary factorization, `őĶ > 0`, `0 < d`, `d ‚Č§ n`, and VC dimension at most `d` ‚Üí logarithmic covering bound | Converts the VC covering bound into the usual entropy display using the standard binomial-sum estimate. |

#### 12'.e4b `Stat/Concentration/HausslerPacking.lean`

Haussler-style finite-VC packing bounds for weighted Boolean Hamming metrics,
isolating the random-coordinate extraction core from the deterministic
Sauer-Shelah cardinality step.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `weightedHammingSq` | Coordinate weights and two Boolean vectors on `Fin n` ‚Üí weighted Hamming pseudo-distance squared | Sums the weights of coordinates where the two Boolean vectors differ. |
| `subsamplePattern` | Coordinate map `Fin m ‚Üí Fin n` and Boolean vector ‚Üí `Finset (Fin m)` | Records the true coordinates after restricting a Boolean vector to a sampled coordinate list. |
| `weightedHammingSq_nonneg` | Nonnegative weights ‚Üí `0 ‚Č§ weightedHammingSq w a b` | Basic nonnegativity of the weighted Hamming pseudo-distance squared. |
| `card_subsample_family_le_succ_pow` | Restricted trace VC dimension at most `d` ‚Üí restricted trace cardinality at most `(m + 1)^d` | Applies Sauer-Shelah and a binomial-sum bound to a sampled coordinate family. |
| `subsample_image_vcDim_le` | Original true-set family VC dimension at most `d` ‚Üí every coordinate restriction has VC dimension at most `d` | Shows restriction along any coordinate map cannot increase VC dimension. |
| `finite_averaging_exists_separating_subsample` | Total weight at most `r¬≤`, `őĶ¬≤`-separation, `2 ‚Č§ #P`, and `0 < őĶ ‚Č§ r` ‚Üí a separating restricted trace with `m ‚Č§ 1 + (2*r¬≤/őĶ¬≤)*log #P` | Isolates the finite product-sum averaging step for the random-coordinate extraction; currently the remaining `sorry` in this module. |
| `exists_separating_subsample` | VC control, total weight at most `r¬≤`, `őĶ¬≤`-separation, `2 ‚Č§ #P`, and `0 < őĶ ‚Č§ r` ‚Üí a separating restricted trace with `m ‚Č§ 1 + (2*r¬≤/őĶ¬≤)*log #P` and restricted VC dimension at most `d` | Combines finite averaging with VC-dimension preservation for sampled coordinate families. |
| `self_log_solve` | `L ‚Č§ d*log(2 + a*L)` with `d ‚Č• 1`, `a ‚Č• 2`, and `L ‚Č• 0` ‚Üí an explicit `1 + 2*d*log(2*a*d)` bound | Scalar self-logarithm inequality used after Sauer-Shelah. |
| `vc_weightedHamming_packing_card_le` | VC control, total weight at most `r¬≤`, and `őĶ¬≤`-separation ‚Üí `log #P ‚Č§ 1 + 2*d*log(4*d*r¬≤/őĶ¬≤)` | Logarithmic Haussler őĶ-packing bound with no dependence on the ambient coordinate count, modulo the isolated extraction lemma. |
| `vc_weightedHamming_log_packing_le` | Same hypotheses ‚Üí `log #P ‚Č§ 1 + 2*d*log(4*d*r¬≤/őĶ¬≤)` | Alias for the clean logarithmic weighted VC packing bound. |

#### 12'.e4c `Stat/Concentration/VCLocalizedCovering.lean`

Metric bridges connecting binary-factored real classes with the weighted
Boolean Hamming geometry used in localized finite-VC covering arguments.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `empiricalDist_sq_eq_weightedHammingSq` | Binary factorization `F i (S j) = ŌÜ j (ŌÄ i (S j))` ‚Üí squared empirical L¬≤ distance equals weighted Hamming squared distance with weights `(ŌÜ j true ‚ąí ŌÜ j false)¬≤/n` | Converts empirical metric separation for a binary-factored real class into weighted Boolean trace separation on the same sample. |

#### 12'.e5 `Stat/Concentration/VCLocalizedRegime.lean`

Localized finite-VC Rademacher regime used by the sharp localized uniform
deviation layer.  The elementary sub-root and critical-radius algebra is proved
directly.  The remaining Massart finite-realization construction is isolated as
an explicit analytic sublemma rather than packaged as a hypothesis field.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BinaryFactoredVCClass` | Real-valued class `F` factors through a Boolean class whose sample growth family has VC dimension at most `d` | Packages the binary trace substrate used by VC covering arguments. |
| `BinaryTraceEntropyControl` | Either samplewise VC-dimension control or direct `(m + 1)^d` growth-cardinality control | Common entropy input for the localized star-hull theorem. |
| `LocalizedVCDudleyHypotheses` | Empirical radius and total boundedness fields | Deterministic hypothesis bundle for the sample-path localized Dudley step. |
| `localizedVCDudleyHypotheses_of_empiricalRadius` | Bounded binary-factored finite-VC class plus an explicit samplewise empirical-radius bridge ‚Üí `LocalizedVCDudleyHypotheses` | Constructor for the localized Dudley hypothesis bundle. For population L¬≤ localization norms, the empirical-radius bridge remains a real extra obligation; boundedness alone does not give deterministic empirical-radius control on every sample. |
| `starHullZeroOut_empirical_rademacher_massart_vc` | Binary trace entropy + localized hypotheses ‚Üí Massart finite-realization bound with rate `r * sqrt((2 d log(n+1)+2)/n)` | Isolates the remaining analytic finite-pattern Massart construction. |
| `absolute_dudley_vc_starHullZeroOut_linear_residual_shared` | Same localized star-hull bound with either VC-dimension or direct growth-cardinality trace control, assuming `K ‚Č• 1` | Shared residual used by the downstream VC localized bounds. |
| `vcLocalizedRademacherUpperBound` | Binary-factored finite-VC class + localized hypotheses ‚Üí local Rademacher upper bound and critical-radius rate | Headline localized finite-VC Rademacher envelope. |
| `vcLocalizedEnvelope` | Finite-VC localized assumptions ‚Üí `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp` envelope | Connects the finite-VC Rademacher regime to the localized uniform-deviation theorem. |

#### 12'.f `Stat/Concentration/Hoeffding.lean`

Causalean-facing corollaries of Mathlib's sub-Gaussian framework
(`Mathlib.Probability.Moments.SubGaussian`) specialised to the `IIDSample`
model. All declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.map_eq` | `lemma` | Each sample point has law `P`: `őľ.map (S.Z i) = P`. |
| `IIDSample.integral_comp_eq` | `lemma` | Population-mean identity `‚ąę f (S.Z i) ‚ąāőľ = ‚ąę f ‚ąāP` for measurable `f`. |
| `IIDSample.iIndepFun_comp` | `lemma` | Independence of the composed family `fun i ‚Ü¶ f ‚ąė S.Z i` from `S.indep`. |
| `hoeffding_ge` | `theorem` | One-sided Hoeffding: for `f` valued in `[a,b]` (`a<b`) `P`-a.e., `P(XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí E[f] ‚Č• őĶ) ‚Č§ exp(‚ąí2nőĶ¬≤/(b‚ąía)¬≤)`. |
| `Concentration.measureReal_abs_dev_le_two_sided` | `lemma` | Generic two-sided assembly: from one-sided bounds `őľ.real{őĶ ‚Č§ T‚ąím} ‚Č§ B` and `őľ.real{őĶ ‚Č§ ‚ąíT+m} ‚Č§ B`, concludes `őľ.real{őĶ ‚Č§ |T‚ąím|} ‚Č§ 2B`. Shared by `hoeffding_abs_ge` and `bernstein_abs_ge` (the former duplicated union+calc boilerplate). |
| `hoeffding_abs_ge` | `theorem` | Two-sided Hoeffding: `P(|XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí E[f]| ‚Č• őĶ) ‚Č§ 2¬∑exp(‚ąí2nőĶ¬≤/(b‚ąía)¬≤)`. Two-sided assembly via `measureReal_abs_dev_le_two_sided`. |

#### 12'.f' `Stat/Concentration/TailBounds/BinomialCount.lean`

Multiplicative one-sided tail bounds for Bernoulli counts observed through the
common `IIDSample` interface.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.Concentration.TailBounds.BinomialCount -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.Concentration.bernoulliCount` | `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample ő© ūĚí≥ őľ P ‚Üí (ūĚí≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The number of the first `m` observations on which `f` is one. |
| `Stat.Concentration.bernoulliCount_measurable` | `Measurable f ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (m : ‚Ąē), Measurable (Causalean.Stat.Concentration.bernoulliCount S f m)` | The number of the first `m` observations on which `f` is one. |
| `Stat.Concentration.bernoulliCount_upper_tail` | `Measurable f ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (x : ūĚí≥), f x = 0 ‚ą® f x = 1) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {p a : ‚ĄĚ}, ‚ąę (x : ūĚí≥), f x ‚ąāP = p ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {m : ‚Ąē}, ‚ÜĎm * p < a / 2 ‚Üí őľ.real {ŌČ \| a < Causalean.Stat.Concentration.bernoulliCount S f m ŌČ} ‚Č§ Real.exp (-a * (Real.log 2 - 1 / 2))` | Upper multiplicative tail for an i.i.d. Bernoulli count. If its mean `m * p` is below half the threshold `a`, then the probability of exceeding `a` is at most `exp (-a * (log 2 - 1/2))`. |
| `Stat.Concentration.bernoulliCount_lower_tail` | `Measurable f ‚Üí (‚ąÄ (x : ūĚí≥), f x = 0 ‚ą® f x = 1) ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {p a : ‚ĄĚ}, 0 ‚Č§ p ‚Üí ‚ąę (x : ūĚí≥), f x ‚ąāP = p ‚Üí ‚ąÄ {m : ‚Ąē}, 2 * a < ‚ÜĎm * p ‚Üí őľ.real {ŌČ \| Causalean.Stat.Concentration.bernoulliCount S f m ŌČ ‚Č§ a} ‚Č§ Real.exp (-(‚ÜĎm * p) / 8)` | Lower multiplicative tail for an i.i.d. Bernoulli count. If its mean `m * p` is more than twice the threshold `a`, then the probability of being at most `a` is at most `exp (-(m*p)/8)`. |
