#### 12'.g `Stat/Concentration/SubExponential.lean`

Sub-exponential MGF framework mirroring Mathlib's sub-Gaussian design, in the
`(v, b)` parameterization (validity interval encoded as `b¬∑|t| < 1`, so `b = 0`
recovers the sub-Gaussian/all-`t` case). All declarations **proved, sorry-free,
axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasSubexponentialMGF X v b őľ` | `structure` | `exp(t¬∑X)` integrable and `mgf X őľ t ‚Č§ exp(v t¬≤/2)` for every `t` with `b¬∑|t| < 1`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.isFiniteMeasure` | `lemma` | A sub-exponential variable lives on a finite measure. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.neg` | `lemma` | Closure under negation (same `(v,b)`). |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.congr` | `lemma` | Transfer along an a.e.-equality of variables. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.const_mul` | `lemma` | Scaling `r¬∑X` has parameters `(r¬≤v, |r|b)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.add_of_indepFun` | `lemma` | Independent variables add: `X+Y` has parameters `(vX+vY, max bX bY)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.zero` | `lemma` | The constant `0` is sub-exponential with parameters `(0, b)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.measure_ge_le` | `theorem` | Chernoff tail `P(X ‚Č• őĶ) ‚Č§ exp(‚ąíőĶ¬≤/(2(v + bőĶ)))` (single explicit minimizer `t = őĶ/(v+2bőĶ)`). |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.sum_range_of_iIndepFun` | `lemma` | A sum of `n` independent variables with common `(v,b)` is sub-exponential with `(n‚ÄĘv, b)`. |

#### 12'.h Mathlib sub-Gaussian re-use index

The sub-Gaussian half of the toolbox is consumed directly from Mathlib's
`ProbabilityTheory` namespace (no Causalean duplication); the Causalean wrappers above
build on it. Key upstream names:

| Mathlib declaration | Description |
|---|---|
| `HasSubgaussianMGF X c őľ` | Sub-Gaussian MGF predicate `mgf X őľ t ‚Č§ exp(c t¬≤/2)` for all `t`. |
| `hasSubgaussianMGF_of_mem_Icc` | Hoeffding's lemma: a `[a,b]`-valued RV is sub-Gaussian with proxy `((b‚ąía)/2)¬≤` after centering. |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.measure_ge_le` | Sub-Gaussian Chernoff right tail. |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.measure_sum_range_ge_le_of_iIndepFun` | Hoeffding's inequality for sums of independent sub-Gaussians (used by `hoeffding_ge`). |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.add_of_indepFun` / `sum_of_iIndepFun` | Independent sums (parameters add). |

#### 12'.i `Stat/Concentration/Bernstein.lean`

Bernstein's inequality routed through the constant-`v` sub-exponential framework.
**Fully proved and `sorry`-free** (all four declarations are axiom-clean:
`propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound` only). The earlier power-series blocker
is avoided entirely: the sub-exponential packaging only needs the MGF bound on
the validity interval `c|t| < 1`, where `|tX| ‚Č§ 1` a.e. lets the elementary
pointwise bound `exp u ‚Č§ 1 + u + u¬≤` integrate term-by-term.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `exp_le_one_add_add_sq` | `lemma` | Pointwise `exp u ‚Č§ 1 + u + u¬≤` for `|u| ‚Č§ 1`; a real corollary of `Real.norm_exp_sub_one_sub_id_le`. **Proved.** |
| `bounded_mgf_le_exp_sq` | `lemma` | For mean-zero `X`, `|X| ‚Č§ c`, `E[X¬≤] ‚Č§ ŌÉ¬≤`: `mgf X őľ t ‚Č§ exp(ŌÉ¬≤t¬≤)` when `c|t| ‚Č§ 1`. Integrates the pointwise bound using `E[X]=0`, `E[X¬≤]‚Č§ŌÉ¬≤`. **Proved.** |
| `bounded_hasSubexponentialMGF` | `lemma` | A bounded mean-zero variable is sub-exponential with parameters `(2ŌÉ¬≤, c)` (note `2ŌÉ¬≤¬∑t¬≤/2 = ŌÉ¬≤t¬≤`). **Proved.** |
| `bernstein_ge` | `theorem` | One-sided Bernstein: `P(XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí E[f] ‚Č• őĶ) ‚Č§ exp(‚ąín őĶ¬≤ / (2(2ŌÉ¬≤ + cőĶ)))`. From `bounded_hasSubexponentialMGF` + independent-sum + Chernoff. **Proved.** |
| `bernstein_abs_ge` | `theorem` | Two-sided Bernstein: `P(|XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí E[f]| ‚Č• őĶ) ‚Č§ 2¬∑exp(‚ąín őĶ¬≤ / (2(2ŌÉ¬≤ + cőĶ)))`. **Proved.** |

> **Constant note.** The variance enters with proxy `2ŌÉ¬≤` and the scale is `c`
> (so the tail is `exp(‚ąínőĶ¬≤/(2(2ŌÉ¬≤+cőĶ)))`). The sharper textbook constant
> `exp(‚ąínőĶ¬≤/(2(ŌÉ¬≤+cőĶ/3)))` is *not* attainable through the constant-`v`
> sub-exponential structure ‚ÄĒ it needs a Bernstein-specific Chernoff optimisation
> on the denominator-form MGF ‚ÄĒ and was deliberately traded for this cleaner,
> looser constant. The `c|t| < 1` validity interval forces the scale `b ‚Č• c`, so
> `b = c` is optimal for this route.

---

#### 12'.j `Stat/Concentration/ConfidenceInterval.lean`

Inverts the two-sided Hoeffding and Bernstein tails (12'.f, 12'.i) into explicit
*finite-sample* confidence half-widths. For a confidence level `őī ‚ąą (0,1]` and
sample size `n`, each result gives a half-width `w(n,őī)` such that the population
mean `m = ‚ąę f ‚ąāP` is covered by the random interval `[XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí w, XŐĄ‚āô + w]` with
probability `‚Č• 1 ‚ąí őī`. Both a miss-probability form (`P(w ‚Č§ |XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí m|) ‚Č§ őī`) and
its coverage complement are provided. Holds at every fixed `n`, unlike the
asymptotic Wald interval. **Fully proved and `sorry`-free.**

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hoeffdingCIHalfWidth` | `(a b : ‚ĄĚ) (n : ‚Ąē) (őī : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚ĄĚ` | Hoeffding half-width `(b‚ąía)¬∑‚ąö(log(2/őī)/(2n))` for an `[a,b]`-valued statistic. | **Proved** (def) |
| `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` | `(c ŌÉ : ‚ĄĚ) (n : ‚Ąē) (őī : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚ĄĚ` | Variance-adaptive Bernstein half-width `2ŌÉ¬∑‚ąö(log(2/őī)/n) + 2c¬∑(log(2/őī)/n)`; leading term scales with `ŌÉ`. | **Proved** (def) |
| `hoeffding_ci_miss` | `‚Ä¶ : őľ.real {ŌČ | hoeffdingCIHalfWidth a b n őī ‚Č§ |XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí m|} ‚Č§ őī` | Miss bound: the mean falls outside the Hoeffding interval w.p. `‚Č§ őī`. Inverts `hoeffding_abs_ge`. | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_ci_miss` | `‚Ä¶ : őľ.real {ŌČ | bernsteinCIHalfWidth c ŌÉ n őī ‚Č§ |XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí m|} ‚Č§ őī` | Miss bound for the variance-adaptive Bernstein interval; exact quadratic-slack inversion of `bernstein_abs_ge`. | **Proved** |
| `hoeffding_ci_cover` | `‚Ä¶ : 1 ‚ąí őī ‚Č§ őľ.real {ŌČ | |XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí m| < hoeffdingCIHalfWidth a b n őī}` | Coverage complement of `hoeffding_ci_miss`. | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_ci_cover` | `‚Ä¶ : 1 ‚ąí őī ‚Č§ őľ.real {ŌČ | |XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí m| < bernsteinCIHalfWidth c ŌÉ n őī}` | Coverage complement of `bernstein_ci_miss`. | **Proved** |

---

#### 12'.k `Stat/Concentration/EmpiricalBernstein.lean`

Sample-variance infrastructure plus an **elementary, fully rigorous** data-driven
("empirical") Bernstein confidence interval. The full Maurer‚ÄďPontil bound is out
of scope; instead the population variance `ŌÉ¬≤ = ‚ąę(f‚ąím)¬≤` is bounded above by the
*observed* sample variance `VŐā‚āô` plus a deterministic slack `ŌĄ` (via two Hoeffding
deviations ‚ÄĒ one for `f`, one for `f¬≤` ‚ÄĒ at level `őī/2` each), and `ŌÉ ‚Č§ ‚ąö(VŐā‚āô+ŌĄ)`
is fed into the Bernstein half-width using monotonicity in `ŌÉ`. Constants are
looser than Maurer‚ÄďPontil's (the second-moment Hoeffding step is wasteful) but
every step is elementary. **Fully proved and `sorry`-free** (axiom-clean:
`propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound` only).

The explicit slack (with `M := max |a| |b|`, `d := ‚ąö(log(4/őī)/(2n))`) is
`ŌĄ = M¬≤¬∑d + 2 M (b‚ąía)¬∑d`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance` | `(S : IIDSample ő© X őľ P) (f : X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (n : ‚Ąē) : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Biased (`1/n`-normalised) empirical variance `(1/n) ‚ąĎ_{i<n} (f(ZŠĶĘ ŌČ) ‚ąí XŐĄ‚āô(ŌČ))¬≤` (in namespace `Causalean.Stat` for dot-notation). | **Proved** (def) |
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance_nonneg` | `‚Ä¶ (ŌČ) : 0 ‚Č§ S.sampleVariance f n ŌČ` | The sample variance is nonnegative (a scaled sum of squares). | **Proved** |
| `IIDSample.measurable_sampleVariance` | `(hf : Measurable f) (n) : Measurable (S.sampleVariance f n)` | The sample variance is measurable in the sample point `ŌČ`. | **Proved** |
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance_eq` | `‚Ä¶ (ŌČ) : S.sampleVariance f n ŌČ = (n:‚ĄĚ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * ‚ąĎ i ‚ąą range n, (f (Z i ŌČ))^2 ‚ąí (XŐĄ‚āô ŌČ)^2` | Computational form: empirical second moment minus squared empirical mean. | **Proved** |
| `empiricalVarianceSlack` | `(a b : ‚ĄĚ) (n : ‚Ąē) (őī : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚ĄĚ` | The deterministic slack `ŌĄ = M¬≤¬∑d + 2M(b‚ąía)¬∑d` (`M = max|a||b|`, `d = ‚ąö(log(4/őī)/(2n))`). | **Proved** (def) |
| `empirical_variance_concentration` | `(hf) (hab : a<b) (hbound : f‚ąą[a,b] a.e.) (n) (hn) (hőī0) (hőī1) : őľ.real {ŌČ | VŐā‚āô + ŌĄ < ŌÉ¬≤} ‚Č§ őī` | Data-driven variance bound: w.p. `‚Č• 1‚ąíőī`, `ŌÉ¬≤ ‚Č§ VŐā‚āô + empiricalVarianceSlack a b n őī`. Union of two level-`őī/2` Hoeffding events (for `f` and `f¬≤`) plus a null set for the a.e. `|XŐĄ‚āô| ‚Č§ M` bound. | **Proved** |
| `empiricalBernsteinCIHalfWidth` | `(S) (f) (a b c : ‚ĄĚ) (n) (őī) : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Data-driven half-width: `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` with oracle `ŌÉ` replaced by the observed `‚ąö(VŐā‚āô(ŌČ) + ŌĄ)`. | **Proved** (def) |
| `empirical_bernstein_ci_miss` | `(hf) (hab) (hbound_ab) (hc : 0‚Č§c) (hbound : |f‚ąím|‚Č§c a.e.) (hposvar : 0<ŌÉ¬≤) (n) (hn) (hőī0) (hőī1) : őľ.real {ŌČ | empiricalBernsteinCIHalfWidth S f a b c n őī ŌČ ‚Č§ |XŐĄ‚āô ‚ąí m|} ‚Č§ 2őī` | Data-driven Bernstein CI miss bound: the mean falls outside the observed-variance interval w.p. `‚Č§ 2őī`. Unions the oracle Bernstein miss (`ŌÉ=‚ąöŌÉ¬≤`) with the variance-bound failure, using monotonicity of the width in `ŌÉ` on the good event. | **Proved** |

---

## 12g. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/` ‚ÄĒ Abstract DML estimation and inference framework

Generic moment-functional layer that consolidates the DML asymptotic-linearity
machinery so any concrete instance (currently AIPW) reduces to instantiating
the abstract structure. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dml_ate.tex`
generalised away from AIPW-specific algebra.

### 12g.1 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/MomentFunctional.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GeneralMoment ő© őľ Z P_Z H` | structure | Abstract bundle: moment functional `m : H ‚Üí Z ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, truth `ő∑‚āÄ : H`, target `őł‚āÄ : ‚ĄĚ`, perturbation set `H_őĶ ‚äÜ H`, bilinear seminorms `(ŌĀ‚āĀ, ŌĀ‚āā) : H ‚Üí H ‚Üí NNReal`, joint measurability of `m _ ¬∑ _`, and `ő∑‚āÄ ‚ąą H_őĶ`. |
| `GeneralMoment.J‚āÄ` | field, `‚ĄĚ` | Jacobian `‚ąā_őł ‚ąę m(ő∑‚āÄ, z, őł) dP_Z |_{őł=őł‚āÄ}` of the population moment. Scalar form (vector-őł generalisation deferred). For AIPW (linear score `m = Ōą ‚ąí őł`), `J‚āÄ = ‚ąí1`. |
| `GeneralMoment.J‚āÄ_ne_zero` | field, `Prop` | Non-singularity witness; ensures `J‚āÄ_inv` is well-defined. |
| `GeneralMoment.J‚āÄ_inv` | `noncomputable def` | `J‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`. **Proved.** |
| `GeneralMoment.J‚āÄ_mul_J‚āÄ_inv` | `lemma`, `@[simp]` | `J‚āÄ * J‚āÄ_inv = 1`. **Proved.** |
| `MeanZero M` | `Prop` | `‚ąę z, M.m M.ő∑‚āÄ z M.őł‚āÄ ‚ąāP_Z = 0`. |
| `H_őĶ_PerturbClosed M` | `Prop` | `M.H_őĶ` is closed under convex combinations between `ő∑‚āÄ` and `ő∑ ‚ąą H_őĶ`. |

##### `LinearMoment` (linear-score extension)

Extension of `GeneralMoment` carrying the decomposition `m ő∑ z őł = m_a ő∑ z * őł + m_b ő∑ z` (Chernozhukov 2018 Thm 3.1 "linear score" form). AIPW is the canonical instance with `m_a ő∑ z = -1` and `m_b ő∑ z = Ōą_AIPW(ő∑, z)`, giving `J‚āÄ = -1`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinearMoment` | structure (extends `GeneralMoment`) | Adds `m_a`, `m_b`, measurability, decomposition `m_decomp`, and consistency `J‚āÄ_eq : J‚āÄ = ‚ąę m_a ő∑‚āÄ z dP_Z`. |

### 12g.2 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DirectionalDeriv.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasDirDeriv M` | structure | Pointwise directional derivative data: function `dM : H ‚Üí Z ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, pointwise `Tendsto` witness for the difference quotient along the segment `ő∑‚āÄ ‚Üí ő∑`, and measurability of `dM ő∑`. |

### 12g.3 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/NeymanOrthogonal.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NeymanOrthogonal M D` | `Prop` | `‚ąÄ ő∑ ‚ąą M.H_őĶ, ‚ąę z, D.dM ő∑ z ‚ąāP_Z = 0`. |
| `DiffQuotientEnvelope M` | `Prop` | Existence of an L¬Ļ(P_Z) envelope dominating the difference quotient near `t = 0`. |
| `integratedMoment_diffQuotient_tendsto_zero` | `theorem` | DCT bridge: `NeymanOrthogonal` + `DiffQuotientEnvelope` ‚áí integrated difference quotient ‚Üí 0 along `ūĚďĚ[‚Č†] 0`. **Proved** (forward-compat; not on AIPW path). |

### 12g.4 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/RemainderBound.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BilinearRemainder M C` | `Prop` | `‚ąÄ ő∑ ‚ąą M.H_őĶ, |‚ąę z, M.m ő∑ z M.őł‚āÄ ‚ąāP_Z| ‚Č§ C ¬∑ ŌĀ‚āĀ(ő∑, ő∑‚āÄ) ¬∑ ŌĀ‚āā(ő∑, ő∑‚āÄ)`. |
| `bilinear_remainder_of_smoothness` | `theorem` | NeymanOrthogonal + uniform second-order envelope on the linearization residual ‚áí `‚ąÉ C, BilinearRemainder M C`. **Proved** (after signature strengthening: uniform `K, g`). |

### 12g.5. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLChernozhukov.lean`

Classical Chernozhukov-form DML theorem; conclusion uses IF `‚ąíJ‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ m(ő∑‚āÄ, ¬∑, őł‚āÄ)` and asymptotic variance `J‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ő£ J‚āÄ‚ĀĽŠĶÄ`. Matches Chernozhukov et al. 2018 Thm 3.1 / 3.2. The earlier zero-centered `dml_asymptoticLinear` (`Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DML.lean`) was deleted on 2026-05-08 since AIPW migrated and no other instance used it.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlChernozhukovEstimator M sample split ő∑_hat` | `noncomputable def` | One-step Chernozhukov estimator: `őłŐā_n := őł‚āÄ ‚ąí J‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ P‚āô_B m(ő∑Őā, ¬∑, őł‚āÄ)`. Reduces to `(1/|B|) ő£ Ōą(ő∑Őā, Z_i)` for AIPW (J‚āÄ = ‚ąí1). |
| `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Asymptotic linearity of `dmlChernozhukovEstimator` at `M.őł‚āÄ` with IF `‚ąíJ‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ m(ő∑‚āÄ, ¬∑, őł‚āÄ)`, indexed over `split.foldB`. **Proved by Codex** (G+B decomposition with the Chernozhukov-form score `m(ő∑Őā, ¬∑, őł‚āÄ)` and a `‚ąíJ‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` constant-multiplication wrapper; bundled an inline `const_mul_isLittleOp_one` helper). **API refactored 2026-05-06:** dropped the `D : HasDirDeriv M`, `_hNO : NeymanOrthogonal M D`, and `_h_in_HőĶ : ‚ąÄ n ŌČ, ő∑_hat n ŌČ ‚ąą M.H_őĶ` parameters; replaced the ‚ąÄ-quantified `_hBR : BilinearRemainder M Crem` with the per-ő∑Őā_n bound `_hBR_at : ‚ąÄ n ŌČ, |‚ąę z, M.m (ő∑_hat n ŌČ) z M.őł‚āÄ ‚ąāP_Z| ‚Č§ Crem ¬∑ ŌĀ‚āĀ(ő∑Őā_n, ő∑‚āÄ) ¬∑ ŌĀ‚āā(ő∑Őā_n, ő∑‚āÄ)`. Net effect: instances supply only what they actually need (orthogonality is implicit in the per-ő∑Őā_n bound), removing the artificial ‚ąÄ-over-`H_őĶ` hypothesis. **Further trimmed 2026-05-09:** dropped the unused `_hc_lt : c < 1`, `_h_indiv_rate_ŌĀ‚āĀ`, `_h_indiv_rate_ŌĀ‚āā` parameters (all discarded in the body ‚ÄĒ the abstract proof needs only the product rate); callers (`AIPWInstance`, `AutoDebias.DML`) keep them at their own level when needed locally. |

### 12g.5'. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`

K-fold cross-fitted version of `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Same Chernozhukov form, K folds, score evaluated independently of nuisance training fold per Chernozhukov et al. 2018 ¬ß3.2. Concludes asymptotic linearity at the **full-sample** index `Finset.range n` (the K fold-k sub-aggregations are reweighted into a `(1/‚ąön)`-normalised sum modulo `o_p(1)`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlCrossFitEstimator M sample split ő∑_hat` | `noncomputable def` | K-fold averaged Chernozhukov estimator: `őłŐā := M.őł‚āÄ ‚ąí J‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ K‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ ő£_k \|fold n k\|‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ ő£_{i ‚ąą fold n k} m(ő∑Őā^{(-k)}, Z_i, őł‚āÄ)`. |
| `foldNormalizedSum_isBigOp` | private lemma | `(1/‚ąö\|fold n k\|) ő£_{i ‚ąą fold n k} Ōą(Z_i) = O_p(1)` whenever `Ōą` is mean-zero and `L¬≤(P_Z)`. Markov + the abstract `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` with trivial training ŌÉ-algebra `m_A = ‚ä•` (independence is automatic via `ProbabilityTheory.indep_bot_left`); mean-zero collapses centered = uncentered. **Proved.** |
| `foldNormalizedSumOverN_isBigOp` | private lemma | `(1/‚ąön) ő£_{i ‚ąą fold n k} Ōą(Z_i) = O_p(1)`. Bound by `foldNormalizedSum_isBigOp` √ó `‚ąö(\|fold n k\|/n)` (the latter is bounded eventually via `split.cover` + `split.partition` giving `(fold n k).card ‚Č§ n`). **Proved.** |
| `dml_crossFit_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Asymptotic linearity of `dmlCrossFitEstimator` at `M.őł‚āÄ` with IF `‚ąíJ‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ m(ő∑‚āÄ, ¬∑, őł‚āÄ)`, indexed over `fun n => Finset.range n`. Hypotheses: per-fold measurability/integrability (`_h_m_meas`, `_h_m_train`, `_h_m_train_uncurry`, `_h_m_int`, `_h_m_sq_int`), per-fold score-difference rate (`_h_score_diff_rate k`), per-(n,k,ŌČ) bilinear remainder bound `_hBR_at`, individual ŌĀ-rates, product rate, and `_hK_pos : 1 < K` (tightened from `0 < K` because the K-fold bias step needs `(K:‚ĄĚ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ąą (0, 1)`; `K = 1` reduces to the one-shot estimator and is out of scope). Proof structure: `mean_zero` and `finite_var` by constant-rescaling of `MeanZero M` / `_hFV`; the `remainder` decomposes into `h_fold_centered` + `h_fold_bias` (per-fold, lifted from the OneShot proof in `DMLChernozhukov.lean` via `KFoldSplit.fold_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` and an inline bias derivation analogous to OneShot `hB`) and `h_full_reweighting` (the cover/partition + ratio rescaling). The reweighting step uses `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` for the weight-discrepancy "ratio piece" and `IsBigOp.mul_isLittleOp_one_isLittleOp` for the "score piece"; finite K-fold sum closed via a small inline `IsLittleOp` finite-Finset additivity helper. **Proved by Codex (A5).** **API refactored 2026-05-09:** mirrors the OneShot 2026-05-06 refactor ‚ÄĒ dropped the `D : HasDirDeriv M`, `_hNO : NeymanOrthogonal M D`, `_h_in_HőĶ`, and ‚ąÄ-quantified `_hBR : BilinearRemainder M Crem` parameters; replaced by the per-(n,k,ŌČ) bound `_hBR_at`. |

### 12g.6 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AIPWInstance.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipwGeneralMoment S h_e_pointwise` | `noncomputable def` | AIPW realization of `GeneralMoment` with `ŌĀ‚āĀ ő∑ ő∑' = ‚ÄĖőĒőľ_T‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_X)} + ‚ÄĖőĒőľ_F‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_X)}`, `ŌĀ‚āā ő∑ ő∑' = ‚ÄĖőĒe‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_X)}`, `H_őĶ = ATE.H_őĶ őĶ`. |
| `aipwGeneralMoment.J‚āÄ`, `J‚āÄ_ne_zero` | fields | AIPW Jacobian `J‚āÄ = ‚ąí1` (linear score `m = Ōą ‚ąí őł`). |
| `aipw_meanZero` | `theorem` | Wraps `aipw_mean_zero`. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | `aipw_remainder_bound` reshaped into `BilinearRemainder` via `Fintype.sum_bool` distribution. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear` | `theorem` | Headline AIPW DML AL, retargeted to use `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Conclusion: `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator (aipwGeneralMoment S _) _ _ _) S.őł‚āÄ (fun z => ‚ąíJ‚āÄ_inv ¬∑ aipwMomentFunctional S.ő∑‚āÄ z S.őł‚āÄ)`. **Refactored 2026-05-06:** dropped the ‚ąÄ-quantified `h_L2 : ‚ąÄ ő∑ ‚ąą H_őĶ őĶ, ‚Ä¶` hypothesis (and the `hőĶ_pos` / `hőĶ_le` extracts that only fed the now-removed `aipw_neymanOrthog` / `aipwHasDirDeriv` calls). Now takes per-ő∑Őā_n L¬≤: `h_mu_diff_memLp : ‚ąÄ n ŌČ a, MemLp (őľŐā(n,ŌČ,a) ‚ąí őľ_val a) 2 P_X` and `h_e_diff_memLp : ‚ąÄ n ŌČ, MemLp (√™(n,ŌČ) ‚ąí e_val) 2 P_X`. Body builds the per-ő∑Őā_n bilinear remainder bound directly via `aipw_remainder_bound` (followed by `Fintype.sum_bool` to align with the `ŌĀ‚āĀ ¬∑ ŌĀ‚āā = (‚ÄĖőĒőľ_T‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖőĒőľ_F‚ÄĖ) ¬∑ ‚ÄĖőĒe‚ÄĖ` shape) and feeds it as `_hBR_at` to the refactored abstract theorem. The G√Ęteaux-derivative `aipwHasDirDeriv` / `aipw_neymanOrthog` wrappers (and the underlying `Neyman.lean` / `NeymanAux/` machinery) are no longer on the AL critical path and were archived to `Estimation/ATE/Archived/` on 2026-05-06. **Proved.** |

### 12g.7. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/Riesz.lean`

Riesz representer pattern for linear-in-ő≥ functionals (ATE, policy value, average derivative). Light skeleton; concrete instances live next to their estimators.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `RieszRepresentation H_ő≥ ő≥_target L P_X` | structure | A Riesz representer `őĪ‚āÄ : X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with `representation : ‚ąÄ ő≥, L ő≥ = ‚ąę x, őĪ‚āÄ x ¬∑ ő≥ x ‚ąāP_X`, plus measurability and L¬Ļ witnesses. |
| `rieszScore ő≥_target L proj_X Y_obs ő≥ őĪ őł z` | `noncomputable def` | The Riesz orthogonal score `m(z; őł, ő≥, őĪ) := L ő≥ + őĪ(proj_X z) ¬∑ (Y_obs z ‚ąí ő≥_target ő≥ (proj_X z)) ‚ąí őł`. |
| `rieszScore_meanZero` | `theorem` | Under the regression-orthogonality assumption `‚ąę z, őĪ‚āÄ(proj_X z)¬∑(Y_obs z ‚ąí ő≥_target ő≥‚āÄ (proj_X z)) ‚ąāP_Z = 0`, the population score at the truth `(ő≥‚āÄ, őĪ‚āÄ, L ő≥‚āÄ)` integrates to zero. **Proved.** |
| `rieszScore_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | Bilinear remainder identity: `‚ąę z, m(z; L ő≥‚āÄ, ő≥, őĪ) ‚ąāP_Z ‚ąí ‚ąę z, m(z; L ő≥‚āÄ, ő≥‚āÄ, őĪ‚āÄ) ‚ąāP_Z = ‚ąí ‚ąę x, (őĪ x ‚ąí őĪ‚āÄ x)¬∑(ő≥_target ő≥ x ‚ąí ő≥_target ő≥‚āÄ x) ‚ąāP_X` under pushforward `P_X = P_Z.map proj_X`, dual regression-orthogonality, integrability of the perturbed residual, and integrability of the four product terms. **Proved.** |

### 12g.8. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/Parametric.lean`

Parametric (no-nuisance) specialisation of `GeneralMoment` (`H = Unit`). With `H = Unit`, the abstract `dmlChernozhukovEstimator` collapses to the classical parametric one-step estimator and the rescaled remainder is identically zero. OLS, IV-2SLS, MLE instantiate this pattern.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `parametricMoment m_par őł‚āÄ J‚āÄ hJ m_meas` | `noncomputable def` | Constructs `GeneralMoment ő© őľ Z P_Z Unit` from a user-supplied moment `m_par : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí Z ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, target `őł‚āÄ`, and Jacobian `J‚āÄ ‚Č† 0`. Bilinear seminorms are zero (no nuisance to perturb). |
| `parametric_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator (parametricMoment ‚Ä¶) sample split (fun _ _ => ())) őł‚āÄ (fun z => ‚ąíJ‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ¬∑ m_par őł‚āÄ z) sample split.foldB` from population mean-zero `‚ąę m_par(őł‚āÄ, ¬∑) dP_Z = 0` and L¬≤-finite `Integrable ((m_par őł‚āÄ)¬≤) P_Z`. The remainder collapses identically: the parametric one-step estimator equals the influence-function partial sum exactly. **Proved.** |

### 12g.9. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/SecondStageOperator.lean`

Abstract second-stage regression operator and stability framework for the DR-Learner CATE estimator (Kennedy 2023). Operator-level / target-agnostic content: takes a real-valued pseudo-outcome `f : ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` and returns a real-valued estimate at a query point `x : ő≥`. No AIPW algebra, no causal content. Mirrors `def:est-cate-second-stage` and `def:est-cate-stability` in `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SecondStageOperator ő© őľ ő≥` | structure | Abstract bundle: `evalAt : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí (ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ő≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` taking sample size, randomness scope, pseudo-outcome, and query point; plus a minimal joint-measurability stub `meas_evalAt_const`. Linearity in input is left as a separate predicate `IsLinearInInput`. |
| `SecondStageOperator.IsLinearInInput op` | `Prop` | `evalAt n ŌČ (f + g) x = evalAt n ŌČ f x + evalAt n ŌČ g x`. Linear smoothers satisfy this; tree mean estimators with random splits typically do not. |
| `SecondStageOperator.oracleEstimator op f` | `def` | Operator applied to a fixed "true" pseudo-outcome `f` (Kennedy's `\tilde\tau_n`). |
| `SecondStageOperator.oracleRiskScale op f target x n` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | `R^*_n(x) := ‚ąö(‚ąę ŌČ, (op.evalAt n ŌČ f x ‚ąí target x)¬≤ ‚ąāőľ)`. Pointwise oracle risk scale. |
| `IsConditionalBias` | removed | The old placeholder `Prop` alias (`True`) was removed. Conditional-bias identification now enters through a caller-supplied `BiasIdent` predicate parameter on `Stable` and `oracle_expansion`. |
| `Stable op target d_n x BiasIdent` | `Prop` | Def 4 stability: for any `(fHat_n, f, bHat_n)` with `BiasIdent fHat_n f bHat_n`, `d_n ‚Üí_p 0` implies the operator-level discrepancy `op.evalAt(fHat_n) ‚ąí op.evalAt(f) ‚ąí op.evalAt(bHat_n)` is `o_p(R^*_n(x))`. |
| `oracle_expansion` | `theorem` | Headline: under `Stable op target d_n x BiasIdent`, `Tendsto_inProb d_n 0`, and a `BiasIdent fHat_n f bHat_n` witness, the operator-level oracle expansion `op.evalAt(fHat_n) ‚ąí op.evalAt(f) ‚ąí op.evalAt(bHat_n) = o_p(R^*_n(x))` holds. **Proved by Codex** (one-line application of `Stable`). |

**Refactored 2026-05-09:** the placeholder `IsConditionalBias := True` was replaced by an explicit Prop predicate parameter `BiasIdent` that callers supply, restoring semantic content.

### 12g.10. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/LinearSmoother.lean`

Abstract weighted-H√∂lder bound for linear-smoother second-stage operators. Mirrors `prop:est-cate-linear-smoother-bound` in `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinearSmootherOp ő© őľ ő≥` | structure (extends `SecondStageOperator`) | Adds `weights : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ő≥ ‚Üí (ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`. The actual weighted-sum identity `evalAt n ŌČ f x = ő£ w ¬∑ f(z_i)` is supplied as a separate predicate `IsLinearSmoother` rather than baked in. |
| `LinearSmootherOp.IsLinearSmoother op n ŌČ x B w xs` | `Prop` | Predicate witnessing the weighted-sum identity `‚ąÄ f, op.evalAt n ŌČ f x = ő£_{i ‚ąą B} w i ¬∑ f (xs i)` for explicit weights `w : őĻ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` and data points `xs : őĻ ‚Üí ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ`. |
| `WeightedNorm B w g p` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Weighted LŠĶĖ norm `(ő£_i ‚ąą B, |w i| / (ő£_j |w j|) ¬∑ |g i|^p)^(1/p)`. Index type `őĻ` abstract; uses `Real.rpow`. |
| `WeightedNorm_nonneg` | `lemma` | Non-negativity of `WeightedNorm`. **Proved.** |
| `smoother_bias_holder` | `theorem` | `|op.evalAt n ŌČ (fun z => g z.1) x| ‚Č§ c_n ¬∑ WeightedNorm B w (g ‚ąė xs.1) 1` under `IsLinearSmoother op n ŌČ x B w xs` and `ő£ |w| ‚Č§ c_n`. **Proved by Codex** via triangle inequality + factor-out-`ő£|w|` rearrangement of the weighted-L¬Ļ norm. |
| `smoother_bias_product_holder` | `theorem` | H√∂lder for `g = g‚āĀ¬∑g‚āā`: `|op.evalAt n ŌČ (fun z => g‚āĀ(z.1) ¬∑ g‚āā(z.1)) x| ‚Č§ c_n ¬∑ ‚ÄĖg‚āĀ ‚ąė xs.1‚ÄĖ_{w,p} ¬∑ ‚ÄĖg‚āā ‚ąė xs.1‚ÄĖ_{w,q}` under `IsLinearSmoother`, `ő£ |w| ‚Č§ c_n`, and `Real.HolderConjugate p q`. **Proved by Codex** via `Real.inner_le_Lp_mul_Lq_of_nonneg` applied to `f i := |w i|^{1/p} ¬∑ |g‚āĀ (xs i).1|`, `g i := |w i|^{1/q} ¬∑ |g‚āā (xs i).1|` with `1/p + 1/q = 1`. |

### 12g.11. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/` ‚ÄĒ Automatic debiasing module

Reusable construction that converts any **linear-in-regression target** (or, more generally, any **regression-nuisance moment**) into a Neyman-orthogonal score by adding the Riesz-representer correction `őĪ(X)¬∑(Y ‚ąí g(X))`. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/automatic_debiasing.tex`. The module contains an algebraic core (linear functional case), a general-moment layer, and a one-shot Auto-DML estimator with its asymptotic-linearity theorem.

#### 12g.11.a `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/Linear.lean` ‚ÄĒ Linear functional core

Defs 1, 3, 5 and Props 4, 6 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinRegFnSys` | structure | Linear regression-functional system: types `Z, X, H_ő≥` with measure/AddCommGroup/Module instances, `proj_X, Y_obs, ő≥_target` (linear in `H_ő≥`), observation-level linear-in-ő≥ functional `m_lin : Z ‚Üí H_ő≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, regression truth `g‚āÄ`, pushforward `P_X = P_Z.map proj_X`, and the integrated dual-orthogonality `regression_resid_orthog`. |
| `L_of_m S ő≥` | `noncomputable def` | Population linear functional `ő≥ ‚Ü¶ ‚ąę m_lin(z, ő≥) dP_Z`. |
| `L_of_m_add`, `L_of_m_smul` | `theorem`, `theorem` | Linearity of `L_of_m` in `ő≥` (under integrability). **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore S ő≥ őĪ őł z` | `noncomputable def` | Alias of `rieszScore` from `Estimation.OrthogonalMoments.Riesz` specialised to a `LinRegFnSys`. |
| `linRieszScore_meanZero` | `theorem` | Population score at the truth `(g‚āÄ, őĪ‚āÄ, L g‚āÄ)` integrates to zero (Prop 6, mean-zero). Wraps `rieszScore_meanZero` via `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore_directional_g_zero` | `theorem` | Directional zero in the regression direction: `(‚ąę m_lin z őĹ_g dP_Z) ‚ąí ‚ąę rep.őĪ‚āÄ ¬∑ ő≥_target őĹ_g dP_X = 0` (Prop 6, part b). Direct from `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore_directional_őĪ_zero` | `theorem` | Directional zero in the representer direction: residual orthogonality of `őĹ_őĪ` against `Y_obs ‚ąí ő≥_target g‚āÄ` (Prop 6, part c). Direct call to `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszLoss S őĪ` | `noncomputable def` | Linear Riesz loss `‚ąę (ő≥_target őĪ x)¬≤ dP_X ‚ąí 2 ¬∑ L_of_m S őĪ` (Def 3). |
| `linRieszLoss_excess_eq_l2dist` | `theorem` | Prop 4, second half: `linRieszLoss őĪ ‚ąí linRieszLoss őĪ‚āÄ_idx = ‚ąę (ő≥_target őĪ ‚ąí ő≥_target őĪ‚āÄ_idx)¬≤ dP_X` when `rep.őĪ‚āÄ = ő≥_target őĪ‚āÄ_idx` pointwise. Complete-the-square via `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszLoss_FOC_iff_representer` | `theorem` | Prop 4, first half: `őĪ‚āÄ_idx` is a directional minimizer of `linRieszLoss` along every line `őĪ‚āÄ_idx + t ‚ÄĘ őĹ` iff `L(őĹ) = ‚ąę ő≥_target őĪ‚āÄ_idx ¬∑ ő≥_target őĹ dP_X` for every `őĹ`. Parabolic-identity FOC argument with quadratic-coefficient case split on `a = ‚ąę (ő≥_target őĹ)¬≤ dP_X`. **Proved.** |

#### 12g.11.b `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/General.lean` ‚ÄĒ General-moment layer

Defs 8, 9, 10, 12, 13 and Prop 11 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `RegNuisanceMomentSys` | structure | Regression-nuisance moment system. Same data layer as `LinRegFnSys`; replaces the linear functional `m_lin` with a general moment `M : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí H ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` (kernel `m : H ‚Üí Z ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, `M_truth : M őł‚āÄ g‚āÄ = 0`, `m_population : M őł g = ‚ąę m g z őł dP_Z`) and a precomputed Gateaux derivative `D_g_M : H ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with linearity laws. |
| `AutoDebiasRepresenter S` | structure | The representer `őĪ‚āÄ : X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` characterised by `D_g_M őĹ = ‚ąę őĪ‚āÄ ¬∑ ő≥_target őĹ dP_X` (Def 9). |
| `autoDebiasedScore S g őĪ őł z` | `noncomputable def` | Observation-level form: `m g z őł + őĪ(proj_X z) ¬∑ (Y_obs z ‚ąí ő≥_target g (proj_X z))`. |
| `autoDebiasedMoment S g őĪ őł` | `noncomputable def` | `‚ąę z, autoDebiasedScore S g őĪ őł z ‚ąāP_Z` (Def 10). |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_meanZero_at_truth` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part a: `autoDebiasedMoment S g‚āÄ rep.őĪ‚āÄ őł‚āÄ = 0` from `M_truth` + `regression_resid_orthog` + `m_population`. **Proved.** |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_directional_g_zero` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part b: `D_g_M őĹ_g ‚ąí ‚ąę rep.őĪ‚āÄ ¬∑ ő≥_target őĹ_g dP_X = 0`. Direct from `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_directional_őĪ_zero` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part c: residual orthogonality of arbitrary measurable `őĹ_őĪ`. Direct call to `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `genRieszLoss S őĪ` | `noncomputable def` | General Riesz loss `‚ąę (ő≥_target őĪ x)¬≤ dP_X ‚ąí 2 ¬∑ D_g_M őĪ` (Def 12). FOC ‚ÜĒ representer characterisation is structurally the same as `linRieszLoss_FOC_iff_representer`; not re-proved here. |
| `finDiffRieszLoss S Z_data őł_hat g_hat őĶ őĪ C n ŌČ` | `noncomputable def` | Finite-difference representer loss (Def 13): empirical second moment minus a centred finite-difference approximation of `D_g M(őłŐā_n, ńĚ_n)[őĪ]` with perturbation scale `őĶ n`. Pure definition; intended target of future RieszNet-style analysis. |

#### 12g.11.c `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/DML.lean` ‚ÄĒ Auto-DML estimator and AL theorem

Def 6 and Thm 7 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `linAutoNuisance S` | `def` | Joint nuisance type `S.H_ő≥ √ó (S.X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` with componentwise `AddCommGroup` (`Prod.instAddCommGroup`) and `Module ‚ĄĚ` (`Prod.instModule`). |
| `linAutoGeneralMoment őľ S rep őĶ hőĶ_nn h_score_meas` | `noncomputable def` | `GeneralMoment` instance over `linAutoNuisance S`. `m (ő≥, őĪ) z őł := linRieszScore S ő≥ őĪ őł z`; `ő∑‚āÄ := (S.g‚āÄ, rep.őĪ‚āÄ)`; `őł‚āÄ := L_of_m S S.g‚āÄ`; `H_őĶ := { (ő≥, őĪ) | ‚ÄĖő≥_target ő≥ ‚ąí ő≥_target g‚āÄ‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_X)} ‚Č§ őĶ ‚ąß ‚ÄĖőĪ ‚ąí őĪ‚āÄ‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_X)} ‚Č§ őĶ }`; `ŌĀ‚āĀ`/`ŌĀ‚āā` are the corresponding L¬≤ norms; `J‚āÄ = ‚ąí1`. The measurability witness `h_score_meas : ‚ąÄ ő∑ őł, Measurable (fun z => linRieszScore S ő∑.1 ő∑.2 őł z)` and non-negativity `hőĶ_nn : 0 ‚Č§ őĶ` are passed as parameters because `linAutoNuisance` does not internally constrain `őĪ : X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` to be measurable. |
| `linAuto_meanZero` | `theorem` | `MeanZero` of the linear Auto-DML moment at the truth. Direct call to `linRieszScore_meanZero`. **Proved.** |
| `linAuto_int_pred S rep ő∑` | `private def` | Per-ő∑ integrability bundle (10 components) exposing the exact API shape required by `rieszScore_bilinearRem`. Used as a hypothesis in `linAuto_bilinearRem`. |
| `linAuto_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | Bilinear remainder bound: `\|‚ąę z, score ő∑ z őł‚āÄ ‚ąāP_Z\| ‚Č§ C ¬∑ ŌĀ‚āĀ(ő∑, ő∑‚āÄ) ¬∑ ŌĀ‚āā(ő∑, ő∑‚āÄ)` for `ő∑ ‚ąą H_őĶ` with `linAuto_int_pred S rep ő∑` and explicit `MemLp` witnesses for the two L¬≤(P_X) differences. Proof factors via `rieszScore_bilinearRem` then applies H√∂lder-2 (`integral_mul_norm_le_Lp_mul_Lq`). **Proved.** |
| `linAutoDMLEstimator S rep őĶ hőĶ_nn h_score_meas sample split ő∑_hat` | `noncomputable def` | One-shot linear Auto-DML estimator (Def 6), built as `dmlChernozhukovEstimator (linAutoGeneralMoment őľ S rep őĶ hőĶ_nn h_score_meas) sample split ő∑_hat`. Equivalently: `(1/\|B(n)\|) ő£_{i ‚ąą B(n)} [m_lin(Z_i; ńĚ_n) + őĪŐā_n(X_i)¬∑(Y_i ‚ąí ńĚ_n(X_i))]`. |
| `linAutoDML_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Thm 7 headline: `IsAsymLinear (linAutoDMLEstimator ‚Ä¶) (L_of_m S S.g‚āÄ) (fun z => ‚ąíJ‚āÄ_inv ¬∑ linRieszScore S S.g‚āÄ rep.őĪ‚āÄ őł‚āÄ z) sample split.foldB` under `MeanZero`, finite L¬≤ of the oracle score, per-ő∑ bilinear remainder, the standard fold-A measurability/integrability hypotheses, and the score-difference / individual / product rate hypotheses inherited from `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Proof: direct composition. **Proved.** |

The module skips the ATE-equivalence remark from the tex (Remark 7) per design.

## 13'''. `Estimation/CATE/` ‚ÄĒ DR-Learner CATE estimation (Kennedy 2023)

Specific-system layer instantiating `BackdoorEstimationSystem` for the function-valued target `ŌĄ‚āÄ(x) = őľ_val(true, x) ‚ąí őľ_val(false, x)`. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`. The DR-Learner is built on top of the same AIPW pseudo-outcome already used for ATE; the new content is (1) the function-valued target with `condDistrib`-style pointwise interpretation, (2) the DR conditional-bias identity (Prop 2 / `prop:est-cate-dr-bias-identity`, the milestone), and (3) the second-stage regression operator with stability hypothesis driving the oracle expansion (Theorem 5).

### 13'''.a `Estimation/CATE/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `CATEEstimationSystem P ő≥` | structure (extends `BackdoorEstimationSystem`) | Empty extension ‚ÄĒ the CATE target `ŌĄ_val` is purely derived. All back-door substrate (consistency, exchangeability, overlap, integrability, `őľ_val` / `e_val`, ŌÉ(X)-compatibility) is inherited. |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.ŌĄ_val S x` | `def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Value-space CATE: `ŌĄ_val x := őľ_val true x ‚ąí őľ_val false x`. Definition `def:est-cate-system`. |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.measurable_ŌĄ_val` | `lemma` | Measurability of `ŌĄ_val`. **Proved.** |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.tau_val_eq_CATE` | `theorem` | `(fun ŌČ => ŌĄ_val (factualX ŌČ)) =ŠĶź őľ[Y(true) ‚ąí Y(false) | ŌÉ(X)]` under the back-door assumptions. **Proved by Codex** (combine `őľ_compat true/false` with `condExp_sub`). |

### 13'''.b `Estimation/CATE/PseudoOutcome.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phi_eta z ő∑` | `noncomputable def` | Uncentered AIPW pseudo-outcome (Kennedy's `ŌÜ_ő∑`): `aipwMoment z ő∑.őľ_fn ő∑.e_fn 0`. Definition `def:est-cate-dr-pseudo-outcome`. |
| `phi‚āÄ S z` | `noncomputable def` | True pseudo-outcome `ŌÜ_0 := ŌÜ_{ő∑_0}`. |
| `measurable_phi_eta`, `measurable_phi‚āÄ` | `lemma` | Measurability in the data argument. **Proved by Codex** (mirror `measurable_Ōą_AIPW`). |

### 13'''.c `Estimation/CATE/PseudoOutcomeMean.lean` (Prop 1)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phi‚āÄ_factualZ_cond_exp` | `theorem` | ŌÉ(X)-conditional form of `prop:est-cate-dr-cond-mean`: `őľ[ŌÜ‚āÄ(factualZ) | ŌÉ(X)] =ŠĶź ŌĄ_val ‚ąė factualX` under back-door + strict overlap. **Proved by Codex** via local `weighted_residual_cond_exp_zero` helper + `cond_exp_residual_zero` + `condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left`. |
| `phi_eta_cond_exp_eq_tau` | `theorem` | Value-space `P_X`-a.e. form: `‚ąÄŠĶź x ‚ąāP_X, ‚ąę z, ŌÜ‚āÄ(z) ‚ąācondDistrib factualZ factualX P.őľ x = ŌĄ_val x`. Hypotheses add `[StandardBorelSpace ő≥] [Nonempty ő≥]` (for `condDistrib`) and `Integrable (ŌÜ‚āÄ ‚ąė factualZ) P.őľ`. **Proved by Codex** by transporting `phi‚āÄ_factualZ_cond_exp` through `condExp_ae_eq_integral_condDistrib` and pushing forward to `P_X` via `MeasureTheory.ae_map_iff` (measurability of the equality set comes from `StronglyMeasurable.integral_condDistrib` for the LHS and `measurable_ŌĄ_val` for the RHS). |

### 13'''.d `Estimation/CATE/ConditionalBias.lean` (Prop 2 ‚ÄĒ milestone)

The DR conditional-bias identity. Pure ŌÉ(X)-conditional algebra; the closed-form cross-product remainder driving the double-robustness corollary (`rem:est-cate-double-robust`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `condBias ő∑ ő∑‚āÄ x` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Closed form: `ő£_{a‚ąąBool} ((ő∑.e_fn x ‚ąí ő∑‚āÄ.e_fn x)¬∑(ő∑.őľ_fn a x ‚ąí ő∑‚āÄ.őľ_fn a x)) / (if a then ő∑.e_fn x else 1 ‚ąí ő∑.e_fn x)`. |
| `measurable_condBias` | `lemma` | Measurability in `x`. **Proved by Codex** via `Finset.measurable_sum` + product-of-measurables. |
| `cond_exp_residual_at_h` | `private lemma` | Generalized form of `cond_exp_residual_zero` (parameterised over arbitrary measurable `h : ő≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`): `őľ[1{D=d}¬∑(Y ‚ąí h(X)) | ŌÉ(X)] =ŠĶź propScore d ¬∑ (őľ_val d (factualX) ‚ąí h (factualX))`. **Proved by Codex** mirroring `MeanZero.lean` lines 109‚Äď196. |
| `phi_eta_minus_phi‚āÄ_cond_exp` | `theorem` | ŌÉ(X)-conditional bias identity (Prop 2 form on ő©): `őľ[ŌÜ_ő∑(factualZ) ‚ąí ŌÜ_0(factualZ) | ŌÉ(X)] =ŠĶź condBias ő∑ ő∑‚āÄ ‚ąė factualX` under per-arm outcome-regression integrability and strict overlap. **Proved by Codex** with a 100-line expansion: 4 calls to `cond_exp_residual_at_h` + `e_compat` / `propScore_false_ae` substitution + `field_simp` + `ring`. **Hypothesis additions vs. plan**: `(h_őľ_ő∑_int : ‚ąÄ a, Integrable (ő∑.őľ_fn a ‚ąė factualX) P.őľ)` and `(hőĶ_pos : 0 < őĶ)` were added so the algebra is well-defined. |
| `phi_eta_minus_phi‚āÄ_at_x` | `theorem` | Value-space `P_X`-a.e. form: `‚ąÄŠĶź x ‚ąāP_X, ‚ąę z, (phi_eta z ő∑ ‚ąí phi‚āÄ S z) ‚ąācondDistrib factualZ factualX P.őľ x = condBias ő∑ ő∑‚āÄ x`. Hypotheses add `[StandardBorelSpace ő≥] [Nonempty ő≥]`, `0 < őĶ`, and per-arm ő∑-integrability. **Proved by Codex** by transporting `phi_eta_minus_phi‚āÄ_cond_exp` through `condExp_ae_eq_integral_condDistrib` and pushing forward to `P_X` via `MeasureTheory.ae_map_iff` (measurability of the equality set from `StronglyMeasurable.integral_condDistrib` and `measurable_condBias`). |
| `condBias_zero_of_propensity_match`, `condBias_zero_of_outcome_match` | `lemma` | Double-robustness corollaries (`rem:est-cate-double-robust`): `condBias = 0` if propensity matches truth, or if both outcome arms match. **Proved by Codex** via `Finset.sum_eq_zero` + `sub_eq_zero` substitution. |
| `cond_exp_phi_eta_dir_deriv_at_truth_zero` | `theorem` | Differentiated companion of `phi_eta_minus_phi‚āÄ_cond_exp`: ŌÉ(X)-conditional of `phi_eta_dir_deriv ő∑‚āÄ v (factualZ)` is zero a.e. under strict overlap on `ő∑‚āÄ` and bounded `v` (uniform bounds on `v.őľ_fn b` and `v.e_fn`). **Proved by Codex** by 5-summand decomposition of the closed form; the two `e_fn`-residual terms vanish via `cond_exp_residual_zero` (d=true / d=false) and the two `őľ_fn`-pullouts cancel via `S.e_compat` (propScore = e_val a.e.) plus the derived `propScore false =ŠĶź 1 ‚ąí e_val ‚ąė factualX`. |

### 13'''.d‚Ä≤ `Estimation/CATE/PhiEtaDeriv.lean`

Closed-form directional derivative of the DR pseudo-outcome `phi_eta` in its nuisance argument `ő∑`, evaluated at an anchor `g‚āÄ : NuisanceVec ő≥` along a direction `v : NuisanceVec ő≥`. Because `phi_eta` is reciprocal in `ő∑.e_fn`, the value-difference `phi_eta z ő∑ ‚ąí phi_eta z g‚āÄ` is **not** the directional derivative; this file computes the genuine derivative and proves the pointwise difference-quotient tendsto under strict overlap.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `phi_eta_dir_deriv g‚āÄ v z` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Closed-form directional derivative `D_g phi_eta(z, g‚āÄ)[v] = (v.őľ_fn true X ‚ąí v.őľ_fn false X) + indA(z)¬∑(‚ąív.e_fn(X)/g‚āÄ.e¬≤¬∑(Y ‚ąí g‚āÄ.őľ_fn true X) ‚ąí v.őľ_fn true X / g‚āÄ.e_fn X) ‚ąí (1 ‚ąí indA(z))¬∑(v.e_fn(X)/(1 ‚ąí g‚āÄ.e_fn X)¬≤¬∑(Y ‚ąí g‚āÄ.őľ_fn false X) ‚ąí v.őľ_fn false X / (1 ‚ąí g‚āÄ.e_fn X))`. Used by `Estimation/CATE/OrthogonalLearning/DRLearner/Analytic.lean` to identify the opaque `NuisanceDirDeriv.dPhi` field via uniqueness of limits. |
| `measurable_phi_eta_dir_deriv` | `lemma` | Measurability in `z`. Proved by composing the measurabilities of the five summands. |
| `phi_eta_dir_deriv_tendsto` | `theorem` | Pointwise tendsto: `(phi_eta z (g‚āÄ + t ‚ÄĘ v) ‚ąí phi_eta z g‚āÄ) / t ‚Üí phi_eta_dir_deriv g‚āÄ v z` as `t ‚Üí 0` along `ūĚďĚ[‚Č†] 0`, under strict overlap (`g‚āÄ ‚ąą H_őĶ őĶ`, `0 < őĶ`). **Proved by Codex** via a private `phi_eta_real_line_hasDerivAt` HasDerivAt helper on ‚ĄĚ + standard real-analysis combinators; the strict-overlap hypothesis ensures the denominators `g‚āÄ.e_fn X` and `1 ‚ąí g‚āÄ.e_fn X` are nonzero. |

### 13'''.e `Estimation/CATE/Kennedy/DRLearner.lean`

Thin instantiation of `SecondStageOperator` to the AIPW pseudo-outcome.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `drLearnerEstimator S op ő∑_hat n ŌČ x` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | `op.evalAt n ŌČ (fun z => phi_eta z (ő∑_hat n ŌČ)) x`. Kennedy's `\hat\tau^{DR}_n(x)`. |
| `drOracleEstimator S op n ŌČ x` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | `op.evalAt n ŌČ (fun z => phi‚āÄ S z) x`. Kennedy's `\tilde\tau_n(x)`. |
| `drOracleRiskScale S op x n` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Specialisation of `oracleRiskScale` to `(phi‚āÄ S, S.ŌĄ_val, x)`. |
| `drOracleEstimator_eq_oracleEstimator` | `lemma` | Sanity unfolding: `drOracleEstimator S op = op.oracleEstimator (phi‚āÄ S)`. **Proved** by `rfl`. |

### 13'''.f `Estimation/CATE/Kennedy/OracleExpansion.lean` (Theorem 5)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dr_oracle_expansion` | `theorem` | AIPW specialisation of `General.oracle_expansion`: under `CATEEstimationSystem`, `Stable op S.ŌĄ_val d_n x BiasIdent`, `Tendsto_inProb d_n 0`, and a `BiasIdent` witness for `phi_eta`, `phi‚āÄ`, and `condBias`, `(drLearner ‚ąí drOracle ‚ąí op.evalAt(condBias)) = o_p(R^*_n(x))`. **Proved by Codex** as a direct call to `General.oracle_expansion`. |
| `dr_oracle_efficient` | `theorem` | Corollary: if additionally the smoothed-bias term is `o_p(R^*_n(x))`, then `(drLearner ‚ąí drOracle) = o_p(R^*_n(x))`. **Proved by Codex** via `IsLittleOp.add_eventually_nonneg_rate` (newly exposed from `Estimation/ATE/RemainderIdentity.lean`) + algebraic cancellation via `convert ... ; funext ; ring`. |

### 13'''.g `Estimation/CATE/Kennedy/LinearSmootherCATE.lean` (Prop 6)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cate_linear_smoother_bias_bound` | `theorem` | H√∂lder bound for the smoothed DR-bias term: `\|op.evalAt n ŌČ (condBias ő∑_hat ő∑‚āÄ ‚ąė z.1) x\| ‚Č§ aipw_rem_const őĶ ¬∑ c_n ¬∑ ‚ÄĖőĒŌÄ‚ÄĖ_{w,p} ¬∑ ő£_a ‚ÄĖőĒőľ_a‚ÄĖ_{w,q}` under `IsLinearSmoother op n ŌČ x B w xs`, `ő£ \|w\| ‚Č§ c_n`, `Real.HolderConjugate p q`, and `ő∑_hat n ŌČ ‚ąą H_őĶ őĶ`. **Proved by Codex (filled)** ‚ÄĒ codex left 4 mechanical errors (unknown `Finset.sum_bool` instead of `Fintype.sum_bool`; summand-order mismatch; sum-distributivity rearrangements) which were patched inline. The proof: bound `\|condBias y\| ‚Č§ aipw_rem_const őĶ ¬∑ ő£_a \|őĒŌÄ y\| ¬∑ \|őĒőľ_a y\|` pointwise (mirrors `aipw_remainder_bound` in `RemainderBound.lean`), then apply `smoother_bias_product_holder` arm-by-arm to a constructed `absOp` with absolute weights. |
| `cate_dr_oracle_efficient_linear` | `theorem` | Corollary plugging a linear-smoother `LinearSmootherOp` into `dr_oracle_efficient` (uses `op.toSecondStageOperator` projection). **Proved** by direct application of `dr_oracle_efficient`. |

### 13'''.h `Estimation/CATE/SecondOrderBias.lean` (honest second-order product bias ‚ÄĒ reusable core)

The genuine double-robustness content: the DR-Learner nuisance bias weighted by an arbitrary bounded ŌÉ(X)-test function `w` is a **product** of the two nuisance L¬≤-errors. Generalises `aipw_remainder_bound` (the `w ‚Č° 1` / ATE case) to a test function. All sorry-free + axiom-clean.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `integral_phiDiff_mul_eq_condBias` | `theorem` | Conditioning identity: `‚ąę z, (phi_eta z ő∑ ‚ąí phi‚āÄ S z)¬∑w z.1 ‚ąāP_Z = ‚ąę x, condBias ő∑ ő∑‚āÄ x ¬∑ w x ‚ąāP_X` for measurable `w`. **Proved** by pulling the ŌÉ(X)-measurable factor `w(X)` through `condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_right` and substituting `phi_eta_minus_phi‚āÄ_cond_exp` (the `dr_scoreZero` pull-out idiom). |
| `abs_integral_phiDiff_mul_le_product` | `theorem` | Second-order product bound: `\|‚ąę z, (phi_eta z ő∑ ‚ąí phi‚āÄ S z)¬∑w z.1 ‚ąāP_Z\| ‚Č§ (B/őĶ)¬∑ő£_a ‚ÄĖő∑.őľ_fn a ‚ąí őľ_val a‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_X)}¬∑‚ÄĖő∑.e_fn ‚ąí e_val‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_X)}` for `\|w\| ‚Č§ B`, `ő∑ ‚ąą H_őĶ`. **Proved** by the identity + pointwise `\|condBias¬∑w\| ‚Č§ (B/őĶ)(\|őĒőľ_T¬∑őĒe\| + \|őĒőľ_F¬∑őĒe\|)` (strict overlap) + `integral_abs_mul_le_eLpNorm_mul_eLpNorm` per arm ‚ÄĒ the Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz route of `aipw_remainder_bound`. |

### 13'''.i `Estimation/CATE/OrthogonalLearning/SecondOrderBias.lean` (DR-Learner `Bias_n` is second-order)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.drBias_le_product` | `theorem` | `\|Bias_n (drOSLSystem) ((drMixedDirDeriv).Dőł_at ő∑‚āÄ) ((drMixedDirDeriv).Dőł_at h) őłŐā\| ‚Č§ (2B/őĶ)¬∑ő£_a ‚ÄĖh.őľ_fn a ‚ąí őľ_val a‚ÄĖ‚āā¬∑‚ÄĖh.e_fn ‚ąí e_val‚ÄĖ‚āā` for `\|D.dEval őłŐā\| ‚Č§ B`, `h ‚ąą H_őĶ`. **Proved**: both `drMixedDirDeriv` bundles have `d‚Ąď_őł = -2¬∑(phi_eta z ő∑ ‚ąí eval őł‚āÄ z.1)¬∑D.dEval őł z.1` (literal field), so `Bias_n` telescopes (the `eval őł‚āÄ` term cancels) to `2¬∑‚ąę(phi_eta¬∑h ‚ąí phi‚āÄ)¬∑D.dEval őłŐā`, then `abs_integral_phiDiff_mul_le_product` with `w := D.dEval őłŐā`. **Replaces the trivial `OSL.Bias_taylor_form` existential placeholder.** Axiom-clean. |

### 13'''.j `Estimation/CATE/OrthogonalLearning/LocalEmpProcess/DRLearnerHonest.lean` (honest fixed-nuisance Kennedy oracle inequality)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.oracle_inequality_drLearner_highProb_honest` | `theorem` | Same setup as `oracle_inequality_drLearner_highProb`, with the DD bundles fixed to `drMixedDirDeriv` and a uniform bound `\|D.dEval őł\| ‚Č§ B` on `őė_set`. Conclusion replaces the symbolic `Bias_n` term with the **explicit** product bias: `‚ÄĖŌĄŐā ‚ąí őł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ (4(1+ŌÉ)/ŌÉ¬≤)¬∑ŌĀ¬≤_{n,őī} + (4/ŌÉ)¬∑(2B/őĶ)¬∑ő£_a ‚ÄĖőĒőľ_a‚ÄĖ‚āā‚ÄĖőĒe‚ÄĖ‚āā + (4/ŌÉ)¬∑r_opt n` w.p. ‚Č• 1‚ąíőī. **Proved** by composing the existing end-to-end with `drBias_le_product` (`(4/ŌÉ)¬∑Bias_n ‚Č§ (4/ŌÉ)¬∑\|Bias_n\| ‚Č§ ‚Ä¶`). Axiom-clean. |

### 13'''.k `Estimation/CATE/OrthogonalLearning/LocalEmpProcess/DRLearnerRandomNuisance.lean` (un-pinned random cross-fitted nuisance)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.oracle_inequality_drLearner_random_nuisance` | `theorem` | Un-pins the nuisance from fixed `h` to a random fold-A estimate `ń• : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí NuisanceVec ő≥` (each `ń• n ŌČ ‚ąą H_őĶ`). Conclusion carries the **random** product bias `(4/ŌÉ)¬∑(2B/őĶ)¬∑ő£_a ‚ÄĖń• n ŌČ.őľ_fn a ‚ąí őľ_val a‚ÄĖ‚āā¬∑‚ÄĖń• n ŌČ.e_fn ‚ąí e_val‚ÄĖ‚āā` on a high-prob set per `n`. **Proved** by the deterministic `oracle_inequality_plugin_ERM` (already accepts random `ghat`) + `drBias_le_product` at `ń• n ŌČ` (already holds ‚ąÄ `h ‚ąą H_őĶ`). Takes the realised empirical-process modulus event `hMod` as a hypothesis ‚ÄĒ discharging it is the cross-fitting frontier (keystone now proved, see `RandomParam.lean`). Axiom-clean. |

### 13'''.l `Estimation/OrthogonalLearning/LocalEmpProcess/RandomParam.lean` (cross-fit conditioning keystone)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.randomParam_event_le` | `theorem` | **Cross-fit conditioning keystone.** Given a fold-A sub-ŌÉ-algebra `m_A`, the fold-B coordinate map `Y : ő© ‚Üí ő≤` with `őľ.map Y = őĹ` and `Indep m_A ŌÉ(Y)`, a fold-A-measurable bad-set family `Bad : ő© ‚Üí Set ő≤` (joint-measurable in `m_A.prod mő≤`) with each section `őĹ (Bad ŌČ) ‚Č§ őī`, then the *random* event `őľ {ŌČ : Y ŌČ ‚ąą Bad ŌČ} ‚Č§ őī` ‚ÄĒ plugging a fold-A-measurable random parameter into fold-B events of uniform mass ‚Č§ őī preserves the bound. **Proved** via `indep_trim_prod_map_eq` (product factorisation `őľ.map (ŌČ ‚Ü¶ (ŌČ,YŌČ)) = (őľ.trim m_A).prod őĹ`) + `Measure.prod_apply` (Tonelli on the őĹ-section) + `‚ąę(‚Č§őī)=‚Č§őī`. Axiom-clean. The measure-theoretic heart of lifting the fixed-nuisance modulus to a random cross-fitted nuisance ‚ÄĒ now consumed by `RandomNuisance.lean` below. |

### 13'''.l.2 `Estimation/OrthogonalLearning/LocalEmpProcess/RandomNuisance.lean` (cross-fit lift of the modulus to a random nuisance)

Closes the OSL/Kennedy G2 frontier: the fixed-`g` bridge `localEmpProcessModulus_of_bounded_rademacher` is lifted to a *random fold-A-measurable nuisance* `ń• : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí G` via the keystone. All declarations sorry-free and axiom-clean.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSL.LocalEmpProcessModulusRandom S S_iid split ŌĀ őī ń•` | `def`, `Prop` | Random-`g` analogue of `LocalEmpProcessModulus`: for each `n` a high-prob (`‚Č• 1‚ąíőī`) event on which the centred excess risk at the random nuisance `ń• n ŌČ` obeys the modulus inequality uniformly in `őł`. Exactly the `hMod` shape consumed by `oracle_inequality_drLearner_random_nuisance`. |
| `OSL.foldBCoord S_iid split n` | `noncomputable def`, `ő© ‚Üí Fin (foldB n).card ‚Üí Z` | Fold-B coordinate map (reindex of the fold-B subsample by `orderIsoOfFin`). |
| `OSL.badDataSet S S_iid split n b őī R g` | `noncomputable def`, `Set (Fin (foldB n).card ‚Üí Z)` | Product-space bad set for nuisance `g`: `{s : 2‚ÄĘrademacherComplexity + 2b‚ąö(2 log(1/őī)/m) ‚Č§ uniformDeviation (centred-loss class at g) s}`. |
| `OSL.modulusRadius split b őī R` | `noncomputable def`, `‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Deterministic radius `ŌĀ n = ‚ąö(if card=0 then 2b else 2 R n + 2b‚ąö(2 log(1/őī)/card))`. |
| `OSL.foldBCoord_law` | `theorem` | `őľ.map (foldBCoord S_iid split n) = Measure.pi P_Z` ‚ÄĒ the fold-B coordinate law (mirrors the monolith `hY_law`, via `foldB_pi_law` + `piCongrLeft`). |
| `OSL.foldBCoord_meas` | `theorem` | `foldBCoord` is measurable. |
| `OSL.badDataSet_mass_le` | `theorem` | `Measure.pi P_Z (badDataSet ‚Ä¶ g) ‚Č§ őī` (McDiarmid tail bound `uniform_deviation_tail_bound_separable_of_pos` at the centred class; needs `0 < b`, `0 < card`). |
| `OSL.modulus_of_not_badData` | `theorem` | Off the bad set (`foldBCoord ŌČ ‚ąČ badDataSet ‚Ä¶ g`), the modulus inequality holds uniformly over `őė_set` (mirrors the monolith on-event branch; re-derives the dense-range Rademacher bridge inline since the monolith's bridge lemmas are `private`). |
| `OSL.localEmpProcessModulus_random_of_bounded_rademacher` | `theorem` | **Cross-fit lift (frontier capstone).** Given a random nuisance `ń•` with uniform bound `b>0`, continuity, and Rademacher bound `R` *at every realisation* `ń• n ŌČ`, plus the cross-fit structural facts `hindep n` (fold A ‚ä• fold-B coords) and `hbad_joint n` (the bad set depends jointly measurably on `(ŌČ,s)` w.r.t. `m_A n ‚äó ŌÉ(foldB)`), concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulusRandom S S_iid split (modulusRadius ‚Ä¶) őī ń•`. **Proved** by `badDataSet_mass_le` (per-section mass) + `randomParam_event_le` (condition on fold A) for the high-prob event, `modulus_of_not_badData` on the complement, with an `card=0` trivial branch. Axiom-clean. `hindep`/`hbad_joint` are named structural hypotheses (true by the cross-fit construction; same spirit as taking `RademacherBound` as a hypothesis). |

## 13''''. `Estimation/OrthogonalLearning/` ‚ÄĒ Orthogonal Statistical Learning (function-valued targets)

Sibling submodule to `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/` and `Estimation/CATE/`: when the target is a function (e.g. CATE) instead of a scalar functional, "orthogonality" is Neyman-orthogonality of the *loss*, and the headline guarantee is a deterministic **oracle inequality** in the target-space norm rather than a CLT. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/orthogonal_statistical_learning.tex`. Phase-1 + Phase-2 + Phase-3 land the deterministic theorems and the DR-Learner orthogonality witness (abstract ‚ÄĒ concrete discharge of the mixed-DD bundle / DCT bridge / Riesz cancellation deferred to `Estimation/CATE/OrthogonalLearning/DRLearner/Analytic.lean`); concentration / modulus realisation (Phase 4) is deferred.

### 13''''.a `Estimation/OrthogonalLearning/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSLSystem ő© őľ Z P_Z őė G` | `structure` | Bundle: convex `őė_set ‚äÜ őė` (Hilbert-space-like), nuisance set `G_set ‚äÜ G`, loss `‚Ąď : Z ‚Üí őė ‚Üí G ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, joint measurability, truth `(őł‚āÄ, g‚āÄ)`. Statistical-learning analogue of `GeneralMoment`. Definition `def:est-osl-system`. |
| `OSLSystem.L S őł g` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Population risk `‚ąę ‚Ąď z őł g ‚ąāP_Z`. |
| `őė_PerturbClosed`, `G_PerturbClosed` | `def`, `Prop` | Closure of `őė_set` (resp. `G_set`) under line segments from the truth. |

### 13''''.h `Estimation/OrthogonalLearning/Sparse/Setup.lean`

| `RestrictedCone S‚āÄ` | `noncomputable def`, `Set` | Cone `{őĹ : ‚ÄĖőĹ_{S‚āÄŠ∂ú}‚ÄĖ‚āĀ ‚Č§ 3 ‚ÄĖőĹ_{S‚āÄ}‚ÄĖ‚āĀ}`. Definition `def:est-osl-sparse-plugin-erm` (cone). |
| `mem_RestrictedCone_iff` | `lemma` | Unfolding lemma. **Proved by Codex.** |

### 13''''.k' `Estimation/CATE/OrthogonalLearning/DRLearner/Analytic.lean` (Phase-3 analytic discharge)

Refines `drNeymanOrthog_witness` by *constructing* the closed-form `HasMixedDirDeriv` for the DR-Learner squared loss and discharging the pointwise tendsto obligations. The DCT swap and integrated orthogonality remain as hypotheses ‚ÄĒ see footnote.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `EvalDirDeriv őė_set őł‚āÄ eval` | `structure` | Directional-derivative bundle for the candidate-evaluation map `eval : őė ‚Üí ő≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`: carries `dEval őł x` (the dir-deriv value at `őł‚āÄ` in direction `őł ‚ąí őł‚āÄ`), pointwise tendsto, measurability, and a uniform `L‚ąě` bound on `dEval őł` (needed for DCT). For the linear function-class case `eval őł x = őł x`, `dEval őł x = őł x ‚ąí őł‚āÄ x`. |
| `NuisanceDirDeriv g‚āÄ` | `structure` | Directional-derivative bundle for `phi_eta` in the *nuisance* direction at `g‚āÄ`: carries `dPhi : NuisanceVec ő≥ ‚Üí (ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with pointwise tendsto and measurability. Required because `phi_eta` is reciprocal in `ő∑.e_fn`, hence non-linear in `ő∑`, and the value-difference `phi_eta z ő∑ ‚ąí phi_eta z g‚āÄ` is **not** the directional derivative. |
| `drMixedDirDeriv S hA hőĶ_pos h_overlap_ő∑‚āÄ őė őė_set őė_convex őł‚āÄ őł‚āÄ_mem eval eval_meas eval_őł‚āÄ D ND` | `noncomputable def`, `HasMixedDirDeriv (drOSLSystem ...)` | Closed-form mixed directional-derivative bundle for the DR-Learner loss: `(Dőł_at ő∑).d‚Ąď_őł őł z = ‚ąí2¬∑(phi_eta z ő∑ ‚ąí eval őł‚āÄ z.1)¬∑D.dEval őł z.1` and `d‚Ąď_őłg őł ő∑ z = ‚ąí2¬∑ND.dPhi ő∑ z¬∑D.dEval őł z.1`. Pointwise tendsto for both fields **proved by Codex** via the chain rule `a¬≤ ‚ąí b¬≤ = (a‚ąíb)(a+b)` on `(phi_eta z ő∑ ‚ąí eval őł z.1)¬≤` for the target field, and via direct substitution + `ND.pointwise` for the mixed field. |
| `dr_scoreZero_of_bounded` | `lemma` | **Honest discharge of the integrated-orthogonality obligation.** For any candidate target `őł` and any nuisance perturbation `ő∑ : NuisanceVec ő≥` whose direction `ő∑ ‚ąí ő∑‚āÄ` has uniformly bounded `őľ_fn`/`e_fn`, `‚ąę z, ND.dPhi ő∑ z ¬∑ D.dEval őł z.1 ‚ąāP_Z = 0`. **Proved by Codex** by (i) identifying `ND.dPhi ő∑ z = phi_eta_dir_deriv ő∑‚āÄ (ő∑ ‚ąí ő∑‚āÄ) z` via uniqueness of `nhdsWithin` limits applied to `ND.pointwise` and `phi_eta_dir_deriv_tendsto`, (ii) converting the `P_Z`-integral to a `P.őľ`-integral via `MeasureTheory.integral_map`, (iii) applying the conditional-expectation tower + ŌÉ(X)-measurable pull-out of `D.dEval ‚ąė factualX`, and (iv) plugging in `cond_exp_phi_eta_dir_deriv_at_truth_zero` to get the inner conditional is `0` a.e. Replaces the tautological `dr_mixedScoreDCTBridge` passthrough that previously sat in this file. |
| `dr_scoreFlat ... D ND hBridge` | `theorem` | Score-flatness on `BoundedNuisanceDirs ő∑‚āÄ`: for every `őł ‚ąą őė_set` and every `ő∑ ‚ąą BoundedNuisanceDirs ő∑‚āÄ`, the integrated score difference quotient tends to zero as `t ‚Üí 0`. The single remaining analytic hypothesis is `hBridge` (the DCT swap); the integrated-orthogonality obligation is discharged inside the proof by `dr_scoreZero_of_bounded`, applied to the boundedness witnesses extracted from `ő∑ ‚ąą BoundedNuisanceDirs ő∑‚āÄ`. The closing computation is `‚ąę M.d‚Ąď_őłg = ‚ąí2 ¬∑ ‚ąę ND.dPhi ¬∑ D.dEval = ‚ąí2 ¬∑ 0 = 0`. |
| `drNeymanOrthog ... D ND hBridge` | `theorem` | `NeymanOrthogLoss` for the DR-Learner loss at `(ŌĄ‚āÄ, ő∑‚āÄ)` over `BoundedNuisanceDirs ő∑‚āÄ`, parameterised by `D : EvalDirDeriv` and `ND : NuisanceDirDeriv`. The score-zero obligation is fully internalised: the only remaining analytic hypothesis is `hBridge` (the DCT swap). One-line proof: feed `hBridge` and `dr_scoreFlat` into `neymanOrthog_iff_score_deriv_zero`. Restricting `drOSLSystem.G_set` to `BoundedNuisanceDirs ő∑‚āÄ` is the standard semiparametric move that lets `dr_scoreZero_of_bounded` discharge the integrated-orthogonality obligation directly without integral-defaults-to-zero conventions for unbounded directions. |

### 13''''.l `Estimation/OrthogonalLearning/LocalEmpProcess/Rademacher.lean` (Phase-4a bridge)

Bridge between the vendored Rademacher / McDiarmid / Symmetrization
chain in `Causalean/Stat/Concentration/` and the OSL `LocalEmpProcessModulus`
predicate.  Pure consumption of FoML headlines plus
`Stat/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.foldB_pi_law` for the
`őľ‚ĀŅ`-on-`Fin m ‚Üí ő©` ‚ÜĒ `őľ`-on-`ő©` transport, and a generic-`DenseRange`
analog of `separableSpaceSup_eq_real` for the sup-lifting step.

**Why not `[Countable S.őė_set]`?**  Combined with `S.őė_convex`,
countability forces `S.őė_set.Subsingleton` (a non-trivial convex subset
of a real vector space contains a line segment, which is uncountable).
The bridge therefore takes an **explicit countable enumeration**
`idx : ‚Ąē ‚Üí S.őė_set` with `DenseRange idx` plus a continuity hypothesis
`LossContinuousOnőėset`, and uses the dense-range analog of FoML's
`separableSpaceSup_eq_real` to lift sup-over-‚Ąē to sup-over-`‚Ü•S.őė_set`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `foldB_pi_law` | `lemma` | Public alias of the (private) `oneShot_iid` from `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.lean`: `őľ.map (ŌČ ‚Ü¶ (i ‚Ü¶ S.Z i ŌČ)) = Measure.pi (fun _ ‚Ü¶ P)` over `i ‚ąą split.foldB n`. The bridge consumed by the McDiarmid/symmetrization chain to transport `őľ‚ĀŅ`-conclusions to fold B on `őľ`. |
| `RademacherBound S S_iid split g idx R` | `def`, `Prop` | Hypothesis: for every `n`, `0 ‚Č§ R n` and `rademacherComplexity (split.foldB n).card (k ‚Ü¶ ‚Ąď z (idx k).val g ‚ąí ‚Ąď z őł‚āÄ g) őľ (S_iid.Z 0) ‚Č§ R n`. Indexed over the *countable* index `‚Ąē` via the user-supplied `idx : ‚Ąē ‚Üí S.őė_set`. |
| `UniformlyBoundedLoss S g b` | `def`, `Prop` | `‚ąÄ z, ‚ąÄ őł ‚ąą őė_set, |‚Ąď z őł g| ‚Č§ b`. |
| `LossContinuousOnőėset S g` | `def`, `Prop` | `‚ąÄ z, Continuous (fun (őł : ‚Ü•S.őė_set) => S.‚Ąď z őł.val g)`. Needed by the dense-range lifting. |
| `separableSup_via_denseRange` | `private lemma` | Generic `DenseRange` analog of `FoML.separableSpaceSup_eq_real`: for continuous `f : X ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` on nonempty topological `X` with `DenseRange idx`, `‚®Ü x : X, f x = ‚®Ü k : ‚Ąē, f (idx k)`. Proved. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_eq_denseRange`, `rademacherComplexity_eq_denseRange` | `private lemma` | Empirical / population Rademacher complexity is preserved under re-indexing from `X` to `‚Ąē` via dense range, given continuity in `őł`. Proved. |
| `rademacherComplexity_map_id` | `private lemma` | `rademacherComplexity n f (őľ.map sample) id = rademacherComplexity n f őľ sample`. Pushforward simplification used in the transport step. Proved. |
| `populationRisk_abs_le_of_uniform`, `populationRisk_sub_le_two_mul_bound` | `private lemma` | `|S.L őł g| ‚Č§ b` and `S.L őł g ‚ąí S.L őł‚āÄ g ‚Č§ 2 b` for `őł ‚ąą őė_set`, derived from `UniformlyBoundedLoss` + `[IsProbabilityMeasure P_Z]`. The boundedness fallback used in the empty-fold (`m = 0`) case. Proved. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_of_bounded_rademacher` | `theorem` | **Phase-4a headline (proved).** Under `UniformlyBoundedLoss S g b`, `LossContinuousOnőėset S g`, an explicit countable dense `idx : ‚Ąē ‚Üí S.őė_set`, and `RademacherBound` against `idx`, conclude `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with `ŌĀ n := ‚ąö(if (split.foldB n).card = 0 then 2b else 2 R n + 2b ¬∑ ‚ąö(2 log(1/őī) / (split.foldB n).card))`. The nonempty-fold branch is the textbook `‚ąö(2 R_n + őĶ)` rate with `őĶ = 2b ¬∑ ‚ąö(2 log(1/őī)/|B(n)|)`; the `m = 0` branch uses `‚ąö(2b)` to dominate the boundedness fallback `|L őł g ‚ąí L őł‚āÄ g| ‚Č§ 2b`. Proof: case split on `m = 0` vs `m ‚Č• 1`, then on `b = 0` vs `b > 0`; for `m ‚Č• 1, b > 0` chains `expectation_le_rademacher` + `mcdiarmid_inequality_pos'` + `uniformDeviation_bounded_difference` on the ‚Ąē-indexed centred class, transports via `foldB_pi_law` + `Finset.orderIsoOfFin`, and lifts to sup-over-`‚Ü•S.őė_set` via `separableSup_via_denseRange`. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_singleton` | `theorem` | Smoke test: `őė_set = {őł‚āÄ}` ‚áí modulus holds with `ŌĀ ‚Č° 0`. Proved. |

### 13''''.m `Estimation/CATE/OrthogonalLearning/LocalEmpProcess/DRLearner.lean` (Phase-4a end-to-end)

Apply the Phase-4a bridge to the DR-Learner OSL system.  Real success
criterion for Phase 4a: yields `LocalEmpProcessModulus` for `(ŌĄŐā_n, ń•_n)`
under bounded eval / outcomes / nuisance L‚ąě / overlap.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DREvalBounded őė_set eval M_őė` | `def`, `Prop` | Uniform `|eval őł x| ‚Č§ M_őė` over `őė_set √ó ő≥`. |
| `DROutcomeBounded S M_Y` | `def`, `Prop` | Bounded outcomes type-level: `0 ‚Č§ M_Y ‚ąß ‚ąÄ z : ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ, |z.2.2| ‚Č§ M_Y`. The `‚ąÄ z` form (rather than `‚ąÄ ŌČ`) is needed because `UniformlyBoundedLoss` quantifies over all `z`. |
| `DRNuisanceOverlap S őė_set h őĶ` | `def`, `Prop` | Strict-overlap predicate: `0 < őĶ ‚ąß őĶ ‚Č§ 1/2 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ x, őĶ ‚Č§ h.e_fn x ‚ąß h.e_fn x ‚Č§ 1 ‚ąí őĶ`. Carries the propensity-bracketing slice condition (no longer a `True`-stub). |
| `DRNuisanceMuBounded h M_őľ` | `def`, `Prop` | Uniform L‚ąě bound on the outcome-regression nuisance: `0 ‚Č§ M_őľ ‚ąß ‚ąÄ b x, |h.őľ_fn b x| ‚Č§ M_őľ`. Required for boundedness of the AIPW pseudo-outcome. |
| `dr_loss_uniformly_bounded` | `theorem` | Bounded eval + outcomes + nuisance L‚ąě + overlap ‚áí DR squared loss is uniformly bounded over `őė_set` at `h` by the **explicit** value `b := (M_őė + 2¬∑M_őľ + 2¬∑(M_Y + M_őľ)/őĶ)¬≤`. Proved by Codex via the AIPW pseudo-outcome triangle bound `\|phi_eta z h\| ‚Č§ 2 M_őľ + 2 (M_Y + M_őľ) / őĶ`. (Returns `UniformlyBoundedLoss ... b` directly ‚ÄĒ no existential ‚ÄĒ so callers can write FS-style hypotheses against the explicit value.) |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_drLearner` | `theorem` | End-to-end: instantiates `localEmpProcessModulus_of_bounded_rademacher` for the DR-Learner `OSLSystem`. Takes `idx : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ü•(drOSLSystem ...).őė_set`, `idx_dense : DenseRange idx`, and `LossContinuousOnőėset` for the DR loss in addition to the `(M_őė, M_Y, M_őľ, őĶ)` quartet. Concludes the tightened piecewise rate, with textbook `‚ąö(2 R_n + őĶ)` on nonempty folds and `‚ąö(2b)` when the fold is empty. Proved as a thin wrapper. |

### 13''''.o `Stat/Concentration/{StarHull,Contraction,CriticalRadius,LocalRademacher,UniformDeviationLocalized}.lean` (Phase-4b localized chain)

Path-B (McDiarmid + symmetrization + contraction) localized empirical-process toolbox. Sibling to Phase-4a `Stat/Concentration/{Rademacher,...}` shims, but contains *new* formalization (not vendored). Headline: `localized_uniform_deviation` with shape `r¬∑őī_n + őī_n¬≤` matching Foster‚ÄďSyrgkanis 2019 Lemma 29 up to log factors. Plan reference: `.claude/plans/i-just-added-a-rosy-hennessy.md`, Phase 4b.

#### 13''''.o.1 `Stat/Concentration/StarHull.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `starHull F` | `def`, `Set (ūĚí≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | `{őĪ ‚ÄĘ F i : i ‚ąą őĻ, őĪ ‚ąą [0,1]}`. Bartlett‚ÄďBousquet‚ÄďMendelson 2005 star-hull around 0. |
| `starHullIndex F` | `noncomputable def` | Subtype re-index `{f // f ‚ąą starHull F}` for downstream Rademacher arguments. |
| `mem_starHull_self`, `zero_mem_starHull`, `starHull_mono`, `starHull_smul_mem` | `lemma`s (`sorry`) | Algebra: `F i ‚ąą starHull F`, `0 ‚ąą starHull F`, monotonicity, scalar closure on `[0,1]`. |

#### 13''''.o.2 `Stat/Concentration/Contraction.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LipschitzAt0 ŌÜ L` | `def`, `Prop` | `ŌÜ 0 = 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ x y, |ŌÜ x ‚ąí ŌÜ y| ‚Č§ L |x ‚ąí y|`. The Lipschitz-with-`ŌÜ(0)=0` regime needed by Ledoux‚ÄďTalagrand. |
| `rademacher_contraction` | `theorem` | **Ledoux‚ÄďTalagrand 1991 Thm 4.12 (signed form).** `RŐā_n^{noabs}(ŌÜ ‚ąė F, S) ‚Č§ L ¬∑ RŐā_n^{noabs}(F, S)` for L-Lipschitz `ŌÜ` with `ŌÜ 0 = 0`. **Proved by Codex** via the hybrid-induction argument: a sign-pair Lipschitz inequality (`sup_pair_lipschitz_scaled`) bounds the two-sign sum at one coordinate, then a hybrid average `hybridAverage` interpolating between `ŌÜ ‚ąė F` and `L ¬∑ F` is shown monotone in the number of swapped coordinates (`hybridAverage_mono_step`); telescoping recovers the headline. |
| `rademacher_contraction_abs` | `theorem` | Absolute-value form: `RŐā_n(ŌÜ ‚ąė F, S) ‚Č§ 2¬∑L¬∑RŐā_n(F, S)`. **Proved by Codex** by reducing to the signed form applied to `ŌÜ` and `‚ąíŌÜ` (via `lipschitzAt0_neg`), bounding `|x| ‚Č§ x‚Āļ + x‚ĀĽ` over the Rademacher sup. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_contraction_abs_of_bddAbove` | `theorem` | **Infinite-index contraction.** Same `RŐā_n(ŌÜ ‚ąė F, S) ‚Č§ 2L¬∑RŐā_n(F, S)` as `rademacher_contraction_abs` but for an ARBITRARY `[Nonempty őĻ]` (not just `Fintype`), assuming a uniform bound `|F i x| ‚Č§ M`. Reduces the infinite-index case to the Fintype lemma by choosing, for each of the finitely many sign vectors, an `őĶ`-approximate maximizer; their finite collection is a finite subindex. This is what lets the squared-loss kernel/lasso ERM (whose weight ball is infinite) use the contraction. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_sub_le` | `theorem` | **Sub-additivity over class differences.** `RŐā_n(F ‚ąí G, S) ‚Č§ RŐā_n(F, S) + RŐā_n(G, S)` (uniform bounds on `F`, `G`). Splits the centered squared loss `‚ü™w,x‚ü©¬≤ ‚ąí 2y‚ü™w,x‚ü©` into its quadratic and cross parts. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_smul_class`, `empiricalRademacherComplexity_without_abs_smul_class` | `theorem` | Smoke test: `RŐā_n(c‚ÄĘF) = |c|¬∑RŐā_n(F)` (resp. signed analogue). **Proved.** |
| `rademacher_swap_step`, `rademacher_telescope` | `private lemma` | Sign-swap proof scaffolds; **proved** as wrappers around the headline `rademacher_contraction` after the hybrid-induction proof landed. Kept private to flag the proof outline. |
| `SignAtom`, `flipSign`, `flipSignEquiv` | `private def` / `Equiv` | `Signs n` infrastructure: `SignAtom = {-1, 1}` finset element, single-coordinate sign flip and the sign-flip equivalence used to re-index `‚ąĎ_ŌÉ` after a coordinate swap. **Proved.** |
| `sup_pair_lipschitz_scaled` | `private lemma` | Sign-pair Lipschitz inequality on `iSup`: `(‚®Ü a + c¬∑s¬∑ŌÜ b) + (‚®Ü a ‚ąí c¬∑s¬∑ŌÜ b) ‚Č§ (‚®Ü a + c¬∑s¬∑L¬∑b) + (‚®Ü a ‚ąí c¬∑s¬∑L¬∑b)` for `s ‚ąą {‚ąí1, +1}`, `c ‚Č• 0`. The single-coordinate engine of the contraction proof. **Proved.** |
| `hybridInner`, `hybridAverage`, `hybridBase` | `private noncomputable def` | The hybrid signed-Rademacher quantity: at parameter `m`, replaces `ŌÜ(F i (S k))` by `L¬∑F i (S k)` for `k < m` and keeps `ŌÜ` for `k ‚Č• m`. `hybridInner` is the per-`ŌÉ`-per-`i` summand; `hybridAverage` is `(1/2‚ĀŅ) ő£_ŌÉ ‚®Ü_i hybridInner`; `hybridBase` factors out the `k`-th coordinate for the swap step. |
| `hybridAverage_mono_step` | `private lemma` | Monotonicity of the hybrid average in `m`: `hybridAverage m ‚Č§ hybridAverage (m+1)` for `m < n`, by applying `sup_pair_lipschitz_scaled` to the two-sign sum at coordinate `m` after re-indexing via `flipSignEquiv`. **Proved.** |
| `rademacher_contraction_core` | `private lemma` | The actual contraction inequality on the without-abs form, before the `L`-scaling re-arrangement. Iterates `hybridAverage_mono_step` from `m = 0` to `m = n`, then closes by the without-abs scaling lemma. **Proved.** |
| `lipschitzAt0_neg` | `private lemma` | `LipschitzAt0 ŌÜ L ‚Üí LipschitzAt0 (fun x => ‚ąíŌÜ x) L`. **Proved.** |
| `iSup_abs_le_iSup_add_iSup_neg_of_exists_zero` | `private lemma` | `‚®Ü |x i| ‚Č§ (‚®Ü x i) + (‚®Ü (‚ąíx i))` when `0` lies in the family (i.e. for any class containing the zero function). **Proved.** |
| `withZero F` | `private def` | The class `F` augmented with the zero function, used to ensure both sup's are non-negative for the abs-form bound. |

#### 13''''.o.6 `Stat/Concentration/LocalizedEnvelopeExpectation.lean`

In-expectation (and self-localizing offset) forms of the localized deviation bound, for downstream rate arguments that need an expectation rather than a high-probability envelope. Both are general empirical-process facts over a `LocalizedRegime`; the margin-coupled / VC specializations (turning the `norm`-radius into a problem-specific regret radius) are supplied by the caller. Built to discharge the localized finite-VC envelope gates of `stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `localized_uniform_deviation_expectation` | `theorem` | **Fixed-radius in-expectation envelope.** Steps 1‚Äď2 of the Path-B argument (symmetrization via FoML `uniform_deviation_expectation_le_two_smul_rademacher_complexity` + critical radius via `localRademacher_le_critical_radius`), stopping before the McDiarmid tail. For the radius-`r` zero-out localization, `ūĚĒľ[uniformDeviation over {norm(F i) ‚Č§ r}] ‚Č§ 2¬∑r¬∑criticalRadius(Ōą n)`. The in-expectation analogue of `localized_uniform_deviation`. **Proved (Codex), axiom-clean.** |
| `localized_offset_expectation` | `theorem` | **Self-localizing offset positive-part envelope.** Given a margin coupling `norm(F i) ‚Č§ A¬∑(őĒ i)^őļ` (the caller's regret radius `őĒ`, `0<őļ<1`), `ūĚĒľ[‚®Ü i, {2¬∑|(P‚āô‚ąíP)F i| ‚ąí őĒ i/4}_+] ‚Č§ offsetPeelingConstantC (1/8) őļ ¬∑ (16ŌĀA)^{1/(1‚ąíőļ)} + 10ŌĀ¬≤ + 4¬∑R.b¬∑őī`. Assembled from the high-probability `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp`, the deterministic Young/AM-GM optimisation `OffsetPeeling.offset_peeling_coeff`, and a bounded bad-event tail split. Caller picks `ŌĀ` = VC critical-radius rate and `őī` polynomially small for a clean `(B¬≤/n)^{A_őĪ}(log n)^p` rate. **Proved (Codex), axiom-clean.** |

Sibling to `OrthogonalLearning/LocalEmpProcess/Rademacher.lean` (Phase 4a). Discharges `LocalEmpProcessModulus` from `localized_uniform_deviation` with the localized rate `ŌĀ_n ‚Čą criticalRadius Ōą`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OSLLocalizedRegime S S_iid split g idx norm Ōą b` | `def`, `Prop` | Phase 4b redesigned predicate: `0 ‚Č§ b`, centred-loss boundedness `|‚Ąď z őł g ‚ąí ‚Ąď z őł‚āÄ g| ‚Č§ b`, `SubRoot Ōą`, and `RademacherUpperBound` for the centred ‚Ąē-indexed loss class (re-indexed via dense `idx : ‚Ąē ‚Üí S.őė_set`) on a fold-B-sized population sample. The Lipschitz-link slot `L_ŌÜ` is **dropped** ‚ÄĒ the envelope `Ōą` lives directly on the centred loss class. |
| `empRiskFoldB_centered_abs_le` | `private lemma` | Pointwise centred-loss bound `\|‚Ąď z őł g ‚ąí ‚Ąď z őł‚āÄ g\| ‚Č§ b` averages to `\|empRiskFoldB őł ‚ąí empRiskFoldB őł‚āÄ\| ‚Č§ b`. **Proved.** |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_bounded` | `theorem` | **Uniform-bound fallback bridge (legitimate low-hypothesis option).** Under `OSLLocalizedRegime` + a population centred-risk bound `hpop_center : ‚ąÄ őł ‚ąą őė_set, \|S.L őł g ‚ąí S.L őł‚āÄ g\| ‚Č§ b`, concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with the *constant* envelope `ŌĀ n := ‚ąö(2b)`. **Proved.** Does NOT use `Ōą`, critical-radius, or any Lipschitz / Rademacher-integrability machinery ‚ÄĒ purely bounded-loss. Useful when those inputs are unavailable; rate does not vanish in `n`. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_sharp` | `theorem` | **Phase-4b sharp headline ‚ÄĒ Foster‚ÄďSyrgkanis Lemma 29 form (proved).** Under `OSLLocalizedRegime` plus an explicit Lipschitz constant `(L : ‚ĄĚ) (hL_nonneg) (hF_lip : norm (z‚Ü¶‚Ąď z őł g ‚ąí ‚Ąď z őł‚āÄ g) ‚Č§ L ¬∑ ‚ÄĖőł‚ąíőł‚āÄ‚ÄĖ)`, diameter `Rmax`, the n-dependent critical-radius hypotheses, Rademacher prerequisites, and the **peeling-aware** FS critical-radius lower bound `hőī_dom : ‚ąÄ n K, 0 < (split.foldB n).card ‚Üí Rmax ‚Č§ őī_n ¬∑ 2^K ‚Üí b ¬∑ ‚ąö(2 log(2(K+1)/őī)/m) ‚Č§ őī_n¬≤`, concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with the FS Lemma 29 rate `ŌĀ n := if (split.foldB n).card = 0 then ‚ąö(2b) else (8L+3) ¬∑ criticalRadius (Ōą (split.foldB n).card)`. **Proved (Codex)** via dyadic peeling + fold-B transport (mirroring `OrthogonalLearning/LocalEmpProcess/Rademacher.lean`) + FS-absorption. Axiom-clean. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_singleton` | `theorem` | Singleton smoke-test (őė_set = {őł‚āÄ} ‚áí ŌĀ ‚Č° 0). **Proved.** |

Apply `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized` to the DR-Learner. Verification step 6 of Phase 4b.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `DRCriticalRadius S őė_set eval idx h norm Ōą` | `def`, `Prop` | Phase 4b redesigned predicate: `SubRoot Ōą ‚ąß ‚ąÄ n, RademacherUpperBound (centred quadratic DR-loss class indexed by idx) norm S.P_Z id n Ōą`. The DR centred class is `(ŌÜ_h(z) ‚ąí eval (idx k) z.1)¬≤ ‚ąí (ŌÜ_h(z) ‚ąí S.ŌĄ_val z.1)¬≤`. (Deterministic / population ‚ÄĒ Risk #10.) |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_localized_drLearner` | `theorem` | **Phase-4b success criterion (sharp Foster‚ÄďSyrgkanis Lemma 29 form, proved).** End-to-end: instantiates `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_sharp` for the DR-Learner OSL system. Takes `(M_őė, M_Y, M_őľ, őĶ)` boundedness/overlap, `[Countable őė_set]`, dense `idx`, `DRCriticalRadius`, an explicit Lipschitz constant `(L, hL_nonneg, hF_lip)` for the centred DR-loss class, the diameter `Rmax`, the n-dependent critical-radius facts, the Rademacher prerequisites, and the **peeling-aware** FS lower-bound `hőī_dom : ‚ąÄ n K, 0 < |B(n)| ‚Üí Rmax ‚Č§ őī_n ¬∑ 2^K ‚Üí 2¬∑b_loss¬∑‚ąö(2 log(2(K+1)/őī)/m) ‚Č§ őī_n¬≤` (stated against `b_loss = (M_őė + 2¬∑M_őľ + 2¬∑(M_Y+M_őľ)/őĶ)¬≤`). Concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with the FS Lemma 29 rate `ŌĀ n := if (split.foldB n).card = 0 then ‚ąö(2b) else (8L+3) ¬∑ criticalRadius (Ōą (split.foldB n).card)`. When chained with `OracleInequality`, gives the Foster‚ÄďSyrgkanis `O_p(őī_n¬≤ + nuisance¬≤)` rate. |
| `localEmpProcessModulus_localized_drLearner_bounded` | `theorem` | **Phase-4b end-to-end (uniform-bound fallback variant).** Working chain that calls `localEmpProcessModulus_of_localized_bounded` with `b := 2¬∑dr_loss_bound`. Concludes `LocalEmpProcessModulus` with the *constant* rate `ŌĀ n := ‚ąö(2 ¬∑ 2b)` ‚ÄĒ does NOT use the critical radius. **Proved.** Useful as a low-hypothesis option when the sharp hypotheses (Lipschitz, Rademacher integrability, FS lower bound) are not available. |
