## 13gm. `Estimation/GaussMarkov/` ‚ÄĒ Finite Gauss-Markov / BLUE layer

Reusable finite Gauss-Markov theory: the variance of a linear estimator, spherical/general covariance, the OLS weight via the normal-equations pseudoinverse, and the minimum-variance (BLUE) ordering theorems. Built on Mathlib `Matrix` (`mulVec`/`vecMul`/`dotProduct`, `nonsing_inv`, `PosSemidef`) and `ProbabilityTheory.covariance`/`variance`. Files: `QuadForm.lean` (variance object), `LeastNorm.lean` (algebraic ordering), `OLS.lean` (OLS weight), `Variance.lean` (probability bridge), `BLUE.lean` (variance-form BLUE). Consumed by the imputation event-study efficiency theorem (¬ß9i0a-ImputationEventStudyEfficiency). No `sorry`/`admit`/`axiom`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GaussMarkov.quadVar` | `Matrix Obs Obs ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí (Obs ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Variance of a linear estimator with weight `w` under covariance `ő£`: the quadratic form `w ‚¨ĚŠĶ• ő£ *ŠĶ• w` (`= wŠĶÄő£w`) | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.SphericalErrors` | `Matrix Obs Obs ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí Prop` | Spherical (homoskedastic, uncorrelated) covariance: `ő£ = ŌÉ¬≤ ‚ÄĘ I` | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.quadVar_spherical` | `SphericalErrors S ŌÉ ‚Üí quadVar S w = ŌÉ¬≤ * (w ‚¨ĚŠĶ• w)` | Under spherical errors the variance form reduces to `ŌÉ¬≤‚ÄĖw‚ÄĖ¬≤` | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.quadVar_nonneg` | `S.PosSemidef ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ quadVar S w` | Variance form of a PSD covariance is nonnegative | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.colSpan_dotProduct_leftNull` | `z ŠĶ•* X = 0 ‚Üí (X *ŠĶ• g) ‚¨ĚŠĶ• z = 0` | Column span ‚üā left null space (the orthogonality at the heart of Gauss-Markov) | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.gauss_markov_spherical` | `SphericalErrors S ŌÉ ‚Üí wStar = X *ŠĶ• g ‚Üí wStar ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí w ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí quadVar S wStar ‚Č§ quadVar S w` | **Finite Gauss-Markov, spherical:** among weights with the same unbiasedness image, an OLS weight (in the column span of `X`) minimizes variance (Pythagoras) | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.gauss_markov_gls` | `S.PosSemidef ‚Üí S *ŠĶ• wStar = X *ŠĶ• g ‚Üí wStar ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí w ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí quadVar S wStar ‚Č§ quadVar S w` | **Finite Gauss-Markov, general (GLS):** for any PSD covariance, a GLS weight (`ő£ wStar` in column span) minimizes variance | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.olsWeight` | `Matrix Obs Param ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí (Param ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Obs ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | OLS weight via the normal-equations pseudoinverse: `X (XŠĶÄX)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ c` | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.olsWeight_unbiased` | `IsUnit (XŠĶÄ * X).det ‚Üí olsWeight X c ŠĶ•* X = c` | The OLS weight is unbiased when `XŠĶÄX` is invertible (full column rank) | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.olsWeight_mem_colSpan` | `olsWeight X c = X *ŠĶ• ((XŠĶÄ * X)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ *ŠĶ• c)` | The OLS weight lies in the column span of `X` | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.olsWeight_blue_spherical` | `IsUnit (XŠĶÄ*X).det ‚Üí SphericalErrors S ŌÉ ‚Üí w ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí quadVar S (olsWeight X c) ‚Č§ quadVar S w` | OLS is BLUE (variance-form) under spherical errors | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.covMatrix` | `(Obs ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Measure ő© ‚Üí Matrix Obs Obs ‚ĄĚ` | Covariance matrix of a finite random family, `ő£ i j = cov(YŠĶĘ, Y‚Īľ)` | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.variance_linearCombination` | `(‚ąÄ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 őľ) ‚Üí Var[‚ąĎ i, w i * Y i; őľ] = quadVar (covMatrix Y őľ) w` | **Probability bridge:** the variance of a random linear estimator equals the covariance quadratic form | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.SphericalFamily` | `(Obs ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Measure ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí Prop` | Random family with spherical errors: each cell variance `ŌÉ¬≤`, distinct cells uncorrelated | **Defined** |
| `GaussMarkov.sphericalFamily_covMatrix` | `SphericalFamily Y őľ ŌÉ ‚Üí SphericalErrors (covMatrix Y őľ) ŌÉ` | A spherical family has scalar-identity covariance matrix | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.covMatrix_posSemidef` | `(‚ąÄ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 őľ) ‚Üí (covMatrix Y őľ).PosSemidef` | A covariance matrix is positive semidefinite | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.variance_blue_spherical` | `(‚ąÄ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 őľ) ‚Üí SphericalFamily Y őľ ŌÉ ‚Üí wStar = X *ŠĶ• g ‚Üí wStar ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí w ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí Var[‚ąĎ wStar¬∑Y] ‚Č§ Var[‚ąĎ w¬∑Y]` | **BLUE (variance form), spherical:** OLS minimizes actual `ProbabilityTheory.variance` in the linear-unbiased class | **Proved** |
| `GaussMarkov.variance_blue_gls` | `(‚ąÄ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 őľ) ‚Üí (covMatrix Y őľ) *ŠĶ• wStar = X *ŠĶ• g ‚Üí wStar ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí w ŠĶ•* X = c ‚Üí Var[‚ąĎ wStar¬∑Y] ‚Č§ Var[‚ąĎ w¬∑Y]` | **BLUE (variance form), general:** GLS minimizes variance for the family's own covariance | **Proved** |

---

## 13. `Estimation/ATE/` ‚ÄĒ Back-door ATE estimation and inference

Specific-system layer instantiating `POBackdoorSystem`. Mirrors
`doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/{aipw_if,plug_in_ate,dml_ate}.tex`. The
concrete `dml_ATE_isAsymLinear` in `Estimation/ATE/DML.lean` remains the
production AIPW asymptotic-linearity headline; the abstract framework in
`Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/*` provides the abstractions, and `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear`
is **proved** (the őł‚āÄ-shift gap was closed by the 2026-05-06 per-ő∑Őā bilinear-remainder
refactor; see ¬ß12g.6).

### 13a. `Estimation/ATE/InfluenceFunction.lean` (umbrella)

Single-import aggregator that re-exports the five sub-modules below.
`PlugIn.lean` and `DML.lean` import this file. Refactored from the original
~900-line monolithic file.

#### 13a.1 `Estimation/ATE/Setup.lean`

`BackdoorEstimationSystem` structure, data law, value-space estimand.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BackdoorEstimationSystem P ő≥` | structure | Extends `POBackdoorSystem` with value-space `őľ_val : Bool √ó ő≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, `e_val : ő≥ ‚Üí (0,1)`, and the **observable** compat fields `őľ_reg_compat : őľ_val d ‚ąė factualX =ŠĶź adjustedCE d` (the ML/regression target ‚ÄĒ NO assumptions; see `regression_adjustment`), `e_compat : propScore true =ŠĶź e_val ‚ąė factualX`. **The counterfactual reading `őľ[Y(d)\|ŌÉX] =ŠĶź őľ_val d ‚ąė factualX` is no longer a field** ‚ÄĒ it is the derived lemma `őľ_compat` below. |
| `BackdoorEstimationSystem.őľ_compat` | `(hA : Assumptions) (d) ‚Üí őľ[Y(d)\|ŌÉX] =ŠĶź őľ_val d ‚ąė factualX` | **Derived counterfactual compatibility** (was a structure field): `cate_backdoor hA d` composed with `őľ_reg_compat`. The counterfactual binding now correctly requires identification, not structural data. **Proved.** |
| `POBackdoorSystem.toBackdoorEstimationSystem` | `(S : POBackdoorSystem) (hov : two-sided overlap a.e.) (hY : Integrable factualY) ‚Üí BackdoorEstimationSystem P ő≥` | **Derivability constructor.** Certifies the structure adds no assumptions beyond `POBackdoorSystem` + overlap + integrability: `őľ_val := regFn`, `e_val := eLift` clamped into (0,1) off-support; all fields discharged (`őľ_reg_compat` from `regression_adjustment`, `e_pos`/`e_lt_one` from the clamp, `e_compat` from overlap). **Proved.** |
| `StrictOverlap S őĶ` | `Prop` | `0 < őĶ`, `őĶ ‚Č§ 1/2`, and `őĶ ‚Č§ propScore true ŌČ ‚Č§ 1‚ąíőĶ` a.s. |
| `P_X`, `P_Z`, `factualZ` | `def`s | Covariate marginal `őľ.map factualX`, joint data law `őľ.map (X,A,Y)`, and the data triple. |
| `measurable_factualZ` | `Measurable S.factualZ` | Product-measurability. **Proved.** |
| `őł‚āÄ S` | `def` | Value-space estimand `‚ąę x, őľ_val(1,x) ‚ąí őľ_val(0,x) ‚ąāP_X`. |
| `őł‚āÄ_eq_ATE` | `S.őł‚āÄ = S.ATE` | Reduces to `integral_map` + `őľ_compat hA` + `condExp_sub` + `integral_condExp`. **Proved.** |

#### 13a.5 `Estimation/ATE/Archived/` ‚ÄĒ G√Ęteaux-derivative Neyman witness (archived 2026-05-06)

The textbook G√Ęteaux-derivative Neyman orthogonality witness for AIPW
(`aipw_neyman` and the supporting `NeymanAux/*` helpers) was archived after
the 2026-05-06 refactor of `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear`, which closes the DML
asymptotic-linearity proof through the per-ő∑Őā bilinear-remainder bound
(`aipw_remainder_bound`) directly without going through a directional
derivative.  The archived files are retained for reference; they no longer
participate in the live build.

* `Archived/Neyman.lean` ‚ÄĒ `aipw_neyman` (Sorry; G√Ęteaux-form Neyman orthogonality on `H_őĶ`).
* `Archived/NeymanAux.lean` ‚ÄĒ umbrella import.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/Overlap.lean` ‚ÄĒ perturbation algebra and overlap bounds.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/Score.lean` ‚ÄĒ assignment-residual pull-out.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/{MuTrueResidual,MuFalseResidual,ETrueResidual,EFalseResidual}.lean` ‚ÄĒ four directional residual-zero integrals.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/Residual.lean` ‚ÄĒ residual umbrella.
* `Archived/NeymanAux/Directional.lean` ‚ÄĒ `aipwDirDeriv`, `measurable_aipwDirDeriv`, `aipwMoment_diff_quotient_tendsto`, `integral_aipwDirDeriv_eq_zero` (Sorry), `integral_aipwMoment_diff_quotient_tendsto` (Sorry).

L¬≤(P_Z) continuity of `m_AIPW(ő∑, ¬∑, őł‚āÄ)` in `ő∑` on `H_őĶ`. Consumed by the DML `R‚āā` empirical-process step.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `K_AIPW őĶ` | `def` | Lipschitz constant `1 + 2/őĶ + 2/őĶ¬≤`. |
| `aipw_score_diff_pointwise_bound` | `‚ąÄŠĶź z ‚ąāP_Z, \|m_AIPW(ő∑,z,őł‚āÄ) ‚ąí m_AIPW(ő∑‚āÄ,z,őł‚āÄ)\| ‚Č§ K_AIPW őĶ ¬∑ (ő£_a \|őĒőľ_a\| + (\|y‚ąíőľ‚āĀ\|+\|y‚ąíőľ‚āÄ\|)¬∑\|őĒe\|)` | `P_Z`-a.e. Lipschitz on `H_őĶ`. Statement is a.e. (not pointwise) so `S.StrictOverlap őĶ` (`P.őľ`-a.s.) suffices: it transfers via `e_compat` and pushforward to `őĶ ‚Č§ e_val ‚ąė projX ‚Č§ 1‚ąíőĶ` `P_Z`-a.s., then the algebraic Lipschitz step closes. **Proved by Codex.** |

Lipschitz bound, `P_Z.map projX = P_X`, and a truncation argument for the residual-weighted propensity-error cross term. **Proved.** |

### 13b. `Estimation/ATE/PlugIn.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `plugInEstimator` | `def` | `(1/\|B(n)\|) ő£_{i ‚ąą B(n)} (őľŐā(n)(1, X_i) ‚ąí őľŐā(n)(0, X_i))`. |
| `Ōą_plugin` | `def` | Plug-in IF `Ōą_plugin(z) = őľ_val(1, x) ‚ąí őľ_val(0, x) ‚ąí őł‚āÄ`. |
| `plugIn_isAsymLinear` | Anchor (C) | Asymptotic linearity at `őł‚āÄ` with IF `Ōą_plugin`, **indexed over `split.foldB`**. Hypotheses now include joint measurability `h_mu_meas : ‚ąÄ n a, Measurable (fun (p : P.ő© √ó ő≥) => őľ_hat n p.1 a p.2)`, the new joint fold-A measurability `h_mu_uncurry_foldA : ‚ąÄ n a, Measurable[m_A.prod ŌÉ_ő≥] (fun p => őľ_hat n p.1 a p.2)` (consumed by the new `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` bridge), and `MemLp` `h_mu_memLp : ‚ąÄ n ŌČ a, MemLp (fun x => őľ_hat n ŌČ a x) 2 S.P_X` (classical implicit assumptions). All three bullets **proved**: `mean_zero`, `finite_var` inline; `remainder` decomposes into `G + B` (centered fold-B sum + bias) via `foldB_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` and `sqrtFoldB_integral_isLittleOp_one` plus the `S.P_Z.map projX = S.P_X` bridge. **Modulo the single probability sorry in `IIDCenteredSum.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le` + the pre-existing Markov + DCT sorry in `foldB_centered_sum_truncated_isLittleOp_one`**. |
| `plugIn_tendstoNormal` | `‚ąö\|B(n)\| (őłŐā‚ĀŅ ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ‚ąęŌą¬≤_plugin dP_Z)` | "In particular ..." clause of `thm:est-plug-in-ate-al`: composes `plugIn_isAsymLinear` with `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB` from `Stat/SampleSplit/PartialFoldCLT.lean`. **Proved** modulo the existing AL `sorry`. The `‚ąön`-rate form `‚ąön (őłŐā‚ĀŅ ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ŌÉ¬≤/c)` is one further Slutsky scaling step (cf. `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB_sqrt_n`). |

### 13c. `Estimation/ATE/DML.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlEstimator` | `def` | `(1/\|B(n)\|) ő£_{i ‚ąą B(n)} m_AIPW(ő∑Őā(n), Z_i, _)`. |
| `dml_ATE_isAsymLinear` | Anchor (B) | Asymptotic linearity at `őł‚āÄ` with IF `Ōą_AIPW`, **indexed over `split.foldB`**. **Migrated 2026-05-06** to a thin wrapper over the abstract `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear` (Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AIPWInstance.lean): builds `ő∑_hat n ŌČ : NuisanceVec ő≥` from `(őľ_hat n ŌČ, e_hat n ŌČ)`, derives per-ő∑Őā_n L¬≤-of-differences from `h_mu_memLp` / `h_e_memLp` (plus inline derivations of `MemLp (S.őľ_val a)` via condExp and `MemLp S.e_val` via boundedness), translates the production rate hypotheses into the abstract `ŌĀ‚āĀ = ‚ÄĖőĒőľ_T‚ÄĖ‚āā + ‚ÄĖőĒőľ_F‚ÄĖ‚āā` / `ŌĀ‚āā = ‚ÄĖőĒe‚ÄĖ‚āā` shape (sum-of-`o_p` arguments), passes the score measurability/integrability bundle, applies the abstract theorem, then transports the conclusion field-by-field via two pointwise equalities: (i) IF equality `‚ąíJ‚āÄ_inv ¬∑ aipwMomentFunctional S.ő∑‚āÄ z S.őł‚āÄ = S.Ōą_AIPW z` (`J‚āÄ = ‚ąí1` ‚áí `‚ąíJ‚āÄ_inv = 1`); (ii) rescaled-error equality `‚ąö\|B(n)\| ¬∑ (dmlChern ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) = ‚ąö\|B(n)\| ¬∑ (dmlEstimator ‚ąí őł‚āÄ)` proved by case-split on `\|B(n)\| = 0` (both sides vanish via `‚ąö0 = 0`) vs `\|B(n)\| > 0` (algebraic identity using `aipwMoment(z, őľŐā, √™, 0) = aipwMoment(z, őľŐā, √™, S.őł‚āÄ) + S.őł‚āÄ`). The two-step transport correctly handles the empty-fold edge case where the underlying estimators differ pointwise but their rescaled errors agree. Net effect: the original ~615-line proof body (centered fold-B sum + bias decomposition expanded inline) is replaced by ~150 lines of structural translation, with all heavy lifting delegated to the OrthogonalMoments/ abstractions. New hypothesis vs the old version: `h_e_val_pointwise : ‚ąÄ x, őĶ ‚Č§ S.e_val x ‚ąß S.e_val x ‚Č§ 1 ‚ąí őĶ` (needed to construct `aipwGeneralMoment`'s `ő∑‚āÄ_mem` field). The `h_L2 : ‚ąÄ ő∑ ‚ąą H_őĶ őĶ, ‚Ä¶` hypothesis was NOT added ‚ÄĒ it's eliminated by the parallel (2b) refactor of `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear`. **Proved** (no sorries). |
| `dml_ATE_tendstoNormal` | `‚ąö\|B(n)\| (őłŐā‚ĀŅ ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ‚ąęŌą¬≤_AIPW dP_Z)` | "In particular ..." clause of `thm:est-dml-ate-al`: composes `dml_ATE_isAsymLinear` with `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB`. **Proved.** Signature gained one extra hypothesis `h_e_val_pointwise` (forwarded to `dml_ATE_isAsymLinear` after the 2026-05-06 migration). The `‚ąön`-rate / `ŌÉ¬≤/c` form is the same downstream Slutsky scaling. |

### 13d. `Estimation/ATE/Remainder.lean`

AIPW second-order remainder bound (split into `RemainderIdentity.lean` and
`RemainderBound.lean`).  Combines the algebraic remainder identity with the
overlap-bounded Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz step to convert the L¬≤-product rate hypothesis
from `DML.lean` into the `o_p(n^{-1/2})` cross-term that closes `R‚āĀ` of the
three-remainder DML decomposition.  After the 2026-05-06 refactor this is the
sole route to Neyman orthogonality on the live AL critical path; the
G√Ęteaux-derivative witness `aipw_neyman` was archived to
`Estimation/ATE/Archived/Neyman.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipw_rem_const őĶ` | `def` | The constant `2 / (őĶ(1‚ąíőĶ))`, the uniform weight bound on `H_őĶ`. |
| `plugin_bias_le_eLpNorm` | `\|‚ąę (őľŐā ‚ąí őľ_val) dP_X\| ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖőľŐā ‚ąí őľ_val‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_X)}` | Direct corollary of `abs_integral_le_eLpNorm_two` since `P_X` is a probability measure. **Proved.** (`RemainderIdentity.lean`) |
| `aipw_remainder_identity` | `‚ąę aipwMomentFunctional ő∑ z őł‚āÄ dP_Z = ‚ąęx ...` | Exact identity rewriting the remainder as cross terms before Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz. **Proved.** (`RemainderIdentity.lean`) |
| `aipw_remainder_bound` | `\|‚ąę aipwMomentFunctional ő∑ z őł‚āÄ dP_Z\| ‚Č§ aipw_rem_const őĶ ¬∑ ő£_a ‚ÄĖőĒőľ_a‚ÄĖ‚āā ¬∑ ‚ÄĖőĒe‚ÄĖ‚āā` for `ő∑ ‚ąą H_őĶ őĶ` | Algebraic expansion of `aipw_remainder_identity` + weight bound + Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz. **Proved.** (`RemainderBound.lean`) |
| `aipw_remainder_op` | `(ő∑Őā(n) ‚ąą H_őĶ őĶ)` ‚ąß `‚ÄĖőĒőľ_a‚ÄĖ‚āā ¬∑ ‚ÄĖőĒe‚ÄĖ‚āā = o_p(n^{-1/2})` ‚áí `‚ąę aipwMomentFunctional ő∑Őā z őł‚āÄ dP_Z = o_p(n^{-1/2})` | Stochastic-order corollary consumed at `DML.lean:152` (the `R‚āĀ` cross-term). **Proved.** (`RemainderBound.lean`) |

## 13plr. `Estimation/PLR/{Nuisance,Moment,Setup,MeanZero,RemainderBound,DML,ScoreL2,Feasible,JacobianConsistency}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Partially Linear DML

Instantiates the abstract DML `GeneralMoment` framework (¬ßOrthogonalMoments) with
the Robinson partialling-out score for the partially linear model, built on the PO
causal layer ¬ß9g'''. Yields the doubly-robust moment whose product-rate remainder
drives ‚ąön-asymptotic normality of the DML estimator of the structural slope `őł`.
The score is a *linear score* with `m_a = ‚ąí(D‚ąím(X))¬≤`, so the Jacobian
`J‚āÄ = ‚ąíE[(D‚ąím_val(X))¬≤]` is DGP-dependent (unlike AIPW's `J‚āÄ = ‚ąí1`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PLRNuisance ő≥` | structure | Bundled nuisance `(‚Ąď, m)` of two measurable covariate functions, with `AddCommGroup`/`Module ‚ĄĚ` instances ‚ÄĒ the DML nuisance space `H`. |
| `plrResidual ő∑ z` | `‚ĄĚ` | The treatment residual `d ‚ąí m(x)` of observation `z=(x,d,y)`. |
| `plrMomentFunctional ő∑ z őł` | `‚ĄĚ` | The Robinson partialling-out score `(y‚ąí‚Ąď(x)‚ąíőł(d‚ąím(x)))¬∑(d‚ąím(x))`. |
| `plrMoment_decomp` | `Ōą = m_a¬∑őł + m_b` | Linear-in-parameter decomposition, `m_a = ‚ąí(d‚ąím)¬≤`, `m_b = (y‚ąí‚Ąď)(d‚ąím)`. |
| `measurable_plrMomentFunctional` | measurability | The score is measurable in the data (the nuisance carries its own measurability). |
| `PLRSystem` | structure (extends model) | Value-space estimation system: a `POPartialLinearModel` plus regression representatives `‚Ąď_val, m_val`, their compatibility with `E[Y‚ą£ŌÉX]`/`E[D‚ą£ŌÉX]`, and nondegeneracy `E[(D‚ąím_val(X))¬≤]‚Č†0`. |
| `PLRSystem.P_Z` / `P_X` | `Measure ‚Ä¶` | Joint observed-data law `(X,D,Y)` and covariate marginal. |
| `PLRSystem.integral_P_Z` | `‚ąę f dP_Z = ‚ąę f‚ąė(X,D,Y) dőľ` | Change-of-variables bridge between `P_Z`-level and `őľ`-level statements. |
| `PLRSystem.plrGeneralMoment` | `GeneralMoment ‚Ä¶` | **The DML moment instance**: score, truth `ő∑‚āÄ=(‚Ąď_val,m_val)`, target `őł‚āÄ=őł`, L¬≤(P_X) bilinear seminorms, Jacobian `J‚āÄ=‚ąíE[(D‚ąím_val(X))¬≤]`. |
| `PLRSystem.plr_meanZero` | `MeanZero plrGeneralMoment` | The score has zero population mean at the truth (reduces, via CoV + compat, to `integral_U_resid`). |
| `PLRSystem.plr_remainder_bound` | `\|E[Ōą(ő∑,¬∑,őł‚āÄ)]\| ‚Č§ (1+\|őł‚āÄ\|)¬∑ŌĀ‚āĀ¬∑ŌĀ‚āā` | **Doubly-robust bilinear remainder**: the population bias at an estimated nuisance is the *product* of the two L¬≤(P_X) nuisance errors; the framework's `o_p(n^{-1/2})` product-rate is exactly the textbook "both nuisances at `o_p(n^{-1/4})`." |
| `PLRSystem.plr_finite_var` | `Integrable Ōą(ő∑‚āÄ,¬∑,őł‚āÄ)¬≤` | Finite variance of the influence function, `E[U¬≤¬∑(D‚ąím‚āÄ(X))¬≤]<‚ąě`. |
| `PLRSystem.plr_dml_isAsymLinear` | `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator ‚Ä¶) őł‚āÄ Ōą ‚Ä¶` | **Asymptotic linearity** of the one-step partially linear DML estimator: builds mean-zero/finite-var/doubly-robust-remainder from the PLR facts and invokes the proven `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear` engine (measurability + nuisance-rate conditions taken as hypotheses, as in the AIPW headline). |
| `PLRSystem.plr_dml_tendstoNormal` | `‚ąö\|B(n)\|¬∑(őłŐā‚ĀŅ‚ąíőł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ‚ąęŌą¬≤ dP_Z)` | **‚ąön-asymptotic normality** of the one-step DML estimator of the structural slope, via the generic `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB` CLT bridge; asymptotic variance `E[U¬≤(D‚ąím‚āÄ)¬≤]/E[(D‚ąím‚āÄ)¬≤]¬≤`. |
| `PLRSystem.plr_score_diff_isLittleOp_one` | `‚ÄĖŌą(ő∑Őā,¬∑,őł‚āÄ)‚ąíŌą(ő∑‚āÄ,¬∑,őł‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤(P_Z)} = o_p(1)` | Score-difference L¬≤-consistency (`ScoreL2.lean`): from bounded truth residuals + a uniform sup-bound on the treatment-regression error + the two L¬≤(P_X) nuisance rates. Discharges the `h_score_diff_rate` hypothesis of `plr_dml_isAsymLinear` (so it need not be assumed). Proof: the purely algebraic 5-term doubly-robust expansion + eLpNorm triangle/L‚ąě¬∑L¬≤ bounds. |
| `PLRSystem.plrFeasibleEstimator` | `P‚āô[(Y‚ąí‚ĄďŐā)(D‚ąímŐā)] / P‚āô[(D‚ąímŐā)¬≤]` | The **feasible solved** Robinson DML estimator (`Feasible.lean`) ‚ÄĒ the ratio actually computed in practice, obtained by solving the empirical orthogonal moment for `őł`. |
| `PLRSystem.plr_dml_feasible_tendstoNormal` | `‚ąö\|B(n)\|¬∑(őłŐā_feas‚ąíőł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, ‚ąęŌą¬≤ dP_Z)` | **‚ąön-asymptotic normality of the feasible solved estimator**, with the same limiting variance as the one-step estimator. Reduces to `plr_dml_tendstoNormal` via the asymptotic equivalence `‚ąö\|B\|(őłŐā_feas‚ąíőłŐā_os) = (J‚āÄ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ‚ąí(P‚āôm‚āź)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)¬∑(‚ąö\|B\|¬∑P‚āôŌą) = o_p(1)¬∑O_p(1)`, taking the fold-B Jacobian consistency `P‚āôm‚āź(ő∑Őā) ‚Üí‚āö J‚āÄ` as a hypothesis. |
| `PLRSystem.integral_plrMomentA_ő∑‚āÄ_eq_J‚āÄ` | `‚ąę m‚āź(ő∑‚āÄ,¬∑) dP_Z = J‚āÄ` | The population integral of the partialling-out moment at the truth equals the Jacobian `J‚āÄ=‚ąíE[(D‚ąím_val(X))¬≤]` (`JacobianConsistency.lean`). Change of variables + the `m‚āź=‚ąí(residual)¬≤`/`residSecondMoment` defs. |
| `PLRSystem.integral_plrMomentA_diff_eq` | `‚ąę (m‚āź(ő∑Őā)‚ąím‚āź(ő∑‚āÄ)) dP_Z = ‚ąí‚ÄĖőĒm‚ÄĖ¬≤‚āā` | **Bias of the empirical-Jacobian increment**: its population integral is minus the squared L¬≤(P_X) treatment-regression error; the cross term vanishes by orthogonality of the residual (`integral_condExpZero_mul_comp_factualX` + `condExp_resid_sigmaX`). |
| `PLRSystem.plr_jacobian_consistency` | `P‚āôm‚āź(ő∑Őā) ‚Üí‚āö J‚āÄ` | **Fold-B Jacobian consistency** (`JacobianConsistency.lean`): the empirical partialling-out Jacobian at the estimated nuisance, averaged over fold B, converges in probability to its population value `J‚āÄ`. This is exactly the `hJ_consist` hypothesis of `plr_dml_feasible_tendstoNormal`, so feasible-estimator normality no longer needs it assumed. Proof: fold-B WLLN on `m‚āź(ő∑‚āÄ,¬∑)` for the truth average; the bias of the increment is `‚ąí‚ÄĖőĒm‚ÄĖ¬≤‚āā = o_p(1)` by the L¬≤(P_X) rate; the centered fluctuation is `o_p(1)` from the fold-B centered empirical-process bound renormalized by `(‚ąö\|B\|)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ‚Üí0`. |

## 13'. `Estimation/ATT/` ‚ÄĒ Back-door ATT estimation and inference (parallel to ¬ß13)

Specific-system layer instantiating `POBackdoorSystem` for the **Average Treatment effect on the Treated** (ATT). Mirrors the ATE pipeline of ¬ß13 but uses the Hahn (1998) ATT AIPW form: nuisance is `(őľ‚āÄ, e)` (no `őľ‚āĀ`); one-sided overlap (`e ‚Č§ 1 ‚ąí őĶ`); rescaled moment `m_AIPW^ATT(ő∑, z, őł) = A¬∑(Y ‚ąí őľ‚āÄ(X)) ‚ąí (1‚ąíA)¬∑(e/(1‚ąíe))¬∑(Y ‚ąí őľ‚āÄ(X)) ‚ąí A¬∑őł`; Jacobian `J‚āÄ = ‚ąíŌÄ_T` (not `‚ąí1`); influence function `Ōą_ATT(z) = (1/ŌÄ_T) ¬∑ m_AIPW^ATT(ő∑‚āÄ, z, őł_ATT)`.

Imports the Phase-1 identification result `PO/ID/Exact/ATT.lean` (¬ß9g').

### 13'a. `Estimation/ATT/InfluenceFunction.lean` (umbrella)

Single-import aggregator that re-exports Setup, AIPWMoment, ScorePullout, MeanZero, FiniteVar.

Documented in a `--` comment. |

### 13'e. `Estimation/ATT/DML.lean` ‚ÄĒ user-facing wrapper

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlEstimator_ATT` | `def := (1/ŌÄ_val) ¬∑ (1/\|B(n)\|) ő£_{i ‚ąą B(n)} aipwMomentATT(Z_i, őľŐā‚āÄ(n,ŌČ), √™(n,ŌČ), 0)` | Empirical Hahn-form ATT estimator. |
| `Ōą_ATT_integral_zero` | `‚ąę Ōą_ATT dP_Z = 0` under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, `0 < ŌÄ_T`, `Y¬≤, Y(0)¬≤ ‚ąą L¬Ļ`, truth IPW integrability | Mean-zero of the centered population-`ŌÄ_T` IF. Derived from `aipw_mean_zero_ATT` + `‚ąę A dP_Z = ŌÄ_T` and `m(ő∑‚āÄ,z,0) = m(ő∑‚āÄ,z,őł‚āÄ) + A¬∑őł‚āÄ`. **Proved.** |
| `Ōą_ATT_finite_var` | `Integrable (Ōą_ATT)¬≤ P_Z` under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, `Y¬≤, Y(0)¬≤ ‚ąą L¬Ļ` | Finite variance of the centered IF. Derived from `aipw_finite_var_ATT` (moment-at-`őł‚āÄ` in `L¬≤`) + boundedness of `A`, via `Ōą_ATT = (1/ŌÄ_T)¬∑(m(ő∑‚āÄ,¬∑,őł‚āÄ) + őł‚āÄ¬∑A) ‚ąí őł‚āÄ ‚ąą L¬≤(P_Z)`. **Proved.** |
| `dml_ATT_isAsymLinear` | Asymptotic linearity at `őł‚āÄ` with IF `Ōą_ATT`, indexed over `split.foldB`, under `OneSidedOverlap`, `Assumptions`, true/estimated propensity bounds, `0 < ŌÄ_T`, `Y¬≤, Y(0)¬≤ ‚ąą L¬Ļ`, joint/fold-A measurability, per-ŌČ L¬≤ nuisance hypotheses, score integrability, and individual + product L¬≤ rates | Wrapper over `att_dml_isAsymLinear`. Builds `ő∑_hat` from `(őľŐā‚āÄ, √™)`, forwards the abstract score regularity bundle, and transports the Chernozhukov remainder to the population-`ŌÄ_T` estimator with the centered `Ōą_ATT`. The `IsAsymLinear` mean-zero / finite-variance fields are **derived** (via `Ōą_ATT_integral_zero` / `Ōą_ATT_finite_var`), and the truth / per-learner IPW integrability gates are **derived** (via `ipw_truth_integrable` / `ipw_estimated_integrable`) ‚ÄĒ none taken as hypotheses. **Proved in `DML.lean`.** |

### Phase-2 status summary

Twelve files written; ten with full proofs, two with one documented sorry each. Two sorries remain in the production chain (RemainderIdentity and AIPWScoreL2 isLittleOp), both stemming from the genuinely simpler statement requirements of the ATT setting that don't directly mirror ATE. Headline mathematical content (mean-zero, finite variance, Lipschitz bound, OR/AIPW remainder identity at the population level, abstract DML asymptotic linearity composition, DML headline transport) is proved except for the documented upstream gaps.

---

## 13''. `Estimation/DTR/` ‚ÄĒ Sequential AIPW (DTR, n = 2) DML estimation and inference

Dynamic-treatment-regime counterpart of the ATE DML pipeline.  The target is the fixed-regime mean `őł‚āÄ = E[Y(dŐĄ)]` for `dŐĄ : Fin 2 ‚Üí őī`; the score is the explicit two-stage sequential AIPW moment with stagewise IPW weights, centered by `őł‚āÄ`.  The population remainder is a cross-product identity over the stage-history marginals `P_H‚āÄ` and `P_H‚āĀ`, with `J‚āÄ = -1` in the abstract `GeneralMoment` instance.

Phase A scope: this module is intentionally specialized to horizon `n = 2`; Phase B generalizes to arbitrary finite horizons.  The treatment space `őī` is discrete enough for equality indicators, via `[MeasurableSingletonClass őī]`.

### 13''a.1 `Estimation/DTR/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|

### Phase A status summary

Ten files implement the `n = 2` DTR sequential-AIPW DML pipeline.  Three documented sorries remain: `seqDR_remainder_identity`, `seqDR_score_diff_isLittleOp_one`, and `dml_DTR_isAsymLinear`.  All three are large algebraic / infrastructure proofs that mirror the corresponding ATE files at larger stagewise scale; downstream typechecking succeeds because the sorries are inside lemma bodies, not statements.  Phase B (general horizon `n`) is deferred.

---

## 13'''''. `Estimation/NPIV/` ‚ÄĒ TRAE / NPIV doubly-robust estimation and inference track

Formalizes the non-iterated Tikhonov regularized adversarial estimator (TRAE) of
Bennett et al. (2023) for linear inverse-problem functionals ‚ÄĒ the canonical
NPIV setup.  Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/trae_inverse_problems.tex`.
**The entire primal one-step Tikhonov track is fully proved (zero sorries).**
Phase A (mixed-bias identity), Phase B (TRAE-DR asymptotic linearity +
asymptotic normality), Phase C-lite + Phase D (operator layer `T` + primal rate
theorem), and Phase E (adjoint `T‚Ä†`, real-CFC spectral calculus, Tikhonov bias
discharge, and the localized empirical-process event) are all complete.  The
dual TRAE (`def:est-trae-dual`) and its symmetric rate theorem are *not*
formalized ‚ÄĒ only the `DualSolution` structure the DR score needs is.  See the
per-phase Status section at the end.

### Phase A files

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `InverseProblemSystem ő© őľ` | structure | Linear inverse-problem functional system (`def:est-trae-system`).  Carries observation type `ūĚí≤` with projections `xOf : ūĚí≤ ‚Üí ūĚí≥`, `zOf : ūĚí≤ ‚Üí ūĚíĶ`; random observation `W : ő© ‚Üí ūĚí≤`; candidate sets `Hbar ‚äÜ (ūĚí≥ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)`, `Qbar ‚äÜ (ūĚíĶ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` (closure under subtraction; no Hilbert structure in Phase A); observation-level moment maps `m, m_e` with linearity-over-subtraction; per-class measurability + integrability witnesses; primal nuisance `h‚āÄ ‚ąą Hbar`; primal moment identity `E[m(W; q)] = E[h‚āÄ(X) q(Z)]` for `q ‚ąą Qbar`. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.X`, `.Z` | `def`s | Derived random variables `X := xOf ‚ąė W`, `Z := zOf ‚ąė W`. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.őł‚āÄ` | `def` | Scalar target `őł‚āÄ := E[m_e(W; h‚āÄ)]`. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.phiVal h q w` | `def` | Pointwise pseudo-outcome `m_e(w; h) + m(w; q) ‚ąí q(zOf w) h(xOf w)`. Reusable for empirical fold averages. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.phi h q ŌČ` | `def` | `phiVal h q (W ŌČ)` ‚ÄĒ the ŌČ-indexed score. |
| `InverseProblemSystem.őė h q` | `def` | `őė(h, q) := E[ŌÜ_{h, q}(W)]` (`def:est-trae-dr-functional`). |
| `InverseProblemSystem.DualSolution S q‚āÄ` | structure | `q‚āÄ ‚ąą Qbar` plus the dual moment identity `E[m_e(W; h)] = E[q‚āÄ(Z) h(X)]` for `h ‚ąą Hbar` (`def:est-trae-dual-solution`). |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/MixedBias.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mixed_bias_identity` | `őė(h, q) ‚ąí őł‚āÄ = E[(q‚āÄ(Z) ‚ąí q(Z))(h(X) ‚ąí h‚āÄ(X))]` for `h ‚ąą Hbar, q ‚ąą Qbar` and a dual solution `q‚āÄ` | The **mixed-bias / DR identity** (`prop:est-trae-mixed-bias`).  First NPIV milestone.  Pure integral-linearity proof using `m_e_sub`, the dual identity on direction `h ‚ąí h‚āÄ ‚ąą Hbar`, and the primal moment on `q ‚ąą Qbar`. |
| `őė_h‚āÄ_eq_őł‚āÄ` | `őė(h‚āÄ, q) = őł‚āÄ` | DR cancellation at the truth on the primal side. Specialization of `mixed_bias_identity` at `h = h‚āÄ`. |
| `őė_q‚āÄ_eq_őł‚āÄ` | `őė(h, q‚āÄ) = őł‚āÄ` | DR cancellation at the truth on the dual side. Specialization at `q = q‚āÄ`. |

| `mean_zero_of_DualSolution_mu`, `mean_zero_of_DualSolution` | `‚ąę ŌĀ‚āÄ = 0` | Derive oracle-score centering from the primal/dual moment identities. The `P_W` version transports the structural-law identity along the observation-law bridge `őľ.map S.W = P_W` and consumes oracle-score measurability. |
| `TRAEDRRemainderHyps S hq‚āÄ sample split h_hat q_hat` | structure | Bundles (i)‚Äď(iv) of `thm:est-trae-dr-al-criterion`: (i) `range` (h_hat / q_hat live in Hbar / Qbar); (ii) `mixed_bias` (`‚ąö|B(n)|` √ó mixed-bias integral is `o_p(1)`); (iii) `ep_remainder` (centered fold-B sum of `ŌÜŐā_n ‚ąí ŌÜ_0` is `o_p(1)` after `‚ąö|B(n)|` scaling); (iv) `finite_var` (`E[ŌĀ‚āÄ¬≤] < ‚ąě` under `P_W`). Mean-zero is derived by `mean_zero_of_DualSolution`, not bundled as a hypothesis. |

The signature includes the standard observation-law bridge `őľ.map S.W = P_W` and oracle-score measurability so the IF centering is derived rather than assumed. |

### Phase C-lite + Phase D files (rate theorem track)

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator.lean`

Realises the projected conditional-expectation operator `T = ő†_{QŐĄ} ‚ąė E[¬∑ | ŌÉ(Z)]` concretely on the ambient `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` (no pushforward boilerplate).  The single population identity `T_inner_eq_integral` is what the rate theorem consumes; `T_add` / `T_sub` are linearity helpers used by the weak-rate triangle step.  `T*` (adjoint) is **not** scaffolded ‚ÄĒ the simplified rate proof (note lines 250‚Äď384) does not need it.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OperatorSystem ő© őľ` | `structure ... extends InverseProblemSystem ő© őľ` | `InverseProblemSystem` plus the L¬≤ realisation: `m_Z` (ŌÉ(Z) ŌÉ-algebra coarser than `m‚āÄ`), `IsFiniteMeasure őľ`, closed submodules `Hbar_L2, Qbar_L2 : Submodule ‚ĄĚ (Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ)`, an `HasOrthogonalProjection` instance for `Qbar_L2`, `MemLp 2`-witnesses for `h ‚ąė X` and `q ‚ąė Z`, and coherence (the `Lp` class lies in the closed subspace). |
| `OperatorSystem.hL2 hh`, `.qL2 hq` | `def`s | Lift `h ‚ąą Hbar` (resp. `q ‚ąą Qbar`) to its `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` class using the `MemLp` witness. |
| `OperatorSystem.condExpStep f` | `def` | Conditional-expectation step `E[¬∑ | ŌÉ(Z)]` as a self-map of `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ`. |
| `OperatorSystem.T f` | `def` | The concrete operator `ő†_{QŐĄ} ‚ąė condExpStep f` (`def:est-trae-system` step iii of the operator triple). |
| `OperatorSystem.condExpStep_sub`, `T_add`, `T_sub` | `lemma`s | ‚ĄĚ-linearity helpers; `T_sub` is the workhorse for the weak-rate triangle inequality. |
| `OperatorSystem.QbarAEMeasurableMZ` | `def` (predicate) | `q ‚ąė Z` is `m_Z`-AEStronglyMeasurable for every `q ‚ąą Qbar`.  Kept as a `def` for backwards compatibility with downstream consumers; **derived** by `qbar_aestrongly_measurable_mZ` from `m_Z_eq + meas_of_Qbar`, so it is not a separate hypothesis on the structure. |
| `OperatorSystem.qbar_aestrongly_measurable_mZ` | `(S : OperatorSystem ő© őľ) : S.QbarAEMeasurableMZ` | **Proved** (no extra hypothesis): combines `S.m_Z_eq : m_Z = comap (zOf ‚ąė W) instūĚíĶ` with `comap_measurable` and `meas_of_Qbar` to get `m_Z`-strong measurability of `q ‚ąė zOf ‚ąė W` for every `q ‚ąą Qbar`. |
| `OperatorSystem.T_inner_eq_integral` | `‚ü™T (hL2 hh ‚ąí hL2 hh'), qL2 hf‚üę_{L¬≤(őľ)} = ‚ąę (h(X) ‚ąí h'(X)) ¬∑ f(Z) dőľ` | **Population identity** ‚ÄĒ the only fact about `T` the rate proof uses.  Proved via projection invisibility against `QŐĄ`-elements, `condExpL2` self-adjointness (consuming `qbar_aestrongly_measurable_mZ` internally ‚ÄĒ no longer takes the measurability hypothesis as an explicit argument), and `Lp` inner-product unfolding. |
| `OperatorSystem.strongNorm`, `.weakNorm` | `def`s | `strongNorm f := ‚ÄĖf‚ÄĖ`, `weakNorm f := ‚ÄĖS.T f‚ÄĖ`.  Sugar around the standard `Lp` norm. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/SourceCondition.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SourceCondition S ő≤` | structure | ő≤-source condition at `h‚āÄ` (`def:est-trae-source-condition`).  Carries witness `w‚āÄ ‚ąą Hbar` and the actual spectral identity `S.hL2 S.h‚āÄ_mem = Complexification.realCFC S.Tstar_T (fun x => Real.rpow (max x 0) (ő≤/2)) (S.hL2 w‚āÄ_mem)` (uses the real-CFC built in `Operator/Complexification.lean`). |
| `TikhonovBiasBound S ő≤ őĽ sc` | structure | Tikhonov bias hypothesis at level `őĽ` (proof sketch lines 276‚Äď303).  Bundles a Tikhonov solution `h*_őĽ ‚ąą Hbar` and a constant `C ‚Č• 0` with three inequalities: `strong_bias` (`‚ÄĖh*_őĽ ‚ąí h‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ C¬∑‚ÄĖw‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬∑őĽ^{min ő≤ 2}`), `weak_bias` (`‚ÄĖT(h*_őĽ ‚ąí h‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ C¬∑‚ÄĖw‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬∑őĽ^{min(ő≤+1) 2}`), and `strong_convexity` (the population-level inequality `őĽ‚ÄĖń• ‚ąí h*_őĽ‚ÄĖ¬≤ + ‚ÄĖT(ń• ‚ąí h*_őĽ)‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖT(ń• ‚ąí h‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚ąí ‚ÄĖT(h*_őĽ ‚ąí h‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ¬≤ + őĽ(‚ÄĖń•‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚ąí ‚ÄĖh*_őĽ‚ÄĖ¬≤)` for every `ń• ‚ąą Hbar`).  Phase E discharges all three from `SourceCondition` via spectral calculus. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Primal/Estimator.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `TRAEClasses S` | structure | Candidate class `H ‚äÜ Hbar` and critic class `F ‚äÜ Qbar` for the primal TRAE optimisation (`def:est-trae-population-criterion`). Closure / non-emptiness fields included. |
| `innerIntegrand`, `innerObjective`, `supObjective` | `noncomputable def`s | Pointwise integrand `2{m(W;f) ‚ąí h(X)f(Z)} ‚ąí f(Z)¬≤ + őĽ h(X)¬≤`, its fold-A empirical average, and the `iSup` over `f ‚ąą F` (`def:est-trae-primal`). |
| `IsTRAEPrimalEstimator S TC sample split lambda h_hat` | structure | Post-hoc property characterising the primal TRAE: `mem_H : h_hat n ŌČ ‚ąą TC.H`, an optimality-style inequality `opt`, and a measurability witness.  No `argmin-sup` formalism ‚ÄĒ the rate theorem only needs the inequality form. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Primal/Rate.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `TRAERatePrimalHyps S TC sample split lambda ő≤ ő∂ delta h_hat is_estimator` | structure | Deterministic, paper-facing hypotheses for `thm:est-trae-rate-theorem`: source condition, Tikhonov bias bound, realisability `h*_őĽ ‚ąą H`, closedness of the critic class for residuals, mean-square continuity of `m`, almost-sure boundedness, and the paper-scale őī-floor for the fixed confidence level `ő∂`, with positive constant `c > 0`: `őī_n ‚Č• c¬∑‚ąö((log log(n+1) + log(1/ő∂)) / n)`.  Making `ő∂` a parameter avoids forcing one fixed `delta` sequence to satisfy every confidence level at once. |
| `TRAERatePrimalAbstractHyps S TC sample split lambda ő≤ ő∂ delta h_hat is_estimator` | structure | Extends `TRAERatePrimalHyps` with the packaged **`empirical_process_event`** field ‚ÄĒ the post-substitution-and-CR form of the localized empirical-process inequality (`SC_RHS ‚Č§ K_ep ¬∑ (R_b¬≤ + őī‚ÄĖTőĒ‚ÄĖ + őī¬≤ + őĽőī‚ÄĖőĒ‚ÄĖ + őĽőī¬≤)`) on a high-probability event of mass `‚Č• 1 ‚ąí ő∂`.  This is the modular deterministic theorem input, not the paper-facing rate assumption bundle. |
| `combined_metric_algebra` | private real-arithmetic helper | AM-GM absorption: `lambda¬∑x¬≤ + y¬≤ ‚Č§ K_ep¬∑(R¬≤ + őīy + őī¬≤ + őĽőīx + őĽőī¬≤) ‚Üí lambda¬∑x¬≤ + y¬≤ ‚Č§ 4(K_ep¬≤ + K_ep + 1)¬∑(R¬≤ + őī¬≤)`. |
| `combined_metric_rate` | `őĽ¬∑őĒ_strong¬≤ + őĒ_weak¬≤ ‚Č§ K ¬∑ (R_b¬≤ + őī_n¬≤)` on `Aő∂` | **Combined-metric rate** (helper, deterministic).  Chains `tikhonov_bias.strong_convexity` with `empirical_process_event` and applies `combined_metric_algebra` for AM-GM absorption. |
| `strong_rate_from_combined` | `‚ÄĖń• ‚ąí h‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ C_strong ¬∑ (őī¬≤/őĽ + ‚ÄĖw‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬∑őĽ^{min ő≤ 1})` | **Strong-metric rate** (helper, deterministic).  Triangle inequality `‚ÄĖa ‚ąí c‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖa ‚ąí b‚ÄĖ + ‚ÄĖb ‚ąí c‚ÄĖ` plus drop of `őĒ_weak¬≤` from the combined inequality, plus `weak_bias` (gives `R_b¬≤/őĽ ‚Č§ C¬∑‚ÄĖw‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬∑őĽ^{min ő≤ 1}`) and `strong_bias` (absorbed using `őĽ^{min ő≤ 2} ‚Č§ 2 ¬∑ őĽ^{min ő≤ 1}` for `őĽ < 2`). |
| `weak_rate_from_combined` | `‚ÄĖT(ń• ‚ąí h‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ C_weak ¬∑ (őī¬≤ + ‚ÄĖw‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬∑őĽ^{min(ő≤+1) 2})` | **Weak-metric rate** (helper, deterministic).  Triangle inequality on the weak metric using `T_add` / `norm_add_le`, plus drop of `őĽ¬∑őĒ_strong¬≤` from the combined inequality, plus `weak_bias`.  `C_weak := 2K + (2K + 2)¬∑C`. |
| `trae_primal_rate_from_empirical_process` | both rates simultaneously on a single `Aő∂` of mass `‚Č• 1 ‚ąí ő∂` | Abstract TRAE primal rate theorem.  Consumes `TRAERatePrimalAbstractHyps`, so the empirical-process event is already packaged.  Stitches `combined_metric_rate` ‚Üí `strong_rate_from_combined` ‚Üí `weak_rate_from_combined` on a common high-probability event. |
| `trae_primal_rate` | both rates simultaneously on a single `Aő∂` of mass `‚Č• 1 ‚ąí ő∂` | **Paper-facing TRAE primal rate theorem.**  Lives in `Rate.lean` for discoverability.  Consumes deterministic `TRAERatePrimalHyps`, localized regimes, and explicit absorption/small-`n` obligations; internally calls `empirical_process_event_of_absorption` to build `TRAERatePrimalAbstractHyps`, then invokes `trae_primal_rate_from_empirical_process`. |

### Status

- Phase A: **fully proved** (3/3). `mixed_bias_identity`, `őė_h‚āÄ_eq_őł‚āÄ`, `őė_q‚āÄ_eq_őł‚āÄ`.
- Phase B: **fully proved** (8/8). `trae_dr_isAsymLinear`, `mixed_bias_sufficient`, `trae_dr_asymp_normal`, `trae_dr_studentized`, `trae_dr_wald_coverage`.
- **Phase C-lite + Phase D**: **fully proved** (5/5).
  - `T_inner_eq_integral` (population identity for `T`): **proved** (Codex via `condExpL2` self-adjointness + projection invisibility + `Lp` inner-product unfolding).
  - `combined_metric_rate`: **proved** (Codex with hand-decomposed AM-GM helper `combined_metric_algebra`).
  - `strong_rate_from_combined`: **proved** (Codex; uses triangle in `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` plus `őĽ^{min ő≤ 2} ‚Č§ 2¬∑őĽ^{min ő≤ 1}` case-split).
  - `weak_rate_from_combined`: **proved** (Codex; uses pre-emptively scaffolded `T_sub` / `T_add` helpers in `Operator.lean` for the weak-metric triangle inequality).
  - `trae_primal_rate` (headline): **proved** (Codex stitch).
- **Phase E** ‚ÄĒ **spectral discharge fully closed**.  Adjoint layer
  fully proved.  The pure-Hilbert-space Tikhonov layer
  (`Operator/Tikhonov.lean`) discharges the existence of the population
  minimiser and population strong convexity via Lax‚ÄďMilgram on the
  closed subspace `Hbar_L2`.  The real-CFC layer
  (`Operator/Complexification.lean`) is fully closed; it provides the
  spectral API the Tikhonov bias discharge consumes.  `SourceCondition`
  now carries the *actual* real-CFC spectral identity, and
  `SpectralSourceCondition` reduces to two structural fields
  (`beta_nonneg`, `Hbar_L2_eq_top`); the closedness witness and
  nonnegative spectrum facts are derived.  The resolvent identification
  of the Lax‚ÄďMilgram minimiser (`tikhonovMinimiserL2_eq_resolvent`) and
  the two Tikhonov bias bounds (`strong_bias`, `weak_bias`) are
  **proved** theorems on
  `SpectralSourceCondition` (Codex Phase E2 fill, using
  `Complexification.realCFC_*`).  `tikhonov_bias_from_spectral`
  packages these plus `tikhonovMinimiserL2_strong_convexity` into a
  fully-derived `TikhonovBiasBound`.  Discharge of
  `empirical_process_event` is **fully closed** (no sorries): the
  localized-empirical-process bookkeeping is supplied by the
  `LocalizedRegimeBundle` hypotheses (law / realizability / boundedness /
  diameter / peeling-slack / critical-radius), exactly as the paper
  assumes `őī_n` upper-bounds the critical radii, and the master
  concentration inequality `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp`
  (`Stat/Concentration/UniformDeviationLocalized.lean`) is itself proved.
  See files below.

### Phase E files

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator/Adjoint.lean` (~110 lines, fully proved)

Bundles the bare-function operator `OperatorSystem.T : Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚Üí Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ`
as a continuous linear map and exposes the adjoint `T‚Ä†` and the self-adjoint
composite `T‚Ä†T`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OperatorSystem.condExpStepL` | `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` | Conditional-expectation step `E[¬∑ \| ŌÉ(Z)]` as a CLM, via `(lpMeas).subtypeL.comp (condExpL2 ‚ĄĚ ‚ĄĚ S.m_Z_le)`. |
| `OperatorSystem.Tlin` | `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` | Bundled CLM version of `S.T`: `Qbar_L2.starProjection.comp condExpStepL`. |
| `OperatorSystem.Tlin_apply` | `S.Tlin f = S.T f` | Coherence: bundled CLM agrees with the bare-function `T`. **Proved (`rfl`).** |
| `OperatorSystem.Tadjoint` | `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` | Adjoint `T‚Ä† := ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint S.Tlin`. |
| `OperatorSystem.Tstar_T` | `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` | Self-adjoint, PSD composite `T‚Ä† T = Tadjoint.comp Tlin`. The operator the ő≤-source condition refers to. |
| `OperatorSystem.Tstar_T_isSelfAdjoint` | `IsSelfAdjoint S.Tstar_T` | Self-adjointness of `T‚Ä† T` via `IsSelfAdjoint.star_mul_self` after identifying adjoint with `star`. **Proved.** |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator/Tikhonov.lean` (~200 lines, sorry-only scaffold)

Pure-real-Hilbert-space layer for the Tikhonov minimisation problem on
the closed subspace `Hbar_L2`.  Discharges the existence of the
population minimiser and the population strong-convexity inequality via
Mathlib's Lax‚ÄďMilgram theorem (`IsCoercive.continuousLinearEquivOfBilin`)
‚ÄĒ **no continuous functional calculus needed**.  These were previously
baked fields of `SpectralSourceCondition`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OperatorSystem.tikhonovBilin S őĽ` | `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] ‚ĄĚ` | Bilinear form `(u, v) ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®T u, T v‚ü© + őĽ ‚ü®u, v‚ü©` on the ambient `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ`. |
| `tikhonovBilin_apply`, `tikhonovBilin_symm` | unfold + symmetry | Pointwise formula and symmetry of the bilinear form. |
| `OperatorSystem.tikhonovBilinSub S őĽ` | `S.Hbar_L2 ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] S.Hbar_L2 ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] ‚ĄĚ` | The bilinear form restricted to the closed subspace `Hbar_L2` (via `Submodule.subtypeL` pre/post-composition).  Argument fed into Lax‚ÄďMilgram. |
| `tikhonovBilinSub_apply` | unfold | Pointwise formula linking the restricted form back to `tikhonovBilin`. |
| `tikhonovBilinSub_isCoercive` | `IsCoercive (S.tikhonovBilinSub őĽ)` for `0 < őĽ` | Coercivity with constant `őĽ`, since `bilin u u = ‚ÄĖT u‚ÄĖ¬≤ + őĽ ‚ÄĖu‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č• őĽ ‚ÄĖu‚ÄĖ¬≤`. |
| `OperatorSystem.tikhonovTargetSub S` | `S.Hbar_L2 ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] ‚ĄĚ` | Target functional `v ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®T h‚āÄ, T v‚ü©` on `Hbar_L2`, used in the variational identity. |
| `tikhonovTargetSub_apply` | unfold | Pointwise formula for the target functional. |
| `OperatorSystem.tikhonovMinimiserL2 S őĽ` | `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` | Population Tikhonov minimiser at level `őĽ`, defined for `0 < őĽ` by Lax‚ÄďMilgram applied to `tikhonovBilinSub` and `tikhonovTargetSub`; arbitrary value (`0`) for `őĽ ‚Č§ 0`. |
| `tikhonovMinimiserL2_mem` | `‚ąą S.Hbar_L2` | The minimiser lies in the closed candidate subspace. |
| `tikhonovMinimiserL2_optimality` | `‚ü®T h*, T v‚ü© + őĽ‚ü®h*, v‚ü© = ‚ü®T h‚āÄ, T v‚ü©` for `v ‚ąą Hbar_L2` | Variational identity (Lax‚ÄďMilgram statement, restated using the L¬≤-level inner product). |
| `tikhonovMinimiserL2_strong_convexity` | `őĽ‚ÄĖń•‚ąíh*‚ÄĖ¬≤ + ‚ÄĖT(ń•‚ąíh*)‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖT(ń•‚ąíh‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚ąí ‚ÄĖT(h*‚ąíh‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ¬≤ + őĽ(‚ÄĖń•‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚ąí ‚ÄĖh*‚ÄĖ¬≤)` | **Population strong convexity at the minimiser.**  Pure Hilbert-space Taylor-expansion identity at the minimiser; the first-order term cancels via `tikhonovMinimiserL2_optimality`. |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator/Complexification.lean` (~1090 lines, fully proved)

Real continuous functional calculus on `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` via
complexification.  Embeds the real `L¬≤` isometrically into the complex
`L¬≤` (`őĻLp` / `reLp` / `imLp`), lifts any real CLM `A` to a complex CLM
`complexLift A` whose action on the real subspace recovers `A`, and
defines `realCFC A f := reLp ‚ąė cfc (f ‚ąė Complex.re) (complexLift A) ‚ąė őĻLp`.
Provides the full API the spectral discharge consumes (`realCFC_apply`,
`realCFC_id`, `realCFC_mul`, `realCFC_resolvent_mul_self`,
`realCFC_isSelfAdjoint`, `realCFC_norm_le`).

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Operator/SpectralCalculus.lean` (~1230 lines, fully proved)

Spectral source condition + Tikhonov bias discharge using the real-CFC
API from `Complexification.lean`.  `SourceCondition` (in `../SourceCondition.lean`)
carries the actual spectral identity, so `SpectralSourceCondition`
only adds the structural assumptions the spectral argument needs.
The two bias bounds and the resolvent identification of the Lax‚ÄďMilgram
minimiser are now theorems on `SpectralSourceCondition`, all proved.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SpectralSourceCondition S ő≤` | `structure ... extends SourceCondition S ő≤` | Strengthens `SourceCondition` with: `beta_nonneg : 0 ‚Č§ ő≤` and `Hbar_L2_eq_top : S.Hbar_L2 = ‚ä§` (full-space candidate class).  The closedness witness `Hbar_L2_hasProj` is derived from `Hbar_L2_eq_top`, and `Tstar_T_spectrum_nonneg` is derived from positivity of `T‚Ä†T`. |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.sourceSymbol ő≤` / `continuous_sourceSymbol` | `‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` / `Continuous` | The continuous extension `x ‚Ü¶ Real.rpow (max x 0) (ő≤/2)` of `x^{ő≤/2}` to all of ‚ĄĚ. |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.spectralPower sc` | `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ ‚ÜíL[‚ĄĚ] Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` | Defined as `Complexification.realCFC S.Tstar_T (sourceSymbol ő≤)`; the operator `(T‚Ä†T)^{ő≤/2}` consumed by the spectral identity. |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.biasConst sc` | `‚ĄĚ` | Uniform constant `Real.rpow (max 1 (‚ÄĖS.Tstar_T‚ÄĖ+1)) ő≤` absorbing both the small-ő≤ and large-ő≤ regimes of the sup-on-spectrum analysis. |
| `tikhonovMinimiserL2_eq_resolvent` | `S.tikhonovMinimiserL2 őĽ = realCFC S.Tstar_T (x ‚Ü¶ x/(őĽ+x)) (S.hL2 S.h‚āÄ_mem)` | Identifies the Lax‚ÄďMilgram minimiser with the resolvent expression `(T‚Ä†T+őĽI)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ T‚Ä†T h‚āÄ`.  Uses `Hbar_L2_eq_top` to lift the variational identity to the full ambient space.  **Proved** (Codex; uses private helpers `realCFC_const_add` and `resolvent_left_inverse` built on `realCFC_resolvent_mul_self`). |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.strong_bias` | `‚ÄĖh*_őĽ ‚ąí h‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ biasConst ¬∑ ‚ÄĖw‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤ ¬∑ őĽ^{min ő≤ 2}` | Strong-metric Tikhonov bias, derived spectrally from the resolvent identity + `realCFC_norm_le` + sup-on-spectrum lemmas (`residual_symbol_bound_small/_large` private helpers handle ő≤‚Č§2 / ő≤>2 cases).  **Proved** (Codex). |
| `SpectralSourceCondition.weak_bias` | `‚ÄĖT(h*_őĽ ‚ąí h‚āÄ)‚ÄĖ¬≤ ‚Č§ biasConst ¬∑ ‚ÄĖw‚āÄ‚ÄĖ¬≤ ¬∑ őĽ^{min(ő≤+1) 2}` | Weak-metric Tikhonov bias.  Reduces `‚ÄĖT u‚ÄĖ¬≤ = ‚ü®T‚Ä†T u, u‚ü©` (via `Tstar_T`, `adjoint_inner_left`), expresses it as `‚ü®realCFC A (x ¬∑ f¬≤) w, w‚ü©` via `realCFC_mul` + self-adjointness, then bounds via Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz + `realCFC_norm_le` with the tight sup `weak_residual_bound` (ő≤‚Č§1 / ő≤>1 case split).  **Proved** (Codex). |
| `TikhonovPullback S ő≤ őĽ sc` | `structure` | Function-level pullback (unchanged):  L¬≤ class of the function-level minimiser equals `tikhonovMinimiserL2 S őĽ`. |
| `tikhonov_bias_from_spectral S ő≤ őĽ őĽ_pos sc pb` | `TikhonovBiasBound S ő≤ őĽ sc.toSourceCondition` | Packages the spectral `strong_bias` / `weak_bias` theorems plus `tikhonovMinimiserL2_strong_convexity` into a `TikhonovBiasBound` with `C := sc.biasConst ¬∑ ‚ÄĖw‚āÄ‚ÄĖ`.  **Fully proved** (no sorries). |

#### `Estimation/NPIV/Primal/EmpiricalProcessEvent/` ‚ÄĒ localized EP discharge

Split implementation of the discharge of
`TRAERatePrimalAbstractHyps.empirical_process_event` from four localized-regime
instances on `star(H ¬∑ F), star(m ‚ąė F), star(F), star(H)`.  The umbrella file
`Primal/EmpiricalProcessEvent.lean` re-exports the final discharge module.

| File | Contents | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Algebra.lean` | `young_cross_envelope` | Deterministic Young / AM-GM envelope converting the localized additive cross terms into the `R¬≤ + őī¬∑w + őī¬≤ + őļ¬≤` shape used by the EP RHS. |
| `Regime.lean` | `LocalizedRegimeBundle`, `LocalizedRegimes` | Bundles one localized deviation regime per abstract function class, including nonnegativity of the abstract localization norm, plus law, realizability, boundedness, closedness, diameter, peeling slack, and interpretation fields connecting the abstract classes to the concrete NPIV losses. |
| `Core.lean` | imports `Regime` | Small compatibility umbrella for the algebra and regime declarations. |
| `LocalizedEventsBase.lean` | `integral_comp_law_W`, `localized_omega_event_sharp_for_bundle` | Shared law-change helper for rewriting integrals under `P_W` back to integrals over `ő©`, plus the NPIV-facing wrapper around `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp` for a single `LocalizedRegimeBundle`. The wrapper pulls the product-space sharp event back along the IID sample, uses the bundle's localization-norm nonnegativity, keeps the diameter obligation per concrete index, and rewrites the population mean through `regime.law_W`. |
| `LocalizedEventH.lean` | `localized_omega_event_for_H` | Fixed-diameter ő©-side localized deviation event for squared candidate differences, used by the centred regulariser bound. |
| `LocalizedEventHF.lean` | `localized_omega_event_for_HF`, `localized_omega_event_for_HF_pair`, `localized_omega_event_for_HF_pair_peeled` | ő©-side localized deviation events for the product class `H ¬∑ F`, including the peeled pair-gap Foster interpretation.  The peeled helper now delegates the dyadic peeling to `localized_omega_event_sharp_for_bundle`. |
| `LocalizedEventMF.lean` | `localized_omega_event_for_mF`, `localized_omega_event_for_mF_pair_peeled` | ő©-side localized deviation events for the moment class `m ‚ąė F`, including the peeled critic-pair form.  The peeled helper now delegates the dyadic peeling to `localized_omega_event_sharp_for_bundle`. |
| `LocalizedEventF.lean` | `localized_omega_event_for_F`, `localized_omega_event_for_F_pair_peeled` | ő©-side localized deviation events for the squared critic class `F`, including the peeled critic-pair form.  The peeled helper now delegates to `localized_omega_event_sharp_for_bundle` and states the leading term at the public `őī_n` scale. |
| `LocalizedEvents.lean` | imports the localized event files | Small compatibility umbrella for all ő©-side localized deviation events. |
| `EPMasterEvent.lean` | `population_closedness_critic_gap_eq`, `empirical_critic_argmax_localized`, `empirical_critic_argmax_localized_from_components`, `ep_master_event_from_localized` | Objective-level EP master event plus the deterministic Foster argmax bridge. `population_closedness_critic_gap_eq` proves the population quadratic-curvature identity at a closedness witness; `empirical_critic_argmax_localized` turns empirical critic optimality plus a one-sided loss-deviation bound into an L¬≤ localization bound for the empirical sup-attainer; `empirical_critic_argmax_localized_from_components` packages that bridge from the existing single-critic `m‚ąėF`, `H¬∑F`, and `F¬≤` deviations without adding critic linearity assumptions. `ep_master_event_from_localized` exposes the raw objective-level localized EP modulus before final őī-schedule absorption. |
| `EPPerN.lean` | `ep_pop_inner_at_closedness_witness`, `ep_per_n_inequality_from_deviations`, `ep_inequality_from_localized` | Per-horizon deterministic EP bridge and public EP inequality. `ep_pop_inner_at_closedness_witness` is the population-side identity `2¬∑E[m¬∑f] ‚ąí 2¬∑E[h¬∑f] ‚ąí E[f¬≤] = weak(h - h‚āÄ)¬≤` at a closedness witness. `ep_per_n_inequality_from_deviations` consumes the raw objective-level localized modulus and removes the empirical sup-objective excess using estimator optimality. `ep_inequality_from_localized` extracts the witness payload from `ep_master_event_from_localized` and feeds it through `ep_per_n_inequality_from_deviations`; `Discharge.lean` then absorbs the explicit rate into the final TRAE population shape. |
| `EPInequality.lean` | imports `EPPerN` | Small compatibility umbrella for the explicit empirical-process inequality. |
| `Regulariser.lean` | `measure_iInter_nat_ge_one_sub_tsum_of_ge`, `centred_regulariser_bound_from_localized` | All-`n` centred empirical regulariser bound obtained from the H-side localized event. |
| `Discharge.lean` | `empirical_process_event_from_localized`, `explicitRate`, `populationShape`, `empirical_process_event_of_absorption` | Combines the EP inequality and regulariser bound, then exposes the `Rate.lean`-shaped empirical-process event conditional on the explicit-rate absorption hypothesis.  The paper-facing theorem using it is `trae_primal_rate` in `Rate.lean`. |

---

## 13W. `Causalean/Panel/Weighted/` ‚ÄĒ Weighted-Finite-Index Substrate (FWL / WLS Core)

**FWL/WLS-centric framing.** Estimand-characterization papers (Sloczynski,
Goodman-Bacon, Sun-Abraham, BJS, DCDH, MTW, Angrist-Imbens, Wooldridge, ‚Ä¶)
all express their estimators as `őłŐā = ‚ąĎ_r ŌČ_r ¬∑ ŌĄ_r`; those `ŌČ_r` are the
implicit FWL / WLS regression weights this substrate makes explicit. The
eight files below build, in order, a finite weighted index (`WeightedSupport
R`), its weighted inner product, the nuisance-subspace projection, the WLS
optimality theorem, the FWL identity, plus reusable indicator-span /
normalized-weight / additive-span subspace constructions. Both `Panel/`
(via `Cells I T := WeightedSupport (I √ó T)`) and
`Panel/EstimandCharacterization/` (via partition-cell, ordered-IV, response-type,
or cohort-period instantiations of `R`) are thin facades over this layer.

### 13W.a `Weighted/Support.lean`

The substrate: a finite index `R` carrying weights `ŌČ_r ‚Č• 0` (strictly
positive on the observed subset) summing to 1. Generalizes `Panel/Cells.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport R` | `structure` over `[Fintype R] [DecidableEq R]` | Bundles `observed : Finset R` (nonempty), `weight : R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, `weight_pos`, `weight_zero_off`, `weight_sum_one`. |
| `WeightedSupport.weight_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ c.weight r` | Pointwise nonnegativity. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.sum_weight_univ` | `‚ąĎ r, c.weight r = ‚ąĎ r ‚ąą c.observed, c.weight r` | Sum over `Finset.univ` reduces to sum over `observed`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.sum_weight` | `‚ąĎ r ‚ąą c.observed, c.weight r = 1` | Restated normalization. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.sum_weight_univ_eq_one` | `‚ąĎ r, c.weight r = 1` | Normalization over `Finset.univ`. **Proved.** |

### 13W.b `Weighted/InnerProduct.lean`

Weighted inner product `‚ü®A, B‚ü©_ŌČ := ‚ąĎ_{r‚ąąobserved} ŌČ_r ¬∑ A_r ¬∑ B_r` and its
matrix-valued lift to vector arrays `Fin K ‚Üí (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.ip` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) ‚Üí (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Scalar weighted inner product `c.ip A B = ‚ąĎ r ‚ąą observed, ŌČ_r ¬∑ A r ¬∑ B r`. |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_symm` | `c.ip A B = c.ip B A` | Symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_add_left` / `ip_add_right` | bilinearity | Additivity in each argument. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_smul_left` / `ip_smul_right` | `c.ip (s ‚ÄĘ A) B = s ¬∑ c.ip A B` (and sym.) | Scalar homogeneity. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_self_summand_nonneg` | `‚ąÄ r ‚ąą observed, 0 ‚Č§ ŌČ_r ¬∑ A r ¬∑ A r` | Per-summand nonnegativity. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_self_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ c.ip A A` | Positivity (semi-definite). **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_self_eq_zero_iff` | `c.ip A A = 0 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ r ‚ąą observed, A r = 0` | Definiteness on the observed support. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ipMat` | `(Fin K ‚Üí R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Fin K ‚Üí R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ‚ĄĚ` | Entrywise inner product `(c.ipMat A B) j k = c.ip (A j) (B k)`. |
| `WeightedSupport.ipMat_transpose` | `(c.ipMat A B).transpose = c.ipMat B A` | Transposed symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ipMat_add_left` / `ipMat_add_right` | entrywise bilinearity | Additivity in each tuple argument. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ipMat_smul_left` / `ipMat_smul_right` | `c.ipMat (s ‚ÄĘ A) B = s ‚ÄĘ c.ipMat A B` (and sym.) | Scalar homogeneity entrywise. **Proved.** |

### 13W.c `Weighted/Subspace.lean`

Weighted orthogonal projection onto a nuisance subspace `H ‚äÜ R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, and the
residual maker `M_H := id ‚ąí P_H`. Existence is via
`Causalean.Mathlib.SemiInnerProjection.exists_orthogonalProjection_of_posSemidef`
(since `c.ip` is only positive-semidefinite, not definite, off the observed
support).

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.weighted_orthogonal_projection_exists` | `‚ąÉ P : (R‚Üí‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] (R‚Üí‚ĄĚ), ‚Ä¶` | Existence of the weighted projection onto `H`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.proj` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) ‚Üí Submodule ‚ĄĚ (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Chosen weighted projection `P_H`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.proj_mem` / `proj_orthogonal` | membership / orthogonality | Defining properties of `proj`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.proj_apply_of_mem` | `X ‚ąą H ‚Üí proj X = X` on observed | Projection is identity on its image, on the observed support. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.proj_apply_eq_of_mem_orthogonal` | any orthogonal nuisance candidate matches `proj X` on observed | Uniqueness of the projection on the observed support. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.proj_idem_apply` | `proj (proj X) = proj X` on observed | Idempotence on observed cells. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize` | `Submodule ‚ĄĚ (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Residual maker `M_H = id ‚ąí P_H`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.tildeX` | scalar residualization | `XŐÉ := M_H X`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.tildeXVec` | column-wise residualization | `Fin K ‚Üí (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` tuple of residualized columns. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize_in_orthogonal` | `c.ip (c.tildeX H X) h = 0` for `h ‚ąą H` | Residuals are `H`-orthogonal. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize_self_of_mem` | `X ‚ąą H ‚Üí tildeX X = 0` on observed | Nuisance-space arrays residualize to zero on observed cells. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize_idem_apply` | `residualize (residualize X) = residualize X` on observed | Residual maker idempotent on observed cells. **Proved.** |

### 13W.d `Weighted/WLS.lean`

Headline: **weighted projections ARE the WLS solutions.** Characterizes
`proj` / `residualize` as the unique-modulo-off-support solutions to the
weighted least-squares minimization problem.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.proj_eq_argmin` | `‚ąÄ h ‚ąą H, c.ip (X ‚ąí proj X) (X ‚ąí proj X) ‚Č§ c.ip (X ‚ąí h) (X ‚ąí h)` | The weighted projection minimizes the WLS objective `‚ü®X ‚ąí h, X ‚ąí h‚ü©_ŌČ` over `h ‚ąą H`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.residualize_orth_iff_argmin` | residual-orthogonality at `p ‚ąą H` ‚ÜĒ WLS-optimality at `p` | The WLS first-order condition: orthogonality of `X ‚ąí p` to all of `H` is equivalent to `p` being the WLS minimizer. **Proved.** |

### 13W.e `Weighted/FWL.lean`

The **headline of the substrate**: the Frisch‚ÄďWaugh‚ÄďLovell identity. This is
the **finite-cell** FWL; the parallel measure-theoretic / L¬≤ FWL lives at
`Causalean/Panel/Analysis/Residualization.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.Q_XX` | `WeightedSupport R ‚Üí (Fin K ‚Üí R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Submodule ‚ĄĚ (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized Gram matrix `‚ü®XŐÉ, XŐÉ‚ü©_ŌČ`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.rhsVec` | `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Fin K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Residualized RHS / score vector `‚ü®XŐÉ, Y‚ü©_ŌČ`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.thetaHat` | `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Fin K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | FWL coefficient `Q_XX‚ĀĽ¬Ļ *ŠĶ• rhsVec`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.RankCondition` | `IsUnit (Q_XX c X H).det` | Nonsingularity of `Q_XX` (finite algebraic form of `Q_XX ‚ČĽ 0`). |
| `WeightedSupport.Q_XX_mulVec_thetaHat` | `RankCondition ‚Üí Q_XX *ŠĶ• thetaHat = rhsVec` | `thetaHat` solves the residualized normal equations. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.fwl_identity` | long-regression ő≤ = residualized-short-regression őłŐā | The joint minimizer `ő≤` of the long WLS objective over `(ő≤, őĪ ‚ąą H)` equals `thetaHat` of the H-residualized regressors. **Proved.** |

### 13W.f `Weighted/IndicatorSpan.lean`

Finite-cell partition algebra: singleton-cell indicators on a classifier
`G : R ‚Üí ūĚíĘ`, the linear span they generate, and partition-cell statistics
(cell mass, cell mean). Two-axis variant for cohort+period nuisance classes.
This file ports the **finite-cell** part of the archived
`Panel/CellBridge.lean`; the L¬≤ / measure-theoretic part
lives in `Causalean/Panel/Analysis/Residualization.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Weighted.cellIndicator` | `(G : R ‚Üí ūĚíĘ) ‚Üí ūĚíĘ ‚Üí R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Real-valued singleton-cell indicator `r ‚Ü¶ if G r = g then 1 else 0`. |
| `Weighted.indicatorSpan` | `(G : R ‚Üí ūĚíĘ) ‚Üí Submodule ‚ĄĚ (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Linear span of the singleton indicators `{cellIndicator G g}_{g : ūĚíĘ}`. |
| `Weighted.mem_indicatorSpan_iff` | `h ‚ąą indicatorSpan G ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ c : ūĚíĘ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ, h = ‚ąĎ g, c g ‚ÄĘ cellIndicator G g` | Characterization of membership. **Proved.** |
| `Weighted.cellIndicator_mem_indicatorSpan` | `cellIndicator G g ‚ąą indicatorSpan G` | Generators belong to the span. **Proved.** |
| `Weighted.twoAxisIndicatorSpan` | `(G : R ‚Üí ūĚíĘ) ‚Üí (T : R ‚Üí ūĚíĮ) ‚Üí Submodule ‚ĄĚ (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Two-axis indicator span: sup of `indicatorSpan G` and `indicatorSpan T`. Used for cohort+period nuisance classes (Goodman-Bacon, Sun-Abraham). |
| `Weighted.indicatorSpan_le_twoAxisIndicatorSpan` / `_right` | left and right injection | Each one-axis span sits inside the two-axis span. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.cellMass` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) ‚Üí (G : R ‚Üí ūĚíĘ) ‚Üí ūĚíĘ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Cell mass `‚ąĎ_{r ‚ąą observed, G r = g} ŌČ_r`. |
| `WeightedSupport.cellMass_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ c.cellMass G g` | Nonnegativity. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.cellMean` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) ‚Üí (F : R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (G : R ‚Üí ūĚíĘ) ‚Üí ūĚíĘ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Totalized cell mean of `F` on cell `g`. `noncomputable`. |
| `WeightedSupport.jointCellMass` | `(c : WeightedSupport R) ‚Üí (G : R ‚Üí ūĚíĘ) ‚Üí (T : R ‚Üí ūĚíĮ) ‚Üí ūĚíĘ ‚Üí ūĚíĮ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Joint cell mass `‚ąĎ_{r : G r = g, T r = k} ŌČ_r`. |

### 13W.g `Weighted/NormalizedWeights.lean`

Paper-agnostic finite weighted-sum algebra: normalized weights `a_i / ‚ąĎ a_j`
and weighted centered covariance / variance identities. Consumed by
estimand-characterization papers when re-expressing a regression coefficient
`őłŐā` as `‚ąĎ_r ŌČ_r ŌĄ_r`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NormalizedWeights.normalizedWeight` | `(őĻ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí őĻ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Generic normalized weight `a_i / ‚ąĎ k, a k`. `noncomputable`. |
| `NormalizedWeights.normalizedWeight_nonneg` | `(‚ąÄ i, 0 ‚Č§ a i) ‚Üí 0 < ‚ąĎ a ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ normalizedWeight a i` | Nonnegativity from nonneg raw weights and positive total mass. **Proved.** |
| `NormalizedWeights.sum_normalizedWeight_eq_one` | `0 < ‚ąĎ a ‚Üí ‚ąĎ i, normalizedWeight a i = 1` | Normalized weights sum to one. **Proved.** |
| `NormalizedWeights.weighted_center_cov` | `(‚ąĎ p = 1) ‚Üí ‚ąĎ p (x ‚ąí xŐĄ)(y ‚ąí »≥) = ¬Ĺ ‚ąĎ‚ąĎ p p' (x ‚ąí x')(y ‚ąí y')` | Weighted centered covariance equals one half the ordered pairwise cross-product average. **Proved.** |
| `NormalizedWeights.weighted_center_var` | `(‚ąĎ p = 1) ‚Üí ‚ąĎ p (x ‚ąí xŐĄ)¬≤ = ¬Ĺ ‚ąĎ‚ąĎ p p' (x ‚ąí x')¬≤` | Weighted centered variance equals one half the ordered pairwise squared-gap average. **Proved.** |

### 13W.h `Weighted/AdditiveSpan.lean`

Generic two-axis additive subspace: for a generic `R` with two projections
`f‚āĀ : R ‚Üí A` and `f‚āā : R ‚Üí B`, the additive span is `{ h | ‚ąÉ a b, h r = a (f‚āĀ
r) + b (f‚āā r) }`. Specialized to `R = I √ó T`, `f‚āĀ = Prod.fst`, `f‚āā =
Prod.snd`, this is the two-way fixed-effect subspace `H_twfe` used in
`Panel/FixedEffect.lean` and across the estimand-characterization layer
(Goodman-Bacon, Sun-Abraham, DCDH, BJS, ‚Ä¶).

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AdditiveSpan` | `(f‚āĀ : R ‚Üí A) ‚Üí (f‚āā : R ‚Üí B) ‚Üí Submodule ‚ĄĚ (R ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)` | Generic two-axis additive span. |
| `AdditiveSpan.mem_iff` | `h ‚ąą AdditiveSpan f‚āĀ f‚āā ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ a b, ‚ąÄ r, h r = a (f‚āĀ r) + b (f‚āā r)` | Characterization of membership. **Proved.** |
| `AdditiveSpan.const_mem` | constants lie in the additive span | `‚ąÄ c‚āÄ : ‚ĄĚ, (fun _ => c‚āÄ) ‚ąą AdditiveSpan f‚āĀ f‚āā`. **Proved.** |
| `AdditiveSpan.finiteDimensional` | `instance [Finite R] : Module.Finite ‚ĄĚ (AdditiveSpan f‚āĀ f‚āā)` | Finite-dimensionality. **Proved.** |
| `twoAxisAdditiveSpan` | `(I T : Type*) ‚Üí Submodule ‚ĄĚ ((I √ó T) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) := AdditiveSpan Prod.fst Prod.snd` | Specialization to `R = I √ó T`. |
| `IsUnitTimeAdditive` | `(Unit ‚Üí Time ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Prop` | Back-compat membership predicate matching the original `EstimandCharacterization.AdditiveSpan` API. |

### 13W.i `Weighted/OfProbabilityMeasure.lean`

Bridge (audit helper **M1**) connecting the finite weighted-support tower to a
probability measure on a finite measurable space. For a probability measure `őľ`
on a `Fintype` `R` with measurable singletons, the point masses
`ŌČ_r := (őľ {r}).toReal` form a `WeightedSupport R`, and its weighted inner
product equals the `L¬≤(őľ)` integral pairing. Lets the measure-theoretic
cell-bridge layer (`Residualization`/`CellBridge`) and the finite FWL tower be
used interchangeably on a finite probability space.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.ofProbabilityMeasure` | `(őľ : Measure R) [IsProbabilityMeasure őľ] ‚Üí WeightedSupport R` | Weighted support induced by `őľ`: weight `r ‚Ü¶ (őľ {r}).toReal`, observed set = positive-mass support. `noncomputable`. **Proved** (all structure fields discharged). |
| `WeightedSupport.ofProbabilityMeasure_weight` | `(ofProbabilityMeasure őľ).weight r = (őľ {r}).toReal` | Weight unfolds to the point mass. `@[simp]`. **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.ip_eq_integral` | `(ofProbabilityMeasure őľ).ip A B = ‚ąę r, A r * B r ‚ąāőľ` | **M1 bridge**: the induced weighted inner product is the `L¬≤(őľ)` integral pairing. **Proved.** |

### 13W.j `Weighted/ScalarFWL.lean`

Scalar (`K = 1`) Frisch‚ÄďWaugh‚ÄďLovell from the weighted **normal equations**
(audit helper **M2**) ‚ÄĒ the finite weighted-support counterpart of the
measure-theoretic `Causalean.PO.residualizedCoefficient_eq_of_normalEqs`. Bridges
the normal-equation inputs estimand papers produce to the residualized-coefficient
ratio, without going through the joint-minimization `fwl_identity`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightedSupport.ip_tildeX_self` | `c.ip (c.tildeX H X) X = c.ip (c.tildeX H X) (c.tildeX H X)` | The residualized regressor pairs identically with `X` and its residual (projection part is orthogonal). **Proved.** |
| `WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs` | `őĪ ‚ąą H ‚Üí 0 < ‚ü®XŐÉ,XŐÉ‚ü©_ŌČ ‚Üí ‚ü®Y‚ąíő≤X‚ąíőĪ, X‚ü©_ŌČ = 0 ‚Üí (‚ąÄ h ‚ąą H, ‚ü®Y‚ąíő≤X‚ąíőĪ, h‚ü©_ŌČ = 0) ‚Üí ő≤ = ‚ü®XŐÉ,Y‚ü©_ŌČ / ‚ü®XŐÉ,XŐÉ‚ü©_ŌČ` | **M2**: scalar FWL from the two weighted normal equations; derives the coefficient ratio (does not assume it). **Proved.** |

## 9i0a-FWLInstanceL2. `Panel/FWLInstanceL2.lean` ‚ÄĒ L¬≤(őľ) instance of the abstract FWL

Instantiates the abstract, panel-agnostic FWL development of `Mathlib/FWL.lean`
(¬ß10f) on the genuine inner-product space `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ`, with a finite-dimensional
nuisance subspace `H`. This is the **measure-theoretic instance** of the canonical
projection layer (see
`doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/INTERFACE_canonical_projection.md`):
the cell-indicator nuisance classes of the conditional-mean estimand papers
(Goodman-Bacon, Sun-Abraham, MTW, SŇāoczyŇĄski) are finite-dimensional subspaces of
`Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ`, so the abstract FWL coefficient, residualized normal equations, and
least-squares optimality apply directly. **This file is what makes `Mathlib/FWL.lean`
a live dependency rather than an orphan.** (Re-proving the raw-function
`Causalean.PO.residualizedCoefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` *through* this instance ‚ÄĒ the
full de-duplication of the measure-theoretic FWL tower ‚ÄĒ is a documented follow-up
requiring the raw-function ‚ÜĒ `Lp`-quotient coercion bridge.)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FWLInstanceL2.hasOrthogonalProjection_of_finiteDimensional` | `instance (H : Submodule ‚ĄĚ (Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ)) [FiniteDimensional ‚ĄĚ H] : H.HasOrthogonalProjection` | Finite-dim subspaces of `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 őľ` are complete, hence admit an orthogonal projection (free, via `HasOrthogonalProjection.ofCompleteSpace`). | **Proved** |
| `FWLInstanceL2.inner_eq_integral` | `inner ‚ĄĚ f g = ‚ąę a, f a * g a ‚ąāőľ` | L¬≤ inner product = population covariance: bridges the abstract FWL inner products to the integrals the papers manipulate. | **Proved** |
| `FWLInstanceL2.gramResid_mulVec_fwlCoef` | `IsUnit (gramResid H X).det ‚Üí gramResid H X *ŠĶ• fwlCoef H X Y = residInnerVec H X Y` | Residualized normal equations on `L¬≤(őľ)` (specialization of the abstract lemma). | **Proved** |
| `FWLInstanceL2.fwlCoef_original_minimizes` | `IsUnit ‚Ä¶ ‚Üí h ‚ąą H ‚Üí originalObjective ‚Ä¶ (fwlCoef ‚Ä¶) (P_H ‚Ä¶) ‚Č§ originalObjective X Y ő≤ h` | FWL least-squares optimality on `L¬≤(őľ)`. | **Proved** |
| `FWLInstanceL2.fwlCoef_eq_of_original_minimizer` | minimizer hypothesis `‚Üí ő≤ = fwlCoef H X Y` | ő≤-block uniqueness on `L¬≤(őľ)`. | **Proved** |

## 13sa. `Estimation/MinimaxATE/` ‚ÄĒ Structure-agnostic ATE optimality (Jin‚ÄďSyrgkanis 2024)

> **Entry point:** `Estimation/MinimaxATE.lean` (the roll-up beside the folder) imports the
> whole development and carries a docstring index of the headline theorems. Lower bounds:
> `minimax_lower_bound` (base, `ChiSquaredCore.lean`), `‚Ä¶_gen`/`‚Ä¶_var`/`‚Ä¶_continuous`,
> `parametric_lower_bound`. Achievability + capstone: `aipw_minimaxMiss_le`,
> `aipw_attains_minimax_rate` (both `Optimality.lean`).

Causal layer for the **converse** direction of doubly-robust ATE estimation: the
structure-agnostic minimax lower bound showing the DR/AIPW product-bias rate
`‚ąöőĶg¬∑‚ąöőĶm` cannot be beaten (Jin‚ÄďSyrgkanis 2024, *Structure-agnostic Optimality of
Doubly Robust Learning for Treatment Effect Estimation*, arXiv:2402.14264). Built
on the causal-agnostic `Stat/Minimax/` Le Cam layer (¬ß12w). The paper's
construction makes nuisances piecewise-constant on `M = 2^m` cells of `[0,1]^K`,
so we collapse each cell to a point and use a **finite covariate** `X : Fin M`
(uniform), `D Y : Bool` ‚ÄĒ all measures finite PMFs.

**Scope of the finite-`X` model (read before citing as "the paper's theorem").**
The paper's covariate is *continuous* (`X` uniform on `[0,1]^K`); only the
nuisances are cell-constant, so within a cell the actual position of `X` is still
observed. Our `X : Fin M` observes *only the cell index* ‚ÄĒ a strictly more
collapsed object. This loses nothing of the **research content**: the
perturbation construction, the `‚ąöőĶg¬∑‚ąöőĶm` ATE gap, and the Ingster Ōá¬≤ second-moment
bound never touch the within-cell continuum (the within-cell position is
*ancillary* ‚ÄĒ its conditional law is cell-uniform for every DGP in the class). So
the finite-`X` and continuous-`X`-with-cell-constant-nuisances models are
statistically equivalent for this functional, and a finite-`X` lower bound implies
the continuous-`X` lower bound by a **soft ancillarity reduction** (tensor both
hypotheses with the common within-cell law; the ancillary factor cancels in the
Ōá¬≤). That reduction is now **formalized** in `ContinuousX.lean` (¬ß13sa.6):
`two_point_lower_bound_continuous` proves the same `1/4` two-point bound
for an arbitrary within-cell ancillary space `(W, ŌĀ)`.

The model covariate is kept as a general finite type `C` (so `Model/Witness/Bump`
read `C`, instantiated to `C = Fin K √ó Bool` by the explicit construction).

**What the headline matches in the paper, and what it does not.** The paper's
Theorem 1 lower-bounds the **minimax `(1‚ąíő≥)`-quantile risk** of the *squared* error,
`ūĚĒź‚āô,ő≥(‚ĄĪ) = inf_est sup_{(m,g)‚ąą‚ĄĪ} ūĚí¨_{1‚ąíő≥}(|est ‚ąí őł|¬≤)` (eq. for `ūĚĒź‚āô,ő≥`), for
`ő≥ ‚ąą (1/2,1)`. A quantile bound `ūĚĒź‚āô,ő≥ ‚Č• ŌĀ` is *by definition* the statement that
every estimator misses the truth by `‚ąöŌĀ` with probability `‚Č• 1‚ąíő≥` on some in-class
DGP. So our `minimax_lower_bound` (`1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss ‚Ä¶ s`) **is** the
paper's headline theorem at `ő≥ = 3/4`, `ŌĀ = s¬≤ ‚Čć őĶg¬∑őĶm` ‚ÄĒ the *stronger*
(probability/quantile) form, not the weaker expected-risk form. The paper itself
notes (and we formalize in ¬ß13sa.7) that the quantile bound *implies* the
expected-`ūĚĒľ`-risk bound `sup E[(est‚ąíőł)¬≤] ‚Č• (1‚ąíő≥)ŌĀ`. On the remaining differences from
the paper's full generality: (i) the **constant general center** `(m‚āÄ,g‚āÄ,g‚āĀ) ‚ąą (0,1)¬≥`
(the paper's Assumption 2 in the constant-nuisance case, incl. the known-baseline
remark `ńĚ(0)=g‚āÄ`) **is now formalized** in ¬ß13sa.8 (`minimax_lower_bound_gen`),
generalizing the `(mŐā,ńĚ)‚Č°1/2` headline; the exact `őĽ`-independent ATE gap survives because
the center is constant *within* each Rademacher pair. (i') A **cell-varying center** ‚ÄĒ
nuisances that are arbitrary *functions* of the pair index (genuinely non-constant over the
covariate space), bounded away from `{0,1}` ‚ÄĒ **is now formalized** in ¬ß13sa.9
(`minimax_lower_bound_var`), using the new non-uniform `ingster_bound_general`
(¬ß13sa.9) for the per-pair Ōá¬≤ coefficient `őď‚Īľ`. The earlier note that this "needs a
Taylor-remainder Fano argument" was **wrong**: the paper keeps the two-point/Le Cam
structure, the first-order bump term cancels (`E[ŇĶőĒ]=0`), and the ATE separation is a
`őĽ`-independent *lower bound* ‚ÄĒ packaged abstractly as the **one-sided mixture two-point
lemma** `mixture_two_point_lower_bound` (¬ß13sa.2b). The following are still **not**
formalized: (i'') the *within-pair-varying* center (the two cells of a pair carry different
nuisance values ‚ÄĒ the only residual of the fully-functional Case-1 construction; needs the
one-sided lemma + the 4-distinct-values-per-pair overlap algebra); (ii) the weight function
is `w ‚Č° 1` (no `‚ÄĖw‚ÄĖ‚ąě¬≤`/`‚ÄĖw‚ÄĖ‚āā¬≤` factors); (iii) the additive `1/n` parametric-variance
term **is now formalized** (unweighted, `‚ÄĖw‚ÄĖ‚āā¬≤=1`) in ¬ß13sa.10
(`parametric_lower_bound`); (iv) only Case 1 (`őĶg ‚Č≥ őĶm`) of the bias construction is
built (Case 2 `őĶm > őĶg` would recover `‚ąöőĶgőĶm` in that regime).
