|---|---|---|---|
| `Obs M` | `abbrev := Fin M √ó Bool √ó Bool` | One observation `(X, D, Y)` with finite covariate. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `ValidDGP m g` | `structure`, `Prop` | Propensity `m : Fin M ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` and both outcome arms `g : Bool ‚Üí Fin M ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` take values in `[0,1]`. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `obsReal m g z` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Real mass `(1/M)¬∑P(D=d\|x)¬∑P(Y=y\|d,x)` of observation `z`. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `obsReal_sum` | `‚ąĎ z, obsReal m g z = 1` (needs `[NeZero M]`) | Total mass is 1 (sum over `Y`, then `D`, then `X`). **Proved.** | **Proved** |
| `obsReal_nonneg` | `ValidDGP ‚Üí 0 ‚Č§ obsReal m g z` | Nonnegativity from validity. | **Proved** |
| `obsPMF hv`, `obsLaw hv` | `noncomputable def`, `PMF (Obs M)` / `Measure (Obs M)` | Single-observation law as a PMF / probability measure (via `PMF.ofFintype`). `obsLaw_isProb` instance. | **Proved** |
| `ate g` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ := (1/M) ő£_x (g true x ‚ąí g false x)` | The ATE functional; depends only on `g`. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `l2sq a b` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ := (1/M) ő£_x (a x ‚ąí b x)¬≤` | Squared `L¬≤(P_X)` distance. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `InClass mhat ghat őĶg őĶm m g` | `structure`, `Prop` | Structure-agnostic nuisance class `‚ĄĪ`: valid + each outcome arm within `őĶg` and propensity within `őĶm` of fixed estimates `(mhat, ghat)`. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `InClassDGP ‚Ä¶` | `def`, subtype | A DGP packaged with its `InClass` proof. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `productLaw hv n` | `noncomputable def`, `Measure (Fin n ‚Üí Obs M) := Measure.pi ‚Ä¶` | `n`-sample i.i.d. product law; `productLaw_isProb` instance. | **Proved** |
| `nMiss hv n est s` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ` | `(productLaw hv n).real {x \| s ‚Č§ \|est x ‚ąí ate g\|}`: probability `est` misses the true ATE by `s`. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `nMSE hv n est` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ := ‚ąę x, (est x ‚ąí ate g)¬≤ ‚ąā(productLaw hv n)` | Mean-squared error of `est`: the expected-risk functional (the paper's weaker `ūĚĒľ`-risk form). | ‚ÄĒ |
| `nMiss_sq_le_nMSE` | `(hv) (n) (hs : 0 ‚Č§ s) ‚Üí s¬≤¬∑nMiss hv n est s ‚Č§ nMSE hv n est` (needs `[MeasurableSingletonClass C]`) | **Chebyshev/Markov bridge**: probability-of-miss controls MSE. Quantitative form of "quantile bound ‚üĻ expected-risk bound". Via `mul_meas_ge_le_integral_of_nonneg`; integrability free on the finite sample space. | **Proved, axiom-clean** |
| `minimaxMiss mhat ghat őĶg őĶm n est s` | `noncomputable def`, `‚ĄĚ := ‚®Ü p : InClassDGP ‚Ä¶, nMiss ‚Ä¶` | Worst-case-over-class miss probability; a lower bound on it is a minimax lower bound. | ‚ÄĒ |
| `bddAbove_nMiss_range`, `nMiss_le_minimaxMiss` | bddAbove / `nMiss ‚Č§ minimaxMiss` | Each in-class miss `‚Č§ 1`, so the sup is well-defined and dominates each member. | **Proved** |

### 13sa.2 `Estimation/MinimaxATE/Reduction/Witness.lean` ‚ÄĒ two-point reduction

Abstract Le Cam reduction (over a general finite covariate `C`). The explicit
witness is now built (¬ß13sa.4); this layer is correct regardless of how it is built.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TwoPointWitness C n mhat ghat őĶg őĶm` | `structure` (needs `[Fintype C] [Nonempty C] [MeasurableSpace C]`) | Le Cam witness: separation `s`, TV budget `c`, two `n`-sample laws `Q : Bool ‚Üí Measure ‚Ä¶` (`prob`), true ATEs `őł : Bool ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with `sep : 2s ‚Č§ \|őł true ‚ąí őł false\|`, `tvBound : tvDist (Q false) (Q true) ‚Č§ c`, and the realizability field `dominated` (each `Q j`-miss `‚Č§ minimaxMiss`, discharged by a mixture-of-in-class-DGPs construction). | ‚ÄĒ |
| `twoPointWitness_lower_bound` | `(W) (hest : Measurable est) ‚Üí (1 ‚ąí W.c)/2 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss ‚Ä¶ est W.s` | **Headline.** Le Cam two-point bound (`Stat.two_point_lower_bound_of_tvDist_le`) + realizability domination. **Proved, axiom-clean.** | **Proved** |
| `twoPointWitness_quarter` | `(W) (hc : W.c ‚Č§ 1/2) (hest) ‚Üí 1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss ‚Ä¶ est W.s` | A witness with TV budget `‚Č§ 1/2` forces every estimator to miss the truth by `s` with prob `‚Č• 1/4` somewhere in the class. **Proved, axiom-clean.** | **Proved** |

Small reusable algebra for the squared `L¬≤(P_X)` distance, used to verify

exactly on the budget boundary).

| `l2sq_comm` | `l2sq a b = l2sq b a` | Symmetry. | **Proved** |
| `l2sq_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ l2sq a b` | Nonnegativity (no `[NeZero M]` needed). | **Proved** |
| `l2sq_bump` | `(‚ąÄ x, (ŌÉ x)¬≤ = 1) ‚áí l2sq (fun x ‚Ü¶ b x + őī¬∑ŌÉ x) b = őī¬≤` (needs `[Nonempty C]`) | **Constant-magnitude (Rademacher) bump**: a `¬Īőī` perturbation has `l2sq = őī¬≤`, so `őī = ‚ąöőĶ` lands the perturbation on the boundary of `‚ĄĪ(őĶ,¬∑)`. | **Proved, axiom-clean** |

### 13sa.4 Explicit construction (Jin‚ÄďSyrgkanis ¬ß4.4, Case 1) ‚ÄĒ `ConstCenterHalf/` (`Construction/Membership/Gap/ExplicitWitness.lean`)

The explicit lower-bound witness, on the **paired-cell covariate** `C = Fin K √ó Bool`
(pair index √ó position) with centered estimates `mŐā = ńĚ ‚Č° 1/2` and unweighted ATE.
A Rademacher sign vector `őĽ : Fin K ‚Üí Bool` drives the bump
`őĒ őĽ (j,b) = signOf b ¬∑ signOf (őĽ j) ‚ąą {¬Ī1}` (`őĒ¬≤ = 1`, balanced per pair). Every
declaration **sorry-free + axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `őĒ`, `őĒ_sq`, `őĒ_mem` | Construction | `őĒ őĽ x = signOf x.2¬∑signOf (őĽ x.1)`; `őĒ¬≤ = 1`; `őĒ ‚ąą {¬Ī1}` | The Rademacher bump and its sign properties. |
| `mPerturbed`, `gPerturbed` | Construction | `mőĽ = 1/2 ‚ąí ő≤¬∑őĒ`; `gőĽ(1) = (1/2+őĪ¬∑őĒ)/(1‚ąí2ő≤¬∑őĒ)`, `gőĽ(0)=1/2` | The asymmetric (non-linear-in-`őĽ`) perturbed DGP (eq. 17, `mŐā=ńĚ=1/2`). |
| `validDGP_perturbed` | Construction | `0‚Č§őĪ, 0‚Č§ő≤, őĪ+2ő≤‚Č§1/2 ‚áí ValidDGP (mPerturbed ő≤ őĽ) (gPerturbed őĪ ő≤ őĽ)` | The perturbed nuisances stay in `[0,1]`. |
| `inClass_perturbed` | Membership | `ő≤¬≤‚Č§őĶm, (őĪ+ő≤)¬≤/(1‚ąí2ő≤)¬≤‚Č§őĶg ‚áí InClass mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm (mőĽ) (gőĽ)` | Class membership: `l2sq(mőĽ,mŐā)=ő≤¬≤` exactly, `l2sq(gőĽ(1),ńĚ(1))‚Č§(őĪ+ő≤)¬≤/(1‚ąí2ő≤)¬≤`. |
| `ate_gPerturbed` | Gap | `ate (gPerturbed őĪ ő≤ őĽ) = 2ő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(1‚ąí4ő≤¬≤)` | **Exact ATE ‚ÄĒ independent of `őĽ`** (the per-pair sum is sign-invariant). The ‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm) gap. |
| `Qfalse`, `Qtrue`, `signWeight` | ExplicitWitness | `Qfalse = PŐā^‚äón`; `Qtrue = (1/2^K)ő£_őĽ QőĽ^‚äón` (uniform `mixture`) | The two `n`-sample data laws (null vs. uniform mixture of perturbations). |
| `explicitWitness` | ExplicitWitness | `‚Ä¶ (htv : tvDist Qfalse Qtrue ‚Č§ 1/2) ‚Üí TwoPointWitness (Fin K√óBool) n mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm` | Assembled witness; `dominated` discharged by `mixtureReal_le` (all `QőĽ` in-class, common ATE). |
| `explicit_minimax_lower_bound` | ExplicitWitness | budgets + `htv : tvDist Qfalse Qtrue ‚Č§ 1/2` ‚áí `1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm n est (ő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(1‚ąí4ő≤¬≤))` | Conditional form (carries `htv`); `minimax_lower_bound` (¬ß13sa.5) discharges it. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.5 Ōá¬≤ indistinguishability core ‚ÄĒ `ConstCenterHalf/` (`ChiSqOverlap/Ingster/ChiSquaredCore.lean`)

The Ingster Ōá¬≤ second-moment bound that discharges `htv`, making the lower bound
**unconditional** (only the natural regime hypotheses remain). All sorry-free +
axiom-clean. Builds on the finite Ōá¬≤ formula `finite_one_add_chiSqDiv` (¬ß12w).

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `obsLaw_real_singleton` | ChiSqOverlap | `(obsLaw hv).real {z} = obsReal m g z` | The single-observation PMF point mass (generic `C`). |
| `chiSqOverlap`, `chiSqOverlap_eq` | ChiSqOverlap | `chiSqOverlap őĪ ő≤ őĽ őĽ' := ‚ąĎ_z qőĽ(z)qőĽ'(z)/pŐā(z) = 1 + (2ő≥/K)¬∑ő£_j signOf(őĽ j)signOf(őĽ' j)`, `ő≥ = őĪ¬≤+2őĪő≤+3ő≤¬≤` | **Exact closed form** of the single-obs Ōá¬≤-overlap (finite algebra; per-pair sign-balanced cancellation). |
| `ingster_bound` | Ingster | `0‚Č§ő≥, 2ő≥‚Č§1, 2n¬≤ő≥¬≤ ‚Č§ K¬∑log 2 ‚áí ő£_{őĽ,őĽ'} (2^K)‚ĀĽ¬≤¬∑(1+(2ő≥/K)ő£_j s_j s'_j)‚ĀŅ ‚Č§ 2` | **The Ingster bound** (standalone real analysis): `(1+x)‚Č§eň£` + Rademacher independence (factorize to `cosh(nc)^K`) + `cosh y ‚Č§ e^{y¬≤/2}`. |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_Qtrue_Qfalse` | ChiSquaredCore | `1 + Ōá¬≤(Qtrue‚ÄĖQfalse) = ő£_{őĽ,őĽ'} (2^K)‚ĀĽ¬≤¬∑chiSqOverlap(őĽ,őĽ')‚ĀŅ` | **Mixture 2nd-moment identity**: finite Ōá¬≤ formula + product point mass (`pi_real_singleton`) + `Fintype.sum_pow`. |
| `chiSqDiv_Qtrue_Qfalse_le_one` | ChiSquaredCore | regime ‚áí `Ōá¬≤(Qtrue‚ÄĖQfalse) ‚Č§ 1` | Combine the identity with `chiSqOverlap_eq` + `ingster_bound`. |
| `tvDist_Qfalse_Qtrue_le_half` | ChiSquaredCore | regime ‚áí `tvDist Qfalse Qtrue ‚Č§ 1/2` | `tvDist ‚Č§ ¬Ĺ‚ąöŌá¬≤ ‚Č§ 1/2` (discharges `htv`). |
| `minimax_lower_bound` | ChiSquaredCore | budgets + `2ő≥‚Č§1` + `2n¬≤ő≥¬≤ ‚Č§ K¬∑log 2` + `Measurable est` ‚áí `1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm n est (ő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(1‚ąí4ő≤¬≤))` | **Headline (unconditional).** No estimator is within `s ‚Čć ‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm)` of the true ATE w.p. `> 3/4` over the class, in the regime `n¬≤¬∑(őĶg+őĶm)¬≤ ‚Č≤ K`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

Helpers: `absolutelyContinuous_of_singleton_pos` (full support ‚áí AC), `productLaw_real_singleton`, `Qfalse_singleton_ne_zero`, `signWeight_toReal` (`= (2^K)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`).

### 13sa.6 Continuous covariate ‚ÄĒ `ContinuousX.lean`

The ancillarity reduction to the paper's continuous-`X` statement. The within-cell
position is modelled by an **arbitrary** probability space `(W, ŌĀ)`; the continuous
`n`-sample laws are the finite-cell laws tensored with `n` ancillary `ŌĀ`-draws.
Tensoring both hypotheses with the common factor leaves the Ōá¬≤ unchanged
(`chiSqDiv_prod_ancillary`, ¬ß12w), so the bound transfers verbatim. Sorry-free +
axiom-clean.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ancillaryLaw ŌĀ n`, `QfalseCont`, `QtrueCont` | `Measure.pi (fun _ => ŌĀ)`; `(Qfalse).prod (ancillaryLaw ŌĀ n)`; `(Qtrue ‚Ä¶).prod (ancillaryLaw ŌĀ n)` | The `n` within-cell ancillary draws and the continuous null / alternative laws on `(Fin n ‚Üí Obs (Fin K√óBool)) √ó (Fin n ‚Üí W)`. |
| `chiSqDiv_QtrueCont_QfalseCont_le_one` | regime ‚áí `Ōá¬≤(QtrueCont‚ÄĖQfalseCont) ‚Č§ 1` | The ancillary factor cancels (`chiSqDiv_prod_ancillary`), reducing to the finite-cell `chiSqDiv_Qtrue_Qfalse_le_one`. |
| `tvDist_QfalseCont_QtrueCont_le_half` | regime ‚áí `tvDist QfalseCont QtrueCont ‚Č§ 1/2` | Continuous-`X` indistinguishability. |
| `two_point_lower_bound_continuous` | budgets + regime + `Measurable est` ‚áí `1/4 ‚Č§ max (QtrueCont.real {miss 2s}) (QfalseCont.real {miss 0})` | **Headline (continuous covariate).** Two-point (Le Cam) obstruction on the richer continuous experiment; the ancillary within-cell position cannot help. Bounds the two-hypothesis max-miss, not `minimaxMiss`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.7 Expected-risk (MSE) form ‚ÄĒ `MSE.lean`

The paper's quantile lower bound (¬ß13sa.5) *implies* the weaker expected-squared-error
bound used by Balakrishnan et al. (`ūĚĒź‚āô,ő≥ ‚Č• ŌĀ ‚üĻ minimax `ūĚĒľ`-risk `‚Č• (1‚ąíő≥)ŌĀ`). This
file makes that implication explicit and **unconditional** via the Chebyshev bridge
`nMiss_sq_le_nMSE` (¬ß13sa.1). The witness DGP is pulled out of the two-point bound
`1/4 ‚Č§ max(‚Ä¶)`: either the null `(mŐā,ńĚ)` (left branch) or a single Rademacher-perturbed
DGP extracted from the uniform mixture `Qtrue` by `exists_real_ge_mixture` (a finite
average is `‚Č§` its maximal component). Sorry-free + axiom-clean.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `minimax_lower_bound_mse` | budgets + regime + `Measurable est` ‚áí `‚ąÉ p : InClassDGP mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm, (ő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(1‚ąí4ő≤¬≤))¬≤/4 ‚Č§ nMSE p.2.valid n est` | **Expected-risk headline.** Every estimator has MSE `‚Č• s¬≤/4 ‚Čć őĶg¬∑őĶm` on *some* in-class DGP ‚ÄĒ the product rate is an unbeatable `ūĚĒľ`-squared-error floor. The `(1‚ąíő≥)`-factored (`ő≥=3/4`) consequence of the quantile bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

(No continuous-covariate MSE form: on the non-finite continuous sample space the
squared error of an arbitrary measurable estimator need not be integrable, so an
unconditional Chebyshev step is unavailable ‚ÄĒ the continuous result stays in its
native quantile/two-point form, which is already the paper's risk notion.)

### 13sa.8 General constant center ‚ÄĒ `ConstCenterGeneral/` (`Construction/Gap/Membership/ChiSqOverlap/LowerBound/MSE.lean`, `namespace GenConstr`)

Generalizes the `(mŐā,ńĚ)‚Č°1/2` development (¬ß13sa.4‚Äď7) to an **arbitrary constant
nuisance center** `(m‚āÄ,g‚āÄ,g‚āĀ) ‚ąą (0,1)¬≥` bounded away from `{0,1}` ‚ÄĒ the paper's
Assumption 2 in the constant case, including the known-baseline remark. The center
is held constant *within* each Rademacher pair, so the ATE gap stays **exactly
`őĽ`-independent** (no Taylor/Fano needed) and the abstract `ingster_bound` (¬ß13sa.5)
is reused verbatim with `ő≥ := őď/2`. The `1/2` headline is the instance
`m‚āÄ=g‚āĀ=1/2`. All declarations **sorry-free + axiom-clean**; live in `namespace GenConstr`.

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `GenConstr` | Construction | `structure` (fields `őĪ ő≤ m‚āÄ g‚āÄ g‚āĀ : ‚ĄĚ` + the bounded-away / smallness inequalities `hő≤g‚āĀ : ő≤<g‚āĀ`, `hőĪg‚āĀ : őĪ‚Č§g‚āĀ`, `hgU : g‚āĀ+őĪ+ő≤/g‚āĀ‚Č§1`, `hmU : m‚āÄ(1+ő≤/g‚āĀ)‚Č§1`) | Bundles the bump scalars, the constant center, and exactly the inequalities keeping every perturbed probability in `[0,1]`. |
| `mhatG`, `ghatG`, `mPertG`, `gPertG` | Construction | `mŐā‚Č°m‚āÄ`; `ńĚ(1)‚Č°g‚āĀ,ńĚ(0)‚Č°g‚āÄ`; `mőĽ=m‚āÄ(1‚ąí(ő≤/g‚āĀ)¬∑őĒ)`; `gőĽ(1)=(g‚āĀ+őĪ¬∑őĒ)/(1‚ąí(ő≤/g‚āĀ)¬∑őĒ)`, `gőĽ(0)=g‚āÄ` | The general-center perturbed DGP (collapses to ¬ß13sa.4 at `m‚āÄ=g‚āĀ=1/2`, `ő≤/g‚āĀ=2ő≤`). |
| `denomG_pos`, `validDGP_hatG`, `validDGP_pertG` | Construction | `0<1‚ąí(ő≤/g‚āĀ)¬∑őĒ`; null & perturbed are `ValidDGP` | Positivity of the denominator and `[0,1]`-validity. |
| `gPertG_true_eq` | Gap | `gőĽ(1,x) = (g‚āĀ¬≤+őĪ¬∑g‚āĀ¬∑őĒ)/(g‚āĀ‚ąíő≤¬∑őĒ)` | **Cleared treated arm** (clears the inner `ő≤/g‚āĀ` fraction); used throughout. |
| `ate_ghatG`, `ate_gPertG` | Gap | `ate ńĚ = g‚āĀ‚ąíg‚āÄ`; `ate gőĽ = (g‚āĀ‚ąíg‚āÄ) + g‚āĀő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(g‚āĀ¬≤‚ąíő≤¬≤)` (needs `[NeZero K]`) | **Exact ATE ‚ÄĒ independent of `őĽ`** (per-pair sum sign-invariant). |
| `ate_gapG` | Gap | `ate gőĽ ‚ąí ate ńĚ = g‚āĀő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(g‚āĀ¬≤‚ąíő≤¬≤)` | The `‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm)` gap (collapses to `2ő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(1‚ąí4ő≤¬≤)` at `g‚āĀ=1/2`). |
| `l2sq_mPertG`, `l2sq_gPertG_true_le`, `inClassG` | Membership | `l2sq(mőĽ,mŐā)=(m‚āÄő≤/g‚āĀ)¬≤` exactly; `l2sq(gőĽ(1),ńĚ(1))‚Č§g‚āĀ¬≤(őĪ+ő≤)¬≤/(g‚āĀ‚ąíő≤)¬≤`; budgets `(m‚āÄő≤/g‚āĀ)¬≤‚Č§őĶm`, `g‚āĀ¬≤(őĪ+ő≤)¬≤/(g‚āĀ‚ąíő≤)¬≤‚Č§őĶg ‚áí InClass mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm mőĽ gőĽ` | Class membership. |
| `őď`, `chiSqOverlapG`, `chiSqOverlap_eqG` | ChiSqOverlap | `őď = m‚āÄőĪ¬≤/g‚āĀ + m‚āÄ(őĪ+ő≤/g‚āĀ)¬≤/(1‚ąíg‚āĀ) + m‚āÄ¬≤ő≤¬≤/(g‚āĀ¬≤(1‚ąím‚āÄ))`; `chiSqOverlapG őĽ őĽ' = 1 + (őď/K)¬∑ő£_j s_j s'_j` | **Exact single-obs Ōá¬≤-overlap closed form** (linear-`őĒ` terms cancel by construction; `őď=2(őĪ¬≤+2őĪő≤+3ő≤¬≤)` at `m‚āÄ=g‚āĀ=1/2`). |
| `QfalseG`, `QpertG`, `QtrueG`, `őď_nonneg` | LowerBound | `PŐā^‚äón`; `QőĽ^‚äón`; `(1/2^K)ő£_őĽ QőĽ^‚äón`; `0‚Č§őď` | The two `n`-sample laws and nonnegativity of the overlap coefficient. |

| `minimax_lower_bound_gen` | LowerBound | budgets + `őď‚Č§1` + `2n¬≤(őď/2)¬≤‚Č§K¬∑log 2` + `Measurable est` ‚áí `1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm n est (g‚āĀő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(2(g‚āĀ¬≤‚ąíő≤¬≤)))` | **Headline (general constant center, unconditional).** No estimator is within `s ‚Čć ‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm)` of the true ATE w.p. `>3/4` over the class, for any constant bounded-away `(m‚āÄ,g‚āÄ,g‚āĀ)`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `minimax_lower_bound_mse_gen` | MSE | same hyps ‚áí `‚ąÉ p : InClassDGP mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm, (g‚āĀő≤(őĪ+ő≤)/(2(g‚āĀ¬≤‚ąíő≤¬≤)))¬≤/4 ‚Č§ nMSE p.2.valid n est` | **Expected-risk form (general center).** MSE `‚Č• s¬≤/4` on some in-class DGP; the `(1‚ąíő≥)`-factored consequence. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.9 Cell-varying center ‚ÄĒ `VaryingCenterCase1/` (`Construction/Gap/Membership/ChiSqOverlap/LowerBound.lean`, `namespace VarConstr`) + `Reduction/WitnessMixture.lean` + `VaryingCenterCase1/Ingster.lean` (non-uniform Ingster)

Generalizes ¬ß13sa.8 from a *constant* center to a **cell-varying center**: the
nuisance estimates are arbitrary *functions* of the pair index `j : Fin K` (bounded
away from `{0,1}`), genuinely non-constant over the covariate space ‚ÄĒ the finite-model
analogue of the paper's functional center, for centers constant *within* each
Rademacher pair (the residual within-pair variation is gap (i'') of the ¬ß13sa scope
note). Because the center is shared by a pair's two positions, the per-pair ATE gap is
still **exactly `őĽ`-independent** (no Taylor remainder), but the Ōá¬≤-overlap coefficient
`őď‚Īľ` now **varies per pair**, so the bound needs the non-uniform Ingster inequality.
Two reusable pieces of infrastructure were added; everything **sorry-free + axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mixture_two_point_lower_bound` | Reduction/WitnessMixture | null in-class DGP + finite weighted family of in-class alternatives with **per-component** separation `2s ‚Č§ \|ate g‚āÄ ‚ąí ate(gőĽ i)\|` + `tvDist(Q‚āÄ, mixture) ‚Č§ c` ‚áí `(1‚ąíc)/2 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss` | **One-sided mixture two-point lemma.** Sharper than `TwoPointWitness`: the alternatives need not share one ATE ‚ÄĒ a per-component *lower* bound suffices (the asymmetric testing argument measures each component at its own ATE). The abstract key enabling fully-functional centers. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `ingster_bound_general` | Ingster | `(‚ąÄj,0‚Č§d j)`, `ő£‚Īľ d j ‚Č§ 1`, `(n¬≤/2)ő£‚Īľ(d j)¬≤ ‚Č§ log 2` ‚áí `ő£_{őĽ,őĽ'}(2^K)‚ĀĽ¬≤(1+ő£‚Īľ d j¬∑s_j s'_j)^n ‚Č§ 2` | **Non-uniform Ingster Ōá¬≤ bound.** Per-pair coefficients `d j` (vs the constant `2ő≥/K` of `ingster_bound`); factorizes to `‚ąŹ‚Īľ cosh(n¬∑d j)` via `Finset.prod_univ_sum`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `VarConstr` | Construction | `structure` (fields `őĪ ő≤ : ‚ĄĚ`, `m‚āÄ g‚āÄ g‚āĀ : Fin K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` + per-pair bounded-away/smallness inequalities) | Bundles the bump scalars and the **cell-varying** center (one bounded-away value per pair). |
| `mhatV`, `ghatV`, `mPertV`, `gPertV`, `validDGP_hatV`, `validDGP_pertV` | Construction | `mŐā x=m‚āÄ x.1`; `ńĚ(1,x)=g‚āĀ x.1, ńĚ(0,x)=g‚āÄ x.1`; `mőĽ=m‚āÄ x.1(1‚ąí(ő≤/g‚āĀ x.1)őĒ)`; `gőĽ(1,x)=(g‚āĀ x.1+őĪőĒ)/(1‚ąí(ő≤/g‚āĀ x.1)őĒ)` ; both `ValidDGP` | The cell-varying perturbed DGP, indexed through the pair index `x.1`. |
| `ate_ghatV`, `ate_gPertV`, `ate_gapV`, `ate_gap_nonneg` | Gap | `ate gőĽ` is a `őĽ`-independent sum over pairs; `ate gőĽ ‚ąí ate ńĚ = (card)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑2ő≤(őĪ+ő≤)¬∑ő£‚Īľ g‚āĀ‚Īľ/(g‚āĀ‚Īľ¬≤‚ąíő≤¬≤) ‚Č• 0` | **Exact per-pair ATE gap** (sign-invariant within each pair), nonnegative. |
| `l2sq_mPertV_le`, `l2sq_gPertV_true_le`, `inClassV` | Membership | per-pair budgets `(m‚āÄ‚Īľ¬∑ő≤/g‚āĀ‚Īľ)¬≤‚Č§őĶm`, `g‚āĀ‚Īľ¬≤(őĪ+ő≤)¬≤/(g‚āĀ‚Īľ‚ąíő≤)¬≤‚Č§őĶg` (all `j`) ‚áí `InClass mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm mőĽ gőĽ` | Class membership via average-of-bounded-terms (per-pair sup bounds are *sufficient*). |
| `őďV`, `őďV_nonneg`, `chiSqOverlapV`, `chiSqOverlap_eqV` | ChiSqOverlap | `őď‚Īľ = m‚āÄ‚ĪľőĪ¬≤/g‚āĀ‚Īľ + m‚āÄ‚Īľ(őĪ+ő≤/g‚āĀ‚Īľ)¬≤/(1‚ąíg‚āĀ‚Īľ) + m‚āÄ‚Īľ¬≤ő≤¬≤/(g‚āĀ‚Īľ¬≤(1‚ąím‚āÄ‚Īľ))`; `chiSqOverlapV őĽ őĽ' = 1 + ő£‚Īľ (őď‚Īľ/K)¬∑s_j s'_j` | **Per-pair Ōá¬≤-overlap closed form** (linear-`őĒ` terms cancel; coefficient varies per pair ‚Üí feeds `ingster_bound_general` with `d j = őď‚Īľ/K`). |
| `chiSqDiv_QtrueV_QfalseV_le_one`, `tvDist_QfalseV_QtrueV_le_half` | LowerBound | `ő£‚Īľ őď‚Īľ/K ‚Č§ 1`, `(n¬≤/2)ő£‚Īľ(őď‚Īľ/K)¬≤ ‚Č§ log 2` ‚áí `Ōá¬≤‚Č§1` ‚áí `tvDist‚Č§1/2` | Ōá¬≤ indistinguishability via the non-uniform Ingster bound. |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var` | LowerBound | per-pair budgets + `ő£‚Īľ őď‚Īľ/K ‚Č§ 1` + `(n¬≤/2)ő£‚Īľ(őď‚Īľ/K)¬≤ ‚Č§ log 2` + `Measurable est` ‚áí `1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm n est (¬Ĺ(ate gőĽ ‚ąí ate ńĚ))` | **Headline (cell-varying center, Case 1 `őĶg ‚Č≥ őĶm`).** No estimator is within `s` of the true ATE w.p. `>3/4`, for a nuisance center varying arbitrarily across pairs. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.9b Cell-varying center, Case 2 (`őĶm > őĶg`) ‚ÄĒ `VaryingCenterCase2/Construction/Gap/Membership/ChiSqOverlap/LowerBound.lean`

The **symmetric second construction** (Jin‚ÄďSyrgkanis 2024 eq. (18), `f_n > e_n'`).
`VaryingCenterCase1`'s Case-1 construction attains the product rate `s ‚Čć ‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm)` only when the
outcome budget dominates (`őĶg ‚Č≥ őĶm`); this file covers the opposite regime by
**swapping the two nuisances' roles**: the propensity deviation is the large one
(`O(őĪ+ő≤)`, carrying `‚ąöőĶm`) and the outcome deviation the small one (`O(ő≤)`, carrying
`‚ąöőĶg`). In the finite-cell model the construction is `gőĽ(0,¬∑)=g‚āÄ`,
`gőĽ(1,x)=g‚āĀ‚Īľ/D`, `mőĽ=m‚āÄ‚Īľ(1+őĪg‚āĀ‚ĪľőĒ)D` with `D=1+(ő≤/g‚āĀ‚Īľ)őĒ‚ąíőĪő≤`. Although non-linear in
`őĽ`, the observed masses are polynomial in `őĒ` (the `D` cancels in `mőĽ¬∑gőĽ(1)`; the
propensity collapses to `mőĽ=m‚āÄ‚Īľ(1+őļ‚ĪľőĒ)`, `őļ‚Īľ=ő≤/g‚āĀ‚Īľ+őĪg‚āĀ‚Īľ‚ąíőĪ¬≤ő≤g‚āĀ‚Īľ`). Reuses
`VaryingCenterCase1/Ingster`'s `ingster_bound_general`. **All proved, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `VarConstr2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Construction | `structure` (fields `őĪ ő≤ : ‚ĄĚ`, `m‚āÄ g‚āÄ g‚āĀ : Fin K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` + per-pair `hgU`/`hőļ`/`hmU` inequalities) | Bundles the bump scalars and cell-varying center for the propensity-dominant construction; `hgU` forces `D>0` and `gőĽ(1)‚Č§1`, `hőļ` keeps `mőĽ‚Č•0`, `hmU` keeps `mőĽ‚Č§1`. |
| `őļ`, `D2`, `mhat2`, `ghat2`, `mPert2`, `gPert2`, `mPert2_eq`, `validDGP_hat2`, `validDGP_pert2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Construction | `őļ‚Īľ=ő≤/g‚āĀ‚Īľ+őĪg‚āĀ‚Īľ‚ąíőĪ¬≤ő≤g‚āĀ‚Īľ`; `D=1+(ő≤/g‚āĀ)őĒ‚ąíőĪő≤`; `mőĽ=m‚āÄ(1+őĪg‚āĀőĒ)D`, `gőĽ(1)=g‚āĀ/D`; `mPert2_eq : mőĽ=m‚āÄ(1+őļőĒ)`; both `ValidDGP` | The Case-2 perturbed DGP and its affine `őĒ`-collapse (via `őĒ¬≤=1`), proved valid. |
| `ate_ghat2`, `ate_gPert2`, `ate_gap2`, `ate_gap2_nonneg`, `gPert2_true_eq2`, `clearedDenom_pos`, `denomE_pos` | VaryingCenterCase2/Gap | `ate gőĽ` is `őĽ`-independent; `ate gőĽ ‚ąí ate ńĚ = (card)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑2ő≤¬∑ő£‚Īľ g‚āĀ‚Īľ(őĪg‚āĀ‚Īľ¬≤(1‚ąíőĪő≤)+ő≤)/E‚Īľ ‚Č• 0`, `E‚Īľ=g‚āĀ‚Īľ¬≤(1‚ąíőĪő≤)¬≤‚ąíő≤¬≤` | **Exact per-pair ATE gap** (cleared treated arm `g‚āĀ¬≤/(g‚āĀ(1‚ąíőĪő≤)+ő≤őĒ)`; the two-position sum collapses `ŌÉ¬≤`), nonnegative. |
| `l2sq_mPert2_le`, `l2sq_gPert2_true_le`, `gPert2_true_sub`, `inClass2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Membership | per-pair budgets `(m‚āÄ‚Īľőļ‚Īľ)¬≤‚Č§őĶm` (exact, propensity), `ő≤¬≤(őĪg‚āĀ‚Īľ+1)¬≤/(1‚ąíő≤/g‚āĀ‚Īľ‚ąíőĪő≤)¬≤‚Č§őĶg` (worst-case, outcome) ‚áí `InClass mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm mőĽ gőĽ` | Class membership in the swapped regime: `mőĽ‚ąímŐā=m‚āÄőļőĒ` is **exact** (`O(őĪ+ő≤)`‚Üí`‚ąöőĶm`), `gőĽ(1)‚ąíńĚ(1)=ő≤(őĪg‚āĀ‚ąíőĒ)/D` is `O(ő≤)`‚Üí`‚ąöőĶg`. |
| `őďV2`, `őďV2_nonneg`, `chiSqOverlapV2`, `obsReal_pert2_eq`, `chiSqOverlap_eq2` | VaryingCenterCase2/ChiSqOverlap | `őď‚Īľ = m‚āÄ‚ĪľőĪ¬≤g‚āĀ‚Īľ¬≥ + m‚āÄ‚Īľ(ő≤/g‚āĀ‚Īľ+őĪg‚āĀ‚Īľ(1‚ąíg‚āĀ‚Īľ)‚ąíőĪ¬≤ő≤g‚āĀ‚Īľ)¬≤/(1‚ąíg‚āĀ‚Īľ) + m‚āÄ‚Īľ¬≤őļ‚Īľ¬≤/(1‚ąím‚āÄ‚Īľ)`; `chiSqOverlapV2 őĽ őĽ' = 1 + ő£‚Īľ(őď‚Īľ/K)¬∑s_j s'_j` | **Per-pair Ōá¬≤-overlap closed form** (constant part `=1`, the finite analogue of `E_őĽ qőĽ=pŐā`; only the `őĽ¬∑őĽ'` cross term survives ‚Üí feeds `ingster_bound_general` with `d j=őď‚Īľ/K`). |
| `chiSqDiv_Qtrue2_Qfalse2_le_one`, `tvDist_Qfalse2_Qtrue2_le_half` | VaryingCenterCase2/LowerBound | `ő£‚Īľ őď‚Īľ/K ‚Č§ 1`, `(n¬≤/2)ő£‚Īľ(őď‚Īľ/K)¬≤ ‚Č§ log 2` ‚áí `Ōá¬≤‚Č§1` ‚áí `tvDist‚Č§1/2` | Ōá¬≤ indistinguishability via the non-uniform Ingster bound. |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var2` | VaryingCenterCase2/LowerBound | propensity-dominant per-pair budgets + `ő£‚Īľ őď‚Īľ/K ‚Č§ 1` + `(n¬≤/2)ő£‚Īľ(őď‚Īľ/K)¬≤ ‚Č§ log 2` + `Measurable est` ‚áí `1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss mŐā ńĚ őĶg őĶm n est (¬Ĺ(ate gőĽ ‚ąí ate ńĚ))` | **Headline (cell-varying center, Case 2 `őĶm > őĶg`).** The symmetric construction covering the regime `minimax_lower_bound_var` cannot; together they give the full product rate `‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm)` in both regimes. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.10 Parametric `1/n` term ‚ÄĒ `Parametric.lean`

The additive `1/n` (parametric-variance) term of the paper's `ő©(őĶg őĶm + 1/n)` bound,
via a **separate** two-point construction independent of the Rademacher/Ingster bias
machinery. Both hypotheses are single product laws (no mixture): a constant center
`(m‚āÄ,g‚āÄ,g‚āĀ)` and a constant **outcome shift** `őī` on the treated arm only. The single-
observation Ōá¬≤ has the exact closed form `Ōá¬≤‚āĀ = m‚āÄőī¬≤/(g‚āĀ(1‚ąíg‚āĀ))`, so tensorizing
(`one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid`) and bounding `(1+Ōá¬≤‚āĀ)^n ‚Č§ e^{nŌá¬≤‚āĀ} ‚Č§ 2` keeps `tvDist ‚Č§ ¬Ĺ`
once `n¬∑Ōá¬≤‚āĀ ‚Č§ log 2` ‚ÄĒ i.e. `őī ‚Čć 1/‚ąön`. The ATE gap is exactly `őī`. **Axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_obsPert_obsNull` | `1 + chiSqDiv (obsLaw pert) (obsLaw null) = 1 + m‚āÄőī¬≤/(g‚āĀ(1‚ąíg‚āĀ))` | **Exact single-obs Ōá¬≤** for a constant treated-arm shift `őī` (the `d=0` block cancels; `d=1` block gives `m‚āÄ(1+őī¬≤/(g‚āĀ(1‚ąíg‚āĀ)))`). |
| `parametric_lower_bound` | `0‚Č§őī`, `g‚āĀ+őī‚Č§1`, `őī¬≤‚Č§őĶg`, `n¬∑m‚āÄőī¬≤/(g‚āĀ(1‚ąíg‚āĀ)) ‚Č§ log 2`, `Measurable est` ‚áí `1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMiss (fun _=>m‚āÄ) (fun d _=>if d then g‚āĀ else g‚āÄ) őĶg őĶm n est (őī/2)` | **Headline (parametric term).** With `őī ‚Čć 1/‚ąön` (so `s = őī/2 ‚Čć 1/‚ąön`, `s¬≤ ‚Čć 1/n`), no estimator is within `s` of the ATE w.p. `>3/4` ‚ÄĒ the `1/n` minimax floor even with a perfectly-known nuisance class. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.11 Achievability ‚ÄĒ `AIPWEstimator.lean`

The **matching upper bound**: the fixed-center AIPW estimator attains the `‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm)` rate.
Because the structure-agnostic class is centered at *fixed* nuisances `(mhat, ghat)` (not
data-estimated), the natural estimator is the plug-in AIPW **sample average**
`őłŐā(sample) = (1/n) ő£_i Ōą_AIPW(Z_i; mhat, ghat)`. Everything stays in the finite `Obs C` /
`productLaw` world (no potential-outcome lift, no asymptotics), so the pairing with the
`minimaxMiss` converse is direct. This is the finite specialization of the general AIPW DML
asymptotic-normality theorem `Estimation/ATE/DML.lean` (`dml_ATE_tendstoNormal`). **All proved,
axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipwScoreFin mhat ghat z` | `def`, `‚ĄĚ` | Fixed-center AIPW score `(ńĚ‚āĀ x ‚ąí ńĚ‚āÄ x) + D¬∑(Y‚ąíńĚ‚āĀ x)/mŐā x ‚ąí (1‚ąíD)¬∑(Y‚ąíńĚ‚āÄ x)/(1‚ąímŐā x)` at one observation `z=(x,D,Y)` (`yReal` reads `Y:Bool` as `{0,1}`). |
| `estAIPW mhat ghat n` | `def`, `(Fin n ‚Üí Obs C) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | The estimator: sample average `(n)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ő£_i aipwScoreFin mhat ghat (sample i)`. |
| `aipw_pop_mean` | `‚ąę aipwScoreFin ‚Ä¶ ‚ąā(obsLaw hv) = ő£_z obsReal m g z ¬∑ aipwScoreFin ‚Ä¶` | Population mean of the score is the finite PMF-weighted sum. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bias_identity` | `(ő£_z obsReal¬∑score) ‚ąí ate g = (1/\|C\|) ő£_x [(1‚ąím/mŐā)(ńĚ‚āĀ‚ąíg‚āĀ) ‚ąí (1‚ąí(1‚ąím)/(1‚ąímŐā))(ńĚ‚āÄ‚ąíg‚āÄ)]` | **Doubly-robust remainder identity** (the analytic heart): the plug-in bias factors into products of nuisance errors. **Proved.** |
| `exists_center_overlap` | `(‚ąÄx, 0<mŐā x<1) ‚áí ‚ąÉ őĶ>0, ‚ąÄx, őĶ ‚Č§ mŐā x ‚ąß őĶ ‚Č§ 1‚ąímŐā x` | Uniform center overlap from the finite `min` (so the score weights `1/mŐā`, `1/(1‚ąímŐā)` are bounded). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bias_bound` | `\|bias\| ‚Č§ őĶ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑((‚ąöl2sq(g‚āĀ,ńĚ‚āĀ)+‚ąöl2sq(g‚āÄ,ńĚ‚āÄ))¬∑‚ąöl2sq(m,mŐā))` | Finite Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz over uniform `P_X`: the bias is bounded by the product of `L¬≤` nuisance errors. **Proved.** |
| `aipwScore_bound` | `\|aipwScoreFin mhat ghat z\| ‚Č§ 1 + 2/őĶ` | The score is bounded (center valid + `mŐā` `őĶ`-off `{0,1}`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_var_bound` | `variance (estAIPW mhat ghat n) (productLaw hv n) ‚Č§ (1+2/őĶ)¬≤/n` | Variance of the i.i.d. average (via `variance_sum_pi` + bounded score). **Proved.** |

### 13sa.12 Capstone ‚ÄĒ `Optimality.lean` (minimax rate is `őė(‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm))`, attained by AIPW)

Pairs the converse (`minimax_lower_bound_var`) with the AIPW achievability into the
optimality statement. **All proved, axiom-clean** (`aipw_attains_minimax_rate` uses
only `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipw_mean_eq` | `0<n ‚áí ‚ąę estAIPW ‚Ä¶ ‚ąā(productLaw hv n) = ő£_z obsReal¬∑score` | Mean of the i.i.d. average equals the single-obs population mean (via `measurePreserving_eval` + `aipw_pop_mean`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_nMiss_le` | bias `‚Č§ b`, `variance ‚Č§ V`, `b<s` ‚áí `nMiss hv n (estAIPW ‚Ä¶) s ‚Č§ V/(s‚ąíb)¬≤` | **Bias-centered Chebyshev** miss bound (`meas_ge_le_variance_div_sq`; `MemLp` free on the finite domain via `eLpNorm_lt_top_of_finite`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_inclass_bias_bound` | `InClass mhat ghat őĶg őĶm m g ‚áí \|bias\| ‚Č§ őĶ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑(2‚ąöőĶg¬∑‚ąöőĶm)` | Uniform-over-class bias bound: `aipw_bias_bound` + `l2sq ‚Č§ őĶg/őĶm` via `Real.sqrt` monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_minimaxMiss_le` | `s > őĶ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑2‚ąöőĶg‚ąöőĶm ‚áí minimaxMiss mhat ghat őĶg őĶm n (estAIPW ‚Ä¶) s ‚Č§ ((1+2/őĶ)¬≤/n)/(s ‚ąí őĶ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑2‚ąöőĶg‚ąöőĶm)¬≤` | **Worst-case AIPW miss bound**, uniform over the class (`ciSup_le` + the three pieces), `‚Üí 0` as `n¬∑őĶg¬∑őĶm ‚Üí ‚ąě`. **Proved.** |
| `MinimaxRateThreshold mhat ghat őĶg őĶm n` | `structure` | Reusable "rate attained + unbeatable" vocabulary: a single `estimator` with a converse miss-lower-bound (`probLower` at `sepLower`) and an achievability miss-upper-bound (`missUpper` at `sepUpper`). |
| `aipw_attains_minimax_rate` | `noncomputable def`, `MinimaxRateThreshold P.mhatV P.ghatV őĶg őĶm n` | **Capstone.** For the cell-varying construction `P` with the lower-bound budgets/regularity: every estimator misses by `s_P = gap/2 ‚Čć ‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm)` w.p. `‚Č• 1/4` (`converse` ‚Üź `minimax_lower_bound_var`), while the AIPW estimator's worst-case miss at any `sepUpper > őĶ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑2‚ąöőĶg‚ąöőĶm` (also `őė(‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm))`) is `‚Č§ ((1+2/őĶ)¬≤/n)/(sepUpper‚ąí‚Ä¶)¬≤`. Hence the minimax ATE rate is `őė(‚ąö(őĶg¬∑őĶm))` and DR/AIPW attains it (constant-factor, not constant-sharp). **Axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.C Causal grounding ‚ÄĒ `Causal/Construction.lean`, `Causal/Bridge.lean`, `Causal/Minimax.lean`

Grounds the observed-data contrast `ate g` in the potential-outcome framework: the minimax lower bound is a bound on the genuine causal ATE `E[Y(1) ‚ąí Y(0)]`, identified by backdoor adjustment, not merely a regression contrast. `Construction.lean` builds ‚ÄĒ from a finite DGP `(m, g)` on a finite covariate `C` ‚ÄĒ the first concrete `POBackdoorSystem` in the library: a backdoor SCM `Un ‚Üí Xc ‚Üí A ‚Üí Y`, `Xc ‚Üí Y` with latent noise roots `Ea ‚Üí A`, `Ey ‚Üí Y` (`A = 1{Ea ‚Č§ m Xc}`, `Y = 1{Ey ‚Č§ g A Xc}`), lifted through `POSystem.ofSCM`. `Bridge.lean` defines the causal estimand and proves it equals the contrast.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dgpSCM` / `dgpPO` / `dgpBackdoor` | the witness gSCM, its `ofSCM` PO system, and the extracted `POBackdoorSystem (dgpPO m g) C` | The concrete finite backdoor construction (treatment `A`, outcome `Y`, covariate `Xc`); `structFun_measurable`/`isProbability_latent` discharged | **Defined** |
| `causalATE m g` | `(dgpBackdoor m g).ATE` | The causal estimand `E[Y(1) ‚ąí Y(0)]` of the constructed system | **Defined** |
| `dgp_consistency` / `dgp_unconfoundedness` / `dgp_propScore_eq_m` / `dgp_adjustedCE_eq_g` / `dgp_overlap` | the backdoor `Assumptions` fields | Consistency (free from `ofSCM_consistency`); unconfoundedness `A ‚üā (Y(1),Y(0))‚ą£X` (via `ofSCM_condIndepCF_of_dSep` + `condIndepCF_congr_cond`); `P[A=1‚ą£X]=m(X)` and `E[Y‚ą£A=d,X]=g(d,X)` (uniform-coordinate conditional expectations); two-sided overlap from strict `m ‚ąą (0,1)` | **Proved** |
| `causalATE_eq_ate` | `(hv : ValidDGP m g) (hso : ‚ąÄ x, m x ‚ąą Ioo 0 1) ‚Üí causalATE m g = ate g` | **Estimand bridge.** The causal ATE equals the observed-data contrast `ate g`, via `BackdoorEstimationSystem.őł‚āÄ_eq_ATE` + uniform `P_X`. So every minimax lower bound transfers to `E[Y(1) ‚ąí Y(0)]`. Axiom-clean (standard axioms + `native_decide` for the graph checks) | **Proved** |
| `minimaxMissCausal mhat ghat őĶg őĶm n est s` | `‚®Ü p : InClassDGP mhat ghat őĶg őĶm, (productLaw p.valid n).real {x | s ‚Č§ \|est x ‚ąí causalATE p.m p.g\|}` | Causal-centered analogue of `minimaxMiss`, with each miss event centered at the concrete backdoor causal ATE instead of the observed-data contrast. | **Defined** |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var_causal` | `hm`, `hg`, strict `‚ąÄj, P.m‚āÄ j * (1 + P.ő≤ / P.g‚āĀ j) < 1`, budgets/regularity ‚áí `1/4 ‚Č§ minimaxMissCausal P.mhatV P.ghatV ‚Ä¶ gap/2` | Causal version of the cell-varying minimax lower bound. The additional strict-overlap hypothesis keeps the perturbed Var witnesses inside the overlap region, allowing `causalATE_eq_ate` to transfer the observed-data lower bound to `E[Y(1) ‚ąí Y(0)]`. | **Proved** |

---

## 13eff. `Estimation/Efficiency/` ‚ÄĒ Hahn (1998) semiparametric efficiency bound

The **third vertex** of the ATE efficiency triangle, joining achievability
(`dml_ATE_isAsymLinear`, ¬ß13c) and the minimax lower-bound tower (¬ß13sa): the
*semiparametric efficiency bound* `= Var(efficient influence function)`, the
theorem that the AIPW influence function `Ōą_AIPW` (¬ß13a.2) **is** Hahn's efficient
influence function, and the "role of the propensity score" (the bound does not
depend on whether the propensity score is known). Reference: Hahn (1998),
*On the Role of the Propensity Score in Efficient Semiparametric Estimation of
Average Treatment Effects*, Econometrica 66(2):315‚Äď331. Plan: `doc/hahn_efficiency_plan.md`.

### 13eff.1 `Estimation/Efficiency/TangentProjection.lean` ‚ÄĒ abstract Hilbert efficiency machine

Pure Hilbert-space geometry over a real inner-product space `H` with a tangent
subspace `T` (`[T.HasOrthogonalProjection]`). The reusable abstraction every
future estimator-efficiency theorem cites. All **sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsGradient T g Ōą` | `Prop := ‚ąÄ s ‚ąą T, ‚ü™Ōą, s‚üę_‚ĄĚ = ‚ü™g, s‚üę_‚ĄĚ` | `Ōą` is an influence function / gradient agreeing with reference `g` on every tangent direction. |
| `efficientIF T g` | `noncomputable def := ‚ÜĎ(orthogonalProjection T g)` | The efficient influence function = orthogonal projection of any gradient onto the tangent space. |
| `effBound T g` | `noncomputable def := ‚ÄĖefficientIF T g‚ÄĖ^2` | The semiparametric efficiency bound. |
| `efficientIF_mem` | `efficientIF T g ‚ąą T` | The EIF lies in the tangent space. |
| `isGradient_iff_sub_mem_orthogonal` | `IsGradient T g Ōą ‚ÜĒ Ōą ‚ąí g ‚ąą TŠóģ` | Gradients form the affine coset `g + TŠóģ`. |
| `efficientIF_isGradient` | `IsGradient T g (efficientIF T g)` | The EIF is itself a gradient. |
| `orthogonalProjection_eq_of_isGradient` | `IsGradient T g Ōą ‚áí orthogonalProjection T Ōą = efficientIF T g` | The projection onto `T` is the same for every gradient. |
| `normSq_gradient_decomp` | `IsGradient T g Ōą ‚áí ‚ÄĖŌą‚ÄĖ¬≤ = ‚ÄĖefficientIF T g‚ÄĖ¬≤ + ‚ÄĖŌą ‚ąí efficientIF T g‚ÄĖ¬≤` | Pythagoras decomposition along the tangent space. |
| `effBound_le_normSq` | `IsGradient T g Ōą ‚áí effBound T g ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖŌą‚ÄĖ¬≤` | **The efficiency lower bound**: no gradient beats the EIF's norm. |
| `norm_eq_iff_eq_efficientIF` | `IsGradient T g Ōą ‚áí (‚ÄĖŌą‚ÄĖ¬≤ = effBound T g ‚ÜĒ Ōą = efficientIF T g)` | Sharpness: equality only at the EIF. |
| `efficientIF_unique` | `IsGradient T g Ōą ‚Üí Ōą ‚ąą T ‚Üí Ōą = efficientIF T g` | A gradient lying in `T` is the EIF. |
| `efficientIF_eq_self_of_mem` | `g ‚ąą T ‚áí efficientIF T g = g` | A tangent reference is its own EIF. |
| `effBound_eq_of_mem_sub` | `T' ‚Č§ T ‚Üí g ‚ąą T' ‚áí efficientIF T' g = g ‚ąß efficientIF T g = g ‚ąß effBound T' g = effBound T g` | **Tangent-shrinking** corollary: removing tangent directions orthogonal to `g` leaves the bound unchanged. |

### 13eff.2 `Estimation/Efficiency/Submodel.lean` ‚ÄĒ regular parametric submodels via exponential tilting

The analytic engine for pathwise derivatives. For a base law `P` and a bounded
mean-zero score `s`, the exponential tilt `P_t = (e^{t¬∑s}/c(t))¬∑P` realizes `s`
as a regular submodel. All **sorry-free**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tiltNorm P s t` | `noncomputable def := ‚ąę e^{t¬∑s} dP` | Normalizing constant `c(t)`. |
| `tiltExp P s ŌÜ t` | `noncomputable def := (‚ąę ŌÜ¬∑e^{t¬∑s} dP)/tiltNorm` | Tilted expectation `E_{P_t}[ŌÜ]`. |
| `tiltMeasure P s t` | `noncomputable def := (ofReal c(t))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ÄĘ P.withDensity (ofReal e^{t¬∑s})` | The tilted probability measure `P_t`. |
| `tiltNorm_zero` | `tiltNorm P s 0 = 1` | At `t=0` the tilt is `P`. |
| `hasDerivAt_tilt_numerator` | `HasDerivAt (fun t => ‚ąę ŌÜ¬∑e^{t¬∑s} dP) (‚ąę ŌÜ¬∑s dP) 0` | Differentiation under the integral sign (Mathlib `hasDerivAt_integral_of_dominated_loc_of_deriv_le`), bounded score + integrable `ŌÜ`. |
| `hasDerivAt_tiltExp` | bounded mean-zero `s`, integrable `ŌÜ` ‚áí `HasDerivAt (tiltExp P s ŌÜ) (‚ąę ŌÜ¬∑s dP) 0` | **The tilt-derivative lemma**: `d/dt E_{P_t}[ŌÜ]|‚āÄ = Cov_P(ŌÜ,s) = ‚ąę ŌÜ¬∑s dP`. The analytic core of every pathwise-derivative argument. |
| `tilt_exp_integrable` | `Integrable (e^{t¬∑s}) P` | Bounded score ‚áí density integrable (comparison with constant). |
| `tiltNorm_pos` | `0 < tiltNorm P s t` | Positivity of the normalizer (lower-bounded by `e^{‚ąí|t|M}`). |
| `isProbabilityMeasure_tiltMeasure` | `IsProbabilityMeasure (tiltMeasure P s t)` | The tilt has total mass 1 (`ofReal_integral_eq_lintegral_ofReal` + `ENNReal.inv_mul_cancel`). |
| `integral_tiltMeasure` | `‚ąę h ‚ąā(tiltMeasure P s t) = tiltExp P s h t` | Bridge: expectations under `P_t` are tilted expectations (`integral_withDensity_eq_integral_smul‚āÄ`). |

### 13eff.3 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATEVariance.lean` ‚ÄĒ the Hahn variance decomposition

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `hahn_weight_true_Linf`, `hahn_weight_false_Linf` | `MemLp (a/e)`/`((1‚ąía)/(1‚ąíe)) ‚ä§ P.őľ` | Overlap ‚áí the IPW weights are bounded (private helpers). |
| `hahn_mu_L2` | `MemLp (őľ_val d ‚ąė X) 2 P.őľ` | Conditional-Jensen `L¬≤` bound on the regression (private helper). |
| `aipw_variance_hahn_decomposition` | `‚ąę Ōą_AIPW¬≤ dP_Z = ‚ąę (őľ‚āĀ‚ąíőľ‚āÄ‚ąíŌĄ)¬≤ dP_X + ‚ąę (a/e¬≤)(Y‚ąíőľ‚āĀ)¬≤ dP_Z + ‚ąę ((1‚ąía)/(1‚ąíe)¬≤)(Y‚ąíőľ‚āÄ)¬≤ dP_Z` | **The Hahn efficiency-bound formula.** `Ōą = A+B‚ąíC`, `BC=0` (`a‚ąą{0,1}`), the two cross-terms vanish via `weighted_residual_integral_zero`. The last two terms are `E[ŌÉ¬≤‚āĀ/e]`, `E[ŌÉ¬≤‚āÄ/(1‚ąíe)]`. **Proved.** |
| `dml_ATE_attains_hahn_bound` | `‚ąö\|B(n)\|¬∑(őłŐā‚ĀŅ ‚ąí őł‚āÄ) ‚áí N(0, V_H)` | AIPW/DML attains the Hahn bound: `dml_ATE_tendstoNormal` with the limit variance rewritten as the 3-term `V_H`. **Proved.** |

### 13eff.4 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATETangent.lean` ‚ÄĒ Ōą_AIPW is the efficient influence function

Instantiates the abstract machine (¬ß13eff.1) in `H = Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 P_Z`, where the
mean-zero tangent space is `Tfull = (‚ĄĚ‚ąô1)Šóģ`. All **sorry-free**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `propensity_score_residual_integral_zero` | `‚ąę h(X)¬∑(1_{D=1} ‚ąí e_val(X)) dőľ = 0` | The propensity residual is mean-zero given `ŌÉ(X)` (private helper). |
| `aipw_orthogonal_propensity_score` | `‚ąę Ōą_AIPW¬∑(őĪ(X)¬∑(indA ‚ąí e_val(X))) dP_Z = 0` | **Role of the propensity score**: `Ōą_AIPW` is orthogonal to every propensity-score direction (B/C terms via `weighted_residual_integral_zero`, A term via the residual helper). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasure_P_Z` | `IsProbabilityMeasure S.P_Z` | The data law is a probability measure. |
| `aipw_memLp` | `MemLp Ōą_AIPW 2 P_Z` | `Ōą_AIPW ‚ąą L¬≤(P_Z)` (from `aipw_finite_var`). |
| `aipwLp`, `oneLp`, `Tfull` | `Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 P_Z` elements / `(‚ĄĚ‚ąôoneLp)Šóģ` | `Ōą_AIPW` and constant `1` as `L¬≤` classes; the mean-zero tangent space. |
| `inner_oneLp` | `‚ü™f, oneLp‚üę = ‚ąę f dP_Z` | The `L¬≤` inner product against `1` is the mean. |
| `aipwLp_mem_tangent` | `aipwLp ‚ąą Tfull` | `Ōą_AIPW` is mean-zero (`aipw_mean_zero`), so lies in the tangent space. |
| `aipw_is_efficientIF` | `efficientIF Tfull aipwLp = aipwLp` | **Ōą_AIPW is its own efficient influence function** (it lies in the tangent space). |
| `effBound_eq_variance`, `effBound_eq_hahn` | `effBound Tfull aipwLp = ‚ąę Ōą_AIPW¬≤ dP_Z = V_H` | The efficiency bound equals `Var(Ōą_AIPW)` equals the Hahn formula. |
| `efficiency_bound_optimal` | `IsGradient Tfull aipwLp Ōą ‚áí effBound Tfull aipwLp ‚Č§ ‚ÄĖŌą‚ÄĖ¬≤` | **The semiparametric efficiency bound**: every influence function of the ATE has variance `‚Č• V_H`; `Ōą_AIPW` attains it. |
| `effBound_eq_of_known_propensity` | `T' ‚Č§ Tfull ‚Üí aipwLp ‚ąą T' ‚áí effBound T' aipwLp = effBound Tfull aipwLp` | **Propensity-invariance** (Hahn's headline): the bound is unchanged when the tangent space shrinks to the propensity-known model (`Ōą_AIPW` is still in it by `aipw_orthogonal_propensity_score`), so knowing the propensity score does not improve efficiency. |

### 13eff.5 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATEEfficientIF.lean` ‚ÄĒ pathwise ATE efficient influence function

This capstone module connects Hahn's pathwise derivative calculation to the
abstract pathwise-gradient and projection machinery. It records the bridge from
the AIPW covariance formula to the `L¬≤(P_Z)` inner product, identifies the full
mean-zero space as the nonparametric tangent space under the supplied density
hypothesis, and states the conditional Hahn theorem that `Ōą_AIPW` is the
canonical efficient influence function for the backdoor ATE.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.ATEEfficientIF -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.inner_aipwLp_eq_integral` | `inner ‚ĄĚ (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2) f = ‚ąę (z : ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ), S.Ōą_AIPW z * ‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎf z ‚ąāS.P_Z` | The covariance pairing between the AIPW influence function and any square-integrable score equals the Hilbert-space inner product against the AIPW element in the observed-data square-integrable space. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.score_mem_Tfull` | `m.score ‚ąą S.Tfull` | Every regular submodel score is mean-zero, so every such score belongs to the full nonparametric tangent space for the observed-data law. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.isTangentSpace_Tfull` | `S.Tfull ‚Č§ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.tangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z ‚Üí Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsTangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z S.Tfull` | The full mean-zero space is a genuine tangent space once the supplied nonparametric-model hypothesis says it is contained in the closed span of regular-submodel scores. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.aipw_isPathwiseGradient_ATE` | `(‚ąÄ (m : Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.RegularSubmodel S.oneLp S.P_Z), HasDerivAt (fun t => Ōą (m.path t)) (‚ąę (z : ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ), S.Ōą_AIPW z * ‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎm.score z ‚ąāS.P_Z) 0) ‚Üí Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z Ōą (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2)` | Hahn's pathwise-derivative identity makes the AIPW score a pathwise gradient of the backdoor ATE functional along every regular submodel. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.aipw_is_efficientInfluenceFunction` | `(‚ąÄ (m : Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.RegularSubmodel S.oneLp S.P_Z), HasDerivAt (fun t => Ōą (m.path t)) (‚ąę (z : ő≥ √ó Bool √ó ‚ĄĚ), S.Ōą_AIPW z * ‚ÜĎ‚ÜĎm.score z ‚ąāS.P_Z) 0) ‚Üí S.Tfull ‚Č§ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.tangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z ‚Üí Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z Ōą (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2) ‚ąß S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2 ‚ąą S.Tfull ‚ąß ‚ąÄ (g : ‚Ü•(MeasureTheory.Lp ‚ĄĚ 2 S.P_Z)), Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z Ōą g ‚Üí Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.efficientIF S.Tfull g = S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2` | The AIPW influence function is the efficient influence function for the backdoor average treatment effect in the nonparametric observed-data model. |
<!-- /GEN -->

---

## 14. `Panel/` ‚ÄĒ Panel regression algebra

> **PARTIALLY ARCHIVED 2026-06-12.** The dead CausalSmith research *question grammar* and its
> population machinery (`Regression/`, `PathLimit/`, `Probability/`, `DGP/`,
> `Baseline/`, `Assignment/`, `Target/`, `Question.lean`, `Bridge.lean`) were moved
> to `archive/Causalean/Panel/` and their API descriptions removed from this file
> (CausalSmith research now proposes free-form questions; no live consumer; `CausalSmith`
> imports nothing from `Causalean.Panel`). See `archive/README.md` and
> `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-panel-substrate-unification-design.md`.

The cell / inner-product / projection / FWL machinery lives in
`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/` (see ¬ß13W). The live `Panel/` layer provides the
`R = I √ó T` instantiation (`Cells I T := WeightedSupport (I √ó T)`) plus the
panel-specific apparatus: balanced panels (`Cells.balanced`), the two-way
fixed-effect subspace (`H_twfe` via `Weighted.twoAxisAdditiveSpan I T`), the
cell-level potential-outcome layer (`PO/`), the share-weighted two-way panel
(`WeightedTwoWayPanel`, ¬ß14c-WTWP), and the TWFE indicator closed forms
(`FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms`, ¬ß14d-IndicatorClosedForms). It is
**algebraic, not estimation-theoretic**: finite-sample statements are identities
conditional on the treatment path.

Live subsections: ¬ß14a (`Cells` + `InnerProduct`), ¬ß14b (`Subspace` +
`FixedEffect`), ¬ß14c (`PO/`), ¬ß14c-WTWP (`WeightedTwoWayPanel`),
¬ß14d-IndicatorClosedForms (`FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms`).

### 14a. `Panel/Cells.lean` + `Panel/InnerProduct.lean`

#### 14a.1 `Panel/Cells.lean`

Observed unit-period cells `R ‚äÜ I √ó T` with strictly positive weights summing
to 1.  Mirrors Definition 2.1 of the projection note.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells I T` | `structure` (over `[Fintype I] [Fintype T] [DecidableEq I] [DecidableEq T]`) | Bundles `observed : Finset (I √ó T)` (nonempty), `weight : I √ó T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, `weight_pos`, `weight_zero_off`, `weight_sum_one`. |
| `Cells.weight_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ c.weight r` for all `r` | Pointwise nonnegativity (positive on `observed`, zero off it). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight` | `‚ąĎ r ‚ąą c.observed, c.weight r = 1` | Restated normalization. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight_univ` | `‚ąĎ r, c.weight r = ‚ąĎ r ‚ąą c.observed, c.weight r` | Sum over `Finset.univ` agrees with sum over `observed`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight_univ_eq_one` | `‚ąĎ r, c.weight r = 1` | Normalization restated over `Finset.univ`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.balanced` | `Cells I T` (under `[Nonempty I] [Nonempty T]`) | Balanced panel: `observed = univ`, `weight = 1/(\|I\|¬∑\|T\|)`. `noncomputable`. |
| `Cells.balanced_observed`, `balanced_weight` | `@[simp]` | Definitional unfoldings of `balanced`. |

#### 14a.2 `Panel/InnerProduct.lean`

Weighted inner product on scalar arrays and its matrix-valued lift to vector
arrays modeled as `Fin K ‚Üí ((I √ó T) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells.ip` | `(c : Cells I T) ‚Üí (I √ó T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (I √ó T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | `c.ip A B = ‚ąĎ r ‚ąą c.observed, c.weight r ¬∑ A r ¬∑ B r`. |
| `Cells.ip_def` | `@[simp]` unfolding | Direct unfolding of `c.ip A B`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_symm` | `c.ip A B = c.ip B A` | Symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_add_left` / `ip_add_right` | bilinearity | Additivity in left / right argument. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_smul_left` / `ip_smul_right` | `c.ip (s ‚ÄĘ A) B = s ¬∑ c.ip A B` (and symmetrically) | Scalar homogeneity. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_summand_nonneg` | `‚ąÄ r ‚ąą c.observed, 0 ‚Č§ c.weight r * A r * A r` | Per-summand nonnegativity, used by both positivity and definiteness. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ c.ip A A` | Positivity. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_eq_zero_iff` | `c.ip A A = 0 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ r ‚ąą c.observed, A r = 0` | Definiteness on the support `R`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat` | `(Fin K ‚Üí I √ó T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí (Fin K ‚Üí I √ó T ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) ‚ĄĚ` | Entrywise inner product `(c.ipMat A B) j k = c.ip (A j) (B k)`. |
| `Cells.ipMat_apply` | `@[simp]` unfolding | Direct unfolding. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_transpose` | `(c.ipMat A B).transpose = c.ipMat B A` | Transposed symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_add_left` / `ipMat_add_right` | bilinearity | Additivity in each tuple argument (entrywise). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_smul_left` / `ipMat_smul_right` | `c.ipMat (s ‚ÄĘ A) B = s ‚ÄĘ c.ipMat A B` (and symmetrically) | Scalar homogeneity entrywise. **Proved.** |

### 14b. `Panel/Subspace.lean` + `Panel/FixedEffect.lean`

Nuisance spaces, weighted residualization, and the two-way fixed-effect
subspace from Definition 2.2.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells.V` | `(I T : Type*) ‚Üí Type _ := (I √ó T) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Scalar cell-array space used throughout `Panel/`. |
| `Cells.weighted_orthogonal_projection_exists` | `‚ąÉ P : V ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] V, ...` | Existence of a weighted orthogonal projection onto a subspace. Proved by packaging `c.ip` as a positive-semidefinite bilinear form and applying the generic semidefinite-projection helper. |
| `Cells.proj` | `Cells I T ‚Üí Submodule ‚ĄĚ (V I T) ‚Üí V I T ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] V I T` | Chosen weighted projection onto a nuisance subspace. |
| `Cells.proj_mem` / `proj_orthogonal` | membership and orthogonality lemmas | Defining properties of `proj`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.proj_apply_of_mem` / `proj_apply_eq_of_mem_orthogonal` / `proj_idem_apply` | supportwise projection identities | Projection agrees with members of `H` on observed cells; any orthogonal candidate nuisance component agrees with the chosen projection on observed cells; projection is idempotent there. |
| `Cells.residualize` | `V I T ‚Üí‚āó[‚ĄĚ] V I T` | Residual maker `M_H = id - P_H`. |
| `Cells.tildeX` / `tildeXVec` | scalar and column-wise residualization | Residualized scalar array and `Fin K` tuple of arrays. |
| `Cells.residualize_in_orthogonal` | `c.ip (c.tildeX H X) h = 0` for `h ‚ąą H` | Key residual orthogonality. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.residualize_self_of_mem` / `residualize_idem_apply` | supportwise residual identities | Residuals of nuisance-space arrays vanish on observed cells; `M_H` is idempotent on observed cells. |
| `Cells.H_twfe` | `Submodule ‚ĄĚ (V I T)` | Two-way fixed-effect subspace `{h : h(i,t)=a_i+b_t}`. |
| `Cells.const_mem_H_twfe` | constants are in `H_twfe` | Fixed-effect space contains constant arrays. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.H_twfe_finiteDimensional` | `Module.Finite ‚ĄĚ (c.H_twfe)` | Finite-dimensionality inherited from the finite ambient cell-array space. |

### 14c. `Panel/PO/` ‚ÄĒ cell-level potential outcomes

Statement-level scaffolding for the panel potential-outcomes layer.
Mirrors Definitions 2.3‚Äď2.4 and Assumption 2.1 of
`CausalSmith/doc/general_projection_carryover_note.tex`.  Treatment paths,
exposure mappings, the bundled `PanelPOSystem`, and the (definitional)
consistency lemma.  Conditional means `m_r^0(D)` and exposure responses
`ŌĄ_r(e | D)` are defined via `MeasureTheory.condExp` against the
ŌÉ-algebra `ŌÉ(D)`.

#### 14c.1 `Panel/PO/TreatmentPath.lean`

Realized treatment paths and finite-memory histories.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `TreatmentPath` | `(I T A : Type*) ‚Üí Type _ := I ‚Üí T ‚Üí A` | A unit's treatment trajectory. Mirrors `D = (D_{it})` of Def. 2.3. |
| `TreatmentPath.History` | `[Inhabited A] (p : ‚Ąē) (D : TreatmentPath I (Fin T‚āÄ) A) (i : I) (t : Fin T‚āÄ) : Fin (p+1) ‚Üí A` | Length-`p+1` history `(D_{it}, ‚Ä¶, D_{i,t-p})` clamped to `default` for out-of-range lags. Implements the `D_{is} = 0` boundary convention. |
| `TreatmentPath.History_zero` | `History p D i t 0 = D i t` | Lag-0 unfolding (contemporaneous treatment). `@[simp]`. **Proved.** |

#### 14c.2 `Panel/PO/Exposure.lean`

Exposure mappings and baseline exposures.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Exposure` | `(I T A : Type*) (E : I √ó T ‚Üí Type*) ‚Üí Type _ := (r : I √ó T) ‚Üí TreatmentPath I T A ‚Üí E r` | Cell-indexed known function `E_r = e_r(D)` of the realized treatment paths. Mirrors Def. 2.3. |
| `BaselineExposure` | `{I T : Type*} (E : I √ó T ‚Üí Type*) ‚Üí Type _ := (r : I √ó T) ‚Üí E r` | Baseline `e_r^0 ‚ąą E_r` for each cell. Mirrors `e_r^0` of Def. 2.4. |

A binary finite-memory builder is intentionally not shipped (awkward dependent-type instantiation); downstream files build the `Exposure` inline against `TreatmentPath.History`.

#### 14c.3 `Panel/PO/CellPO.lean`

Bundled panel potential-outcomes system and its derived objects.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PanelPOSystem` | `structure` | Bundles `I, T, A, E, e, e0, ő©, őľ, D, Y` together with the typeclass instances (`Fintype`, `DecidableEq`, `MeasurableSpace`, `IsProbabilityMeasure`) and measurability/integrability hypotheses (`D_meas`, `Y_meas`, `Y_integrable`). Mirrors Defs. 2.3‚Äď2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.observedExposure` | `(r : I √ó T) ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí E r := e r (D ŌČ)` | Realized exposure at cell `r`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.observedY` | `(r : I √ó T) ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ := Y r (observedExposure r ŌČ) ŌČ` | Realized outcome at cell `r`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.DSigmaAlg` | `MeasurableSpace ő©` | ŌÉ-algebra `ŌÉ(D)` generated by the treatment-path random variable, defined as the supremum over `(i, t)` of the comap of `(fun ŌČ => D ŌČ i t)`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.DSigmaAlg_le` | `DSigmaAlg ‚Č§ ambient ŌÉ-alg` | `ŌÉ(D)` is a sub-ŌÉ-algebra, proved from the cellwise measurability hypotheses `D_meas` via `measurable_iff_comap_le` and `iSup_le`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.m0` | `(r : I √ó T) ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Baseline conditional mean `m_r^0(D) := E[Y_r(e_r^0) | ŌÉ(D)]`. `noncomputable`. Mirrors `m_r^0` of Def. 2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.tau` | `(r : I √ó T) ‚Üí E r ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Cell-level exposure response `ŌĄ_r(e | D) := E[Y_r(e) ‚ąí Y_r(e_r^0) | ŌÉ(D)]`. `noncomputable`. Mirrors `ŌĄ_r(e | D)` of Def. 2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.realizedTau` | `(r : I √ó T) ‚Üí ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ := tau r (observedExposure r ŌČ) ŌČ` | Realized exposure response `ŌĄ_r(E_r | D)`. `noncomputable`. |

#### 14c.4 `Panel/PO/Assumptions/Consistency.lean`

Panel consistency (Assumption 2.1).  Definitional under the structure setup.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PanelPOSystem.observedY_eq_potentialOutcome` | `observedY r ŌČ = Y r (observedExposure r ŌČ) ŌČ` | Pointwise consistency, by `rfl`. **Proved.** |
| `PanelConsistency` | `(P : PanelPOSystem) ‚Üí Prop` | Universally-quantified consistency statement: `‚ąÄ r ŌČ, observedY r ŌČ = Y r (observedExposure r ŌČ) ŌČ`. Named alias for Assumption 2.1. |
| `panelConsistency_holds` | `PanelConsistency P` | Consistency holds by `rfl` under the structure setup. **Proved.** |

#### 14c.5 `Panel/PO/Mobius.lean`

Boolean-cube inclusion-exclusion for Lemma 3.1.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PO.Mobius.indicator` | `Finset (Fin (p+1)) ‚Üí Fin (p+1) ‚Üí Fin 2` | Binary history with ones on the selected subset. |
| `PO.Mobius.indicator_empty` | `indicator ‚ąÖ = fun _ => 0` | Empty-subset indicator is the zero history. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.delta` | `((Fin (p+1) ‚Üí Fin 2) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí Finset (Fin (p+1)) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Boolean-cube interaction coefficient `őī_S` by alternating sums. |
| `PO.Mobius.prod_indicator_eq` | product over `S` equals `1` iff `S` is contained in active history support | Converts binary products to subset tests. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.coeff_sum` | alternating coefficient sum is `1` on `A=B`, else `0` | Inclusion-exclusion coefficient identity. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.mobius_expansion` | `ŌĄ h = ‚ąĎ_{nonempty S} őī ŌĄ S * ‚ąŹ_{k‚ąąS} h k` when `ŌĄ 0 = 0` | Lemma 3.1 Boolean-cube expansion. **Proved.** |

### 14c-WTWP. `Panel/WeightedTwoWayPanel.lean` ‚ÄĒ Share-weighted two-way panel (L1 unification target)

Share-weighted generalization of `Panel/UniformTwoWayPanel`
(audit `shared_core.md` item **M5**): two-way demeaning under a general unit pmf
`p` (Goodman-Bacon cohort shares; `p ‚Č° 1/|Unit|` recovers the uniform panel) with
a uniform period measure. Intended as the single L1 demeaning/FWL layer the
EstimandCharacterization papers instantiate (migration is a separate, review-gated
step).

A **bridge** section connects these concrete closed forms to the generic
`WeightedSupport` FWL substrate (`Panel/Weighted/`, ¬ß13W): the panel is the cell
support `R = Unit √ó Time` with weight `ŌČ_{(i,t)} = p_i/|Time|`, the `p`-weighted
inner product is `|Time|` times `WeightedSupport.ip`, and `ddot` is the generic
residual maker against the two-axis additive span. The abstract FWL coefficient
lemma `Weighted.WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs` is thus reusable through
the bridge, so the former bespoke (and consumer-less)
`finite_residualized_coefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` and its helper
`finiteResidualizedCoefficient` were removed. (The same-named live helpers in
`UniformTwoWayPanel`, ¬ß9i0a-UniformTwoWayPanel, are unaffected.)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `WeightedTwoWayPanel.UnitWeights` | `structure { p, pos, sum_one }` | Unit weights as a probability vector (`0 < p i`, `‚ąĎ p = 1`) | **Defined** |
| `unitMean` / `timeMean` / `grandMean` | `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Uniform-time unit mean, `p`-weighted time mean `‚ąĎ_i p_i V_{it}`, `p`-weighted grand mean | **Defined** |
| `ddot` | `(w) (V) (i) (t) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | Double-demeaned residual `V ‚ąí unitMean ‚ąí timeMean + grandMean` | **Defined** |
| `inner` | `(w) (V W) ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` | `p`-weighted finite inner product `‚ąĎ_i ‚ąĎ_t p_i ¬∑ V_{it} W_{it}` | **Defined** |
| `IsUnitTimeAdditive` | `abbrev` | Shared additive-class predicate `h_it = a_i + b_t` | **Defined** |
| `ddot_reconstruct` / `sub_ddot_eq_unitTimeProjection` / `unitTimeProjection_additive` | identities | Reconstruction + residual/projection identities | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_unit` | `(hT : 0 < card Time) ‚Üí ‚ąĎ_i ‚ąĎ_t p_i (ddot¬∑a_i) = 0` | `ddot ‚üā` unit-only functions (period sum of `ddot` vanishes per unit) | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_time` | `‚ąĎ_i ‚ąĎ_t p_i (ddot¬∑b_t) = 0` | `ddot ‚üā` time-only functions (`p`-weighted unit sum vanishes per period) | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_unit_time` | `IsUnitTimeAdditive h ‚Üí inner w (ddot V) h = 0` | `ddot ‚üā` the whole additive class | **Proved** |
| `cellSupport` *(bridge)* | `(w) ‚Üí WeightedSupport (Unit √ó Time)` | The panel as a cell-indexed weighted support, weight `ŌČ_{(i,t)} = p_i/|Time|` (every cell observed); needs `[Nonempty Unit] [Nonempty Time]` | **Defined** |
| `mem_twoAxisAdditiveSpan_iff` *(bridge)* | `h ‚ąą twoAxisAdditiveSpan ‚ÜĒ IsUnitTimeAdditive (uncurry h)` | Two-axis additive span membership = the panel additive-class predicate | **Proved** |
| `inner_eq_card_smul_ip` *(bridge)* | `inner w V W = |Time| ¬∑ (cellSupport w).ip V W` | The `p`-weighted panel inner product is `|Time|` times the generic `WeightedSupport.ip` | **Proved** |
| `ddot_eq_residualize` *(bridge)* | `(cellSupport w).residualize (twoAxisAdditiveSpan) V (i,t) = ddot w V i t` | Concrete‚ÜĒabstract: `ddot` is the generic residual maker against the two-axis additive span (pointwise, every cell observed) | **Proved** |

### 14d-IndicatorClosedForms. `Panel/FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms.lean` ‚ÄĒ TWFE residual closed forms

Reusable substrate for `Cells.tildeX c.H_twfe A` when `A` is a structured
indicator (cohort-period, switch-on diagonal, triangular). Bridges the gap
identified in `Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis/Helpers.lean` (L3, L4) and is the
cleanest place to put any future "closed-form residual under TWFE" lemma.
Includes a self-contained `cohortPeriodCells` construction (mirrors
`positiveCohortPeriodCells` from the staggered Q1 attempt; no Q-instance
import).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Cells.tildeX_const` | `c.tildeX c.H_twfe (fun _ => k) r = 0` on observed cells | Constants are absorbed by `H_twfe` (one-line via `residualize_self_of_mem`). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_unit_indicator` | unit-only indicator ‚Üí 0 | `ind(i = i‚āÄ)` is a member of `H_twfe` via `a(i) := ind(i = i‚āÄ)`, `b(t) := 0`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_period_indicator` | period-only indicator ‚Üí 0 | Symmetric: `ind(t = t‚āÄ)` ‚ąą `H_twfe`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_product_indicator_balanced` | `tildeX (1_{S_I}(i)¬∑1_{S_T}(t)) = (ind ‚ąí |S_I|/|I|)¬∑(ind ‚ąí |S_T|/|T|)` on observed | Centered-product residual under balanced weights. Proof: candidate `g ‚ąą H_twfe` plus `H_twfe_orthogonal_iff` plus balanced row/column centered-sum lemmas, then `proj_apply_eq_of_mem_orthogonal`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.cohortPeriodCells` | `(law : CohortLaw C) ‚Üí Cells (Fin C) (Fin S)` with weights `ŌÄ(g)/S` | Generic positive-cohort cells construction. **Defined.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_cell_indicator_cohortPeriod` | cell `ind(g = g‚āÄ ‚ąß t = t‚āÄ)` ‚Üí four-term factorization | Centered-cell residual under cohort-period weights. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_diagonal_indicator_cohortPeriod` | staggered diagonal `ind(g.val = t.val)` | Closed form with the explicit grand-mean correction `(1/S)¬∑‚ąĎ_{g.val<S} ŌÄ(g)`. Proof uses row/column normal equations plus the diagonal-hit Finset reindexing. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_triangular_indicator_cohortPeriod` | staggered triangular `ind(g.val < t.val)` | Closed form with the explicit grand-mean correction. Proof uses row/column normal equations plus finite-sum Fubini for the triangular predicate. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.H_twfe_orthogonal_iff` (private) | unit/period basis test suffices for `H_twfe`-orthogonality | Helper used by the substantive proofs. **Proved**: forward by specialization to unit/period indicators; backward by decomposing arbitrary `h(p) = a(p.1) + b(p.2)` into the `e_{i‚āÄ}`/`f_{t‚āÄ}` basis via Fintype sum manipulation. |

## 15. `ML/` ‚ÄĒ Standalone machine-learning spine

A causal-free supervised-learning library: basic regression methods as first-class
objects with their defining-optimization / closed-form-or-structure / population-target
properties. Does **not** import OSL/SCM/PO/Estimation. Design + plan:
`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-causalean-ml-module-design.md`,
`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-15-causalean-ml-module.md`.

### 15a. `ML/Core/` ‚ÄĒ the spine (Phase 0, complete)

Dual-view: a parametric `Predictor` (optimization lives here) and an extensional
`HypothesisClass` (population targets live here), joined by one `Bridge`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `squaredLoss`, `bool01`, `sigmoid`, `softplus`, `logisticScoreLoss` | `Core/Losses.lean` | Pointwise losses + logistic link; with `sigmoid_pos/lt_one`, `softplus_pos`, `squaredLoss_nonneg`. |
| `Predictor` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Parametrized predictor family: admissible `paramSet` + `predict : őė ‚Üí X ‚Üí Y`. |
| `HypothesisClass` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Extensional class: a set of measurable functions `X ‚Üí Y`. |
| `FeatureMap` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Feature transform `X ‚Üí (K ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)`; identity gives linear regression, other choices give polynomial/spline/Fourier (sieve) regression. |
| `Loss`, `empiricalRisk(P)`, `populationRisk(P)`, `iidEmpiricalRisk`, `HasFinitePopulationRisk` | `Core/Risk.lean` | Risk functionals in both views; `iidEmpiricalRisk` via `Stat.IIDSample.sampleMean`. |
| `IsERMP`, `IsApproxERMP`, `RegularizedERM`, `IsERM`, `IsPopulationRiskMinimizer` | `Core/ERM.lean` | Candidate-wise minimizer predicates via `IsMinOn` (parametric + extensional). |
| `imageClass`, `isERMP_to_extensional`, `populationTarget_pushforward` | `Core/Bridge.lean` | The realized class of a predictor; parametric minimizers push forward to extensional minimizers. |
| `empiricalRiskP_convexOn_of_loss_convex`, `convexOn_comp_inner`, `exists_isMinOn_of_isCompact`, `exists_isMinOn_univ_of_coercive`, `fderiv_eq_zero_of_isMinOn_univ`, `SubgradientAt`, `subgradientAt_zero_iff_isMinOn` | `Core/Convex.lean` | Convex-analysis substrate: convexity of empirical risk, linear-composition convexity, compact/coercive existence, Fermat's rule, subgradient‚ÜĒminimizer. |
| `populationRisk_eq_of_both_minimizers`, `populationRisk_minimizer_eq_target`, `IsL2Projection`, `square_loss_population_target_of_isL2Projection` | `Core/PopulationTarget.lean` | P3 scaffold + squared-loss keystone: a residual orthogonal to all functions of the covariate (the L¬≤-projection / regression function) minimizes squared population risk. |

### 15b. `ML/Linear/`, `ML/Ridge/` ‚ÄĒ linear least squares and ridge (Phase 1, complete)

Linear-in-features regression: predictor `x ‚Ü¶ ‚ąĎ‚āĖ ő≤‚āĖ (ŌÜ x)‚āĖ`; the `FeatureMap` makes
polynomial/spline/Fourier (sieve) regression instances of the same theorems.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `linearPredict`, `olsObjective`, `ols_is_squaredLoss_ERM_of_normalEq`, `empiricalRisk_squaredLoss_linear` | `Linear/Finite.lean` | OLS objective; P1: a normal-equation solution `XŠĶÄX ő≤Őā = XŠĶÄy` minimizes SSE; bridge to the spine's `empiricalRisk`. |
| `olsCoef`, `ols_normalEq_of_minimizer`, `olsCoef_normalEq`, `olsCoef_unique` | `Linear/ClosedForm.lean` | P2: minimizer ‚áí normal equations (converse); closed form `(XŠĶÄX)‚ĀĽ¬ĻXŠĶÄy` and uniqueness under `IsUnit (XŠĶÄX).det`. |
| `IsPopulationOLS`, `bestLinearPredictor_minimizes_populationRisk` | `Linear/Population.lean` | P3: a coefficient with residual uncorrelated to every feature minimizes squared population risk over the linear class (best linear predictor). |
| `ridgeObjective`, `ridge_is_regularized_squaredLoss_ERM_of_normalEq` | `Ridge/Finite.lean` | Ridge objective `SSE + őĽ‚ÄĖő≤‚ÄĖ¬≤`; P1: a ridge normal-equation solution `(XŠĶÄX+őĽI)ő≤Őā = XŠĶÄy` minimizes it (őĽ ‚Č• 0). |
| `ridgeCoef`, `ridgeGram_posDef`, `ridgeCoef_normalEq`, `ridgeCoef_unique` | `Ridge/ClosedForm.lean` | P2: for őĽ>0 the ridge Gram is positive definite ‚áí closed form `(XŠĶÄX+őĽI)‚ĀĽ¬ĻXŠĶÄy`, unique without a full-rank assumption. |
| `populationRidgeObjective`, `IsPopulationRidge`, `populationRidge_minimizes` | `Ridge/Population.lean` | P3: the regularized population normal equations `E[(Y‚ąí‚ü™ő≤,ŌÜ‚üę)ŌÜ‚āĖ] = őĽő≤‚āĖ` characterize the (shrunk) population ridge minimizer. |

### 15b'. `ML/Ridge/Rate.lean` ‚ÄĒ ridge root-n estimation rate (learning theory)

The causal-free learning-theory result `ML/Causal/RateAssembly` consumes to discharge the
DML `o_p(n^{-1/4})` nuisance condition: the sample ridge predictor converges to the population
ridge minimizer at the L¬≤(P_X) root-n rate. Built from the i.i.d.-sample second-moment engine
`Stat.IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_isBigOp` and the in-probability continuous-mapping /
stochastic-order substrate in `Stat/Limit/ContinuousMapping.lean` and
`Stat/EmpiricalProcess/CrossFitRate.lean`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `empiricalGram`, `empiricalCross`, `sampleRidgeCoef`, `sampleRidgePredictor`, `populationGram` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The sample ridge estimator over the first `n` i.i.d. points: empirical feature Gram `ńú‚āô`, cross moment `ńą‚āô`, coefficient `(ńú‚āô+őĽI)‚ĀĽ¬Ļńą‚āô`, the induced predictor, and the entrywise population Gram `G‚āĖ‚āó = ‚ąę ŌÜ‚āĖŌÜ‚āó`. |
| `empiricalGram_posSemidef`, `sampleRidgeCoef_sub_eq` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | `ńú‚āô` is PSD (average of rank-one `ŌÜŌÜŠĶÄ`); the algebraic crux factoring the coefficient error `ő≤Őā‚āô‚ąíő≤‚čÜ = (ńú‚āô+őĽI)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(ńą‚āô‚ąíńú‚āôő≤‚čÜ‚ąíőĽő≤‚čÜ)`. |
| `centered_score_mean_isBigOp` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The centered empirical score mean `ńą‚āô‚ąíńú‚āôő≤‚čÜ‚ąíőĽő≤‚čÜ` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})` (centered i.i.d. vector mean with square-integrable score coordinates; population mean `őĽő≤‚čÜ` from `IsPopulationRidge`). |
| `sampleRidgeCoef_isBigOp` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The ridge coefficient error `‚ÄĖő≤Őā‚āô‚ąíő≤‚čÜ‚ÄĖ` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})`: the regularized empirical-Gram inverse is `O_p(1)` (entrywise matrix LLN + inverse continuity at the PosDef limit) times the centered score mean. |
| `continuousAt_matrixInv_entry` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | Matrix-topology helper: at any nonsingular finite matrix, each inverse entry `M ‚Ü¶ (M‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)‚āĖ‚āó` is continuous. |
| `eLpNorm_predictor_sub_le` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | Deterministic: the prediction L¬≤(P_X) error is bounded by a finite constant times the coefficient error, `‚ÄĖ‚ąĎ‚āĖ őī‚āĖŌÜ¬∑‚āĖ‚ÄĖ_{L¬≤} ‚Č§ C‚ÄĖőī‚ÄĖ`. |
| `ridge_achievesL2Rate` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | **Headline.** Under the population ridge normal equation, `őĽ>0`, the L‚Āī feature moment and square-integrable score, the sample ridge predictor attains the root-n L¬≤ rate toward the population ridge minimizer. |

### 15c. `ML/Lasso/` ‚ÄĒ L1-regularized least squares (Phase 2)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `l1penalty`, `lassoObjective`, `softThreshold`, `l1penalty_nonneg`, `convexOn_l1penalty`, `convexOn_olsObjective`, `convexOn_lassoObjective` | `Lasso/Finite.lean` | L1 penalty and lasso objective; convexity of the (non-smooth) objective. |
| `softThreshold_isMinOn` | `Lasso/Optimality.lean` | P2: the soft-threshold `S_őĽ(a)` is the exact minimizer of the scalar prox objective `(u‚ąía)¬≤ + 2őĽ|u|`. |
| `rademacherComplexity_l1_ball_le` | `Lasso/Rate.lean` | **Rademacher rate (high-dim).** Over `L‚ąě`-bounded features, the L¬Ļ-ball linear class has `rademacherComplexity ‚Č§ (X‚ąě¬∑W/‚ąön)¬∑‚ąö(2 log 2d)` ‚ÄĒ the `‚ąö(log d / n)` lasso rate. |
| `lasso_erm_excess_rate` | `Lasso/Rate.lean` | **Lasso ERM excess-risk rate (linear functional).** Instantiates the separable ERM oracle inequality for the L¬Ļ-ball linear class on the L‚ąě-bounded feature domain, giving a tail bound at threshold `4¬∑(X‚ąě¬∑W/‚ąön)¬∑‚ąö(2 log 2d) + 2őĶ`. Loss is the bare linear functional. |
| `LassoFeat`, `LassoWeight`, `lasso_erm_squaredLoss_excess_rate` | `Lasso/SquaredLoss.lean` | **Lasso ERM squared-loss excess-risk rate.** Genuine regression loss `(‚ąĎ‚Īľ w‚Īľx‚Īľ‚ąíy)¬≤` (L‚ąě-bounded features, responses in `[‚ąíYb,Yb]`). Same centered-loss decomposition as the kernel version (quadratic part via the infinite-index contraction + clamped square, cross part `2y¬∑x` as a rescaled lasso linear class), carrying the `‚ąö(2 log 2d)` factor: tail bound at threshold `4¬∑((4(X‚ąěW)¬≤+2¬∑Yb¬∑X‚ąě¬∑W)/‚ąön)¬∑‚ąö(2 log 2d) + 2őĶ`. |
| `margin_erm_surrogate_excess_rate` | `Margin/Rate.lean` | **Margin classification surrogate excess-risk rate.** For ANY `L`-Lipschitz surrogate `ŌÜ` (hinge, logistic, smoothed 0‚Äď1), the margin loss `ŌÜ(y¬∑‚ü™w,x‚ü©)` over the `W`-ball of linear classifiers (features in `Xb`-ball, labels in `[‚ąíYb,Yb]`). Single contraction: the margin `y¬∑‚ü™w,x‚ü©=‚ü™w,y¬∑x‚ü©` is a linear class over rescaled features and `ŌÜŐÉ=ŌÜ‚ąíŌÜ(0)` is `LipschitzAt0`, so `RC ‚Č§ 2L¬∑Yb¬∑Xb¬∑W/‚ąön`; tail bound at threshold `8¬∑L¬∑Yb¬∑Xb¬∑W/‚ąön + 2őĶ`. |
| `effectiveDimension`, `effectiveDimension_nonneg`, `summable_effectiveDimension_term`, `effectiveDimension_le_trace_div`, `effectiveDimension_le_card`, `effectiveDimension_antitone` | `Kernel/EffectiveDimension.lean` | **Effective dimension** `N(őĽ)=‚ąĎŠĶĘ őľŠĶĘ/(őľŠĶĘ+őĽ)=tr(T(T+őĽ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)` of a trace-class operator (summable nonneg eigenvalues `őľ`), the quantity the Caponnetto‚ÄďDe Vito kernel-ridge optimal rate is stated in terms of. Definitional foundation + structural properties: nonneg, summand-summability, the dimension-free bound `N(őĽ)‚Č§(tr T)/őĽ`, the finite bound `N(őĽ)‚Č§#őĻ`, and antitonicity in `őĽ`. The downstream optimal-rate theorem additionally needs Hilbert-space operator-concentration (operator Bernstein), a separate substrate ‚ÄĒ see the file docstring. |

Full subgradient/KKT optimality for general designs and the population L1 target are deferred.

### 15d. `ML/Binary/`, `ML/Surrogate/` ‚ÄĒ logistic regression and convex ERM (Phase 2)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `logisticEmpRisk`, `convexOn_softplus`, `convexOn_logisticScoreLoss`, `convexOn_logisticEmpRisk`, `continuous_logisticEmpRisk`, `logistic_exists_minimizer_on_compact` | `Binary/Logistic.lean` | P1/P2: logistic log-loss is convex (softplus convex via monotone sigmoid derivative); a minimizer exists on any compact parameter set. |
| `bernoulliCE`, `bernoulliCE_isMinOn` | `Binary/FisherConsistency.lean` | P3: cross-entropy is minimized at the true probability on the interior `(0,1)` (Gibbs inequality). The interior is essential ‚ÄĒ the closed-interval version is false under `Real.log 0 = 0`. |
| `ProperBinaryLoss`, `properLoss_population_minimizer_recovers_eta` | `Surrogate/GenericERM.lean` | A proper loss is Fisher consistent: the population-risk minimizer recovers the conditional probability `ő∑` (via `integral_mono`). |

### 15d'. `ML/Binary/Rate.lean` ‚ÄĒ L¬≤-regularized logistic estimation rate (learning theory)

Binary-response analogue of the ridge rate: the `őĽ‚ÄĖő≤‚ÄĖ¬≤` penalty makes the logistic objective
globally `2őĽ`-strongly convex, so the coefficient error is controlled by a strong-convexity basic
inequality (the role ridge's `őĽI` played) rather than a closed-form inverse. Reuses the same
centered-score `O_p(n^{-1/2})` machinery and the shared linear-predictor L¬≤ bound.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `regLogisticGrad`, `IsPopulationRegLogistic`, `logisticPredictor` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | The empirical penalized-logistic gradient `n‚ĀĽ¬Ļő£(ŌÉ‚ü®ő≤,ŌÜŠĶĘ‚ü©‚ąíyŠĶĘ)ŌÜŠĶĘ + 2őĽő≤`, the penalized population first-order condition `E[(ŌÉ‚ü®ő≤‚čÜ,ŌÜ‚ü©‚ąíY)ŌÜ‚āĖ] + 2őĽő≤‚čÜ‚āĖ = 0`, and the fitted-probability predictor `x ‚Ü¶ ŌÉ(‚ü®ő≤,ŌÜ(x)‚ü©)`. |
| `regLogistic_basic_inequality` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | Deterministic strong-convexity crux: a sample point solving the empirical FOC `‚ąá‚āô(ő≤Őā)=0` satisfies `2őĽ‚ÄĖő≤Őā‚ąíő≤‚čÜ‚ÄĖ ‚Č§ ‚ąĎ‚āĖ\|‚ąá‚āô(ő≤‚čÜ)‚āĖ\|` (gradient strong monotonicity: ŌÉ monotone ‚áí logistic part ‚Č• 0, penalty gives `2őĽ¬∑id`). |
| `regLogisticGrad_coord_isBigOp` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | Each coordinate of the empirical gradient at `ő≤‚čÜ` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})` ‚ÄĒ a centered i.i.d. mean (population mean `0` by `IsPopulationRegLogistic`), via `sampleMean_sub_isBigOp`. |
| `regLogisticCoef_isBigOp` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | The coefficient error `‚ÄĖő≤Őā‚āô‚ąíő≤‚čÜ‚ÄĖ` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})`: the basic inequality times the centered gradient. |
| `regLogistic_achievesL2Rate` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | **Headline.** The fitted probability `ŌÉ(‚ü®ő≤Őā‚āô,ŌÜ‚ü©)` attains the root-n L¬≤ rate toward `ŌÉ(‚ü®ő≤‚čÜ,ŌÜ‚ü©)`, via the `1/4`-Lipschitz `ŌÉ` and `eLpNorm_predictor_sub_le`. |

### 15e. `ML/Tree/`, `ML/NeuralNet/`, `ML/Kernel/` ‚ÄĒ trees, networks, kernels (Phase 3)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `FinitePartitionPredictor`, `eval`, `eval_eqOn_cell` | `Tree/FinitePartitionPredictor.lean` | Piecewise-constant tree predictor; constant on each cell. |
| `RandomForest`, `eval`, `eval_mem_Icc` | `Tree/Forest.lean` | Forest = average of trees; averaging preserves pointwise `[a,b]` bounds. |
| `DenseLayer`, `Activation`, `layerMap`, `evalLayers`, `evalLayers_append`, `evalLayers_lipschitz` | `NeuralNet/Layer.lean`, `NeuralNet/FeedForward.lean` | Feedforward composition class; evaluation respects concatenation; Lipschitz with the product of per-layer constants. (No universal approximation / training.) |
| `IsRKHS` | `Kernel/RKHS.lean` | Abstract reproducing-kernel Hilbert space interface (`f x = ‚ü™f, k‚āď‚üę`); Mathlib has no RKHS hierarchy. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_innerBall_le`, `rkhs_ball_empiricalRademacher_le` | `Kernel/RKHSRademacher.lean` | **Intrinsic RKHS-ball Rademacher rate.** The empirical complexity of a Hilbert norm-ball linear class, and hence of an RKHS ball with bounded representers, is bounded by `őļ¬∑r/‚ąön`. |
| `gram`, `IsPSDkernel`, `gram_add_lambda_posDef` | `Kernel/Gram.lean` | Kernel Gram matrix; `K + őĽI ‚ČĽ 0` for `őĽ > 0`. |
| `krrRisk`, `representer_theorem` | `Kernel/Ridge.lean` | P2: any kernel-ridge minimizer lies in the span of the sample representers (orthogonal-decomposition proof). |
| `rademacherComplexity_l2_ball_le` | `Kernel/Rate.lean` | **Rademacher rate.** Over `L¬≤`-bounded features, the `W`-ball linear class (dual/RKHS-ball view of kernel ridge) has `rademacherComplexity ‚Č§ Xb¬∑W/‚ąön`. |
| `kernel_erm_excess_rate` | `Kernel/Rate.lean` | **Kernel ERM excess-risk rate (linear functional).** Instantiates the separable ERM oracle inequality for the closed `W`-ball of linear predictors on the closed `Xb`-ball feature domain, giving a tail bound at threshold `4¬∑Xb¬∑W/‚ąön + 2őĶ`. Loss is the bare linear functional `‚ü™w,x‚ü©`. |
| `clampedSq`, `clampedSq_eq_sq`, `clampedSq_le_sq`, `lipschitzAt0_clampedSq`, `continuous_clampedSq` | `Surrogate/ClampedSquare.lean` | Globally `2c`-Lipschitz surrogate `(clamp_{[-c,c]} t)¬≤` for `t ‚Ü¶ t¬≤`, agreeing with the square on `[-c,c]`; the Lipschitz function fed to the contraction principle so the (non-Lipschitz) squared loss can be pulled out of the Rademacher complexity on a bounded prediction range. |
| `KFeat`, `KWeight`, `kernel_erm_squaredLoss_excess_rate` | `Kernel/SquaredLoss.lean` | **Kernel ERM squared-loss excess-risk rate.** Genuine regression loss `(‚ü™w,x‚ü©‚ąíy)¬≤` (responses in `[‚ąíYb,Yb]`). RC of the centered squared-loss class is bounded by decomposing `‚ü™w,x‚ü©¬≤‚ąí2y‚ü™w,x‚ü©`: quadratic part via the infinite-index Ledoux‚ÄďTalagrand contraction with the clamped square, cross part `2y‚ü™w,x‚ü©=‚ü™w,2y¬∑x‚ü©` as a linear class over rescaled features. Tail bound at threshold `4¬∑(4(XbW)¬≤+2¬∑Yb¬∑Xb¬∑W)/‚ąön + 2őĶ`. |

### 15f. `ML/Causal/` ‚ÄĒ ML learners as causal nuisance estimators (causal-coupling corner)

The **only** ML files permitted to import the causal layers (`PO`/`Estimation`); the
`Causalean.ML` core stays causal-free. Connects the ML population targets to the
AIPW/DML nuisances via Mathlib `condExp`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `covarSigma`, `covarSigma_le`, `isL2Projection_of_condExp`, `condExp_of_isL2Projection` | `Causal/RegressionBridge.lean` | The conditional mean `E[Y‚ą£X]` (Mathlib `condExp`) **is** an `IsL2Projection`, and conversely ‚ÄĒ full equivalence linking the ML squared-loss keystone to the regression-function nuisance. |
| `mlOutcomeRegression_ae_eq`, `mlPropensity_ae_eq`, `mlNuisanceVec` | `Causal/Nuisance.lean` | The squared-loss ML target on arm `d` is the outcome regression `E[Y‚ą£X,D=d]`; on the treatment indicator it is the propensity `P(D=1‚ą£X)`; `mlNuisanceVec` packages two ML targets into `Estimation.ATE.NuisanceVec`. |
| `aipw_mlNuisance_meanZero_of_wellSpecified` | `Causal/AIPWExample.lean` | End-to-end: well-specified ML nuisances make the AIPW estimating equation mean-zero at the ATE `őł‚āÄ` (composes `mlNuisanceVec` with `Estimation.ATE.aipw_mean_zero`). |
| `AchievesL2Rate`, `isLittleOp_one_of_le_one`, `isLittleOp_mul_quarter`, `isLittleOp_quarter_of_isBigOp_sqrt` | `Core/Rate.lean` | Learning-rate abstraction (estimator attains L¬≤-rate toward its population target) + generic rate algebra: per-nuisance `o_p(n^{-1/4})` rates give DML's individual `o_p(1)` and product `o_p(n^{-1/2})` conditions; a root-n `O_p(n^{-1/2})` rate is `o_p(n^{-1/4})`. (Moved here from `Causal/DMLRate`; causal-free common currency between method rate-proofs and the `Causal` assembly.) |

Note: `ML/Core/PopulationTarget.IsL2Projection` quantifies over **measurable** test functions
(required for the `condExp` bridge); the keystone threads this measurability.

Deferred follow-ups: complexity/generalization bounds, sieve approximation theory, raw-kernel‚ÜíRKHS
construction, NN universal approximation, RF consistency; and (causal-ML trajectory) an end-to-end
AIPW-ATE example with ML nuisances, then the fast-rate layer discharging DML's `o(n^{-1/4})`
nuisance-rate condition.
