## 16. `Mathlib/Probability/DarmoisSkitovich.lean` ‚ÄĒ Bernstein's theorem (finite-variance Darmois‚ÄďSkitovich)

Mathlib-staging probability result: the analytic engine behind linear non-Gaussian
causal discovery (LiNGAM, Shimizu et al. 2006). Proves that non-Gaussianity of
disturbances breaks the rotational symmetry the Gaussian case suffers from. Built
entirely on Mathlib's `charFun` infrastructure (`contDiff_charFun`,
`iIndepFun.charFun_map_sum_eq_prod`, `iteratedDeriv_charFun_zero`,
`Measure.ext_of_charFun`, `charFun_gaussianReal`); none of this result is in Mathlib.
Sorry-free; axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` only.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bernstein_charFun_funeq` | `f(u+v)¬∑g(u‚ąív) = f u¬∑g u¬∑(f v¬∑g(‚ąív))` for `f = charFun (P.map X)`, `g = charFun (P.map Y)` | Functional equation: joint characteristic function of `(X,Y)` computed two ways, using `X ‚üā Y` and `(X+Y) ‚üā (X‚ąíY)`. Plumbing. **Proved** |
| `charFun_eventually_ne_zero` | `‚ąÄŠ∂† t in ūĚďĚ 0, charFun őľ t ‚Č† 0` (probability `őľ` on `‚ĄĚ`) | `charFun` is continuous and `1` at `0`, so nonzero near `0`; this is what makes the logarithmic derivative `(charFun őľ)'/charFun őľ` well defined. **Proved** |
| `charFun_contDiff_two` | `ContDiff ‚ĄĚ 2 (charFun (P.map Z))` given `MemLp Z 2 P` | `C¬≤`-regularity of the characteristic function under a finite second moment, via `contDiff_charFun`. **Proved** |
| `gaussianForm_of_funeq` | functional equation + `C¬≤` + value `1` at `0` ‚üĻ `‚ąÉ c`, locally `f t = exp(f'(0)¬∑t + c¬∑t¬≤/2)` and `g t = exp(g'(0)¬∑t + c¬∑t¬≤/2)` (shared `c`) | The hard pure-analysis core (no probability). Log-derivative argument with no `Complex.log`: differentiate the functional equation once in each variable to force the second logarithmic derivative constant, then solve the resulting ODE. **Proved** |
| `bernstein_charFun_gaussian_nhds_zero` | `‚ąÉ mf mg ŌÉ¬≤ ‚Č• 0`, locally `charFun (P.map X) t = exp(mf¬∑t¬∑I ‚ąí ŌÉ¬≤¬∑t¬≤/2)` and likewise `Y` with shared `ŌÉ¬≤` | Wires the analytic core to characteristic-function facts: identifies the constants as moments (`mf = E[X]`, `ŌÉ¬≤ = Var X`) via `iteratedDeriv_charFun_zero`; the shared `ŌÉ¬≤` (= `Var X = Var Y`) feeds the doubling bootstrap. **Proved** |
| `bernstein` | `X ‚üā Y`, `MemLp ¬∑ 2`, `(X+Y) ‚üā (X‚ąíY)` ‚üĻ `IsGaussian (P.map X) ‚ąß IsGaussian (P.map Y)` | **Bernstein's theorem** (smallest non-degenerate Darmois‚ÄďSkitovich instance; the 2-variable LiNGAM identification core). Doubling bootstrap `f(2t) = f(t)¬≤¬∑g(t)¬∑g(‚ąít)` propagates the local Gaussian form to all of `‚ĄĚ`, then `Measure.ext_of_charFun` against `gaussianReal`. **Proved** |

## 17. `Discovery/LiNGAM/` ‚ÄĒ Linear Non-Gaussian Acyclic Model (causal discovery)

Formalization of Shimizu, Hoyer, Hyv√§rinen & Kerminen (JMLR 2006). The non-Gaussianity
engine used here lives in `Mathlib/Probability/DarmoisSkitovich.lean` (¬ß16), and the
proved headline theorem uses the kurtosis route rather than the abandoned general
Darmois-Skitovich attempt.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `perm_uniqueness` (`PermutationUniqueness.lean`) | for lower-triangular `M` with non-zero diagonal and permutations `ŌÉ, ŌĄ`, `(‚ąÄ i, M (ŌÉ i) (ŌĄ i) ‚Č† 0) ‚ÜĒ ŌÉ = ŌĄ` | **Appendix A, Lemma 1.** Uniqueness of the zero-free-diagonal row permutation; this is what pins the disturbance‚ÜĒvariable correspondence (resolving ICA's column-permutation indeterminacy). Slick proof: nonzero diagonal + lower-triangularity ‚üĻ `ŌĄ i ‚Č§ ŌÉ i` ‚ąÄi, and equal permutation index-sums force equality. **Proved** |
| `lingam_reduction` (`Identifiability.lean`) | for unit-diagonal `C, C'` with `C` lower-triangular in order `ŌÉ`, if `C' i j = d i ¬∑ C (ŌĄ i) j` then `C = C'` | **Structural identifiability core (ICA ‚Üí matrix).** Given the ICA gen-permutation relation between the `I ‚ąí B` matrices, acyclicity + unit-diagonal normalization force equality. Reuses the Appendix-A sum argument. **Proved** |
| `lingam_identifiable` (`Identifiability.lean`) | for `B, B'` with zero diagonal, `B` acyclic in order `ŌÉ`, if `(1 ‚ąí B') i j = d i ¬∑ (1 ‚ąí B) (ŌĄ i) j` then `B = B'` | **LiNGAM coefficient identifiability (modulo ICA).** `B`-level packaging of `lingam_reduction` for `C = I ‚ąí B`. **Proved** |
| `affine_of_continuous_of_second_diff_zero` (`Mathlib/Analysis/FrechetFunctionalEquation.lean`) | continuous `f : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí E` with `f (x+s+s) + f x = f (x+s) + f (x+s)` ‚üĻ `‚ąÉ a b, f x = a + x ‚ÄĘ b` | **Fr√©chet functional equation, degree one.** A standalone finite-difference helper: midpoint-affine ‚üĻ additive ‚üĻ `‚ĄĚ`-linear (`AddMonoidHom.toRealLinearMap`). **Proved** |
| `genPerm_of_invertible_of_colSupport` (`GenPerm.lean`) | invertible `W` with ‚Č§1 non-zero entry per column ‚üĻ `‚ąÉ ŌĄ d, (d i ‚Č† 0) ‚ąß W i j = if j = ŌĄ i then d i else 0` | Generalized-permutation extraction: invertibility upgrades "‚Č§1 per column" to "exactly one per row". **Proved** |
| `kurt` / `cross_fourth_cumulant_eq_sum` / `colSupport_of_kurtosis` (`Kurtosis.lean`) | excess kurtosis `őļ‚āĄ`; `cum(yŠĶĘ,yŠĶĘ,y‚āĖ,y‚āĖ) = ő£‚Īľ WŠĶĘ‚Īľ¬≤W‚āĖ‚Īľ¬≤őļ‚āĄ(e‚Īľ)`; and: independent centered finite-4th-moment sources with non-zero same-sign kurtosis + `yŠĶĘ ‚üā y‚āĖ` ‚üĻ `WŠĶĘ‚Īľ¬∑W‚āĖ‚Īľ = 0` | **Marcinkiewicz-free column support.** The 4th-cumulant route to the `genPerm` input, avoiding Darmois‚ÄďSkitovich entirely. Identity proved by the full Isserlis 4-index expansion under independence; column support by sum-of-same-sign-is-zero. Sorry-free, axiom-clean. **Proved** |
| `ica_genPerm_relation` / `lingam_identifiability_kurtosis` (`LiNGAMKurtosis.lean`) | column support + invertibility ‚üĻ gen-permutation relation; and the headline: invertible acyclic unit-diagonal mixing + independent centered finite-4th-moment same-sign-kurtosis disturbances + equal observed law ‚üĻ `A‚ĀĽ¬Ļ = A'‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` (`B = B'`) | **Complete, fully sorry-free LiNGAM identification theorem (kurtosis route).** Chains `colSupport_of_kurtosis` ‚Üí `ica_genPerm_relation` ‚Üí `lingam_reduction`; no Darmois‚ÄďSkitovich, no Marcinkiewicz, no Fr√©chet ladder. `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. **Proved** |

## 17b. `Discovery/LinearDisentanglement/` ‚ÄĒ Linear causal disentanglement via interventions (Squires‚ÄďSeigal‚ÄďBhate‚ÄďUhler 2023)

Formalization of Squires, Seigal, Bhate & Uhler, *Linear Causal Disentanglement via
Interventions* (ICML 2023, `arXiv:2211.16467`). Latent variables `Z` follow a linear SEM
`B‚āĖZ = őĶ` per context, observed only through a full-rank mixing `X = GZ`; a single perfect
intervention per latent node identifies the latent DAG and parameters **up to `S(ūĚíĘ)`**
(order-preserving relabelings). This is the interventional/second-moment counterpart of
LiNGAM: it **reuses** LiNGAM's structural pinning (`lingam_reduction` / `perm_uniqueness`)
but replaces the non-Gaussianity engine with interventions. Taken at the **algebraic
(matrix-level)** view of the paper's Theorem 2: a model is the tuple `(H, {B‚āĖ}, {i‚āĖ}, {őĽ‚āĖ})`
and identifiability compares two tuples producing the same precision matrices `{őė‚āĖ}` (no
Moore‚ÄďPenrose pseudoinverse needed). Currently a **complete compiling scaffold** with the
algebraic backbone proven and the deep linear-algebra steps staged (see status column).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Solution` / `permMat` / `Theta0` / `Theta` / `InSG` (`Model.lean`) | model tuple (full-row-rank `H`, edge relation with `j‚Üíi ‚üĻ i<j`, upper-triangular positive-diagonal `B‚āÄ` with support = edges, perfect single-node interventions `B‚āĖ=B‚āÄ+e_{i‚āĖ}c‚āĖŠĶÄ`, `c‚āĖ=őĽ‚āĖe_{i‚āĖ}‚ąíB‚āÄŠĶÄe_{i‚āĖ}`); `őė‚āĖ=HŠĶÄB‚āĖŠĶÄB‚āĖH`; `S(ūĚíĘ)` order-preserving perms; permutation matrix `(P_ŌÉ)_{ij}=‚ü¶i=ŌÉj‚üß` | Model layer + the two `permMat` orthogonality lemmas (`permMat ŌÉ¬∑(permMat ŌÉ)ŠĶÄ=1` and transpose). **Proved (defs + orthogonality, sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `fact_transpose_mul` / `key_identity` (`KeyIdentity.lean`) | `BŠĶÄB=ő£ŠĶĘ(BŠĶÄeŠĶĘ)‚äó¬≤`; and `őė‚āĖ‚ąíőė‚āÄ=(HŠĶÄB‚āĖŠĶÄe_{i‚āĖ})‚äó¬≤‚ąí(HŠĶÄB‚āÄŠĶÄe_{i‚āĖ})‚äó¬≤` | **Algebraic backbone (their key Proposition).** A single-node intervention changes only one row of `B`, so the rank-one decompositions cancel except in the target row; the precision difference is a difference of two outer products (rank ‚Č§ 2). **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `sigma_solutions` (`SigmaSolutions.lean`) | conjugating `B‚āÄ,B‚āĖ` by `permMat ŌÉ` and permuting rows of `H` leaves every `őė‚āÄ,őė‚āĖ` unchanged | **(‚äá) direction of Theorem 2** (`prop:sigma_solutions`): each `ŌÉ‚ąąS(ūĚíĘ)` gives a solution. Pure orthogonality of `permMat ŌÉ`. **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `pa`/`Pa`/`anc`/`An`/`prec`/`preceq`, `IsPORQ`, `prec_lt`, `inner_toLp_eq_dotProduct`, `dotProduct_self_pos`, `eq_of_mul_eq_mul_row_indep`, `row_indep_of_mul_row_indep` (`PartialOrderRQ.lean`) | parents/ancestors and the partial order `i‚Čļj ‚ÜĒ j‚ąąan(i)`; **Definition 1** (partial order RQ decomposition `H=RQ`, `R` `‚™Į`-supported with `RŠĶĘŠĶĘ‚Č•0`, rows of `Q` unit-norm and orthogonal along `‚Čļ`); + the `‚¨ĚŠĶ•`‚ÜĒ`EuclideanSpace` inner-product bridge, positive-definiteness, and full-row-rank cancellation helpers | Graph order + Def 1 + reusable helpers. The helpers (bridge, cancellation, rank propagation) are **proved, axiom-clean**. **Proved (helpers)** |
| `porq_exists` / `porq_unique` (`PartialOrderRQ.lean`) | existence and uniqueness of the partial order RQ decomposition of a full-row-rank `H` | **Existence** by a bespoke partial-order Gram‚ÄďSchmidt recursion (`porqResidual`, projecting onto the ancestor-row span along `‚Čļ`, normalized via the `‚¨ĚŠĶ•`‚ÜĒ`EuclideanSpace` bridge `inner_toLp_eq_dotProduct`); **uniqueness** by downward induction pinning each `qŠĶĘ` (`Q=Q'`) then full-row-rank cancellation (`eq_of_mul_eq_mul_row_indep`) for `R=R'`. **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `rowspan_inclusion_a` / `rowspan_inclusion_b` / `rowspan_inclusion_c` (`Rowspan.lean`) | (a) `rowspan(őė‚āĖ‚ąíőė‚āÄ)‚äÜ‚ü®hŠĶĘ:i‚ąąūĚďė‚ü© ‚ÜĒ Pa(i‚āĖ)‚äÜūĚďė`; (b) `‚äÜ‚ü®qŠĶĘ:i‚ąąAn(i‚āĖ)‚ü©`; (c) `Pa(i‚āĖ)‚äĄūĚďė ‚üĻ ‚äĄ‚ü®qŠĶĘ:i‚ąąūĚďė‚ü©` (all given `őė‚āĖ‚Č†őė‚āÄ`) | **Lemma 1** linking observable precision differences to the latent graph. (a),(b),(c) **all proved sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Two faithfulness fixes the formalization forced: (a)/(c) need the paper's non-degeneracy `őė‚āĖ‚Č†őė‚āÄ` (Assumption 1(b) genericity ‚ÄĒ absent, a degenerate source intervention `őĽ‚āĖ=(B‚āÄ)_{i‚āĖi‚āĖ}` makes them false); (c) additionally needs `ūĚďė` **ancestor-closed** (the algorithm's actual usage ‚ÄĒ a "diamond" graph with non-closed `ūĚďė` is a genuine counterexample to (c) as the paper states it). Key helpers: `vec_mem_rowSpan_vecMulVec_sub` (Gram extraction), `hSpan_An_eq_qSpan_An` (inverse-PORQ support, by dimension count), `Q_row_dotProduct_H_row_eq_zero` (PORQ orthogonality). **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `disentanglement_uniqueness` (`Uniqueness.lean`) | one intervention per node (`target` **bijective**) + non-degenerate (`őė‚āĖ‚Č†őė‚āÄ`) + equal `{őė‚āĖ}` ‚üĻ the two solutions differ by a single `ŌÉ‚ąąS(ūĚíĘ)` **up to nonzero signed diagonal scaling**: `H'=diag(őľ)¬∑PŌÉ¬∑H` (őľ nonzero, any sign), `B'‚āĖ¬∑(diag őľ¬∑PŌÉ)=diag(őĹ)¬∑PŌÉ¬∑B‚āĖ` (őĹ=¬Ī1), targets `i'‚āĖ=ŌÉ i‚āĖ` | **(‚äÜ) direction of Theorem 2.** The clean orthogonal-matrix route: change-of-basis recovery `H'=MH` (L1), Gram congruence (L2), `gram_to_orthogonal` (L3), the **sum trick** `MŠĶÄ¬∑diag(d')¬∑M=diag(d)` ‚Üí orthogonal `O`, the row-collapse induction ‚Üí `M` signed monomial, signed-Cholesky read-off ‚Üí per-context relations, `InSG ŌÉ` and target read-off. Reuses `cholesky_unique`. **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `orthogonal_upperTri_signed_diag` / `signed_cholesky` / `permMat_transpose_eq` / `permMat_conj_apply'` / `permMat_mul_diagonal` (`Uniqueness.lean`, private) | orthogonal upper-triangular matrix is a `¬Ī1` diagonal; `PŠĶÄP=BŠĶÄB` with `B` upper-tri pos-diag ‚üĻ `P=diag(s)¬∑B` with `s i=¬Ī1` (signed Cholesky); `(permMat ŌÉ)ŠĶÄ=permMat ŌÉ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ`; `(permMat ŌÉ)ŠĶÄ¬∑X¬∑permMat ŌÉ` relabels by `ŌÉ`; `permMat ŌÉ¬∑diag s=diag(s‚ąėŌÉ‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)¬∑permMat ŌÉ` | Reusable signed-Cholesky / permutation-conjugation helpers feeding the read-off step (F). The signed Cholesky generalizes `cholesky_unique` to a single sign-fixed factor. **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `monomial_relations_from_orthogonal_core` (`Uniqueness.lean`, private) | from the orthogonal `O = diag(‚ąöd')¬∑M¬∑diag(‚ąöd‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)` of the sum trick: `M=diag őľ¬∑permMat ŌÉ` (nonzero `őľ`) and the per-context signed relations `B'‚āĖ¬∑M=diag őĹ¬∑permMat ŌÉ¬∑B‚āĖ` with shared `őĹ=sign‚ąėőľ` | The geometric core (`prop:orthogonal-correctness`). **(D)** decreasing parent-elimination induction on `O`'s rows (via `central_rank2_eq`, the `e_c`-support argument + orthogonality) ‚üĻ row `ŌÉn` of `O` is `¬Īe‚āô`; **(E)** monomial form of `M`; **(F)** signed-Cholesky read-off (`signed_cholesky`/`bRel`) with shared sign `sign‚ąėőľ`, whose ŌÉ-triangularity input `S'.B‚āÄ(ŌÉa)(ŌÉb)=0` for `b<a` is read off directly from the `(b,a)` entry of `central_rank2_eq` (no extra induction). **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `disentanglement_identifiability` (`Identifiability.lean`) | **flagship Theorem 2 (algebraic, up to S(ūĚíĘ) and signed scaling):** one intervention per node + non-degenerate + equal `{őė‚āĖ}` ‚üĻ solutions related by a single `ŌÉ‚ąąS(ūĚíĘ)` and a nonzero signed diagonal scaling of latent directions; with `sigma_solutions` (‚äá) this characterizes the solution set | **Headline, fully proved.** Reduces to `disentanglement_uniqueness`. `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |

## 17c. `Discovery/InvariantPrediction/` ‚ÄĒ Invariant Causal Prediction (Peters‚ÄďB√ľhlmann‚ÄďMeinshausen 2016)

Formalization of the setup and soundness of Peters, B√ľhlmann & Meinshausen, *Causal inference
using invariant prediction* (JRSS-B 2016, `arXiv:1501.01332`). An **environment family**
`EnvFamily` is a `Fintype`-indexed family of generalized SCMs `{M i}` over common observed/latent
node sets, sharing the target's parent set (E4 `hParents`), its structural mechanism (E2 `hStruct`,
as `HEq`), the latent-noise law (E3 `hLatent`, as `HEq`), and carrying an **exogeneity** field
`hExo` (the target's latent parents are independent of its observed parents under each environment's
joint law ‚ÄĒ the SEM assumption `őĶŠĶČ ‚ä• XŠĶČ_{S*}` that rules out hidden confounding into `Y`).
A predictor set `S` is **invariant** when the conditional law of the target given `X_S` is one fixed
kernel across all environments (an a.e. statement w.r.t. each environment's predictor marginal).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `EnvFamily` / `paObs` / `paLat` / `Invariant` (`Model.lean`, `Invariance.lean`, `MechanismFactor.lean`) | environment-family structure with shared parents/mechanism/latent law + exogeneity `hExo`; observed/latent parents of the target; the invariance predicate (one shared conditional kernel a.e. per environment) | Model + grammar layer. **Defs** |
| `mechanism_invariant` (`Invariance.lean`) | the target's observed parents `paObs` form an invariant set: in every environment the conditional law of the target given its observed parents equals one fixed structural factor | **Headline (the engine of soundness), proved.** Witness = law of `structFun Y` driven by the latent-parent noise (reference environment); per-environment disintegration via exogeneity (`hExo`), cross-environment agreement via shared E2/E3/E4. `#print axioms` = `[propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, where `sorryAx` is **only** the isolated `s_eq_on_fixed_parents` regularity gap below. **Proved (modulo one regularity gap)** |
| `icp_sound` / `idSet` / `invariantSets` (`Soundness.lean`, `IdentifiedSet.lean`) | **Theorem 1 (soundness):** the identified set `S(E) = ‚čā` (invariant sets) is contained in the target's observed parents ‚ÄĒ ICP never selects a non-cause | Immediate from `mechanism_invariant`. Same axiom profile (reduces to `s_eq_on_fixed_parents`). **Proved (modulo the same gap)** |
| `mechanismFun` / `condDistrib_target_eq_mechanismKernel` / `mechanismKernel_env_eq` / `jointKernel_map_paLat_eq_latentProduct_map` / `structFun_yNode_apply_eq` / `latentProduct_heq` / `map_heq_transport` (`MechanismFactor.lean`) | the structural mechanism as a measurable map of observed+latent parents; per-environment factorization of the target conditional through the mechanism kernel; cross-environment equality of that kernel; latent-parent marginal = latent-product marginal; cross-environment `structFun Y` agreement; HEq transport of latent product measures / push-forwards | Supporting machinery for `mechanism_invariant` (Steps A/B/C). **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `s_eq_on_fixed_parents` (`MechanismFactor.lean`) | environments assign the same value to any **fixed** (intervened) parent of the target | **The one residual `sorry`.** Holds vacuously when the target has no fixed parents; not derivable from the bare `EnvFamily` fields (the `EnvFamily` shares the parent set and forbids changing it, but does not pin the intervention *value* on a fixed parent), so it is isolated here as the structural regularity gap (ICP's "no intervention on a direct cause of `Y` with a shifting value"). **`sorry` (isolated gap)** |
| `parent_omitted_not_invariant` / `icp_complete` / `RichEnv` (`Completeness.lean`) | **Theorem 2 (completeness):** under richness/faithfulness primitives `S(E)` equals the parents | Completeness direction; the hard propagation lemma `parent_omitted_not_invariant` is a pre-existing escalated `sorry` (unchanged). **Partial (`sorry`)** |

### `LinearGaussian/` ‚ÄĒ completeness in the linear-Gaussian model (`prop:1`(i), sorry-free)

A self-contained linear-Gaussian sub-development (random-variable encoding, NOT built on the
nonparametric SWIG/kernel layer above) that proves the **converse** `S(E) ‚äá PA(Y)` ‚ÄĒ the only setting
in which the paper establishes it ‚ÄĒ via the do-intervention **mean-shift** argument. The observational
SEM `ObsSEM` carries the coefficient matrix `ő≤`, an acyclic `DAG` (`edge k j ‚ÜĒ ő≤‚Īľ‚āĖ ‚Č† 0`), Gaussian
noises `őĶ‚Īľ ~ N(0,ŌÉ‚Īľ¬≤)`, and the paper's **Assumption-1 exogeneity** `hYexo` (`őĶ‚āÄ ‚ä• X‚āĖ` for each parent
`k ‚ąą PA(Y)`); `Env` is a single do-intervention environment on the same probability space (with its own
`hExo`); `EnvFamily` bundles the observational SEM with finitely many environments. The regression layer
(`Regression.lean`) defines the residual `R = Y ‚ąí ő£ ő≥‚āĖ X‚āĖ`, the regression-invariance null `H_{0,S}`
(`InvarianceNull`: a shared coefficient `ő≥` and residual law `FőĶ`, residual ‚ä• predictors and same law
across environments), and the identified set `S(E) = ‚čā{S : H_{0,S}}`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ObsSEM` / `Env` / `EnvFamily` / `InvarianceNull` / `identifiedSet` (`Model.lean`, `Regression.lean`) | observational linear-Gaussian SEM with exogeneity field `hYexo`, do-intervention environment with `hExo`, environment family; the regression residual, invariance null `H_{0,S}`, and identified set `S(E)` | Model + observable layer. **Defs** |
| `eps_integrable` / `eps_integral_zero` (`Helpers/Moments.lean`) | each noise `őĶ‚Īľ` is integrable with `E[őĶ‚Īľ] = 0` (Gaussian first moment) | Moment helpers. **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `obsResidual_eq_eps` / `envResidual_eq_eps` (`Helpers/Residual.lean`) | with the causal coefficient `ő≥* = ő≤‚āÄ,¬∑`, the residual equals the target noise `őĶ‚āÄ` a.e. in both the observational and every interventional environment | Algebraic helpers (target never intervened, `ő≤‚āÄ‚āÄ = 0`). **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `nonDescendant_invariance` (`Helpers/Invariance.lean`) | under the single do-intervention `do(X_{k‚āÄ}=a)`, every coordinate that is neither `k‚āÄ` nor a descendant of `k‚āÄ` keeps its observational value a.e. | Structural backbone of the mean-shift (strong induction on topological order). **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `icp_sound_linearGaussian` (`Completeness.lean`) | **Soundness:** `S(E) ‚äÜ PA(Y)` ‚ÄĒ `PA(Y)` satisfies the invariance null with `ő≥* = ő≤‚āÄ,¬∑` and residual law `N(0,ŌÉ‚āÄ¬≤)` (residual `=ŠĶź őĶ‚āÄ`, exogeneity gives independence, `hGauss` gives the shared law) | Reuses the exogeneity fields `hYexo`/`hExo`. **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `exists_youngest_nonzero` (`Completeness.lean`) | among a nonempty index set there is a youngest one (largest topological order), no directed path to any other element | Youngest-node selection. **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `residual_mean_shift_of_doIntervention` (`Completeness.lean`) | under `do(X_{k‚āÄ}=a)` with `a ‚Č† E[X¬Ļ_{k‚āÄ}]`, `őĪ_{k‚āÄ} ‚Č† 0`, and the youngest-node property `hyoung`, the interventional and observational residuals have different means (gap `őĪ_{k‚āÄ}¬∑(a ‚ąí E[X¬Ļ_{k‚āÄ}])`), so they are not `IdentDistrib` | The eq:help1/help2 mean-shift; `hyoung` kills all non-`k‚āÄ` terms. **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `icp_complete_linearGaussian` (`Completeness.lean`) | **Theorem `prop:1`(i) (completeness):** with shifted single do-interventions on every predictor, `S(E) = PA(Y)` | Combines soundness with the mean-shift: a missing parent forces a youngest support index whose intervention breaks the shared residual law. `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. **Proved (sorry-free)** |

## 18. `Experimentation/` ‚ÄĒ design-based (randomization) inference under interference

The experimentation cluster: a shared, paper-agnostic **design-based substrate**
(`Experimentation/DesignBased/`, namespace `Causalean.Experimentation.DesignBased`) plus one
folder per formalized paper that consumes it (`Experimentation/ExposureMappingInterference/` and
`Experimentation/TwoStageInterference/`, namespaces `Causalean.Experimentation.{ExposureMappingInterference,TwoStageInterference}`).
The substrate is the finite-population,
fixed-potential-outcome flavor of the potential-outcomes framework ‚ÄĒ probability comes from the
experimenter's randomization over a *finite* assignment space `ő©` (a sibling of the
measure-theoretic superpopulation `PO/`, which it never imports), with a deliberately lightweight
finite-sum layer (`E X = ‚ąĎ z, p z ¬∑ X z`) so all algebraic identities are `Finset` algebra.
Further experimentation papers (two-stage experiments, ‚Ä¶) slot in as sibling folders under
`Experimentation/`, reusing `DesignBased`. First paper: Aronow & Samii (2017, AOAS), "Estimating
Average Causal Effects Under General Interference" (arXiv:1305.6156; plan `doc/aronow_samii_plan.md`).
Second paper: Hudgens & Halloran (2008, JASA), "Toward Causal Inference With Interference" ‚ÄĒ the
two-stage / partial-interference estimand zoo (direct/indirect/total/overall effects), which forced
the reusable `Product`/`TwoStage` substrate combinators (plan `doc/hudgens_halloran_plan.md`).

**Main results** (the paper's named theorems; flagged as headlines in
`doc/library_review/Experimentation.json` ‚ÄĒ everything else is supporting substrate):
`Experimentation.DesignBased.E_htEffect` (Lemma 4.1, HT effect unbiasedness ‚ÄĒ substrate),
`Experimentation.DesignBased.Var_htTotal` (Prop 4.4, HT variance ‚ÄĒ substrate),
`Experimentation.ExposureMappingInterference.htMean_consistent_of_var` (Prop 6.4, consistency),
`‚Ä¶ExposureMappingInterference.wald_coverage_of_conditions` (Prop 6.5, oracle-variance Wald coverage from
primitive conditions), `‚Ä¶ExposureMappingInterference.wald_coverage_feasible_of_conditions` (Prop 6.5/6.6, the
feasible estimated-VŐā interval ‚ÄĒ the flagship), and
`‚Ä¶ExposureMappingInterference.var_NsqVhat_tendsto_zero_of_conditions` (Prop 6.6, variance-estimator consistency).
Hudgens‚ÄďHalloran main results: `‚Ä¶TwoStageInterference.E_estDirect` / `E_estIndirect` / `E_estTotal`
(Theorems 1‚Äď3, unbiasedness of the direct/indirect/total effect estimators), `‚Ä¶TwoStageInterference.CE_total_decomp`
(total = direct + indirect), `‚Ä¶TwoStageInterference.Var_tauHat` (Theorem 5, the within-group Neyman variance), and
`‚Ä¶TwoStageInterference.Var_popEst` (Theorem 4, the two-stage variance decomposition), and
`‚Ä¶TwoStageInterference.Var_estDirect` (Theorem 6, the direct-effect-estimator variance).
The substrate (`DesignBased/`: finite-sum `E`/`Var`/`Cov`, exposure, HT, Chebyshev, product/compound
designs, the abstract `var_edge_sum_le`, normal-CDF facts, the measure bridge) is reusable
architecture, not main results.

### `Experimentation/DesignBased/Design.lean` ‚ÄĒ finite randomization design + `E`/`Var`/`Cov` algebra

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `FiniteDesign` | structure: `p : ő© ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ`, `p_nonneg`, `p_sum : ‚ąĎ p = 1` | A randomization design = a probability mass function on a finite assignment space `ő©`. |
| `FiniteDesign.E` / `Var` / `Cov` | `E X = ‚ąĎ z, p z ¬∑ X z`; `Var X = E[(X‚ąíE X)¬≤]`; `Cov X Y = E[(X‚ąíE X)(Y‚ąíE Y)]` | Finite-sum expectation, variance, covariance of real random variables under the design. |
| `ind` / `Pr` | `ind A = fun z => if A z then 1 else 0`; `Pr A = E (ind A)` | Indicator of an event and its design probability. |
| `E_const`/`E_add`/`E_sub`/`E_const_mul`/`E_mul_const`/`E_neg`/`E_sum`/`E_congr` | linearity of `E` | Expectation is linear and respects finite sums and pointwise equality. |
| `Var_eq` / `Cov_eq` | `Var X = E[X¬≤] ‚ąí (E X)¬≤`; `Cov X Y = E[X¬∑Y] ‚ąí E X ¬∑ E Y` | The computational forms of variance and covariance. |
| `Cov_self` / `Cov_comm` / `Var_congr` / `Cov_congr` | `Cov X X = Var X`; symmetry; congruence | Basic covariance/variance identities. |
| `Cov_const_mul_left/right` / `Cov_sub_left/right` / `Cov_sum_left/right` | bilinearity of `Cov` | Covariance is bilinear over scalar multiples, differences, and finite sums. |
| `Cov_linear_comb` / `Var_linear_comb` | `Cov (‚ąĎ cŠĶĘXŠĶĘ) (‚ąĎ e‚ĪľY‚Īľ) = ‚ąĎ‚ąĎ cŠĶĘe‚Īľ Cov(XŠĶĘ,Y‚Īľ)`; variance specialization | Covariance/variance of finite linear combinations as double sums ‚ÄĒ the workhorse for HT variance. |
| `Var_sub` / `Var_const_mul` | `Var(X‚ąíY)=Var X+Var Y‚ąí2Cov(X,Y)`; `Var(c¬∑X)=c¬≤¬∑Var X` | Variance of a difference and under scaling. |
| `E_ind` / `ind_sq` / `Var_ind` | `E(ind A)=Pr A`; `(ind A)¬≤=ind A`; `Var(ind A)=Pr A¬∑(1‚ąíPr A)` | Indicator expectation/idempotence/variance. |
| `E_nonneg` / `E_le_one` / `ind_nonneg` / `ind_le_one` / `Pr_nonneg` / `Pr_le_one` | `[0,1]` bounds | Elementary bounds: expectations of `[0,1]`-valued variables and probabilities lie in `[0,1]`. |

### `Experimentation/DesignBased/Exposure.lean` ‚ÄĒ exposure mappings and generalized probability of exposure

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `expo` / `expoInd` | `expo f őł i z = f z (őł i)`; `expoInd f őł i d = ind (expo i = d)` | The exposure unit `i` receives under assignment `z`, and its `{0,1}` indicator. |
| `prop` / `propPairSame` / `propPairCross` | `ŌÄ_i(d)=Pr(expo i=d)`; `ŌÄ_{ij}(d)=E[1ŠĶĘ1‚Īľ]`; `ŌÄ_{ij}(d,d')=E[1ŠĶĘ(d)1‚Īľ(d')]` | Generalized probability of exposure and joint exposure probabilities. |
| `E_expoInd` / `sum_prop_eq_one` | `E(expoInd i d)=ŌÄ_i(d)`; `‚ąĎ_d ŌÄ_i(d)=1` | Propensity as an expectation; propensities sum to one over exposures. |
| `Cov_expoInd_same` / `Cov_expoInd_cross` | `Cov(1ŠĶĘ(d),1‚Īľ(d))=ŌÄ_{ij}(d)‚ąíŌÄ_iŌÄ_j` (and cross) | Covariance of exposure indicators in terms of joint/marginal propensities. |
| `expoInd_mul_self_of_ne` / `propPairCross_self_of_ne` | for `d‚Č†d'`: `1ŠĶĘ(d)¬∑1ŠĶĘ(d')=0`; `ŌÄ_{ii}(d,d')=0` | A unit cannot occupy two distinct exposures at once. |

### `Experimentation/DesignBased/PotentialOutcome.lean` ‚ÄĒ properly-specified mapping (Cond 1) and consistency (Cond 2)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ProperlySpecified` | `‚ąÄ i z, yr i z = y i (expo i z)` | Condition 1: interference acts only through the exposure (potential outcome factors through it). |
| `Yobs` | `Yobs y f őł i z = y i (expo i z)` | Observed outcome of unit `i` under assignment `z`. |
| `Yobs_eq_sum` | `Yobs i z = ‚ąĎ_d 1ŠĶĘ(d)¬∑y i d` | Condition 2 (consistency), as a derived lemma. |
| `expoInd_mul_Yobs` / `expoInd_mul_Yobs_sq` / `expoInd‚āā_mul_Yobs` | on-event substitutions `1¬∑Yobs = 1¬∑y` (and squared / two-indicator forms) | The exposure indicator forces the observed outcome to its potential-outcome value. |

### `Experimentation/DesignBased/HT/{Estimator,Unbiased,Variance}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Horvitz‚ÄďThompson estimators

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `htTotal` / `htMean` / `htEffect` | `Ň∑ŠĶÄ(d)=‚ąĎŠĶĘ 1ŠĶĘ(d)YŠĶĘ/ŌÄ_i(d)`; `őľŐā=Ň∑ŠĶÄ/N`; `ŌĄŐā=őľŐā(dk)‚ąíőľŐā(dl)` | Inverse-probability-weighted total, mean, and effect estimators. |
| `muTrue` / `tauTrue` / `htTotal_eq` | `őľ(d)=(1/N)‚ąĎ y i d`; `ŌĄ=őľ(dk)‚ąíőľ(dl)`; HT total in `y i d` form | Estimands and the indicator-forced rewrite of the estimator. |
| `E_htTotal` / `E_htMean` / `E_htEffect` | unbiasedness (Lemma 4.1, Prop 4.4) | The HT total/mean/effect estimators are unbiased under positive propensities. **Proved.** |
| `Var_htTotal_cov` / `Var_htTotal` | variance as double-sum-of-covariances and expanded form `eq:total_variance` | Randomization variance of `Ň∑ŠĶÄ(d)`: `‚ąĎ ŌÄ_i(1‚ąíŌÄ_i)(y/ŌÄ)¬≤ + ‚ąĎ_{i‚Č†j}(ŌÄ_{ij}‚ąíŌÄ_iŌÄ_j)(y/ŌÄ)(y/ŌÄ)`. **Proved.** |
| `Cov_htTotal_cov` / `Cov_htTotal` | covariance forms `eq:totals_covariance` | `Cov[Ň∑ŠĶÄ(dk),Ň∑ŠĶÄ(dl)]`; the diagonal yields the famously unidentified `‚ąí‚ąĎŠĶĘ yŠĶĘ(dk)yŠĶĘ(dl)` term (needs `dk‚Č†dl`). **Proved.** |

### `Experimentation/ExposureMappingInterference/Variance/Conservative.lean` ‚ÄĒ conservative variance estimators (¬ß5, positive-joint + zero-pairwise)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `htVarEst` / `htCovEst` / `htEffectVarEst` | the HT variance/covariance/effect-variance estimators | Design-based estimators of `Var[Ň∑ŠĶÄ]`, `Cov[Ň∑ŠĶÄ,Ň∑ŠĶÄ]`, `Var[ŌĄŐā]` (positive-joint regime). |
| `E_htVarEst` | `E[VŐā[Ň∑ŠĶÄ(d)]] = Var[Ň∑ŠĶÄ(d)]` (Lemma 5.1) | The variance estimator is exactly unbiased in the positive-joint regime. **Proved.** |
| `E_htCovEst_le` | `E[ńąov] ‚Č§ Cov` (Prop 5.4) | The covariance estimator is nonpositively biased (Young's inequality on the diagonal). **Proved.** |
| `E_htEffectVarEst_ge` | `Var[ŌĄŐā] ‚Č§ E[VŐā[ŌĄŐā]]` (Prop 5.7) | The assembled effect-variance estimator is conservative (nonnegative bias). **Proved.** |
| `E_htVarEst_eq_addBias` | `E[VŐā[Ň∑ŠĶÄ(d)]] = Var[Ň∑ŠĶÄ(d)] + A` (Prop 5.2) | Bias of the variance estimator when some joint probabilities vanish, `A = ‚ąĎ_{ŌÄ_{ij}(d)=0} y_i(d)y_j(d)`. **Proved.** |
| `htA2` / `E_htVarEst_add_htA2_ge` | `Var[Ň∑ŠĶÄ(d)] ‚Č§ E[VŐā + √ā‚āā]` (Prop 5.3) | Young correction `√ā‚āā` over zero-joint pairs makes the variance estimator conservative without the positive-joint assumption. **Proved.** |
| `E_htCovEst_eq_of_noEffect` | `E[ńąov] = Cov` under `y_i(dk)=y_i(dl)` (Prop 5.5) | The covariance estimator is exactly unbiased when there is no effect (the Young diagonal correction is exact). **Proved.** |
| `htCovEstA` / `E_htCovEstA_le` | `E[ńąov_A] ‚Č§ Cov` (Prop 5.6) | General covariance estimator handling zero cross-joints; Young correction over all `j` with `ŌÄ_{ij}(dk,dl)=0` (incl. the diagonal `j=i`, subsuming the unidentified `‚ąí‚ąĎŠĶĘ y_i(dk)y_i(dl)` term). Nonpositively biased with no positive-cross-joint assumption. **Proved.** |
| `htEffectVarEstA` / `E_htEffectVarEstA_ge` | `Var[ŌĄŐā] ‚Č§ E[VŐā_A[ŌĄŐā]]` (general Prop 5.7) | General conservative effect-variance estimator assembling `√ā‚āā` and `ńąov_A`; nonnegative bias with no joint-positivity hypotheses. **Proved.** |

### `Experimentation/DesignBased/{Chebyshev,GaussianCDF,EdgeVarianceBound,FiniteDesignMeasure}.lean` (substrate) + `Experimentation/ExposureMappingInterference/Asymptotics/{Consistency,SteinCLT,SteinInstance,Intervals,VarianceConsistency,VarEstQuadBound,VarEstConsistencyConditions}.lean` (paper) ‚ÄĒ large-sample theory

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `FiniteDesign.chebyshev` | `Pr[|X‚ąíE X|‚Č•őĶ] ‚Č§ Var X/őĶ¬≤` | Finite Chebyshev inequality (the convergence-in-probability engine). **Proved.** |
| `Experiment` / `gdep` / `N` | sequence-of-experiments bundle; pairwise dependency indicator; population size | Substrate for asymptotics over a sequence of nested finite-population experiments. |
| `Var_htMean_le` | `Var[őľŐā(d)] ‚Č§ c¬≤(N+‚ąĎg)/N¬≤` | Variance bound under bounded weights and pairwise dependence. **Proved.** |
| `htMean_consistent_of_var` | `Var‚Üí0 ‚áí Pr[|őľŐā‚ąíőľ|‚Č•őĶ]‚Üí0` (Prop 6.4) | Consistency of the HT estimator via Chebyshev + unbiasedness. **Proved.** |
| `stdNormalCdf` / `stdNormalCdf_neg` / `monotone_stdNormalCdf` | `ő¶(t)=(ūĚí©(0,1)).real(Iic t)`; `ő¶(‚ąít)=1‚ąíő¶(t)`; monotone | Standard-normal CDF (`GaussianCDF.lean`), agrees with Mathlib `cdf`; symmetry from `gaussianReal_map_neg` + atomlessness. **Proved.** |
| `Experiment.studentizedEffect` | `(ŌĄŐā‚ąíŌĄ)/‚ąöVar[ŌĄŐā]` | The studentized HT effect statistic. |
| `LocalDependenceCLT` | interface premise: studentized statistic `‚Üíd ūĚí©(0,1)` (Chen‚ÄďShao 2004 Thm 2.7, bounded-neighborhood case) | **DISCHARGED** by `localDependenceCLT_of_stein` (`SteinInstance.lean`) via the from-scratch Stein CLT below ‚ÄĒ no longer an open gate. |
| `wald_coverage` | `1‚ąíőĪ ‚Č§ liminf` of Wald-interval coverage (Prop 6.5, "at least `1‚ąíőĪ`") | **Proved** from `LocalDependenceCLT` + nonzero variance + CDF symmetry. |
| `GaussianCDF`/`FiniteDesignMeasure`/`SteinInstance` | `stdNormalCdf`+symmetry; `D.toMeasure := ‚ąĎ p(z)¬∑őī_z` bridge (`‚ąę=E`,`.real=Pr`,`variance=Var`); `localDependenceCLT_of_stein`, `wald_coverage_of_stein` | Bridge from the lightweight design layer to the measure-theoretic Stein CLT + the discharge. `wald_coverage_of_stein` is **end-to-end** A‚ÄďS asymptotic coverage from primitive conditions (no CLT premise). **All axiom-clean.** |
| `continuous_stdNormalCdf` | `ő¶` is continuous (Gaussian atomless) | Used by the *feasible* interval (`GaussianCDF.lean`). **Proved.** |
| `wald_coverage_feasible` | `1‚ąíőĪ ‚Č§ liminf` coverage of `ŌĄŐā ¬Ī z¬∑‚ąöVŐā` (Prop 6.5, the paper's **estimated**-variance interval) | The faithful interval, from CLT + a single variance-estimator-consistency input `hVhat` (`Intervals.lean`). **Proved.** |
| `htEffectVarEst_undershoot_tendsto_zero` / `relVar_of_NsqVar_tendsto` / `wald_coverage_feasible_of_relVar` | reduce `hVhat` to `Var[VŐā]/Var¬≤‚Üí0`; bridge `Var[N¬∑VŐā]‚Üí0`+Cond4 ‚áí that; capstone | Chebyshev reduction + conservativeness `E[VŐā]‚Č•Var` isolate the variance-consistency input to one limit (`VarianceConsistency.lean`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `FiniteDesign.var_edge_sum_le` | `Var[‚ąĎŠĶĘ‚Īľ bŠĶĘ‚Īľ] ‚Č§ 8M¬≤m¬≥N` for bounded edge-functions vanishing off a degree-‚Č§`m` graph with unlinked-edge `Cov=0` | Abstract pure-FiniteDesign formalization of the appendix `O(N‚ĀĽ¬Ļ)` quadruple-sum count (`EdgeVarianceBound.lean`). Reusable. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `var_htEdgeStat_le` | `Var[Ň∑Var(dk)+Ň∑Var(dl)‚ąí2ńąov] ‚Č§ 8¬∑vbBound¬≤¬∑m¬≥¬∑N` | Per-population bound: `VŐā_raw` written as an edge-sum, then `var_edge_sum_le` under Conditions 1, 1‚Ä≤ (joint overlap `1/ŌÄ_{ij}‚Č§c‚āÉ`), 3 (`VarEstQuadBound.lean`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `var_NsqVhat_tendsto_zero_of_conditions` / `wald_coverage_feasible_of_conditions` | `N¬≤¬∑Var[VŐā]‚Üí0` from uniform Conditions 1/1‚Ä≤/3 + `N‚Üí‚ąě`; end-to-end feasible coverage `‚Č•1‚ąíőĪ` from the primitive conditions | Discharges the variance-consistency input to primitives (`VarEstConsistencyConditions.lean`). The only assumption beyond the literal Conditions 1/3/4 is the explicit joint-overlap bound `1/ŌÄ_{ij}‚Č§c‚āÉ` that the paper's appendix uses **implicitly**. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

**Faithfulness note (Prop 6.6).** The paper's appendix derivation of `Var[N¬∑VŐā]‚Üí0` treats each
variance-estimator summand `aŠĶĘ‚Īľ ‚ąĚ 1/ŌÄ_{ij}` as `O(1)`, but Condition 1 bounds only `1/ŌÄ_i`, never
the joint `1/ŌÄ_{ij}`; the bound therefore needs a joint-overlap lower bound on dependent pairs that
the literal Conditions 1/3/4 omit. We make this explicit as **Condition 1‚Ä≤** (`1/ŌÄ_{ij}(d) ‚Č§ c‚āÉ`)
and recover the paper's claim under it. See `doc/aronow_samii_varest_consistency_plan.md`.

The Chen‚ÄďShao dependency-graph CLT is built from scratch in
`Causalean/Mathlib/Probability/SteinMethod/{Solution,Bounds,DependencyCLT,CLT}.lean` (Mathlib has no
Stein's method or Wasserstein metric): the Stein equation `steinSol_hasDerivAt`, the three
Chen‚ÄďGoldstein‚ÄďShao solution bounds `‚ÄĖf_h‚ÄĖ,‚ÄĖf_h'‚ÄĖ,‚ÄĖf_h''‚ÄĖ‚Č§2L` (incl. a from-scratch Mills-ratio
bound), the local-dependence Stein bound `stein_local_dependence_bound`, and the abstract CLT
`stein_cdf_clt` (via the `clt` package's L√©vy continuity + portmanteau). Reusable / promotable.

### `Experimentation/DesignBased/{Product,TwoStage}.lean` ‚ÄĒ product & compound (two-stage) designs (substrate)

The reusable two-stage substrate that Hudgens‚ÄďHalloran (paper #2) forced. Paper-agnostic.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `prodDesign` | `(prodDesign D).p w = ‚ąŹ i, (D i).p (w i)` | Product of a finite family of designs `D i : FiniteDesign (őĪ i)` on the dependent product `‚ąÄ i, őĪ i`. Makes cross-coordinate (cross-group) independence a structural fact, not an assumption. |
| `FiniteDesign.E_prod_prod` / `E_prod_apply` | `E(‚ąŹŠĶĘ gŠĶĘ(wŠĶĘ))=‚ąŹŠĶĘ E gŠĶĘ`; `E(g(w‚Īľ))=(D j).E g` | Expectation of a product of single-coordinate functions factors; a function of one coordinate has that coordinate's marginal expectation. **Proved.** |
| `compound` | `(compound D‚āĀ D‚āā).p (s,w) = D‚āĀ.p s ¬∑ ‚ąŹŠĶĘ (D‚āā s i).p (wŠĶĘ)` | Compound (two-stage) design: stage-1 design `D‚āĀ` on `ő©‚āĀ`, then conditionally on `s`, independent within-coordinate designs `D‚āā s i` (which may depend on `s`). |
| `FiniteDesign.E_compound` / `E_compound_factor` | `E[h(s)¬∑g(w‚Īľ)] = E_s[h(s)¬∑(D‚āā s j).E g]` | The stage-2 collapse engine: a stage-1 quantity times a single-group within-function reduces the inner randomization to that group's conditional marginal. **Proved.** |
| `FiniteDesign.E_compound_tower` | `(compound D‚āĀ D‚āā).E F = E_s[(prodDesign (D‚āā s)).E (w ‚Ü¶ F(s,w))]` | Tower property: the joint expectation is the stage-1 expectation of the stage-2 conditional expectation (`CompoundVariance.lean`). **Proved.** |
| `FiniteDesign.Var_compound_eq_tower` | `Var X = E_s[Var_{w‚ą£s} X] + Var_s(E_{w‚ą£s} X)` | **Law of total variance** for the two-stage design: total variance = expected within-stage variance + variance of the stage-2 conditional mean. The gateway to the between-group / within-group variance decomposition (HH Thm 4/6). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `FiniteDesign.E_prod_apply‚āā` / `Cov_prod_apply_of_ne` / `Var_prod_apply` (`ProductVariance.lean`) | `E[g(wŠĶĘ)h(w‚Īľ)]=Eg¬∑Eh` (`i‚Č†j`); `Cov=0` (`i‚Č†j`); `Var(g(w‚Īľ))=(D j).Var g` | Cross-coordinate independence of the product design: functions of distinct coordinates are uncorrelated. **Proved.** |
| `FiniteDesign.Var_prod_linear_comb` | `(prodDesign D).Var (‚ąĎŠĶĘ cŠĶĘ gŠĶĘ(wŠĶĘ)) = ‚ąĎŠĶĘ cŠĶĘ¬≤¬∑(D i).Var gŠĶĘ` | Variance of a linear combination of single-coordinate functions = sum of coordinate variances (the within-group variance term). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### `Experimentation/TwoStageInterference/{Basic,Stratified,Unbiased,Effects}.lean` ‚ÄĒ two-stage estimands, stratified interference, unbiasedness

Hudgens & Halloran (2008). Groups `őĻ`, group `i` of size `n i`; within-group assignment
`WAssign n i = Fin (n i) ‚Üí Bool`; two-stage `jointDesign D‚āĀ Ōą ŌÜ = compound D‚āĀ (fun s i => if s i then Ōą i else ŌÜ i)`.
Partial interference is structural (the product over groups); the only modeling restriction is
*within*-group stratified interference, needed only for the variance theory (Phase 1 unbiasedness
holds without it). Stage-1 and within-group design propensities (`C/N`, `mŠĶĘ/nŠĶĘ`) enter as
hypotheses ‚ÄĒ the design-based known-propensity stance; Assumption 1's mixed strategy satisfies them.
Plan: `doc/hudgens_halloran_plan.md`. Namespace `Causalean.Experimentation.TwoStageInterference`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `WAssign` / `StratAssign` / `jointDesign` | `Fin (n i)‚ÜíBool`; `őĻ‚ÜíBool`; `compound D‚āĀ (‚Ä¶)` | Within-group and stage-1 assignment spaces and the joint two-stage design. |
| `indMean` / `groupMean` / `popMean` / `indMarg` / `popMarg` | `»≥_ij(z;ŌĀ)` conditional mean; group/population averages; marginal versions | The average-potential-outcome estimands (conditional on own treatment `z`, and the marginal forms). |
| `CE_direct` / `CE_indirect` / `CE_total` / `CE_overall` | `»≥(0;Ōą)‚ąí»≥(1;Ōą)`; `»≥(0;ŌÜ)‚ąí»≥(0;Ōą)`; `»≥(0;ŌÜ)‚ąí»≥(1;Ōą)`; `»≥(ŌÜ)‚ąí»≥(Ōą)` | The four population causal-effect contrasts (direct, indirect/spillover, total, overall). |
| `groupEst` / `popEst` / `estDirect` / `estIndirect` / `estTotal` | within-group H√°jek mean; population average over the selected groups; the effect contrasts | The estimators (fixed-count H√°jek form, hence linear in the treatment indicators). |
| `numTreatedOthers` / `stratExpo` / `StratifiedInterference` / `exists_strat_factor` | count of other treated; `(w‚Īľ, #others)`; Assumption 2; factorization `Y i j w = g (stratExpo i j w)` | Stratified interference (`Stratified.lean`): outcomes depend on the assignment only through own treatment and the count of other treated units; hence factor through the exposure. **Proved.** |
| `E_groupEst` | `E[Ň∂_i(z;ŌĀ)] = »≥_i(z;ŌĀ)` | Within-group unbiasedness under constant treatment propensity `mŠĶĘ/nŠĶĘ`. **Proved.** |
| `E_popEst` / `E_popEst_pick` | `E[Ň∂(z;Ōą)] = »≥(z;Ōą)` and the strategy-agnostic generalization | **Theorem 1**: population unbiasedness, via the stage-2 collapse engine; `E_popEst_pick` covers either allocation strategy. **Proved.** |
| `E_estDirect` / `E_estIndirect` / `E_estTotal` | `E[effect estimator] = CE_{D,I,T}` | **Theorems 1‚Äď3**: the direct/indirect/total effect estimators are exactly unbiased for their estimands. **Proved.** |
| `CE_total_decomp` | `CE^T = CE^D + CE^I` | The total effect decomposes into the direct plus indirect effects. **Proved.** |

### `Experimentation/TwoStageInterference/{Variance,VarianceMoments,VarianceConservative}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Theorem 5 (within-group Neyman variance) + conservative estimator (conservativeness proved)

Hudgens & Halloran (2008, JASA), "Toward Causal Inference With Interference". Within one group under
a completely randomized experiment that always treats exactly `K` of `n` units, and under stratified
interference (Assumption 2), each unit's outcome on the design support is two-valued ‚ÄĒ `a j` when
treated, `b j` when in control ‚ÄĒ so the difference-in-means estimator is linear in the treatment
indicators and its randomization variance is the classical Neyman completely-randomized variance.
The two-valued outcomes `a, b` are taken as data; the link to the stratified factorization
(`exists_strat_factor`) is upstream. The completely-randomized first/second-order propensities enter
as hypotheses `hmean` (`E[T‚Īľ]=K/n`) and `hpair` (`E[T‚ĪľT‚āĖ]=K(K‚ąí1)/(n(n‚ąí1))`, `j‚Č†k`). Namespace
`Causalean.Experimentation.TwoStageInterference`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `T` | `T j = ind (fun w => w j = true)` | Treatment indicator of unit `j` on the assignment space `Fin n ‚Üí Bool` (design-independent). |
| `tauHat` | `(‚ąĎ‚Īľ b‚Īľ(1‚ąíT‚Īľ))/(n‚ąíK) ‚ąí (‚ąĎ‚Īľ a‚ĪľT‚Īľ)/K` | Difference-in-means estimator `»≥(0)‚ąí»≥(1)`: control mean minus treated mean as a function of the assignment. |
| `popMeanV` / `S1` / `S0` / `Stau` | `xŐĄ=(‚ąĎx)/n`; `S‚āĀ=‚ąĎ(a‚Īľ‚ąíńĀ)¬≤/(n‚ąí1)`; `S‚āÄ=‚ąĎ(b‚Īľ‚ąíbŐĄ)¬≤/(n‚ąí1)`; `SŌĄ=‚ąĎ((a‚Īľ‚ąíb‚Īľ)‚ąí(ńĀ‚ąíbŐĄ))¬≤/(n‚ąí1)` | Population sample variances (Neyman, `n‚ąí1` denominator) of treated-state, control-state outcomes and unit effects. |
| `FiniteDesign.Var_add_const` | `Var(X+c)=Var X` | Variance is shift-invariant (substrate helper). |
| `sum_sum_ite_quadratic` | `‚ąĎ‚Īľ‚ąĎ‚āĖ c‚Īľc‚āĖ(if j=k then vd else vo) = vo(‚ąĎc)¬≤+(vd‚ąívo)‚ąĎc¬≤` | The double-sum-to-split-form algebraic core turning `Var_linear_comb` into the Neyman split. |
| `sum_sub_mean_sq` | `‚ąĎ‚Īľ(x‚Īľ‚ąíxŐĄ)¬≤ = ‚ąĎ‚Īľx‚Īľ¬≤ ‚ąí (‚ąĎx)¬≤/m` | Sum of squared deviations equals raw second moment minus squared first moment over `m`. |
| `cov_diag` / `cov_offdiag` | `Cov(T‚Īľ,T‚Īľ)=(K/n)(1‚ąíK/n)`; `Cov(T‚Īľ,T‚āĖ)=K(K‚ąí1)/(n(n‚ąí1))‚ąí(K/n)¬≤` (`j‚Č†k`) | Complete-randomization indicator covariances from `hmean`/`hpair`. **Proved.** |
| `Var_tauHat` | `ŌĀ.Var(tauHat) = S‚āĀ/K + S‚āÄ/(n‚ąíK) ‚ąí SŌĄ/n` | **Theorem 5 (Neyman form).** Derived (not assumed) from the indicator covariances via the linear-comb double sum and moment algebra. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `obsMeanTreated` / `obsMeanControl` | `(‚ąĎ‚ĪľT‚Īľa‚Īľ)/K`; `(‚ąĎ‚Īľ(1‚ąíT‚Īľ)b‚Īľ)/(n‚ąíK)` | Empirical means among observed treated / control units (`VarianceConservative.lean`). |
| `ShatTreated` / `ShatControl` | `‚ąĎ‚ĪľT‚Īľ(a‚Īľ‚ąíńĀ_obs)¬≤/(K‚ąí1)`; `‚ąĎ‚Īľ(1‚ąíT‚Īľ)(b‚Īľ‚ąíbŐĄ_obs)¬≤/(n‚ąíK‚ąí1)` | Observed sample variances among treated / control units. |
| `varHat` | `Ňú‚āĀ/K + Ňú‚āÄ/(n‚ąíK)` | Conservative variance estimator (empirical analogue of the first two Neyman terms), computable from one realized assignment. |
| `varHat_nonneg` | `0 ‚Č§ varHat` (for `1‚Č§K`, `K+1‚Č§n`) | Pointwise nonnegativity: sum of two nonnegative sample variances over positive counts. **Proved.** |
| `E_congr_supp` / `E_Shat` (`VarianceMoments.lean`) | support-congruence for `E`; `E[(1/(M‚ąí1))‚ąĎ U‚Īľ(x‚Īľ‚ąíxŐĄ_U)¬≤] = S_x` | Generic unbiasedness of the observed sample variance under a `{0,1}` selection family `U` with the CRE moments (first `M/n`, pairwise `M(M‚ąí1)/(n(n‚ąí1))`, deterministic support total `M`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `E_ShatTreated` / `E_ShatControl` | `ŌĀ.E Ňú‚āĀ = S‚āĀ`; `ŌĀ.E Ňú‚āÄ = S‚āÄ` | The observed treated/control sample variances are unbiased for the population variances under sampling without replacement (`E_Shat` with `U=T`,`M=K` and `U=1‚ąíT`,`M=n‚ąíK`; control moments derived from `hmean`/`hpair`/`hsupp`). **Proved.** |
| `E_varHat_conservative` | `ŌĀ.Var(tauHat) ‚Č§ ŌĀ.E(varHat)` | **Conservativeness (HH Eq. 9).** `E[vŐāar] = S‚āĀ/K + S‚āÄ/(n‚ąíK) = Var + SŌĄ/n ‚Č• Var`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** Hypotheses: CRE moments `hmean`/`hpair`, deterministic treated count on the support `hsupp` (`‚ąĎ‚ĪľT‚Īľ=K`), and non-degeneracy `2‚Č§K`, `K+2‚Č§n` (so both sample variances are well-defined). |

### `Experimentation/TwoStageInterference/{StageOne,BetweenGroup}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Theorem 4 (two-stage variance decomposition)

The between-group / within-group variance decomposition of the population estimator `Ň∂(z;Ōą)` under
the two-stage mixed-strategy design, assembled from the law of total variance
(`Var_compound_eq_tower`), the product-design within-group variance (`Var_prod_linear_comb`), and the
stage-1 SRS sample-mean variance. Namespace `Causalean.Experimentation.TwoStageInterference`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SmuVar` / `Var_srs_mean` (`StageOne.lean`) | `SmuVar őľ = ‚ąĎ(őľŠĶĘ‚ąíőľŐĄ)¬≤/(N‚ąí1)`; `D‚āĀ.Var((1/m)‚ąĎŠĶĘ UŠĶĘőľŠĶĘ) = (1‚ąím/N)/m ¬∑ SmuVar őľ` | The variance of a simple-random-sample mean of `m` of `N` fixed numbers is the finite-population correction `(1‚ąím/N)/m` times their population variance ‚ÄĒ the between-group variance term. Given the SRS selection moments (first `m/N`, pairwise `m(m‚ąí1)/(N(N‚ąí1))`, diagonal `(m/N)(1‚ąím/N)`). **Proved, derived constant.** |
| `Var_groupAgg` (`BetweenGroup.lean`) | `Var((‚ąĎŠĶĘ 1(SŠĶĘ=Ōą)¬∑gŠĶĘ(wŠĶĘ))/C) = (1‚ąíC/N)/C¬∑SmuVar((Ōą¬∑).E g¬∑) + (1/(CN))‚ąĎŠĶĘ (Ōą i).Var gŠĶĘ` | The abstract two-stage variance decomposition for an arbitrary per-group statistic `g`; Theorems 4 and 6 are corollaries (instantiating `g` as a single-treatment mean / a per-group effect difference). Assembled via the law of total variance. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `Var_popEst` (`BetweenGroup.lean`) | `Var(Ň∂(z;Ōą)) = (1‚ąíC/N)/C ¬∑ ŌÉ¬≤_D + (1/(CN))‚ąĎŠĶĘ Var(Ň∂_i(z;Ōą)‚ą£SŠĶĘ=Ōą)` | **Theorem 4**: the two-stage randomization variance of the population mean estimator splits into a between-group SRS term (finite-population correction `(1‚ąíC/N)/C` on the group-level means `»≥_i(z;Ōą)`) plus a within-group term averaging the per-group conditional variances. A corollary of `Var_groupAgg`. Hypotheses: within propensities `mŠĶĘ/nŠĶĘ`, stage-1 marginal `C/N` and pair `C(C‚ąí1)/(N(N‚ąí1))`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `Var_estDirect` (`BetweenGroupEffect.lean`) | `Var(ńąE^D(Ōą)) = (1‚ąíC/N)/C ¬∑ ŌÉ¬≤_D + (1/(CN))‚ąĎŠĶĘ Var(ńąE^D_i(Ōą)‚ą£SŠĶĘ=Ōą)` | **Theorem 6**: the two-stage variance of the direct-*effect* estimator `Ň∂(0;Ōą)‚ąíŇ∂(1;Ōą)` ‚ÄĒ between-group SRS term over the group-level direct effects `»≥_i(0;Ōą)‚ąí»≥_i(1;Ōą)` plus a within-group term averaging the per-group direct-effect-estimator variances. Corollary of `Var_groupAgg` with `gŠĶĘ` the per-group direct-effect estimator (`Ň∂(0;Ōą)‚ąíŇ∂(1;Ōą) = (‚ąĎŠĶĘ 1(SŠĶĘ=Ōą)¬∑dŠĶĘ(wŠĶĘ))/C`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### `Experimentation/TwoStageInterference/Asymptotic/{Setup,Consistency,CLT,CLTDischarge,CLTDischargeMain,Wald}.lean` ‚ÄĒ Liu‚ÄďHudgens (2014) large-sample inference (minimal)

Liu & Hudgens (2014, JASA), "Large-Sample Randomization Inference of Causal Effects in the Presence of
Interference" ‚ÄĒ the asymptotic theory for the two-stage design, minimal direct-effect slice. Regime:
many groups (`m ‚Üí ‚ąě`), exact permutation first stage, **homogeneity**. The Prop 5.1 CLT is proved the
paper's own way ‚ÄĒ condition on the selection (groups are then independent), conditional CLT, lift to
unconditional via homogeneity ‚ÄĒ so **no finite-population (H√°jek) CLT is needed**. The conditional CLT
is now discharged from primitives (the independent-summands CLT `prodDesign_clt`), so Prop 5.1 rests
on explicit homogeneity + regularity, not a CLT black box. Plan: `doc/liu_hudgens_plan.md`. Namespace
`Causalean.Experimentation.TwoStageInterference`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LHExperiment` / `E_estD` / `var_estD` (`Setup.lean`) | the sequence-of-experiments bundle; `jointD.E estD = DEbar`; `jointD.Var estD = directVar` | The bundle carrying the two-stage design + its known propensities; the unbiasedness and Theorem-6 variance bridges (from `E_estDirect` / `Var_estDirect`). |
| `estDirect_consistent` (`Consistency.lean`) | `Var ‚Üí 0 ‚üĻ DŐāE(őĪ‚āĀ) ‚ąí DEŐÖ ‚Üí‚āö 0` | Consistency of the direct-effect estimator via Chebyshev + the vanishing design variance. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `tendsto_E_of_uniformBound` / `Pr_compound_eq_E_condPr` (`CLT.lean`) | mixture lifting (`E F_n ‚Üí L` from uniform bounds); `(compound D‚āĀ D‚āā).Pr P = D‚āĀ.E (conditional Pr)` | Reusable substrate: the unconditional law is the design-average of the conditional laws (from `E_compound_tower`), and uniform conditional convergence lifts through the average. **Proved.** |
| `directEffect_clt` (`CLT.lean`) | studentized `(DŐāE ‚ąí DEŐÖ)/‚ąöVar ‚Üí_d N(0,1)` | **Proposition 5.1** (direct effect), modulo the uniform conditional-CLT regularity `hcond` (the paper's Lindeberg + homogeneity). Proved by the tower bridge + mixture lifting. The `hcond`-free primitive form is `directEffect_clt_homogeneous` below. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `tendsto_E_of_uniformBound_ae` / `Var_sub_const` (`CLTDischarge.lean`) | support-restricted mixture lifting (`E F_n ‚Üí L` from bounds only where `D‚āĀ.p s ‚Č† 0`); `Var(X‚ąíc) = Var X` | The "a.e." variant of `tendsto_E_of_uniformBound`: the stage-1 average only weights support points, so a uniform bound on the support suffices to lift the conditional limit ‚ÄĒ needed because homogeneity (`hhom`) only relates conditional CDFs across the support. Plus the constant-shift variance invariance. **Proved.** |
| `Homogeneous` / `DEbar_eq_of_homogeneous` / `directVar_eq_of_homogeneous` / `stud_eq_sum_of_homogeneous` (`CLTDischarge.lean`) | the homogeneity + regularity bundle; `DEbar = őī`; `directVar = v/C`; `stud(s,w) = ‚ąĎŠĶĘ g‚āõ,ŠĶĘ(wŠĶĘ)` on the support | The faithful encoding of Prop 5.1's hypotheses (constant group direct effects `őī`, constant within-group variance `v n > 0`, bounded centered per-group estimator `M`, exactly-`C` selections on the support, the `B ‚Üí 0` / `card¬∑B¬≥ ‚Üí 0` rates, and selection-independence `hhom`), with the reductions that turn the conditional studentized statistic into a normalized independent sum. **Proved.** |
| `condCLT_ref` (`CLTDischargeMain.lean`) | conditional CDF at the reference selection `‚Üí ő¶(t)` | The conditional CLT, obtained by feeding the homogeneity-reduced per-coordinate summands `g‚āõ‚āÄ,ŠĶĘ` (mean-zero, unit total variance via `Var_prod_linear_comb`, uniformly bounded) into the independent-summands CLT `prodDesign_clt`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `directEffect_clt_homogeneous` (`CLTDischargeMain.lean`) | studentized `(DŐāE ‚ąí DEŐÖ)/‚ąöVar ‚Üí_d N(0,1)` | **Proposition 5.1, fully primitive.** Identical conclusion to `directEffect_clt` but `hcond`-free: it rests on the explicit `Homogeneous` bundle. The conditional CLT is `condCLT_ref` (from `prodDesign_clt`); `hhom` lifts the uniform bound across the stage-1 support; `tendsto_E_of_uniformBound_ae` averages it. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `prodDesign_Pr_reindex` (`DesignBased/ProductReindex.lean`) / `hhom_of_identical` / `directEffect_clt_identical` (`Asymptotic/Identical.lean`) | `(prodDesign D).Pr(P‚ąė(¬∑‚ąėŌÉ)) = (prodDesign (D‚ąėŌÉ)).Pr P`; `hhom` for identical groups; the CLT under identical groups | **Prop 5.1 on literal identical groups** ‚ÄĒ `hhom` is *derived*, not assumed. The reindex lemma (a pure finite-sum permutation invariance) shows the conditional CDF is selection-independent when all groups are identical (a permutation `ŌÉ` matching two equal-size selections, built via `Equiv.sumCompl` + `Fintype.equivOfCardEq`, relabels one conditional law to the other). `directEffect_clt_identical` then rests only on identical groups + bounded outcomes + the many-groups rate. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `wald_coverage_oracle` (`Wald.lean`) | `1 ‚ąí ő≥ ‚Č§ liminf Pr(\|DŐāE ‚ąí DEŐÖ\| ‚Č§ z¬∑‚ąöVar)` | Asymptotic coverage of the oracle Wald interval `DŐāE ¬Ī z¬∑‚ąöVar`, from the CLT + standard-normal CDF symmetry (transplant of the A-S oracle `wald_coverage`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `wald_coverage_feasible` (`WaldFeasible.lean`) | `1 ‚ąí ő≥ ‚Č§ liminf Pr(\|DŐāE ‚ąí DEŐÖ\| ‚Č§ z¬∑‚ąöVŐā)` | Asymptotic coverage of the **feasible** Wald interval `DŐāE ¬Ī z¬∑‚ąöVŐā` using an *estimated* variance, from the per-threshold CLT + a single conservative-consistency input `hVhat` (`Pr(VŐā < (1‚ąíőĶ)¬∑Var) ‚Üí 0`), with the estimator `Vh` abstract ‚ÄĒ transplant of the A-S feasible `wald_coverage_feasible`. Discharging `hVhat` to a concrete two-stage estimator (the A-S `_of_conditions` analogue) is future work. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### `UnknownInterference/` ‚ÄĒ S√§vje‚ÄďAronow‚ÄďHudgens (2021), EATE under unknown interference (arXiv:1711.06399)

Bernoulli-design core of "Average treatment effects in the presence of unknown interference." Units `U`, treatment vector `z : U ‚Üí Bool`, potential outcomes `y i z` depending on the *whole* assignment (interference), of unknown form. Scope: Bernoulli design with the Horvitz‚ÄďThompson **and H√°jek** estimators (the paper's conceptual heart). Deferred (see `doc/savje_aronow_hudgens_plan.md`): complete/paired/arbitrary designs (őĪ-mixing), the variance-estimator results, external validity. No CLT ‚ÄĒ the paper proves Chebyshev is sharp and Gaussian approximations generally fail under these conditions.

| Declaration (file) | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Interferes` / `InterfDep` / `dbar` (`Basic.lean`) | interference indicator `I ‚Ąď i`; interference dependence (some `‚Ąď` interferes with both `i`,`j`); average dependence `dŐĄ = n‚ĀĽ¬Ļ‚ąĎŠĶĘ‚ąĎ‚Īľ 1[InterfDep i j]` | The interference structure; `dŐĄ` is the basis for "restricted interference" `dŐĄ = o(n)`. **Defs** |
| `tau` / `ACATE` / `EATE` / `htEst` (`Basic.lean`) | `ŌĄ_i(z‚āčŠĶĘ)=y_i(1;z‚āčŠĶĘ)‚ąíy_i(0;z‚āčŠĶĘ)`; `ACATE(z)=n‚ĀĽ¬Ļ‚ąĎŠĶĘŌĄ_i`; `EATE = E[ACATE(Z)]`; the Horvitz‚ÄďThompson estimator | The estimand (assignment-conditional ATE marginalized over the design; generalizes ATE) + the HT estimator. **Defs** |
| `y_eq_of_agree_on_interferers` (`Basic.lean`) | if `z`,`z'` agree on every unit interfering with `i` then `y i z = y i z'` | A unit's outcome depends only on its interferers ‚ÄĒ the bridge to disjoint-block independence. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `bernoulliDesign` (`Bernoulli.lean`) | `prodDesign` of per-unit coin flips with `Pr(Z_i=1)=p_i` | The Bernoulli design; cross-unit independence is structural. **Def + marginal lemmas** |
| `htEst_unbiased` (`Unbiased.lean`) | `E[ń§T] = EATE` under the Bernoulli design | HT is **exactly unbiased** for EATE (`Z_i ‚ä• Z‚āčŠĶĘ` ‚áí `E[Z_iY_i/p_i]=E[y_i(1;Z‚āčŠĶĘ)]`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `var_htEst_le` (`VarianceBound.lean`) | `Var(ń§T) ‚Č§ k‚Āī¬∑dŐĄ/n` | The quantitative heart (paper's body bound): covariances vanish off the interference-dependence graph (disjoint-block independence `Cov_prod_disjoint_zero`), surviving ones bounded by `k‚Āī`, counted by `dbarCount = n¬∑dŐĄ`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `htEst_consistent_eate` (`Consistency.lean`) | along a sequence with `k‚Āī¬∑dŐĄ/n ‚Üí 0`, `Pr(\|ń§T ‚ąí EATE\| ‚Č• őĶ) ‚Üí 0` | **Flagship (Prop, Bernoulli rate).** HT is consistent for EATE under restricted interference, no structural knowledge of the interference ‚ÄĒ Chebyshev on the variance bound. The paper's `O_p(‚ąö(dŐĄ/n))` rate is exactly this Chebyshev reading. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `root_n_var` (`Consistency.lean`) | `dŐĄ ‚Č§ C ‚áí n¬∑Var(ń§T) ‚Č§ k‚Āī¬∑C` | **Root-n consistency (Corollary)** under bounded interference: the variance is `O(1/n)`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `hajekEst` / `hajek_consistent_eate` (`Hajek.lean`) | the H√°jek (ratio/IPW) estimator `√ā‚āĀ/BŐā‚āĀ ‚ąí √ā‚āÄ/BŐā‚āÄ`; along a sequence with `k‚Āī¬∑dŐĄ/n ‚Üí 0`, uniformly bounded `k`, and bounded potential-outcome moments, `ń§A ‚Üí‚āö EATE` | **H√°jek consistency.** Each numerator `√ā‚āú ‚Üí‚āö »≥(t)` (vanishing variance) and each realized-weight normalizer `BŐā‚āú ‚Üí‚āö 1` (`E[BŐā‚āú]=1`); the Slutsky ratio step gives `ń§A ‚Üí‚āö »≥(1)‚ąí»≥(0) = EATE`. Adds the uniform-`k` bound and Assumption-C potential-outcome moments beyond the HT hypotheses (disclosed). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `VhatBer` / `E_VhatBer_bias` (`Confidence.lean`) | conventional variance estimator `VŐā_Ber = n‚ĀĽ¬≤‚ąĎń§TŠĶĘ¬≤`; `E[VŐā_Ber] ‚ąí Var(ń§T) = n‚ĀĽ¬≤(‚ąĎ(E ń§TŠĶĘ)¬≤ ‚ąí ‚ąĎ_{i‚Č†j}Cov(ń§TŠĶĘ,ń§T‚Īľ))` | **Anti-conservativeness mechanism** (the warning): the off-diagonal covariances (nonzero only on the interference graph, either sign) are the bias making `VŐā_Ber` understate uncertainty. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `var_htEst_le_inflated` (`Confidence.lean`) | `(‚ąÄ i, degDep i ‚Č§ D) ‚áí Var(ń§T) ‚Č§ (1+D)¬∑E[VŐā_Ber]` | **Conservative inflation** (the fix, in expectation): inflating by an interference-degree bound restores conservativeness ‚ÄĒ the cleaner cousin of the paper's `d_max`/spectral-radius estimators. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chebyshev_ci_eate` / `eate_ci_kbound` (`Confidence.lean`) | `Var(ń§T) ‚Č§ V ‚áí Pr(\|ń§T ‚ąí EATE\| ‚Č§ ‚ąö(V/őĪ)) ‚Č• 1‚ąíőĪ`; concretely the interval `ń§T ¬Ī ‚ąö(k‚Āī¬∑dŐĄ/(n¬∑őĪ))` covers EATE `‚Č• 1‚ąíőĪ` | **Finite-sample confidence interval for EATE** under unknown interference, Chebyshev-based (the paper proves a CLT fails). Exact, from HT unbiasedness + the proven variance bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `FiniteDesign.E_prod_block_mul` / `Cov_prod_disjoint_zero` (`DesignBased/ProductBlock.lean`) | functions of disjoint coordinate blocks of a product design factor in expectation / are uncorrelated | Substrate (block generalization of `E_prod_apply‚āā`/`Cov_prod_apply_of_ne`), proved via the existing product-measure independence bridge; requires `MeasurableSpace`/`MeasurableSingletonClass` on the coordinate types. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `FiniteDesign.TendstoInProb` / `tendstoInProb_of_var` / `tendstoInProb_div_one` / `.sub` / `.add` / `.abs` / `.const_mul` ¬∑ `BoundedInProb` / `boundedInProb_of_var_bound` / `TendstoInProb.mul_boundedInProb` (`DesignBased/InProb.lean`) | convergence in probability for finite designs; the Chebyshev consistency engine; the Slutsky ratio step (`X‚Üí‚āöa`, `Y‚Üí‚āö1`, `|a|‚Č§M` ‚áí `X/Y‚Üí‚āöa`); closure under sums/differences/absolute value/scaling; uniform tightness (`BoundedInProb`) with a variance-bound criterion and the product-tightness engine `o_p √ó O_p = o_p` | Reusable in-probability substrate for ratio estimators (H√°jek); `mul_boundedInProb` is the delta-method-remainder engine behind the bipartite denominator-tightness discharge. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

## 11. `SCM/ID/` ‚ÄĒ Backdoor / Frontdoor do-calculus assembly theorems

The do-calculus Backdoor and Frontdoor identification theorems live in
`SCM/ID/Backdoor.lean`, `SCM/ID/BackdoorCriterion.lean`, and
`SCM/ID/Frontdoor.lean` (sorry-free), with the adjustment functionals in
`SCM/ID/Adjustment.lean`. See the SCM/ID sections above for the
per-declaration tables.

---

## Proof strategies for concrete DAGs

| Goal type | Recommended tactic | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `v ‚ąą G.parents w` | `by decide` | Finite membership |
| `G.parents v = ‚ąÖ` | `by native_decide` | Finset equality |
| `G.isRoot v` / `¬¨G.isRoot v` | `by native_decide` | Unfolds to `parents = ‚ąÖ` |
| `G.dSep X Y Z` / `¬¨G.dSep X Y Z` | `by decide` | Bayes Ball is computable |
| `directlyConfounded v w` | `by decide` | Finite search |
| `v ‚ąą cComponentOf w` | `by native_decide` | BFS computation |
| `cComponentOf v = S` | `by native_decide` | Finset equality |
| `G.isAncestor u v` | Term-mode `.edge`/`.trans` | More informative than `decide` |
| `¬¨G.isAncestor v v` | `G.isAncestor_irrefl v` | Direct theorem application |
| `edgeType u v = t` | `rfl` | Definitional equality |
